original_activities: Define the strategy of the general management, organize and coordinate its activities.
Ensure the organization and proper functioning of all activities linked to parliamentary committees in the field of budgetary affairs of the European Union.

Ensure institutional assistance and support to the parliamentary committees responsible for the budgetary affairs of the European Union, including with regard to the financial regulation, the system of own resources or the negotiations on the multiannual financial framework.
Providing specialist advice, in-depth analysis and research in the areas of budgeting and discharge.
Advise the Secretary General and cooperate with other Directorates General and the Legal Department.

Represent and/or ensure the representation of the general management in the bodies of Parliament, in internal meetings, as well as in interinstitutional forums and in joint or other committees and ensure their monitoring.

Ensure personnel management; organize and chair several management meetings (directors, unit heads) per month. Manage mail assigned to general management and verify all outgoing mail for the signature of the Secretary General and the President.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
24.refined_description: Unit 24 operates as the apex strategic management and coordination entity within its Directorate-General, reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament and exercising integrated leadership across general management governance and specialized parliamentary budgetary affairs support. The unit defines and implements strategic organizational direction while coordinating all consequent general management activities (20% of workload) and simultaneously ensures the comprehensive organization and proper functioning of all parliamentary committee activities specifically related to European Union budgetary affairs (20% of workload). Unit 24 provides essential institutional assistance and specialized technical support to parliamentary committees responsible for EU budgetary matters, delivering expert guidance on financial regulation, own resources systems, and multiannual financial framework negotiations (15% of workload), while concurrently conducting in-depth analytical research and delivering specialized advisory services on budgeting and discharge procedures (15% of workload). The unit maintains critical coordination relationships through advisory functions to the Secretary General and cooperative liaison with other Directorates-General and the Legal Department (10% of workload), and ensures strategic representation of general management across parliamentary bodies, internal meetings, interinstitutional forums, and joint committees with comprehensive monitoring oversight (6% of workload). Unit 24 exercises delegated authorizing officer financial authority (5% of workload) and manages personnel administration through monthly directorial and unit head management meetings, mail coordination, and outgoing correspondence verification for Secretary General and Presidential signature authority (5% of workload). The unit supervises three subordinate operational units—24-10, 24A, and 24B—ensuring integrated organizational performance across budgetary governance and parliamentary committee coordination domains.
24.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership & General Management Governance** (25%): Define organizational strategy and implement strategic direction for the Directorate-General (20%); exercise delegated authorizing officer financial authority (5%)

• **Parliamentary Committee Support & Budgetary Affairs Coordination** (35%): Ensure comprehensive organization and proper functioning of all parliamentary committee activities related to EU budgetary affairs (20%); provide institutional assistance, specialized technical support, and expert guidance on financial regulation, own resources systems, and multiannual financial framework negotiations (15%)

• **Expert Analysis, Advisory Services & Research** (15%): Deliver specialist advice, conduct in-depth analytical research, and provide expert counsel in budgeting and discharge procedures and related regulatory matters (15%)

• **Interinstitutional Coordination & Representation** (16%): Advise Secretary General and maintain cooperative liaison with other Directorates-General and Legal Department (10%); represent general management in parliamentary bodies, internal meetings, interinstitutional forums, and joint committees with comprehensive monitoring oversight (6%)

• **Personnel & Administrative Management** (5%): Manage personnel administration, conduct monthly management meetings with directors and unit heads, coordinate general management mail and outgoing correspondence verification for Secretary General and Presidential signature authority (5%)

original_activities: Ensure and support the human resources management of the Directorate-General, in particular with recruitments, mission orders, reporting, advice and counselling to staff.

Ensure and support the financial management of the Directorate-General, in particular through financial initiation, ex ante-verification, monitoring the budget and regular reporting, controlling the legality and compliance, advising on procurement and contract management.

Set up the Directorate-General's professional training plan, organise meaningful and high-level training sessions and events for the Directorate-General's staff, identify topics and content for which training is needed, liaise with the general training service and find trainers inside and outside the House, detect and promote external training options.
Develop and implement the Directorate-General's communication strategy, create and maintain communication tools such as the intranet pages, social media channels, newsletters and events.

Take care of the staff's IT and logistical needs, in cooperation with the dedicated services of the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity and the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics.

Support the Director-General and the DG in all administrative matters, liaise with other DGs and services, perform reporting duties, ensure presence in cross-DG networks and working groups, exchange and coordinate in particular with other policy DGs.
24-10.refined_description: Unit 24-10 functions as the administrative and operational backbone of the Directorate-General, serving as a Direction-level support entity within the parent organizational structure (Unit 24) and the parliamentary budgetary affairs management framework. The unit's integrated mission encompasses five core functional domains: comprehensive human resources management (40% of workload) including recruitment administration, mission order processing, personnel reporting mechanisms, and direct counselling and advisory services to staff; substantial financial stewardship and budgetary control (30% of workload) encompassing financial initiation procedures, ex ante-verification protocols, continuous budget monitoring, regular financial reporting, legality and compliance verification, and specialized advisory services on procurement and contract management matters; professional development and organizational learning infrastructure (10% of workload) through establishment of training plans, organization of high-calibre training sessions and professional development events, identification of training needs and skill gaps, liaison with the central training service, and identification and promotion of internal and external training resources; organizational communication strategy development and maintenance (10% of workload) including creation and maintenance of digital and traditional communication platforms such as intranet infrastructure, social media channels, newsletters, and organizational events; and integrated support services and cross-organizational coordination (10% of workload) encompassing staff information technology and logistical support coordination in active cooperation with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity and the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, comprehensive administrative support to the Director-General and senior management, inter-departmental and inter-DG liaison activities, execution of reporting obligations, participation in cross-DG networks and working groups, and strategic coordination mechanisms with policy-focused Directorates-General across the institution.
24-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources and Personnel Management** (40%): Recruitment administration and processes, mission order processing and administration, personnel reporting mechanisms, staff counselling and advisory services, and comprehensive human resources support across the Directorate-General

• **Financial Stewardship and Budgetary Control** (30%): Financial initiation and ex ante-verification procedures, continuous budget monitoring and regular reporting cycles, legality and compliance verification and control, specialized advisory services on procurement and contract management matters, and strategic financial guidance

• **Professional Development and Organizational Learning** (10%): Professional training plan development and implementation, organization of high-calibre training sessions and professional development events, identification of training needs and skill gaps, liaison with central training services, and identification and promotion of internal and external training resources and opportunities

• **Organizational Communication and Internal Engagement** (10%): Communication strategy development and implementation, intranet infrastructure creation and maintenance, social media channel and newsletter management, and organizational event planning and execution

• **Support Services and Cross-Organizational Coordination** (10%): Information technology and logistical support coordination with specialized DGs, inter-departmental and inter-DG liaison activities, comprehensive administrative support to Director-General and senior management, execution of reporting obligations, participation in cross-DG networks and working groups, and strategic coordination with policy-focused Directorates-General

24A.name: 24A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and supervision of the tasks and work of the two management units, including the implementation of the objectives of the General Directorate of Budgetary Affairs.

Supervise and coordinate within the General Secretariat of the European Parliament, the organization of annual procedures: budgetary procedure from preparation (N+1) and the discharge procedure (N-2), including the final publication of the budget, monitoring and implementation of decisions of the European Parliament (transfers, budgetary amendments, amending budgets).

Coordinate tasks related to important legislative files under the responsibility of the Committee on Budgets and Budgetary Control, including with other committees (Multiannual Financial Framework, Own Resources, Financial Regulation, etc.) throughout the year.

Assist political authorities in formal and informal interinstitutional relations concerning budgetary matters and budgetary control: representing the European Parliament at administrative level (working groups, interinstitutional dialogues).
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
24A.refined_description: Unit 24A functions as the strategic Direction within the General Directorate of Budgetary Affairs, serving as the primary coordination and supervisory hub for parliamentary budgetary governance and financial management within the European Parliament's institutional framework. The unit exercises comprehensive authority over two subordinate management units (24A10 and 24A20), implementing the overarching objectives and strategic priorities of the parent Directorate-General while cascading institutional performance standards and priorities throughout its subordinate structure. Unit 24A maintains direct supervisory and coordination responsibilities for critical annual budgetary procedures within the General Secretariat, encompassing the complete multi-year budgetary cycle from preparation phases (N+1 forward planning) through discharge procedures (N-2 retrospective review), including final budget publication protocols, systematic monitoring and implementation of European Parliament decisions regarding transfers, budgetary amendments, and amending budgets. The unit actively coordinates substantive legislative files under the purview of the Committee on Budgets and Budgetary Control, maintaining cross-institutional collaboration with other parliamentary committees on complex financial instruments including the Multiannual Financial Framework, Own Resources Decisions, and Financial Regulation throughout the parliamentary calendar. Additionally, Unit 24A provides advisory and representational support to political authorities in formal and informal interinstitutional relations concerning budgetary matters and budgetary control, representing the European Parliament at the administrative level through participation in working groups and interinstitutional dialogue mechanisms. The unit further exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions, thereby maintaining direct financial accountability and authorization authority within the European Parliament's budgetary execution framework.
24A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Directorate Management and Supervisory Authority** (40%): Direction, coordination, and comprehensive supervisory oversight of subordinate management units (24A10 and 24A20); implementation of General Directorate objectives; cascading of institutional priorities and performance standards

• **Annual Budgetary and Discharge Procedures** (30%): Supervision and coordination of budget preparation cycles (N+1 forward planning), discharge procedures (N-2 retrospective review), final budget publication protocols; systematic monitoring and implementation of European Parliament budgetary decisions including authorization of transfers, budgetary amendments, and amending budgets within the General Secretariat framework

• **Legislative Coordination and Committee Support** (20%): Coordination of substantive legislative files under Committee on Budgets and Budgetary Control responsibility; cross-committee coordination on Multiannual Financial Framework, Own Resources Decisions, and Financial Regulation throughout the parliamentary calendar

• **Interinstitutional Relations and Political Support** (5%): Advisory assistance to political authorities in formal and informal interinstitutional budgetary relations and budgetary control; administrative-level representation at working groups and interinstitutional dialogue mechanisms

• **Financial Authorization and Accountability Functions** (5%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities; direct financial accountability and authorization authority within European Parliament budgetary execution framework

24A10.name: 24A10
original_activities: Ensure the smooth running of the work of the BUDG committee. Organize meetings, coordinator meetings, preparatory meetings, programming, minutes, etc.

Organize interinstitutional meetings (own resources, Next generation EU, payments) and work related to the budgetary procedure and the multiannual financial framework (including trilogues and conciliation). Ensure contacts with other institutions.
Organize joint meetings. Manage the task force on the implementation of the 'Recovery and Resilience Facility'. Prepare joint files. Organize public hearings and delegations outside the workplace.
Prepare draft reports/opinions (legislative, non-legislative and budgetary procedures).
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Contribute to working groups and other horizontal issues within the general management.
24A10.refined_description: Unit 24A10 functions as the critical operational and substantive backbone of Direction 24A within the General Directorate of Budgetary Affairs, serving as the essential administrative and technical support unit for the European Parliament's Committee on Budgets (BUDG). The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses ensuring seamless parliamentary budgetary governance through integrated procedural coordination, interinstitutional engagement, and multi-framework policy support across the Parliament's budgetary authority and fiscal oversight responsibilities. Operating across five interconnected functional domains, Unit 24A10 simultaneously manages committee administration and procedural operations, orchestrates complex interinstitutional coordination mechanisms with other EU bodies, oversees joint parliamentary governance and implementation frameworks, develops legislative and policy documentation, and provides direct institutional support to individual Members of Parliament. At the procedural level, the unit ensures smooth BUDG committee functioning through comprehensive organization of all committee meetings, coordinator sessions, preparatory meetings, programming schedules, and official documentation/minutes management. At the interinstitutional level, the unit orchestrates formal meetings and substantive coordination addressing own resources, Next Generation EU funding mechanisms, and payment procedures, while simultaneously managing budgetary procedures, multiannual financial framework negotiations, trilogues, and conciliation processes as the primary institutional liaison point with other EU institutions. The unit additionally organizes joint parliamentary meetings, manages the Recovery and Resilience Facility implementation task force, prepares consolidated joint committee files, and coordinates public hearings and external delegations, while concurrently preparing comprehensive draft reports and opinions across legislative, non-legislative, and budgetary procedural frameworks, and providing direct written and oral support to MEPs serving as committee presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen. These interconnected activities collectively position Unit 24A10 as the operational nexus enabling the European Parliament's budgetary authority, fiscal governance, and institutional oversight functions.
24A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Administration and Procedural Operations** (25%): Ensures smooth functioning of BUDG committee through comprehensive organization of all committee meetings, coordinator meetings, preparatory sessions, programming schedules, and official documentation/minutes management; serves as operational foundation for committee governance

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Budgetary Framework Negotiations** (25%): Orchestrates interinstitutional meetings addressing own resources, Next Generation EU funding, and payment mechanisms; manages budgetary procedures, multiannual financial framework negotiations, trilogues, and conciliation processes; maintains institutional liaison functions with other EU bodies

• **Joint Parliamentary Governance and Recovery Implementation Oversight** (15%): Organizes joint parliamentary meetings, manages Recovery and Resilience Facility implementation task force, prepares consolidated joint committee files, and coordinates public hearings and external delegations

• **Legislative and Policy Document Development** (15%): Prepares comprehensive draft reports and opinions across legislative procedures, non-legislative procedures, and budgetary procedural frameworks

• **Member Support and Institutional Assistance** (20%): Provides direct written and oral support to MEPs exercising functions as committee presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen (15%); contributes to horizontal working groups and general management cross-cutting initiatives (5%)

24A20.name: 24A20
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the CONT commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.). Preparation of meeting files.
Prepare working documents, draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), draft opinions.

Ensure contact with other institutions, the Court of Auditors, OLAF and EPPO. Examine files that do not necessarily give rise to reports, in particular current files concerning the management by the Institutions or by the Member States of the budgetary resources of the European Union.
Organization of joint meetings and working group missions. Organize public hearings and delegations outside the workplace. Organizing remote meetings (Verkhovna Rada).
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Contribution to working groups and other horizontal issues within the general management.
Organize consultation of confidential documents.
24A20.refined_description: Unit 24A20 operates as the specialized secretariat and operational support unit for the Committee on Budgets (CONT Commission) within Direction 24A of the General Directorate for Budgetary Affairs, functioning as the critical administrative and substantive backbone for parliamentary budgetary governance and institutional oversight. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses five integrated functional domains: primary secretariat management (50% of workload) ensuring complete operational coordination of all CONT Commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming schedules, official minutes documentation, and preparation of detailed meeting files; substantive policy development (25% of workload) involving preparation of working documents, draft legislative and non-legislative reports, and draft opinions that inform committee deliberations; critical interinstitutional liaison responsibilities (10% of workload) establishing and maintaining contact with the European Court of Auditors, the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF), and the European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO), while simultaneously examining budgetary management files concerning both EU institutions and Member States' utilization of European Union financial resources; direct parliamentary support services (5% of workload) providing written and oral assistance to Members of the European Parliament serving in formal leadership roles (president, vice-president, rapporteur, opinion draftsman) and organizing joint commission meetings, working group missions, public hearings, external delegations, and remote engagement mechanisms (including sessions with the Verkhovna Rada); and administrative governance support (5% of workload) contributing to horizontal working groups and general management initiatives while managing consultation protocols for confidential documentation. As a leaf-node operational unit functioning alongside sibling unit 24A10 within Direction 24A, Unit 24A20 serves as an essential coordinating mechanism and facilitator of parliamentary budgetary oversight, legislative processes, and inter-institutional accountability within the European Parliament's institutional framework.
24A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Secretariat and Meeting Management** (50%): Complete operational coordination of CONT Commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming schedules, official minutes documentation, and comprehensive meeting file preparation—representing the unit's foundational and primary operational responsibility.

• **Substantive Policy Development and Legislative Support** (25%): Preparation of working documents, draft legislative and non-legislative reports, and draft opinions that provide analytical and procedural foundations for Commission deliberations and parliamentary decision-making on budgetary matters.

• **Interinstitutional Liaison and Budgetary Oversight** (10%): Establishment and maintenance of institutional contact with the European Court of Auditors, OLAF, and EPPO; examination of budgetary management files concerning EU institutions and Member States' administration of European Union financial resources, with analytical review conducted regardless of formal report generation.

• **Meeting Organization, Delegations, and Direct Member Support** (5%): Organization of joint commission meetings, working group missions, public hearings, external delegations, and remote engagement mechanisms (including Verkhovna Rada sessions); provision of written and oral assistance to MEPs exercising formal parliamentary roles (president, vice-president, rapporteur, opinion draftsman).

• **Horizontal Coordination and Information Governance** (5%): Contribution to horizontal working groups and general management initiatives (3%); organization and management of consultation protocols for confidential documentation access (2%).

24B.name: 24B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and supervision of the units and services of the department, as well as personnel management and the general organization of the department.

Provide committees, other parliamentary bodies as well as the President of Parliament with specialist advice and know-how using summary notes, in-depth analyzes and studies in the areas of budget and discharge.
Ensure project management in the areas of expertise of the management units.
Represent the institution in various internal committees and interinstitutional forums.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
24B.refined_description: Unit 24B operates as a strategic Directorate-level management and coordination function within the European Parliament's budgetary affairs framework, serving as the primary operational hub for the parent Directorate (Unit 24) while exercising direct supervisory authority over subordinate units 24B10 and 24B20, and functioning as a peer coordination entity with sibling units 24A and 24-10. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses five integrated functional activities totaling 100% of documented workload allocation, positioning it as both the operational and strategic nerve center for budgetary governance within the Parliament. The dominant activity—representing 50% of workload—involves ensuring comprehensive direction, coordination, and supervision of all departmental units and services, coupled with full responsibility for personnel management and general organizational administration of the directorate. A substantial secondary function—comprising 40% of workload—centers on delivering specialized expert counsel and institutional know-how to parliamentary committees, other parliamentary bodies, and the President of Parliament through the development and delivery of summary notes, in-depth analytical studies, and comprehensive examinations specifically addressing budgetary and discharge matters. Supporting these core functions, Unit 24B dedicates 5% of workload to project management activities within its established areas of technical expertise, 3% to institutional representation across internal committees and interinstitutional forums, and 2% to exercising subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for budgetary transactions and commitments. Collectively, these activities position Unit 24B as both an administrative command center for departmental operations and a critical analytical and advisory resource for parliamentary decision-making on budgetary and financial accountability matters.
24B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Leadership, Management, and Personnel Administration** (50%): Ensures comprehensive direction, coordination, and supervision of all departmental units and services (including subordinate units 24B10 and 24B20); manages personnel administration; provides general organizational administration of the directorate

• **Expert Advisory and Analytical Services** (40%): Delivers specialized advisory services and institutional know-how to parliamentary committees, other parliamentary bodies, and the President of Parliament through summary notes, in-depth analytical studies, and comprehensive examinations in budgetary and discharge domains

• **Project Management** (5%): Exercises project management responsibilities within areas of established technical expertise

• **Institutional Representation** (3%): Represents the institution in internal committees and interinstitutional forums

• **Financial Delegation and Authorization** (2%): Exercises subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for budgetary transactions and commitments

24B10.name: 24B10
original_activities: Provide of in-house expertise, fact-sheets, horizontal matters concerning decentralised bodies, pilot projects and preparatory actions.
Organise external studies concerning budgetary and budgetary control matters. Procurement and contract management.
Follow the parliamentary committees work, in particular the budgetary and discharge procedures, including the Help-desk EP discharge.
Enhance visibility (i.e. publishing studies, working on intra- and internet presentations).
Liaise with other bodies (i.e. Poldeps, EPRS, ECPRD, OECD, etc.) and the academic world working in the same area.
Other missions, in particular internal administration.
24B10.refined_description:
Unit 24B10 operates as a specialized analytical and advisory unit within Direction 24B, functioning as a critical knowledge hub for the European Parliament's budgetary governance framework. The unit serves as a leaf-node operational entity within the 24B directorate structure, coordinating with its sibling unit 24B20 to ensure comprehensive coverage of budgetary expertise functions across the institutional framework. The unit's integrated mission encompasses four primary operational domains: delivering in-house technical expertise and horizontal advisory services on decentralized bodies, pilot projects, and preparatory actions through the development of fact-sheets, policy briefs, and specialized guidance; organizing and managing external research studies, procurement processes, and contract administration concerning budgetary and budgetary control matters, thereby extending the Parliament's analytical capacity beyond internal resources; monitoring and supporting parliamentary committee activities with particular emphasis on budgetary and discharge procedures, including operation and maintenance of dedicated Help-desk EP discharge support services; and enhancing organizational visibility and accessibility through the publication of research outputs and development of intranet and internet-based presentation materials. Additionally, Unit 24B10 maintains strategic liaison relationships with external institutional partners including Poldeps, EPRS, ECPRD, and OECD, as well as engagement with academic institutions and research communities working in budgetary governance and related policy domains, positioning the European Parliament within broader policy research networks. The unit also executes internal administrative missions and ad hoc organizational assignments to ensure operational continuity and institutional support functions.

##
24B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Expert Knowledge Development and In-House Advisory Services** (40%): Provision of comprehensive in-house technical expertise, development of fact-sheets and policy briefs, and horizontal analytical support on decentralized bodies, pilot projects, and preparatory actions; establishment of institutional knowledge repositories and advisory capacity for budgetary governance matters.

• **External Research Commissioning and Procurement Management** (30%): Organization and oversight of external studies concerning budgetary and budgetary control matters; management of procurement processes and contract administration for research and analytical services; extension of Parliament's analytical capacity through external partnerships.

• **Parliamentary Process Support and Procedural Engagement** (10%): Active monitoring of parliamentary committee work with emphasis on budgetary and discharge procedures; provision of dedicated Help-desk EP discharge support services; coordination between analytical work and legislative processes.

• **Knowledge Dissemination and Institutional Visibility** (10%): Publication of research studies and analytical findings; development and maintenance of intranet and internet-based presentations to enhance accessibility and visibility of unit outputs to Parliament and external stakeholders.

• **External Stakeholder Engagement, Network Development, and Administrative Functions** (10%): Liaison with external institutional bodies (Poldeps, EPRS, ECPRD, OECD) and academic institutions; engagement with research communities working in budgetary governance; execution of internal administrative missions and ad hoc organizational requirements to ensure operational continuity.

24B20.name: 24B20
original_activities: Make detailed calculations and simulations for committees and other parliamentary bodies to support Parliament's priorities with relevant budgetary facts and figures.
Produce high-quality material and fact-based arguments for scrutiny and discharge purposes.
Collect and centralise available budgetary data.
Develop ways to increase the visibility of European Parliament budget and discharge positions through better communication and visualisation.
24B20.refined_description: Unit 24B20 operates as a specialized analytical and communications unit within Directorate 24B, functioning as the European Parliament's dedicated center for budgetary analysis, data management, and institutional transparency. As a leaf-node unit within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 24B20 maintains parallel coordination with sibling Unit 24B10 while reporting through parent Directorate 24B to advance the Parliament's strategic budgetary governance and oversight objectives. The unit executes four equally distributed workstreams (each representing 25% of total operational capacity) that collectively support Parliament's budgetary decision-making, scrutiny, and discharge responsibilities through integrated analytical, documentary, informational, and communicative functions. These activities ensure that Parliament's budgetary priorities and institutional accountability mechanisms are grounded in rigorous quantitative analysis, comprehensive factual evidence, centralized data management systems, and strategically enhanced public comprehension. By consolidating detailed financial calculations, substantive scrutiny documentation, institutional data repositories, and advanced visualization methodologies, Unit 24B20 strengthens both internal evidence-based decision-making processes and external democratic accountability by enhancing the visibility and accessibility of the European Parliament's budgetary positions and discharge determinations to parliamentary bodies, decision-makers, and broader stakeholder communities.
24B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Analytical Support and Quantitative Modeling** (25%): Execution of detailed financial calculations and simulations for parliamentary committees and institutional bodies, providing rigorous quantitative analysis and evidence-based financial data to substantiate Parliament's budgetary priorities and support informed strategic decision-making.

• **Scrutiny and Accountability Documentation** (25%): Production of high-quality, fact-based analytical materials and substantive evidence-based arguments specifically designed to support Parliament's scrutiny procedures and institutional discharge functions, ensuring accountability mechanisms are grounded in verified evidence and factual integrity.

• **Data Management and Centralization** (25%): Collection, consolidation, organization, and centralized management of available budgetary data across multiple institutional sources, establishing a comprehensive, unified institutional knowledge repository accessible to parliamentary bodies and decision-makers.

• **Strategic Communication and Institutional Visibility** (25%): Development and implementation of enhanced communication strategies and advanced data visualization methodologies to increase public and institutional awareness of the European Parliament's budgetary positions, discharge determinations, and financial accountability frameworks, thereby strengthening institutional transparency and democratic accountability.

original_activities: Define strategy and ensure the development of general management. Contribute to strengthening the role of Parliament in the exercise of its legislative, budgetary and control powers - in execution of the Parliamentary Reform 2024 program - through support to parliamentary committees, including expertise.

Ensure the proper functioning and administrative coordination of the work of parliamentary committees in the areas of cohesion policies, agriculture, fisheries, transport and tourism, social policies as well as the housing crisis in the EU.

Represent the general management within the bodies of Parliament and ensure the monitoring and consistency of parliamentary work. Cooperate with other internal services of the Parliament, including the services responsible for EU policies (DG ECTI, IUST, BUDG, EXPO), the Directorate for the Coordination of Committees and Legislative Activities (CCLA), DG PRES and the Cabinets.
Manage human resources and the general management budget, in collaboration with the management team.
Represent general management in interinstitutional relations.
Coordinate the procedures of the general management and the work of the central secretariat. Ensure compliance and correct application of applicable internal rules and procedures.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
22.refined_description: Unit 22 functions as the strategic apex and primary instrument of general management within the European Parliament, reporting directly to the Secretary General and exercising central coordination responsibilities across the institution's governance infrastructure. The unit's mission encompasses five interconnected pillars of institutional leadership and administration that collectively strengthen Parliament's exercise of legislative, budgetary, and control powers. Unit 22 allocates 25% of its workload to defining organizational strategy and advancing general management development through execution of the Parliamentary Reform 2024 program, including provision of specialized expertise to parliamentary committees across substantive policy domains. Concurrently, the unit dedicates 25% of resources to ensuring proper functioning and administrative coordination of parliamentary committee operations across cohesion policies, agriculture, fisheries, transport, tourism, social policies, and EU housing crisis matters, thereby maintaining institutional continuity and operational coherence. The unit commits 15% of effort to internal institutional representation and cross-functional coordination, functioning as the primary liaison mechanism with critical organizational partners including DG ECTI, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, the Directorate for the Coordination of Committees and Legislative Activities (CCLA), DG PRES, and the Cabinets, while monitoring consistency of parliamentary work. An additional 15% of resources is allocated to human resources management and general management budget administration in collaboration with the management team, ensuring fiscal and personnel stewardship. The unit reserves 10% of workload for representation of general management in interinstitutional relations, positioning Parliament's interests within the broader EU institutional framework, while dedicating 5% to coordination of general management procedures, central secretariat operations, and enforcement of applicable internal rules and regulations, and 5% to exercising delegated authorizing officer functions fulfilling essential fiduciary and administrative authorization responsibilities. Operationally, Unit 22 oversees three subordinate organizational units (22-10, 22A, and 22B), ensuring that strategic direction and administrative standards cascade effectively throughout Parliament's management infrastructure.
22.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership and Institutional Reform** (25%): Define organizational strategy and advance general management development; execute Parliamentary Reform 2024 program; provide specialized expertise to parliamentary committees; strengthen Parliament's legislative, budgetary, and control powers.

• **Parliamentary Committee Coordination and Administrative Operations** (25%): Ensure proper functioning and administrative coordination of parliamentary committee operations across cohesion policies, agriculture, fisheries, transport, tourism, social policies, and EU housing crisis domains; maintain institutional continuity and operational coherence.

• **Institutional Representation and Cross-Functional Coordination** (15%): Represent general management within parliamentary bodies; monitor and ensure consistency of parliamentary work; serve as primary liaison and coordination mechanism with DG ECTI, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, CCLA, DG PRES, and the Cabinets.

• **Resource and Financial Stewardship** (15%): Manage human resources and general management budget in collaboration with the management team; ensure fiscal and personnel stewardship.

• **Interinstitutional Relations and Procedural Governance** (10%): Represent general management in interinstitutional relations; position Parliament's interests within broader EU institutional framework.

• **Procedural Compliance and Administrative Authorization** (5%): Coordinate general management procedures and central secretariat operations; ensure compliance with applicable internal rules and regulations.

• **Delegated Authorizing Officer Functions** (5%): Exercise delegated authorizing officer responsibilities; fulfill essential fiduciary and administrative authorization functions.

original_activities: Manage the recruitment of staff for the DG, the organization chart of permanent, temporary and contractual staff positions, rights and obligations (absences, illness, leave, CCP, retirement, external activities), the mobility exercise, the selection of interns; monitoring of reintegration. Provide analysis and advice on strategic/regulatory issues relating to HR and their evolution; assist the DG in organizing the rating exercise.

Manage the centralized financial system; ensure initiation and financial verification in dedicated FMS and Webcontracts IT systems, management of financial players, archiving; meet the needs of DG units on budgetary management and financial procedures.

Develop the strategic planning and reporting cycle for general management, coordinate risk management and the business continuity plan. Ensure the representation of general management in various horizontal work groups; management and coordination of the DG's EMAS actions; ensure the coordination of 'simplification' actions.

Organize internal training in cooperation with DG PERS services (training plan, budget monitoring, strategic actions, etc.), study visits, secondments, fellowships; organize and finance external training; organize training for new arrivals in general management; organize the reception of trainees; provide a career advice service (RELOP).

Logistics: Ensure the logistical support necessary for the proper functioning of the DG; identify and manage the present and future spaces of the DG; support for office moves; liaise with DG ITEC on IT-related matters; overall coordination in the field of infrastructure and communication with other services of DG CASP, DG INLO and DG ITEC.

Coordinate, establish and draft the general management budget. Monitoring the budgetary envelopes of the DG and execution of its annual budget, ensuring the monitoring of budgetary transfers, discharge, handling audit questions with the internal auditor, in collaboration with the units of the general management. Write periodic activity reports. Ensure the coordination of internal control of the DG.

Management of the budget envelope for missions (3L and H3L), compliance with procedures, exemptions, special events; assist colleagues in units in the preparation of mission orders and expense declarations; management of the budget envelope for interns; management of the overtime regime.

Coordinate internal communication (writing and publication); organize internal events; manage the DG website; communication services to other units; ensure consistency according to the visual identity of the EP.
22-10.refined_description: Unit 22-10 functions as the central administrative and strategic support directorate for Unit 22, serving as the operational backbone of general management within the European Parliament's organizational structure while reporting to the Secretary General through the institutional hierarchy. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility across eight interconnected functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of its operational capacity, operating as an integrated system where human capital management, financial probity, strategic risk mitigation, staff development, operational logistics, budgetary discipline, and institutional communications function as interdependent components of cohesive governance. Unit 22-10 executes its mission in direct coordination with sibling units 22A and 22B while maintaining collaborative relationships with horizontal services including DG PERS, DG CASP, DG INLO, and DG ITEC, ensuring that all administrative and strategic functions align seamlessly with Unit 22's general management mandate. The unit's integrated approach ensures institutional effectiveness through simultaneous management of human resources stewardship, centralized financial systems, strategic planning frameworks, personnel development infrastructure, logistical coordination, budgetary oversight, specialized mission administration, and internal communications—collectively enabling Unit 22's strategic effectiveness and the European Parliament's operational excellence.
22-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources and Personnel Administration** (20%): Comprehensive management of staff recruitment across permanent, temporary, and contractual categories; organizational chart maintenance and updates; administration of employee rights and obligations including absences, illness, leave management, CCP provisions, retirement procedures, and external activity authorization; coordination of mobility exercises and intern selection processes; monitoring of reintegration procedures; provision of strategic HR analysis and advisory services on regulatory evolution; support for rating exercise organization and DG advisory services.

• **Financial and Budgetary Administration** (40%): Centralized financial system management (20%) encompassing financial initiation and verification through FMS and Webcontracts IT platforms; financial actor management and archiving protocols; provision of comprehensive budgetary and financial procedure guidance to all DG units. General management budget establishment, coordination, and drafting (10%) including budgetary envelope monitoring and annual execution tracking; budgetary transfer management and discharge handling; audit inquiry response and internal control coordination; periodic activity reporting. Specialized budget administration (8%) addressing mission expense protocols (3L and H3L compliance), intern budget envelope management, and overtime regime administration.

• **Strategic Planning, Risk Management, and Organizational Continuity** (15%): Development and coordination of strategic planning and reporting cycles for general management; establishment and coordination of comprehensive risk management frameworks; business continuity plan development, coordination, and maintenance; representation of general management in horizontal workgroups; management and coordination of EMAS environmental actions; coordination of institutional simplification initiatives.

• **Personnel Development and Organizational Support** (10%): Organization and coordination of internal training programs in cooperation with DG PERS services including training plan development and budget monitoring; facilitation of study visits, secondments, and fellowships; external training procurement and financing; specialized new arrival orientation and trainee reception coordination; career advisory services through RELOP mechanism.

• **Logistics, Infrastructure, and Internal Communications** (17%): Comprehensive logistical support ensuring proper DG functioning (10%) including present and future space identification and management; office relocation support; IT liaison and coordination with DG ITEC on infrastructure matters; cross-service infrastructure coordination with DG CASP, DG INLO, and DG ITEC. Internal communications coordination (7%) encompassing content creation and publication; internal event organization; DG website administration and maintenance; communication service provision to other units; visual identity consistency assurance across all communication channels.

22A.name: 22A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of the directorate, as well as the management of personnel and the general organization of the directorate.

Advise the deputies of Parliament in their areas of expertise and assist them as members of the parliamentary management committees, and in particular the presidents and rapporteurs, in the exercise of their functions.
Provide parliamentary committees with high-level expertise, up-to-date analysis and independent research in areas covered by the directorate.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Ensure project management in the areas of expertise of the management units and services.
Represent the institution in various internal committees and interinstitutional forums. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
22A.refined_description:
**

Unit 22A functions as the strategic directorate management and coordination hub within the European Parliament's administrative structure, operating at the first hierarchical level beneath the parent Directorate 22 and exercising central institutional governance functions. The unit bears comprehensive responsibility for ensuring the direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services (22A10, 22A20, 22A30, and 22A40), alongside full personnel management and general organizational administration of the directorate, which constitutes 60% of its operational workload. Unit 22A delivers multifaceted parliamentary support through integrated activity streams encompassing: provision of high-level expert advice to Members of Parliament and support for parliamentary management committee members (particularly presidents and rapporteurs) in executing their institutional functions; furnishing parliamentary committees with rigorous, independent research analysis and contemporary expertise within the directorate's substantive areas of responsibility; and exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer authority for budget execution and financial compliance. The unit additionally manages specialized strategic projects within the expertise domains of its subordinate management units and services, and represents the institution across internal committees and interinstitutional forums while maintaining essential operational coordination with Parliament's internal service structures. These integrated activities—allocating 20% of capacity to parliamentary support and advisory functions, 10% to financial and fiduciary authority, and 10% to project management and institutional representation—collectively ensure effective directorate governance, facilitate informed parliamentary decision-making processes, and sustain institutional representation and coordination at both internal and interinstitutional levels.

**
22A.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Directorate Leadership, Management, and Personnel Administration** (60%): Ensure comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services (22A10, 22A20, 22A30, 22A40); exercise full personnel management and general organizational administration; coordinate directorate-wide strategic initiatives and ensure seamless integration between parliamentary substantive work and institutional management requirements

• **Parliamentary Support, Advisory Services, and Expert Analysis** (20%): Provide high-level specialized advice to Members of Parliament in designated domains of expertise and support parliamentary management committee members, presidents, and rapporteurs in executing institutional functions (10%); furnish parliamentary committees with rigorous, independent research analysis and contemporary expertise within the directorate's substantive areas of responsibility (10%)

• **Financial Authority and Budget Governance** (10%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for budget execution and financial compliance; manage fiduciary and financial governance functions within delegated authority parameters

• **Project Management and Institutional Representation** (10%): Manage specialized strategic projects within the expertise domains of subordinate management units and services (5%); represent the institution across internal committees and interinstitutional forums while maintaining essential operational coordination and liaison with Parliament's internal service structures (5%)

22A10.name: 22A10
original_activities: Provide assistance to parliamentary bodies and the secretariats of parliamentary committees in terms of studies, notes and research in the following areas: regional development, agriculture and rural development and fisheries policies (Relevant committees: REGI, AGRI, PECH).
Manage tender procedures and negotiations for carrying out studies and using external expertise.
Control the scientific quality of studies developed by external consultants.
Maintain and develop relationships with DG EPRS services, external experts, research institutes and universities. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
Write and regularly update the 'Thematic Sheets'.
Promote and monitor the impact of the expertise provided.
22A10.refined_description: Unit 22A10 operates as a specialized research and analytical support unit within Direction 22A of the European Parliament, functioning as a leaf-node entity alongside sibling units 22A20, 22A30, and 22A40 while contributing to the directorate's broader strategic coordination and research support objectives. The unit delivers comprehensive, evidence-based expertise services to parliamentary bodies and committee secretariats, with particular focus on supporting the REGI (Regional Development), AGRI (Agriculture and Rural Development), and PECH (Fisheries) committees through the provision of substantive research assistance, analytical notes, and specialized studies across three critical policy domains—regional development, agriculture and rural development, and fisheries policies. To ensure the delivery of rigorous, high-quality analysis, Unit 22A10 executes a full-cycle research management function encompassing the initiation and management of competitive tender procedures and contract negotiations with external consultants and research providers (25% of workload), coupled with rigorous quality assurance and scientific validation activities that verify the methodological soundness and analytical standards of externally-commissioned deliverables (20% of workload). The unit maintains and continuously develops a strategic institutional network (10% of workload) comprising relationships with DG EPRS services, academic institutions, specialized research institutes, external expert communities, and internal Parliament services to facilitate knowledge transfer, institutional coherence, and effective coordination. Additionally, Unit 22A10 enhances its evidence-provision impact through the development and regular maintenance of thematic information sheets as knowledge resources (5% of workload) and systematic monitoring, promotion, and impact assessment of the expertise and policy guidance provided to parliamentary actors (5% of workload), collectively ensuring that research outputs achieve maximum utilization and institutional visibility.
22A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Research and Analytical Support** (35%): Comprehensive provision of assistance to parliamentary bodies and committee secretariats through delivery of substantive studies, research notes, and analytical materials in regional development, agriculture and rural development, and fisheries policy domains, directly supporting REGI, AGRI, and PECH committees

• **Research Procurement and Contract Management** (25%): Initiation, management, and execution of competitive tender procedures and contract negotiations for commissioning external research studies, specialized expertise engagement, and research provider procurement

• **Quality Assurance and Scientific Validation** (20%): Rigorous quality control and verification of scientific rigor, methodological soundness, and analytical standards of studies and deliverables produced by external consultants and research providers

• **Stakeholder Relationship Management and Network Development** (10%): Establishment, maintenance, and continuous development of strategic institutional relationships with DG EPRS services, academic institutions, research institutes, external expert communities, and internal Parliament services to facilitate knowledge exchange and institutional coordination

• **Knowledge Product Development and Impact Monitoring** (10%): Creation and regular updating of thematic information sheets as knowledge resources (5%), combined with systematic promotion, monitoring, and impact assessment of expertise and analytical products delivered to parliamentary stakeholders (5%)

22A20.name: 22A20
original_activities: Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion. Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), draft opinions.
Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, organization and monitoring of working groups, programming, trilogues, minutes, etc.).
Organize public hearings, commission missions outside the workplace, etc.
Ensure follow-up of documents received as part of regional policy programming.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, regional organizations, interested circles and civil society. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
Attend the committee's monitoring activities.
22A20.refined_description:
**

Unit 22A20 operates as a specialized parliamentary support unit positioned at the second hierarchical tier within the European Parliament's organizational structure, reporting directly to parent Unit 22A (the strategic directorate management and coordination hub) while functioning as a leaf-node with no subordinate organizational structures. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses providing integrated legislative, procedural, and administrative support to Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) in their designated institutional roles—including presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and draftsmen for opinions—alongside ensuring full operational functionality of parliamentary commission structures, particularly those addressing regional policy matters. Unit 22A20 maintains systematic coordination with sibling units 22A10, 22A30, and 22A40 under the strategic direction of parent unit 22A to deliver coherent parliamentary support services across the institution. The unit's workload distribution reflects a strategic prioritization of legislative support activities while maintaining comprehensive operational and stakeholder engagement infrastructure. Operating within a complex institutional environment, Unit 22A20 serves as a critical service delivery point that bridges MEP support functions, commission administration, external institutional relations, and committee oversight responsibilities.

---

**
22A20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Legislative and Parliamentary Support Services** (55%): Provision of written and oral contributions assisting MEPs in executing their designated institutional functions (president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion); preparation of working documents, draft reports covering both legislative and non-legislative procedures, and draft opinions that form the substantive foundation of parliamentary work

• **Commission Secretariat and Meeting Administration** (25%): Comprehensive organization and coordination of commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, and working group establishment and monitoring; programming and scheduling of parliamentary activities; trilogue facilitation and management; preparation and maintenance of official minutes, documentation, and records; process monitoring and coordination

• **External Stakeholder Engagement and Institutional Relations** (5%): Establishment and maintenance of strategic contacts with other EU institutions, regional organizations, interested stakeholder circles, civil society entities, and internal Parliament services; coordination with internal services to facilitate information flow and organizational alignment

• **Public Engagement and Field Activities** (5%): Organization of public hearings and commission missions conducted outside the parliamentary workplace; logistical coordination and event management support

• **Regional Policy Programming Follow-up and Committee Monitoring** (10%): Follow-up management and monitoring of documents received as part of regional policy programming frameworks; attendance and support of committee monitoring activities to ensure policy continuity and oversight effectiveness

22A30.name: 22A30
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, trilogues, programming, minutes, etc.).
Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures including trilogues), monitoring 'comitology' acts.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Organize public hearings, working group missions outside the workplace, etc.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society as well as with the internal services of Parliament.
Attend the committee's monitoring activities.
22A30.refined_description:
Unit 22A30 operates as a critical administrative, procedural, and legislative support entity within the European Parliament's organizational framework, functioning at the second hierarchical level under the strategic directorate management of parent Unit 22A. As a leaf-node operational execution point within a peer structure alongside Units 22A10, 22A20, and 22A40, the unit serves as an indispensable operational backbone bridging legislative procedure management with institutional coordination responsibilities. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses ensuring seamless commission secretariat operations through meeting organization and documentation; substantive legislative support via working document and draft report preparation; targeted assistance to Members of the European Parliament in their formal capacities; external engagement and stakeholder liaison; and active participation in committee oversight activities. These functions collectively position Unit 22A30 as an essential institutional entity that enables the effective functioning of parliamentary commissions and their constituent bodies while maintaining operational continuity and institutional memory. The unit coordinates closely with sibling units to support the strategic directorate's overarching mandate of facilitating parliamentary legislative production capacity and commission governance.

##
22A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Commission Secretariat and Meeting Management (40%)**: Ensure the full secretariat of the commission through comprehensive organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, trilogues, programming schedules, and minutes documentation—functions essential to maintaining operational continuity and institutional memory

• **Legislative Document Preparation and Monitoring (30%)**: Prepare working documents and draft reports across both legislative and non-legislative procedures, including trilogues facilitation and systematic monitoring of comitology acts, thereby directly supporting the legislative production capacity of the parent directorate

• **MEP Support and Formal Function Assistance (10%)**: Provide written and oral contributions assisting Members of the European Parliament in the exercise of their formal functions as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and draftsmen for opinions

• **External Engagement, Stakeholder Liaison, and Event Coordination (15%)**: Organize public hearings and working group missions outside the workplace; establish and maintain institutional contacts with external institutions, interested circles, civil society organizations, and internal Parliament services to facilitate institutional coordination

• **Committee Oversight and Monitoring Activities (5%)**: Attend and support the committee's monitoring activities to ensure institutional accountability and compliance

22A40.name: 22A40
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of commission and coordinator meetings, trilogues, shadow meetings, preparation of minutes and records of decisions, scheduling of work, etc.).
Prepare working documents/draft reports/opinions (legislative and non-legislative procedures), budgetary and EFCA discharge opinions; monitoring of delegated acts and implementing acts.
Organize for members of the Fisheries Committee, public hearings and missions or delegations necessary in the exercise of their legislative activity.
Assist, through specific written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, coordinator, rapporteur or draftsman for opinion.
Attend the committee's monitoring activities.

Ensure contacts with other Institutions, as well as interested circles and civil society, in particular national fishing sectors, international fishing organizations, fisheries advisory councils, etc. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
22A40.refined_description: Unit 22A40 functions as a specialized secretariat and administrative support unit within Direction 22A of the European Parliament, serving as the operational backbone of the Fisheries Committee's legislative and procedural activities. The unit operates at the critical intersection between the committee's political mandate and its operational implementation, coordinating with sibling units 22A10, 22A20, and 22A30 under strategic direction from parent unit 22A. Unit 22A40's comprehensive mission encompasses five interconnected functional domains: the organization and management of commission secretariat operations including meetings, trilogues, shadow group assemblies, and coordinator sessions; the preparation of legislative and policy documentation across both legislative and non-legislative procedures; the facilitation of member engagement through public hearings, missions, and delegations; the provision of specialized support to MEPs exercising leadership roles; and the maintenance of critical external relations with European institutions, national fishing sectors, international fishing organizations, fisheries advisory councils, and civil society stakeholders. All activities are conducted with continuous monitoring of delegated and implementing acts implementation, oversight of committee activities, and coordination with internal Parliament services to ensure seamless integration of the committee's fisheries policy mandate across parliamentary governance structures.
22A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Commission Secretariat and Procedural Management** (40%): Organization and coordination of commission meetings, coordinator assemblies, trilogue negotiations, and shadow group meetings; preparation of comprehensive official minutes and decision records; scheduling and overall coordination of parliamentary committee work and procedural timelines to ensure seamless deliberative functioning

• **Legislative Documentation and Policy Monitoring** (20%): Preparation of working documents, draft reports, and committee opinions across legislative and non-legislative procedures; drafting of budgetary assessments and European Fisheries Control Agency (EFCA) discharge opinions; continuous monitoring of the implementation and application of delegated acts and implementing acts within the fisheries policy domain

• **Member Support and Stakeholder Engagement** (20%): Organization of public hearings, fact-finding missions, and delegation activities for Fisheries Committee members to support legislative mandates; provision of targeted written and oral contributions to assist MEPs serving as committee president, vice-president, coordinator, rapporteur, or opinion draftsman in specialized roles

• **Committee Oversight and Monitoring Activities** (5%): Participation in and support of the committee's ongoing monitoring activities to ensure continuity of fisheries policy oversight and implementation tracking

• **External Relations and Institutional Coordination** (5%): Maintenance of strategic contacts and liaison functions with other European institutions, national fishing sector representatives, international fishing organizations, fisheries advisory councils, civil society stakeholders, and relevant internal Parliament services to support committee objectives and parliamentary fisheries governance

22B.name: 22B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of the directorate, as well as the management of personnel and the general organization of the directorate.

Advise the deputies of Parliament in their areas of expertise and assist them as members of the parliamentary management committees, and in particular the presidents and rapporteurs, in the exercise of their functions.
Provide parliamentary committees with high-level expertise, up-to-date analysis and independent research in areas covered by the directorate.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Ensure project management in the areas of expertise of the management units and services.
Represent management in various internal committees and the institution in different interinstitutional forums. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
22B.refined_description: Unit 22B functions as the primary strategic directorate-level management and coordination entity within the European Parliament's organizational hierarchy, reporting directly to Unit 22 (the general management directorate at root level, which itself reports to the Secretary General). Operating as a first-level directorate, Unit 22B bears comprehensive responsibility for the direction, coordination, and animation of all four subordinate units and services within its directorate (22B10, 22B20, 22B30, and 22B0040), alongside full responsibility for personnel management and general organizational administration—collectively constituting 60% of its operational workload and forming the critical operational backbone of the institution's management structure. Beyond internal directorate management, Unit 22B provides multifaceted advisory and analytical support to the parliamentary institution: it delivers specialized expertise and assistance to Members of Parliament and parliamentary management committee members, particularly supporting presidents and rapporteurs in the exercise of their institutional mandates (10% allocation), and provides high-level, independent research and contemporary analytical assessments to parliamentary committees across all substantive areas within the directorate's competencies (10% allocation). Unit 22B exercises subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial and administrative matters, enabling it to execute authorized budgetary and administrative decision-making within institutional parameters (10% allocation). Additionally, Unit 22B manages project execution across the directorate's specialized expertise areas through its subordinate management units and services (5% allocation) and ensures institutional representation through active participation in internal parliamentary committees and interinstitutional forums while maintaining essential cross-service coordination and liaison with sibling units (22-10 and 22A) and Parliament's broader internal services infrastructure (5% allocation).
22B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Directorate Management and Personnel Administration** (60%): Ensure comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services (22B10, 22B20, 22B30, 22B0040); manage personnel affairs and general organizational administration; provide overarching leadership and structural coordination forming the operational backbone of the institution.

• **Parliamentary Advisory and Analytical Support Services** (20%): Advise Members of Parliament and support parliamentary management committee members, presidents, and rapporteurs in the exercise of their institutional mandates (10%); deliver high-level expertise, independent research, and contemporary analytical assessments to parliamentary committees across all areas covered by directorate competencies (10%).

• **Financial and Administrative Authorization** (10%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer functions, enabling authorized financial and administrative decision-making within institutional parameters.

• **Project Management and Institutional Coordination** (10%): Execute project management within the specialized expertise domains of subordinate management units and services (5%); ensure institutional representation in internal parliamentary committees and interinstitutional forums while maintaining essential cross-service coordination and liaison with sibling units (22-10, 22A) and Parliament's internal services (5%).

22B10.name: 22B10
original_activities: Provide assistance to parliamentary bodies and the secretariats of parliamentary committees in terms of studies, notes and research in the following areas: transport and tourism policies, employment and social affairs and the housing crisis in the European Union (Relevant committees: TRAN, EMPL, HOUS).
Manage tender procedures and negotiations for carrying out studies and using external expertise.
Control the scientific quality of studies developed by external consultants.
Maintain and develop relationships with DG EPRS services, external experts, research institutes and universities. Ensure contacts with the internal services of Parliament.
Write and regularly update thematic sheets.
Promote and monitor the impact of the expertise provided.
22B10.refined_description:
Unit 22B10 operates as a specialized research and analytical support entity within the European Parliament's organizational hierarchy, functioning as a subordinate unit under directorate management coordination structure of Unit 22B, alongside sibling units 22B20 and 22B30. The unit serves as a critical bridge between parliamentary decision-making bodies and the external research ecosystem, delivering comprehensive intellectual and research assistance to the secretariats of the Transport (TRAN), Employment and Social Affairs (EMPL), and Housing (HOUS) committees through evidence-based studies, analytical notes, and policy analysis covering transport and tourism policies, employment and social affairs, and the European Union's housing crisis. To operationalize this research function, Unit 22B10 administers the complete lifecycle of external expertise engagement, including managing tender procedures, conducting contract negotiations, and procuring specialized external consultant services while maintaining rigorous quality assurance mechanisms that control and validate the scientific quality and methodological soundness of all studies developed by external consultants. The unit cultivates and sustains strategic relationships with the Directorate General for European Parliamentary Research Service (DG EPRS), external subject matter experts, academic research institutes, universities, and relevant internal Parliament service infrastructure to facilitate coordinated knowledge exchange and integrated support delivery. Beyond direct research delivery, Unit 22B10 contributes to knowledge dissemination and institutional learning through the preparation and regular updating of thematic policy sheets, while actively promoting and monitoring the measurable impact, institutional utilization, and practical effectiveness of the expertise and research products provided to parliamentary committees. These interconnected activities position Unit 22B10 as an essential intermediary supporting evidence-based legislative processes across multiple policy domains.

##
22B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Research & Policy Support** (35%): Provision of comprehensive assistance to parliamentary bodies and committee secretariats through the delivery of studies, analytical notes, and evidence-based research outputs covering transport and tourism policies, employment and social affairs, and the European Union's housing crisis, with direct engagement to the TRAN, EMPL, and HOUS committees, constituting the unit's primary strategic contribution to parliamentary legislative processes.

• **External Expertise Management & Procurement** (25%): Administration of the complete lifecycle of external expertise engagement, including management of tender procedures, contract negotiations, and procurement processes for securing external consultancy services and specialized research expertise to support parliamentary research requirements.

• **Quality Assurance & Scientific Validation** (20%): Implementation of systematic quality control mechanisms to evaluate, validate, and ensure the scientific rigor, methodological integrity, and intellectual soundness of studies and research outputs produced by external consultants and service providers.

• **Stakeholder Relations & Network Development** (10%): Cultivation and maintenance of strategic relationships with DG EPRS services, external research experts, academic institutions, universities, and internal parliamentary service units to facilitate coordinated knowledge exchange, integrated support delivery, and institutional learning across Parliament's service infrastructure.

• **Knowledge Dissemination & Impact Management** (10%): Preparation and regular updating of thematic policy sheets (5%) combined with active promotion and monitoring of the institutional impact, practical utilization, and measurable effectiveness of expertise and research products delivered to parliamentary committees (5%).

22B20.name: 22B20
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the Transport and Tourism Committee (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitor 'comitology' acts, transposition and application of European Union law.
Provide secretarial support for the TRAN negotiation teams during trilogues.
