Organize public hearings, working group missions or delegations outside the workplace, etc.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, European deputies in the exercise of their functions as President, Vice-President, Rapporteur, Rapporteur for opinion.
Attend the committee's control activities (Scrutiny).
Ensure contacts with other institutions, as well as with Parliament's internal services, interested circles and civil society (including Website, Newsletter, eMeeting and eCommittee).
Ensure contacts with EMSA, EASA and ERA agencies.
22B20.refined_description: Unit 22B20 functions as the dedicated secretariat and administrative support entity for the European Parliament's Transport and Tourism Committee (TRAN), operating as a Level 2 leaf node within the Direction 22B hierarchy and coordinating with sibling units 22B10, 22B30, and 22B0040 to deliver integrated parliamentary support services. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management of committee operations and facilitation of legislative processes across transport and tourism policy domains through four equally weighted core operational pillars: committee secretariat functions (meeting organization, coordinators' coordination, programming, and minutes management); legislative and non-legislative document preparation (working documents, draft reports, comitology monitoring, and EU law transposition oversight); specialized secretarial support for TRAN negotiation teams during interinstitutional trilogues; and supplementary support activities spanning public hearings, working group missions, external delegations, and Member assistance. The unit maintains strategic external stakeholder relations with other EU institutions, Parliament internal services, interested circles, and civil society through digital engagement platforms (Website, Newsletter, eMeeting, eCommittee systems), while simultaneously sustaining specialized liaison functions with three key European agencies—the European Maritime Safety Agency (EMSA), European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA), and European Railway Agency (ERA). Additionally, the unit provides written and oral contributions supporting Members in their formal roles as Presidents, Vice-Presidents, Rapporteurs, and Rapporteurs for Opinion, and participates in committee scrutiny and control activities. This comprehensive operational structure ensures that Unit 22B20 delivers multifaceted administrative, legislative, and stakeholder engagement support essential to the Transport and Tourism Committee's effectiveness within the European Parliament's institutional framework.
22B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Secretariat and Administrative Management** (25%): Comprehensive organization of all Transport and Tourism Committee meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming schedules, and official minutes documentation; foundational operational support for committee functioning

• **Legislative Support and Regulatory Oversight** (25%): Preparation of working documents and draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures; monitoring of comitology acts; oversight of transposition and application of European Union law within transport and tourism sectors

• **Trilogues and Inter-Institutional Negotiations** (25%): Provision of dedicated secretarial support to TRAN negotiation teams during interinstitutional trilogue proceedings, directly facilitating legislative negotiations between Parliament, Council, and Commission

• **Public Engagement and External Delegations** (7%): Organization of public hearings, working group missions, and external delegations outside the workplace

• **Member Support and Rapporteur Assistance** (6%): Written and oral contributions supporting European Parliament Members in their formal capacities as Presidents, Vice-Presidents, Rapporteurs, and Rapporteurs for Opinion

• **Institutional Oversight and Stakeholder Relations** (5%): Participation in committee scrutiny and control activities; maintenance of strategic contacts with other EU institutions, Parliament's internal services, interested circles, and civil society

• **Digital Engagement and Institutional Communications** (5%): Management of external stakeholder relations through digital platforms including Website, Newsletter, eMeeting, and eCommittee systems

• **Specialized Agency Liaison** (2%): Maintenance of dedicated liaison functions with European Maritime Safety Agency (EMSA), European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA), and European Railway Agency (ERA)

22B30.name: 22B30
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitor 'comitology' acts, transposition and application of European Union law.
Provide secretarial support to the EMPL negotiation teams during trilogues.
Organize public hearings and missions of commission members outside the workplace, etc.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, European deputies in the exercise of their functions as President, Vice-President, Rapporteur, Rapporteur for opinion.
Attend the committee's control activities (Scrutiny).

Ensure contacts with the European Training Foundation (Turin), the Agency for Health and Safety at Work (Bilbao), the European Center for the Development of Vocational Training CEDEFOP (Thessaloniki), the European Foundation for the Improvement of Living and Working Conditions (Dublin), and the European Labor Authority (Bratislava).
Ensure contacts with other institutions, as well as with the internal services of Parliament, interested circles and civil society.
22B30.refined_description: Unit 22B30 functions as the specialized administrative and secretarial backbone of the Employment and Social Affairs Committee (EMPL), operating as a leaf node within Direction 22B of the European Parliament's organizational structure alongside sibling units 22B10, 22B20, and 22B0040. The unit serves as a critical coordination node translating strategic directives into procedural execution while maintaining institutional continuity across legislative cycles, operating at the operational frontline of parliamentary administrative support. Unit 22B30's core mission encompasses eight distinct operational activities distributed across five integrated functional domains, each contributing to the committee's legislative, negotiation, oversight, and stakeholder engagement capabilities. The unit maintains dual primary responsibilities: ensuring comprehensive secretariat functionality for commission organization and operations, and preparing legislative and non-legislative working documents while monitoring EU law transposition and comitology acts. Additionally, Unit 22B30 provides specialized secretarial support to EMPL negotiation teams during trilogue proceedings—a critical function in inter-institutional legislative negotiations between Parliament, Council, and Commission—while simultaneously managing parliamentary oversight functions, member support services, and external stakeholder engagement. The unit maintains formal liaison relationships with five specialized European agencies (European Training Foundation in Turin, European Agency for Safety and Health at Work in Bilbao, CEDEFOP in Thessaloniki, European Foundation for the Improvement of Living and Working Conditions in Dublin, and European Labor Authority in Bratislava), while establishing and sustaining contacts with other parliamentary institutions, internal Parliament services, and civil society organizations, thereby serving as an essential coordination and support mechanism enabling the committee's comprehensive institutional mandate.
22B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Commission Secretariat and Meeting Administration** (25%): Ensures comprehensive secretariat of commission operations including organization of meetings, coordination of coordinators' convocations, programming of activities, and preparation of minutes and procedural documentation.

• **Legislative Documentation and EU Law Compliance Oversight** (25%): Prepares and develops working documents and draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures; monitors comitology acts; tracks and supervises transposition and application of European Union law across member states.

• **Trilogue Negotiation Support and Legislative Facilitation** (25%): Provides specialized secretarial and administrative support services to EMPL negotiation teams during trilogue proceedings, facilitating inter-institutional legislative negotiations between Parliament, Council, and Commission.

• **Parliamentary Member Support and Public Engagement** (10%): Provides written and oral contributions assisting European deputies in exercise of formal roles as President, Vice-President, Rapporteur, and Rapporteur for opinion (5%); organizes public hearings and facilitates external missions for commission members (5%).

• **Institutional Relations, Agency Liaison, and Oversight Functions** (15%): Maintains formal liaison relationships with five specialized European agencies—ETF (Turin), EUOSHA (Bilbao), CEDEFOP (Thessaloniki), Eurofound (Dublin), and ELA (Bratislava)—(5%); ensures institutional contacts with other Parliament bodies, internal services, stakeholder organizations, and civil society representatives (5%); conducts scrutiny activities and committee control functions (5%).

22B0040.name: 22B0040
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the special commission on the housing crisis in the European Union (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).

Organize and implement the work program in order to respond to the mandate of the special commission, including public hearings, exchanges of views, missions of Members outside the workplace and any other activity including that of monitoring the Commission in the development of its plan on affordable housing.

Prepare documents relating to the report (draft report, amendments document, etc.), as well as any other working document, sheet and analysis in relation to the subjects dealt with by the special commission.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as President, Vice-President, Rapporteur and shadow Rapporteurs.

Ensure contacts with other institutions including the Council, the Commission (and its 'Task force'), the EIB, the advisory committees, interested circles and civil society, as well as with Parliament's internal services.
22B0040.refined_description: Unit 22B0040 functions as the dedicated secretariat and operational support entity for the European Parliament's Special Commission on the Housing Crisis in the European Union, operating as a Level 2 leaf node within the Direction 22B organizational hierarchy while maintaining coordination with sibling units 22B10, 22B20, and 22B30. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses five integrated operational domains that collectively constitute its full workload allocation. The unit manages all secretariat and administrative operations for the Special Commission, including the organization and coordination of plenary meetings, coordinators' meetings, work programming, and official minutes preparation. Concurrently, it organizes and implements the Special Commission's substantive work program in direct response to its institutional mandate, encompassing the facilitation of public hearings, structured exchanges of views among Members, coordination of Members' external missions, and systematic monitoring activities regarding the European Commission's affordable housing plan development and related policy initiatives. The unit prepares all documentation required for the Special Commission's reporting and analytical functions, including draft reports, amendments documentation, working papers, analytical sheets, and specialized analyses addressing housing crisis-related subjects. Additionally, it provides direct written and oral advisory and drafting support to Members of the European Parliament serving in designated leadership roles—specifically the President, Vice-Presidents, Rapporteur, and shadow Rapporteurs—in the exercise of their institutional functions. Finally, the unit establishes and maintains critical institutional liaison contacts with external stakeholders including the Council of the European Union, the European Commission and its designated Task Force on housing, the European Investment Bank (EIB), relevant advisory committees, civil society organizations, interested constituencies and stakeholder representatives, and Parliament's internal service directorates to facilitate coordination and information exchange.
22B0040.refined_activity_summary:
• **Secretariat and Administrative Operations** (30%): Ensure comprehensive secretariat functions for the Special Commission, encompassing organization of all plenary meetings, coordinators' meetings, work program scheduling, official minutes preparation, and all associated administrative and logistical support duties.

• **Work Program Implementation and Substantive Mandate Execution** (30%): Organize and implement the Special Commission's work program through public hearings, facilitation of structured exchanges of views among Members, coordination of external missions by Members, and systematic monitoring of European Commission initiatives and policy development concerning affordable housing.

• **Parliamentary Documentation and Analytical Production** (25%): Prepare substantive parliamentary documentation including draft reports, amendments documentation, working papers, analytical sheets, and specialized analyses directly addressing housing crisis-related subjects within the Special Commission's purview.

• **Member Support Services and Advisory Contributions** (10%): Provide written and oral advisory and drafting support to Members serving as President, Vice-Presidents, Rapporteur, and shadow Rapporteurs in the exercise of their designated institutional functions and leadership roles.

• **External Institutional Relations and Stakeholder Liaison** (5%): Establish and maintain liaison contacts with the Council of the European Union, the European Commission and its Task Force on housing, the European Investment Bank, relevant advisory committees, civil society organizations, stakeholder representatives, interested constituencies, and Parliament's internal service directorates to facilitate inter-institutional coordination and information exchange.

original_activities: Design and monitor the execution of the information strategy and institutional communication, throughout the legislature and in particular in preparation for the European elections.
Coordinate activities between directorates and horizontal units.
Represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
Represent and/or ensure representation of general management at interinstitutional level.
04.refined_description: Unit 04 functions as the strategic communications and institutional coordination directorate of the European Parliament, reporting directly to the Secretary General and serving as the central nexus for institutional messaging, organizational alignment, and executive representation. The unit's operational mandate is structured across five integrated functional domains: (1) designing and monitoring the execution of the institution's comprehensive information strategy and institutional communication initiatives throughout the full legislative term, with particular emphasis on strategic preparation and execution in advance of European elections (40% of workload); (2) coordinating activities across all directorates and horizontal units—including Units 04-10 (Institutional Press and Strategy), 04-20 (Strategic Communications), 04-30 (Editorial and Thematic Coordination), 04-40 (Public Opinion Monitoring), 04A, 04E, and three additional specialized subordinate units—to ensure organizational coherence, operational synchronization, and unified institutional positioning across the Parliament's multiple communication channels (40% of workload); (3) representing general management within the European Parliament's internal governance bodies and ensuring executive presence and accountability within parliamentary structures (10% of workload); (4) exercising delegated authorizing officer responsibilities for budgetary and financial oversight, thereby performing financial stewardship and administrative authorization functions (5% of workload); and (5) representing or ensuring appropriate representation of general management at the interinstitutional level, maintaining strategic relationships with other EU institutions, bodies, and external stakeholders (5% of workload). Through this integrated portfolio of strategic, coordinative, and representative activities, Unit 04 operationalizes institutional direction while ensuring organizational coherence and integrated operational delivery across the Parliament's complex structural framework.
04.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Communications and Electoral Preparation** (40%): Design, monitor, and execute the institution's comprehensive information strategy and institutional communication initiatives throughout the full legislative term, with enhanced strategic focus on preparation and execution in advance of European elections and alignment of strategic messaging

• **Cross-Directorate Coordination and Organizational Integration** (40%): Coordinate activities across all directorates and horizontal units (including Units 04-10, 04-20, 04-30, 04-40, 04A, 04E, and three additional specialized units) to ensure organizational coherence, operational synchronization, unified institutional positioning, and integrated delivery across the Parliament's communication channels and parliamentary functions

• **Internal Governance and Management Representation** (10%): Represent general management within the European Parliament's internal bodies and governance structures, ensuring executive presence and accountability within parliamentary governance frameworks

• **Financial Stewardship and Administrative Authority** (5%): Exercise delegated authorizing officer responsibilities for budgetary and financial oversight, performing financial stewardship and administrative authorization functions

• **Interinstitutional Engagement and Strategic Representation** (5%): Represent or ensure appropriate representation of general management at the interinstitutional level, maintaining strategic relationships and engagement with other EU institutions, bodies, and external stakeholders

original_activities: Answer press questions mainly for what is not directly linked to the legislative activity of the EP; provide language elements to the MEPs, the spokesperson, the press officers and communicators of DG COMM.

Define and implement the EP's strategy to counter information manipulation; work with other services to detect, analyze, prevent the manipulation of information, boost resilience and act when the reputation of the EP is called into question.

Work in close collaboration with other services of DG COMM; participate in the 'Content coordination committee', the editorial committee and its various bodies; ensure close liaison with the other DGs.
Monitor main institutional issues, in particular the Constitutional Affairs and Budget Committee; prepare LTTs (lines to take) on these questions.
Prepare LTTs and support institutional communication on any decision of the governing bodies of Parliament (Bureau, COP) which may have an impact in the media and social media.
Assist the spokesperson in the crisis unit (CBMT level 3) and in managing business continuity; collaborate with other EP bodies on crisis communication (levels 1 and 2).
04-10.refined_description: Unit 04-10 functions as the European Parliament's primary institutional press and strategic communications hub, operating as a Direction-level entity within the Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM) and serving as a critical interface between Parliament's governing bodies, media stakeholders, and internal communicators across the institution. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 04-10 operates collaboratively with sibling units (04-20, 04-30, 04-40, 04A, 04E, and three additional units) under the strategic oversight of Unit 04, which reports directly to the Secretary General. The unit's mandate encompasses two equally substantial pillars of activity: external communications management and information integrity protection, each commanding 35% of workload allocation. Unit 04-10 responds to press inquiries on non-legislative institutional matters while providing strategic language elements and messaging guidance to Members of the European Parliament, designated spokespersons, press officers, and communication professionals throughout DG COMM to ensure consistent institutional voice and narrative coherence; simultaneously, the unit detects, analyzes, and prevents information manipulation targeting the institution, implements the EP's comprehensive counter-disinformation strategy, enhances organizational resilience against reputational threats, and coordinates active institutional responses when Parliament's reputation requires defense. Complementing these primary activities, the unit dedicates 20% of workload to institutional governance and strategic messaging functions: 10% to monitoring Constitutional Affairs and Budget Committee developments and preparing Lines to Take (LTTs) on these substantive policy areas, and 7% to preparing LTTs and communications support for decisions emanating from Parliament's governing bodies (the Bureau and Conference of Presidents) that carry media and social media implications. An additional 10% addresses collaborative content coordination across DG COMM, including participation in the Content Coordination Committee and editorial governance structures while maintaining essential liaison mechanisms with all other Directorates General. Finally, 3% of the unit's capacity is reserved for direct crisis management support, including assistance to the spokesperson within the Crisis Management and Business Continuity Team (CBMT) at Level 3 escalation and collaborative engagement with other EP bodies on crisis communications at operational levels 1 and 2.
04-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **External Communications and Media Relations** (35%): Respond to press inquiries concerning non-legislative institutional matters; furnish strategic language elements and messaging guidance to Members of the European Parliament, official spokespersons, press officers, and communication professionals across DG COMM; ensure consistent institutional voice, narrative coherence, and communication support across the Parliament's communicating bodies.

• **Information Integrity and Institutional Reputation Protection** (35%): Design and implement the European Parliament's comprehensive counter-disinformation strategy; detect, analyze, and prevent information manipulation targeting the institution; strengthen organizational resilience against false narratives and reputational threats; coordinate active institutional responses when Parliament's reputation faces external challenge.

• **Institutional Governance and Strategic Messaging** (17%): Monitor key institutional developments within Constitutional Affairs and Budget Committee domains and prepare Lines to Take (LTTs) on governance matters (10%); prepare institutional communication guidance and messaging support for decisions emanating from Parliament's governing bodies—specifically the Bureau and Conference of Presidents—ensuring media and social media readiness for high-impact announcements (7%).

• **Internal Coordination and Cross-Directorate Liaison** (10%): Collaborate with other DG COMM services through the Content Coordination Committee and editorial governance structures; maintain essential liaison mechanisms with all other Directorates General; ensure integrated institutional communications across organizational boundaries.

• **Crisis Management and Business Continuity Support** (3%): Provide direct support to the official spokesperson within the Crisis Management and Business Continuity Team (CBMT) at Level 3 escalation; coordinate crisis communications with other EP bodies at operational levels 1 and 2; ensure continuity of institutional messaging during crisis situations.

original_activities: Prepare and monitor the decisions of the administrative or political authorities of DG COMM under the direct authority of the Director General.
Coordinate DG COMM's relations with the political authorities of the EP (notably the President's Office and the Bureau Secretariat).
Support the transformation process within DG COMM towards a communications approach focused on core missions, by providing strategic advice and maintaining political relationships.
Ensure alignment of communication activities with the objectives of DG COMM.
Provide a strategic approach to communication. Develop a culture of innovation based on modern practices and a state of mind, and support the change process within the different departments.
Promote a better understanding of the role of DG COMM throughout the European Parliament.
Support the Director General in various administrative tasks.
04-20.refined_description: Unit 04-20 functions as the strategic executive office and principal advisory directorate within DG COMM, operating at the Directorate level under direct authority of the Director General as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures. Positioned as the central nexus for high-level institutional coordination and strategic direction-setting across the Directorate General, Unit 04-20 serves as the critical interface between communications management and European Parliament political leadership while simultaneously functioning as the institutional intelligence mechanism essential to DG COMM's strategic positioning within the Parliament's governance architecture. The unit operates within the parent organizational framework of Unit 04 (the central strategic coordination hub reporting to the Secretary General) and functions as a peer to sibling units 04-10, 04-30, 04-40, 04A, 04E, and three additional directorate-level units. Unit 04-20's primary mission encompasses five integrated functional pillars that collectively constitute its complete operational mandate: executive decision preparation and monitoring for administrative and political authorities under the Director General's direct purview; strategic coordination of DG COMM's institutional relationships with European Parliament political authorities, including the President's Office and Bureau Secretariat; transformation facilitation and strategic advisory support for the Directorate's evolution toward mission-focused communications approaches while maintaining critical political relationships; dual responsibility for ensuring alignment of all communication activities with organizational objectives while simultaneously promoting internal understanding of DG COMM's institutional role throughout the Parliament; and support for the Director General in diverse administrative functions coupled with strategic focus on innovation culture development and change management processes across departmental structures. This comprehensive portfolio positions Unit 04-20 as an essential strategic asset for DG COMM's institutional coherence, political liaison, organizational transformation, and operational alignment within the European Parliament's institutional framework.
04-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Governance & Decision Support** (25%): Preparation and monitoring of all administrative and political authority decisions emanating from DG COMM under direct Director General authority; ensuring executive coherence, implementation oversight, and institutional accountability across decision-making processes.

• **Political Stakeholder Relations & Institutional Liaison** (20%): Coordination of DG COMM's institutional relations with European Parliament political authorities, specifically the President's Office and Bureau Secretariat; functioning as the critical interface between communications management and parliamentary leadership; serving as the primary liaison mechanism for parliamentary engagement.

• **Strategic Transformation & Change Management** (15%): Provision of strategic advisory support and guidance to leadership for DG COMM's organizational evolution toward mission-focused communications approaches; facilitation of change processes across departmental structures; maintenance of essential political relationships throughout transformation initiatives.

• **Strategic Alignment & Innovation Development** (20%): Ensuring alignment of all communication activities with established DG COMM objectives and maintenance of strategic coherence across operational units (10%); developing and promoting organizational culture of innovation grounded in modern practices and contemporary methodologies across departmental functions (10%).

• **Institutional Advocacy & Administrative Support** (10%): Promotion of enhanced understanding of DG COMM's institutional role and mission throughout the European Parliament; provision of essential administrative support services to the Director General in diverse functional areas.

original_activities: Coordinate and plan the editorial products of the DG COMM, streamlining main messages and actions across different units and directorates through the Editorial Committee and the five thematic teams.
Ensure relations with Committees. The five thematic team coordinators are the DG COMM's main contact points for committees for communication plans.

Animate and coordinate the DG COMM's five cross-directorate thematic teams to ensure consistent communication on priority files. Create synergies and exchange of information between the editorial units.
Develop a long term priority planning. The unit is in charge of developing a long term overview over communication priorities for the 'Committee of Directors on Content & Planning'.
04-30.refined_description: Unit 04-30 functions as the Editorial Coordination and Strategic Planning directorate within the Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), serving as the central orchestration mechanism for harmonizing institutional messaging across the European Parliament. Operating at the Directorate level (Hierarchy Level 1) under Unit 04, which reports directly to the Secretary General, this leaf-node unit executes four equally balanced strategic functions that collectively constitute 100% of its operational workload. The unit coordinates and plans all editorial products across DG COMM through the Editorial Committee and five thematic teams, ensuring message consistency and streamlined organizational action (25% allocation). Simultaneously, it maintains primary liaison responsibilities with Parliamentary Committees, with five thematic team coordinators serving as DG COMM's designated contact points for committee-specific communication planning and institutional relations management (25% allocation). The unit animates and coordinates five cross-directorate thematic teams to ensure consistent, synchronized communication on priority legislative and institutional files while fostering synergies and facilitating information exchange between editorial units across the directorate (25% allocation). Additionally, Unit 04-30 develops comprehensive long-term strategic communication priorities and provides institutional oversight of communication planning to the Committee of Directors on Content & Planning (25% allocation). As a strategic nerve center, Unit 04-30 operates within the broader DG COMM ecosystem alongside sibling units (04-10, 04-20, 04-40, 04A, 04E, and others) to deliver integrated, coherent institutional communication.
04-30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Editorial Coordination and Strategic Planning** (25%): Coordinate and plan editorial products across DG COMM through the Editorial Committee and five thematic teams; streamline main messages and organizational actions to ensure consistency; develop comprehensive long-term strategic communication priorities and provide institutional oversight to the Committee of Directors on Content & Planning

• **Committee Relations and Liaison Management** (25%): Establish and maintain primary institutional relations with Parliamentary Committees; position five thematic team coordinators as DG COMM's designated contact points for committee-specific communication planning and liaison responsibilities

• **Cross-Directorate Thematic Team Animation and Synergy Development** (25%): Animate and coordinate five cross-directorate thematic teams to ensure consistent, synchronized communication on priority legislative and institutional files; foster synergies and facilitate information exchange between editorial units across the directorate

• **Strategic Institutional Oversight and Long-Term Planning** (25%): Maintain comprehensive institutional overview of communication priorities; provide strategic guidance and planning recommendations to the Committee of Directors on Content & Planning; ensure coherent coordination strategy across DG COMM's multiple operational units and external stakeholder engagement with Parliamentary governance structures

original_activities: Design Parliament's public opinion surveys and provide in-depth analysis of citizens' attitudes and expectations towards the EU and the EP. The Eurobarometer surveys allow for longitudinal analysis on major trend issues.

Link audience insight data to Parliament's legislative and political activities. Members are serviced with a newsletter on citizens' views on key topics of the Plenary agenda, whereas the Cabinet of the President receives tailor-made public opinion briefings.

Provide strategic survey data to decision makers and operational units. In the framework of specific DG COMM campaigns, POMU advises on the design of relevant survey questions and provides Eurobarometer data.

Monitor political trends in all EU Member States and national voting intention polls in view of the next European elections. Monitor changes in the composition of the European Parliament and its political groups.
Improving access to Eurobarometer data by developing an inter-institutional database and visual dashboard, bringing together Eurobarometer data into one place.
04-40.refined_description: Unit 04-40, designated as the Public Opinion and Monitoring Unit (POMU), operates as a specialized strategic intelligence and evidence-based analysis directorate within the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), functioning under the parent Unit 04 structure which reports to the Secretary General. The unit serves as the institutional center for comprehensive public opinion research infrastructure, public sentiment analysis, and strategic insight dissemination, positioning itself as an essential intelligence resource that transforms citizen voice data into actionable institutional knowledge to inform parliamentary decision-making and enhance democratic accountability. Operating collaboratively alongside sibling units (04-10, 04-20, 04-30, 04A, 04E, and others), Unit 04-40 executes a multifaceted mission encompassing the design and execution of Parliament's public opinion surveys with particular emphasis on the Eurobarometer programme, the translation of audience research findings into targeted deliverables for parliamentary leadership and Members, the provision of strategic data advisory services and campaign consultation across institutional decision-making frameworks, continuous real-time monitoring of political trends and electoral dynamics across all EU Member States, and the progressive development of inter-institutional data infrastructure and visual analytics platforms. The unit's workload allocation reflects a deliberately structured approach to balancing research design and execution (35%), strategic intelligence dissemination to institutional leadership (25%), advisory services and campaign support (20%), electoral and political monitoring (15%), and data accessibility enhancement (5%), thereby ensuring that citizen perspective data flows systematically through Parliament's legislative and political agendas while maintaining institutional capacity for strategic foresight and evidence-based decision-making.
04-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Public Opinion Research Design & Execution** (35%): Comprehensive design and execution of Parliament's public opinion surveys, with particular emphasis on Eurobarometer programme methodology; longitudinal trend analysis of citizen attitudes, expectations, and policy concerns regarding the European Union and European Parliament; in-depth analytical assessment of major EU-related issues

• **Strategic Intelligence Dissemination to Leadership** (25%): Systematic translation of audience insight data into targeted institutional deliverables; delivery of citizen perspective newsletters to Members addressing key Plenary agenda topics and legislative priorities; provision of customized public opinion briefings to the Cabinet of the President linking research findings to political agendas; direct connection of research outputs to Parliament's legislative and political activities

• **Campaign Support & Strategic Data Advisory Services** (20%): Provision of targeted survey data and expert consultation to decision-makers and operational units across DG COMM and the broader institution; design consultation and optimization for specific campaign initiatives; Eurobarometer data analytics and survey question refinement; strategic guidance on survey design and Eurobarometer application within campaign frameworks

• **Electoral & Political Monitoring** (15%): Real-time surveillance and continuous monitoring of evolving political trends across all EU Member States; tracking of national voting intention patterns and dynamics; analysis of European Parliament composition changes and political group evolution; support for institutional strategic planning and political group analysis in preparation for successive European elections

• **Data Infrastructure & Accessibility Enhancement** (5%): Development of inter-institutional Eurobarometer database systems and integrated visual analytics dashboards; consolidation of previously dispersed survey data into unified, accessible platforms; enhancement of institutional data accessibility and institutional knowledge management through improved visual analytics infrastructure

04A.name: 04A
original_activities: Ensure the coordination of directorate units in their support activities to the media and MEPs (including human resources issues, training and programming), as well as relations with other directorates of DG COMM.

Provide assistance to political and administrative bodies: formulate elements of response as well as write analysis notes and proposals for action to the hierarchy and political authorities of the Institution.

Coordinate key management projects: grants awarded by Parliament to the media, press review contracts in the 27 Member States, Daphne Caruana Galizia Prize for Journalism, Training Program for young European journalists.
Identify the objectives and priorities for the Media Department and follow the short and long term editorial policy.
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer.
04A.refined_description: Unit 04A functions as the strategic coordination and governance hub within the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), positioned as a Level 1 Directorate directly beneath parent unit 04 (which reports to the Secretary General) and exercising direct oversight of five specialized subordinate operational units: 04A0010 (Financial and Budgetary Management Service), 04A10 (Parliamentary Communications Interface), 04A20 (Press Relations and Media Engagement), 04A30 (Audiovisual Production), and 04A70 (Media Analysis and Monitoring). The unit's multifaceted mission encompasses comprehensive coordination of directorate-wide support functions, institutional liaison, strategic media management initiatives, and financial administration, positioning it as the operational and strategic nexus connecting DG COMM's internal coordination mechanisms with broader institutional governance structures and political authorities. Unit 04A operationalizes its strategic directives across five primary functional domains: ensuring seamless coordination of all directorate units in delivering support activities to Members of the European Parliament and media partners (encompassing human resources administration, professional training programs, editorial programming, and cross-directorate liaison); providing analytical and strategic assistance to the European Parliament's political and administrative bodies through formulation of response elements, preparation of analytical notes, and development of actionable proposals for senior institutional leadership; managing critical strategic projects including European Parliament media grants programs, coordination of press review contracts across all 27 Member States, administration of the Daphne Caruana Galizia Prize for Journalism, and supervision of the Training Program for young European journalists; establishing strategic priorities and editorial policy frameworks through identification of departmental objectives and monitoring of short and medium-term editorial direction; and exercising subdelegated authorizing officer functions encompassing financial and administrative authorization responsibilities as delegated by higher institutional authority.
04A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Coordination and Internal Support Services** (40%): Comprehensive coordination of all directorate units in delivering support activities to Members of the European Parliament and media partners; management of human resources administration, professional training programs, and editorial programming; maintenance of inter-directorate relations within DG COMM; operationalization of strategic directives through five specialized subordinate units

• **Strategic Institutional Analysis and Policy Advisory Services** (30%): Provision of specialized analytical and strategic assistance to the European Parliament's political and administrative bodies; formulation of response elements and analytical notes; development of actionable proposals directed to institutional hierarchy and political authorities; support to senior institutional leadership

• **Key Media Projects and Institutional Initiatives Management** (15%): Oversight and coordination of European Parliament grants awarded to media entities; coordination of press review contracts across all 27 Member States; administration and stewardship of the Daphne Caruana Galizia Prize for Journalism; supervision of the Training Program for young European journalists

• **Strategic Planning and Editorial Policy Governance** (10%): Identification of Media Department objectives and institutional priorities; establishment and monitoring of short and long-term editorial policy direction and frameworks; strategic priority-setting for departmental operations

• **Financial and Administrative Authorization** (5%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer functions; fulfillment of financial and administrative authorization responsibilities within delegated scope as assigned by higher institutional authority

04A0010.name: 04A0010
original_activities: Assist, in order to respect the principle of good financial management, the management and all subdelegated authorizing officers of the Media Directorate with budgetary and financial matters: budgetary programming, financial implementation, management and monitoring of budgetary consumption, public procurement, subsidies.
Define and manage the different implementation methods in terms, in particular, of instructions and advice to operational colleagues.
Collaborate with (financial) colleagues from the directorates of DG COMM and other directorates-general (the General Directorate of Finance, etc.).
Participate in the implementation of the new application for managing invitations from journalists and other opinion multipliers.
Manage the human resources of the Service.
04A0010.refined_description: Unit 04A0010 is the Financial and Budgetary Management Service of the Media Directorate within the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), functioning as a second-level organizational unit subordinate to parent Unit 04A and operating cohesively with sibling units 04A10, 04A20, 04A30, and 04A70. The unit's overarching mission is to serve as the essential financial backbone of the Media Directorate by ensuring rigorous adherence to the principle of good financial management across all directorate operations while providing comprehensive financial stewardship and advisory services to the Directorate's management and all subdelegated authorizing officers. The unit's primary operational focus (75% of workload) encompasses direct assistance with critical financial responsibilities including budgetary programming, financial implementation, comprehensive management and monitoring of budgetary consumption, public procurement administration, and subsidies management and oversight. Complementing this core mission, the unit dedicates 10% of its workload to defining and managing diverse implementation methodologies through the development and dissemination of detailed instructions and advisory guidance to operational colleagues across the directorate, while allocating 8% to establishing and maintaining collaborative relationships with financial specialists across peer directorates within DG COMM and external directorates-general such as the General Directorate of Finance, thereby ensuring coordinated financial management and institutional coherence. The unit also contributes 5% of its capacity to the technical implementation and operational management of a new digital application system designed to manage journalist invitations and engagement with opinion multipliers and external stakeholders, and maintains 2% of its workload for human resources management and personnel administration within the Service itself. Through these integrated activities, Unit 04A0010 fulfills its essential role in supporting the Media Directorate's strategic communications objectives while maintaining compliance with European Parliament financial governance standards.
04A0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Stewardship and Budgetary Management** (75%): Provision of comprehensive financial assistance to Media Directorate management and subdelegated authorizing officers encompassing budgetary programming, financial implementation, budgetary consumption management and monitoring, public procurement administration, and subsidies management and oversight—ensuring adherence to good financial management principles and European Parliament financial governance standards

• **Implementation Methodology and Operational Guidance** (10%): Definition and management of diverse implementation methodologies through development and dissemination of detailed instructions and advisory guidance to operational colleagues across the Media Directorate

• **Inter-institutional Financial Coordination** (8%): Establishment and maintenance of collaborative relationships with financial specialists across DG COMM directorates and external directorates-general (particularly the General Directorate of Finance) to ensure coordinated financial management and institutional financial coherence

• **Digital Systems Implementation and Stakeholder Management** (5%): Technical implementation and operational management of a new digital application system for managing journalist invitations and engagement with opinion multipliers and external stakeholders

• **Internal Service Management** (2%): Human resources management and personnel administration within the Service unit itself

04A10.name: 04A10
original_activities: Monitor and support the activities of parliamentary committees and delegations, and their rapporteurs, with media representatives on legislative and political subjects and ensure their dissemination in several (sometimes 23) languages.
Maintain and develop lasting, trusting relationships with journalists accredited to institutions in Brussels. Organize press briefings on specific subjects.
Manage press rooms and press conferences.
Ensure coordination and editorial planning with other units of the directorate and other directorates of DG COMM by ensuring a close link with parliamentary work within the committees.
Co-manage (with the Audiovisual unit) the accreditation of journalists and participate in the inter-institutional accreditation committee.
04A10.refined_description:
**

Unit 04A10 operates as a specialized parliamentary communications interface within Directorate 04A of the Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), functioning as the primary liaison and coordination mechanism between the European Parliament's legislative bodies and external media stakeholders. Positioned as a second-level hierarchical unit reporting directly to Direction 04A and coordinating with sibling units (04A0010, 04A20, 04A30, and 04A70), Unit 04A10 executes five integrated functional areas that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation across strategic communication, operational management, and inter-institutional governance domains. The unit's core mission encompasses monitoring and actively supporting the communication activities of parliamentary committees, delegations, and their designated rapporteurs in their interactions with media representatives on legislative and political subjects, ensuring comprehensive dissemination of parliamentary messaging across multiple language versions (up to 23 languages) to maximize reach across EU member states and international audiences. Additionally, Unit 04A10 cultivates and maintains sustained, trust-based professional relationships with journalists accredited to the Brussels institutional complex while organizing specialized press briefings focused on particular legislative subjects and policy initiatives, thereby facilitating informed media coverage of parliamentary work. The unit assumes operational responsibility for the day-to-day management of physical press facilities (including press rooms) and the coordination of press conferences, while simultaneously performing critical cross-directorate coordination functions to ensure editorial alignment and strategic planning synchronization with sibling units within Directorate 04A and parallel units across other DG COMM directorates, maintaining essential connectivity with parliamentary committee work and legislative processes. Finally, Unit 04A10 co-manages journalist accreditation procedures jointly with the Audiovisual unit and participates in the inter-institutional accreditation committee, ensuring standardized access protocols and compliance with external media governance frameworks across EU institutions.

---

**
04A10.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Parliamentary Media Liaison and Multilingual Dissemination** (40%): Monitor and actively support communication activities of parliamentary committees, delegations, and rapporteurs in their interactions with media representatives on legislative and political matters; ensure comprehensive multilingual content dissemination across up to 23 language versions to maximize reach across EU member states and international audiences.

• **Journalist Relations and Specialized Press Engagement** (30%): Cultivate and maintain sustained, trust-based professional relationships with journalists accredited to Brussels-based institutions; organize and conduct targeted press briefings focused on particular legislative subjects and policy initiatives to facilitate informed media coverage.

• **Press Infrastructure and Conference Management** (10%): Manage operational aspects of physical press facilities, including press rooms; coordinate the logistics and execution of press conferences to provide essential infrastructure and support for media engagement activities.

• **Cross-Directorate Coordination and Editorial Planning** (10%): Ensure editorial alignment and strategic planning synchronization with sibling units within Directorate 04A and parallel units across other DG COMM directorates; maintain substantive connectivity with parliamentary committee work and legislative processes to inform integrated communication strategy development.

• **Journalist Accreditation Management and Inter-Institutional Governance** (10%): Co-manage journalist accreditation procedures jointly with the Audiovisual unit; participate in inter-institutional accreditation committee proceedings to ensure standardized access protocols and compliance with external media governance frameworks across EU institutions.

04A20.name: 04A20
original_activities: Contribute within the Media Directorate to increasing coverage of parliamentary news and improving understanding of it by the media.
Coordinate the work of press officers in the EPLOs.

Organize seminars for the press in Brussels and in member countries explaining the functioning of the institution and its legislative and political work; invite journalists and other media professionals to follow the work of the plenary.
Manage the unit's workforce and organize its tasks with regard to the general objective and specific objectives of an operational nature.
Plan and execute the unit's annual work program and associated appropriations, produce the annual activity report and prepare the forecast for financial year N+1.
04A20.refined_description: Unit 04A20 operates as a specialized Media Relations and Press Coordination unit within the Media Directorate (04A) of the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication, functioning at the second hierarchical level as an operational leaf node without subordinate units. The unit's mission encompasses two equally weighted strategic pillars: enhancing media coverage and public comprehension of parliamentary activities through collaborative efforts within the Media Directorate (30% of workload), and exercising direct coordination authority over press officers stationed across all European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) throughout member countries to ensure consistent and coherent external communication strategies (30% of workload). To operationalize these strategic objectives, Unit 04A20 organizes and delivers structured educational seminars for media professionals and journalists in both Brussels and member state locations, providing comprehensive briefings on institutional functioning, legislative processes, and political developments, while simultaneously facilitating direct journalist participation in parliamentary plenary proceedings and access to parliamentary work (20% of workload). The unit's operational sustainability depends upon internal workforce management and task organization aligned with both general and operational-specific objectives of the Media Directorate, ensuring alignment with directorate strategic goals (10% of workload), as well as comprehensive financial and programmatic planning including annual work program execution and strategy, budget appropriations management, comprehensive activity reporting, and forward financial forecasting for subsequent fiscal years (10% of workload). Through these interconnected activities, Unit 04A20 positions itself as a critical nexus between the European Parliament's internal communication strategy and external media relations, directly supporting the Media Directorate's overarching goal of strengthening parliamentary communication effectiveness while maintaining operational coordination with sibling units 04A0010, 04A10, 04A30, and 04A70.
04A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Media Relations and Coverage Enhancement** (30%): Contribute within the Media Directorate to increasing coverage of parliamentary news and improving media understanding and interpretation of parliamentary activities and institutional operations; advance parliamentary visibility and accessibility to media professionals across the institution's communication ecosystem

• **External Press Officer Network Coordination** (30%): Coordinate and supervise the operational work and strategic alignment of press officers deployed across all European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) throughout member countries; exercise direct coordination authority to ensure consistent and coherent external communication strategies

• **Media Education and Institutional Engagement Programs** (20%): Organize and deliver structured seminars for press professionals and media representatives in Brussels and member state locations, providing comprehensive institutional briefings on parliamentary functioning, legislative procedures, and political work; facilitate journalist attendance, participation, and direct access to parliamentary plenary sessions

• **Unit Management and Operational Governance** (10%): Manage human resources and workforce organization, assign and coordinate tasks and responsibilities, and ensure alignment with general and operational-specific objectives established by the Media Directorate; maintain operational independence as a leaf-node unit

• **Financial Planning and Program Administration** (10%): Plan and execute annual work programs with associated budget appropriations and strategic direction, produce comprehensive annual activity reports, manage appropriations, and develop detailed financial forecasts for subsequent fiscal years (N+1)

04A30.name: 04A30
original_activities: Ensure coverage and dissemination of EP news for the audiovisual media, live debates, production of illustrative images; coverage and webstreaming of plenaries, all parliamentary committees and special events.

Ensure the coordination and management of technical assistance offered to television and radio channels and to MPs: ENG teams, live positions and studios; transmissions; multimedia services (VOX BOX), radio and photo.
Ensure the proper operation, maintenance, renewal, development and optimization of the various AV technical installations and IT infrastructures. Manage audiovisual operations.
Design a promotion strategy, produce promotional materials and organize seminars. Establish and develop contacts with media platforms and strategic partner networks.

Ensure the coordination and programming of content produced within the unit and coordination with the Media Department. Ensure the planning, organization as well as the implementation and running of various special events.
Ensure the archiving, viewing and indexing of plenary sessions, committee meetings and other EP activities as well as illustrative images. Manage the European Parliament Media Library.
Ensure the administrative, financial and budgetary management of the unit, under the responsibility of the head of unit. Manage the framework contract for audiovisual services.
04A30.refined_description:
Unit 04A30 functions as the European Parliament's central audiovisual production and technical operations hub, operating under the strategic direction of Unit 04A within the Directorate General for Communication. The unit holds primary institutional responsibility for comprehensive capture, production, technical management, and archival of all parliamentary audiovisual content while simultaneously providing integrated media support services to Members of the European Parliament and external broadcasting partners. Unit 04A30 manages the complete audiovisual lifecycle—from real-time coverage of parliamentary proceedings across all venues to systematic preservation and accessibility of institutional records—while maintaining coordinated relationships with sibling units (04A0010, 04A10, 04A20, 04A70) to ensure integrated communication strategy implementation. The unit operates as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy without subordinate units, with all operational activities directly managed under the accountability of the unit head. Through its diversified operational portfolio, Unit 04A30 ensures robust institutional visibility, stakeholder engagement, technical excellence, and comprehensive documentation of parliamentary activities across multiple audiovisual platforms and distribution channels.

---

##
04A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Media Coverage, Content Production, and Dissemination** (25%): Comprehensive audiovisual capture and production of European Parliament news and proceedings for dissemination across all media platforms; live debate and plenary session coverage; production and distribution of illustrative imagery; technical execution of webstreaming infrastructure for plenaries, committees, and special parliamentary events; delivery of content to broadcast media outlets and digital audiences.

• **Technical Infrastructure Management and Broadcasting Services** (40%): Coordination and delivery of integrated technical assistance to television and radio broadcasting channels and Members of Parliament; deployment and management of Electronic News Gathering (ENG) teams; establishment, operation, and maintenance of live transmission positions and broadcast studios; management of transmission infrastructure systems; provision of specialized multimedia services including VOX BOX systems, radio production, and photographic services; systematic maintenance, preventive and corrective servicing, strategic renewal, continuous development, and performance optimization of all audiovisual technical installations and information technology infrastructures; direct management of comprehensive audiovisual operations across all parliamentary venues.

• **Content Coordination, Strategic Promotion, and Media Relations** (20%): Coordination and programming of all unit-produced content in close collaboration with the Media Department; design and execution of comprehensive promotion strategies and production of promotional materials; organization of educational seminars; establishment and cultivation of strategic relationships with media platforms and partner networks to enhance institutional visibility and stakeholder engagement; planning, organization, and implementation of high-profile special parliamentary events.

• **Digital Preservation, Archival Management, and Institutional Memory** (10%): Comprehensive archiving, cataloging, systematic indexing, and accessibility management of plenary session recordings, committee meeting documentation, and all parliamentary activities; management of illustrative image collections; operational stewardship and administration of the European Parliament Media Library as the institutional repository for parliamentary audiovisual documentation; ensuring discoverability and long-term preservation of parliamentary records.

• **Administrative and Financial Governance** (5%): Administrative oversight and financial management of unit operations; budgetary control and resource allocation; framework contract management for audiovisual services; head of unit accountability functions for all operational and financial responsibilities.

04A70.name: 04A70
original_activities: Produce qualitative and quantitative analyses on Parliament's presence in the media, media angles, and trends, and ensure media coverage of each plenary session. For key files and important moments/votes, provide analyses throughout the procedure.

Monitor the presence of the European Parliament, its President, and Members in the print, online, and audiovisual media of the 27 EU Member States and produce alerts for the Editorial Committee and hierarchy. Produce daily print and audiovisual press reviews.

Manage the European Parliament Media Monitoring (EPMM) platform. Ensure the handling of EPMM-related questions and training. Act as the central point of contact with the Joint Research Centre and carry out analyses for other DGs.
Coordinate and participate in thematic teams covering the European Parliament's legislative priorities, supporting the Long-Term Editorial Committee in its definition of key communication topics.
Make recommendations on topics of media interest for media plans and communications strategy, including medium and long-term editorial policy.
04A70.refined_description:
Unit 04A70, the Media Analysis and Monitoring Division, operates within the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM) at Hierarchy Level 2 under Direction 04A, functioning as a critical intelligence and analytical hub that bridges media landscape monitoring with institutional strategic communication planning. The unit's primary mission is to provide comprehensive media intelligence, systematic monitoring, and evidence-based analytical support that informs the Parliament's communication strategy and editorial decision-making across all 27 EU Member States. Unit 04A70 executes this mandate through five integrated functional activities that collectively position it as an essential provider of data-driven intelligence to Direction 04A's strategic coordination function and its sibling units (04A0010, 04A10, 04A20, and 04A30). The unit operates in close coordination with the Editorial Committee, Long-Term Editorial Committee, and institutional leadership, while maintaining a central coordination interface with the Joint Research Centre and providing analytical support to other Directorates General. Through its comprehensive monitoring infrastructure and analytical capabilities, Unit 04A70 enables evidence-based communication decisions and medium to long-term editorial policy development across the institution.

---

#
04A70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Media Analysis and Coverage Intelligence (30%)**: Produce qualitative and quantitative analyses examining Parliament's media presence, media angles, and emerging coverage trends; ensure systematic documentation of media coverage for each plenary session; deliver procedure-specific analytical reports throughout legislative processes with particular focus on key legislative files and critical votes; provide comprehensive media footprint monitoring for parliamentary procedures.

• **Multi-Format Media Monitoring and Real-Time Alert Systems (28%)**: Conduct continuous monitoring of the European Parliament, its President, and Members across print, online, and audiovisual media channels across all 27 EU Member States; generate real-time alerts directed to the Editorial Committee and institutional leadership hierarchy; produce daily press reviews encompassing both print and audiovisual media formats.

• **EPMM Platform Management and Inter-Institutional Liaison (25%)**: Operationally manage and administer the European Parliament Media Monitoring (EPMM) platform as the central technical and analytical hub; provide comprehensive user support, technical assistance, and training delivery; serve as primary liaison with the Joint Research Centre; execute comparative media analyses and provide analytical support serving other Directorates General.

• **Strategic Editorial Coordination and Thematic Team Participation (10%)**: Actively coordinate and participate in thematic teams addressing the European Parliament's legislative priorities; provide direct support to the Long-Term Editorial Committee in identifying, defining, and refining key communication topics and strategic messaging angles.

• **Strategic Communications Recommendations and Editorial Policy Input (7%)**: Develop evidence-based recommendations regarding media-relevant topics for integration into media plans and broader communications strategy; provide advisory input for medium and long-term editorial policy formulation and communications strategy refinement.

04E.name: 04E
original_activities: Develop, coordinate, and implement integrated communication outreach activities and multi-channel campaigns directed at citizens, partners, and multipliers with the aim to inform citizens about the EP's legislative and political activities, create positive attitude and trust, and invite audiences to actively engage with the Parliament.
Ensure coordination across the six units in the Directorate for Campaigns on horizontal issues (e.g. human resources, budget, training, programming, reporting, etc.).

Provide appropriate expertise and assistance to other services on consistent communication including a comprehensive branding approach for the EP administration, conception of strategies, assets and products.
Ensure appropriate presence of the European Parliament on relevant social media platforms.
Coordinate and steer the revamp of the existing website of the European Parliament.
Streamlining and coordinating the DG COMM's youth and educational offer across all platforms and touchpoints.
04E.refined_description:
Unit 04E functions as a Direction-level strategic hub within the Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM), reporting to parent Unit 04 and serving as the central coordinating authority for the European Parliament's integrated institutional communication architecture and digital presence management. The unit operates across dual responsibility domains—external stakeholder engagement and internal organizational coordination—exercising primary authority over the six operational child units within the Directorate for Campaigns (04E10 for digital communications, 04E20 for creative production, 04E30 for youth engagement, 04E40 for specialized operations, and 04E60 for cultural programming, plus one additional operational unit). Unit 04E develops, coordinates, and implements comprehensive multi-channel communication campaigns and outreach activities directed at citizens, partners, and multipliers with explicit objectives to inform audiences about the Parliament's legislative and political activities, cultivate positive institutional attitudes and trust, and catalyze active civic engagement while simultaneously managing the Parliament's strategic presence across relevant social media platforms and steering the comprehensive revamp and modernization of the institutional website infrastructure. The unit exercises critical horizontal governance and coordination functions across its subordinate units on human resources management, budget administration, staff training, programming frameworks, and comprehensive institutional reporting, while providing specialized expertise and advisory assistance to other Parliament services on consistent institutional communication strategies, comprehensive branding approaches, strategic asset conception frameworks, and communication product development aligned with EP administration objectives. Additionally, Unit 04E coordinates and integrates DG COMM's youth and educational programming offerings across all digital and physical platforms and audience touchpoints to ensure cohesive institutional messaging and maximum reach across demographic segments. Through these integrated functions spanning strategic campaign development, digital platform management, internal coordination, and educational programming integration, Unit 04E operationalizes the Parliament's institutional communication strategy while maintaining essential governance and coordination services to its child operational units and broader institutional stakeholders.

##
04E.refined_activity_summary:
• **Citizen Engagement & Strategic Communication Campaigns** (25%): Development, coordination, and implementation of integrated multi-channel communication outreach campaigns directed at citizens, partners, and multipliers to inform audiences about EP legislative and political activities, establish positive institutional attitudes and trust, and actively facilitate citizen engagement with Parliament

• **Digital Presence & Platform Management** (30%): Management of the European Parliament's strategic and operationally appropriate presence across relevant social media platforms (15%) and comprehensive steering and coordination of the institutional website revamp and modernization initiative (15%), collectively strengthening digital accessibility and stakeholder interaction channels

• **Horizontal Directorate Coordination & Governance** (15%): Cross-unit coordination across six Directorate for Campaigns operational units on critical functions including human resources management, budget administration, training programs, strategic programming frameworks, and comprehensive institutional reporting mechanisms

• **Strategic Communication Support & Institutional Branding** (15%): Provision of specialized expertise and advisory assistance to other Parliament services on consistent institutional communication strategies, comprehensive branding frameworks, strategic conception of communication assets, and development of communication products aligned with EP administration objectives

• **Youth & Educational Programming Integration** (15%): Coordination and streamlined integration of DG COMM's youth and educational offerings across all digital and physical platforms and institutional touchpoints to ensure cohesive messaging, maximum institutional reach, and effective engagement with younger demographic segments

04E10.name: 04E10
original_activities: Manage different social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, YouTube, LinkedIn, TikTok, etc.): editorial planning, coverage, multilingual daily publications, creation of visuals, videos and infographics. Coordinate Liaison Offices.

Program, coordinate and produce editorial coverage (text, image and multimedia) of EP news for the general public in 24 languages ​​(+ Ukrainian) via its website, as well as through special event sites.
Design and manage the inter-service 'Content Creators' project: definition of strategy, operational implementation, organization of events, analysis of results and reporting.

Design and deliver training on social media, create appropriate content and carry out analyzes of the social accounts of EP MEPs. Strategic and practical training in online communication for other services.
Design and implement the digital promotional communication strategy of DG COMM and all of its flagship campaigns, including the co-management of the paid advertising network.
Data intelligence: research, analysis, reporting, KPIs of social media platforms, websites, promotional campaigns, together.eu activities.
Moderate content across all central platforms, including managing responses in 24 languages ​​and combating misinformation.
Implement and analyze the impact of social media strategy. Monitoring trends and developing strategic documents relating to communication on social media.
04E10.refined_description: Unit 04E10 functions as the European Parliament's integrated central hub for digital communications and social media operations, positioned within Direction 04E of the Directorate General for Communication. The unit holds primary responsibility for orchestrating the institution's comprehensive multifaceted digital presence across all major social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, YouTube, LinkedIn, TikTok, and emerging platforms), encompassing editorial planning, daily multilingual content publication in 24 languages plus Ukrainian, creation of visual assets, videos and infographics, and coordination of Liaison Office engagement activities. Unit 04E10 simultaneously programs, coordinates, and produces comprehensive editorial coverage in text, image, and multimedia formats for the EP's public-facing website and specialized event platforms across all 24 languages plus Ukrainian, ensuring consistent messaging across all digital touchpoints. The unit designs and manages the cross-departmental 'Content Creators' strategic project through definition, operational implementation, event organization, results analysis, and institutional reporting, while developing and delivering specialized training on social media best practices and strategic online communication guidance to EP Members and other institutional services, including analytical assessment of MEP account performance. Unit 04E10 designs and implements DG COMM's overarching digital promotional communication strategy and co-manages the paid advertising network for flagship campaigns, supported by continuous data intelligence operations encompassing research, analysis, reporting, and KPI measurement across social media platforms, websites, promotional campaigns, and together.eu initiatives. The unit maintains quality assurance through multilingual content moderation across all central platforms with 24-language response management and active misinformation mitigation, while implementing and evaluating social media strategy impact through trend analysis and strategic documentation development. Collectively, these activities position Unit 04E10 as the Parliament's operational engine for its digital communication ecosystem, ensuring data-informed, quality-assured, multilingual messaging while maintaining strategic alignment with broader institutional communication objectives and coordinating with sibling units 04E20, 04E30, 04E40, and 04E60.
04E10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Social Media Platform Management & Multilingual Content Production** (35%): Direct management of six major social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, YouTube, LinkedIn, TikTok) and emerging platforms including editorial planning, daily multilingual publications (24 languages plus Ukrainian), creation of visuals, videos, and infographics; coordination of Liaison Office social media activities and engagement

• **Website & Event Platform Editorial Operations** (15%): Programming, coordination, and production of comprehensive editorial coverage in text, image, and multimedia formats for the EP's public-facing website and specialized event platforms; content delivery across 24 languages plus Ukrainian to general public audiences

• **Strategic Projects, Training & Capacity Building** (25%): Design and management of inter-service 'Content Creators' project including strategic definition, operational implementation, event organization, results analysis, and institutional reporting (15%); development and delivery of social media training programs and contextually appropriate training content; provision of strategic and practical digital communication guidance to EP Members and other institutional services; analytical assessment of MEP account performance (10%)

• **Digital Strategy & Paid Advertising Campaign Management** (10%): Design and implementation of DG COMM's overarching digital promotional communication strategy; co-management of paid advertising network infrastructure; oversight of flagship campaign execution and strategic alignment

• **Data Intelligence, Analytics & Content Moderation** (15%): Data intelligence research, analysis, and reporting on social media platforms, websites, promotional campaigns, and together.eu initiatives; KPI measurement and performance tracking (5%); multilingual content moderation across all central platforms with response management in 24 languages; active identification and mitigation of misinformation; implementation and impact analysis of social media strategy through trend monitoring; development of strategic communications documents informing institutional social media policy (5%)

04E20.name: 04E20
