original_activities: Conceive and develop campaigns and assets for communication projects, events and other initiatives of the European Parliament. This includes end-to-end development, from strategy to creative concept to the final product in terms of copywriting, design, video production, etc.

Design, produce and distribute visual assets on a turnkey basis to service direct requests from units in DG COMM and beyond for their projects, events and regular day-to-day activities. Visuals are produced across all formats and platforms.

Ensure brand compliant communication across all communication touch-points in DG COMM and across the EP administration. Provide branding tools to help give Parliament's communication a recognisable, consistent and positive public image.

Manage framework contracts, tender procedures and other administrative actions for the DG COMM, including contracts for graphic design services, video production for campaigns, media buying, promo items and acquisition of photography licenses/rights.
04E20.refined_description: Unit 04E20 functions as the integrated creative and production hub within Direction 04E of the Directorate General for Communication, serving as a critical operational unit positioned as a leaf-node peer to Units 04E10, 04E30, 04E40, and 04E60 under the strategic coordination of Direction 04E. The unit delivers comprehensive communication solutions across the European Parliament's institutional ecosystem through four complementary and interconnected operational pillars that collectively constitute its complete mission mandate. Unit 04E20 executes end-to-end strategic campaign conception and development, encompassing the complete creative lifecycle from strategic ideation and planning through creative conceptualization, copywriting, design, multimedia production, and final deliverables; simultaneously provides turnkey production and distribution of visual assets across all digital and physical formats to service direct requests from DG COMM units and broader EP administration; maintains stewardship of brand governance and compliance assurance across all communication touchpoints throughout DG COMM and the wider EP administration, including development and provision of branding governance tools, standards frameworks, and institutional identity guidelines; and manages procurement and contractual administration for DG COMM, encompassing framework contract oversight, tender procedures, vendor management, and administrative actions for graphic design services, video production campaigns, media purchasing, promotional materials procurement, and photography licensing and intellectual property acquisition. Through this integrated operational structure, Unit 04E20 directly supports the European Parliament's strategic communication objectives by delivering consistent, recognizable, and positive institutional messaging while ensuring coherence across all communication initiatives and maintaining Parliament's communication excellence across internal and external touchpoints.
04E20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Campaign Conception & Creative Development** (30%): End-to-end conception, strategic planning, and execution of communication campaigns and institutional initiatives, encompassing complete creative lifecycle including strategic ideation, copywriting, design, multimedia and video production, and final product delivery across all Parliament communication initiatives and events

• **Visual Asset Production & Distribution** (30%): Turnkey production and distribution of visual materials across all digital and physical formats to service direct requests from DG COMM units and broader EP administration, supporting both specialized projects and routine day-to-day operational communication needs

• **Brand Governance, Compliance & Institutional Identity** (25%): Establishment, maintenance, and stewardship of brand-compliant communication standards across all DG COMM and EP administration touchpoints, including development and provision of branding governance tools, compliance frameworks, and institutional identity guidelines to ensure consistent, recognizable, and positive public representation

• **Procurement Management & Contractual Administration** (15%): Management of framework contracts, tender procedures, vendor relations, and administrative procurement actions for DG COMM, including vendor contracts for graphic design services, video production campaigns, media purchasing, promotional materials procurement, and photography rights and intellectual property acquisition

04E30.name: 04E30
original_activities: Manage the biannual event "EYE": 'meeting young Europeans', including all preparation, evaluation and follow-up activities, as well as other events and meeting points, ongoing dialogue between young people and Parliament.

Develop a lasting relationship between the EP and youth organizations by creating a network of partner organizations, to work together on projects aimed at young Europeans and amplify communication about the EP and the EU.

Communicate with young people through online platforms (EYE, youth hub), social media, events and activities of institutional partners. Participate in communication projects at management and general management level.

Coordinate and develop the PE's educational offering, bringing together educational materials into a coherent, flexible and user-friendly toolbox, adapted to different age groups (11 to 24 years) and learning levels.

Coordinate the implementation of the youth strategy at DG COMM level, being the main unit in charge. Improve the offer for young people through better connection and coherence between the activities organized by the general management.

Collaborate with the Directorate General of Personnel in the area of ​​activation of current and former interns to promote the role of the EP and the importance of actively participating in democratic life in Europe through their networks.
Promote the EP's offer for youth as well as best practices to stimulate the democratic engagement of young people among EP MEPs, colleagues from other European services and institutions.
04E30.refined_description: Unit 04E30 functions as the European Parliament's dedicated youth engagement and strategic communications hub, operating as a specialized leaf-node unit within Direction 04E of the Directorate General for Communication (DG COMM). The unit's primary institutional mission is to establish meaningful, sustained dialogue between the European Parliament and young Europeans across multiple engagement channels and platforms, while simultaneously amplifying institutional communication regarding the EP and EU initiatives. Operating in coordination with sibling units 04E10, 04E20, 04E40, and 04E60 to deliver integrated communication outcomes across the DG COMM portfolio, Unit 04E30 executes a comprehensive portfolio of youth-focused activities spanning direct engagement, strategic partnerships, digital communications, educational programming, and democratic participation initiatives. The unit's workload distribution reflects a balanced approach to both flagship programming and foundational relationship-building activities, with particular emphasis on sustaining direct youth engagement while establishing the institutional infrastructure necessary for long-term democratic participation and institutional connectivity among young Europeans aged 11-24 years across varying learning proficiency levels.
04E30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct Youth Engagement and Flagship Event Management** (45%): Comprehensive management of the biannual "EYE" (European Youth Event) encompassing full lifecycle activities including preparatory logistics, real-time event execution, post-event evaluation, and systematic follow-up initiatives; coordination of supplementary events and sustained dialogue mechanisms designed to foster direct, ongoing interaction between young people and parliamentary representatives.

• **Strategic Partnership Development and Network Building** (15%): Establishment, cultivation, and maintenance of a coordinated, sustainable network of partner youth organizations; collaborative project development targeting young Europeans; amplification of institutional communication regarding the European Parliament and EU objectives through partner organization channels.

• **Multi-Channel Youth Communication and Digital Outreach** (15%): Implementation of integrated communication strategies across digital platforms (EYE portal, youth hub, social media channels); event-based activations; institutional partnership communications; participation in management-level and strategic communication initiatives within DG COMM.

• **Educational Programming and Youth Strategy Coordination** (10%): Consolidation of diverse educational materials into a comprehensive, adaptable toolbox calibrated to serve age cohorts 11-24 years with varying learning proficiency levels; principal responsibility for implementing DG COMM's youth strategy; enhancement of youth-oriented offerings through improved inter-departmental coordination and organizational coherence.

• **Democratic Engagement Activation and Institutional Advocacy** (10%): Collaboration with the Directorate General of Personnel to activate current and former interns as democratic ambassadors within their professional networks, promoting parliamentary engagement and European democratic participation; concurrent advocacy and promotion of the Parliament's youth-focused offerings and evidence-based best practices among Members of the European Parliament, counterpart European service colleagues, and international institutional partners.

04E40.name: 04E40
original_activities: Ensure evolutionary, adaptive and corrective maintenance, technical migrations and deployments of websites forming part of the unit's portfolio: Topics, News, Multimedia Centre, About Parliament, At your Service, Contracts and Grants, EPLOs, SG, President, Engagement platform, online presence for elections and hearings.
Design new websites, in particular to support the EP's communication campaigns and the renewal of the Institution's online presence via the 'A Citizen Centric Web Presence' project.
Ensure evolutionary, adaptive and corrective maintenance, technical migrations and deployments of contribution systems linked to websites forming part of the unit's portfolio.
Ensure the updating of sections according to the evolution of the EP and the editorial development of new sections. Answer questions asked by Internet users to the webmaster.
Ensure the administrative, financial and budgetary management of the unit, as well as the coordination of Internet activities at the interinstitutional level (IOCC).
Provide training to EP internal users on the content contribution systems of the Europarl site (Engagement platform, Scribo, etc.), administer these systems and provide help and support to users.
04E40.refined_description: Unit 04E40, positioned as a specialized operational and technical management unit within Direction 04E of the Directorate General for Communication, functions as the European Parliament's primary hub for digital infrastructure management, web presence optimization, and citizen-centric digital engagement. The unit executes a comprehensive operational mandate encompassing the full lifecycle management of the Parliament's extensive institutional website portfolio—including Topics, News, Multimedia Centre, About Parliament, At your Service, Contracts and Grants, EPLOs, Secretariat General, President, Engagement platform, and specialized online presences for elections and parliamentary hearings—through evolutionary, adaptive, and corrective maintenance activities, technical migrations, and deployment operations ensuring continuous service availability and performance optimization. Concurrently, Unit 04E40 drives institutional digital transformation through strategic design and development of new websites and digital platforms, particularly in support of the European Parliament's strategic communication campaigns and the comprehensive renewal of its online institutional presence via the 'A Citizen Centric Web Presence' project initiative. The unit maintains parallel responsibility for the technical stewardship of contribution systems and user engagement platforms linked to its website portfolio, ensuring their evolutionary development, adaptive functionality, technical migrations, and successful deployment. Editorial governance and user engagement functions encompass the continuous updating of web sections in accordance with institutional evolution, development of new content areas, and direct responsiveness to Internet user inquiries through the webmaster function. The unit's operational sustainability is supported through dedicated administrative, financial, and budgetary management, alongside coordination of Internet activities at the interinstitutional level through participation in the Interinstitutional Online Cooperation Committee (IOCC). Finally, Unit 04E40 provides comprehensive training, system administration, and technical support services to European Parliament internal users regarding content contribution systems, including the Engagement platform and Scribo authoring environments. Operating as a leaf-node unit within the Direction structure, Unit 04E40 coordinates with sibling units 04E10, 04E20, 04E30, and 04E60 to collectively advance Direction 04E's strategic mandate for integrated digital communication excellence.
04E40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Website Portfolio Maintenance and Technical Operations** (30%): Execution of evolutionary, adaptive, and corrective maintenance activities; technical migrations; and deployment operations across the comprehensive institutional website portfolio (Topics, News, Multimedia Centre, About Parliament, At your Service, Contracts and Grants, EPLOs, Secretariat General, President, Engagement platform, and specialized online presences for elections and parliamentary hearings); ensures continuous service availability and performance optimization of critical digital assets.

• **Digital Transformation and Strategic Web Development** (30%): Design and development of new websites and digital platforms to support the European Parliament's strategic communication campaigns and comprehensive institutional digital transformation through the 'A Citizen Centric Web Presence' project; drives innovation in citizen-facing digital engagement and online presence renewal.

• **Content Contribution Systems and Platforms Management** (20%): Evolutionary maintenance, adaptive functionality enhancement, corrective interventions, technical migrations, and deployment operations for contribution systems and user engagement platforms directly integrated with the unit's website portfolio; ensures operational capability and continuous evolution of these critical systems.

• **Editorial Governance and User Engagement Services** (10%): Continuous updating of web section content aligned with institutional evolution; development of new content areas; direct responsiveness to Internet user inquiries through webmaster function; ensures content relevance, accessibility, and citizen engagement responsiveness.

• **Administrative, Financial, Institutional Coordination, and User Enablement** (10%): Administrative and budgetary management of unit operations; financial stewardship; coordination of Internet activities at the interinstitutional level through the Interinstitutional Online Cooperation Committee (IOCC); system administration of content contribution platforms (Engagement platform, Scribo); delivery of comprehensive training to internal Parliament users; and provision of technical support services ensuring operational capability across all contribution and engagement systems.

04E60.name: 04E60
original_activities: Design and produce exhibitions on themes linked to the legislative work of the EP and to democracy. These exhibitions are based partly on works contained in the EP's art collection and partly on loans. Organize guided tours.

Increase the visibility and scope of the EP's cultural activities, in collaboration with the competent services and cultural partners, highlighting the link between the activity and the legislative work of the Institution.

Build a network of partner organizations in the field of culture. In particular, establish strong relationships with umbrella organizations in the field of culture and follow up on engagement projects proposed by cultural organizations.

Manage the Institution's contemporary art collection in collaboration with the quaestor in charge of the art collection and the artistic committee (acquisitions, donations, storage, insurance, copyright and restoration of works of art).

Coordinate the calendar of cultural activities of the Citizens' Garden, organize part of the garden's activities, so as to complement the concerts offered by the General Directorate of the Presidency.
04E60.refined_description:
**

Unit 04E60 operates as a specialized cultural affairs and collections management entity within Direction 04E of the Directorate General for Communication, serving as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for institutionalizing culture as a strategic vehicle for democratic engagement, institutional visibility, and legislative communication. The unit executes five interconnected functional responsibilities that collectively constitute 100% of its operational workload, positioning it as the institutional nexus for translating the EP's democratic mission into accessible cultural experiences while maintaining rigorous stewardship of valuable artistic assets. The unit's activities span the complete spectrum of cultural operations: designing and producing thematic exhibitions that synthesize the EP's legislative priorities with its institutional art collection and external artistic loans while organizing interpretive guided tours to enhance public understanding; amplifying the visibility and reach of EP cultural initiatives through strategic collaboration with internal competent services and external cultural partners; cultivating and maintaining a comprehensive network of cultural sector relationships, particularly with umbrella organizations, while systematically evaluating engagement proposals from the cultural community; exercising direct stewardship responsibility for the institution's contemporary art collection through collaborative governance with the designated quaestor and artistic committee, encompassing the full lifecycle of acquisitions, donations, storage, insurance, copyright administration, and conservation/restoration; and coordinating the cultural calendar of the Citizens' Garden while organizing selected garden-based programming designed to complement concert offerings provided by the General Directorate of the Presidency. These activities collectively position Unit 04E60 as a critical instrument for advancing the Institution's mission to enhance democratic engagement and institutional credibility through deliberate cultural expression and community engagement.

**
04E60.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Exhibition Design, Production, and Public Engagement** (25%): Design, produce, and curate thematic exhibitions that explicitly connect the EP's legislative activities and democratic values to contemporary cultural discourse; incorporate works from the institution's proprietary art collection and strategic external loans; organize and facilitate interpretive guided tours to maximize public understanding, educational impact, and visitor engagement with exhibition content.

• **Cultural Visibility, Strategic Positioning, and Partnership Development** (45%): Amplify the scope and visibility of EP cultural activities through integrated collaboration with internal competent services and external cultural partners, establishing transparent linkages between cultural programming and the Institution's legislative mandate and democratic mission (25%); build, cultivate, and maintain a strategic network of cultural partner organizations, including umbrella organizations in the cultural sector, while systematically managing and following up on engagement proposals and collaborative initiatives proposed by cultural organizations (20%).

• **Contemporary Art Collection Stewardship and Asset Management** (20%): Manage the EP's contemporary art collection in collaborative partnership with the designated quaestor responsible for art collection governance and the artistic committee; oversee the complete lifecycle of collection stewardship including acquisitions, donation processing, secure storage, insurance administration, copyright protection and management, and conservation/restoration of artworks; ensure proper curatorial governance and preservation of institutional cultural assets.

• **Citizens' Garden Cultural Programming and Coordination** (10%): Coordinate the comprehensive cultural calendar of the Citizens' Garden; organize and execute selected garden-based cultural programming designed to complement and extend the concert offerings provided by the General Directorate of the Presidency; ensure synergistic integration of garden activities with broader institutional cultural strategy.

04B.name: 04B
original_activities: Ensure the consistency of all the activities of the Liaison Offices with the priorities of the EP and the communication strategy of the general management. Ensure synergy between Offices. Oversee the reform and simplification process within management.
Ensure the direction, coordination and management of the units of the Liaison Offices Directorate, relations with the other directorates of DG COMM and with the other general directorates.
Perform the functions of principal subdelegated authorizing officer, supervise budget execution through the Coordination Unit.

Manage the human resources of the Directorate: 31 units and approximately 280 people, who work in 35 different duty stations in the Member States. Supervise administrative matters related to Liaison Offices.
Lead management projects and cooperate on major projects such as the Europa Experiences. Write analysis notes and action proposals to the hierarchy and political authorities of the Institution.
Supervise the activities of the Liaison Offices in order to promote the essential functions of the EP to citizens, opinion multipliers, young people and the general public.
04B.refined_description:
**

Unit 04B functions as the strategic Direction-level management hub and central coordinating authority of the European Parliament's Liaison Offices Directorate within the DG COMM framework, exercising comprehensive oversight of 31 subordinate specialized units and approximately 280 personnel distributed across 35 distinct duty stations throughout the Member States. As a first-level hierarchical unit, 04B serves as the essential operational and strategic nexus connecting the parent Directorate General COMM with its subordinate child units—including the Financial and Operational Support Centre (04B20) and specialized Liaison Offices throughout Member States—while coordinating laterally with sibling units (04-10, 04-20, 04-30, 04-40, 04A, and others) within the institutional communication framework. The unit's primary mission encompasses ensuring organizational coherence, operational excellence, and strategic alignment of all Liaison Office activities with European Parliament institutional priorities and the established communication strategy of general management, while actively overseeing synergy mechanisms between offices and managing reform and simplification processes within management structures. Unit 04B exercises principal subdelegated authorizing officer functions and maintains rigorous financial accountability through comprehensive budget execution supervision via the Coordination Unit, concurrently managing comprehensive human resources administration for the geographically dispersed organizational structure—encompassing personnel administration, duty station logistics, and administrative compliance—while providing strategic direction and coordination of all Directorate units and maintaining essential collaborative relations with other DG COMM directorates and general directorates across the institution. The unit leads significant management initiatives and contributes substantively to major institutional projects including the Europa Experiences program, while producing analytical documentation and strategic action proposals directed to institutional hierarchy and political authorities to support institutional decision-making and strategic development. Additionally, Unit 04B supervises Liaison Office activities to advance and promote the European Parliament's essential functions and institutional visibility among diverse stakeholder audiences including citizens, opinion multipliers, young people, and the broader general public, thereby ensuring effective institutional representation at the Member State level.

---

**
04B.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Strategic Coordination and Institutional Alignment** (23%): Ensure consistency of all Liaison Office activities with European Parliament priorities and general management communication strategy; actively oversee synergy mechanisms between offices; manage reform and simplification processes within management structures; establish organizational coherence across the decentralized network.

• **Directorate Management and Inter-Institutional Relations** (18%): Exercise strategic direction, coordination, and management of all Liaison Offices Directorate units; maintain collaborative relations with other DG COMM directorates and general directorates; facilitate cross-directorate communication, operational coordination, and institutional information flows; coordinate laterally with sibling units within the communication framework.

• **Financial Governance and Budget Execution** (18%): Execute principal subdelegated authorizing officer functions; supervise comprehensive budget execution through the Coordination Unit; maintain financial accountability mechanisms; ensure fiscal compliance and resource allocation optimization.

• **Human Resources and Administrative Operations** (15%): Manage human resources portfolio for 31 units and approximately 280 personnel across 35 Member State duty stations; oversee personnel administration, duty station logistics, and administrative procedures; supervise administrative matters specific to Liaison Office operations; ensure personnel and administrative compliance.

• **Strategic Projects and Institutional Advocacy** (15%): Lead management initiatives and cooperate on major institutional projects including Europa Experiences; produce analytical documentation and strategic action proposals directed to institutional hierarchy and political authorities; support institutional decision-making processes and strategic institutional development.

• **Stakeholder Engagement and Public Functions Promotion** (11%): Supervise Liaison Office activities promoting essential European Parliament functions to diverse audiences including citizens, opinion multipliers, young people, and the general public; enhance institutional presence, visibility, and communication effectiveness; ensure effective institutional representation at Member State level.

04B20.refined_description: Unit 04B20 operates as the specialized Financial and Operational Support Centre within Direction 04B of DG COMM, serving as the administrative and financial infrastructure backbone for the European Parliament's Liaison Offices Directorate and its citizen engagement initiatives. The unit fulfills three interconnected functional responsibilities that collectively constitute 100% of its documented workload, ensuring the financial sustainability, operational continuity, and regulatory compliance of the Parliament's decentralized presence across Member States. These activities encompass comprehensive financial and operational support delivery (45% allocation), strategic budget stewardship and financial programming management aligned with organizational frameworks (30% allocation), and the establishment and continuous refinement of the institutional financial architecture governing all Liaison Offices and Europa Experiences operations (25% allocation). Through these integrated functions—spanning financial initiation procedures, subsidy and programme monitoring, transaction-level controls, helpdesk services, budget coordination, and procedural governance—Unit 04B20 ensures robust financial controls, strategic budget alignment, and operational consistency across the Parliament's network of member state liaison offices, the Strasbourg office, and citizen engagement initiatives including Europa Experiences and the Euroscola programme.
04B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Initiation, Operational Support, and Transaction Management (45%)**: Comprehensive financial and operational support for unit operations; financial initiation procedures for subsidies; Euroscola programme administration and monitoring; dedicated financial support provisioning for all Liaison Offices and Europa Experiences initiatives; transaction-level controls and first-line helpdesk services to stakeholders.

• **Budget and Financial Programming Management (30%)**: Strategic budget allocation and financial programming for Liaison Offices and Europa Experiences activities; coordination with Liaison Offices Strategic Support unit to align budgetary allocations with established strategic programming frameworks; financial planning and stewardship across the citizen engagement portfolio.

• **Financial Governance and Procedural Architecture Development (25%)**: Definition, implementation, and continuous updating of financial procedures and rules specific to Liaison Offices and Europa Experiences (including the Strasbourg office); design and refinement of financial circuits and operational workflows; establishment and maintenance of institutional financial standards and regulatory compliance frameworks.

Implement the new concept transforming Europa Experiences into versatile spaces for dynamic participation, by strengthening aspects linked to the 'core business' of the EP and Members.
Coordinate and support the communications activities of the Liaison Offices. Define the management strategy for the following year, ensuring its strategic alignment with the legislative agenda.
Coordinate the activities of the Offices on traditional campaigns and prizes awarded by Parliament (Europe Day, Sakharov Prize, LUX Prize).
Facilitate interinstitutional cooperation, in particular with DG COMM of the European Commission. Promote joint planning, visibility and common narratives.
04B21.refined_description:
Unit 04B21 operates as the strategic coordination and implementation hub within Direction 04B of DG COMM, functioning as the primary interface between the European Parliament's central communications strategy and its distributed network of Liaison Offices across EU Member States. As a second-level organizational unit within the Liaison Offices Directorate, 04B21 orchestrates comprehensive citizen engagement initiatives, institutional communications activities, and management representation across the Parliament's decentralized engagement ecosystem while ensuring coherent strategic messaging and operational alignment with legislative priorities. The unit's mission encompasses seven interconnected operational domains spanning citizen engagement project development, communications campaign management and editorial coordination, patronage request administration, institutional space transformation, Liaison Office communications coordination, institutional campaign and prize management, and interinstitutional cooperation frameworks. Through these activities, Unit 04B21 serves as a critical bridge ensuring consistent strategic coherence, operational flexibility, and institutional visibility across the Parliament's entire communications infrastructure while maintaining alignment with core legislative business and Member-focused priorities. The unit's portfolio positions it as an essential coordination mechanism that balances central strategic direction with the operational autonomy required for effective distributed communications management across the institution's decentralized network.

##
04B21.refined_activity_summary:

Ensure the launch and public visibility of the Europa Experience Athens site while taking advantage of EC-EP interinstitutional cooperation.

04B22.refined_description:
**

Unit 04B22 functions as the European Parliament's Liaison Office in Greece, operating as a specialized second-level organizational unit within Directorate 04B (Liaison Offices Direction) under DG COMM, with no subordinate structures but coordinating strategically among 30 sibling liaison office units across Europe. The office executes a comprehensive mandate centered on amplifying the European Parliament's political presence, institutional messaging, and stakeholder engagement within Greece through operations across two physical locations in Athens (the primary Liaison Office and the Europa Experience Athens site). The unit's primary mission encompasses dual-track strategic communications activities: (1) maintaining and strengthening thematic campaign implementation at national and regional levels through systematic activation of opinion leader networks and facilitation of dialogue with opinion multipliers aligned with Strategic European Foresight (SEF) guidelines, and (2) providing strategic media engagement by disseminating the EP's institutional positions while actively stimulating public debate on legislative priorities and European integration matters, thereby increasing substantive media coverage of parliamentary proceedings and legislative topics. Complementary operational functions include providing comprehensive logistical and administrative support to Members of Parliament and parliamentary committee delegations conducting official activities in Greece; maintaining systematic monitoring and reporting of Greek press coverage, political debate content, and public perception of EP activities to the General Secretariat, senior DG leadership, and political bodies; ensuring the successful launch and sustained public visibility of the Europa Experience Athens site through active exploitation of EC-EP interinstitutional cooperation frameworks; and maintaining strategic contacts with national and regional public authorities (including the Hellenic Parliament), third-party organizations, and civil society actors. Day-to-day administrative, financial, and logistical management operations support all activities across both office locations.

---

**
04B22.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Strategic Communications & Campaign Implementation** (60%): Maintenance and strengthening of thematic campaigns at national and regional levels through activation of opinion leader networks and facilitation of dialogue with opinion multipliers in accordance with SEF guidelines (30%); provision of strategic media information on EP institutional positions while stimulating substantive debate on political priorities and European integration initiatives to increase coverage of legislative activities and plenary proceedings (30%)

• **Operational Support & Member Services** (10%): Provision of comprehensive logistical and administrative assistance to Members of the European Parliament and delegations from parliamentary committees conducting official activities within Greece

• **Intelligence & Institutional Reporting** (10%): Systematic monitoring and reporting of Greek press content, political debate tenor, and public perception of EP activities to General Secretariat services, DG leadership, and political bodies

• **Institutional Visibility & Stakeholder Relations** (10%): Ensuring successful launch and sustained public visibility of the Europa Experience Athens site through EC-EP interinstitutional cooperation frameworks (5%); maintenance of strategic contacts with national and regional public authorities (including the Hellenic Parliament), third-party organizations, and civil society actors (5%)

• **Administrative & Financial Management** (5%): Day-to-day administrative, financial, and logistical management operations across both office locations (primary Liaison Office and Europa Experience Athens site)

04B23.refined_description: Unit 04B23 operates as a specialized operational unit within the Liaison Offices Directorate (04B) of DG COMM, functioning as a critical multifunctional nexus for European Parliament engagement, institutional coordination, and communications management at the Member State level. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses seven integrated operational areas that collectively represent 100% of documented workload, strategically balancing political intelligence gathering, stakeholder engagement, digital communications, and administrative governance. The unit maintains systematic political reporting and monitoring mechanisms while cultivating and sustaining strategic relationships with national authorities, governmental and non-governmental organizations, and inter-institutional partners, positioning itself as a diplomatic hub for institutional engagement (20% of capacity). Complementing this function, the unit dedicates equivalent resources (20%) to strategic stakeholder engagement through civil society organizations, the together.eu initiative, and targeted content creator networks, with particular emphasis on youth-oriented programming including EPAS and Euroscola initiatives that serve as multiplier mechanisms for European Parliament outreach. The unit maintains a dual digital and communications mandate (20%), encompassing comprehensive social media platform management and administration alongside systematic monitoring of online disinformation threats, thereby protecting institutional integrity and public discourse quality in the digital environment. Administrative and operational sustainability represents a fourth major functional pillar (20%), encompassing financial management and budget administration, security protocols and compliance frameworks, buildings and facilities management, institutional reporting obligations, and data protection regulatory compliance. Secondary operational responsibilities include managing the local Europa Experience visitor engagement facility (10%), providing targeted support to the President, rapporteurs, and official parliamentary delegations conducting Member State activities (5%), and maintaining proactive media relations and press engagement functions (5%). Collectively, these seven integrated activity streams position Unit 04B23 as a comprehensive operational platform that simultaneously advances political engagement, public communication, stakeholder relations, and institutional administration within the European Parliament's decentralized liaison office network.

Through these integrated activities, Unit 04B26 maintains the European Parliament's institutional presence, facilitates responsive communication with Irish audiences, and ensures operational continuity for parliamentary members and delegations conducting official missions to Ireland.

The unit serves as a strategic institutional presence advancing European parliamentary engagement and public discourse through a multifaceted operational framework encompassing MEP engagement, public communications, exhibition management, stakeholder relations, and administrative functions. The unit maintains comprehensive operational stewardship of the 'Europa Experience' exhibition facility in Vienna, managing all associated dimensions including day-to-day operational logistics, personnel administration, marketing strategy, and coordination, while contributing to the management and maintenance of the House of Europe infrastructure and collaborating with the European Commission on EMAS (Environmental Management and Audit Scheme) implementation. Additionally, Unit 04B38 executes critical administrative, financial, and operational functions supporting its strategic objectives, provides essential logistical coordination for Members of the European Parliament and official parliamentary delegations conducting activities within Austria, and maintains systematic intelligence-gathering and reporting functions to keep senior leadership—including the European Parliament's President, Secretary-General, Directorate General for Communications, and Management structures—informed of significant political developments and debates within the Austrian Member State.

Unit 04B41 functions as a specialized operational hub within the Liaison Offices Directorate (04B) of the European Parliament's Communications Directorate General (DG COMM), serving as a critical institutional bridge between the Parliament's central decision-making structures and diverse national stakeholder ecosystems at the Member State level. Operating as a second-level organizational unit with documented workload totaling 100%, 04B41 executes an integrated portfolio of seven functional activity streams encompassing strategic communications, stakeholder engagement, political intelligence, and administrative stewardship. The unit prioritizes strategic engagement with civil society multipliers, content creators, and youth-oriented initiatives (EPAS, Euroscola, Charlemagne Youth Prize, European Youth Event, and together.eu networks) representing 32% of operational capacity, while simultaneously maintaining high-level political support functions for the President, Secretary General, rapporteurs, and MEPs through logistical coordination and Member State political intelligence reporting (17% of workload). Complementing these core functions, 04B41 sustains regular political monitoring and inter-institutional relations management with national authorities and governmental/non-governmental stakeholders (15% of workload), operates the local Europa Experience visitor engagement center (10% of workload), manages critical administrative, financial, security, and facilities stewardship functions including co-ownership arrangements and data protection compliance (10% of workload), and executes media relations and strategic communications outreach to national and regional media outlets including media grants administration (8% of workload), alongside digital communications management encompassing social media operations and online disinformation monitoring (8% of workload). Collectively, these integrated activity streams position 04B41 as an essential institutional presence ensuring sustained political engagement, strategic communication, and European Parliament representation within Member State contexts.

**

04B50.refined_description: Unit 04B50 operates as a specialized Liaison Office within the European Parliament's Directorate for Liaison Offices (Unit 04B), functioning under the DG COMM organizational structure as a Member State-focused communication and engagement hub dedicated to advancing European parliamentary democracy and institutional representation at the national level. The unit's mandate encompasses eight core activities totaling 100% of documented workload, collectively positioning it as a strategically integrated bridge between European institutional priorities and national constituencies. Its democratic engagement and stakeholder relations functions (40%) encompass promoting participatory democracy through facilitation of substantive debate on EU-level policy issues and stakeholder dialogue with national and local authorities and civil society organizations (20%), alongside building civic engagement and youth participation through awareness-raising initiatives and community mobilization via the together.eu platform to cultivate citizen multipliers in both digital and physical engagement spaces (20%). Unit 04B50 manages the European Parliament's public-facing presence through strategic social media operations and mass media relations activities (20%), while simultaneously delivering critical institutional and operational support functions: providing political intelligence to senior leadership through regular briefings to the Secretary General and Director General regarding national political developments and parliamentary discourse (10%); delivering specialized operational support to Members of the European Parliament and parliamentary delegations conducting in-country activities (10%); coordinating and executing European Parliament communication campaigns at the national level (10%); fostering inter-institutional cooperation frameworks particularly with European Commission representations (5%); and ensuring sound administrative and financial stewardship of unit resources (5%). Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate organizational structures, Unit 04B50 contributes directly to the broader communications and liaison objectives of the parent Direction 04B while supporting both intra-parliamentary operations and broader democratic participation objectives.
04B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Democratic Engagement & Stakeholder Relations** (40%): Promote participatory democracy through facilitation of substantive debate on EU-level policy issues and stakeholder dialogue with national and local authorities and civil society organizations (20%); Build civic engagement and youth participation through awareness-raising initiatives and community mobilization via the together.eu platform to cultivate citizen multipliers in both digital and physical engagement spaces (20%)

• **Media & Communications Management** (20%): Manage the European Parliament's public-facing presence through strategic social media operations and mass media relations activities

• **Political Intelligence & Parliamentary Support** (20%): Provide political intelligence to senior leadership through regular briefings to the Secretary General and Director General regarding national political developments and parliamentary discourse (10%); Deliver specialized operational support to Members of the European Parliament and parliamentary delegations conducting in-country activities (10%)

• **Inter-Institutional Cooperation & Administrative Governance** (10%): Foster inter-institutional cooperation frameworks particularly with European Commission representations (5%); Ensure sound administrative and financial stewardship of unit resources (5%)

04B51.name: 04B51
original_activities: Ensure regular political reporting and monitoring, develop and maintain relations with national authorities, stakeholders, governmental and non-governmental organisations and foster inter-institutional collaboration (including with the European Commission Representation).
Ensure strategic engagement with multipliers (civil society organisations, together.eu, content creators, etc.) and specific target audiences notably youth (EPAS, Euroscola).
Provide support to the President, rapporteurs and official parliamentary delegations in the Member States.
Ensure administrative and financial management, security and buildings management, reporting and data protection.
Ensure media relations through contacts and media appearances by the Head of EPLO.
Ensure social media management and monitor disinformation online.
Manage the local Europa Experience.
04B51.refined_description:
Unit 04B51 operates as a specialized European Parliament Liaison Office (EPLO) positioned at the second hierarchical level within Directorate 04B of DG COMM's Liaison Offices framework, functioning as a comprehensive institutional bridge between the European Parliament and its stakeholder ecosystem at the designated Member State level. The unit's integrated mission encompasses seven distinct activity streams totalling 100% documented workload allocation: political engagement and institutional representation (22%), strategic stakeholder and youth engagement (18%), direct parliamentary support services (15%), administrative and operational management (15%), communications and digital presence (20%), and visitor experience management (10%). These activities collectively enable the unit to execute regular political reporting and monitoring, develop and maintain strategic relations with national authorities, governmental and non-governmental organisations, foster inter-institutional collaboration mechanisms including direct coordination with the European Commission Representation, engage civil society multipliers and content creators through together.eu networks, deliver youth-focused programming via EPAS and Euroscola initiatives, provide direct support to the European Parliament President, designated rapporteurs, and official parliamentary delegations conducting Member State activities, manage security protocols, buildings administration, institutional reporting requirements, and data protection compliance frameworks, execute media relations through the Head of EPLO's direct engagement and public appearances, manage social media operations and online disinformation monitoring activities, and operate the local Europa Experience facility as a public engagement and institutional visibility platform. This integrated approach ensures simultaneous delivery of both internal parliamentary operations support and external communication objectives, positioning Unit 04B51 as a multifunctional institutional presence capable of addressing complex political, administrative, and communicative requirements at the national level.

##
04B51.refined_activity_summary:
• **Political Engagement & Institutional Relations** (22%): Regular political reporting and monitoring; development and maintenance of strategic relations with national authorities, governmental and non-governmental organisations; inter-institutional collaboration mechanisms including direct coordination with European Commission Representation

• **Strategic Stakeholder & Youth Engagement** (18%): Strategic engagement with civil society multipliers, together.eu networks, and content creators; targeted audience outreach programmes; youth-focused initiatives (EPAS, Euroscola)

• **Parliamentary Support Services** (15%): Direct support provision to the European Parliament President, designated rapporteurs, and official parliamentary delegations conducting Member State activities

• **Administrative, Compliance & Operational Management** (15%): Administrative and financial management; security protocols and buildings administration; institutional reporting requirements; data protection compliance frameworks

• **Media Relations & Public Appearances** (10%): Head of EPLO media contacts and public engagement; institutional visibility and stakeholder communication through direct media interaction

• **Social Media Management & Disinformation Monitoring** (10%): Social media operations and content management; online disinformation monitoring and counter-narrative activities

• **Visitor Experience & Facility Management** (10%): Local Europa Experience facility management and operations; public engagement and institutional visibility platform administration

04B55.name: 04B55
original_activities: Implement the reinforced visitor reception strategy in Strasbourg (management of visitor flows, guided tours, reservations, non-sponsored MEP groups). Ensure the day-to-day management of the Simone Veil Parlamentarium, role play and other facilities.
Manage the Euroscola program.
Organize the Ground Game (ensemble.eu) and other missions and contacts at local level: schools, universities, cross-border tourism authorities, youth associations, European networks.
Ensure, in cooperation with EPLO Paris, continuous communication on social networks in three languages ​​(visits, exhibitions, Euroscola, cultural events, the work of the EP, ensemble.eu events).
Ensure marketing and press contacts.
Fulfill the function of a regional office ensuring liaison with local and regional authorities, international organizations based in Strasbourg and diplomatic bodies.
Participate in cross-functional activities (Europe Day, European Youth Event, Heritage Days).
Prepare and monitor public procurement and manage contracts, including reception staff employed in Strasbourg.
04B55.refined_description: Unit 04B55 functions as the operational hub for the European Parliament's Strasbourg Liaison Office, serving as a specialized institutional unit within Direction 04B of the DG COMM Liaison Offices Directorate. The unit's integrated mandate encompasses comprehensive visitor engagement, educational outreach, democratic communication, and local institutional representation across the Strasbourg seat of the European Parliament. Unit 04B55 implements a reinforced visitor reception strategy encompassing visitor flow management, guided tour coordination, reservation system administration, non-sponsored MEP group facilitation, and day-to-day operational management of the Simone Veil Parlamentarium and associated role-play educational facilities. The unit administers the Euroscola program as a flagship educational initiative connecting young Europeans with parliamentary institutions, while orchestrating the Ground Game initiative through the ensemble.eu platform to establish direct engagement channels with local stakeholders including schools, universities, cross-border tourism authorities, youth associations, and European networks. In close cooperation with EPLO Paris, Unit 04B55 executes continuous multilingual social media communication across three languages promoting institutional activities, exhibitions, Euroscola programming, cultural events, and parliamentary work, while managing marketing and press relations to enhance institutional visibility. The unit additionally fulfills regional office liaison functions with local and regional authorities, international organizations, and diplomatic bodies based in Strasbourg, participates in cross-functional institutional events including Europe Day, the European Youth Event, and Heritage Days, and manages all public procurement processes and employment contracts for Strasbourg-based reception personnel. These integrated activities collectively position Unit 04B55 as a critical interface between the European Parliament's institutional mission and local communities, educational institutions, and diplomatic stakeholders in the Strasbourg region.
04B55.refined_activity_summary:
• **Visitor Reception and Facility Management** (30%): Implementation and day-to-day operational management of the reinforced visitor reception strategy in Strasbourg, encompassing comprehensive visitor flow management, guided tour coordination, reservation system administration, non-sponsored MEP group facilitation, and operational management of the Simone Veil Parlamentarium and role-play educational facilities.

• **Educational Outreach and Youth Engagement Programs** (35%): Administration of the Euroscola program as a flagship educational initiative (20%); coordination and orchestration of the Ground Game initiative through the ensemble.eu platform (15%) with structured local-level engagement partnerships involving schools, universities, cross-border tourism authorities, youth associations, and European networks.

• **Communications, Public Relations, and External Liaison** (20%): Continuous multilingual social media communication in three languages in cooperation with EPLO Paris (10%) covering visits, exhibitions, Euroscola activities, cultural events, and parliamentary work; marketing and press relations management (10%); and regional office liaison functions with local and regional authorities, international organizations, and diplomatic bodies based in Strasbourg (5%).

• **Institutional Events and Administrative Compliance** (10%): Participation in cross-functional institutional activities including Europe Day, the European Youth Event, and Heritage Days (5%); public procurement process management and employment contract administration for Strasbourg-based reception personnel (5%).

04B60.name: 04B60
original_activities: Manage the human resources of the management and the EPLOs, in collaboration with the Personnel unit of the general management: manage the organization charts, define needs and profiles, manage the selection procedures, the mobility exercise, the rating procedure.

Manage the management's missions: establish the 3L mission program and manage the organization charts, manage the H3L missions and the associated budget, ensure the approval of exemptions and mission costs.
Advise and assist the Director and heads of EPLOs on any question relating to human resources, more particularly the management of specific, problematic or conflictual situations.
Define training needs and the development of learning paths for Liaison Office staff in collaboration with the General Directorate Personnel unit.
Manage leave files of all types, teleworking, overtime/extratime, etc.
Coordinate IT issues common to all Liaison Offices and be the liaison point for the IT unit of the General Directorate and DG ITEC.

Be the point of contact for the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics and the General Directorate of Security and Protection for any questions concerning the real estate policy of the Liaison Offices.
04B60.refined_description: Unit 04B60 functions as the integrated Human Resources and Administrative Operations Management hub for the European Parliament's Liaison Offices Directorate (04B), operating within the Communications Directorate General (DG COMM). The unit serves as the essential operational and human resources backbone supporting the Liaison Offices Directorate's mission delivery across the European Parliament's external representation network, providing comprehensive human resources stewardship, administrative coordination, and operational support services to management structures and European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) across all external locations. The unit's core mandate encompasses the complete lifecycle of human resources management for management personnel and EPLO staff (40% of workload), executed in close collaboration with the Personnel unit of the General Directorate, including organizational chart administration and maintenance, workforce needs assessment and professional profile definition, selection procedure management, mobility exercise coordination, and performance rating procedures. A major complementary function (25% of workload) involves comprehensive mission program administration, encompassing the establishment and administration of the 3-level (3L) mission program with associated organizational chart management, the oversight of H3L missions and their budgetary allocations, and the approval of mission exemptions and associated cost authorizations. The unit provides strategic human resources advisory and conflict resolution services (15% of workload) to the Director and heads of EPLOs regarding complex, problematic, or conflictual personnel situations. Supporting these primary functions, Unit 04B60 coordinates four specialized administrative operations (20% of workload): training needs analysis and learning pathway development for Liaison Office staff in partnership with General Directorate Personnel (5%); comprehensive leave file administration encompassing all leave categories, teleworking arrangements, and overtime/extratime management (5%); coordination of information technology issues common to all Liaison Offices and liaison responsibilities with both the General Directorate IT unit and DG ITEC (5%); and serving as the primary institutional contact point for the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics and the General Directorate of Security and Protection regarding Liaison Office real estate policy matters (5%).
04B60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Management and Personnel Administration** (40%): Comprehensive management of human resources for management and EPLOs in close collaboration with the General Directorate Personnel unit; administer and maintain organizational charts; conduct workforce needs assessment and professional profile definition; manage selection procedures; coordinate mobility exercises; oversee performance rating procedures

• **Mission Program Administration and Budget Oversight** (25%): Establish and manage the 3-level (3L) mission program with associated organizational chart administration; oversee H3L missions and corresponding budgetary allocations; ensure approval mechanisms for mission exemptions and cost authorizations

• **Strategic HR Advisory and Conflict Resolution Services** (15%): Provide strategic advisory and assistance capacity to the Director and heads of EPLOs on comprehensive human resources matters; support management of specific, problematic, and conflictual personnel situations

• **Staff Development and Operational Leave Management** (5%): Define training needs and develop learning pathways for Liaison Office staff in coordinated partnership with General Directorate Personnel unit; manage all categories of leave files, teleworking arrangements, and overtime/extratime documentation

• **Information Technology Coordination** (5%): Coordinate information technology issues common to all Liaison Offices; serve as liaison point between Liaison Offices, General Directorate IT unit, and DG ITEC

• **Infrastructure and Real Estate Policy Liaison** (5%): Function as primary institutional contact point with General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics and General Directorate of Security and Protection regarding Liaison Office real estate policy matters

04C.name: 04C
original_activities: Supervise and coordinate projects within the framework of the visitors strategy action plan, in particular those involving several departments of the Directorate, the General Directorate and the General Secretariat (House of European History, Europa Experiences, Info Hub, individual and group visits).
Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units of Directorate C (including human resources issues and training, programming, monitoring of public contracts and budgetary affairs).

Ensure the coordination of the units of Directorate C in the area of ​​reporting (annual work program, activity report, collection of quantitative and qualitative performance indicators, risk management, etc.).
Perform the functions of principal subdelegate authorizing officer.
04C.refined_description: Unit 04C functions as the strategic Direction within Directorate General 04, serving as the primary coordination and governance hub for the European Parliament's visitor engagement and institutional communication initiatives. The unit exercises comprehensive supervisory and directional authority over seven subordinate operational units (04C0010, 04C10, 04C20, 04C30, 04C40, 04C50, and one additional unit), coordinating their activities while simultaneously managing cross-departmental visitor strategy projects through the visitors strategy action plan framework in collaboration with multiple departments across Directorate C, the General Directorate, and the General Secretariat. Unit 04C provides essential executive direction, coordination, and animation for all Directorate C units, managing critical human resources functions including personnel administration, professional development, staff training initiatives, public contract monitoring, and budgetary administration. The unit ensures systematic coordination of institutional reporting mechanisms across Directorate C, encompassing annual work program development, activity reporting, quantitative and qualitative performance indicator collection, and risk management framework implementation. Additionally, Unit 04C carries financial and administrative accountability through its designation as principal subdelegate authorizing officer, providing fiduciary oversight and financial signature authority for institutional transactions. Through these integrated functions, Unit 04C ensures coherent strategic execution, administrative governance, and institutional accountability across Directorate C's operational portfolio while maintaining alignment with broader European Parliament institutional priorities.
04C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Visitor Strategy Coordination and Cross-Institutional Collaboration** (40%): Supervise and coordinate comprehensive visitor engagement initiatives within the visitors strategy action plan framework, including the House of European History, Europa Experiences, Info Hub, and individual and group visit programs, with explicit coordination across multiple Directorate C departments, General Directorate divisions, and General Secretariat entities to ensure integrated institutional delivery

• **Directorate C Executive Management and Administrative Governance** (35%): Exercise directional leadership, coordination, and animation functions for all seven subordinate Directorate C units, encompassing human resources administration and professional development, staff training program delivery, public procurement contract monitoring and oversight, and budgetary affairs management

• **Institutional Reporting, Performance Management, and Risk Governance** (15%): Coordinate comprehensive reporting mechanisms across Directorate C units including annual work program development, activity report preparation, systematic collection of quantitative and qualitative performance indicators, and risk management framework implementation to ensure institutional accountability and evidence-based performance monitoring

• **Financial Authorization and Delegated Authority Functions** (10%): Exercise principal subdelegate authorizing officer responsibilities with financial signature authority, maintaining fiduciary oversight of budgetary execution and administrative compliance within delegated institutional parameters

04C0010.name: 04C0010
original_activities: Assist, advise and guide, in order to respect the principle of good financial management, the Director, the Management and all subdelegated authorizing officers of the Visitors' Department in the financial and budgetary area: budgetary programming, financial implementation / initiation, management and monitoring of budgetary consumption, public procurement procedures.
Define and manage the different implementation methods in terms, in particular, of instructions and advice to operational colleagues.
Collaborate with (financial) colleagues in DG COMM Directorates and other Directorates-General.
Manage the human resources of the Service.
04C0010.refined_description: Unit 04C0010 functions as the Financial Management and Advisory Service within Direction 04C of the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication, serving as the primary fiduciary and budgetary governance hub for the Visitors' Department. Operating as a leaf-node unit within the Direction 04C hierarchy alongside six sibling units (04C10, 04C20, 04C30, 04C40, 04C50, and one additional unit), the unit's core institutional mission is to ensure rigorous adherence to the principle of good financial management across all financial and budgetary operations of the Visitors' Department. The unit provides comprehensive specialized assistance, advisory guidance, and institutional oversight to the Director, Management structures, and all subdelegated authorizing officers across the full spectrum of financial and budgetary functions—encompassing budgetary programming, financial implementation and initiation processes, active management and continuous monitoring of budgetary consumption, and administration of public procurement procedures in accordance with established governance frameworks. Complementing its primary fiduciary function, the unit develops and disseminates standardized implementation methodologies, instructions, and procedural guidance to operational colleagues within the Visitors' Department to ensure consistent application of financial procedures. The unit simultaneously maintains collaborative relationships with financial and administrative colleagues across multiple Directorates within DG COMM and other Directorates-General, thereby ensuring cross-institutional financial coherence and institutional knowledge-sharing. Additionally, the unit assumes responsibility for human resources management functions pertaining to its own service structure. Collectively, these functions support the broader strategic communication and institutional priorities of the European Parliament.
04C0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Advisory and Budgetary Governance** (75%): Provision of comprehensive specialized assistance, advice, and guidance to the Director, Management, and all subdelegated authorizing officers regarding budgetary programming, financial implementation and initiation, management and continuous monitoring of budgetary consumption, and public procurement procedure administration—ensuring compliance with good financial management principles and institutional fiduciary standards

• **Implementation Framework Development and Operational Guidance** (10%): Definition and active management of implementation methodologies through the development, dissemination, and delivery of standardized instructions and practical advisory materials to operational colleagues within the Visitors' Department to ensure consistent application of financial procedures

• **Inter-institutional Financial Collaboration and Coordination** (10%): Collaborative engagement with financial and administrative colleagues across DG COMM Directorates and other Directorates-General to promote financial coherence, ensure cross-institutional alignment, and facilitate institutional knowledge-sharing

• **Internal Human Resources Management** (5%): Management of personnel resources and administrative oversight of the Service unit's internal organizational structure

04C10.name: 04C10
original_activities: Manage the multi-year global renovation project of the Parlamentarium.
Manage the Parlamentarium and customer service on a daily basis, and ensure the maintenance of the Parlamentarium's IT and infrastructure.
Update the content/narration of the Parlamentarium multimedia stations.
04C10.refined_description: Unit 04C10 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 04C of the European Parliament's Directorate General for Communication, holding primary responsibility for comprehensive management and stewardship of the Parlamentarium facility as a flagship visitor experience and institutional communication platform. Operating as a leaf node without subordinate units while coordinating with sibling units 04C0010, 04C20, 04C30, 04C40, 04C50, and additional units under Direction 04C's governance framework, Unit 04C10 executes three interconnected functional areas that collectively advance the European Parliament's strategic objectives of public engagement and institutional transparency. The unit's dominant priority is strategic capital project management, encompassing the direction, oversight, and execution of a multi-year comprehensive global renovation project of the Parlamentarium facility, representing 60% of workload allocation and constituting the organization's primary resource commitment. Complementing this capital investment initiative, the unit manages daily operational functionality through comprehensive facility operations, customer service delivery, information technology system maintenance, and physical infrastructure stewardship, accounting for 35% of allocated resources and ensuring continuous visitor engagement and facility reliability. The unit further enhances the Parlamentarium's institutional messaging impact through periodic updating, revision, and curation of multimedia content and narration systems deployed throughout the facility's interactive stations, representing 5% of workload allocation and maintaining the accuracy and relevance of visitor educational experiences. These integrated activities position Unit 04C10 as a critical asset within Direction 04C's institutional communication mandate, ensuring the Parlamentarium operates as a modern, engaging facility while undergoing significant modernization.
04C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Capital Project Management** (60%): Direction, oversight, and execution of the multi-year comprehensive global renovation project of the Parlamentarium facility, representing the unit's primary organizational focus and dominant resource allocation priority

• **Operational Facility Management and Infrastructure Stewardship** (35%): Daily operational management of the Parlamentarium facility, comprehensive customer service delivery, information technology system maintenance, and physical infrastructure upkeep to ensure continuous facility functionality, visitor experience quality, and operational reliability

• **Content Curation and Multimedia Enhancement** (5%): Periodic updating, revision, and curation of multimedia station content and narration systems throughout the Parlamentarium's interactive stations to maintain institutional messaging accuracy, visitor engagement relevance, and educational impact

04C20.name: 04C20
original_activities: Schedule, organize and manage the reception of individual visitors and groups of visitors to the EP hemicycle in Brussels and Strasbourg. Organize combined sponsored tours. Manage functional boxes for linguistic sectors.

Prepare and deliver conferences on the role, skills and organization of the EP, adapted to the particularities of visiting groups (sponsored groups invited by MEPs, opinion multipliers or free groups) and individual visitors.

Ensure administrative, financial and budgetary management of financial files relating to groups of sponsored visitors as well as make payments in FMS. Improve booking, confirmation, verification and payment processes.
Promote the services of DG COMM to MEPs and accredited parliamentary assistants; contribute to horizontal projects, communication campaigns and communication events at EP level.
Contribute to the organization of Europe Day, help organize the EYE event (European Youth Event), Multilingualism Day and Heritage Days.
Participate in the implementation of a new system aimed at optimizing the flow of visitors to Brussels and Strasbourg during plenary sessions.
04C20.refined_description: Unit 04C20 functions as a specialized operational hub within Direction 04C of the Directorate General for Communication, serving as the European Parliament's primary institutional visitor engagement and experience management entity. Operating across the Parliament's principal legislative venues in Brussels and Strasbourg, the unit orchestrates comprehensive visitor access management, educational programming, financial stewardship, promotional engagement, and operational systems optimization to advance institutional communication and democratic engagement objectives. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 04C20 collaborates with sibling units (04C0010, 04C10, 04C30, 04C40, 04C50, and additional units) under Direction 04C's strategic coordination while functioning as a critical interface between the institution and its diverse public audiences. The unit's integrated activities encompass the complete visitor lifecycle—from initial scheduling and hemicycle access coordination through educational content delivery, financial file management, promotional outreach, and technological efficiency enhancement—positioning it as essential infrastructure for the Parliament's public-facing institutional communication strategy.
04C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Visitor Reception and Hemicycle Access Management** (50%): Schedule, organize, and manage reception of individual visitors and organized groups accessing the EP hemicycle in Brussels and Strasbourg; administer combined sponsored tours; manage functional boxes for linguistic sectors

• **Educational Outreach and Institutional Communications** (20%): Develop and deliver customized educational conferences articulating the Parliament's institutional role, competencies, organizational structure, and operational mechanisms, with content specifically tailored to diverse visitor demographics including MEP-invited delegations, opinion multiplier groups, and independent visitors

• **Financial and Administrative Stewardship** (15%): Execute comprehensive administrative, financial, and budgetary management of sponsored visitor group funding files; process payments through the Financial Management System (FMS); continuously refine and implement enhanced booking, confirmation, visitor verification, and payment processing workflows

• **Institutional Event Organization and Service Promotion** (10%): Promote DG COMM service offerings to Members of the European Parliament and accredited parliamentary assistants; contribute to horizontal communication initiatives, institutional-level communication campaigns, and Parliament-wide events; organize or support Europe Day, European Youth Event (EYE), Multilingualism Day, and Heritage Days programming

• **Visitor Flow Optimization Systems** (5%): Participate in the implementation of visitor flow optimization systems designed to enhance operational efficiency during plenary sessions across Brussels and Strasbourg venues

04C30.name: 04C30
original_activities: Design communication strategies and products (online and offline) for the promotion of the offer in 24 languages, including decentralized ones (Brussels, Strasbourg, Bazoches, Europa X in Member States); management of promotional items.

Monitor and implement IT projects: website in 24 languages, social media, online reservation systems for all visitor services including decentralized ones, data collection and management, data protection, etc.
Build and maintain partnerships: B2B and B2C events, international fairs, newsletters, relations with the visitor services of national parliaments in Europe and around the world.
Preparation of a new visitors strategy for the period 2024-2029.
Guarantee the continued development, application and promotion of the 'Digital journey to the European Parliament' project. 360 videos.
Organize open days (Europe Day) in Brussels and Strasbourg for the overall coordination of all services concerned and all participants.
Play a support and consultation role for management and other units, especially in the following areas: visitor strategy, communication and promotion, data protection, cross-functional IT projects.
04C30.refined_description:
Unit 04C30 functions as the strategic Visitors' Communication and Digital Engagement Hub within Direction 04C of the European Parliament's Directorate General 04, serving as the critical institutional interface for comprehensive visitor experience management and multilingual institutional promotion across all parliamentary venues (Brussels, Strasbourg, Bazoches, and decentralized Europa X locations in Member States). The unit's dual-pillar operational foundation allocates 35% of capacity to designing and implementing integrated multilingual communication strategies and promotional products across 24 languages, encompassing comprehensive management of promotional items and visitor-facing marketing materials for all centralized and decentralized facilities. An equivalent 35% of workload is dedicated to monitoring and operationally implementing advanced IT infrastructure projects, including the 24-language website platform, social media ecosystem management, online reservation systems for all visitor services across decentralized locations, integrated data collection and management frameworks, and comprehensive data protection compliance protocols.
