The unit strengthens institutional visibility and stakeholder relationships through strategic partnership development activities (10%), encompassing B2B and B2C event coordination, international fair participation, newsletter management, and collaborative engagement with visitor services across European and international national parliaments. Unit 04C30 contributes strategically to institutional priorities through four specialized initiatives distributed across 20% of remaining capacity: preparation of the comprehensive visitors strategy for 2024-2029 (5%); sustained development, technical implementation, and promotion of the innovative 'Digital Journey to the European Parliament' immersive 360-video project (5%); comprehensive coordination and execution of major institutional events including Europe Day celebrations in Brussels and Strasbourg with cross-functional service integration (5%); and provision of expert advisory and consultation services (5%) to management and peer units regarding visitor strategy, communication optimization, data protection compliance, and cross-functional IT project execution. As a leaf-node unit within Direction 04C governance, Unit 04C30 operates in coordination with sibling units (04C0010, 04C10, 04C20, 04C40, 04C50, and one additional unit) to ensure coherent institutional communication and visitor engagement across the Parliament's strategic priorities, maximizing operational efficiency through direct service delivery without subordinate organizational structures.

---

##
04C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Multilingual Communication Strategy and Content Development (35%)**: Design, development, and management of comprehensive communication strategies and promotional products in 24 languages (including decentralized language variants) across all parliamentary visitor locations (Brussels, Strasbourg, Bazoches, and Europa X Member State facilities); comprehensive management and coordination of promotional items and visitor-facing marketing materials.

• **Digital Infrastructure and IT Project Implementation (35%)**: Monitoring, oversight, and operational implementation of critical IT infrastructure projects including the 24-language website platform, social media ecosystem management, online reservation systems for all visitor services across centralized and decentralized locations, integrated data collection and management systems, and comprehensive data protection compliance protocols.

• **Strategic Partnership Development and External Relations (10%)**: Development and maintenance of strategic partnership networks through B2B and B2C event coordination, international fair participation, newsletter communications, and establishment of collaborative relationships with visitor services departments of national parliaments throughout Europe and internationally.

• **Strategic Planning and Digital Innovation (10%)**: Preparation and implementation of the comprehensive visitors' strategy framework for the 2024-2029 strategic period; sustained development, technical application, and institutional promotion of the 'Digital Journey to the European Parliament' immersive project featuring 360-degree video technology for enhanced remote and in-person visitor engagement.

• **Institutional Events Coordination and Cross-Functional Advisory Services (10%)**: Organization and coordination of major institutional events including Europe Day open days in Brussels and Strasbourg with comprehensive cross-service integration and participant coordination; provision of strategic consultation and expert support to management and peer units on visitor strategy, communications and promotion optimization, data protection compliance, and complex cross-functional IT project execution.

04C40.name: 04C40
original_activities: Finalize the installation of the Europa Experience in Zagreb, Athens, Budapest and prepare the installation in The Hague.
Implement the new concept for the Europa Experience with the modernization of existing spaces, interactive content and the adaptation of current projects.
Plan Europa Experience projects in Bratislava (2026), Sofia (2026) and Munich (2027).
Monitor prospecting and contractual negotiations for the 7 European capitals remaining to be equipped.

Support the existing Europa Experience network (Berlin, Strasbourg, Ljubljana, Helsinki, Copenhagen, Tallinn, Paris, Rome, Stockholm, Vienna, Warsaw, Luxembourg, Prague, Dublin, Zagreb, Budapest and Athens).
Coordinate the contributions of services and institutional partners, including the European Commission, involved in the planning and operational management of decentralized visitor services.
04C40.refined_description: Unit 04C40 functions as the integrated operational management and strategic development hub for the Europa Experience initiative within Directorate General 04's institutional communication framework, serving as the primary implementation center for this decentralized network of visitor engagement facilities across European capitals. The unit bears comprehensive responsibility for the complete lifecycle management of the Europa Experience network, encompassing the finalization of active installations in Zagreb, Athens, and Budapest while simultaneously preparing infrastructure establishment in The Hague (30% of workload); the implementation of comprehensive modernization initiatives that integrate new conceptual frameworks, interactive content systems, and strategic project adaptations across all existing facility spaces to meet evolving institutional and visitor engagement standards (25% of workload); the systematic planning and scheduling of geographic expansion through prospective installations in Bratislava and Sofia targeted for 2026 and Munich for 2027 (20% of workload); the active prospecting and contractual negotiation oversight for seven remaining European capitals designated for future equipment deployment and operational establishment (10% of workload); and the provision of continuous operational, technical, and performance support to the established seventeen-location Europa Experience network spanning Berlin, Strasbourg, Ljubljana, Helsinki, Copenhagen, Tallinn, Paris, Rome, Stockholm, Vienna, Warsaw, Luxembourg, Prague, Dublin, Zagreb, Budapest, and Athens (10% of workload). Additionally, Unit 04C40 coordinates cross-institutional contributions and manages strategic partnerships with European Parliament services and the European Commission to ensure coherent planning and integrated operational governance of these decentralized visitor engagement services (5% of workload). Through these integrated activities, the unit directly advances the European Parliament's institutional communication objectives and contributes substantially to Direction 04C's governance mandate while enhancing institutional visibility across European territory.
04C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Active Installation and Infrastructure Development** (30%): Finalization of Europa Experience installations in Zagreb, Athens, and Budapest; concurrent preparation of installation infrastructure in The Hague as the most resource-intensive operational priority

• **Facility Modernization and Content Enhancement** (25%): Implementation of enhanced conceptual frameworks through comprehensive modernization of existing facility spaces, development and integration of interactive content systems, and strategic adaptation of current projects to contemporary institutional and visitor engagement standards

• **Future Expansion Planning and Scheduling** (20%): Prospective planning and systematic project development for scheduled installations in Bratislava (2026), Sofia (2026), and Munich (2027), ensuring strategic geographic expansion across the European Union

• **Network Operations, Prospecting, and Contractual Development** (10%): Active prospecting activities and contractual negotiation monitoring for seven remaining European capitals; continuous operational support, technical oversight, and performance monitoring of the seventeen established Europa Experience centers ensuring sustained operational excellence and visitor service quality

• **Inter-institutional Coordination and Partnership Management** (5%): Coordination of institutional contributions and strategic partnership management with European Parliament services and the European Commission for integrated planning and operational governance of decentralized visitor engagement facilities

04C50.name: 04C50
original_activities: Be responsible for designing, managing, coordinating and delivering communications activities, including hosting public and stakeholder events, such as conferences, debates, book launches, film screenings, exhibitions and information sessions.
Help inform and mobilize support among visitors and event participants by presenting the political and legislative priorities of the European Union.
Ensure an advisory role to other services of the Parliament and MEPs - in their institutional capacity - who organize their own events at the Info Hub.
Organize activities to attract and engage civil society stakeholders, multipliers, partners and specialist interest groups.

Enrich Parliament's offering to visitors by bringing together content from across the Institution, in particular from DG EPRS and DG COMM, and by facilitating the exchange of skills between Parliament's services.
04C50.refined_description:
Unit 04C50 functions as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for strategic communications and public engagement, serving as a specialized component within Direction 04C of the Directorate General responsible for institutional communications. Operating as a leaf node without subordinate units while coordinating with sibling units (04C0010, 04C10, 04C20, 04C30, 04C40, and additional Direction 04C units), the unit holds comprehensive responsibility for designing, managing, coordinating, and delivering communications activities across the Parliament's information and visitor engagement infrastructure. The unit's mandate encompasses organizing and hosting diverse public and stakeholder events—including conferences, debates, book launches, film screenings, exhibitions, and information sessions—thereby establishing itself as the Parliament's premier venue for interactive institutional communication (45% of workload). A substantial portion of the unit's operations focuses on informing and mobilizing support among visitors and event participants by presenting and contextualizing the European Union's political and legislative priorities through audience engagement and political education initiatives (25% of workload). Unit 04C50 provides strategic advisory services and quality assurance to other Parliament services and Members of the European Parliament in their institutional capacity who independently organize events at the Info Hub facility, ensuring consistency and institutional standards across the Parliament's event ecosystem (10% of workload). The unit actively designs and executes targeted outreach activities to attract, engage, and build relationships with civil society stakeholders, multiplier organizations, strategic partners, and specialist interest groups, thereby expanding the Parliament's reach beyond traditional constituencies (10% of workload). Finally, Unit 04C50 enriches and diversifies the Parliament's visitor experience by synthesizing and integrating content from across institutional services—particularly leveraging resources from DG EPRS (European Parliamentary Research Service) and DG COMM (Directorate General for Communication)—while actively facilitating inter-service skill exchange initiatives and collaborative capacity-building that strengthen institutional knowledge sharing and enhance organizational capabilities (10% of workload).

##
04C50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Event Design, Management & Delivery** (45%): Primary responsibility for designing, managing, coordinating, and delivering comprehensive communications activities through diverse event formats including conferences, debates, book launches, film screenings, exhibitions, and information sessions; establishment of the Parliament's premier venue for interactive institutional communication

• **Audience Engagement & Political Education** (25%): Informing and mobilizing support among visitors and event participants by presenting and contextualizing the European Union's political and legislative priorities; building constituent awareness and support through educational engagement platforms

• **Advisory Services & Quality Assurance** (10%): Providing strategic advisory role to other Parliament services and Members of the European Parliament organizing their own events at the Info Hub facility; ensuring institutional consistency, quality control, and standards compliance across the Parliament's event ecosystem

• **Civil Society & Stakeholder Mobilization** (10%): Designing and executing targeted outreach activities to attract and actively engage civil society stakeholders, multiplier organizations, strategic partners, and specialist interest groups; expanding institutional reach beyond traditional constituencies

• **Cross-Institutional Content Integration & Capacity Building** (10%): Enriching and diversifying visitor offerings by aggregating and synthesizing content from DG EPRS and DG COMM; facilitating inter-service skill exchange initiatives and collaborative capacity-building that strengthen institutional knowledge sharing and enhance organizational capabilities

04C60.name: 04C60
original_activities: Prepare, produce and manage temporary and traveling exhibitions. Implement the MHE's digital strategy, including the five sub-projects (virtual tours, online collection, virtual events, digital platforms accompanying exhibitions, digital resources for teachers).
Provide services to visitors (including educational programs, guided tours, organization of events, management of group reservations).
Manage collections (including conservation, loan management, acquisition and transport of objects, database management).

Manage the permanent exhibition (including updates of exhibitions and tablets, supervision of 'reception agents' services, security, exhibition maintenance, building monitoring), as well as the digitalization of exhibitions.
Carry out communication actions (including MHE promotional activities, website management, social media, publications).
Prepare and participate in meetings of the MHE bodies (management board and scientific committee), networking, participation in conferences and presentations.
Manage budget, finances, contracts, inventory, human resources and administration, tenders, legal advice.
Manage the Maison Jean Monnet and its offer to visitors, including its exhibitions, educational offers and communication actions.
04C60.refined_description: Unit 04C60 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 04C of the European Parliament's Directorate General 04, functioning as the comprehensive management and programming hub for the Maison de l'Histoire de l'Europe (MHE) and its satellite venue, the Maison Jean Monnet. The unit serves as the institutional cornerstone connecting the European Parliament's heritage mission with contemporary digital engagement strategies and visitor-centered programming, operating under the strategic direction of its parent unit 04C while maintaining operational coordination with sibling units 04C0010, 04C10, 04C20, 04C30, and 04C40. Unit 04C60 orchestrates the institution's complete visitor experience and digital transformation agenda through five integrated sub-projects: virtual tours, online collection platforms, virtual events programming, digital platforms accompanying physical exhibitions, and digital educational resources for teachers. The unit's mandate encompasses comprehensive stewardship of exhibition development, visitor engagement services, collections management, permanent exhibition operations, institutional communications, governance participation, administrative and financial operations, and specialized management of satellite facilities. Collectively, these activities position Unit 04C60 as the operational cornerstone that synthesizes exhibition curation, digital innovation, visitor experience optimization, institutional memory preservation, and organizational administration into a cohesive institutional strategy.
04C60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Exhibition Development and Digital Transformation** (25%): Preparation, production, and management of temporary and traveling exhibitions; implementation of comprehensive MHE digital strategy encompassing virtual tours, online collection platforms, virtual events infrastructure, digital platforms accompanying physical exhibitions, and digital educational resources for teachers

• **Visitor Engagement and Educational Services** (20%): Provision of comprehensive visitor services including educational programming, guided tours, event organization, and group reservation management (15%); specialized management of Maison Jean Monnet satellite venue including its exhibition programming, educational offerings, and targeted communication activities (5%)

• **Collections and Facilities Management** (25%): Collections stewardship encompassing conservation protocols, loan administration, object acquisition and transportation logistics, and database management systems maintenance (15%); permanent exhibition stewardship including display updates, interactive tablet technology management, reception agent supervision, security oversight, infrastructure maintenance, and exhibition digitalization initiatives (10%)

• **Strategic Communications and Outreach** (10%): Strategic communication actions including MHE promotional activities, website governance, social media engagement, and publication development

• **Governance, Administration, and Financial Operations** (20%): Governance participation through preparation and facilitation of management board and scientific committee meetings, institutional networking, conference participation, and professional presentations (10%); comprehensive administrative and financial management encompassing budget administration, financial operations, contract procurement and oversight, inventory control, human resources coordination, tender management, and legal advisory services (10%)

04D.name: 04D
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and leadership of the four units of Directorate D in compliance with regulatory provisions and in a spirit of service to customers with a view to achieving the objectives of the general management.
Propose and implement strategies for efficient use of resources allocated by the budgetary authority to DG COMM, ensuring coordination of planning exercises with the responsible services.
Establish 'screening' type analyzes of resources (positions, contracts, external personnel), programs to maximize the capacity of general management, particularly through training.
Ensure relations with other entities of the general management and with other general directorates, in particular through participation in the group of Directors of Resources.

Ensure consistent application within the general management of regulations on the protection of personal data (advice and support to services and staff, coordination and monitoring of activities, training actions).

Provide support and legal advice to operational services or competent bodies, particularly in the following areas: access to documents, OLAF and other contentious files, coordination of public contracts and subsidies.
Analyze and propose actions to the hierarchy in particularly sensitive cases, linked for example to grading, mobility, cases of long-term illness/disability, etc.
Contribute to and ensure coherent implementation of Parliament's environmental policy (EMAS) within the DG.
04D.refined_description: Unit 04D functions as the strategic Direction within Directorate General Communications (DG COMM), exercising comprehensive executive leadership and operational coordination over four subordinate units (04D10, 04D20, 04D30, 04D40) while maintaining strict adherence to institutional regulatory frameworks and advancing the Secretary General's general management objectives. The unit serves as the primary operational and administrative hub for its constituent units, ensuring unified command structure and customer-service orientation aligned with institutional governance standards. Unit 04D simultaneously develops, proposes, and implements resource optimization strategies for budgetary allocations to DG COMM, coordinating all planning exercises with responsible services to ensure efficient deployment of institutional assets and financial stewardship. The unit conducts systematic screening-type resource analyses encompassing personnel positions, contractual arrangements, and external staffing configurations, implementing targeted capacity-building initiatives including staff training programs to maximize organizational effectiveness and workforce development. Unit 04D maintains formal inter-institutional relations with parallel entities across general management structures and participates actively within the Directors of Resources collegiate body, ensuring horizontal alignment throughout the European Parliament's administrative architecture. The unit provides comprehensive governance oversight of personal data protection regulations throughout the general management domain, delivering advisory support to services and staff while coordinating compliance monitoring activities and delivering mandatory training interventions. Additionally, Unit 04D delivers specialized legal counsel and administrative support to operational services and competent bodies in sensitive domains including document access procedures, OLAF investigations, contentious administrative files, and public procurement and subsidy coordination frameworks; analyzes and formulates hierarchical recommendations addressing sensitive personnel and organizational matters including grading determinations, staff mobility decisions, and cases involving long-term illness or disability considerations; and ensures coherent implementation and institutional embedding of the European Parliament's Environmental Management and Audit Scheme (EMAS) throughout the DG's operational structures.
04D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction and Unit Leadership** (30%): Ensures comprehensive direction, coordination, and leadership of four subordinate units (04D10, 04D20, 04D30, 04D40) in strict compliance with regulatory provisions and customer service standards, maintaining unified command structure and advancing general management objectives through systematic supervisory oversight.

• **Resource Management, Strategic Planning, and Organizational Capacity Building** (35%): Proposes and implements strategies for efficient utilization of budgetary allocations to DG COMM, coordinating planning exercises with responsible services to optimize institutional resource deployment (25%); conducts systematic screening-type analyses of personnel positions, contracts, and external staffing arrangements, implementing targeted training and capacity-maximization programs to enhance general management operational effectiveness (10%).

• **Institutional Governance, Compliance, and Inter-Institutional Coordination** (20%): Ensures consistent application of personal data protection regulations across general management domain through comprehensive advisory support, compliance monitoring, and mandatory training delivery (10%); maintains formal relations with parallel general management entities and peer directorates through active participation in Directors of Resources collegial body, ensuring horizontal alignment across Parliament's administrative architecture (10%).

• **Legal and Administrative Support Services** (5%): Provides specialized legal counsel and administrative guidance to operational services and competent bodies in critical areas including access to documents procedures, OLAF investigations, contentious administrative file management, and public procurement and subsidy coordination frameworks.

• **Sensitive Case Management and Environmental Stewardship** (10%): Analyzes and formulates hierarchical recommendations addressing sensitive personnel and organizational matters including grading determinations, staff mobility decisions, and cases involving long-term illness or disability considerations (5%); ensures coherent implementation and institutional embedding of the European Parliament's Environmental Management and Audit Scheme (EMAS) within DG operational structures (5%).

04D10.name: 04D10
original_activities: Ensure the planning, management and monitoring of positions in the organization chart and staff movements of the general management: selection procedures, job postings, interviews, recruitment, contract renewals of contractual (AC) and temporary (AT) agents.

Ensure the management and monitoring of specific and horizontal human resources files: specific projects, mobility exercise, harmonization of job descriptions, Photography of services, HR metrics, Annual Activity Report (AAR), Annual Work Program (AWP).

Define needs in the area of ​​learning and development, establish and implement the annual training plan, assist units in the implementation of planned actions, manage external training and the budget envelope.
Ensure the coordination, management, reception and integration of trainees, at headquarters and in the EP Liaison Offices, as well as agents newly recruited or who have joined DG COMM.

Ensure the management of mission orders (individual and Strasbourg organizational charts) and eMISS exemption procedures; planning, management and monitoring of the budget allocated to general management.
Ensure monitoring of the rating procedure: merit points, complaints and promotions.
Provide career advice and professional guidance (network of local career guidance officers (RELOP), exit interviews, Career Days, Career Opportunities Platform, etc.
Other missions (document management, data protection, general management's Confluence site, office supplies, etc.).
04D10.refined_description: Unit 04D10 operates as the integrated Human Resources and Administrative Management hub within Direction 04D of the Directorate General Communications, serving as the primary operational center for comprehensive personnel administration, organizational staffing coordination, and employee development services across the institution's general management structure. Functioning as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while coordinating with sibling units 04D20, 04D30, and 04D40, Unit 04D10 executes the complete lifecycle of human resources management spanning recruitment and selection through career development, performance monitoring, and employee exit management. The unit's operational mandate encompasses personnel recruitment and staffing coordination, comprehensive HR file administration and strategic documentation, employee development and training delivery, career management and professional guidance, budget and mission order administration, and ancillary administrative compliance functions. Unit 04D10 maintains direct responsibility for ensuring planning, management, and monitoring of organizational chart positions and staff movements; executing selection procedures, job postings, interviews, and recruitment processes for both contractual (AC) and temporary (AT) agents; managing specific and horizontal HR files including mobility exercises and job description harmonization; defining and implementing annual training and development plans; coordinating trainee and new agent reception and integration across headquarters and European Parliament Liaison Offices; managing individual and Strasbourg-based mission orders; monitoring rating procedures and performance metrics; providing career guidance through the RELOP network and Career Opportunities Platform; and administering document management, data protection compliance, and general management's Confluence site operations. Through these integrated activities, Unit 04D10 functions as an essential administrative and human capital management resource supporting operational effectiveness across Direction 04D and the broader Directorate General Communications organizational structure.
04D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Personnel Recruitment, Selection, and Organizational Staffing Management** (38%): Planning, management, and monitoring of organizational chart positions and staff movements; execution of selection procedures, job postings, interviews, and comprehensive recruitment processes; management of contract renewals for contractual (AC) and temporary (AT) agents; coordination with general management on staffing requirements and organizational structure adjustments

• **Human Resources File Administration and Strategic HR Documentation** (28%): Management and monitoring of specific and horizontal human resources files; execution and coordination of mobility exercises; harmonization and standardization of job descriptions; service assessment and photography initiatives; HR metrics compilation and analysis; preparation of statutory Annual Activity Reports (AAR) and Annual Work Programs (AWP); strategic HR documentation and reporting

• **Employee Development, Training, and Integration Services** (20%): Definition of learning and development needs; establishment and implementation of annual training plans; provision of implementation support and assistance to organizational units; external training procurement and budget envelope administration; coordination, reception, and integration of trainees at headquarters and European Parliament Liaison Offices; onboarding and integration support for newly recruited and transferred agents

• **Career Management, Professional Guidance, and Performance Monitoring** (5%): Provision of career advice and professional guidance through the network of local career guidance officers (RELOP); organization of exit interviews, Career Days programming, and Career Opportunities Platform management; monitoring of rating procedures, merit points assessment, complaint management, and promotion processes

• **Budget Administration, Mission Orders, and Ancillary Administrative Support** (9%): Management of mission orders for individual and Strasbourg-based organizational operations; administration of eMISS exemption procedures; planning, management, and monitoring of general management budget allocation and resource envelopes; document management and archival; data protection compliance administration; general management's Confluence site maintenance and operations; office supplies coordination; execution of miscellaneous administrative missions

04D20.name: 04D20
original_activities: Advise and guide operational units in the design and execution of calls for tenders. Manage legal matters, particularly matters relating to intellectual property rights. Represent the general management within the Public Procurement Forum of the Parliament.

Schedule calls for tenders from general management and monitor their implementation. Coordinate and monitor interinstitutional procedures. Ensure ex-ante evaluation of procurement and grant procedures.

Provide assistance in the design, drafting, evaluation and contractualization of grant programs managed by the operational units of the general management. Provide procedural and legal assistance for the implementation of grants.
Ensure responsibility for procedures for managing financial and legal documents and their archiving at general management level. Ensure the deployment of BRICKS in general management.
04D20.refined_description: Unit 04D20 functions as the specialized Procurement and Grants Management Office within Direction 04D of the Directorate General Communications, serving as the central administrative, legal, and governance hub for institutional procurement and financial assistance operations across the general management's operational portfolio. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinates, 04D20 collaborates strategically with sibling units 04D10, 04D30, and 04D40 under Direction 04D's coordination to provide essential cross-cutting support that enables compliant and efficient financial and procurement operations. The unit's integrated service delivery model ensures institutional responsibility for legal compliance, procedural integrity, and administrative efficiency across all procurement and grant-related activities, while simultaneously functioning as the primary interface between operational units and the Parliament's procurement governance frameworks. Through its four interconnected activity streams—each representing distinct but mutually reinforcing operational domains—Unit 04D20 establishes itself as the institutional authority for managing the complete lifecycle of procurement initiatives, grant programs, legal documentation, and information systems infrastructure at the general management level. This comprehensive positioning enables the unit to deliver seamless advisory guidance, procedural oversight, legal protection mechanisms, and administrative infrastructure that collectively support the European Parliament's communications operations.
04D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Procurement Advisory and Legal Governance** (30%): Providing comprehensive advisory and guidance services to operational units in tender design and execution; managing legal matters with particular emphasis on intellectual property rights protection; representing general management interests within the Parliament's Public Procurement Forum as the primary institutional interface for procurement governance

• **Tender Scheduling, Monitoring, and Interinstitutional Coordination** (25%): Scheduling and monitoring implementation of tender calls initiated by general management; coordinating and supervising interinstitutional procurement procedures; conducting mandatory ex-ante evaluations for all procurement and grant procedures to ensure institutional compliance and procedural integrity

• **Grant Program Management and Legal Support** (25%): Providing specialized assistance in the design, drafting, evaluation, and contractualization phases of grant programs administered by operational units; delivering comprehensive procedural and legal support services throughout grant implementation and execution cycles

• **Financial Documentation Management and Systems Infrastructure** (20%): Managing the complete lifecycle of financial and legal documentation at general management level, including archiving protocols and procedures; ensuring systematic deployment and operational integration of the BRICKS information system across general management divisions

04D30.name: 04D30
original_activities: Provide End User Support: Provide assistance and resolve technical issues encountered by users of IT systems, proactively communicate issues and solutions, troubleshoot hardware and software issues.
Manage infrastructure, IT assets and access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management.
Manage Projects: Planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality.
GBI: Effectively manage spaces in Brussels and Strasbourg. Process and track technical support requests for services. Assist services in monitoring their inventory.
Manage applications in use: lifecycle management of custom or specialized software applications in use, rolling maintenance, training and support, testing to ensure functionality and security.
Business Analyst: ensure project monitoring and facilitate communication and interaction with DG ITEC and other DGs during the execution of approved program projects.
Management consulting: advise units and management on IT aspects such as cybersecurity, framework contracts, innovative technologies and IT governance.
04D30.refined_description: Unit 04D30 is a specialized IT operations and support unit operating at the second hierarchical level within Direction 04D of the Directorate General Communications, functioning as a critical operational backbone for technology service delivery across the European Parliament's Brussels and Strasbourg locations. The unit operates under unified strategic direction within parent unit 04D while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units 04D10, 04D20, and 04D40, and serves dual roles as both an operational service provider and strategic partner to institutional leadership. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive end-user technical support, infrastructure management, IT project execution, and strategic IT governance advisory, with resources systematically allocated across seven distinct operational domains to ensure institutional IT service continuity and technological modernization. These activities enable the unit to deliver proactive technical assistance and issue resolution to Parliament users across multiple platforms; plan, provision, monitor and maintain IT infrastructure components including hardware, software, network systems, and user access controls; execute and supervise IT projects within defined constraints of scope, time, budget and quality standards; manage physical and technical spaces in both parliamentary locations through GBI facility management while processing technical support requests and monitoring organizational inventory; ensure lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications through maintenance, testing, security assurance, and user training; facilitate project monitoring and cross-directorate communication with DG ITEC and other organizational units; and provide management consulting on cybersecurity frameworks, framework contract optimization, emerging technology assessment, and institutional IT governance structures. Through this integrated portfolio of activities, Unit 04D30 directly supports operational continuity and institutional technology modernization objectives while maintaining coordinated engagement with the broader Communications Directorate ecosystem.
04D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **End-User Technical Support and Issue Resolution** (35%): Provision of comprehensive technical assistance including proactive system issue communication, hardware and software troubleshooting, and systematic solution delivery to Parliament users across multiple platforms and locations

• **Infrastructure and Asset Management** (15%): Planning, provisioning, monitoring and maintenance of IT infrastructure components including hardware, software, and network systems; comprehensive user access management protocols; IT asset planning and administration across the institution

• **IT Project and Program Management** (15%): Execution and supervision of technology initiatives within defined constraints of scope, time, budget and quality standards; project monitoring coordination; facilitation of institutional communication with DG ITEC and other Directorates General during approved program execution

• **Facility Management and Technical Operations** (15%): GBI-led management of physical and technical spaces in Brussels and Strasbourg locations; technical support request processing; organizational inventory monitoring and assistance; coordination of building infrastructure operations across parliamentary locations

• **Application Lifecycle Management** (10%): Lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications including maintenance, testing, security assurance, user training, and functionality assurance for operational applications in use

• **Business Analysis and Project Coordination** (5%): Facilitation of project monitoring and cross-directorate communication with DG ITEC and other organizational units; coordination of institutional communication during approved program execution

• **Strategic IT Advisory and Governance** (5%): Management consulting services addressing cybersecurity frameworks, framework contract optimization, emerging technology assessment, and institutional IT governance structures; advisory services to units and institutional leadership

04D40.name: 04D40
original_activities: Establish an effective and coherent way to measure the performance of all DG COMM activities through indicators assessing either customer satisfaction or the productivity of units and value chains.
Ensure budgetary planning, risk management and internal control, preparation of the annual activity report, monitoring of discharge and monitoring of audit reports.
Ensure ex-ante verification of all financial transactions (commitments, payments, recovery and regularization orders) in order to control their legality and regularity.
Ensure the management of financial systems; provide helpdesk support for accounting and financial systems; distribute invoices between services, operations and accounting reports.
04D40.refined_description: Unit 04D40 functions as the integrated Financial Management and Performance Monitoring unit within Direction 04D of the Directorate General for Communications, serving as the critical administrative and fiduciary control center for the entire DG COMM organizational structure. Operating as a leaf-node unit within Direction 04D and coordinating with sibling units 04D10, 04D20, and 04D30, Unit 04D40 executes four primary functional domains that collectively ensure institutional financial integrity, operational transparency, performance accountability, and evidence-based organizational management. The unit establishes and maintains a comprehensive, coherent performance measurement framework comprising quantitative indicators that assess customer satisfaction metrics and productivity benchmarks across all DG COMM activities, units, and value chains, thereby enabling continuous improvement and institutional accountability. Concurrently, the unit exercises preventive ex-ante financial verification over all monetary transactions—including commitments, payments, recovery orders, and regularization procedures—to systematically control the legality and regularity of financial operations before execution. The unit simultaneously oversees robust budgetary planning, risk management protocols, and internal control mechanisms while preparing annual activity reports, monitoring discharge procedures, and systematically tracking audit report implementation and recommendations to ensure compliance with European institutional governance standards. Additionally, Unit 04D40 manages financial systems infrastructure, provides specialized helpdesk support for accounting and financial platform users across the organization, coordinates invoice distribution to appropriate service cost centers, and generates comprehensive accounting reports and documentation.
04D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Performance Measurement and Organizational Analytics** (30%): Establishment and maintenance of a comprehensive, coherent, institution-wide performance measurement framework utilizing quantitative indicators that assess customer satisfaction metrics and productivity benchmarks across all DG COMM activities, units, and value chains to enable evidence-based organizational management and continuous improvement

• **Financial Governance, Compliance Oversight, and Institutional Accountability** (30%): Execution of budgetary planning, risk management protocols, internal control mechanisms, preparation of annual activity reports, monitoring of discharge procedures, and systematic tracking of audit report implementation and recommendations to ensure institutional fiduciary compliance and adherence to European governance standards

• **Ex-Ante Financial Transaction Control and Preventive Verification** (30%): Comprehensive preventive verification of all financial transactions—encompassing commitments, payments, recovery orders, and regularization procedures—to systematically control the legality and regularity of financial operations prior to execution

• **Financial Systems Management and Operational Support Services** (10%): Management of financial systems infrastructure, provision of specialized helpdesk support for accounting and financial platform users, coordination of invoice distribution to appropriate service cost centers and accounting records, and generation of comprehensive accounting reports and documentation

original_activities: Define the strategy of the general management, organize and coordinate its activities.
Ensure institutional assistance and support to the parliamentary committees competent in the policies covered by the general management.

Ensure the organization and proper functioning of all activities linked to parliamentary committees and thematic departments in the areas of economic, monetary and fiscal affairs, the internal market, consumer protection, industry, research, energy, environment, public health and food safety.

Provide committees, other parliamentary bodies as well as the President of Parliament with specialist advice and know-how using summary notes, in-depth analyzes and studies on all aspects of the policies covered by the Directorate-General.
Advise the Secretary General and cooperate with other directorates general and the Legal Service.

Represent and/or ensure the representation of the general management in the bodies of Parliament, in internal meetings, as well as in interinstitutional forums and in joint or other committees and ensure their monitoring.

Ensure personnel management; organize and chair several management meetings (directors, unit heads) per month. Manage mail assigned to general management and verify all outgoing mail for the signature of the Secretary General and the President.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
21.refined_description:
Unit 21 functions as the executive directorate-general of the European Parliament, operating at the apex organizational level with direct reporting responsibility to the Secretary General, serving as the strategic and operational command center for institutional governance and policy coordination. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses economic, monetary and fiscal affairs, internal market regulation, consumer protection, industrial policy, research and innovation, energy, environmental policy, public health, and food safety, executed through integrated activity streams that balance strategic direction, parliamentary support, analytical expertise, inter-institutional coordination, and administrative management. Unit 21 maintains hierarchical oversight of three subordinate organizational units (21-10, an integrated administrative and operational directorate; and directional hubs 21A and 21B), thereby ensuring coherent implementation of strategic institutional policy across the Parliament's economic and social affairs portfolio. The unit discharges its mandate through a structured allocation of organizational resources and workload, with particular emphasis on defining general management strategy while simultaneously providing comprehensive institutional assistance to all competent parliamentary committees, organizing and ensuring the proper functioning of all parliamentary committee activities and thematic departmental operations across its substantive policy domains, and delivering specialized analytical expertise through summary notes, in-depth analyses, and comprehensive policy studies. Complementary functions encompassing inter-directorate coordination, institutional representation and monitoring, personnel management, correspondence and mail administration for signature authority, and delegated authorizing officer responsibilities ensure Unit 21 operates as an fully integrated command and control mechanism for the Parliament's economic and social policy governance framework.

---
21.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction, Management Coordination, and Institutional Governance** (15%): Define and coordinate general management strategy; organize and coordinate all directorate-general activities; ensure coherent institutional direction and operational alignment across the Parliament's economic and social affairs portfolio.

• **Parliamentary Committee Support and Institutional Assistance** (30%): Provide comprehensive institutional assistance and support to all competent parliamentary committees operating within the unit's policy domains (15%); organize and ensure proper functioning of all parliamentary committee activities and thematic departmental operations across economic, monetary, fiscal, internal market, consumer protection, industrial, research, energy, environmental, public health, and food safety policy sectors (15%).

• **Specialist Advisory Services and Policy Analysis** (15%): Deliver specialized analytical expertise and know-how to committees, parliamentary bodies, and the President through summary notes, in-depth analyses, and comprehensive policy studies covering all directorate-general policy areas and substantive domains.

• **Inter-institutional Coordination, Executive Advisory Support, and Institutional Representation** (25%): Advise the Secretary General and maintain inter-directorate coordination with other directorates-general and the Legal Service (15%); represent or ensure representation of general management in Parliament's internal bodies, interinstitutional forums, and joint committees while ensuring ongoing systematic monitoring of these engagements (10%).

• **Personnel Management and Administrative Operations** (10%): Execute personnel management functions and chair multiple monthly management meetings with directors and unit heads (7%); manage directorate-general correspondence and verify all outgoing communications for Secretary General and Presidential signature authority (3%).

• **Delegated Authorizing Officer Functions** (5%): Exercise delegated financial and fiduciary authority with associated administrative and accountability responsibilities.

original_activities: HR: Manage the recruitment of staff for DG ECTI units (civil servants, temporary, contractual, END); Manage the organization chart of permanent and temporary positions (fillings, travel, reinstatements); Manage the mobility exercise for AD and AST colleagues (information sessions, bilateral interviews); Manage the selection of interns (T-People, Sxb missions); Monitoring of recruitment/reintegration (reception, management of internship/confirmation reports, rating).

FINANCES: Carry out ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded in the DG; provide assistance and advice on all questions relating to budget execution, calls for tenders and financial procedures and circuits, including the regular updating of the DG's financial and budgetary procedures.

HR: Manage rights and obligations (absences, illness, leave, CCP, retirement, external activities); Manage study visits, secondments, fellowships; Develop statistics; Assist during the scoring exercise; Assist in the organization of teleworking; Manage conflicts, advice (e.g. after long-term absences/illness/CCP).

HR: Manage the budget envelope for missions (3 locations and outside 3 locations); Manage and control Strasbourg organizational charts; Control and monitor 3-site and non-3-site missions (compliance with procedures, exemptions, Back to School, special events such as EYE, JPO).

FINANCES: Coordinate the preparation and monitoring of the establishment of the budget, monitor budget execution, follow the various budgetary procedures, for example: collection, annual closing, etc. and proceed with the distribution of credits on the budget lines.

FINANCES: Ensure coordination and ensure representation of the DG in various committees and working groups in the area of ​​budgetary and financial management; Prepare periodic and annual activity reports, reports to the hierarchy on budget execution and management, as well as financial and budgetary reports to the central services of the institution.

COMMUNICATION: Ensure the internal and external communication of the DG by creating, coordinating and disseminating internal information; represent the DG in various committees and inter-DG working groups in the field; collaborate with DG COMM, DG ITEC and the CCLA COMS unit to design and produce internal communications materials.
COMMUNICATION: Develop and manage the DG intranet, in cooperation with DG ITEC; Manage DG participation in inter-DG events, e.g. Open Day or EYE.
21-10.refined_description: Unit 21-10 functions as the integrated administrative and operational directorate of DG ECTI within the European Parliament's executive structure, reporting directly to Unit 21 (the parent directorate-general) while operating as a peer entity alongside sibling units 21A and 21B, with no subordinate organizational components. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility across three interconnected operational pillars—human resources management, financial administration, and institutional communications—that collectively ensure effective functioning, compliance, and institutional coherence throughout all DG ECTI activities. In its human resources management capacity (50% of total workload), Unit 21-10 manages the complete employment lifecycle across all personnel categories (civil servants, temporary agents, contractual staff, and interns), including recruitment and selection processes, organizational chart maintenance reflecting permanent and temporary position allocations and staff movements, administration of mobility exercises for AD and AST personnel through information sessions and bilateral interviews, and oversight of intern selection through T-People and Strasbourg missions; concurrently, the unit administers employee rights and obligations encompassing absences, illness documentation, leave administration, CCP procedures, retirement processes, external activity authorizations, study visits, secondments, and fellowship placements, while providing conflict resolution and advisory services and managing mission budgets across the three institutional locations and external venues. In its financial administration capacity (45% of total workload), Unit 21-10 performs ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded within DG ECTI, provides comprehensive assistance and advisory services on budget execution, calls for tender procedures, and financial circuits, coordinates preparation and monitoring of budget establishment, oversees budget execution across all phases including various budgetary procedures, credit collection, and annual closure processes, ensures institutional representation within multiple committees and working groups focused on budgetary and financial management, and prepares periodic, annual, and specialized financial and budgetary reports for hierarchy and central institutional services. In its communications capacity (10% of total workload), Unit 21-10 ensures internal and external communication of DG ECTI through creation, coordination, and dissemination of institutional information, represents the DG in communications-focused committees and inter-DG working groups, collaborates strategically with DG COMM, DG ITEC, and the CCLA COMS unit on communications materials design and production, develops and manages the DG intranet in cooperation with DG ITEC, and coordinates DG participation in inter-DG events including the European Parliament Open Day and European Youth Event (EYE).
21-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Recruitment and Staffing Management** (25%): Manages comprehensive recruitment processes for all personnel categories (civil servants, temporary agents, contractual staff, and interns) across all DG ECTI units; maintains and updates organizational charts reflecting permanent and temporary positions, staff movements, and reinstatements; administers mobility exercises for AD and AST personnel through information sessions and bilateral interviews; oversees intern selection through T-People and Strasbourg missions; monitors recruitment and reintegration procedures including reception protocols, internship and confirmation report management, and performance evaluations.

• **Human Resources Administration and Employee Relations** (20%): Administers employee rights and obligations including absences, illness documentation, leave administration, CCP (Congé pour Convenances Personnelles) procedures, retirement processes, and external activity authorizations; facilitates study visits, secondments, and fellowship placements; develops human resources statistics; provides support during scoring exercises and teleworking organization; delivers conflict resolution and advisory services following extended absences, illness episodes, or CCP periods; manages mission budgets across three institutional locations and external venues; controls organizational chart compliance at the Strasbourg site; and ensures procedural adherence for three-site and non-three-site missions while managing exemptions and special events including the European Youth Event (EYE) and Junior Professional Officer (JPO) programs.

• **Financial Verification and Budget Execution** (35%): Performs ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded within DG ECTI; provides comprehensive assistance and advisory services on budget execution, calls for tender procedures, and financial circuits while maintaining current documentation of the DG's financial and budgetary procedures; coordinates preparation and monitoring of budget establishment; oversees budget execution across all phases; manages various budgetary procedures including credit collection and annual closure processes; distributes credits across designated budget lines; ensures institutional representation and coordination within multiple committees and working groups focused on budgetary and financial management; and prepares periodic, annual, and specialized financial and budgetary reports for hierarchy and central institutional services.

• **Institutional Communications and Inter-DG Coordination** (10%): Ensures internal and external communication of DG ECTI through creation, coordination, and dissemination of institutional information; represents the DG in communications-focused committees and inter-DG working groups; collaborates strategically with DG COMM, DG ITEC, and the CCLA COMS unit on communications materials design and production; develops and manages the DG intranet in cooperation with DG ITEC; and coordinates DG participation in inter-DG events including the European Parliament Open Day and European Youth Event (EYE).

21A.name: 21A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of Directorate A.
Manage projects.
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Other missions.
21A.refined_description: Unit 21A functions as the principal directorial office and executive coordination hub of Directorate A within the European Parliament's Secretariat General, positioned at the first hierarchical level beneath the Directorate-General. Operating as the essential connective and supervisory mechanism between the parent Directorate General and its operational structure, Unit 21A bears primary responsibility for ensuring comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services within Directorate A—a function constituting 71% of its documented workload and representing its core institutional mandate. This directional responsibility encompasses direct supervisory oversight of five specialized child units (21A10, 21A20, 21A30, 21A40, and 21A50), which collectively encompass thirty distinct operational activities providing parliamentary support, committee coordination, and policy expertise functions. In parallel coordination with sibling unit 21B, Unit 21A allocates 12% of its workload to institutional project management activities, encompassing planning, implementation oversight, and delivery of cross-functional initiatives across the directorate. The unit further exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions, dedicating 12% of its capacity to budgetary authorization and administrative decision-making on behalf of the parent directorate, thereby maintaining fiscal accountability and compliance within its domain of responsibility. A residual 5% of workload addresses other assigned missions as determined by directorate leadership to support broader institutional objectives. Through these integrated functions, Unit 21A serves as a critical node for organizational coherence, strategic implementation, unified directional leadership, and proper financial stewardship within Directorate A.
21A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction, Coordination, and Organizational Leadership** (71%): Ensures comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all units and services within Directorate A; provides supervisory oversight of five subordinate specialized units (21A10, 21A20, 21A30, 21A40, 21A50) encompassing thirty distinct operational activities; maintains organizational coherence and strategic alignment across the directorate

• **Institutional Project Management and Implementation** (12%): Manages institutional projects across the directorate, encompassing planning, implementation oversight, and delivery of cross-functional initiatives; ensures effective organizational initiative delivery

• **Financial and Administrative Authority** (12%): Performs subdelegated authorizing officer functions, exercising delegated budgetary authorization and administrative decision-making powers on behalf of the parent directorate; maintains fiscal accountability and administrative compliance

• **Supplementary Institutional Missions** (5%): Executes other assigned missions as determined by directorate leadership and organizational requirements to support broader European Parliament institutional objectives

21A10.name: 21A10
original_activities: Manage tender procedures and negotiations for the use of external expertise and ensure financial reporting.

Assist parliamentary bodies by providing studies, briefings, workshops, presentations and research in the areas of economy and growth (in collaboration with the Secretariats of the relevant committees: IMCO, FISC).
Control the scientific quality of studies developed by external consultants and monitor the impact of the expertise.
Write and regularly update the 'Technical Sheets on the European Union'.
Develop relationships with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (peer reviews), external experts, research institutes and universities.
21A10.refined_description:
Unit 21A10 operates as a specialized support and expertise management function within Direction 21A of the European Parliament's Secretariat General, serving as a critical operational intermediary between parliamentary bodies and external research resources. The unit functions as an integrated hub for expert resource management, quality assurance, and parliamentary research support, collectively strengthening the Parliament's legislative and analytical capacity across multiple interconnected operational domains. As a leaf node within the hierarchical structure, Unit 21A10 operates under directorial coordination of parent unit 21A and functions alongside sibling units 21A20, 21A30, 21A40, and 21A50 to collectively support the Parliament's research and analytical infrastructure. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive administration of external expertise procurement, direct analytical support delivery to parliamentary committees, rigorous quality assurance mechanisms, institutional knowledge management, and strategic relationship development with research partners. Through systematic collaboration with committee secretariats and external stakeholders, Unit 21A10 ensures that parliamentary decision-making processes are supported by high-quality, rigorously vetted expertise aligned with legislative priorities in economic policy and growth-related domains. The unit maintains dual accountability mechanisms: institutional compliance with procurement standards and budgetary accountability to the Secretariat General, while simultaneously ensuring scientific rigor and measurable impact of commissioned expertise on parliamentary processes.

##
21A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Procurement and Financial Management** (40%): Comprehensive administration of the full lifecycle of tender procedures, contract negotiations with external expertise providers, and financial reporting mechanisms for external expertise engagements, ensuring compliance with institutional procurement standards, budgetary accountability, and cost-effective acquisition of specialized resources

• **Parliamentary Research Support Services** (25%): Direct provision of specialized studies, policy briefings, workshop facilitation, presentations, and targeted research outputs to the Internal Market and Consumer Protection Committee (IMCO) and the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs (FISC), delivered through systematic collaboration with committee secretariats to align deliverables with legislative priorities in economic policy and growth initiatives

• **Quality Assurance and Impact Assessment** (20%): Rigorous scientific quality control of externally-commissioned studies and systematic monitoring of expertise impact on parliamentary legislative processes to ensure research rigor, institutional value delivery, and tangible influence on parliamentary decision-making

• **Institutional Knowledge Management** (10%): Authorship, maintenance, and periodic updating of the 'Technical Sheets on the European Union' as a continuously evolving institutional reference resource for parliamentary operations

• **Strategic Network Development and Relationship Coordination** (5%): Establishment and maintenance of peer review partnerships with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services, coupled with cultivation of collaborative networks with external experts, research institutes, and academic institutions to enhance the Parliament's access to specialized knowledge and strengthen the research ecosystem

21A20.name: 21A20
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitoring delegated, implementing and 'comitology' acts.

Ensure the secretariat of the committee (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.) including preparation of interinstitutional negotiations (trilogues) and assistance to the Parliament's negotiating team.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Organize public hearings, parliamentary delegations, working group missions outside the workplace, etc.

Organize and manage relationships in the field of financial services: Single Supervisory Mechanism, Single Resolution Mechanism, 3 European supervisory authorities (EBA, EIOPA, ESMA) and with the European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB).
Ensure contact with the European Central Bank and other economic/monetary organizations and organize monetary dialogues.
Ensure contacts with the Council, the Commission, the ETUC, interested circles and civil society.
21A20.refined_description: Unit 21A20 functions as a specialized parliamentary support unit within Direction 21A of the European Parliament's Secretariat General, providing comprehensive legislative, administrative, and technical services to support the Parliament's economic and monetary affairs functions and interinstitutional legislative processes. The unit's primary mission encompasses the preparation and management of legislative documentation across all procedural frameworks (30% of workload), including the drafting of working documents and reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures, alongside systematic monitoring and compliance tracking of delegated acts, implementing acts, and comitology committee decisions across the economic and monetary affairs portfolio. A substantial portion of the unit's capacity (25% of workload) is dedicated to providing full secretariat services for parliamentary committees, encompassing the organization and coordination of committee meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming administration, official minute-taking, and critically, the preparation and support of interinstitutional negotiations including trilogues and substantive assistance to the Parliament's negotiating teams. The unit delivers specialized support to individual MEPs (10% of workload) through written and oral contributions that enable them to effectively discharge their institutional roles as committee presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion drafters. Additionally, the unit organizes and facilitates external parliamentary activities and high-level engagement (10% of workload), including public hearings, parliamentary delegations, and working group missions conducted outside the Parliament's premises. The unit maintains and manages a complex network of institutional relationships with the financial services regulatory ecosystem (15% of workload), encompassing direct liaison with the European Central Bank and other economic and monetary organizations, coordination with the Single Supervisory Mechanism and Single Resolution Mechanism, engagement with the three European supervisory authorities (EBA, EIOPA, ESMA) and the European Systemic Risk Board, and organization of monetary dialogues. The unit further sustains essential external relations (10% of workload) with the Council, Commission, European Trade Union Confederation, relevant professional circles, and civil society stakeholders to ensure informed parliamentary decision-making. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 21A20 operates under the directorial coordination of parent Unit 21A while functioning alongside sibling units 21A10, 21A30, 21A40, and 21A50 to deliver integrated support to the Parliament's economic and financial governance responsibilities.
21A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Documentation and Regulatory Monitoring** (30%): Preparation of working documents and draft reports for legislative and non-legislative procedures; comprehensive monitoring and compliance tracking of delegated acts, implementing acts, and comitology committee decisions to ensure parliamentary oversight across the economic and monetary affairs portfolio.

• **Committee Secretariat and Interinstitutional Coordination** (25%): Organization and management of committee meetings, coordinator meetings, and programming administration; preparation of official minutes; preparation and coordination of interinstitutional negotiations including trilogues; substantive assistance to the Parliament's negotiating teams.

• **Parliamentary Member Support and Facilitation** (10%): Written and oral contributions supporting MEPs in their roles as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion drafters; organization of public hearings, parliamentary delegations, and external working group missions.

• **Financial Supervisory and Monetary Institutional Liaison** (15%): Management of relationships with the Single Supervisory Mechanism and Single Resolution Mechanism; coordination with the three European supervisory authorities (EBA, EIOPA, ESMA) and the European Systemic Risk Board; liaison with the European Central Bank and other economic and monetary organizations; organization of monetary dialogues.

• **External Stakeholder Engagement and Council/Commission Relations** (10%): Maintenance of strategic contacts with the Council, Commission, European Trade Union Confederation, relevant professional circles, and civil society organizations to ensure informed parliamentary decision-making and sustained institutional dialogue.

21A30.name: 21A30
original_activities: Provide timely and high-quality support to IMCO Committee Members in their legislative, budgetary/discharge, non-legislative and scrutiny functions, aligned with Parliament's priorities of strengthening its powers of legislative initiative and democratic scrutiny.
Deliver high-level support, both at committee stage and during inter-institutional negotiations, for all legislative priority files.
Support Members in setting clear priorities and strategic planning.
Ensure the smooth running of IMCO committee's activities, Coordinators' meeting, missions/ad-hoc delegations, public hearings, and exchange of views.
Strengthen the dialogue with the rest of Parliament's administration and other European Union institutions as well as with all relevant groups (interest groups, civil society, etc.).
Promote the visibility of IMCO committee's work via IMCO's webpage, newsletter.
21A30.refined_description: Unit 21A30 operates as a specialized committee support office within Directorate A of the European Parliament's Secretariat General, functioning as the dedicated administrative, legislative, and analytical backbone for the Internal Market and Consumer Protection (IMCO) Committee. Positioned at the second hierarchical level within the directorial structure and maintaining coordinate relationships with sibling units 21A10, 21A20, 21A40, and 21A50, the unit's primary mission is to deliver comprehensive, time-sensitive support to IMCO Committee Members across all parliamentary functions—including legislative drafting, budgetary and discharge procedures, non-legislative initiatives, and democratic scrutiny activities—thereby directly advancing Parliament's institutional priorities of reinforcing legislative initiative powers and enhancing institutional oversight mechanisms. The unit's operational framework encompasses six distinct functional areas that collectively position Unit 21A30 as a critical enabler of the IMCO Committee's legislative effectiveness and democratic accountability. As a leaf node within the 21A directorial structure, the unit operates without subordinate units while maintaining active engagement with Parliament's administrative apparatus, other European Union institutions, and relevant external stakeholders including interest groups and civil society organizations. The unit's workload allocation reflects a deliberate strategic balance between foundational support services, priority legislative engagement, strategic planning facilitation, operational administration, and external visibility functions, ensuring both immediate member responsiveness and long-term institutional impact.
21A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Committee Member Support and Legislative Facilitation** (50%): Provision of timely, high-quality comprehensive support to IMCO Committee Members across all legislative, budgetary/discharge, non-legislative, and scrutiny functions, directly advancing Parliament's institutional priorities for enhanced legislative initiative and democratic oversight.

• **Priority Legislative and Inter-institutional Engagement** (15%): Delivery of specialized high-level support during committee-stage proceedings and inter-institutional negotiations for all designated legislative priority files, ensuring substantive IMCO participation throughout the legislative cycle.

• **Strategic Planning and Priority-Setting** (15%): Assistance to Members in establishing clear organizational priorities and developing comprehensive medium to long-term strategic planning frameworks.

• **Operational Administration and Event Management** (15%): Coordination and administration of IMCO committee activities, including formal committee sessions, Coordinators' meetings, official missions and ad-hoc delegations, public hearings, and structured exchanges of views to ensure seamless operational continuity.

• **Institutional Engagement and External Relations** (3%): Strengthening dialogue and coordination with Parliament's administrative apparatus, other European Union institutions, and relevant external stakeholders including interest groups and civil society organizations.

• **Visibility and Communications** (2%): Promotion of IMCO committee work and institutional transparency through digital platforms and communications channels, including webpage management and newsletter publications.

21A40.name: 21A40
original_activities: Provide internal expertise to Members of the ECON committee in particular in the areas of monetary policy, economic governance and the European Semester, the Recovery and Resilience Facility, Banking Union, and other policy areas where written or oral expertise is required.
Provide expertise on topics of relevance and interest to ECON Members in the form of notes, presentations, in-person or written briefings, and studies.
Provide expertise by outside experts including in the form of presentations or studies.
Support the scrutiny activities of the ECON committee by helping to organise and by contributing to the relevant scrutiny formats.
Prepare and contribute to, with other relevant entities of the European Parliament, relevant meetings with representatives of national parliaments.
21A40.refined_description: Unit 21A40 functions as a specialized policy expertise and analytical support unit within Direction 21A of the European Parliament's Secretariat General, providing comprehensive advisory services to the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs (ECON committee). The unit's primary mission encompasses delivering multilayered internal expert analysis across critical economic policy domains, with particular emphasis on monetary policy, economic governance frameworks, the European Semester process, Banking Union architecture, and the Recovery and Resilience Facility (45% of workload). Beyond direct advisory services, Unit 21A40 actively develops and disseminates diverse knowledge products to ECON Members through multiple delivery mechanisms—including analytical notes, formal presentations, written briefings, in-person consultations, and commissioned studies addressing topics of substantive relevance and demonstrated interest to committee membership (20% of workload). The unit simultaneously facilitates engagement with external expertise by coordinating with specialized external research providers and expert networks to supplement internal analytical capacity through expert presentations and commissioned specialized studies (20% of workload). Unit 21A40 further strengthens parliamentary oversight capacity by contributing substantively to the ECON committee's scrutiny activities through organizational and substantive participation in designated scrutiny mechanisms and formats (10% of workload), while collaboratively coordinating with other relevant European Parliament entities to prepare and facilitate bilateral and multilateral meetings with national parliament representatives to advance inter-parliamentary dialogue on economic policy matters (5% of workload). As a leaf-node unit within the 21A directorial structure, Unit 21A40 operates without subordinate units while maintaining functional coordination with sibling units 21A10, 21A20, 21A30, and 21A50 under the overarching direction of Unit 21A.
21A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct Expert Advisory Services to ECON Committee Members** (45%): Delivery of specialized internal expertise through written consultations, oral briefings, and technical guidance on monetary policy, economic governance, European Semester mechanisms, Banking Union development, the Recovery and Resilience Facility, and other policy domains requiring authoritative parliamentary support
