• **Knowledge Development and Dissemination** (20%): Creation and presentation of analytical knowledge products to ECON Members through policy notes, analytical studies, formal presentations, written briefings, and commissioned research addressing topics of strategic interest and relevance to committee membership

• **External Expert Engagement and Research Facilitation** (20%): Coordination and integration of specialized external expertise through commissioned presentations and research studies, leveraging external expert networks and research providers to supplement internal analytical capacity and provide specialized external perspectives

• **Parliamentary Scrutiny Support** (10%): Organization and substantive contribution to ECON committee scrutiny activities, formats, and mechanisms to strengthen parliamentary oversight capacity

• **Inter-Parliamentary Coordination and Dialogue** (5%): Collaborative preparation and facilitation of bilateral and multilateral meetings between European Parliament representatives and national parliament delegations on economic policy matters to advance institutional dialogue

21A50.name: 21A50
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), including preparation of interinstitutional negotiations (trilogues) and assistance to the Parliament's negotiation team.
Ensure the secretariat of the sub-committee (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Organize exchanges of views on tax policy with national parliaments. Organize inter-parliamentary conferences on taxation.
Organize public hearings, parliamentary delegations, working group missions outside the workplace, etc.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Ensure contacts with the Council, the Commission, the CES, the Court of Auditors, interested circles and civil society.
Ensure internal and external communication of the subcommittee (website, eCommittee, newsletter, etc.).
21A50.refined_description:
Unit 21A50 functions as a specialized parliamentary support unit within Direction 21A of the European Parliament's Secretariat General, operating as a leaf-node organizational entity with no subordinate structures and serving alongside sibling units 21A10, 21A20, 21A30, and 21A40. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive secretariat, substantive legislative support, and coordination functions dedicated to a parliamentary subcommittee with specialized focus on taxation and fiscal policy matters. Unit 21A50 executes four equally-weighted primary operational domains: legislative development and interinstitutional negotiation (20%), subcommittee secretariat and administrative operations (20%), interparliamentary relations and taxation policy dialogue (20%), and parliamentary activity support encompassing both MEP assistance and external engagement (40%). The unit maintains critical institutional liaison relationships with the Council, Commission, Economic and Social Committee, Court of Auditors, and civil society organizations, while simultaneously managing comprehensive internal and external communications through digital platforms including website management, eCommittee systems, and newsletter dissemination. These integrated functions position Unit 21A50 as a critical operational and substantive support mechanism within the broader Direction 21A structure, facilitating both legislative procedures and democratic engagement on taxation matters across multiple institutional and parliamentary levels.

---
21A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Development and Interinstitutional Negotiation** (20%): Preparation of working documents and draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures; substantive preparation of interinstitutional negotiations (trilogues); direct assistance to Parliament's negotiation teams in bilateral and multilateral negotiations

• **Subcommittee Secretariat and Administrative Operations** (20%): Organization and management of subcommittee meetings, coordinators' meetings, and programming; preparation of official minutes documentation and record-keeping; administrative coordination of all subcommittee proceedings

• **Interparliamentary Relations and Taxation Policy Dialogue** (20%): Organization of structured exchanges of views on tax policy with national parliaments; coordination and facilitation of inter-parliamentary conferences on taxation matters; administration of international parliamentary engagement on fiscal policy

• **Parliamentary Activity Support and Member Assistance** (25%): Organization of public hearings, parliamentary delegations, and working group missions conducted outside the workplace (15%); provision of written and oral contributions assisting MEPs in exercise of designated functions as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen (10%)

• **Institutional Liaison and Communications Management** (15%): Maintenance of institutional contacts with Council, Commission, Economic and Social Committee, Court of Auditors, and civil society organizations (5%); management of internal and external subcommittee communications including website platforms, eCommittee systems, newsletter dissemination, and related digital communication channels (10%)

21B.name: 21B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of Directorate B.
Manage projects.
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Other missions.
21B.refined_description: Unit 21B functions as the primary Direction-level management and coordination hub within Directorate B of the European Parliament's executive structure, operating as the first hierarchical tier beneath the parent executive directorate-general (Unit 21) and serving as the essential strategic and operational bridge between directorate-level leadership and four specialized subordinate units (21B10, 21B20, 21B30, 21B40). The unit bears primary responsibility for ensuring comprehensive directorial direction, strategic animation, and operational coordination of all subordinate units and services within Directorate B, establishing coherent strategic implementation and resource optimization across the directorate's specialized research expertise, parliamentary support, and coordination functions. Beyond this dominant coordinative mandate, Unit 21B executes three complementary functional portfolios: project management responsibilities that advance cross-functional organizational initiatives requiring coordination across directorate boundaries; subdelegated authorizing officer functions that maintain financial accountability, budgetary oversight, and administrative compliance within its designated authority scope; and ancillary institutional missions as required by emerging organizational needs and parent directorate priorities. Through this integrated portfolio of strategic direction (71% of workload), project management (12% of workload), financial and administrative stewardship (12% of workload), and supplementary institutional functions (5% of workload), Unit 21B fulfills its critical intermediary role in ensuring governance framework compliance, coherent operational alignment, and effective resource deployment throughout Directorate B.
21B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction and Organizational Coordination** (71%): Provides comprehensive directorial direction, coordination, and strategic animation of all subordinate units and services within Directorate B (units 21B10, 21B20, 21B30, 21B40), ensuring coherent operational alignment, strategic coherence, and establishment of the primary directorial oversight mechanism for specialized service delivery components.

• **Project and Initiative Management** (12%): Manages cross-functional and cross-directorate institutional projects requiring coordinated implementation and oversight, supporting organizational development and strategic initiatives within the unit's purview.

• **Financial and Administrative Stewardship** (12%): Exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions to maintain financial accountability, budgetary oversight, administrative compliance, and effective resource deployment within the unit's designated authority scope.

• **Supplementary Institutional Functions** (5%): Executes other assigned missions, ad-hoc responsibilities, and emerging institutional priorities as determined by parent directorate leadership and organizational needs.

21B10.name: 21B10
original_activities: Provide high-level assistance to parliamentary bodies and especially Committees with studies, notes and other forms of expertise and research, including 'accompanying expertise', in the following policy areas: transformation, innovation and health (Committees concerned: ITRE, ENVI and SANT).
Manage tender procedures and negotiations for external studies and expertise, and ensure the necessary financial reporting.
Guarantee the scientific quality and excellence of research and expertise products prepared either in-house or by external consultants and scholars.

Maintain and further collaboration with external experts, research institutions and universities, as well as with other information, research and expertise providers within the European Parliament, including the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Research Services.
Draft and regularly update the 'Fact Sheets on the European Union' falling under the remit of the Unit.
Promote and monitor the impact of research and expertise provided.
21B10.refined_description: Unit 21B10 operates as a specialized research and expertise provision unit within Directorate B of the European Parliament, functioning at the second hierarchical level beneath its parent directional unit 21B and positioned as a leaf node entity with no subordinate organizational structures. The Unit's primary mission encompasses delivering comprehensive, high-quality research support and specialized analytical expertise to parliamentary bodies, with particular emphasis on serving the Committee on Industry, Research and Energy (ITRE), Committee on Environment, Public Health and Food Safety (ENVI), and Committee on Health (SANT) in the substantive policy domains of transformation, innovation, and health. Unit 21B10 integrates six core operational functions: delivering high-level studies, research notes, analytical briefings, and accompanying expertise products (35%); managing the complete lifecycle of external study procurement including tender procedures, expert negotiations, and financial reporting mechanisms (20%); maintaining rigorous quality assurance and scientific excellence standards for all research and expertise products whether developed through in-house capacity or external consultancy arrangements (20%); cultivating and sustaining collaborative relationships with external experts, research institutions, universities, and information providers, including formal coordination with the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Research Services and coordination with sibling units (21B20, 21B30, 21B40) within the broader directorial framework (15%); drafting and systematically updating 'Fact Sheets on the European Union' within its substantive remit (5%); and monitoring and promoting the measurable impact and institutional utilization of research and expertise outputs (5%). These integrated activities collectively position Unit 21B10 as a critical knowledge management and research excellence hub within the European Parliament's analytical infrastructure, ensuring that parliamentary decision-making processes are informed by rigorous, timely, and scientifically validated expertise.
21B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Research and Expertise Provision** (35%): Deliver high-level assistance to parliamentary committees (ITRE, ENVI, SANT) through comprehensive studies, research notes, analytical briefings, and accompanying expertise addressing transformation, innovation, and health policy domains to inform parliamentary deliberation and legislative processes

• **Procurement and Financial Management** (20%): Manage tender procedures, conduct negotiations for external studies and expertise acquisition, administer procurement contracts, and execute necessary financial reporting and budget compliance functions

• **Quality Assurance and Scientific Excellence** (20%): Guarantee scientific rigor, methodological excellence, and research quality standards for all research and expertise products, whether produced through in-house capacity or external consultant, academic scholar, and specialized research organization engagement

• **External Relations and Institutional Collaboration** (15%): Maintain and develop collaborative relationships with external experts, research institutions, universities, and information providers; coordinate strategically with the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Research Services and other internal expertise resources; coordinate operationally with sibling units (21B20, 21B30, 21B40) within Directorate B

• **Knowledge Products and Impact Monitoring** (10%): Draft and systematically update 'Fact Sheets on the European Union' within the Unit's substantive policy remit (5%) and promote and monitor the measurable impact and institutional utilization of research and expertise outputs throughout the parliamentary institution (5%)

21B20.name: 21B20
original_activities: Work on Reports, Opinions, Motion for resolutions, etc. (legislative and non-legislative files), trilogues, comitology, scrutiny.
Attend to the organisation and follow-up of Committees and Coordinators meetings etc.
Assist Members in the exercise of their function.
Organise public hearings and missions etc.
Attend to the website, newsletter, eMeeting and eCommittee.
Maintain contacts with the ACER, BEREC, ENISA and the JRS etc.
Maintain general contacts with the other institutions.
21B20.refined_description:
**

Unit 21B20 operates as a specialized parliamentary support unit functioning at the second hierarchical level within Direction 21B of the European Parliament, serving as a terminal service unit without subordinate divisions while collaborating with sibling units 21B10, 21B30, and 21B40. The unit's primary mission centers on providing comprehensive legislative and procedural support for parliamentary work, with its core function (79% of workload) focused on substantive engagement with legislative processes through the preparation, drafting, and management of Reports, Opinions, and Motions for Resolutions across both legislative and non-legislative files, alongside active participation in trilogues, comitology procedures, and parliamentary scrutiny mechanisms. Supporting this foundational mission, Unit 21B20 dedicates 10% of its capacity to organizing and coordinating Committee and Coordinator meetings to ensure effective governance of parliamentary bodies, while allocating 5% to direct Member assistance in exercising their parliamentary functions. The unit maintains operational public engagement through 3% allocation to organizing public hearings and official parliamentary missions, and sustains digital infrastructure through 1% of workload devoted to website management, newsletter production, and electronic meeting/committee platform administration. Additionally, Unit 21B20 preserves critical institutional relationships by allocating 1% to specialized liaison with European regulatory agencies (ACER, BEREC, ENISA, and the JRS) and 1% to general inter-institutional coordination with other European institutions.

**
21B20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Legislative Documentation and Parliamentary Procedures** (79%): Preparation, drafting, and management of Reports, Opinions, and Motions for Resolutions for both legislative and non-legislative parliamentary files; active participation in trilogue negotiations, comitology procedures, and parliamentary scrutiny mechanisms

• **Committee and Member Support Services** (15%): Organization and coordination of Committee and Coordinator meetings with follow-up activities (10%); direct assistance to Members in exercising their parliamentary functions (5%)

• **Public Engagement and External Events** (3%): Organization and facilitation of public hearings and official parliamentary missions

• **Digital Infrastructure and Communications Management** (1%): Management of unit website, newsletter production, and electronic meeting/committee platforms (eCommittee and eMeeting systems)

• **Institutional and Regulatory Relations** (2%): Maintenance of specialized agency contacts with European regulatory bodies—ACER, BEREC, ENISA, and the JRS (1%); management of general inter-institutional coordination and relationship management with other European institutions (1%)

21B30.name: 21B30
original_activities: Ensure the organization and monitoring of interinstitutional negotiations (trilogues, informal meetings).
Prepare working documents/draft reports/draft opinions (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitoring delegated acts and 'comitology' acts (including objections).
Assist the president and prepare ENVI committee meetings and coordinator meetings, including for hearings of new Commissioners.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, coordinators, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.

Ensure contacts with the following agencies: European Medicines Agency (EMA) (until January 2025), European Environment Agency (EEA), European Food Safety Authority (EFSA), European Center for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC), (until January 2025) European Chemicals Agency (ECHA).
Ensure contacts with interested circles and civil society (also through newsletters).
Organize public hearings and the participation of deputies in delegations (international conferences, etc.).
21B30.refined_description: Unit 21B30 functions as a specialized legislative support and interinstitutional coordination unit operating at the second hierarchical level within Directorate B of the European Parliament, directly subordinate to parent unit 21B and without subordinate units of its own. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management of the Environment, Public Health and Food Safety (ENVI) Committee's legislative workflow, strategic interinstitutional engagement processes, and provision of substantive support to committee leadership and individual MEPs exercising formal roles. Unit 21B30 dedicates equal proportions (35% each) to two core legislative functions: organizing and monitoring interinstitutional negotiations (including trilogues and informal meetings between Parliament, Council, and Commission to advance parliamentary positions through co-decision processes) and preparing legislative and non-legislative working documents, draft reports, and draft opinions while simultaneously monitoring delegated acts and comitology procedures, including management of parliamentary objection mechanisms to ensure effective executive oversight. The unit allocates 22% of combined resources to committee operations and leadership support, comprising 12% for administrative and substantive preparation of ENVI committee meetings, coordinator gatherings, and specialized Commissioner hearings, and 10% for providing written and oral technical assistance to MEPs holding formal positions including president, vice-president, coordinators, rapporteurs, and opinion draftspersons. Unit 21B30 dedicates 6% of workload to maintaining institutional liaison relationships with five designated EU agencies (the European Medicines Agency [EMA], European Environment Agency [EEA], European Food Safety Authority [EFSA], European Center for Disease Prevention and Control [ECDC], and European Chemicals Agency [ECHA]—with the latter two subject to temporal restrictions until January 2025), while simultaneously engaging with civil society organizations and interested stakeholder circles through structured communication mechanisms including newsletters and direct engagement. The remaining 2% of resources addresses external engagement activities including organization of public hearings and coordination of MEP participation in international delegations and specialized conferences. As a leaf node within the 21B directorial structure, Unit 21B30 operates in coordination with sibling units 21B10, 21B20, and 21B40 to provide comprehensive legislative and administrative support across the Parliament's committee system.
21B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Interinstitutional Negotiation and Legislative Process Management** (35%): Organization and real-time monitoring of trilogues, informal negotiation meetings, and interinstitutional engagement mechanisms between Parliament, Council, and Commission to advance and ensure effective representation of parliamentary legislative positions through co-decision processes.

• **Legislative Documentation Preparation and Regulatory Oversight** (35%): Drafting and preparation of legislative and non-legislative working documents, committee reports, and draft opinions; concurrent monitoring of delegated acts and comitology procedures with management of formal parliamentary objection mechanisms to maintain effective parliamentary oversight of executive rule-making.

• **Committee Operations and MEP Leadership Support** (22%): Administrative and substantive preparation of ENVI committee meetings, coordinator sessions, and specialized Commissioner hearings (12%); provision of written and oral technical assistance to MEPs exercising formal positions including president, vice-president, coordinators, rapporteurs, and opinion draftspersons (10%).

• **Institutional Agency and Stakeholder Liaison** (6%): Maintenance of formal institutional liaison relationships with five designated EU agencies—European Medicines Agency (EMA), European Environment Agency (EEA), European Food Safety Authority (EFSA), European Center for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC), and European Chemicals Agency (ECHA, with restrictions until January 2025)—and engagement with civil society organizations and interested stakeholder circles through structured communication channels including newsletters and direct communications (3% combined).

• **Public Engagement and International Representation** (2%): Organization of public hearings and coordination of MEP participation in international delegations, conferences, and external parliamentary engagement activities.

21B40.name: 21B40
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports/draft opinions (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitoring delegated acts and 'comitology' acts (including objections).
Ensure the organization and monitoring of interinstitutional negotiations (trilogues, informal meetings).
Assist the president and prepare SANT commission meetings and coordinator meetings.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, coordinators, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Organize public hearings and the participation of deputies in delegations (international conferences, etc.).
Ensure contacts with the following agencies: European Medicines Agency (EMA), European Center for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC).
Ensure contacts with interested circles and civil society (also through newsletters).
21B40.refined_description: Unit 21B40 functions as a specialized parliamentary support unit within Direction 21B of the European Parliament, serving as a critical operational node in the legislative and institutional coordination infrastructure that supports the SANT (Committee on the Environment, Public Health and Food Safety) Commission. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive preparation and management of legislative documentation across both legislative and non-legislative procedures, systematic monitoring of delegated acts and comitology acts including formal objection processing, and organization of complex interinstitutional negotiations including trilogues and informal meetings between Parliament, Council, and Commission. Unit 21B40 provides essential administrative and substantive support to parliamentary leadership through preparation and monitoring of SANT Commission meetings and coordinator gatherings, while simultaneously furnishing written and oral contributions to support MEPs functioning as presidents, vice-presidents, coordinators, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen in the exercise of their specialized institutional roles. The unit maintains dedicated liaison functions with key European health agencies—specifically the European Medicines Agency (EMA) and the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC)—and engages with external stakeholders through organized public hearings, coordination of MEP participation in international delegations and conferences, direct contact with interested circles and civil society organizations, and newsletter communications. Operating as a leaf node within the 21B directorial structure alongside sibling units 21B10, 21B20, and 21B30, Unit 21B40 collectively contributes to the European Parliament's health policy development, legislative coordination, and institutional effectiveness objectives.
21B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Documentation and Regulatory Monitoring** (35%): Preparation of working documents, draft reports, and draft opinions across both legislative and non-legislative procedures; systematic monitoring and management of delegated acts and comitology acts; formal processing of objections to regulatory instruments

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Negotiation Management** (25%): Organization and monitoring of trilogues and informal interinstitutional meetings; facilitation of negotiation processes and coordination between Parliament, Council, and Commission

• **Parliamentary Leadership and Commission Support** (23%): Administrative preparation and substantive monitoring of SANT Commission meetings and coordinator gatherings (15%); provision of written and oral assistance to MEPs serving in leadership roles including presidents, vice-presidents, coordinators, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen (8%)

• **External Engagement, Agency Liaison, and Stakeholder Relations** (17%): Maintenance of formal liaison relationships with the European Medicines Agency (EMA) and European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC) (6%); organization of public hearings and coordination of MEP participation in international delegations and conferences (6%); direct engagement with interested circles, civil society organizations, and stakeholder communications including newsletter distribution (5%)

DS.name: DS
original_activities: Advise the Secretary General, the President, the political bodies of Parliament on the tasks assigned to the Deputy Secretary General (legislative coordination) and in the areas of competence of the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services.
Define the strategy of the Department of Commission Coordination and Legislative Activities, organize and coordinate its activities.
Define the strategy of the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, organize and coordinate its activities.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
If necessary, act as interim Secretary General and represent the institution.
DS.refined_description: The Department of Strategy (DS) operates as a Directorate-General-level strategic unit within the European Parliament's administrative hierarchy, reporting directly to the Secretary General and functioning as the institutional apex for parliamentary coordination, research governance, and strategic advisory services. DS executes its institutional mandate across four integrated functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation. The unit provides high-level advisory services to the Secretary General, President, and Parliament's political bodies regarding Deputy Secretary General responsibilities in legislative coordination and the operational competencies of the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, while simultaneously maintaining contingency capacity to assume interim Secretary General functions and represent the institution when operationally required. DS defines and executes the strategic direction of both the Department of Commission Coordination and Legislative Activities and the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services through comprehensive organizational planning, strategic framework development, and activity coordination mechanisms that ensure alignment between legislative support services, research functions, and institutional governance objectives. Additionally, DS exercises delegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial and administrative authority, enabling budgetary decision-making and administrative commitments as delegated by the Secretary General. Through this balanced allocation of resources across strategic advisory, organizational coordination, and administrative functions, DS ensures institutional continuity, coherent direction, and effective Parliament-Commission interface across the institution's major operational and research services.
DS.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Advisory Services and Institutional Representation** (30%): Provision of high-level strategic counsel to the Secretary General, President, and parliamentary political bodies regarding Deputy Secretary General legislative coordination responsibilities and General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services operational competencies; includes contingency capacity to assume interim Secretary General duties and represent the institution when required

• **Strategic Planning and Coordination – Commission Coordination and Legislative Activities** (30%): Definition and implementation of strategic frameworks, comprehensive organizational planning, and activity coordination for the Department of Commission Coordination and Legislative Activities, ensuring alignment between Parliament's legislative agenda and institutional coordination mechanisms with effective Parliament-Commission interface management

• **Strategic Planning and Coordination – Parliamentary Research Services** (30%): Definition and implementation of strategic frameworks, comprehensive organizational planning, and activity coordination for the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, ensuring coherent research support and strategic direction across all parliamentary research functions

• **Financial and Administrative Authority** (5%): Exercise of delegated authorizing officer functions encompassing budgetary commitment authority and administrative decision-making powers as delegated by the Secretary General

• **Institutional Continuity and Operational Contingency** (5%): Assumption of interim Secretary General responsibilities and institutional representation functions as required by operational necessity to ensure institutional continuity

DSA.name: DSA
original_activities: Ensure the management and coordination of the five Directorate for Committee Coordination and Legislative Activities units (LEGI, CoordLeg, Calendar Unit, COMS, and PCL-Hub).

Assist the Chair of the Conference of Committee Chairs (CCC) and prepare the CCC meetings, as well as meetings of the senior management (CMC) and Heads of Units (CDCM) of the five policy Directorates-General and ensure the appropriate follow-up. Represent committees in the Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG) and in the preparatory meetings of the CoP; assist the CCC Chair in the CoP meetings.

Oversee the organisation and/or coordination of horizontal committee activities (legislative negotiations with the Council, own-initiative reports, public hearings, missions, monitoring of delegated acts, implementation of MFF programs, contribution to the CWP, scrutiny of the executive and cooperation with other European Union institutions and bodies (CoR, EESC), and national parliaments, etc.).

Connect all the resources and the services needed to have well-organised and multilingual committee and delegation meetings, and missions. Ensure the smooth running of parallel meetings and that all the meeting requests are duly justified in order to achieve the best possible allocation of resources. Submit to the Cabinets the requests for extraordinary meetings of committees and delegations. Provide input on the committees and delegations needs in relation to PHS renovation and trilogue needs.
Assist committees, policy departments, and support units in the dissemination of their activities and of their products both on the internet and intranet platforms.

Provide advice, trainings and expertise to the five Policy Directorates-General for the common business processes of committees and delegations, in particular in relation to the IT applications they use for their legislative, scrutiny and budgetary activities.

Coordinate major horizontal files (e.g. Omnibuses, post-2027 MFF package) and monitor the use of the urgent procedure (Rule 170), the use of Article 122 TFEU, and the measures taken by the EU following Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine.
Exercise the function of Authorising Officer by Subdelegation.
DSA.refined_description:
The Directorate for Strategic Affairs (DSA) operates as a Directorate-General-level strategic coordination unit reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament, functioning as the institutional nexus for horizontal committee governance, legislative coordination, and operational support across the Parliament's five Policy Directorates-General. DSA exercises comprehensive management and coordination responsibilities over five specialized units (LEGI, CoordLeg, Calendar Unit, COMS, and PCL-Hub) that collectively support legislative and scrutiny activities, while simultaneously providing substantive support to the Conference of Committee Chairs (CCC) through meeting preparation, senior management coordination (CMC and CDCM), and representation at the Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG) and Conference of Presidents (CoP) preparatory meetings. The directorate maintains substantial oversight of horizontal committee activities encompassing legislative negotiations with the Council, own-initiative reports, public hearings, institutional missions, delegated acts monitoring, Multiannual Financial Framework program implementation, Common Work Programme contributions, executive scrutiny, and cooperative engagement with the Committee of the Regions (CoR), European Economic and Social Committee (EESC), Court of Auditors, and national parliaments. DSA manages the logistical and technical infrastructure supporting multilingual committee and delegation meetings and missions, including resource optimization, parallel meeting coordination, extraordinary meeting request submission to Cabinets, and provision of input on Parliament Hemicycle Spaces (PHS) renovation and trilogue facility requirements. The directorate facilitates knowledge dissemination across committees, policy departments, and support units through internet and intranet platforms while providing comprehensive advisory services, training programs, and technical expertise to the five Policy Directorates-General regarding common business processes and information technology applications supporting legislative, scrutiny, and budgetary functions. DSA coordinates major horizontal legislative files including Omnibus procedures and post-2027 Multiannual Financial Framework packages, monitoring the application of urgent parliamentary procedures (Rule 170), Treaty provisions (Article 122 TFEU), and European Union institutional responses to geopolitical crises including Russia's military aggression against Ukraine. The directorate exercises financial authority as Authorising Officer by Subdelegation, completing a comprehensively integrated operational framework positioned as the indispensable coordination backbone enabling effective committee functioning and interinstitutional legislative activity.

---
DSA.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Governance, Strategic Coordination, and Committee Management** (40%): Management and coordination of five specialized committee coordination units (LEGI, CoordLeg, Calendar Unit, COMS, PCL-Hub); substantive support to the Conference of Committee Chairs (CCC) through meeting preparation and senior management coordination (CMC and CDCM meetings); representation at Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG) and Conference of Presidents (CoP) preparatory structures; coordination across five Policy Directorates-General ensuring institutional alignment and appropriate follow-up.

• **Horizontal Committee Operations and Legislative Activities** (20%): Oversight and coordination of legislative negotiations with the Council, own-initiative reports, public hearings, institutional missions, delegated acts monitoring, Multiannual Financial Framework program implementation, Common Work Programme contributions, executive scrutiny, and cooperation with external EU institutions (CoR, EESC, Court of Auditors) and national parliaments; coordination of major horizontal legislative files including Omnibus procedures and post-2027 MFF packages.

• **Crisis Response and Urgent Procedures Monitoring** (9%): Monitoring and coordination of urgent parliamentary procedures (Rule 170), Treaty-based mechanisms (Article 122 TFEU), and institutional responses to geopolitical crises including Russia's military aggression against Ukraine.

• **Meeting Infrastructure, Resource Management, and Logistical Support** (10%): Connection and coordination of resources and services for multilingual committee and delegation meetings and missions; optimization of parallel meeting scheduling; justification and allocation of meeting resources; submission of extraordinary meeting requests to Cabinets; provision of input on Parliament Hemicycle Spaces (PHS) renovation requirements and trilogue facility needs.

• **Knowledge Dissemination, Communications, and Digital Platform Support** (10%): Facilitation of committee, policy department, and support unit activities dissemination on internet and intranet platforms; assistance in enhancing institutional transparency and stakeholder engagement across digital channels.

• **Technical Expertise, Advisory Services, and Training Programs** (10%): Provision of advisory services, training programs, and technical expertise to five Policy Directorates-General regarding common business processes and information technology applications supporting legislative, scrutiny, and budgetary activities.

• **Financial Authority and Budgetary Management** (1%): Exercise of Authorising Officer by Subdelegation function for financial and budgetary management responsibilities.

DSA10.name: DSA10
original_activities: Ensure horizontal and strategic monitoring of legislative files throughout the ordinary legislative procedure, by participating in trilogue, shadow, and technical meetings, by contributing to the evolution of good practices on the implementation of the regulation, and by responding to various questions (e.g. transparency/access to PLO documents). Produce monthly summary tables (files in progress and under negotiation) and list of mandates adopted by committees (for CPC, COP).

Delegated acts/Implementing acts ('DIAs'): implementing the 2016 agreement (Interinstitutional Agreement 'Better Lawmaking') and response to requests from committees; meetings of the DIAs network (exchange of good practices) in collaboration with the Legal Department; develop documents for the COP and CPC, including the monitoring process and monthly overview on delegated acts, summer and winter suspension periods, and 'early no-objections'.

Ensure horizontal monitoring of legislative files, especially by preparing notes on current topics such as trends observed during trilogues/code of conduct on negotiation methods. Contribute to the reflection work of the Conference of Commission Presidents (CPC). Organize networks on legislative work and international agreements.

Carry out training activities, including for Members of Parliament ('Learn MEP', 'Simulation game'), and provide information both inside and outside Parliament on the subjects covered by the Unit (training for future Council presidencies, on the regulation and delegated and implementing acts). Publication of manuals on the PLO procedure, delegated and implementing acts, as well as international agreements.
Regularly prepare summaries on the status of ongoing files in the PLO procedure (Practical guide, summary notes, presentations, tables, lists, etc.).

Ensure coordination with counterpart services of the Commission and the Council. Organize meetings between future Council Presidencies and the presidents of parliamentary committees ('speed dating'). Organize the procedure for signing LEX texts adopted in the ordinary legislative procedure with a view to their publication.
Contribute to: the implementation and proper use of existing IT tools (ITER); continue to encourage the use of new IT tools (Trilogue Table Editor (TTE)).
Continue to reflect on trends in the ordinary legislative procedure (i.e. emergency, simplified procedures of Article 122 of the Treaty on the Functioning of the EU).
DSA10.refined_description: DSA10 is a Directorate-General-level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament, functioning as the central institutional hub for legislative procedure coordination, inter-institutional liaison, and capacity development across the European Parliament's legislative ecosystem. The unit exercises comprehensive horizontal and strategic oversight of legislative files throughout the ordinary legislative procedure, actively participating in trilogue, shadow, and technical meetings while monitoring implementation practices and addressing transparency and access requests regarding PLO documents. DSA10 manages the complex framework of delegated and implementing acts through specialized expertise and coordination with the Legal Department's DIAs network, implementing the 2016 Better Lawmaking Interinstitutional Agreement while tracking suspension periods and early no-objections procedures. The unit contributes substantively to institutional reflection on legislative trends, negotiation methodologies, code of conduct implementation, and emerging procedural innovations, providing analytical support to the Conference of Presidents and Conference of Committee Presidents. DSA10 fulfills a critical capacity-building mandate through structured training programs including "Learn MEP" initiatives and simulation exercises for Members of Parliament, alongside development and publication of comprehensive manuals addressing ordinary legislative procedure, delegated and implementing acts, and international agreements, with this educational function extended to external stakeholders including future Council presidencies. The unit orchestrates horizontal coordination with Commission and Council counterpart services through strategic "speed dating" meetings between incoming Council Presidencies and parliamentary committee presidents, while managing LEX text signature and publication procedures for adopted legislation. DSA10 actively promotes digital transformation of legislative workflows by implementing and optimizing existing IT infrastructure (ITER) while driving adoption of emerging technological solutions (Trilogue Table Editor), and engages in forward-looking institutional analysis regarding procedural innovations including emergency procedures and simplified procedures under Article 122 TFEU.
DSA10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative File Monitoring and Procedural Oversight** (45%): Horizontal and strategic monitoring of legislative files throughout the ordinary legislative procedure via participation in trilogue, shadow, and technical meetings (30%); preparation of monthly summary tables, mandate lists for CPC/COP, and analytical notes on current negotiation trends, code of conduct implementation, and emerging legislative methodologies (15%); production of status summaries, practical guides, presentations, and analytical documentation addressing file progression and institutional developments.

• **Delegated and Implementing Acts Framework Management** (15%): Implementation of the 2016 Better Lawmaking Interinstitutional Agreement; coordination of the DIAs network in collaboration with the Legal Department; development of monitoring processes, monthly overview documentation, suspension period tracking, and early no-objections procedures for both COP and CPC; exchange of good practices and regulatory framework expertise.

• **Capacity Building and Knowledge Dissemination** (25%): Delivery of structured training activities including "Learn MEP" programs and legislative simulation exercises for Members of Parliament (15%); development and publication of comprehensive manuals on ordinary legislative procedure, delegated and implementing acts, and international agreements; provision of formal training to future Council presidencies and external stakeholders on regulatory frameworks and procedural requirements (10%).

• **Inter-Institutional Coordination and External Relations** (10%): Coordination with Commission and Council counterpart services; organization of strategic "speed dating" meetings between incoming Council Presidencies and parliamentary committee presidents; management of LEX text signing procedures and publication coordination for adopted ordinary legislative procedure texts (7%); contribution to Conference of Commission Presidents reflection work on legislative trends and emerging practices (3%).

• **Digital Infrastructure and Procedural Innovation** (5%): Implementation and optimization of existing IT tools (ITER) and promotion of emerging digital solutions (Trilogue Table Editor); forward-looking institutional analysis regarding procedural innovations, emergency procedures, and simplified procedures under Article 122 TFEU.

DSA20.name: DSA20
original_activities: Prepare and organise CCC meetings and their follow-up, including recommendations for draft agendas of upcoming plenary part-sessions and recommendations on modalities for cooperation among committees.

Prepare recommendations and guidelines on horizontal matters of importance for committees, both at administrative level as well as for political endorsement at the level of the Conference of Committee Chairs, for which it acts as the secretariat.

Coordinate working processes (where relevant) and support the work of parliamentary committees, develop and share best practices regarding committee working methods; provide advice and guidance to committee secretariats on procedural questions and application of the Rules of Procedure.
Organise and assume the follow-up of management meetings of the Policy Directorates-General.

Serve as a contact point for committees for relations with the secretariats of the Conference of Presidents, Bureau, the Cabinets of the President and the Secretary General as well as other relevant services of the European Parliament.
Represent committees' interests and maintain regular contacts with the Commission and Council as well as other institutions and bodies.
DSA20.refined_description: DSA20 is a Directorate-General-level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament, functioning as the central secretariat and coordination nexus for parliamentary committee operations, inter-institutional relations, and horizontal policy guidance across the institution. The unit's integrated mission encompasses comprehensive administrative, procedural, and strategic support of the Conference of Committee Chairs (CCC) and all parliamentary committees, positioning it as the essential administrative and coordinative hub for parliamentary committee governance and external institutional engagement. DSA20 operates across five interconnected operational domains that collectively ensure coherent committee operations, institutional policy alignment, and effective external representation of parliamentary committee interests. The unit's workload allocation reflects its dual responsibility as both an internal administrative support mechanism for committee coordination and governance, and as an external-facing liaison entity maintaining institutional relationships with the Conference of Presidents, Bureau, Presidential and Secretary General Cabinets, the European Commission, Council, and other relevant external institutions and bodies. Through its integrated activities spanning committee meeting governance, horizontal policy development, procedural excellence, management coordination, and inter-institutional liaison, DSA20 serves as the indispensable administrative and strategic platform for ensuring operational coherence across parliamentary committees while simultaneously representing and advancing committee interests within the broader institutional and inter-institutional context.
DSA20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Meeting Governance and Coordination** (30%): Preparation and organization of Conference of Committee Chairs meetings with comprehensive follow-up procedures; formulation of recommendations for plenary part-session draft agendas; development and establishment of inter-committee cooperation modalities and frameworks

• **Horizontal Policy Development and Institutional Guidance** (25%): Development and preparation of recommendations and guidelines on matters of horizontal significance affecting all committees at both administrative and political levels; provision of secretariat services for the Conference of Committee Chairs; facilitation of political endorsement processes at appropriate institutional levels

• **Procedural Support and Operational Excellence** (20%): Coordination of working processes across parliamentary committees; development and dissemination of best practices regarding committee methodologies and working methods; provision of specialized advisory services to committee secretariats on procedural compliance, procedural questions, and Rules of Procedure application

• **Management and Strategic Coordination** (15%): Organization and management of follow-up activities for management meetings convened by Policy Directorates-General; ensuring coherent strategic alignment across the institution's operational structure

• **Inter-Institutional Relations and External Advocacy** (10%): Service as designated primary contact point for committees' relations with the Conference of Presidents, Bureau, and the Cabinets and secretariats of the President and Secretary General (5%); representation of committee interests through regular engagement and maintenance of contacts with the European Commission, Council, and other relevant external institutions and bodies (5%)

DSA30.name: DSA30
original_activities: Process the meeting requests for the Conference of Committee Chairs, extraordinary committee meetings, coordinators, trilogues, shadows, working and monitoring groups, missions and ad-hoc delegations, annual committee-related events, the Conference of Delegation Chairs, inter-parliamentary delegations, democracy support activities, technical and staff meetings of DGs ECTI, CASP, IUST, BUDG, EXPO and CCLA Directorate.
Establish the annual planning of ordinary committee meetings in close cooperation with the committee secretariats based on the committees' workflow, legislative and political priorities.
Manage meeting resources (rooms and interpretation teams), including handling of committee requests to use the hemicycle and providing access to the KOHL Secure Reading Room.

Liaise with the Directorate-General for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences for interpretation (bookings, modifications and cancellations of resources) and with other technical services in various DGs (in charge of Interactio, connection for remote speakers, electronic vote, technical equipment in the rooms, ushers, etc.).

Represent committee and delegation needs within the context of specific projects (e.g. renovation of the SPAAK building or creation of trilogue rooms in Strasbourg) with the aim of safeguarding meeting facilities during the renovation phase as well as new meeting facilities based on the analysis of the evolving meeting trends.

Ensure compliance with the applicable rules on meetings' slots (Code of Conduct on Multilingualism, Rules governing the holding of meetings simultaneously with plenary sittings, Rules governing meetings of the political groups) and handling the derogation requests in close cooperation with the President's Cabinet and the Secretary-General's Cabinet.
Provide a follow-up to the meetings in case of interpretation/technical incidents and ensuring business continuity.
DSA30.refined_description: DSA30 is a Directorate-General-level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary-General, functioning as the central administrative hub for parliamentary meeting coordination, resource management, and regulatory compliance across the European Parliament's comprehensive committee and delegation infrastructure. The unit exercises direct operational authority over meeting logistics, scheduling, resource allocation, and compliance management across all committee structures, delegations, inter-parliamentary bodies, and technical services originating from six specialized Directorates-General (ECTI, CASP, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, and CCLA) as well as the CCLA Directorate. DSA30 maintains essential liaison and coordination relationships with the Directorate-General for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences regarding interpretation resource management (bookings, modifications, and cancellations), and orchestrates technical service coordination across multiple DGs responsible for Interactio systems, remote speaker connectivity, electronic voting infrastructure, technical room equipment, and ushering services. The unit represents committee and delegation facility requirements within strategic infrastructure initiatives—including SPAAK building renovations and Strasbourg trilogue room development—with dual objectives of preserving operational meeting capacity during renovation phases while establishing enhanced facilities informed by evolving meeting trend analysis. DSA30 operates within a strict regulatory framework encompassing the Code of Conduct on Multilingualism, Rules governing simultaneous meetings during plenary sittings, and Rules governing political group meetings, with delegated authority to process derogation requests through coordinated engagement with both the President's Cabinet and the Secretary-General's Cabinet. All operational functions prioritize business continuity through comprehensive post-meeting follow-up procedures addressing interpretation and technical incidents.
DSA30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Meeting Request Processing & Coordination** (45%): Process and manage comprehensive meeting requests across the full spectrum of parliamentary bodies including the Conference of Committee Chairs, extraordinary committee meetings, coordinators, trilogues, shadow rapporteurs, working and monitoring groups, missions and ad-hoc delegations, annual committee-related events, the Conference of Delegation Chairs, inter-parliamentary delegations, democracy support activities, and technical/staff meetings originating from DGs ECTI, CASP, IUST, BUDG, EXPO, and the CCLA Directorate.

• **Strategic Planning & Resource Allocation** (30%): Establish annual planning frameworks for ordinary committee meetings in close collaboration with committee secretariats, ensuring alignment with committees' operational workflows, legislative agendas, and political priorities (20%); manage critical meeting resources including room allocation, interpretation team coordination, hemicycle utilization requests, and KOHL Secure Reading Room access (10%).

• **Technical Services Liaison & Infrastructure Coordination** (15%): Maintain essential liaison functions with the Directorate-General for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences regarding interpretation resource management and coordinate with technical service providers across multiple DGs responsible for Interactio systems, remote speaker connectivity, electronic voting infrastructure, technical room equipment, and ushering services (10%); represent committee and delegation facility requirements within strategic infrastructure projects such as SPAAK building renovations and Strasbourg trilogue room development, preserving operational meeting facilities during renovation phases while establishing enhanced facilities informed by evolving meeting trend analysis (5%).

• **Regulatory Compliance & Governance** (5%): Ensure strict adherence to applicable meeting regulations including the Code of Conduct on Multilingualism, Rules governing simultaneous meetings during plenary sittings, and Rules governing political group meetings, while processing derogation requests through coordinated engagement with both the President's Cabinet and the Secretary-General's Cabinet.

• **Incident Management & Business Continuity** (5%): Provide comprehensive post-meeting follow-up procedures addressing interpretation and technical incidents while maintaining uninterrupted business continuity across all parliamentary meeting functions.

DSA40.name: DSA40
original_activities: Promote committee activities by editing, drafting and publishing announcements on the committees' main page on Europarl; promote the use of multimedia products on their webpages and inter-linkages between the five policy DGs and COMM products; promote the coherence and quality of webpages and their development. Assist and support committee secretariats on communication issues, for example through the Communication network, and support the implementation of a coordinated visual approach.

Promote the work of Policy Departments and support units by managing publications on the Register and coordinating the production of Fact Sheets; support the units on editorial questions and to increase the visibility and accessibility of their work; support the implementation of the coordinated visual approach; produce common monthly newsletter and handouts for events; cooperate with DG ITEC/EPRS in developing IT solutions, such as the management tool for Fact Sheets.

Cooperate with DG COMM on communication actions: planning, coordination, information sharing and exchange of expertise; following the Bureau WG for Communication; coordinate the web streaming of public committee + delegation meetings and act as a contact point; support DG COMM in its Web streaming 4.0 project and the SEF project on EP Web Accessibility, participate and contribute to working groups and taskforces.

Cooperate with DG ITEC on the evolution of committee webpages, eCommittee sites and PETI Portal; manage other projects with DG ITEC, including improvements to search engines, the coordination of roll-out plans, and the organisation of trainings; assist in the continuing implementation of SEO for committees.

Support the improvement of CCLA's internal communication by managing and feeding CCLA's intranet site, including its news homepage; liaise with the EP intranet and Newshound services to promote CCLA-related news/activities; provide assistance for the production of the CCLA newsletter.

Coordinate the participation of the five policy Directorates-General for the 2025 Europe Day and EYE event, in coordination with the Directorate-General for Communication and the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Research Services, including in the elaboration of ideas, production of material and the invitation and coordination of volunteers.

Ensure the setting of editorial guidelines for communication products and tools; provide assistance/advice to the five policy DGs on communication needs, tools and editorial matters; provide support for the organisation of trainings tailored for committee communication activities.
DSA40.refined_description:
DSA40 functions as a specialized communication and digital coordination unit operating at the Directorate-General level within the European Parliament, reporting directly to the Secretary General. The unit serves as a critical strategic bridge between the five policy Directorates-General, parliamentary committees, the Directorate-General for Communication, and the Directorate-General for Information Technology and e-Services, ensuring coherent visual presentation, coordinated messaging, enhanced digital accessibility, and optimized information discoverability across parliamentary communication channels. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, DSA40 operates through direct collaboration and matrix relationships with peer units and parent directorates, positioning itself as a critical facilitator of institutional communication coherence and digital accessibility across the European Parliament's policy and committee infrastructure. The unit's operational mandate encompasses seven distinct but interconnected activity streams that collectively support both external stakeholder engagement and internal organizational communication needs. Through integrated activities spanning committee visibility enhancement, policy output amplification, digital infrastructure collaboration, strategic communication coordination, internal communication governance, institutional event orchestration, and editorial standards establishment, DSA40 ensures that parliamentary work is disseminated accessibly and coherently across multiple platforms while providing specialized communication advisory services to all policy directorates.

##
DSA40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee and Policy Department Visibility Enhancement** (40%): Promotion of committee activities through editing, drafting, and publishing announcements on Europarl's committees' main page with multimedia integration and inter-DG linkages (20%); management of Policy Department publications via the Register, Fact Sheet coordination, monthly newsletter production, event handout development, and editorial support to increase visibility and accessibility of parliamentary research outputs (20%).

• **Digital Infrastructure and Web Technology Coordination** (30%): Cooperation with DG ITEC on the evolution of committee webpages, eCommittee sites, PETI Portal management, search engine optimization implementation, training organization, and management of IT solution development partnerships including fact sheet management tools and accessibility enhancements (15%); collaboration with DG COMM on web streaming coordination for public committee and delegation meetings, participation in Web Streaming 4.0 and SEF (EP Web Accessibility) projects, contribution to Bureau Working Groups for Communication, and liaison on institutional communication frameworks (15%).

• **Institutional Communication Standards and Advisory Support** (10%): Establishment and maintenance of editorial guidelines for communication products and tools; provision of specialized advisory services to the five policy Directorates-General on communication needs, tools, strategies, and editorial matters; delivery of tailored training programs for committee communication activities and digital communication competencies.

• **Internal Communication Infrastructure and Institutional Liaison** (10%): Management and maintenance of CCLA's intranet site and news homepage; liaison with EP-wide intranet and Newshound services to promote CCLA-related news and activities; production and coordination of CCLA newsletter communications and internal communication support to policy directorates.

• **Strategic Outreach and Flagship Event Coordination** (10%): Multi-directorate coordination of the five policy Directorates-General participation in the 2025 Europe Day and European Youth Event in partnership with DG COMM and DG EPRS, encompassing ideation, material production, volunteer recruitment and management, and comprehensive cross-DG collaboration and event coordination activities.

DSA50.name: DSA50
original_activities: Follow the full lifecycle of the IT applications used by committees and delegations in the workflow of parliamentary documents, including business analysis, communication with developers, testing and offering training and assistance/helpdesk, in particular in relation to the Iter application that manages the workflow of committee activities and to the XML-based authoring tools for parliamentary documents in the context of the eLegislate project.

Support the Directorate and the five Policy Directorates-General in the identification of the common business needs of committee and delegation users, in their prioritisation based on feasibility and wider strategic considerations, and in the preparation and follow-up of proposals for the Agile IT Plan.
Offer a tailored newcomers' training to the new recruits of the Directorate and of the five Policy Directorates-General on issues of horizontal relevance.
Organise, in cooperation with the other units in the Directorate, recurrent and ad-hoc training sessions on the activities and processes supported by committee secretariats.

Compile and disseminate the functional knowledge base of the work of committee secretariats. Maintain updated documentation on the intranet, in cooperation with the other units in the Directorate and with other Directorates-General as appropriate.

Serve as a point of contact with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity for the Directorate and the five Policy Directorates-General, as appropriate, as well as with other DGs, in relation to parliamentary document production and workflow.
Represent or support the Directorate and/or the five Policy Directorates-General, as appropriate, in networks and groups related to the area of expertise of the unit.
DSA50.refined_description: DSA50 is a Directorate-General level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary General that functions as the primary technical and functional steward for parliamentary document workflows, committee operations systems, and knowledge management infrastructure within the European Parliament. The unit maintains comprehensive oversight of the full lifecycle management of IT applications utilized by committees and delegations, encompassing business analysis, developer coordination, quality assurance testing, user training delivery, and technical helpdesk support—with particular emphasis on the Iter application for committee workflow management and XML-based authoring tools within the eLegislate project framework. Operating as a strategic bridge between the Directorate and five Policy Directorates-General, DSA50 identifies, prioritizes, and advances common business requirements based on feasibility assessments and institutional strategic considerations, preparing and monitoring proposals for the Agile IT Plan. The unit delivers specialized onboarding training to new personnel across the Directorate and five Policy Directorates-General on horizontally-relevant institutional issues while organizing collaborative recurrent and ad-hoc training sessions with other Directorate units addressing committee secretariat activities and processes. DSA50 curates and disseminates the institutional functional knowledge base documenting committee secretariat operations through comprehensive, coordinated intranet documentation maintained with peer units and relevant Directorates-General. The unit serves as the designated liaison point with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, facilitating institutional coordination on parliamentary document production and workflow matters, while representing the institution in external networks and expert groups aligned with its specialized domain expertise.
DSA50.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Application Lifecycle Management & Technical Support** (35%): Full-cycle stewardship of committee and delegation IT applications including business analysis, developer communication, quality assurance testing protocols, user training delivery, and technical helpdesk assistance; specialized focus on Iter workflow management application and eLegislate XML-based document authoring tools

• **Strategic IT Planning & Requirements Coordination** (15%): Collaborative identification and prioritization of shared business requirements across the Directorate and five Policy Directorates-General; feasibility assessment and strategic alignment; preparation and monitoring of Agile IT Plan proposals aligned with institutional strategic objectives

• **Training & Capability Development** (30%): Delivery of tailored newcomer onboarding programs for staff across the Directorate and five Policy Directorates-General on cross-cutting institutional topics (15%); organization of recurring and ad-hoc collaborative training sessions with other Directorate units addressing committee secretariat processes and activities (15%)

• **Knowledge Management & Documentation** (10%): Compilation, curation, and dissemination of functional knowledge resources documenting committee secretariat operations; maintenance of updated intranet documentation through coordinated partnerships with peer Directorate units and external Directorates-General

• **Institutional Liaison & External Representation** (10%): Service as primary coordination point with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity and relevant Directorates-General regarding parliamentary document production and workflow matters (5%); representation and participation in specialized professional networks and working groups aligned with unit expertise (5%)

original_activities: Management of the entire mandate of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS) and its entities.
11.refined_description: Unit 11 serves as the apex strategic and operational management entity of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS) within the European Parliament, functioning at the root organizational level with direct accountability to the Secretary General. The unit exercises comprehensive stewardship over the entire institutional mandate of EPRS, representing 100% of documented workload allocation, and maintains unified governance of seven operationally distinct child units (11-20, 11-40, 11-50, 11A, 11B, 11C, and one additional unit) that collectively execute 32 distinct activities. Unit 11's singular but expansive mandate encompasses strategic direction, institutional coordination, policy oversight, resource allocation governance, and operational oversight across all downstream divisions, ensuring integrated organizational coherence and unified resource stewardship. The unit's responsibilities span governance of strategic coordination and stakeholder engagement functions (Unit 11-20), research services production and delivery (Unit 11-40), integrated human resources and administrative management (Unit 11-50), and four directional/operational leadership entities (Units 11A, 11B, 11C, and one additional unit). By consolidating mandate management at this hierarchical apex, Unit 11 translates institutional objectives into operational outcomes through systematic oversight of specialized research, analytical, and support services divisions. The unit's positioning as the sole root-level directorate-general management entity reflects its critical role in maintaining integrated oversight of research capacity, personnel administration, external stakeholder relations, and specialized research output across all thematic domains, thereby enabling the Parliament's core legislative and informational requirements.
11.refined_activity_summary: • **Institutional Mandate Management, Strategic Governance & Operational Leadership** (100%): Comprehensive management of the entire Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services mandate, encompassing strategic direction, institutional coordination, organizational policy oversight, resource allocation governance, unified stewardship of all EPRS entities and subordinate operational units, oversight of research capacity and specialized research output across all thematic domains, personnel administration, and external stakeholder relations to enable the European Parliament's legislative and informational requirements.

original_activities: Ensure strategy, innovation and horizontal coordination.
Identify and respond to Members' needs (including trainings).
Outreach to governmental organizations at all levels of the multi-level governance system (European, national, regional, local and global). Link with universities and academia.
Ensure multimedia, internal and external communication.
Ensure EPRS event organization.
11-20.refined_description: Unit 11-20 operates as the strategic coordination and stakeholder engagement hub within the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a first-level Direction (Level 1) under parent Unit 11. As a leaf node with no subordinate units, Unit 11-20 serves as the critical nexus for aligning institutional strategy, fostering innovation, and ensuring horizontal coordination across EPRS operations while simultaneously functioning as the primary interface between EPRS's research and analytical capabilities and the broader European institutional ecosystem. The unit maintains direct responsibility for identifying, analyzing, and systematically responding to the substantive needs of European Parliament Members through the design and delivery of targeted training programs and customized capacity-building initiatives. Unit 11-20 actively cultivates and maintains a comprehensive stakeholder engagement framework encompassing multi-level governance outreach to governmental organizations across European, national, regional, local, and global institutional tiers, while simultaneously establishing and strengthening collaborative relationships with universities and academic research centers. The unit ensures the effectiveness and cohesion of multimedia communications strategies, managing both internal organizational communications and external institutional messaging to amplify the visibility and accessibility of parliamentary research services across institutional and external audiences. Additionally, Unit 11-20 assumes responsibility for the logistical organization and execution of EPRS-sponsored events and conferences that facilitate knowledge-sharing and stakeholder convening. Operating in parallel coordination with sibling units (11-40, 11-50, 11A, 11B, 11C, and one additional unit), Unit 11-20 delivers integrated parliamentary research services and institutional support essential to EPRS's comprehensive institutional mandate.
11-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership, Organizational Development & Horizontal Coordination** (25%): Ensure strategy formulation and development, advance innovation initiatives, and establish horizontal coordination mechanisms across EPRS directorate operations and functions

• **Member Services & Capacity Building** (25%): Identify and systematically respond to Members' substantive institutional needs through tailored support services, targeted training programs, and comprehensive professional development initiatives

• **Multi-Level Stakeholder Engagement & Academic Partnership Development** (25%): Conduct comprehensive outreach to governmental organizations across the complete multi-level governance framework (European, national, regional, local, and global levels); establish and maintain strategic collaborative linkages with universities and academic research institutions

• **Integrated Communications & External Relations** (20%): Manage multimedia communication strategies, coordinate internal organizational communications, and oversee external institutional messaging to enhance visibility and stakeholder awareness across institutional and external audiences

• **Event Management & Institutional Convening** (5%): Organize and execute EPRS-sponsored events, conferences, and institutional programming that facilitate knowledge-sharing and stakeholder engagement

original_activities: Manage and support the production process of all EPRS publications and associated products, including workflow, product planning and development, editorial review, finalization and publication in all appropriate formats, and associated monitoring and reporting.
Through its dedicated support office, carry out high quality graphic and statistical work for publications and other EPRS products.

Develop, promote and ensure the application of quality standards and procedures common to all EPRS publications, including through the production of templates and style guides, IT tools and through the training of colleagues.
Ensure coordination and monitoring of the evolution of the 'Legislative Train Schedule' and the products derived from it.
Ensure management of the 'Members' Hotline'.
11-40.refined_description: Unit 11-40 operates as the Publications and Production Operations directorate within the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a specialized Direction-level entity under the strategic oversight of Unit 11. The unit serves as the operational execution hub for EPRS, orchestrating the comprehensive publication lifecycle across all institutional outputs while simultaneously establishing and enforcing quality standards organization-wide. Unit 11-40's multifaceted mission encompasses four primary functional domains: commanding 63% of operational workload through systematic management of workflow processes, product planning and development cycles, editorial review procedures, finalization protocols, multi-format publication delivery, and associated performance monitoring and reporting mechanisms; allocating 22% of resources to delivering high-quality graphic design and statistical analysis services through a dedicated support office that ensures visual and data integrity across all EPRS publications and derivative products; dedicating 7% of capacity to developing, promoting, and institutionalizing quality assurance standards and procedures applicable organization-wide, including the creation and maintenance of templates, style guides, IT tools, and colleague training programs that establish consistent publication excellence; and assuming responsibility for coordinating and monitoring the evolution of the Legislative Train Schedule and its derived products (5% of workload) while managing the Members' Hotline service function (3% of workload). Collectively, these activities position Unit 11-40 as a critical operational hub ensuring consistency, quality, accessibility, and direct stakeholder engagement across the European Parliament's research infrastructure.
11-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Publication Production and Lifecycle Management** (63%): Comprehensive management and support of all EPRS publication production processes, encompassing workflow administration and optimization, product planning and development, editorial review execution, finalization procedures, multi-format publication delivery, and associated monitoring and reporting activities
