• **Creative and Analytical Support Services** (22%): High-quality graphic design and statistical analysis work delivered through a dedicated support office to enhance visual presentation and data integrity across all EPRS publications and related derivative products

• **Quality Assurance and Institutional Standards Development** (7%): Development, promotion, and systematic application of standardized quality procedures applicable organization-wide, including creation of templates, style guides, IT tool development and provision, and professional training delivery to colleagues to establish consistent publication excellence

• **Legislative Tracking and Stakeholder Services** (8%): Dual-function responsibility encompassing coordination and monitoring of the Legislative Train Schedule and its derivative products (5%), combined with Members' Hotline management and direct support services to parliamentary stakeholders (3%)

original_activities: Ensure personnel management for CCLA and EPRS (selection and recruitment procedures, transfer of staff, secondments, exchanges, mobility exercises, professional orientation, management of positions in the organization chart, management of budgetary envelopes, working time, teleworking, leave and absences): civil servants, temporary agents, contract agents, seconded national experts, temporary workers, interns. Study visits. Rating and promotion.

Ensure the coordination of training for CCLA and EPRS (strategic plan for the DG, organization of tailor-made training, integration of new arrivals, external training, management of budgetary envelopes).
Provide opinions to the Deputy Secretary General and monitor decisions concerning the organization of the CCLA Directorate and DG EPRS.
Manage the missions of CCLA and EPRS staff (three workplaces, off-site, training) and ensure management of the budget envelope.
Coordinate office management with DG INLO.
Coordinate financial management for the CCLA Directorate.
Ensure management of staff well-being.
11-50.refined_description: Unit 11-50 operates as the integrated Human Resources and Administrative Management directorate serving the Constitutional and Legislative Affairs (CCLA) Directorate and the European Parliamentary Research Service (EPRS) within the European Parliament's institutional framework, functioning as a first-level operational support unit reporting to Unit 11 and positioned as a critical administrative and human capital steward. The unit exercises comprehensive personnel management responsibilities encompassing the complete employment lifecycle across all staff categories—civil servants, temporary agents, contract agents, seconded national experts, temporary workers, and interns—through selection and recruitment procedures, staff transfers, secondments, exchanges, mobility exercises, professional orientation, organizational chart position management, budgetary envelope administration, working time regulation, teleworking arrangements, leave and absence management, study visits coordination, and rating and promotion processes (55% of workload). Unit 11-50 orchestrates strategic training coordination by developing comprehensive strategic training plans aligned with DG-wide objectives, organizing tailored professional development programs, facilitating integration of new personnel, managing external training initiatives, and administering associated budgetary envelopes (10% of workload). The unit provides strategic advisory capacity to the Deputy Secretary General through monitoring and opinion-formulation regarding organizational decisions and structural arrangements affecting both the CCLA Directorate and DG EPRS (10% of workload). Operationally, Unit 11-50 manages staff mission deployments across three designated workplaces, off-site locations, and training venues while maintaining rigorous budget envelope oversight (10% of workload). Complementary responsibilities include coordination of office management services with DG INLO (5%), financial administration for the CCLA Directorate (5%), and comprehensive staff well-being programs and initiatives (5%), collectively ensuring holistic organizational support. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy operating in parallel with sibling units 11-20, 11-40, 11A, 11B, 11C, and one additional peer unit, Unit 11-50 functions as an essential administrative infrastructure supporting the Parliament's research and legislative operations.
11-50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Personnel Management and Staff Administration** (55%): Comprehensive management of all employment categories (civil servants, temporary agents, contract agents, seconded national experts, temporary workers, interns) encompassing selection and recruitment procedures, staff transfers, secondments, exchanges and mobility exercises, professional orientation, organizational chart position management, budgetary envelope administration, working time regulation, teleworking arrangements, leave and absence management, study visits coordination, and rating and promotion processes for both CCLA Directorate and DG EPRS personnel

• **Training and Professional Development Coordination** (10%): Strategic planning and execution of training initiatives including DG-aligned strategic plan development, organization of customized professional development programs, integration protocols for new staff arrivals, external training facilitation, and budgetary envelope management for CCLA and EPRS personnel

• **Strategic Advisory and Organizational Governance** (10%): Provision of opinions, recommendations, and strategic counsel to the Deputy Secretary General regarding organizational decisions, structural arrangements, and initiatives affecting the CCLA Directorate and DG EPRS operational frameworks

• **Missions, Mobility and Operations Management** (10%): Coordination and management of staff missions and deployments across three designated workplaces, off-site locations, and training venues, including training-related travel arrangements and associated budget envelope oversight

• **Cross-Functional Administrative Support** (15%): Office management coordination with DG INLO (5%), financial management and administration for the CCLA Directorate (5%), and staff well-being program management and initiatives (5%)

11A.name: 11A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the department and its units (six thematic units and two services), in their research and analysis mission on the basis of individual requests from Members or the provision of publications to them.
Ensure the strategic and operational management of cross-functional projects and those linked to digital technologies, within the EPRS.
Exercise the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer in the terms defined by the Director General.
Be involved in staff recruitment and evaluation procedures.
Cooperate with other services within Parliament and on an interinstitutional basis.
11A.refined_description: Unit 11A functions as the strategic direction and operational coordination hub of the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a first-level directorate reporting to Unit 11 and serving as the critical implementation interface between institutional research policy and operational evidence delivery to parliamentary decision-makers. The unit exercises comprehensive strategic stewardship over eight constituent organizational entities—comprising six thematic research units (11A0010, 11A0020, 11A10, 11A20, 11A30, 11A40) and two specialized services—ensuring their coordinated execution of research and analysis activities conducted on the basis of individual Member requests and systematic publication provision to parliamentary stakeholders. Unit 11A maintains organizational coherence across this subordinate structure while simultaneously managing cross-functional and digitally-enabled strategic projects throughout the EPRS ecosystem, driving technological integration and innovation across departmental boundaries and ensuring alignment with sibling directorate units (11-20, 11-40, 11-50, 11B, 11C, and one additional unit) within the broader DG structure. The unit exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions as defined by the Director General, maintaining comprehensive financial and administrative accountability and budgetary oversight. Additionally, Unit 11A participates actively in human resource management functions including staff recruitment procedures and personnel evaluation processes to sustain organizational capacity and human capital optimization, while fostering collaborative relationships with parallel services within the European Parliament and coordinating interinstitutional engagement to enhance EPRS institutional positioning and facilitate knowledge-sharing across institutional networks.
11A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Departmental Direction, Coordination, and Research Mission Delivery** (55%): Ensure strategic direction, coordination, and institutional animation of eight constituent units (six thematic research units and two specialized services) in executing their research and analysis mission based on individual Member requests and systematic publication provision to parliamentary stakeholders; serve as the operational nexus connecting parliamentary demand signals with research delivery capacity.

• **Digital Technology Integration and Cross-Functional Project Management** (25%): Exercise strategic and operational management of cross-functional initiatives and digitally-enabled projects throughout the EPRS ecosystem, ensuring technological integration, innovation, and project coordination across departmental boundaries while driving organizational modernization efforts.

• **Financial and Administrative Authority** (10%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer functions as defined by the Director General, maintaining comprehensive financial accountability, budgetary oversight, and administrative responsibility.

• **Human Resource Management and Organizational Development** (5%): Participate actively in staff recruitment procedures, personnel evaluation processes, and human capital management to ensure organizational capacity optimization and institutional sustainability.

• **Interinstitutional Cooperation and External Relations** (5%): Foster collaborative partnerships with parallel services within the European Parliament and coordinate cross-institutional engagement to strengthen EPRS institutional positioning, enhance research impact, and facilitate knowledge-sharing across institutional networks.

11A0010.name: 11A0010
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the area of ​​climate policies, including written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the field of climate policies (preparing a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, supporting public debate, organizing events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably DG ECTI, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies, and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A0010.refined_description: Unit 11A0010 is a specialized climate policy research and analysis unit within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a second-level organizational entity under the strategic direction of Unit 11A. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate components, 11A0010 collaborates within a broader cluster of specialized units (11A0020, 11A10, 11A20, 11A30, 11A40, and additional sibling units) that collectively constitute EPRS's comprehensive analytical capacity. The unit's operational structure encompasses three integrated functional areas that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, positioning it as a critical knowledge hub supporting evidence-based climate policy deliberation across the Parliament. This balanced operational configuration enables Unit 11A0010 to simultaneously fulfill immediate parliamentary information needs while advancing independent research capacity and strengthening the institutional networks essential to EPRS's strategic effectiveness within the broader European Parliament ecosystem.
11A0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Client-Responsive Research and Analysis Services (45%)**: Delivering written and oral responses to parliamentary stakeholder requests for research, analysis, and specialized information on climate policies; developing personalized analytical products tailored to specific institutional information needs and client requirements.

• **Proactive Research, Analysis, and Comprehensive Knowledge Dissemination (45%)**: Conducting independent research initiatives and generating comprehensive research, analysis, and synthesis products on climate policies; performing legislative tracking and assessment; managing and updating thematic content on the EPRS website; delivering professional training programs; supporting public debate participation and facilitation; and organizing specialized events, seminars, and forums.

• **Strategic Institutional Coordination, Relationship Management, and Organizational Development (10%)**: Establishing and maintaining collaborative networks with internal European Parliament services (including DG ECTI and political groups), external European institutions, academic institutions, and research-oriented think tanks; contributing to EPRS strategic product development, procedural enhancement, and customer awareness initiatives; supporting broader public debate initiatives; and ensuring compliance with editorial standards and rigorous quality control protocols across all unit outputs.

11A0020.name: 11A0020
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the area of ​​the Next Generation EU, including through written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the area of ​​the Recovery Plan for Europe - Next Generation EU (preparation of a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, support for public debate, organization of events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably DG BUDG, DG ECTI, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies, and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A0020.refined_description: Unit 11A0020 operates as a specialized research and analysis unit within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning at the second hierarchical level under the strategic direction of parent Unit 11A. The unit's primary mission centers on providing comprehensive, evidence-based expertise and analytical support regarding the Next Generation EU initiative and the Recovery Plan for Europe, positioning itself as a critical knowledge resource for parliamentary stakeholders engaged in economic recovery and cohesion policy deliberation. Through an integrated operational model, the unit strategically allocates its capacity across three interconnected functional domains: responsive client-centered services, proactive knowledge development, and strategic institutional coordination. This multifaceted approach ensures that the European Parliament maintains authoritative, current, and accessible knowledge resources on critical EU financial instruments and economic recovery mechanisms, while simultaneously strengthening internal and external stakeholder relationships and advancing broader EPRS strategic objectives.
11A0020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Responsive Client Research and Analysis Services (45%)**: Respond to parliamentary client requests for research, analysis, and information concerning Next Generation EU matters through written analyses, oral briefings, and customized analytical products tailored to specific parliamentary stakeholder needs and policy domains

• **Proactive Research, Knowledge Development, and Information Dissemination (45%)**: Execute forward-looking research initiatives and information activities encompassing the development of diverse analytical and synthesis products, comprehensive legislative analysis and reviews, maintenance and updating of thematic content on the EPRS website, delivery of specialized educational programming and training, facilitation of informed public debate, and organization of substantive events focused on the Recovery Plan for Europe and Next Generation EU frameworks

• **Strategic Partnerships, Institutional Coordination, and Organizational Development (10%)**: Establish and maintain strategic relationships with internal European Parliament directorates (notably DG BUDG and DG ECTI, political groups, and parliamentary structures) and external stakeholders (European institutions, peer research organizations, think tanks, and academic institutions); contribute to EPRS product development, procedural enhancement, and strategic initiatives; support customer awareness and public engagement activities; and maintain rigorous quality control and editorial standards compliance

11A10.name: 11A10
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the area of ​​economic policy, including written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the area of ​​economic policies (preparing a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, supporting public debate, organizing events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably DG ECTI, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia), and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies, and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A10.refined_description: Unit 11A10 is a specialized research and analytical unit within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a leaf-node operational entity under the strategic direction of Unit 11A and functioning in coordinated alignment with sibling units (11A0010, 11A0020, 11A20, 11A30, 11A40, and others) to deliver comprehensive parliamentary research services. The unit serves as a dedicated center of expertise for economic policy analysis and evidence-based parliamentary support, operating through a balanced dual-focused model that integrates reactive client responsiveness with proactive knowledge production. Unit 11A10 allocates 45% of its workload to delivering reactive client-responsive services, providing written and oral responses alongside personalized analytical products that address specific parliamentary requests for economic policy research and information. Concurrently, the unit dedicates 45% of its workload to proactive research and information initiatives, systematically generating a comprehensive range of research, analytical, and synthesis products including legislative assessments, continuous updating of thematic content on the EPRS website, professional development and training activities, facilitation of public debate support, and organization of specialized events and forums. The remaining 10% of workload encompasses strategic relationship management and institutional development activities, including cultivation and maintenance of collaborative partnerships with internal parliamentary services (particularly DG ECTI and political groups), external European institutions, academic institutions, and think tanks, while simultaneously contributing to the enhancement of EPRS products, operational procedures, organizational strategies, and customer engagement activities through rigorous quality assurance mechanisms and editorial standards compliance. Together, these interconnected activity streams position Unit 11A10 as a critical knowledge resource for the Parliament's economic policy deliberations, balancing responsive client service delivery with independent knowledge production and strategic institutional relationship governance.
11A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Client-Responsive Services (45%)**: Delivery of responsive, client-driven research, analysis, and information services in economic policy domains through written and oral communications; development of personalized analytical products tailored to specific parliamentary stakeholder requirements and institutional needs.

• **Proactive Research and Knowledge Production (45%)**: Execution of systematic, forward-looking research and analytical initiatives encompassing diverse research, analytical, and synthesis products; legislative analysis and assessment; website content management and thematic page updates; professional development and training program delivery; public debate facilitation and support; and organization of specialized events and forums.

• **Strategic Partnership Development and Institutional Governance (10%)**: Establishment and maintenance of collaborative relationships with internal parliamentary services (DG ECTI, political groups) and external stakeholders (European institutions, academic entities, think tanks, research organizations); contribution to EPRS organizational development including product enhancement, procedural optimization, and strategic initiatives; implementation of quality assurance mechanisms and editorial standards compliance; advancement of customer awareness activities; and support for public debate facilitation.

11A20.refined_description:

Unit 11A20 is a specialized research and analysis unit within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a second-level operational unit under the strategic direction of Unit 11A. The unit maintains a comprehensive dual-focused operational mandate centered on delivering evidence-based research, analytical, and informational support to European Parliament stakeholders in the critical domain of structural policies, encompassing regional development, transport and tourism, agriculture and rural development, fisheries, culture, and education. The unit's workload is strategically distributed across three complementary functional areas: (1) responsive, client-centered research services allocating 45% of resources to addressing parliamentary requests through written and oral responses, personalized analytical products, and specialized research tailored to specific stakeholder information needs; (2) proactive research and knowledge development activities allocating 45% of resources to conducting active research, developing comprehensive analytical products, performing legislative analysis, maintaining and enhancing thematic content on the EPRS website, delivering specialized training programs, and organizing substantive events designed to advance parliamentary knowledge and public understanding of structural policies; and (3) strategic institutional relationship management and organizational development allocating 10% of resources to cultivating collaborative networks with relevant European Parliament services, thematic Directorate Generals, political groups, other European institutions, academic institutions, and think tanks, while simultaneously supporting public debate engagement, contributing to EPRS product and procedural development, enhancing customer awareness initiatives, and ensuring consistent quality control and compliance with established editorial standards. Through this integrated approach combining reactive responsiveness and proactive knowledge advancement, Unit 11A20 functions as a critical knowledge hub supporting evidence-based parliamentary decision-making within its designated policy domains while maintaining professional standards and institutional coordination mechanisms that advance the broader institutional objectives of EPRS and the European Parliament.

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably DG IUST, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. 11A30.refined_description: Unit 11A30 operates as a specialized research and analytical service within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning at the second hierarchical level under the strategic direction of Unit 11A and positioned as a peer unit alongside units 11A0010, 11A0020, 11A10, 11A20, 11A40, and additional sibling units within the directorate structure. The unit's primary mission centers on delivering comprehensive, evidence-based research, analysis, and information services focused on citizen-related policy domains to support parliamentary decision-making and public engagement. Unit 11A30 allocates 45% of its workload to reactive service delivery, responding to client requests from Members of the European Parliament, parliamentary committees, and other institutional stakeholders through written responses, oral briefings, and personalized analytical products tailored to citizen-related policy domains. Concurrently, the unit dedicates 45% of its operational capacity to proactive research initiatives and information dissemination activities, encompassing the development and publication of diverse research and synthesis products with particular emphasis on legislative analysis and parliamentary scrutiny monitoring, maintenance and enhancement of thematic content on the EPRS website, provision of training and capacity-building services, facilitation of public debate, and organization of policy-focused events and seminars. The remaining 10% of the unit's workload is directed toward strategic relationship management and quality assurance functions, including the development and maintenance of collaborative networks with relevant internal services (particularly DG IUST and political groups), external European institutions, academic institutions, and think tanks, while simultaneously contributing to the continuous improvement and evolution of EPRS products, methodologies, procedures, and institutional strategies, supporting customer awareness initiatives, and ensuring compliance with rigorous editorial and quality control standards. Through this balanced allocation of resources and integrated activities, Unit 11A30 contributes substantively to the EPRS directorate's broader mandate of providing Parliament with high-quality, evidence-based research support while advancing informed public discourse on citizen-centered policy issues.
11A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Research and Analytical Services** (45%): Responding to client requests for research, analysis, and information on citizen-related policies from Members of the European Parliament, parliamentary committees, and institutional stakeholders; delivering written responses, oral briefings, and personalized analytical products tailored to specific parliamentary and policy needs

• **Proactive Research and Information Dissemination** (45%): Conducting independent research initiatives; developing and publishing comprehensive research, analysis, and synthesis products with emphasis on legislative analysis and parliamentary scrutiny monitoring; managing and updating thematic content on the EPRS website; delivering training and capacity-building programs; facilitating public debate; organizing policy-focused events and seminars to enhance parliamentary and public understanding of citizen-related policy issues

• **Strategic Relationship Management, Institutional Coordination, and Quality Assurance** (10%): Developing and maintaining collaborative networks with internal services (DG IUST, political groups) and external partners (European institutions, think tanks, academic organizations); contributing to the evolution of EPRS methodologies, products, procedures, and strategic frameworks; supporting customer awareness and engagement initiatives; maintaining rigorous editorial standards and quality control compliance

11A40.name: 11A40
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the field of European Union external policies, including through written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the policy area (preparing a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, supporting public debate, organizing events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably DG EXPO, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A40.refined_description: Unit 11A40 operates as a specialized research and analysis division within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a second-level organizational unit under the strategic direction of Unit 11A and positioned as a leaf node without subordinate units. The unit's primary institutional mission encompasses delivering comprehensive research, analytical expertise, and evidence-based information services on European Union external policies to parliamentary stakeholders, operating through three interconnected activity streams that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload. These operational approaches integrate reactive client-focused research services (45%)—encompassing written and oral responses to parliamentary requests alongside personalized policy analyses tailored to specific institutional and legislative contexts—with proactive research and synthesis operations (45%)—involving independent research initiatives, development of diverse research outputs, legislative assessments, thematic website content updates, educational programming, public engagement support, and event organization. The unit allocates the remaining 10% of capacity to strategic relationship management and quality assurance functions, including stakeholder coordination across internal parliamentary services (DG EXPO and political groups), external European institutions, academic networks, and think tanks, while simultaneously supporting public debate facilitation, contributing to EPRS product and procedural development, enhancing customer awareness initiatives, and maintaining compliance with rigorous editorial standards and organizational quality control mechanisms. These three interconnected activity streams collectively position Unit 11A40 as a critical institutional knowledge resource that bridges parliamentary information demands with authoritative, accessible, and timely policy intelligence on EU external affairs, thereby advancing EPRS's overarching mission to support evidence-based parliamentary decision-making while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units (11A0010, 11A0020, 11A10, 11A20, 11A30, and others).
11A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Client-Focused Research Services (45%)**: Deliver timely written and oral responses to parliamentary client requests for research, analysis, and information on European Union external policies; provide personalized analytical assessments and evidence-based intelligence tailored to specific institutional needs and legislative contexts.

• **Proactive Research, Synthesis & Knowledge Products (45%)**: Conduct independent research initiatives and information synthesis operations; develop comprehensive research outputs, legislative analyses, and thematic assessments; maintain and update specialized content on the EPRS website; deliver training and educational programming; support public debate and democratic engagement; organize policy-focused events and seminars.

• **Strategic Stakeholder Relations, Quality Assurance & Institutional Development (10%)**: Cultivate and maintain collaborative relationships with internal parliamentary services (DG EXPO, political groups), external partners (European institutions, think tanks, academic organizations), and public debate stakeholders; contribute to EPRS product development, procedural improvements, and strategic initiatives; implement customer awareness and engagement activities; ensure compliance with editorial standards and maintain appropriate quality control mechanisms.

11A50.name: 11A50
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the area of ​​budgetary policies, including written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the area of ​​budgetary policies (preparation of a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, supporting public debate, organizing events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably thematic DGs, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies, and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A50.refined_description: Unit 11A50 operates as a specialized research and analytical unit within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a second-level operational unit under the strategic coordination of Direction 11A. The unit executes an integrated dual-mandate operational model focused exclusively on budgetary policy domains, allocating 45% of its workload to reactive, client-driven research services that respond directly to parliamentary and external stakeholder requests through customized written responses, oral briefings, and personalized analytical products addressing budgetary policy questions. Concurrently, 45% of the unit's capacity is dedicated to proactive research and knowledge dissemination initiatives, encompassing the independent development and publication of diverse research and synthesis products, comprehensive legislative analysis, maintenance of thematic content on the EPRS digital platform, delivery of training programs, facilitation of public debate support, and organization of knowledge-dissemination events. The remaining 10% of organizational effort is allocated to stakeholder relationship development and strategic institutional advancement, specifically encompassing the cultivation and maintenance of collaborative contacts across multiple constituencies—including thematic Directorates General and political groups within Parliament, external European institutions, academic institutions, and think tanks—while simultaneously contributing to the advancement of EPRS organizational products, procedures, and strategic positioning, supporting customer awareness activities, and maintaining rigorous quality control and compliance with established editorial standards. Through this comprehensive, evidence-based approach, Unit 11A50 serves as both a responsive knowledge provider to parliamentary decision-makers and a proactive contributor to informed public discourse on European budgetary matters.
11A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Client Services and Personalized Analysis (45%)**: Responding to parliamentary and external stakeholder requests for research, analysis, and information in budgetary policy domains through delivery of customized written responses, oral briefings, and personalized analytical products tailored to specific institutional and policy needs.

• **Proactive Research, Knowledge Products, and Public Engagement (45%)**: Conducting independent research and information work in budgetary policy domains, including preparation and publication of diverse research and synthesis products, comprehensive legislative analysis, maintenance of thematic content on the EPRS digital platform, delivery of training programs, facilitation of public debate initiatives, and organization of knowledge-dissemination events.

• **Stakeholder Relations, Strategic Development, and Quality Assurance (10%)**: Developing and maintaining collaborative contacts with internal parliamentary services (thematic Directorates General, political groups), external European institutions, academic institutions, and think tanks; contributing to EPRS product and procedural strategy development; supporting customer awareness and institutional positioning activities; and ensuring compliance with quality control mechanisms and established editorial standards.

11A70.name: 11A70
original_activities: Respond to client requests for research, analysis and information in the area of ​​digital policy, including through written and oral responses and personalized analyses.

Carry out proactive research and information work as well as analysis in the field of digital policies (preparing a range of research, analysis and synthesis products, including in particular on legislation, updating thematic pages on the EPRS website, training, supporting public debate, organizing events).

Develop and maintain contacts with relevant services both inside and outside the EP (notably thematic DGs, political groups, other European institutions, think tanks and academia) and support for public debate. Contribute to the development of EPRS products, procedures and strategies, and customer awareness activities. Maintain an appropriate level of quality control and compliance with editorial standards.
11A70.refined_description: Unit 11A70 is a specialized research and analysis division within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a second-level hierarchical component under the strategic direction of Unit 11A. The unit delivers comprehensive expertise in digital policy research and analysis to support parliamentary decision-making, legislative processes, and informed public debate on digital governance issues. The unit's operational structure integrates three interconnected functional areas that collectively position it as a critical knowledge hub within the EPRS architecture: reactive client services responding to specific requests from Members of Parliament and institutional stakeholders through written and oral responses and customized analyses; proactive research and information initiatives encompassing original research product development, analytical syntheses, legislative assessments, thematic website content curation and maintenance, training program delivery, public engagement support, and specialized event organization; and institutional coordination with quality assurance functions that cultivate strategic relationships across the European Parliament's internal services and political groups, other European institutions, think tanks, and academic organizations while contributing to the broader development of EPRS organizational products, procedures, strategies, customer engagement activities, and editorial standards compliance. These integrated activities enable Unit 11A70 to bridge parliamentary research requirements with emerging digital policy challenges while maintaining rigorous quality control across all outputs.
11A70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reactive Client Services and Personalized Analysis** (45%): Respond to Member of Parliament and institutional stakeholder requests for research, analysis, and information on digital policy matters; deliver both written and oral responses; produce customized analytical products and tailored analyses addressing specific parliamentary and institutional needs.

• **Proactive Research, Analysis, and Knowledge Dissemination** (45%): Conduct independent research initiatives and develop comprehensive research products, analytical syntheses, and legislative assessments; curate and maintain thematic digital policy website content on EPRS platforms; deliver training programs and capacity-building activities; support public debate engagement; organize specialized events and stakeholder engagement activities within the digital policy domain.

• **Institutional Coordination, Strategic Development, and Quality Assurance** (10%): Develop and maintain collaborative relationships with relevant internal European Parliament services (including thematic directorates general and political groups) and external partners (European institutions, think tanks, and academic organizations); contribute to the advancement of EPRS organizational procedures, product development strategies, and customer awareness initiatives; ensure quality control and editorial standards compliance across all unit outputs.

11B.name: 11B
original_activities: Manage and coordinate the Directorate and its Units and the directorate's reporting instruments, metrics, archives.
Coordinate, manage and assess specific projects such as the Infohub library services for citizens.

Coordinate collaboration with other services within the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Research Services and the European Parliament, concerning communication, logistics, event management and interpretation, training and all human resources issues - from missions to training requests.
Promote relevant inter-institutional cooperation and library partnerships, prepare and follow-up on director's missions.
Support occasionally exceptional activities of the Library units such as moves, large events, participation in open days.
Fulfill the tasks of authorizing officer by sub-delegation. Mission now mainly carried out by the Digital Library Unit. Secretarial support for the large procedures only.
11B.refined_description:
Unit 11B functions as the strategic coordination and administrative nucleus of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), serving as a Direction-level entity positioned as the essential administrative and coordinative backbone linking parent Unit 11 with its five subordinate units (11B0010, 11B10, 11B20, 11B40, and 11B50) and sibling units (11-20, 11-40, 11-50, 11A, and 11C) across the EPRS institutional landscape. The unit dedicates 30% of its operational capacity to comprehensive directorate management and coordination, including oversight of all subordinate units, maintenance of directorate-wide reporting instruments, performance metrics, and institutional archives systems. Unit 11B allocates 40% of its workload across two interconnected operational domains: strategic project coordination and management of specialized citizen-facing initiatives such as the Infohub library services platform (20%), and facilitation of cross-institutional collaboration mechanisms encompassing communication protocols, logistics operations, event management, interpretation services, training administration, and comprehensive human resources management covering mission assignments and professional development requests within EPRS and the broader European Parliament framework (20%). An additional 20% of resources is dedicated to promoting inter-institutional cooperation and library partnerships while preparing, executing, and conducting follow-up activities related to director-level missions and strategic engagements. The remaining 10% comprises occasional operational support for Library unit exceptional activities including facility relocations, large-scale institutional events, and public engagement initiatives such as parliamentary open days (5%), alongside delegated financial authorization officer responsibilities with primary execution now transferred to the Digital Library Unit (11B20) and selective secretarial support limited to major administrative procedures requiring senior authorization (5%).

##
11B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Governance and Administrative Management** (30%): Comprehensive management and coordination of the Directorate and all subordinate units (11B0010, 11B10, 11B20, 11B40, 11B50); maintenance of directorate-wide reporting instruments, performance metrics, and institutional archives systems

• **Strategic Project Coordination and Citizen Services** (20%): Oversight, management, and assessment of specialized initiatives including the Infohub library services platform designed to enhance citizen and parliamentary stakeholder access to information resources

• **Cross-Institutional Collaboration and Human Resources Administration** (20%): Coordination and management of collaboration mechanisms with EPRS and European Parliament entities encompassing communication protocols, logistics coordination, event management, interpretation services, staff training programs, and comprehensive human resources administration spanning mission assignments and professional development requests

• **Inter-Institutional Partnerships and Leadership Mission Support** (20%): Promotion of inter-institutional cooperation and library partnerships; preparation, execution, and follow-up coordination of director-level missions and strategic engagements

• **Operational Support and Financial Administration** (10%): Provision of occasional operational support for exceptional library unit activities including facility relocations, large-scale institutional events, and public engagement initiatives such as parliamentary open days (5%); fulfillment of delegated financial authorization officer responsibilities with primary execution by Digital Library Unit (11B20) and secretarial support exclusively for major administrative procedures requiring senior authorization (5%)

11B0010.name: 11B0010
original_activities: Conduct, initiate, and commission research on the history of the European Parliament. HIST research has a primary empirical historical focus and is based on published and archival sources including the European Parliament's own historical parliamentary documents, those of the political groups, private papers, etc.

Produce and coordinate different types of outputs based on the research, including studies, briefings, etc. as an integral part of EPRS outputs. HIST outputs are delivered in a variety of EPRS established and complementary formats.

Propose, organize and contribute to the organization of relevant EPRS research and presentation events (online, hybrid or onsite) including European Parliament history roundtables, book talks, events on the historical evolution of EP/EU policies, on former European Parliament presidents, analysing the contributions and parliamentary activities of political groups and Members.
Engage with academia, building a network of academics at all levels in Europe and beyond.
11B0010.refined_description:
**

Unit 11B0010 functions as a specialized historical research and knowledge dissemination entity within the European Parliament's Research Services (EPRS), operating as a Level 2 operational unit subordinate to Direction 11B. The unit's integrated mission encompasses advancing institutional knowledge and public understanding of the European Parliament's historical development, institutional evolution, and parliamentary practices through rigorous empirical historical research grounded in primary and secondary sources—including parliamentary archives, political group records, published materials, and private collections—which directly informs its production of multifaceted research outputs systematically integrated into EPRS's established publication and dissemination frameworks. To maximize scholarly impact and accessibility, Unit 11B0010 actively proposes, organizes, and contributes to specialized research and presentation events across multiple modalities (online, hybrid, and onsite), encompassing European Parliament history roundtables, author presentations on parliamentary publications, thematic seminars examining the historical evolution of EP/EU policy frameworks, commemorative events honoring former EP presidents, and analytical presentations on the institutional contributions of political groups and individual Members. Simultaneously, the unit cultivates and maintains strategic academic partnerships and networks with European and international scholarly communities at multiple institutional levels, thereby positioning EPRS as a credible interlocutor within broader academic discourse on European institutional history. As a leaf-node unit within Direction 11B operating cohesively alongside sibling units 11B10, 11B20, 11B40, and 11B50, Unit 11B0010 contributes specialized historical expertise to the coordinated research and analytical operations of the broader Parliamentary Research Services directorate.

**
11B0010.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Historical Research and Archival Analysis** (45%): Conduct, initiate, and commission comprehensive empirical historical research on European Parliament institutional development; leverage published sources, parliamentary archives, European Parliament historical documents, political group records, and private papers to establish evidence-based historical narratives

• **Research Output Production and Knowledge Dissemination** (30%): Produce and coordinate diverse output formats including analytical studies, policy briefings, and complementary formats; ensure systematic integration of historical research findings into established EPRS publication channels and dissemination frameworks

• **Public Engagement Events and Knowledge Amplification** (15%): Propose, organize, and actively contribute to specialized research and presentation events across online, hybrid, and onsite modalities; deliver European Parliament history roundtables, author talks on parliamentary publications, thematic seminars on historical policy evolution, commemorative events for former EP presidents, and analytical presentations examining the institutional contributions and parliamentary activities of political groups and individual Members

• **Academic Networking and Strategic Institutional Partnerships** (10%): Engage strategically with academic institutions and scholars across Europe and internationally; build and maintain comprehensive networks of research collaborators at all academic levels to strengthen EPRS credibility within academic discourse on European institutional history

11B10.name: 11B10
original_activities: Manage the front office – Reading rooms, daily service operations and customer relations in Brussels and Strasbourg; manage reading corners. Welcoming different categories of visitors and researchers. Presentation of the collections and production of the EPRS and the European Parliament through different multi-media means and the creation of small exhibitions.
Collaborate in the management of requests for information and guarantee the application of professional norms and standards to these requests.
Coordinate the management of collections of books, electronic books and periodicals; define and implement the collection development policy.
Provide training for clients on European Union information sources and public policies. Contribute to the preparation of teaching materials for these training courses.
Participate in the organization of events.

Coordinate tasks shared with other library units (Infodesk, training) and participate in the preparation of reading selections. Ensure cooperation with the online digital library for subscription management.
Collaborate with libraries of other European institutions, participation in EUROLIB.
Coordinate and ensure the quality of cataloging and indexing procedures.
11B10.refined_description: Unit 11B10 operates as a specialized library and information services unit within Direction 11B of the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as the primary operational and service-delivery interface between the institution's research infrastructure and diverse user communities across Brussels and Strasbourg locations. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses front-office management and collection stewardship, direct reader services delivery, information request administration, collections coordination, knowledge dissemination, institutional collaboration, and quality assurance functions that collectively support EPRS's research and information accessibility objectives while maintaining professional standards consistent with European institutional library best practices. Unit 11B10 manages reading rooms and daily service operations at multiple locations, receives and supports visitors and researchers across varying categories, administers reading corners, presents Parliament's collections and EPRS resources through multimedia formats and curated exhibitions, and maintains institutional promotion activities. The unit collaborates in information request management while ensuring adherence to professional norms and standards, coordinates the strategic management of physical and electronic collections (books, e-books, and periodicals), implements collection development policy, and oversees cataloging and indexing quality assurance procedures. Unit 11B10 delivers specialized client training on European Union information sources and public policy frameworks with accompanying instructional materials development, organizes events supporting institutional knowledge dissemination, coordinates inter-library task sharing with the Infodesk and training functions, manages digital library subscription coordination, and engages with European institutional library networks through EUROLIB participation. These integrated activities position Unit 11B10 as a critical operational entity that directly enables research accessibility, maintains information quality standards, and strengthens institutional interoperability across the parliamentary research ecosystem.
11B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Reader Services and Front-Office Operations** (30%): Management of reading rooms and daily service operations in Brussels and Strasbourg locations; visitor and researcher reception across multiple categories; reading corner administration; collection and EPRS resource presentation through multimedia channels; curatorial exhibition development and institutional promotion

• **Information Request Management and Quality Assurance** (15%): Collaborative administration of information requests; application and maintenance of professional norms and standards; quality assurance of information service delivery

• **Collections Management and Development** (15%): Coordination of books, electronic books, and periodicals management; definition and implementation of collection development policy; curation and acquisition oversight

• **Cataloging, Indexing, and Information Retrieval Standards** (5%): Coordination of cataloging and indexing quality assurance procedures; strengthening institutional interoperability and retrieval standards

• **Knowledge Dissemination and Training** (10%): Provision of client training on European Union information sources and public policy frameworks; development and preparation of associated educational materials and instructional resources

• **Institutional Coordination and Inter-Library Operations** (25%): Event organization supporting knowledge dissemination and stakeholder engagement (10%); coordination of shared library tasks including information desk operations, training coordination, and reading selection preparation (10%); digital library subscription management cooperation; collaborative engagement with European institutional libraries through EUROLIB participation (5%)

11B20.name: 11B20
original_activities: Manage the digital resources of the European Parliament Library and Library web pages. Coordination and management of subscriptions and acquisitions of digital and paper resources. Coordination of the Advisory Board on Acquisitions. Coordination of the Library Management System (Alma/Primo VE) and implementation of specific library tools and applications with a view to improving the user experience and ease of access to digital resources. Production and coordination of selected online readings.

Coordinate, manage and monitor the budget allocated to the Library in accordance with the principles of sound financial management. Organise and supervise all procurement procedures for the subscription to digital and paper resources and acquisition of related materials and services in compliance with the principle of cost-effectiveness. Signature and management of contracts and licenses and validation of expenses.

Contribute to shared activities of the Library units, in particular, Info Desk tasks, trainings, selection of resources, cataloguing, deployment of RFID in the Reading Room and implementation of the Library pilot project at the Info Hub.

Coordinate the implementation of the European Parliament Open Digital Library Project. Assessment and deployment of state-of-the-art technology, cutting-edge capabilities and AI-powered tools to complete and consolidate the digital transformation of the European Parliament Library.
Collect and analyse statistics on the activities of the Library and the Directorate. Produce and coordinate the Digital Reading Report and contribute to reporting activities at Directorate level.
Supervise compliance with data protection rules and copyright for the Library Management System and for digital resources.
Promote inter-institutional cooperation at European Union-level and partnerships with relevant stakeholders on matters related to digital resources and services.
11B20.refined_description: Unit 11B20 operates as the Digital Resources and Library Systems Management Unit within Direction 11B of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), serving as a specialized operational hub for the European Parliament Library's strategic digital infrastructure, technological advancement, and comprehensive resource stewardship. As a leaf node unit positioned alongside sibling units 11B0010, 11B10, 11B40, and 11B50 under the coordination of parent unit 11B, Unit 11B20 executes a multifaceted mission encompassing the full lifecycle management of the Parliament's digital and physical library collections—from acquisition and subscription administration through innovative digital transformation—while maintaining rigorous financial stewardship and ensuring compliance with sound institutional governance principles. The unit's operational portfolio spans five integrated functional domains: digital resources and systems management (30%), budget management and procurement operations (25%), collaborative library services and operations (20%), digital transformation and technological innovation (15%), and compliance, reporting, and institutional partnerships (10%), collectively advancing the Parliament's capacity to deliver accessible, technologically sophisticated parliamentary research and information services to institutional stakeholders.
11B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Digital Resources and Library Systems Management** (30%): Comprehensive stewardship of the European Parliament Library's digital and physical resource collections; coordination and management of subscription and acquisition processes for both digital and paper resources; oversight of the Advisory Board on Acquisitions; administration and optimization of the Library Management System (Alma/Primo VE); development and implementation of specialized library tools and applications designed to enhance user accessibility and experience; production and coordination of curated online reading materials.

• **Budget Management and Procurement Operations** (25%): Coordination, monitoring, and management of the Library's budget allocations in accordance with sound financial management principles; organization and supervision of all procurement procedures for resource subscriptions and acquisitions; enforcement of cost-effectiveness standards across procurement activities; signature, administration, and management of contracts and licenses; validation, processing, and oversight of all Library-related expenses and service provisions.

• **Collaborative Library Services and Operations** (20%): Contribution to shared Library unit activities including Information Desk operations; provision and coordination of user training programs and staff training initiatives; participation in resource selection and cataloguing activities; deployment and management of Radio-Frequency Identification (RFID) technology in the Reading Room; implementation and support of the Library pilot project at the Information Hub; strengthening institutional service delivery through inter-unit coordination.

• **Digital Transformation and Technological Innovation** (15%): Strategic coordination of the European Parliament Open Digital Library Project; assessment and strategic deployment of state-of-the-art technologies and cutting-edge digital capabilities; integration and implementation of artificial intelligence-powered tools and solutions; consolidation and advancement of the Library's digital transformation infrastructure and technological agenda.

• **Compliance, Reporting, and Institutional Partnerships** (10%): Collection and analysis of Library and Directorate activity statistics (5%); production and coordination of the Digital Reading Report and Directorate-level reporting activities (5%); supervision of data protection rules and copyright compliance for the Library Management System and digital resources (3%); promotion of inter-institutional cooperation and stakeholder partnerships with relevant European Union stakeholders regarding digital resources and services (2%).

11B40.name: 11B40
original_activities: Receive, draft and send responses to citizens' letters addressed to the European Parliament or the President (including coordinated campaigns).
Write, update and publish the 'Model Answers to citizens' inquiries' to help MPs and their teams respond to the messages they receive from citizens.
Check the quality of the responses sent to citizens.
Provide statistical reports on the unit's activities (notably 'Facts & Figures' published on the intranet).
11B40.refined_description: Unit 11B40 functions as the Citizens' Correspondence and Response Management Office within Direction 11B of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a specialized leaf-level operational entity positioned at Hierarchy Level 2 without subordinate structures. The unit serves as a critical institutional interface between the European Parliament, its President, and the citizen constituency, managing direct communication channels and ensuring timely, high-quality responses to public inquiries while coordinating with sibling units 11B0010, 11B10, 11B20, and 11B50. Unit 11B40's core mission encompasses the comprehensive receipt, drafting, and transmission of official institutional responses to citizens' letters and communications addressed to the European Parliament or the Office of the President, including the management of both individual inquiries and coordinated campaign correspondence, which constitutes 75% of its operational workload and represents its primary strategic function. In support of parliamentary communication effectiveness, the unit dedicates 20% of its workload to developing, maintaining, and publishing the standardized 'Model Answers to Citizens' Inquiries' resource, which serves as essential reference guidance enabling Members of Parliament and their administrative teams to formulate consistent, accurate, and efficient constituent responses. The unit maintains rigorous quality assurance mechanisms by allocating 3% of its operational capacity to systematic verification and quality control of responses transmitted to citizens, ensuring compliance with parliamentary communication standards and institutional accuracy protocols. Additionally, Unit 11B40 contributes 2% of its workload to generating and disseminating statistical reports and analytical summaries documenting unit activities and performance metrics, including the 'Facts & Figures' publication distributed through the parliamentary intranet, thereby providing organizational transparency and performance monitoring. Collectively, these integrated functions position Unit 11B40 as an essential operational service provider that directly supports the European Parliament's institutional commitment to democratic responsiveness and constituent accessibility while providing critical administrative support to parliamentary members and their teams.
11B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Citizens' Correspondence Processing and Institutional Response Management** (75%): Receipt, drafting, and transmission of formal responses to citizens' letters and communications directed to the European Parliament and the Office of the President, encompassing both individual inquiries and coordinated campaign correspondence handling, with full compliance to institutional reply protocols

• **Parliamentary Communication Standards and Guidance Development** (20%): Development, maintenance, and publication of the 'Model Answers to Citizens' Inquiries' reference resource designed to support Members of Parliament and parliamentary staff in formulating standardized, consistent, and effective responses to constituent communications

• **Quality Assurance and Response Verification** (3%): Implementation of systematic quality control mechanisms and verification procedures to assess the quality, accuracy, and compliance of responses transmitted to citizens, ensuring adherence to parliamentary communication standards and institutional accuracy requirements

• **Institutional Performance Reporting and Analytics** (2%): Generation and publication of statistical reports, performance summaries, and analytical data regarding unit activities and performance metrics, including the 'Facts & Figures' publication disseminated through parliamentary intranet systems for organizational transparency and performance monitoring

11B50.name: 11B50
original_activities: Serve as a center of knowledge and expertise on the law of the European Union, its Member States and other comparable States and international organizations, as well as on relevant international law, from a comparative law perspective, in particular by means of the publication of studies and the organization of conferences for their presentation and by collaborating and establishing contacts with partners in external institutions and in the EP services.
Organize an annual comparative law forum and other events and coordinate related studies and their versions in five languages.
Actively engage in research and analysis of comparative law.
Identify and organize material and sources to build a broad collection of legal references on comparative law, including in the digital domain.
11B50.refined_description: Unit 11B50 functions as the Comparative Law Centre of Excellence within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating as a specialized analytical unit subordinate to Direction 11B. The unit's core mission encompasses establishing and maintaining an authoritative knowledge hub on European Union law, Member State legal systems, comparable international jurisdictions, and relevant international legal frameworks from an explicitly comparative law perspective, thereby serving as critical intellectual infrastructure supporting evidence-based parliamentary decision-making. This multifaceted mission is operationalized through four integrated and complementary activities: (1) serving as a knowledge repository and expertise center through systematic publication of specialized comparative law studies, organization of scholarly conferences for presentation and debate, and establishment of collaborative partnerships with external institutions and other European Parliament services (40% of workload); (2) organizing and coordinating an annual comparative law forum alongside supplementary thematic events, while simultaneously managing the translation and dissemination of related research studies across five languages to ensure multilingual accessibility throughout the European Parliament's linguistic communities (40% of workload); (3) actively conducting primary research and comparative law analysis that generates original analytical capacity to support parliamentary deliberations, policy development processes, and institutional expertise development (10% of workload); and (4) systematically identifying, organizing, curating, and maintaining legal reference materials, primary sources, and digital resources to develop and sustain a comprehensive collection supporting comparative legal research, immediate parliamentary needs, and long-term institutional knowledge management (10% of workload). These complementary activities collectively position Unit 11B50 as a critical knowledge infrastructure component within EPRS, contributing to the broader research and information provision mandate of Direction 11B through rigorous comparative legal analysis and systematically organized expertise.
11B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Knowledge Hub, Expertise Development, and Stakeholder Engagement** (40%): Establishment and maintenance of authoritative center of expertise on EU law, Member State legal systems, comparable international jurisdictions, and international law from comparative perspectives; publication of specialized comparative law studies; organization of conferences for scholarly presentation and debate; development and maintenance of collaborative partnerships with external institutions and European Parliament services

• **Events Organization and Multilingual Dissemination** (40%): Annual organization of comparative law forum and supplementary thematic events; coordination and management of related research study translations and versions across five languages to ensure broad accessibility and multilingual dissemination throughout the European Parliament

• **Primary Research and Comparative Analysis** (10%): Active engagement in original comparative law research and analysis; generation of specialized analytical work supporting parliamentary deliberations, policy development processes, and institutional expertise development

• **Resource Curation and Digital Infrastructure Development** (10%): Systematic identification, organization, and curation of legal reference materials and primary sources; development and maintenance of comprehensive comparative law collections in both digital and traditional formats supporting immediate parliamentary research needs and long-term institutional knowledge management

11C.name: 11C
original_activities: Manage, coordinate and lead the directorate and its six units in the following areas: impact assessment (ex-ante and ex-post) and European added value, and foresight and technological impact assessment.
Manage projects in the areas of responsibility of the directorate's units.
Act as authorising officer by subdelegation for the six units.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-DG committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., and representing the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those bodies.
11C.refined_description: Unit 11C serves as the strategic directorate and primary governance entity within the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), exercising comprehensive leadership and coordination authority over six specialized subordinate units (11C10, 11C20, 11C30, 11C40, 11C60, and 11C70). As a Hierarchy Level 1 Direction unit positioned within the parent Unit 11 structure—itself the apex strategic and operational management entity of the EPRS directorate-general—Unit 11C maintains essential vertical integration while exercising delegated authority across its constituent units, thereby ensuring coherent strategic direction and effective resource stewardship throughout the directorate. The unit's primary operational mandate encompasses the steering, coordination, and leadership of specialized institutional functions across three interconnected analytical domains: ex-ante and ex-post impact assessment methodologies, European added value assessment, and foresight initiatives with technological impact assessment capabilities, thereby enabling systematic evaluation of legislative proposals and emerging policy challenges. In executing this mandate, Unit 11C manages substantive projects within the directorate's areas of responsibility, exercises financial and administrative authority as authorising officer by subdelegation for all six constituent units, and maintains integrated budgetary oversight and compliance mechanisms across the directorate's operational portfolio. Additionally, Unit 11C represents the directorate and, when required, the entire Directorate-General within intra-directorate, inter-directorate-general, and interinstitutional committee structures, working groups, and representative forums, thereby positioning EPRS within broader European institutional governance structures and ensuring alignment with cross-institutional policy coordination mechanisms. These integrated functions ensure that impact assessment, European added value analysis, and foresight capabilities are coherently directed, effectively resourced, and strategically aligned with institutional priorities.
11C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Directorate Leadership and Coordination** (60%): Manage, coordinate, and lead the directorate and its six specialized units (11C10, 11C20, 11C30, 11C40, 11C60, 11C70) across ex-ante and ex-post impact assessment, European added value assessment, and foresight and technological impact assessment initiatives, serving as the primary strategic direction-setting entity for all impact evaluation and anticipatory governance activities within EPRS

• **Project Management and Financial Administration** (30%): Manage substantive projects within the directorate's areas of responsibility (15%) and exercise authorising officer authority by subdelegation for all six constituent units (15%), ensuring integrated project execution, budgetary oversight, and financial compliance across the directorate's operational portfolio

• **Institutional Representation and Inter-organizational Coordination** (10%): Participate in intra-directorate, inter-directorate-general, and interinstitutional committee structures, working groups, and representative forums, representing the directorate and Directorate-General to ensure alignment with broader European Parliament institutional priorities and cross-institutional policy coordination mechanisms

11C10.name: 11C10
original_activities: Assist parliamentary committees with the scrutiny of the Commission's ex-ante impact assessment work, notably by monitoring Commission legislative and impact assessment planning, automatically providing Initial Appraisals of the quality of the European Commission's Impact Assessments accompanying legislative proposals, and, on request, providing Detailed Appraisals.

Assist parliamentary committees with their own ex-ante impact assessment work, notably by providing, on request, substitute and complementary impact assessments of Commission legislative proposals, and impact assessments of substantial amendments.
Manage tender procedures - in the context of the previous roles, in order to secure the necessary expertise and ensure the quality of work provided.

Provide a central source of information and advice within Parliament on impact assessment and related procedures, and to promote awareness, both internally and externally, of the role of ex-ante impact assessment in the legislative cycle, related inter-institutional commitments and Parliament's activities in this area.

Liaise with the other European Union institutions, as well as Member State bodies and other fora, in view of exchanging and deepening knowledge, best practices, and methodology relating to ex-ante impact assessment and Better Regulation more generally.
11C10.refined_description: Unit 11C10 functions as the European Parliament's specialized centre of expertise for ex-ante impact assessment scrutiny and Better Regulation coordination, operating as a subordinate unit within the directorial coordination structure of Unit 11C (European Parliament Research Services/EPRS Direction). The unit's primary institutional mission encompasses dual-track support to parliamentary committees: rigorous scrutiny of the European Commission's ex-ante impact assessment activities through continuous monitoring and systematic appraisal delivery, and direct assistance with Parliament's own independent impact assessment capacity for legislative proposals and substantial amendments. Operating within a strategic directorial framework, Unit 11C10 strengthens Parliament's evidence-based legislative scrutiny capacity while simultaneously advancing inter-institutional coordination on regulatory quality and Better Regulation principles. The unit maintains technical excellence through specialized procurement management, functions as Parliament's central information and advisory hub on impact assessment methodologies and legislative procedures, and actively engages in knowledge exchange with EU institutions, Member State bodies, and international fora. Through this comprehensive allocation of institutional resources and capabilities, Unit 11C10 positions itself as a critical mechanism for enhancing Parliament's legislative scrutiny capacity and promoting evidence-based policymaking across the European Union institutional framework.
11C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Commission Impact Assessment Scrutiny & Quality Assurance (50%)**: Continuous systematic monitoring of Commission legislative and impact assessment planning; automatic provision of Initial Appraisals evaluating the quality of Commission Impact Assessments accompanying legislative proposals; delivery of Detailed Appraisals upon parliamentary committee request—representing the unit's primary focus area for ensuring rigorous ex-ante evaluation of Commission proposals

• **Parliamentary Impact Assessment Support & Development (30%)**: On-request provision of substitute and complementary impact assessments of Commission legislative proposals; delivery of impact assessments of substantial parliamentary amendments—enabling parliamentary committees to conduct independent evidence-based legislative analysis
