• **Procurement Management & Quality Assurance (10%)**: Management of tender procedures to secure specialized external expertise; maintenance of sustained quality standards across all impact assessment deliverables and institutional work products

• **Knowledge Management, Information Services & External Advocacy (5%)**: Central advisory function and information sourcing on impact assessment methodologies, procedures, and legislative cycle integration; promotion of awareness regarding ex-ante impact assessment's institutional role, inter-institutional commitments, and Parliament's activities to both internal and external stakeholders

• **Inter-Institutional Coordination & Best Practice Exchange (5%)**: Liaison and knowledge-exchange activities with EU institutions, Member State bodies, and relevant international fora; advancement of shared understanding and deepening of knowledge regarding ex-ante impact assessment best practices, methodologies, and Better Regulation principles across the EU institutional framework

11C20.name: 11C20
original_activities: Provide analyses on the future (foresight) for the European Parliament, draft and publish strategic and policy foresight papers; steer Strategic Foresight Conversations (expert consultations, internal workshops, interim reports, final reports); organise related meetings with Members and events. Generate and coordinate analytical work, based on analysis and mapping of risks, vulnerabilities, capabilities, opportunities and gaps of/for the European Union (with other EPRS entities).

Facilitate future-proof policy-making (anticipatory governance) in the European Parliament. Identify and analyse medium- and long-term trends, foster foresight literacy, promote the thinking on and understanding of these trends, challenges and choices within the EP, including through tailor-made training, and support Members in their policy-making responsibilities. Promote the use of foresight methodology alongside the policy cycle, from ex-ante impact assessment to ex-post evaluation.

Support Parliament's contribution to the inter-institutional ESPAS foresight network, including preparation of the annual ESPAS Conference, provide the secretariat to the ESPAS Steering Group, and contribute to other ESPAS-related activities (Global Trends Report process, project team meetings, horizon scanning, Young Talent Network, publications, international outreach, and representation of the ESPAS network at outside events).

Reach out to, network and cooperate with other European Union institutions, national foresight bodies and other strategic and policy planning organisations at European Union and national levels and with global partners, including think tanks and universities (including Foresight club).
11C20.refined_description:
**

Unit 11C20, the Strategic Foresight and Anticipatory Governance division within the European Parliament Research Services (EPRS), operates under the directorial coordination of parent Unit 11C with a dual mandate to generate forward-looking strategic intelligence for the European Parliament and institutionalize future-proof policy-making methodologies across the institution. The unit executes this mandate through four interconnected and sequentially coordinated activity streams that collectively position it as the Parliament's principal mechanism for embedding strategic anticipation and long-term thinking into institutional decision-making processes. Activities 1 and 2 represent equally weighted core functions (35% each) focused on producing comprehensive foresight analyses and strategic policy papers while simultaneously advancing anticipatory governance through trend analysis, institutional capacity-building, and systematic integration of foresight methodologies across the complete policy cycle from ex-ante impact assessment through ex-post evaluation. Activity 3 (25% of workload) positions the European Parliament within the inter-institutional ESPAS (European Strategy and Policy Analysis System) foresight network through secretariat functions, conference coordination, and active participation in Global Trends Report development, horizon scanning, Young Talent Network activities, and international representation. Activity 4 (5% of workload) establishes and maintains strategic partnerships with peer European Union institutions, national foresight bodies, policy planning organizations, academic institutions, think tanks, and international partners through structured external engagement mechanisms. Collectively, these activities transform the European Parliament's institutional capacity to anticipate, analyze, and respond proactively to future challenges and opportunities affecting the European Union.

---

**
11C20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Strategic Foresight Analysis and Intelligence Production** (35%): Develop and disseminate comprehensive foresight analyses and strategic policy papers; design and execute Strategic Foresight Conversations incorporating expert consultations, internal workshops, and iterative reporting cycles; coordinate analytical work mapping EU-level risks, vulnerabilities, capabilities, opportunities, and institutional gaps in collaboration with other EPRS units; organize member-facing meetings, thematic events, and parliamentary proceedings; generate systematic intelligence through risk and opportunity mapping conducted in conjunction with organizational partners.

• **Anticipatory Governance and Institutional Capacity-Building** (35%): Identify and analyze medium- and long-term trends affecting EU policy domains; promote foresight literacy and critical understanding of emerging challenges and policy choices among Members and staff; deliver tailor-made training interventions on foresight methodologies and trend analysis; systematically integrate forward-looking approaches throughout the complete policy cycle from ex-ante impact assessment through ex-post evaluation; support Members' policy-making responsibilities through direct advisory engagement and methodological guidance.

• **Inter-Institutional ESPAS Network Coordination and Representation** (25%): Provide secretariat functions to the ESPAS Steering Group and coordinate the annual ESPAS Conference preparation and delivery; contribute actively to Global Trends Report development and horizon scanning initiatives; support Young Talent Network development and publications management; conduct international outreach and represent the ESPAS network at external forums and events; maintain the European Parliament's active participation in the inter-institutional European Strategy and Policy Analysis System.

• **Strategic Partnership Development and External Engagement** (5%): Establish and maintain cooperative relationships with European Union institutions, national foresight bodies, and strategic planning organizations at European and national levels; engage with global partners including universities, academic institutions, think tanks, and specialized foresight clubs; facilitate knowledge exchange and methodological alignment across European and international foresight ecosystems; sustain the Foresight club network as a mechanism for peer learning and collaborative intelligence-gathering.

11C30.name: 11C30
original_activities: Research questions in the field of evaluation of scientific and technological choices and scientific foresight.
Manage events/meetings (annual conference, ad hoc workshops and conferences, meetings with the European Commission, etc.).
Carry out the activities of the European Science-Media Hub as a center for exchange, analysis and training for better scientific communication.
Organize the work of the Bureau and the Panel.
Manage internal and external communication.
Develop relationships with external experts, journalists, research institutes and universities; external STOA delegations.
Manage the financial aspects of studies and events.
11C30.refined_description: Unit 11C30 operates as a specialized analytical, operational, and coordination hub within the European Parliament Research Services (EPRS), functioning under the strategic direction of its parent unit 11C and serving as a critical interface between parliamentary research functions and the broader scientific, media, expert, and institutional communities. The unit's integrated mission encompasses advancing science-technology policy analysis, facilitating enhanced scientific communication, and providing evidence-based analytical support for parliamentary decision-making processes while maintaining essential operational and governance functions. Operating as an essential strategic interface complementing its sibling units (11C10, 11C20, 11C40, 11C60, 11C70), Unit 11C30 delivers comprehensive research and evidence services through a carefully balanced portfolio of activities that positions it as both a specialized analytical entity and an operational nexus for institutional coordination. The unit's workload distribution reflects its dual mandate: sustaining core research and analytical functions, managing extensive stakeholder engagement and knowledge exchange platforms, operationalizing the European Science-Media Hub as a center for scientific communication capacity-building, ensuring effective institutional governance and oversight, and maintaining the strategic relationships and financial stewardship necessary to support all programmatic activities. Collectively, these functions position Unit 11C30 as an indispensable operational and strategic component of EPRS, directly enhancing the Parliament's capacity to assess emerging technological and scientific developments while strengthening evidence-informed public discourse across European institutional and societal spheres.
11C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Research & Scientific Foresight Analysis** (20%): Conducting research questions in the field of evaluation of scientific and technological choices and scientific foresight to support evidence-based parliamentary decision-making and the Parliament's capacity to assess emerging technological and scientific developments

• **Events Management & Stakeholder Engagement** (20%): Managing a comprehensive portfolio of events and meetings, including annual conferences, ad hoc workshops, and strategic engagements with the European Commission, to facilitate knowledge exchange, institutional dialogue, and stakeholder coordination

• **Science Communication & Hub Operations** (15%): Operating the European Science-Media Hub as a dedicated center for exchange, analysis, and training initiatives designed to enhance scientific communication capabilities, strengthen evidence-informed public discourse, and build capacity across institutional and external networks

• **Governance & Institutional Coordination** (15%): Organizing the operational and administrative work of the Bureau and Panel structures to ensure effective institutional functioning, governance processes, and oversight coordination

• **External Relations, Communications & Financial Management** (30%): Managing internal and external communication strategies (10%), developing and maintaining strategic relationships with external experts, journalists, research institutes, universities, and coordinating external STOA delegations (10%), and overseeing the financial management and fiscal responsibility for studies and events (10%)

11C40.name: 11C40
original_activities: Assist parliamentary committees in their ex-post evaluation work by providing 'European Implementation Assessment' studies when committees undertake Implementation Reports (INI).

Assist parliamentary committees in their ex-post evaluation work by providing 'Implementation Appraisals' on existing legislation which the Commission's Annual Work Programme proposes to update or amend.

Assist parliamentary committees in their ex-post evaluation and scrutiny work by providing 'Rolling Check-List' studies and their relevant complementary digital interface PERFORM, with key reference material on Commission evaluations, review clauses in EU legislation and in EU international agreements.

Follow-up on the Inter-Institutional Agreement on Better Law-Making, in particular its chapter III on 'tools' (impact assessment and ex-post evaluation) : internal European Parliament working groups, liaising with the Commission and its Regulatory Scrutiny Board, conferences, etc.
Assist parliamentary committees in their ex-post evaluation work by providing other ex-post evaluations not linked to implementation reports.
Liaise with relevant European Union and other external partners on both substantive issues and methodological and procedural questions.
Manage tender procedures in order to secure external expertise as necessary, in the context of roles 1 and 5.
11C40.refined_description:
**

Unit 11C40 functions as the European Parliament's specialized analytical hub for ex-post legislative evaluation and implementation scrutiny, operating as a subordinate entity within Unit 11C (the directorial coordination framework of the European Parliament Research Services). The unit's primary institutional mission is to enable parliamentary committees to conduct rigorous, evidence-based assessment of EU legislation implementation effectiveness and regulatory impact through the provision of methodologically sound analytical products and evaluative frameworks. Unit 11C40 delivers this mandate through seven interconnected operational activities that collectively translate parliamentary legislative scrutiny mandates into actionable assessment mechanisms: providing European Implementation Assessment studies (30% of workload) supporting committee Implementation Reports (INI procedures); furnishing Implementation Appraisals (20% of workload) on legislation targeted for Commission amendment or update; supplying Rolling Check-List studies and the complementary PERFORM digital interface (15% of workload) that consolidate Commission evaluations, legislative review clauses, and international agreement provisions; maintaining active engagement with the Inter-Institutional Agreement on Better Law-Making framework, particularly chapter III provisions on impact assessment and ex-post evaluation tools, through internal European Parliament working groups, Commission Regulatory Scrutiny Board liaison, and conference organization (15% of workload); delivering supplementary ex-post evaluation studies not linked to implementation reports (10% of workload); facilitating substantive and methodological partnership relationships with EU and external institutional organizations (5% of workload); and managing tender procedures to procure specialized external expertise supporting analytical capacity (5% of workload). These activities collectively position Unit 11C40 as a critical institutional mechanism for enhancing the European Parliament's democratic oversight capacity and evidence-based decision-making within its committee structure.

**
11C40.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Core Implementation Assessment and Evaluation Delivery** (50%): Provision of European Implementation Assessment studies supporting committee Implementation Reports (INI procedures) (30%); delivery of Implementation Appraisals on legislation targeted for Commission amendment or update (20%), representing the unit's primary analytical function for evidence-based legislative scrutiny.

• **Evaluative Infrastructure, Reference Materials, and Digital Systems** (15%): Development and maintenance of Rolling Check-List studies and the PERFORM digital interface, synthesizing Commission evaluations, EU legislative review clauses, and international agreement provisions to support systematic committee scrutiny and reference consolidation.

• **Inter-Institutional Coordination on Regulatory Quality and Law-Making Standards** (15%): Active engagement with the Inter-Institutional Agreement on Better Law-Making chapter III provisions on impact assessment and ex-post evaluation tools, including facilitation of internal European Parliament working groups, liaison with the Commission and its Regulatory Scrutiny Board, and organization of specialized conferences.

• **Supplementary Evaluation and External Partnership Management** (15%): Delivery of additional ex-post evaluation studies outside implementation report frameworks (10%); substantive and methodological liaison with EU and external institutional partners on evaluative and analytical matters (5%).

• **External Expertise Procurement and Capacity Enhancement** (5%): Management of structured tender procedures to secure specialized external analytical expertise supplementing in-house capacity in support of core evaluation and supplementary evaluation activities.

11C60.name: 11C60
original_activities: Monitor and analyse the delivery of the European Council in respect of the commitments made in the Conclusions of its meetings in the form of a regular Rolling Check-List of commitments to date.
Prepare thematic papers analysing the activities of and delivery by the European Council in specific policy areas.
Provide systematic and timely briefings ahead of and after every European Council through Outlook and Outcome briefings.
Enter European Council conclusions into the ECOS Rolling Check-List database (part of Perform database) which is now accessible to Members and services.
Liaise with other institutions and bodies in the European Union and Member States (such as Permanent Representations) on substantive issues and methodological questions.
Assist the European Parliament President in preparation of European Council meetings.
Prepare and organise events on the role of the European Council and its place in the European Union institutional system.
11C60.refined_description: Unit 11C60 functions as the European Council Monitoring and Analysis Division within the European Parliament Research Services (EPRS), operating under the directorial coordination of parent Unit 11C to provide comprehensive institutional intelligence, analytical support, and oversight mechanisms focused on European Council activities, commitments, and policy implementation. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses systematic monitoring and documentation of European Council commitment delivery, thematic policy analysis across substantive domains, timely executive briefing services, technical database administration, and inter-institutional coordination—collectively enabling the European Parliament to exercise robust oversight of European Council governance and performance across the full legislative and budgetary cycle. Unit 11C60 serves as a critical institutional accountability mechanism by maintaining the Rolling Check-List of European Council commitments, ensuring continuous performance assessment and transparency regarding fulfillment of successive Council conclusions. The unit's analytical products and briefing services (Outlook anticipatory analysis and Outcome post-meeting assessment) provide Members with structured, timely intelligence necessary for informed parliamentary engagement with European Council priorities and outcomes. Through substantive liaison with EU institutions, Member State Permanent Representations, and other stakeholders, combined with direct support to the European Parliament President and facilitation of institutional forums addressing the European Council's constitutional role, Unit 11C60 integrates operational intelligence functions with strategic institutional positioning and advocacy support. The unit's work within the ECOS Rolling Check-List database system (Perform platform) ensures transparent, accessible information infrastructure for Members and institutional services, operationalizing the European Parliament's accountability function regarding European Council institutional dynamics and policy delivery.
11C60.refined_activity_summary:
• **European Council Commitment Monitoring and Delivery Tracking** (25%): Systematic monitoring and analysis of European Council commitment fulfillment through maintenance and regular updating of a Rolling Check-List derived from European Council Conclusions, enabling continuous institutional accountability and comprehensive performance assessment across successive meetings.

• **Thematic Policy Analysis and Institutional Assessment** (25%): Development and preparation of comprehensive thematic analytical papers examining European Council activities, institutional performance, and policy delivery outcomes across specific sectoral, cross-cutting, and substantive policy domains of parliamentary interest.

• **Executive Briefing Intelligence Services** (20%): Provision of timely, structured institutional briefings preceding and following each European Council meeting, delivered through Outlook briefings (anticipatory analysis of forthcoming Council priorities) and Outcome briefings (post-meeting assessment and implications analysis for parliamentary action).

• **Data Management, Database Administration, and Technical Systems** (10%): Technical database management and administration of European Council conclusions within the ECOS Rolling Check-List system integrated into the Perform platform, ensuring transparent accessibility and information infrastructure for Members and institutional services.

• **Inter-Institutional Coordination and Stakeholder Engagement** (10%): Substantive and methodological liaison with EU institutions, bodies, Member State Permanent Representations, and relevant stakeholders on policy matters, procedural approaches, and institutional positioning.

• **Presidential Support and Institutional Engagement** (5%): Direct administrative and analytical support to the European Parliament President in preparation for European Council meetings, ensuring executive-level intelligence availability for institutional leadership.

• **Institutional Events, Forums, and Constitutional Positioning** (5%): Organization and facilitation of institutional events, seminars, and forums addressing the European Council's constitutional role, systemic positioning within the EU institutional framework, and governance architecture implications.

11C70.name: 11C70
original_activities: Draw up European Added Value Assessments to support parliamentary committees in their work on legislative initiative reports, based on Article 225 TFEU.

Provide analytical support to parliamentary committees in the form of Cost of Non-Europe Reports on policy areas or sectors of strategic importance where there is significant scope for greater efficiency or for the realisation of a collective public good through common action at European Union level.
Manage procurement of external expertise as required for studies via the framework contract, negotiated procedure or call for expression of interest list.

Develop and promote a consistent methodology for the analysis and quantification of European added value. Develop the 'European Added Value in Action' Briefing series on existing and possible future benefits of European Union policies for European citizens.
Monitor the Commission's follow-up to EP Legislative Initiative Reports, as well as other tasks.
11C70.refined_description: Unit 11C70 operates as the European Parliament's specialized analytical unit within the European Parliament Research Services (EPRS) directorate 11C, dedicated to quantifying and communicating the European Added Value (EAV) generated through EU-level policy action across diverse Union policy domains. The unit fulfills a critical institutional function by providing empirical, methodologically rigorous assessments that inform parliamentary legislative initiatives and strategic policy deliberations, serving as a peer unit alongside 11C10, 11C20, 11C30, 11C40, and 11C60 within the directorial structure. Unit 11C70's primary analytical focus (80% combined allocation) centers on two complementary functions: the preparation of European Added Value Assessments substantiating parliamentary committees' legislative initiative reports under Article 225 TFEU procedures (40% of workload), and the production of Cost of Non-Europe Reports examining strategically important policy sectors and economic domains where collective EU-level action demonstrably achieves greater efficiency gains, realizes collective public goods, and generates benefits unattainable through member state-level action alone (40% of workload). To operationalize these core analytical functions, the unit manages external expertise procurement through framework contracts, negotiated procedures, and expressions of interest mechanisms (5-10% of workload, depending on analytical demand cycles). Concurrently, Unit 11C70 develops, maintains, and institutionalizes consistent methodological frameworks for analyzing and quantifying European added value across diverse policy contexts, while producing the 'European Added Value in Action' briefing series to communicate existing and prospective benefits of EU policies to European citizens in accessible formats (5% of workload). The unit additionally monitors European Commission follow-up implementation responses to EP Legislative Initiative Reports and executes supplementary institutional tasks assigned by directorial leadership (5% of workload).
11C70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Analytical Assessment Functions (80%)**: Preparation of European Added Value Assessments substantiating parliamentary committees' legislative initiative reports under Article 225 TFEU procedures (40%), and generation of Cost of Non-Europe Reports analyzing strategically important policy sectors demonstrating efficiency gains and collective public goods achievable through EU-level coordination (40%)

• **External Expertise Procurement Management (5-10%)**: Management and procurement of specialized external expertise through framework contracts, negotiated procedures, and calls for expression of interest to support required analytical studies and evidence-based assessments

• **Methodological Development and Public Communication (5%)**: Development, promotion, and institutionalization of consistent analytical methodologies for European Added Value quantification and analysis; creation and dissemination of 'European Added Value in Action' Briefing series communicating existing and prospective benefits of EU policies to European citizens

• **Institutional Monitoring and Supplementary Functions (5%)**: Monitoring European Commission implementation responses to European Parliament Legislative Initiative Reports and execution of additional assigned institutional tasks within the EPRS directorial framework

11D.name: 11D
original_activities: Provide the Director General and the Directorates of the DG with assistance in financial and IT matters.
Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of management units.
Perform the function of subdelegated authorizing officer for the allocated budget lines.
Ensure representation of general management in inter-DG meetings and other relevant forums.
Contribute to the coordination of annual reports, annual work program and indicators, as appropriate.
11D.refined_description: Unit 11D functions as the strategic administrative and operational support nucleus of the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), operating at the Direction level within the organizational hierarchy as a subordinate entity of Unit 11 (the apex strategic and operational management entity). The unit serves as the principal support mechanism for executive leadership and inter-directorate coordination, maintaining direct supervisory relationships with two specialized child units—Unit 11D20, which provides financial and administrative governance functions, and Unit 11D40, which manages information technology operations and support—while operating as a peer entity alongside sibling units 11-20, 11-40, 11-50, 11A, 11B, and others within the broader organizational structure. Unit 11D's primary mission encompasses comprehensive support to the Director General and all Directorates across financial and information technology domains (50% of workload), directional leadership, coordination, and animation of subordinate management units to ensure coherent operational alignment (25% of workload), exercise of delegated budgetary authority through subdelegated authorizing officer functions for allocated budget lines while maintaining fiscal oversight and compliance (10% of workload), and representation of general management interests in inter-directorate meetings and relevant institutional forums to serve as the liaison mechanism between EPRS and broader European Parliament governance structures (10% of workload). Additionally, the unit dedicates 5% of its capacity to coordinating annual reports, annual work programs, and performance indicators across the parent unit structure, facilitating strategic planning and institutional accountability. Collectively, these activities position Unit 11D as an essential management backbone that bridges executive leadership requirements with operational implementation across the research services directorate, ensuring organizational transparency and strategic alignment throughout its span of control.
11D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Support and Financial-Administrative Services (50%)**: Provision of comprehensive financial and information technology assistance to the Director General and all Directorates within EPRS, enabling strategic decision-making and operational infrastructure management across the organization

• **Directional Leadership and Management Unit Coordination (25%)**: Direction, coordination, and animation of subordinate management units (11D20 and 11D40), ensuring coherent operational alignment and coherent operational functioning across the management structure

• **Budget Authority and Financial Governance (10%)**: Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for allocated budget lines, maintaining fiscal oversight, regulatory compliance, and financial control within delegated authority parameters

• **Inter-Institutional Representation and Stakeholder Engagement (10%)**: Representation of general management interests in inter-directorate meetings and relevant institutional forums, maintaining organizational visibility and coordination at cross-departmental levels while serving as liaison mechanism between EPRS and broader European Parliament governance structures

• **Strategic Planning and Institutional Reporting Coordination (5%)**: Coordination of annual reports, annual work programs, and organizational performance indicators to support strategic planning objectives, organizational transparency, and institutional accountability mechanisms

11D20.name: 11D20
original_activities: Check the 'ex-ante' budget.
Verify and supervise public procurement and general management contracts.
Provide financial advice and internal control.
Contribute to the preliminary draft of the budget, monitor budget execution, as well as monitoring the BUDG and CONT Commissions.
Develop reports, monitor external control bodies, archiving, manage IT tools.
Represent general management in different forums, notably in the Inter-DG steering group and the FMP.
11D20.refined_description: Unit 11D20 operates as the General Management and Financial Oversight Unit within Direction 11D of the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), positioned at the second hierarchical level to provide essential administrative and financial stewardship across the directorate. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses rigorous financial governance, budgetary control, and operational oversight through six interconnected activity streams that collectively constitute the unit's complete workload. As a critical operational support component, Unit 11D20 functions as both a preventive financial control mechanism and an active coordinator within broader institutional governance structures, ensuring that Direction 11D's administrative operations align with European Parliament financial regulations, audit requirements, and institutional governance standards. The unit exercises specialized technical expertise in financial advisory services, internal control mechanisms, and procurement compliance, while simultaneously maintaining active liaison with parliamentary oversight structures and cross-directorate governance forums. Through integrated management of budget verification, procurement supervision, financial advisory functions, budget development coordination, and institutional representation, Unit 11D20 ensures financial transparency, operational efficiency, and institutional accountability while embedding EPRS perspectives in cross-directorate policy discussions and governance coordination.
11D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Ex-Ante Budget Review and Fiscal Compliance** (30%): Preventive budget verification and validation procedures ensuring pre-authorization fiscal integrity, budgetary conformance, and compliance with European Parliament financial regulations — representing the unit's highest priority function for rigorous financial scrutiny

• **Procurement and Contract Management** (25%): Verification and supervision of public procurement procedures and general management contracts — safeguarding institutional resources, ensuring expenditure compliance, and maintaining contractual integrity across directorate operations

• **Financial Advisory Services and Internal Control Mechanisms** (20%): Provision of specialized financial advice and implementation of internal control frameworks — supporting evidence-based organizational financial decision-making and control system effectiveness

• **Budget Development, Execution Monitoring, and Parliamentary Liaison** (10%): Substantive contribution to preliminary budget draft development, continuous monitoring of budget execution performance, and active engagement with BUDG (Budget) and CONT (Control) Commissions — integrating unit operations with parliamentary oversight structures and ensuring institutional accountability

• **Reporting, External Monitoring, Systems Administration, and Inter-Directorate Coordination** (15%): Development of specialized analytical reports, monitoring of external control bodies' findings, management of archival systems, administration of IT tools supporting financial operations, and representation of general management interests within the Inter-DG steering group and Financial Management Platform (FMP) — maintaining institutional transparency, operational infrastructure, and horizontal coordination across directorate governance

11D40.name: 11D40
original_activities: Manage applications in use: life cycle management of personalized or specialized software applications in use: evolutionary maintenance, training and support, testing to guarantee functionality and security, alignment with accessibility and data protection standards, management of access rights.

Provide End User Support: Provide assistance, resolve technical issues encountered by users of IT systems, proactively communicate issues and solutions, troubleshoot hardware and software issues, offer advice, and ensure a positive user experience.

Manage Projects: Planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality: project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management and communication with stakeholders.

Manage infrastructure, IT assets and access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management, ensuring the availability, reliability and security of IT resources.

Supervise and coordinate business analysis: understand business needs and objectives, translate them into IT solutions (collection and analysis of needs, facilitation of communication between stakeholders and recommendation of effective technological solutions).

Manage IT portfolios, including requested projects (from/centralized), ongoing projects, and existing applications specific to General Management including activities concerning financial initiation linked to project budget lines and decentralized IT support.

Manage computer and information security: approach and measures to protect the organization's IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities. Design and implementation of secure IT architectures, risk and vulnerability assessments, awareness of cyber security best practices and implementation of protective measures against security threats and breaches.

Develop the IT strategy of the General Directorate, and contribute to the IT strategy of Parliament through participation in the Inter-DG Steering Group for IT, working groups and steering committees.
11D40.refined_description:
Unit 11D40 operates as the Information Technology Operations and Applications Management unit within Direction 11D of the European Parliament's Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services (EPRS), functioning as a critical second-level operational support nucleus without subordinate units. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive IT service delivery, infrastructure stewardship, strategic technology alignment, and governance across the General Management domain, with 100% documented workload allocation distributed across nine integrated functional dimensions. As a leaf node within the 11D Direction structure and operational sibling to Unit 11D20, Unit 11D40 delivers reliable, secure, and user-centered IT services through dual core operational pillars: comprehensive lifecycle management of personalized and specialized applications in active use—encompassing evolutionary maintenance, user training and technical support, functionality and security testing, accessibility and data protection standards compliance, and access rights administration—and full-spectrum End User Support addressing technical issues, proactive communication, hardware and software troubleshooting, user advisory services, and experience optimization. The unit simultaneously executes IT project management encompassing planning, execution, supervision, resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication within defined scope, time, budget, and quality constraints; manages IT infrastructure, assets, and access provisioning to ensure organizational resource availability, reliability, and security; supervises and coordinates business analysis activities translating organizational needs into IT solutions through stakeholder engagement and technological recommendations; and maintains IT portfolio governance including centralized and decentralized project oversight, ongoing initiatives, General Management-specific applications, and associated financial and budget line administration. Unit 11D40 further embeds specialized capabilities in computer and information security—encompassing threat and vulnerability protection, secure IT architecture design, risk assessments, and cyber security best practices implementation—and contributes strategically to institutional IT governance through General Directorate IT strategy development and active participation in Parliament's Inter-DG Steering Group for IT, working groups, and steering committees.

##
11D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core IT Service Delivery Operations** (50%): Management of applications in use encompassing complete lifecycle administration, evolutionary maintenance, user training and technical support, functionality and security testing, accessibility and data protection standards compliance, and access rights administration (25%); provision of comprehensive End User Support including technical issue resolution, proactive communication, hardware and software troubleshooting, user advisory services, and user experience optimization (25%)

• **IT Project and Infrastructure Management** (25%): Planning, execution, and supervision of IT projects with resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication within defined scope, time, budget, and quality parameters (15%); planning, provisioning, maintenance, and monitoring of IT infrastructure encompassing hardware, software, network components, and user access management systems to ensure organizational resource availability, reliability, and security (10%)

• **Strategic Business Analysis and IT Portfolio Governance** (16%): Supervision and coordination of business analysis activities translating organizational needs into IT solutions through stakeholder engagement and technological recommendations (8%); management of IT portfolios encompassing centralized and decentralized project requests, ongoing initiatives, and General Management-specific applications with financial initiation and budget line administration (8%)

• **Computer and Information Security Management** (5%): Design and implementation of secure IT architectures, risk and vulnerability assessments, cyber security best practices implementation, and protective measures against security threats and breaches to protect organizational IT systems, data, and information assets

• **Directorate and Parliamentary IT Strategy Development** (4%): Development of General Directorate IT strategy and contribution to Parliament-wide IT strategy through participation in Inter-DG Steering Group for IT, working groups, and steering committees to ensure institutional technology alignment and governance

original_activities: Advise, assist directly and monitor the decisions of all bodies (CPC, CPD, Multilateral Parliamentary Assemblies) and their Presidents; parliamentary work and organization of internal and external activities.

Represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament and in internal meetings of the General Secretariat and ensure their monitoring; represent the general management in relations with other institutions and with the representations of third countries to the European Union.

Coordinate (pre and post) legislative and control issues related to the (sub)committees of the Union's Directorate General for External Policies, coordinate interactions between the activities of committees, delegations and the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG).
Contribute to preparing, attending, and following up the meeting of the CPG and the Bureau, for matters within the competence of the Directorate General of External Policies of the Union.

Manage mail assigned to the General Directorate and validate all outgoing mail for the signature of the Secretary General, the President and the Presidents of the parliamentary bodies assisted by the General Directorate of External Policies of the Union.
Ensure the organization, preparation and monitoring of several management and coaching meetings per month.

Delegated authorizing officer: ensure a support system for missions outside 3 workplaces; represent the general management within joint committees and other bodies of the Institution, as well as in interinstitutional forums and negotiations; coordinate and manage horizontal work.
03.refined_description: Unit 03 functions as the apex directorate-general office of the European Parliament, operating at the root hierarchical level under direct authority of the Secretary General, and serving as the central institutional hub for executive coordination and governance across the Parliament's external relations, parliamentary governance architecture, and policy apparatus. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses providing advisory and administrative support to all parliamentary bodies—specifically the Conference of Presidents (CPC), Conference of Committee Chairs (CPD), Conference of Delegation Presidents, and Multilateral Parliamentary Assemblies—while directly monitoring their decisions, managing their parliamentary work, and organizing both internal and external activities. Unit 03 ensures institutional coherence and diplomatic positioning through representation of general management across European Parliament bodies, General Secretariat internal meetings, and external relations with other EU institutions and third-country representations to the European Union. The unit orchestrates legislative and control coordination for the Directorate General for External Policies' committees and subcommittees, facilitates interactions between committees, delegations, and the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG), and prepares, attends, and follows up on Conference of Presidents Group (CPG) and Bureau meetings within the External Policies remit. Unit 03 manages institutional communications through comprehensive mail coordination and validation of all outgoing correspondence designated for the Secretary General, President, and parliamentary body Presidents' signatures, while organizing monthly management and coaching meetings to ensure personnel development and strategic alignment. The unit exercises supervisory authority over six specialized subordinate units (03-10, 03-20, 03A, 03B, 03C, 03D) and serves as delegated authorizing officer for external missions across three workplaces, representing general management in joint committees, interinstitutional forums, and horizontal coordination mechanisms, thereby establishing a comprehensive governance structure that bridges parliamentary decision-making with institutional administration.
03.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Governance and Executive Advisory Support** (50%): Advise, assist directly, and monitor decisions of all parliamentary bodies (CPC, CPD, Multilateral Parliamentary Assemblies) and their Presidents; manage parliamentary work organization; coordinate and organize internal and external activities

• **Institutional Representation and Interinstitutional Relations** (15%): Represent general management across European Parliament bodies and General Secretariat internal meetings; ensure institutional representation in relations with other EU institutions and third-country representations to the European Union; maintain institutional coherence and diplomatic positioning

• **Legislative Coordination and Committee Management** (10%): Coordinate pre- and post-legislative and control issues related to Directorate General for External Policies committees and subcommittees; facilitate interactions between committees, delegations, and the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG)

• **High-Level Meeting Preparation and Governance Support** (10%): Prepare, attend, and follow up on Conference of Presidents Group (CPG) and Bureau meetings concerning External Policies Directorate General competence; organize, prepare, and monitor monthly management and coaching meetings for personnel development and strategic alignment

• **Administrative, Communications, and Operational Management** (10%): Manage and validate institutional mail and all outgoing correspondence designated for Secretary General, President, and parliamentary body Presidents' signatures; serve as delegated authorizing officer for external missions across three workplaces; represent general management in joint committees and interinstitutional forums; coordinate horizontal institutional work activities; oversee mail system integrity

• **Supervisory and Structural Governance** (5%): Exercise supervisory authority over six specialized subordinate units (03-10, 03-20, 03A, 03B, 03C, 03D) extending governance and coordination functions across strategic coordination, communications, and directional management operations

original_activities: Support the efforts of the Policy Management team of the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union to increase policy coherence and inter-service collaboration across a variety of policy fields. Make practical suggestions to the Directorate-General's services to enhance coordination, harmonise internal practices.

Strengthen cooperation and information exchanges with other European Parliament Directorates-General. Strengthen links with other European Union Institutions. Develop links and exchanges of information with secretariats of international organisations, academic institutions and research institutes.

Manage efficiently projects related to access to confidential information, including the secure reading room, the flow of political reporting documents from the EEAS and other interinstitutional partners as well as monitor the completion of the Directorate-General's security organigramme.

Coordinate the Directorate-General's contribution to the work of the Cabinets of the President and the Secretary-General of the European Parliament and develop the Directorate-General's interaction with them.

Manage the harmonisation of the Directorate-General's document management system and coordinate the implementation of decisions taken at central level. Raise awareness on data protection principles and procedures within the Directorate-General and coordinate the implementation of decisions taken by the Data Protection Officer – Data Protection Correspondents (DPO-DPCs) network and the European Parliament Data Protection Working Groups.
Other missions: Coordinate the project teams on interinstitutional negotiations Para 40 Better Law Making (BLM) and on interinstitutional negotiations access to CFSP/CSDP documents.
03-10.refined_description: Unit 03-10 functions as a strategic coordination and governance office within the Directorate-General for External Policies, positioned at the Directorate level (Unit 03) within the European Parliament's apex institutional hierarchy and operating directly in support of the Secretary-General's office. The unit serves as the central coordination mechanism ensuring institutional alignment, security compliance, inter-organizational coherence, and executive liaison functions across the DG's operational framework through six integrated activity streams totaling 100% of operational capacity. Its core mission encompasses supporting the Policy Management team's enhancement of policy coherence and inter-service collaboration across diverse policy fields while providing practical recommendations to DG services for strengthened coordination mechanisms and harmonized internal practices; strengthening cooperation and information exchanges with other European Parliament Directorates-General, other European Union Institutions, and developing collaborative links with international organization secretariats, academic institutions, and research institutes; managing confidential information access projects including secure reading room operations, European External Action Service political reporting document flows, interinstitutional document exchanges, and monitoring completion of the DG's security organizational structure requirements; coordinating the DG's substantive contributions to the work of the President's and Secretary-General's Cabinets while developing and maintaining effective institutional interaction protocols and channels with executive leadership offices; managing harmonization of the DG's document management system and coordinating implementation of centralized decisions; and raising data protection awareness while coordinating Data Protection Officer-Data Protection Correspondents network compliance activities and European Parliament Data Protection Working Group decision implementations, alongside coordination of specialized project teams engaged in interinstitutional negotiations concerning Paragraph 40 Better Law Making procedures and CFSP/CSDP classified document access protocols.
03-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Policy Coordination & Inter-Service Collaboration** (25%): Support Policy Management team efforts to enhance policy coherence and inter-service coordination across multiple policy domains; provide practical recommendations to DG services to strengthen coordination mechanisms and harmonize internal operational practices

• **External Relations & Inter-Institutional Engagement** (25%): Strengthen cooperation and information exchanges with other European Parliament Directorates-General and European Union Institutions; develop and maintain collaborative relationships with international organization secretariats, academic institutions, and research institutes; reinforce institutional linkages across the external policy network

• **Classified Information Management & Security Governance** (20%): Manage confidential information access protocols through secure reading room operations; oversee European External Action Service political reporting document flows and interinstitutional document exchanges; monitor implementation and completion of DG security organizational structure requirements

• **Executive Cabinet Coordination & Institutional Liaison** (20%): Coordinate DG substantive contributions to the Cabinets of the European Parliament President and Secretary-General; develop and maintain effective institutional interaction protocols and channels with executive leadership offices

• **Administrative Governance & Data Protection Compliance** (10%): Harmonize document management systems and coordinate implementation of centralized decisions (5%); raise awareness on data protection principles; coordinate Data Protection Officer-Data Protection Correspondents network compliance activities and European Parliament Data Protection Working Group decision implementations; coordinate specialized project teams engaged in interinstitutional negotiations on Paragraph 40 Better Law Making procedures and CFSP/CSDP classified document access protocols (5%)

original_activities: Advocate and coordinate the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union's communication priorities, working with the units, the Directorate-General for Communication and other partners in and beyond the European Parliament.
Develop internal communication to keep Members, their assistants and European Parliament staff abreast of political and administrative developments and procedures.
Oversee ad hoc communication projects in order to support the Directorate-General's political work.

Provide services, communication tools and training to the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union colleagues – and sometimes other Parliament staff – to enhance all forms of communication (internal, intra-Parliament, inter-institutional and public).

Contribute to the Directorate-General's event management, including by ensuring that appropriate communication is planned and executed, including by working with the Directorate-General for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences and the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union colleagues.

Work with the Strategy and Innovation Unit to reinforce the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union's communication and link it to that of other European Union institutions, particularly the European External Action Service.
Represent the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union in European Parliament steering groups, task forces and working groups focused on communications, publications and events.
03-20.refined_description: Unit 03-20 functions as the integrated Communications and Strategic Messaging Office within the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union, operating as a Direction-level organizational entity within the apex DG 03 structure and serving as a critical coordination node for the Parliament's external communications architecture. The unit's core mandate encompasses orchestrating comprehensive communication strategies across multiple institutional dimensions while ensuring alignment with the European Parliament's external policy objectives and broader European Union institutional messaging frameworks, operating in parallel coordination with sibling units (03-10, 03A, 03B, 03C, 03D) to ensure integrated external policy communication across the institution. The unit executes this mandate through seven interconnected functional activities that collectively translate external policy priorities into coherent, multi-channel communications while maintaining operational coordination across internal, inter-parliamentary, inter-institutional, and public-facing modalities. These activities position Unit 03-20 as a critical institutional mechanism for converting strategic external policy directives into actionable, unified messaging that maintains coherence across the Parliament's complex institutional landscape and reinforces alignment with other European Union institutions, particularly the European External Action Service.
03-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Communication Coordination and Advocacy** (20%): Advocate and coordinate the Directorate-General's communication priorities in concert with internal units, the Directorate-General for Communication, and external institutional partners, ensuring unified messaging and strategic alignment with Parliament's external policy objectives while maintaining coherent institutional positioning.

• **Internal Stakeholder Communication and Information Dissemination** (20%): Develop and maintain comprehensive internal communication channels to ensure Members, parliamentary assistants, and European Parliament staff remain informed of political developments, administrative procedures, institutional changes, and external policy operations affecting parliamentary functions.

• **Political Support Communication and Ad Hoc Projects** (20%): Oversee targeted communication initiatives and ad hoc projects specifically designed to operationally support and amplify the Directorate-General's substantive political work, policy implementation, and external policy mandates.

• **Communication Services, Tools, Capacity Building, and Training** (15%): Provide specialized communication services, digital tools, technological platforms, and professional training programs to enhance communication capabilities of DG colleagues and broader Parliament staff across internal, inter-parliamentary, inter-institutional, and public-facing communication modalities.

• **Event Management and Inter-institutional Strategic Coordination** (10%): Contribute to comprehensive event management by planning and executing integrated communication strategies for DG events in partnership with the Directorate-General for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences and DG colleagues, while collaborating with the Strategy and Innovation Unit to strengthen DG communication capacity and establish coherent messaging linkages with other European Union institutions, particularly the European External Action Service.

• **Institutional Representation and Governance Engagement** (5%): Represent the Directorate-General in European Parliament steering groups, task forces, and working groups focused on communications, publications, and events, serving as the DG's voice in cross-institutional coordination mechanisms and governance structures.

03A.name: 03A
original_activities: Provide leadership and coordination of the committee secretariats (AFET, SEDE, DEVE, INTA), the DROI subcommittee and the EXAS unit, including the organization of regular management meetings and staff management.
Participate in internal coordination meetings and meetings of cross-functional bodies.
Manage cross-functional projects, including inter-institutional projects.
Maintain interinstitutional relations, in particular with the European Commission, the Council, the EEAS and the embassies of third countries.
Maintain relationships with other multilateral organizations.
Prepare the WTO parliamentary conference and its steering committee, inter-parliamentary conferences (CFSP/ESDP), and ad hoc missions involving different parliamentary bodies.
Replace the Director General in his absence.
03A.refined_description:
**

Unit 03A functions as the apex directional entity and primary coordination hub within the Directorate General for External Policies (DG 03), operating as a Level 1 strategic leadership unit directly subordinate to the parent Directorate General and the Secretary General. The unit exercises comprehensive executive authority over a complex network of parliamentary structures, including four major committee secretariats—the Committee on Foreign Affairs (AFET), the Subcommittee on Security and Defence (SEDE), the Committee on Development (DEVE), and the Committee on International Trade (INTA)—alongside direct management of the Human Rights Subcommittee (DROI) and the External Action Service Liaison Unit (EXAS), thereby serving as the central strategic coordination hub for the European Parliament's external relations apparatus. Unit 03A directly supervises six specialized operational child units (03A10, 03A1020, 03A20, 03A30, 03A40, and 03A60) and operates within a sibling structure alongside units 03-10, 03-20, 03B, 03C, and 03D within the broader DG 03 framework. The unit's operational mandate encompasses executive leadership and personnel administration, internal and cross-functional coordination, inter-institutional project management, diplomatic engagement with external partners, and parliamentary conference coordination, while maintaining a constitutional reserve capacity to ensure executive continuity. Unit 03A maintains critical interinstitutional relations with principal external partners—including the European Commission, the Council of the European Union, the European External Action Service (EEAS), and diplomatic missions of third countries—and sustains systematic engagement with multilateral organizations to advance the Parliament's international positioning and institutional coordination objectives.

---

**
03A.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Executive Leadership, Governance, and Committee Secretariat Management** (40%): Provide comprehensive leadership and strategic coordination of four major committee secretariats (AFET, SEDE, DEVE, INTA), the DROI subcommittee, and the EXAS unit; organize regular management meetings; execute direct staff administration and personnel management functions; and supervise six specialized operational child units.

• **Internal Coordination and Cross-Functional Collaboration** (20%): Participate actively in internal coordination mechanisms and cross-functional collaborative bodies throughout the institution to facilitate horizontal integration across parliamentary structures.

• **Cross-Functional and Inter-Institutional Project Management** (18%): Design, implement, and oversee complex initiatives spanning multiple parliamentary bodies and requiring coordination across multiple parliamentary and external organizational boundaries with diverse stakeholder engagement.

• **Interinstitutional Relations and Diplomatic Engagement** (16%): Maintain strategic interinstitutional relations with the European Commission, Council of the European Union, European External Action Service (EEAS), and diplomatic missions of third countries (10%); cultivate and sustain engagement relationships with multilateral organizations operating within the international system (6%).

• **Parliamentary Conference Coordination and Ad Hoc Missions** (5%): Prepare and coordinate the World Trade Organization parliamentary conference and its steering committee; organize inter-parliamentary conferences addressing Common Foreign and Security Policy (CFSP) and European Security and Defence Policy (ESDP) matters; and arrange ad hoc missions involving diverse parliamentary bodies.

• **Executive Continuity and Constitutional Reserve** (1%): Provide deputization for the Director General during periods of absence to ensure uninterrupted executive governance and maintenance of directorate authority.

03A10.name: 03A10
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitoring comitology acts.

Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes; organization of the Sakharov Prize in cooperation with DROI, DEVE and the HRAC unit and the work of 5 working groups, EU-United Nations relations, Southern Neighborhood, Eastern Partnership, External Financial Instruments, Western Balkans).
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Organize public hearings, conferences, ad-hoc delegation missions, etc.

Ensure contacts with other institutions, the EEAS, with diplomatic missions accredited to the Union and third countries. Contacts with the United Nations, the IPU, the OSCE PA and the Regional Cooperation Council in the Western Balkans.
Coordinate the work of the commission and its subcommittee (DROI).
Coordinate the tasks of the committee and those of the joint parliamentary committees and interparliamentary delegations, including election observation delegations falling within its competence.
Ensure coordination of AFET with political and administrative bodies (Plenary, Conference of Presidents, CCC, Bureau, etc.).
03A10.refined_description: Unit 03A10 functions as the primary secretariat and coordination hub for the Committee on Foreign Affairs (AFET) within the Directorate General for External Policies, serving as the essential administrative, legislative, and substantive backbone that translates strategic external affairs mandates into concrete institutional practice and parliamentary support mechanisms. Operating as a leaf node without subordinate units within Direction 03A, the unit collaborates with sibling units (03A1020, 03A20, 03A30, 03A40, and 03A60) to ensure comprehensive coverage of external policy functions while maintaining direct coordination with the AFET committee and its DROI subcommittee structure. The unit's workload encompasses four interconnected operational dimensions: legislative support through preparation of working documents, draft reports across both legislative and non-legislative procedures, and monitoring of comitology acts to ensure regulatory compliance and institutional coherence; secretariat management and inter-institutional coordination including organization of commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming functions, and official minutes documentation, alongside stewardship of the Sakharov Prize in collaborative partnership with DROI, DEVE, and HRAC units, and substantive coordination of five specialized working groups addressing EU-United Nations relations, Southern Neighborhood policy, Eastern Partnership initiatives, External Financial Instruments, and Western Balkans regional affairs; direct parliamentary support providing written and oral assistance to MEPs exercising leadership roles as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen, complemented by organization of public hearings, conferences, and ad-hoc delegation missions; and external relations facilitation through establishment and maintenance of institutional contacts with the EEAS, diplomatic missions accredited to the Union and third countries, the United Nations, the Inter-Parliamentary Union, the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly, and the Regional Cooperation Council. The unit further ensures coordination of AFET interactions with joint parliamentary committees, interparliamentary delegations, and election observation delegations within its competence, while maintaining strategic coordination of AFET interactions with political and administrative bodies including the Plenary, Conference of Presidents, Conciliation and Coordination Commission, and Bureau.
03A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative and Regulatory Support** (25%): Preparation of comprehensive working documents and draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative procedures; monitoring and tracking of comitology acts to ensure regulatory compliance and institutional coherence

• **Secretariat Operations and Strategic Coordination** (20%): Organization and management of commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming schedules, and official minutes documentation; stewardship of the Sakharov Prize in partnership with DROI, DEVE, and HRAC units; substantive coordination of five specialized working groups addressing EU-United Nations relations, Southern Neighborhood policy, Eastern Partnership initiatives, External Financial Instruments, and Western Balkans regional affairs

• **MEP Support and External Engagement** (30%): Direct written and oral contributions assisting MEPs in institutional roles as presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen (15%); organization of public hearings, conferences, ad-hoc delegation missions, and stakeholder engagement mechanisms (15%)

• **External Institutional Relations and Diplomatic Engagement** (10%): Establishment and maintenance of contacts with the EEAS, diplomatic missions accredited to the Union and third countries, the United Nations, the Inter-Parliamentary Union, the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly, and the Regional Cooperation Council in the Western Balkans

• **Internal Committee Coordination and Governance Alignment** (15%): Coordination of AFET committee and DROI subcommittee work (5%); coordination of committee tasks with joint parliamentary committees, interparliamentary delegations, and election observation delegations within AFET's competence (5%); institutional coordination between AFET and key political-administrative bodies including Plenary, Conference of Presidents, Conciliation and Coordination Commission, and Bureau (5%)

03A1020.name: 03A1020
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the subcommittee (organization of meetings including exchange of views, public hearings, coordinators' meetings, organization of conferences, and monitoring of the implementation of the human rights and democracy policy of the European Union and the financial thematic program by the executive) and assist the president of the subcommittee in his other activities related to the subcommittee, in particular bilateral meetings.

Assist DROI rapporteurs in drafting the annual report on human rights and democracy in the world, initiative and legislative reports, and opinions. Cooperate with other parliamentary bodies (other committees, delegations, DEGs) on human rights issues and organize joint initiatives. Cooperate with HRAC on the Sakharov Prize.
Organize DROI missions in countries outside the European Union and with international organizations active in the areas of human rights and democracy.

Write letters addressed in particular to the President of Parliament and the Presidents of DROI and AFET on issues related to human rights, including in support of individual cases. Prepare working documents and notes. Write press releases and tweets for the President of DROI. Propose communication plans in connection with DROI meetings.

Ensure cooperation with European institutions, the Council of Europe, the United Nations - including Special Procedures and HRC -, the OSCE and other international and regional organizations, as well as with Member States, in the field of human rights. Ensure exchanges of information with international, regional and local representatives of civil society. Ensure interface with partner country NHRIs and other human rights mechanisms.
Contribute to the emergency resolutions procedure (article 150) through the development of a context document, and monitor these resolutions.
Other missions.
03A1020.refined_description: Unit 03A1020 functions as the integrated operational secretariat and substantive support mechanism for the European Parliament's Subcommittee on Human Rights and Democracy (DROI), operating as a Level 2 unit within Direction 03A of the Directorate General for External Policies. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses full secretariat management, parliamentary report drafting support, international engagement coordination, and institutional advocacy on human rights and democracy issues across multiple organizational levels. As a leaf node unit within the 03A directional structure, 03A1020 operates in coordination with sibling units (03A10, 03A20, 03A30, 03A40, 03A60) while directly supporting the strategic human rights mandate of the parent Direction 03A and maintaining institutional coordination with European institutions, the Council of Europe, the United Nations system (including Special Procedures mechanisms and the Human Rights Council), the Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE), and other international and regional human rights bodies. The unit's workload prioritization reflects its dual role as both administrative backbone and substantive policy support mechanism, allocating resources across secretariat operations, legislative support, international engagement, communications, institutional coordination, and emergency procedures, while maintaining systematic liaison with civil society representatives at international, regional, and local levels and serving as the institutional interface with partner country National Human Rights Institutions (NHRIs) and complementary human rights mechanisms.
03A1020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Secretariat Operations and Presidential Support** (47%): Ensures comprehensive secretariat management of the DROI subcommittee encompassing organization of all meetings (including exchange of views sessions, public hearings, and coordinators' meetings), conference coordination, and thematic event facilitation; monitors implementation of EU human rights and democracy policy and associated financial thematic programs by executive bodies; provides dedicated administrative and logistical support to the subcommittee president for bilateral meetings and related presidential activities.

• **Parliamentary Reports, Legislative Support, and Inter-Parliamentary Cooperation** (20%): Provides substantive drafting assistance to DROI rapporteurs for annual human rights and democracy reports, initiative reports, legislative reports, and committee opinions; coordinates cooperative engagement with other parliamentary committees, delegations, and Democracy and Electoral Groups (DEGs) on human rights issues; organizes joint parliamentary initiatives and manages collaboration with the Human Rights and Reconciliation Committee (HRAC) on Sakharov Prize administration.

• **International Engagement, Fact-Finding Missions, and Organizational Outreach** (10%): Organizes and facilitates DROI fact-finding missions to non-EU countries; coordinates substantive engagement with international organizations active in human rights and democracy promotion; manages organizational outreach and engagement missions with international partners.

• **Communications, Advocacy, and Strategic Documentation** (10%): Manages high-level written communications to the President of Parliament and Presidents of DROI and AFET committees addressing human rights concerns and individual case advocacy; prepares analytical working documents, policy notes, and contextual documentation; produces press releases and social media communications for DROI leadership; develops strategic communication plans for committee activities and meetings.

• **International Institutional Coordination and Civil Society Interface** (5%): Maintains institutional coordination with European institutions, Council of Europe, United Nations (including Special Procedures and Human Rights Council), OSCE, and other international and regional human rights organizations; facilitates information exchange with civil society representatives at international, regional, and local levels; serves as institutional interface with partner country NHRIs and complementary human rights mechanisms.

• **Emergency Procedures and Miscellaneous Institutional Functions** (8%): Contributes to the emergency resolutions procedure under Article 150 through contextual documentation development and ongoing monitoring; executes additional institutional missions and ad hoc assignments as required.

03A20.name: 03A20
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Prepare working documents/draft reports/draft opinions (legislative and non-legislative procedures), negotiations (legislative procedures).
Organize public hearings, study missions, ad hoc missions outside workplaces (developing countries), etc.
Assist with written and oral contributions the President of the European Parliament and the MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society.
03A20.refined_description: Unit 03A20 functions as the central administrative and operational secretariat for parliamentary commission operations within the Directorate General for External Policies (DG 03), operating under the strategic direction of parent unit 03A while maintaining operational coordination with sibling units 03A10, 03A1020, 03A30, 03A40, and 03A60. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive secretariat management, legislative support, stakeholder engagement, and institutional coordination essential to the Parliament's external relations mandate. As a critical operational hub, Unit 03A20 enables the commission's legislative and external policy functions through five integrated activity streams: secretariat operations and administrative coordination; legislative and policy document development; stakeholder engagement and external field activities; parliamentary leadership support; and institutional relationship maintenance. The unit's organizational positioning as a leaf node reporting to Direction 03A establishes it as a primary support mechanism for commission operations, ensuring continuity between parliamentary leadership, legislative procedures, and external engagement activities. Through its coordinated activities, Unit 03A20 facilitates direct stakeholder engagement, field-based research in developing countries, legislative negotiations, and the maintenance of institutional relationships essential to the Parliament's external policy functions.
03A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Commission Secretariat and Administrative Coordination** (35%): Comprehensive secretariat management encompassing organization of all commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, activity programming and scheduling, and preparation of detailed official minutes and procedural documentation

• **Legislative and Policy Document Development** (30%): Preparation of substantive working documents, draft reports, and draft opinions for both legislative and non-legislative parliamentary procedures, alongside active participation in legislative negotiations and deliberations

• **Stakeholder Engagement and External Field Operations** (15%): Organization and facilitation of public hearings, study missions, and ad hoc external missions to developing countries and locations outside standard workplace environments, extending parliamentary engagement beyond institutional confines

• **Parliamentary Leadership Support** (10%): Provision of written and oral contributions supporting the President of the European Parliament and Members of the European Parliament in their exercise of presidential, vice-presidential, rapporteur, and opinion draftsman functions and formal institutional roles

• **Institutional Relations and External Coordination** (10%): Establishment and maintenance of strategic contacts with other European institutions, stakeholder organizations, interested circles, and civil society actors, ensuring institutional relationship continuity essential to external policy functions

03A30.name: 03A30
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures).
