Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Assist with written and oral contributions the President of the European Parliament and the MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
Organize and prepare the commission's missions and delegations.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society. Ensure contact with diplomatic missions accredited to the Union in the field of international trade.
Organize and prepare WTO parliamentary conferences and assist its bodies.
Organize public hearings and ad hoc missions outside the workplace.
03A30.refined_description: Unit 03A30 functions as a specialized support and coordination unit within Direction 03A of the Directorate General for External Policies, serving as a critical operational backbone for parliamentary commission activities in the external trade and international relations domain. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive preparation of legislative and non-legislative parliamentary documentation, continuous secretariat operations, and strategic coordination of institutional relationships and international engagement activities. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy under parent Unit 03A while maintaining functional coordination with sibling units 03A10, 03A1020, 03A20, 03A40, and 03A60, Unit 03A30 delivers integrated support across six substantive functional dimensions that collectively ensure both the commission's policy development capacity and its operational continuity. The unit's resource allocation reflects a strategic prioritization of documentary and legislative support as its dominant value-delivery function, complemented by essential secretariat operations and distributed engagement activities spanning parliamentary leadership support, international coordination, delegations management, and direct stakeholder engagement through public hearings and field missions.
03A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative and Policy Development Support** (45%): Preparation of working documents and draft reports encompassing both legislative procedures and non-legislative parliamentary procedures—the unit's primary substantive contribution to the commission's policy development function and core strategic focus area

• **Commission Secretariat and Administrative Operations** (20%): Comprehensive secretariat functions including organization and coordination of plenary and coordinators' meetings, activity programming, and maintenance of official meeting records and documentation to ensure institutional continuity and governance functionality

• **Parliamentary Leadership Support** (10%): Direct assistive support to the President of the European Parliament and Members of the European Parliament in the exercise of formal institutional functions including presidency, vice-presidency, rapporteur responsibilities, and opinion draftsman roles

• **Commission Missions and Delegations** (10%): Organization and preparation of commission missions and delegations to enhance parliamentary representation and direct policy engagement in the external relations domain

• **International Institutional Relations and WTO Engagement** (10%): Management of external contacts with other EU institutions, diplomatic missions accredited to the Union specializing in international trade relations, and civil society organizations (5%), combined with organization and technical support for World Trade Organization parliamentary conferences and related bodies (5%)

• **Public Engagement and Field Operations** (5%): Organization and preparation of public hearings and ad hoc field missions conducted outside the principal workplace to enhance direct stakeholder engagement and institutional accessibility

03A40.name: 03A40
original_activities: Assist the committees and their secretariats working in the fields of the European Union's external relations by providing expertise (in the form of papers and workshops) delivered by external researchers.
Oversee contracting procedures for obtaining external expertise, following the requests of committees working in the fields of the European Union's external relations (AFET, SEDE, DROI, DEVE, INTA).

Write and coordinate briefings for the committees and/or committee chairs working in the fields of the European Union's external relations (AFET, SEDE, DROI, DEVE, INTA) to prepare their Members for missions, exchanges of views and other political initiatives.
Prepare briefings and other written contributions for the European Parliament's President and Secretary-General, in coordination with other services.
Prepare briefings for the European Parliament's staff (flash notes and other internal papers) and work with policy communities to ensure knowledge flows unimpeded among staff in different services.
Develop synergies with other research services in the Directorate-General and beyond (notably in DG EPRS, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST and DG BUDG).
Nurture contacts with other European Union institutions, external experts, research institutes and universities.
Update the European Parliament's fact sheets.
03A40.refined_description:
**

Unit 03A40 functions as a specialized research and expertise coordination service within Direction 03A of the Directorate General for External Policies (DG 03), serving as a critical knowledge management and analytical support hub dedicated to enhancing the European Parliament's capacity for evidence-based external relations policymaking. Operating as a leaf node with no subordinate units while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units 03A10, 03A1020, 03A20, 03A30, and 03A60, the unit bridges parliamentary committees and institutional leadership with the broader research and policy communities. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses delivering substantive research support, managing external expertise procurement, preparing political briefing materials for parliamentary engagement, and fostering knowledge dissemination across parliamentary services and institutional partners. Unit 03A40 serves five key external relations committees—AFET (Foreign Affairs), SEDE (Security and Defence), DROI (Human Rights), DEVE (Development), and INTA (International Trade)—alongside the European Parliament's President and Secretary-General, ensuring coordinated analytical support across multiple institutional levels. The unit maintains strategic collaborative relationships with peer research services across DG EPRS, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, and DG BUDG, while cultivating external stakeholder networks encompassing EU institutions, academic researchers, think tanks, and universities. Through systematic workload allocation across research delivery, procurement management, political preparation, institutional briefing, and network development activities, Unit 03A40 positions itself as an essential institutional bridge enhancing Parliament's analytical capacity and evidence-informed decision-making in external relations policy formulation and diplomatic engagement.

**
03A40.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Primary Research and Expertise Delivery to Committees** (40%): Commission and facilitate delivery of comprehensive substantive expertise to AFET, SEDE, DROI, DEVE, and INTA committees and their secretariats through commissioned research papers and specialized workshops conducted by external researchers, representing the unit's core knowledge-transfer function for external relations policy development.

• **Expertise Procurement and Contracting Management** (20%): Oversee all contracting procedures and procurement processes for acquiring external expertise in direct response to requests from the five external relations committees, ensuring efficient acquisition of specialized knowledge resources and vendor management aligned with committee priorities.

• **Parliamentary and Institutional Preparation and Briefing Services** (25%): Prepare and coordinate targeted briefing materials for committees, committee chairs, and Members preparing for official missions, exchanges of views sessions, and strategic political initiatives (15%); prepare briefings and analytical written contributions for the European Parliament's President and Secretary-General in coordination with relevant institutional services (10%), providing executive-level analytical support.

• **Internal Knowledge Management and Staff Support** (10%): Produce internal flash notes and policy briefs for Parliament staff while actively fostering knowledge circulation and policy community engagement across departmental boundaries, facilitating unimpeded knowledge flows throughout parliamentary services.

• **Institutional Network Development and Relationship Management** (5%): Develop collaborative synergies with peer research services across DG EPRS, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, and DG BUDG (2%); cultivate and maintain relationships with external stakeholders including EU institutions, external experts, research institutes, universities, and expert networks (2%); maintain and update the European Parliament's institutional fact sheets to ensure current and accessible reference materials (1%).

03A60.name: 03A60
original_activities: Provide the Committee with efficient, effective and competent support (organisation of committee meetings, meetings of coordinators, other political meetings, background notes, minutes, etc.).

Provide expertise and assistance to Members of the Committee when acting as rapporteurs (or co-rapporteurs) in connection with legislative or non-legislative reports and opinions. Ensure the smooth and efficient handling of the related procedures.

Prepare notes, working documents and letters (including chair-notes, background notes, speaking notes, etc.) and providing input on security and defence topics to other services and Cabinets (President, Secretary-General).

Ensure regular contacts with Commission, Council, European External Action Service, relevant agencies (EDA), NATO, UN in all areas related to CSDP; likewise maintain contacts, as required, with the Permanent Representations of the EU Member States and Missions of third countries to the EU.
Organise hearings, workshops (with EXAS) and study presentations (with EXAS).
Organise the official missions of the Committee.

Provide the Secretariat and assistance to DNAT, Parliament's Delegation for Relations with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly (organisation of meetings and missions, preparation of notes, working documents and letters, maintaining contact with the Secretariat of the NATO Parliamentary Assembly).
03A60.refined_description:
Unit 03A60 functions as the specialized secretariat and substantive policy support entity within Direction 03A of the Directorate General for External Policies, serving as the primary administrative and analytical backbone for the European Parliament's Committee on Foreign Affairs (AFET) with specialized focus on Common Security and Defence Policy (CSDP) matters. Operating as a leaf-node component within the Direction 03A hierarchy alongside sibling units 03A10, 03A1020, 03A20, 03A30, and 03A40, the unit delivers comprehensive parliamentary support across seven interconnected operational domains, allocating resources across committee administration (20%), legislative rapporteur support (20%), policy analysis and strategic communications (20%), external institutional relations (15%), and specialized events and delegation coordination (25%). The unit's administrative functions encompass the full spectrum of committee meeting logistics—including plenary sessions, coordinator caucuses, and political gatherings—alongside comprehensive documentation production such as meeting minutes, background briefing materials, and procedural notes. Substantive legislative support provides expert guidance and procedural assistance to Members serving as rapporteurs or co-rapporteurs on legislative and non-legislative reports and opinions, ensuring streamlined handling of all associated parliamentary procedures and substantive quality. The unit generates specialized policy documentation and strategic communications, including chair-notes, background analyses, and speaking notes on security and defence topics, while furnishing substantive input to other European Parliament services and institutional Cabinets (President, Secretary-General). External relations management maintains regular and substantive institutional contacts with the European Commission, Council of the European Union, European External Action Service, specialized agencies (particularly the European Defence Agency), NATO structures, United Nations bodies, and the Permanent Representations of EU Member States alongside diplomatic missions of third countries accredited to the European Union, encompassing the full spectrum of CSDP-related policy domains. Complementary capacity-building and delegation functions include the organization of substantive hearings, specialized workshops developed in coordination with EXAS (External Affairs Service), and study presentations, the logistical coordination and execution of official Committee missions abroad, and the provision of dedicated secretariat services and technical assistance to the DNAT (Parliament's Delegation for Relations with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly), including meeting organization, mission coordination, and liaison with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly Secretariat.

##
03A60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Administration & Procedural Support** (20%): Organization and logistical coordination of committee plenary meetings, coordinator caucuses, and specialized political gatherings; preparation of essential parliamentary documentation including meeting minutes, background briefing materials, and procedural notes; coordination of all meeting logistics and documentation production

• **Legislative & Rapporteur Support** (20%): Provision of expert substantive assistance and specialized technical guidance to Members serving as rapporteurs or co-rapporteurs on legislative and non-legislative reports and opinions; procedural management and efficient handling of all associated parliamentary processes; ensuring seamless substantive quality and procedural efficiency

• **Policy Analysis & Strategic Communications** (20%): Preparation of specialized analytical documentation including chair-notes, background analyses, working documents, and speaking notes on security and defence policy matters; provision of substantive input to other institutional services and Member Cabinets (President, Secretary-General); direct strategic communication support to senior leadership

• **External Relations & International Institutional Engagement** (15%): Establishment and maintenance of regular, substantive institutional contacts and coordination mechanisms with the European Commission, Council of the European Union, European External Action Service, European Defence Agency, NATO structures, United Nations bodies, EU Member State Permanent Representations, and accredited third-country missions; comprehensive liaison encompassing all CSDP-related policy domains and areas

• **Specialized Parliamentary Events & Engagement Activities** (10%): Organization of substantive hearings, specialized workshops developed in coordination with EXAS (External Affairs Service), and study presentations; capacity building and enhanced external stakeholder engagement activities

• **Official Delegations & Mission Coordination** (10%): Logistical coordination and execution of official Committee missions and delegations abroad; comprehensive administrative support for committee-led international engagement activities

• **NATO Delegation Secretariat Services** (5%): Provision of dedicated secretariat support and technical assistance to the DNAT (Parliament's Delegation for Relations with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly); meeting organization, official mission coordination, and liaison activities with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly Secretariat

03B.name: 03B
original_activities: Supervise the work of the 45 delegations.
Coordinate the work of the Conference of Presidents of Delegations.
Supervise the organization of the Union for the Mediterranean Parliamentary Assembly and its bodies as well as the organization of the Partnership Assembly with the United Kingdom and its bodies.
Organize coordination and service meetings with the units and at management level (heads of unit of Directorate B: preparation and monitoring).

Participate in all body meetings (CoP, CCC, CDC, IPMs, etc.), internal coordination meetings at the Directorate General of External Policies of the Union and interservices. Coordinate the work of working groups on working methods within the general management.
Supervise the organization of the OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly, as well as its regional assemblies and other bodies.
Supervise the organization of the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly and its bodies.
Supervise the organization of the EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly and its bodies.
03B.refined_description: Unit 03B functions as the directorate-level coordination hub within the Directorate General for External Policies of the European Parliament, operating at the first hierarchical level beneath parent unit 03 while supervising seven specialized child units (03B10, 03B20, 03B40, 03B50, 03B60, 03B70, and one additional unit) that provide dedicated secretarial, coordination, and advisory support for specific regional and functional parliamentary assemblies. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive oversight and strategic coordination of the European Parliament's extensive multilateral parliamentary engagement architecture, encompassing both bilateral delegation networks and regional parliamentary assembly frameworks across geographically and functionally distributed external relations structures. Substantively, Unit 03B allocates 21% of its workload to direct supervision of 45 parliamentary delegations and 15% to coordinating the Conference of Presidents of Delegations—the apex coordination mechanism for delegation leadership—collectively representing the unit's primary strategic oversight function for coherent parliamentary representation. The unit devotes 14% of resources to institutional organization of the Union for the Mediterranean Parliamentary Assembly and its subsidiary bodies, 9% each to the OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly (including regional assemblies and ancillary bodies) and the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly (encompassing constituent bodies), and 8% to the EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly and its organizational framework, collectively constituting major responsibility for managing regional parliamentary cooperation frameworks. Operationally, Unit 03B commits 12% of capacity to organizing coordination and service meetings with Directorate B units and management-level personnel (including heads of unit), with preparation and monitoring responsibilities, and an additional 12% to participating in all collegial governance body meetings (including Concertation meetings, Crisis Coordination Committees, Conference of Presidents, Coordination and Consultation Committee, and Inter-Parliamentary Meetings) while coordinating working groups on institutional working methods across the Directorate General. These integrated activities ensure coherent external parliamentary relations strategy, operational efficiency across multilateral assembly structures, and alignment with sibling units 03-10, 03-20, 03A, 03C, and 03D within the broader external policy framework.
03B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Delegation Network Supervision and Leadership Coordination** (36%): Direct supervision of 45 parliamentary delegations (21%) and coordination of the Conference of Presidents of Delegations (15%), representing the primary strategic oversight function ensuring coherent representation and diplomatic engagement across all parliamentary delegation activities.

• **Regional and Specialized Parliamentary Assembly Organization** (40%): Institutional oversight of the Union for the Mediterranean Parliamentary Assembly and bodies (14%), the OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly and regional assemblies (9%), the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly and bodies (9%), and the EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly and bodies (8%), collectively constituting the unit's major responsibility for managing regional parliamentary cooperation frameworks across geographic regions.

• **Internal Coordination and Governance** (24%): Organization of coordination and service meetings with Directorate B units and management-level personnel with preparation and monitoring responsibilities (12%), and participation in all collegial body meetings (Concertation meetings, Crisis Coordination Committees, Conference of Presidents, Coordination and Consultation Committee, Inter-Parliamentary Meetings) while coordinating working groups on institutional working methods within general management frameworks (12%), ensuring internal strategic alignment and operational coherence across the external relations function and broader directorate structure.

03B10.name: 03B10
original_activities: Provide secretarial services to interparliamentary delegations, joint parliamentary committees, parliamentary stabilization and association committees and ad hoc delegations, where applicable (organization of preparatory meetings, meetings of Bureau members, programming, minutes, etc.).
Organize interparliamentary meetings in Brussels/Strasbourg and in third countries, and follow-up on them (minutes, reports, recommendations and other documents).
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president and member of delegation.

Ensure coordination with the parliamentary committees and the services concerned, as part of the implementation of the concepts of 'desk officer' and 'policy communities'; monitoring enlargement policy, developments in the European Economic Area, relations with the United Kingdom, relations with Switzerland, Northern policies and internal matters, liaison with EPLO London. Actively participate in the Western Balkans and Türkiye visitor programs.
Assist the hierarchy and the President's office in handling political and administrative issues in the unit's areas of responsibility.

Collaborate with other units to prepare, at the request of the Presidency or his office, notes and responses to various letters; ensure the reception of various personalities from States maintaining relations with the European Union.
Ensure contacts with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union.
Other missions.
03B10.refined_description: Unit 03B10 operates as a specialized interparliamentary relations and coordination hub positioned at the second hierarchical level within Directorate General 03B (External Policies) of the European Parliament, functioning as a leaf node within a broader organizational structure that includes sibling units 03B20, 03B40, 03B50, 03B60, 03B70, and one additional coordinating unit. The unit's core mission encompasses providing comprehensive administrative, logistical, and diplomatic support services to the European Parliament's extensive network of interparliamentary delegations, joint parliamentary committees, parliamentary stabilization and association committees, and ad hoc delegations, while simultaneously serving as a critical coordination nexus for substantive policy monitoring and MEP delegation support. Operating across a fully documented, balanced workload allocation of 100%, Unit 03B10 delivers integrated secretarial services, meeting organization and documentation, substantive assistance to parliamentary leadership, strategic policy coordination, hierarchical support functions, and systematic diplomatic engagement with external partners. The unit maintains operational responsibility for monitoring enlargement policy, European Economic Area developments, United Kingdom and Swiss bilateral relations, Northern policies, and substantive liaison with the European Parliament Liaison Office in London, while actively participating in Western Balkans and Türkiye visitor programs and coordinating with relevant parliamentary committees and institutional services. Through these integrated activities spanning administrative support, policy implementation, presidential coordination, and diplomatic relations, Unit 03B10 ensures seamless parliamentary diplomacy, institutional continuity, and effective external relations management across the Parliament's complex interparliamentary portfolio.
03B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Interparliamentary Secretarial Services and Meeting Administration** (40%): Provision of comprehensive secretarial support to interparliamentary delegations, joint parliamentary committees, parliamentary stabilization and association committees, and ad hoc delegations, including organization of preparatory meetings, Bureau member session coordination, programming, and formal minutes documentation (20%); concurrent organization and management of interparliamentary meetings conducted in Brussels, Strasbourg, and third-country locations with associated follow-up documentation including minutes, reports, recommendations, and supplementary materials (20%)

• **MEP Delegation Support and Substantive Assistance** (15%): Delivery of written and oral contributions supporting Members of the European Parliament in execution of their delegational functions as presidents, vice-presidents, and members, enabling effective legislative and diplomatic engagement across the Parliament's external relations portfolio

• **Policy Coordination, Monitoring, and Strategic Liaison** (15%): Implementation of desk officer and policy community coordination frameworks with parliamentary committees and relevant institutional services; active monitoring of enlargement policy, European Economic Area developments, United Kingdom relations, Swiss relations, and Northern policies; systematic liaison with the European Parliament Liaison Office in London; and substantive participation in Western Balkans and Türkiye visitor programs

• **Hierarchical, Presidential, and Administrative Support** (10%): Provision of administrative and political issue management assistance to unit hierarchy and the President's office within areas of unit responsibility; collaborative preparation of presidential correspondence, notes, and diplomatic communications; coordination of diplomatic reception protocols for personalities from European Union partner states

• **Diplomatic Relations and External Engagement** (10%): Systematic establishment and maintenance of active contacts with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union; coordination of diplomatic protocol functions and intergovernmental liaison activities

• **Miscellaneous Assigned Functions** (5%): Execution of additional missions and responsibilities as designated by organizational authority

03B20.name: 03B20
original_activities: Responsible for the Asia, Australia and New Zealand Unit. Ensure good communication with the hierarchy and the various services of the Institution and other European institutions.

Ensure the functions of 'desk officer' of the European Parliament for Central Asia, including Mongolia, Australia, New Zealand and the secretariat of two interparliamentary delegations, comprising four Parliamentary Cooperation Committees (PCC) and four bilateral interparliamentary relations partnerships (organization of preparatory meetings, meetings of Bureau members, programming, minutes, management of websites, etc.). Ensure follow-up of these.
Organize interparliamentary meetings in Brussels, Strasbourg and third countries and ensure follow-up (minutes, reports and other documents).
Manage the unit's human resources, coordinate the activities of the various 'desk officers'.

Ensure the role of desk-officer for Afghanistan and for the countries of South Asia, in close coordination with the administrator in charge of the secretariat of the corresponding interparliamentary delegations.
Provide oversight of the 'Policy Community on Indo-Pacific'.
Participate, in the interest of the Institution, in conferences and meetings concerning the regions and policies linked to the responsibilities of the Asia, Australia and New Zealand unit.
03B20.refined_description: Unit 03B20 (Asia, Australia and New Zealand Unit) operates as a specialized directorate-level organizational component within Directorate 03B of the Directorate General for External Policies of the European Parliament, functioning as the institutional focal point for comprehensive parliamentary relations and diplomatic engagement across the Asia-Pacific region. The unit's primary institutional mission encompasses management of the European Parliament's bilateral and multilateral external relations with Asian nations, Australia, New Zealand, Central Asia (including Mongolia), Afghanistan, and South Asia, while simultaneously maintaining seamless inter-institutional coordination with Parliament hierarchy, institutional services, and partner European institutions. The unit's operational framework integrates five integrated functional domains: strategic coordination and institutional liaison; desk officer and delegation secretariat administration; interparliamentary meeting organization and documentation; internal human resources management and desk officer coordination; and policy community oversight coupled with external institutional representation. These functions collectively ensure coherent execution of the Parliament's Asia-Pacific engagement strategy while maintaining administrative efficiency and institutional visibility across multiple geographic and thematic portfolios. The unit directly administers two interparliamentary delegations comprising four Parliamentary Cooperation Committees and four bilateral interparliamentary relations partnerships, with full responsibility for preparatory meeting organization, Bureau member convening, programming coordination, comprehensive documentation, and digital platform management. Meeting organization extends across three geographic venues—Brussels, Strasbourg, and third-country locations—with complete follow-up documentation including minutes, analytical reports, and supplementary materials. The unit simultaneously manages specialized desk officer functions for Central Asia, Mongolia, Australia, New Zealand, Afghanistan, and South Asian countries, while providing supervisory oversight for the Policy Community on Indo-Pacific and participating in regional conferences and policy meetings aligned with its geographic and thematic mandate.
03B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Coordination, Institutional Relations, and Desk Officer Administration** (30%): Oversight and management of the unit ensuring effective communication channels between unit leadership, Parliament hierarchy, and institutional services; execution of desk officer responsibilities for Central Asia (including Mongolia), Australia, and New Zealand; administration of two interparliamentary delegations' secretariats encompassing four Parliamentary Cooperation Committees and four bilateral interparliamentary relations partnerships with complete administrative support including preparatory meetings, Bureau member coordination, programming, documentation, and digital platform management.

• **Specialized Regional Desk Officer Functions and Delegation Coordination** (25%): Execution of dedicated desk officer responsibilities for Afghanistan and South Asian countries in close operational coordination with interparliamentary delegation secretariat administrators; provision of comprehensive policy and administrative support ensuring seamless integration of parliamentary and administrative functions across these geographic portfolios.

• **Interparliamentary Meeting Organization, Logistics, and Documentation** (15%): Planning, organization, and logistical coordination of interparliamentary meetings convened across Brussels, Strasbourg, and third-country venues; preparation and management of minutes, analytical reports, supplementary documentation, and comprehensive follow-up procedures.

• **Human Resources Management and Internal Operational Coordination** (10%): Management of unit personnel resources and coordination of desk officer activities across all functional and geographic areas of responsibility; internal resource optimization to ensure coherent regional strategy implementation.

• **Policy Community Governance and Institutional Representation** (10%): Provision of supervisory oversight for the Policy Community on Indo-Pacific; targeted participation in regional conferences and policy meetings addressing Asia-Pacific regions and policy domains aligned with the unit's mandate, thereby enhancing European Parliament visibility and engagement in Indo-Pacific affairs.

• **Additional Institutional Representation Functions** (5%): Participation in conferences and meetings addressing regions and policy domains aligned with the unit's geographic and thematic mandate; contribution to institutional objectives through enhanced external engagement.

03B40.name: 03B40
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the Interparliamentary Delegations DMED, DMAG, DMAS, D-IL, DPAL, D-IQ, D-IR and DARP, of the JPCs for Algeria, Morocco, Tunisia, and of the Delegation of the European Parliament to the PA-UfM, DMED. Organise ordinary meetings in Brussels, Strasbourg as well as incoming and outgoing IPMs and the follow up thereof, with an eye to promoting parliamentary diplomacy.

Provide for the expertise on the MENA region and on the countries concerned, serving the President's cabinet as well as other European Parliament bodies, through briefings, background notes, reporting, in cooperation with the Policy Department.

Advice the President, the vice President responsible for the Mediterranean and the DMED members in their tasks in the framework of the PA-UfM and its bodies, particularly the Bureau, the Enlarged Bureau, the Political Committee and the Working Group on Rules, in close coordination with the current Presidency of the PA-UfM.

Promote collaborative working methods in the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union and beyond on the countries concerned via in particular the three relevant Policy communities for the Maghreb, the Levant and the Arab Peninsula.

Liaise with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union for the third countries concerned, coordinate work with the European External Action Service, Commission and Council WGs on the third countries concerned and promote the role of parliamentary diplomacy.
03B40.refined_description: Unit 03B40 operates as the specialized secretariat and strategic advisory hub for Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, and Arab regional parliamentary relations within the Directorate General for External Policies of the European Parliament, functioning as a Level 2 organizational unit under Direction 03B. The unit maintains comprehensive administrative and substantive support for eight interparliamentary delegations (DMED, DMAG, DMAS, D-IL, DPAL, D-IQ, D-IR, and DARP), four joint parliamentary committees (Algeria, Morocco, Tunisia, and the EU-Mediterranean Parliamentary Assembly), and the European Parliament's delegation to the Parliamentary Assembly of the Union for the Mediterranean (PA-UfM), while orchestrating ordinary meetings in Brussels and Strasbourg, coordinating incoming and outgoing interparlimentary missions, and managing follow-up procedures that advance parliamentary diplomacy. The unit simultaneously delivers specialized MENA regional expertise through briefings, background notes, and comprehensive analytical reporting to the President's cabinet and other European Parliament bodies in close collaboration with the Policy Department, while providing strategic counsel to the President, Vice President responsible for Mediterranean affairs, and PA-UfM bodies including the Bureau, Enlarged Bureau, Political Committee, and Rules Working Group. Beyond direct parliamentary support, Unit 03B40 fosters collaborative policy development across the Directorate General's three relevant Policy communities for the Maghreb, Levant, and Arab Peninsula regions, and maintains strategic external liaison with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union, the European External Action Service, and Commission and Council working groups on relevant third countries, thereby positioning parliamentary diplomacy as a core instrument of the European Parliament's external relations architecture.
03B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Diplomacy Coordination and Secretariat Functions** (50%): Ensures comprehensive administrative secretariat for eight interparliamentary delegations (DMED, DMAG, DMAS, D-IL, DPAL, D-IQ, D-IQ, D-IR, DARP), four joint parliamentary committees (Algeria, Morocco, Tunisia, PA-UfM), and the EP Delegation to PA-UfM; organizes ordinary meetings in Brussels and Strasbourg; coordinates incoming and outgoing interparlimentary missions with integrated follow-up procedures; promotes parliamentary diplomacy as a strategic institutional engagement tool.

• **Regional Expertise and Analytical Intelligence** (25%): Delivers specialized knowledge on MENA region countries and constituent political entities; produces briefings, background notes, and comprehensive analytical reporting serving the President's cabinet and other European Parliament bodies; collaborates with the Policy Department to ensure evidence-based institutional decision-making and strategic positioning.

• **Executive Advisory Services** (15%): Provides substantive strategic counsel to the President, Vice President responsible for Mediterranean Affairs, and DMED members on policy matters within PA-UfM frameworks; delivers guidance on PA-UfM Bureau, Enlarged Bureau, Political Committee, and Rules Working Group proceedings; maintains close operational coordination with the current PA-UfM Presidency.

• **Inter-Institutional Policy Coordination and External Relations** (10%): Promotes collaborative working methodologies across the Directorate General for External Policies through engagement with three relevant Policy communities (Maghreb, Levant, Arab Peninsula); establishes and maintains strategic liaison with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union; coordinates with the European External Action Service, Commission, and Council working groups on third countries concerned.

• **Diplomatic Engagement and Parliamentary Relations Advancement** (5%): Champions the institutional role of parliamentary diplomacy within the European Parliament's broader external relations architecture; reinforces coordination mechanisms with third-country parliamentary bodies and EU institutional partners; advances the Parliament's diplomatic positioning on Mediterranean and Middle Eastern affairs.

03B50.name: 03B50
original_activities: Ensure the organization of the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly and its bodies (9). Ensure the co-secretariat of the Assembly, its Bureau, the 4 committees, the Forums, the working groups and assist the co-president, the co-vice-presidents and the co-rapporteurs of the European Parliament.

Ensure the secretariat of the interparliamentary delegations DLAT, DCAM, DMER, DAND, D-BR and the joint parliamentary committees D-CL and D-MX. Organize interparliamentary meetings in Brussels, Strasbourg, in the country of the rotating presidency and in third countries; ensure follow-up of these (minutes, reports and other documents).
Assist MEPs with written and oral contributions in the exercise of their duties as president, vice-president and delegation members.
Develop reflection documents, summary notes, flashnotes, speeches, web pages, etc.
Ensure coordination with the relevant parliamentary committees and support their work (AFET, INTA, DEVE, DROI, SEDE, LIBE, ENVI). Coordinate the work of the 'Policy communities'.

Ensure contacts with the responsible services of the Council of Ministers, the EEAS, the European Commission, the LAC diplomatic missions and Member States accredited to the Union, as well as with the CELAC-EU Summit, the SOM and the EU-ALC Foundation.

Prepare, at the request of the Presidency or its cabinet as well as the Secretary General, notes and responses to various letters; ensure the reception of various personalities from States maintaining relations with the European Union.
Ensure contact with the academic world and the specialized press and participate in high-level meetings and seminars.
03B50.refined_description: Unit 03B50 operates as the specialized secretariat and coordination hub for European Parliament relations with Latin American and Caribbean (LAC) parliamentary institutions and regional organizations, functioning as a critical operational node within Directorate 03B of the Directorate General for External Policies. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses comprehensive administrative, organizational, and diplomatic support for all European Parliament interparliamentary engagement mechanisms with LAC states and regional bodies, positioning it as the institutional backbone for EU-LAC parliamentary cooperation. Unit 03B50 executes four major functional dimensions: institutional secretariat support for the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly and its nine constituent bodies (Bureau, four specialized committees, Forums, and working groups), including co-secretariat functions and direct assistance to co-presidents, co-vice-presidents, and co-rapporteurs; full secretariat management of six interparliamentary delegations (DLAT, DCAM, DMER, DAND, D-BR) and two joint parliamentary committees (D-CL, D-MX), encompassing organization of multilateral meetings across Brussels, Strasbourg, rotating presidency locations, and third countries with comprehensive documentation management; substantive support to MEPs in their delegation leadership capacities through written and oral contributions; and strategic policy development through reflection documents, summary notes, flashnotes, speeches, and web-based content. Beyond these core functions, Unit 03B50 maintains critical inter-institutional coordination with seven relevant parliamentary committees (AFET, INTA, DEVE, DROI, SEDE, LIBE, ENVI) and Policy communities; active diplomatic engagement with Council of Ministers services, the European External Action Service, European Commission, LAC diplomatic missions, EU Member States, and regional mechanisms (CELAC-EU Summit, Statement of Ministers, EU-ALC Foundation); protocol and administrative responsibilities including preparation of presidential, cabinet, and Secretary General correspondence; management of high-level receptions for visiting state personalities; and engagement with academic institutions, specialized media, and participation in high-level seminars and meetings.
03B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Secretariat and EUROLAT Assembly Organization** (20%): Ensure comprehensive organization and co-secretariat functions of the EUROLAT Parliamentary Assembly and all nine constituent bodies (Bureau, four specialized committees, Forums, working groups); provide administrative and substantive assistance to European Parliament co-presidents, co-vice-presidents, and co-rapporteurs in their institutional roles.

• **Interparliamentary Delegation and Committee Management** (20%): Execute full secretariat operations for six regional delegations (DLAT, DCAM, DMER, DAND, D-BR) and two joint parliamentary committees (D-CL, D-MX); organize and coordinate interparliamentary meetings across multiple venues (Brussels, Strasbourg, rotating presidency countries, third countries); manage comprehensive documentation including minutes, reports, and follow-up materials.

• **Parliamentary Member Support and Strategic Communications** (35%): Provide written and oral contributions to assist MEPs in delegation leadership and member capacities (20%); develop strategic policy and communications materials including reflection documents, summary notes, flashnotes, speeches, and web-based content (15%).

• **Inter-institutional and External Relations Coordination** (20%): Coordinate with seven relevant parliamentary committees (AFET, INTA, DEVE, DROI, SEDE, LIBE, ENVI) and Policy communities (10%); maintain active diplomatic engagement with Council of Ministers, European External Action Service, European Commission, LAC diplomatic missions, EU Member States, CELAC-EU Summit, Statement of Ministers, and EU-ALC Foundation (10%).

• **Protocol, High-Level Engagement, and Stakeholder Relations** (5%): Prepare presidential, cabinet, and Secretary General correspondence and official responses (3%); manage receptions for visiting state personalities; maintain academic and specialized media contacts while participating in high-level seminars and meetings (2%).

03B60.name: 03B60
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of delegations and interparliamentary bodies, including 3 PACs set up within the framework of the Association Agreements signed with three countries under the Eastern Partnership, 2 PCC, 1 PPC and the delegation for relations with Belarus; organize internal and interparliamentary meetings in the workplaces of the European Parliament or abroad.
Ensure the organization of the annual ordinary session of the EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly and the meetings of its political bodies (4 committees, 2 working groups and the Bureau).
Prepare, upon request, various notes, letters and briefings.
Manage the websites of the delegations for which the unit is responsible.
Ensure contact and reception of diplomatic missions from third countries, partner parliaments, other institutions of the European Union and the public (civil society, think tanks, visitors).
Ensure the organization and conduct of conferences, workshops and other extraordinary events.
Ensure coordination with the relevant European Parliament bodies, the organization of interservice meetings.
Other missions.
03B60.refined_description: Unit 03B60 operates as a specialized secretariat and coordination hub within Directorate 03B of the Directorate General for External Policies, functioning as a critical operational center for the European Parliament's engagement with Eastern European and neighboring countries through parliamentary delegations and interparliamentary bodies. The unit's organizational mandate encompasses the comprehensive administration of secretariat functions for three Parliamentary Cooperation Committees (PACs) established under Eastern Partnership Association Agreements, two Parliamentary Cooperation Committees (PCCs), one Parliamentary Partnership Committee (PPC), and the delegation for relations with Belarus—representing the unit's dominant operational focus at 43% of total workload. A substantial secondary responsibility (22% of workload) involves organizing the annual ordinary session of the EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly and coordinating the meetings of its four committees, two working groups, and Bureau structure, with all activities conducted at both European Parliament facilities and external venues to facilitate dynamic interparliamentary engagement. The unit simultaneously provides critical analytical and communications support through the preparation of policy notes, correspondence, strategic briefings, and related documentation upon institutional request (21% of workload), while maintaining institutional digital presence through delegation website management (5% of workload). The unit fulfills diplomatic liaison and engagement functions through facilitating contact and reception of representatives from third-country diplomatic missions, partner parliaments, other European Union institutions, civil society organizations, think tanks, and public visitors (3% of workload), and organizes specialized conferences, workshops, and extraordinary events to advance parliamentary diplomacy objectives (3% of workload). Additionally, the unit coordinates internally with relevant European Parliament bodies and participates in interservice meetings to ensure coherent policy implementation (2% of workload), with remaining capacity allocated to miscellaneous institutional missions as required (1% of workload).
03B60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Delegation & Interparliamentary Secretariat Services** (43%): Provision of comprehensive secretariat functions for three PACs (Eastern Partnership Association framework), two PCCs, one PPC, and Belarus delegation; organization of internal and interparliamentary meetings at European Parliament facilities and external venues

• **EURONEST Parliamentary Assembly Coordination** (22%): Organization of annual ordinary sessions and coordination of political structures including four committees, two working groups, and Bureau meetings

• **Analytical & Strategic Communications Support** (21%): Preparation of policy notes, correspondence, strategic briefings, and analytical materials upon institutional request; management of delegation websites

• **Diplomatic Engagement & Stakeholder Relations** (3%): Facilitation of contact and reception of third-country diplomatic missions, partner parliaments, EU institutions, civil society organizations, think tanks, and public visitors to advance parliamentary diplomacy

• **Events & Institutional Coordination** (3%): Organization and conduct of conferences, workshops, and extraordinary events; coordination with relevant European Parliament bodies and interservice meeting facilitation

• **Miscellaneous Institutional Functions** (2%): Internal coordination activities and additional assigned missions supporting parliamentary operations

03B70.name: 03B70
original_activities: Ensure the organization of the sessions of the four OACPS-EU Parliamentary Assemblies. Ensure co-secretariat of the Assemblies and their Bureaux, and assist the co-president and co-vice-presidents of the European Parliament.

Perform the functions of geographical 'desk officer'; provide country briefings and flash notes, provide assistance and expertise to Members, ensure coordination with relevant parliamentary committees and services. Coordinate the work of Policy Communities that concern ACP countries.

Ensure the secretariat of two interparliamentary delegations DPAP and D-ZA (organization of preparatory meetings, meetings of Bureau members, programming, minutes, etc.) and of the Cariforum - EU Parliamentary Committee. Organize interparliamentary meetings and ensure their follow-up (minutes, reports and other documents).

Ensure good communication and cooperation with the hierarchy and the various departments of the institution. Assist the hierarchy in handling political and administrative issues in the unit's areas of responsibility.

Prepare, at the request of the Presidency or its cabinet, notes, briefings, elements of language and responses to various letters; ensure the reception of various personalities from States maintaining relations with the European Union.
Ensure contacts with other European institutions, with ACP diplomatic missions accredited to the Union, as well as with other external stakeholders, particularly civil society.
Manage and develop delegation websites and information flows (collection, dissemination, archiving).
Welcome and interact with groups of visitors, particularly in the context of the 'European Union Visitors programme'. Other missions.
03B70.refined_description: Unit 03B70 functions as a specialized parliamentary coordination and liaison office within the Directorate for External Policies of the European Parliament, operating as a leaf-node operational unit under directorate-level parent unit 03B. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses comprehensive management of parliamentary cooperation frameworks and diplomatic relations with African, Caribbean, and Pacific (ACP) states and the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS), serving as a critical operational nexus between the European Parliament's external policy architecture and ACP parliamentary and diplomatic constituencies. The unit's workload prioritizes the organization and co-secretariat functions of four OACPS-EU Parliamentary Assemblies—including administrative and logistical support to Assembly co-presidents, co-vice-presidents, and their Bureaux—while simultaneously executing geographical desk officer responsibilities encompassing country briefings, flash notes, specialized policy expertise provision to Members, and coordination with relevant parliamentary committees and Policy Communities focused on ACP countries. Unit 03B70 additionally manages secretariat functions for two interparliamentary delegations (DPAP and D-ZA) and the Cariforum-EU Parliamentary Committee, organizing preparatory meetings, Bureau coordination, documentation management, minutes preparation, and comprehensive follow-up reporting on all interparliamentary activities. The unit facilitates institutional coherence through internal liaison and hierarchical communication regarding political and administrative matters, prepares executive briefings, policy notes, language elements, and correspondence for the Presidency or its cabinet, and coordinates diplomatic reception and protocol arrangements for visiting state personalities. Beyond these core functions, Unit 03B70 maintains strategic external relations with other European institutions, ACP diplomatic missions, and civil society stakeholders; manages and develops delegation websites and information dissemination systems; and coordinates visitor program activities and related institutional outreach missions, collectively positioning the unit as an essential interface advancing the Parliament's external policy objectives within the ACP partnership framework.
03B70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Assembly Administration & Leadership Support** (30%): Organization and co-secretariat of four OACPS-EU Parliamentary Assemblies and their Bureaux; administrative and logistical assistance to Assembly co-presidents and co-vice-presidents of the European Parliament; Bureau member meeting coordination and support functions

• **Geographical Desk and Policy Coordination Functions** (25%): Execution of geographical desk officer responsibilities; production of country briefings and flash notes; provision of specialized policy expertise and assistance to Members; coordination with relevant parliamentary committees and institutional services; establishment and coordination of Policy Communities focused on ACP countries

• **Interparliamentary Delegation and Committee Secretariat** (15%): Secretariat management for interparliamentary delegations DPAP and D-ZA; secretariat coordination for the Cariforum-EU Parliamentary Committee; organization of preparatory meetings and Bureau member meetings; programming, documentation, minutes preparation, and comprehensive follow-up reporting on interparliamentary activities

• **Institutional Liaison and Executive Support** (17%): Internal liaison and cooperation with institutional hierarchy and departmental services on political and administrative matters (10%); preparation of executive briefings, policy notes, language elements, and correspondence responses for the Presidency or its cabinet; diplomatic reception and protocol coordination for visiting state personalities (7%)

• **External Relations, Digital Infrastructure, and Public Engagement** (13%): Establishment and maintenance of strategic contacts with other European institutions, ACP diplomatic missions, and external stakeholders including civil society organizations (5%); management, development, and maintenance of delegation websites; collection, dissemination, and archiving of information flows and digital platform administration (5%); coordination of EU Visitors Programme activities and related institutional outreach missions (3%)

03B80.name: 03B80
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the D-US, D-CA interparliamentary delegations, TLD meetings and TLD Steering Committee (organization of ordinary, preparatory, interparliamentary meetings and meetings of Bureau members - programming, meeting documents etc.); organize presentations for D-US, D-CA members.

Assist with written and oral contributions the Presidents D-US and D-CA, the vice-presidents and members of the delegations (writing briefings, speaking notes, speeches, press releases). Collaborate with the thematic department to prepare notes and studies. Prepare notes and briefings for the Offices of the President and the Secretary General of the European Parliament, for the hierarchy of DG EXPO and the parliamentary committees which visit Canada and the United States.

Organize parliamentary meetings and welcome foreign dignitaries in Brussels, Strasbourg (members of Congress and the American administration and the Canadian government, ambassadors, deputy secretaries of state), ensure the follow-up of these (meetings of various formats: Extended Bureau, specialized members, workshops etc. with related documentation).

Ensure coordination with the relevant parliamentary committees (participation of rapporteurs in interparliamentary meetings) within the framework of the 'desk officer' concept, including the coordination of the 'TLD Administrators' Network' and the 'Policy Communities' and ensure monitoring of these. Liaise with EPLO Washington (videoconference meetings); organize seminars for staff of the US Congress, US State Department, US embassies and EP colleagues.

Follow the work and summits of the G7 & G20. Write notes and briefings for the Office of the President of the European Parliament in order to prepare the Institution's participation in the parliamentary G7 and G20.
Ensure contact with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union and transatlantic think tanks.

Meet and interact with groups of transatlantic visitors, students and journalists by providing them with information on the work of the Secretariat, including in the context of the European Union Visitors program as well as other exchange programs.
Other missions.
03B80.refined_description:
Unit 03B80 operates as the specialized secretariat and coordination office for transatlantic parliamentary relations within Directorate 03B of the Directorate General for External Policies, serving as the essential operational backbone supporting the European Parliament's strategic engagement with North American legislative and executive institutions. As a Level 2 operational unit without subordinate entities, Unit 03B80 functions as a peer alongside units 03B10, 03B20, 03B40, 03B50, 03B60, and one additional sibling unit within the directorate structure, providing comprehensive administrative, logistical, and substantive support for the Delegations for Relations with the United States and Canada (D-US and D-CA). The unit discharges its primary mission through eight interconnected activity streams encompassing full secretariat functions for interparliamentary delegations and their governance structures; executive and institutional communications support to delegation leadership and senior officials; high-level diplomatic engagement and visitor reception coordination; parliamentary committee liaison and professional development programming; strategic monitoring of multilateral summits; stakeholder relationship management with diplomatic missions and think tanks; institutional information services for transatlantic audiences; and ad hoc institutional assignments. Unit 03B80 directly supports the Office of the President, the Office of the Secretary General, the DG EXPO hierarchy, and parliamentary committees conducting official visits to North America through the preparation of analytical briefings, strategic notes, and comprehensive documentation. The unit's work encompasses both routine operational activities—including ordinary meeting organization, documentation production, and member presentation coordination—and high-profile diplomatic functions involving United States Congress members, Canadian government representatives, ambassadors, deputy secretaries of state, and American administration officials visiting Brussels and Strasbourg.

---
03B80.refined_activity_summary:
• **Delegation Secretariat and Meeting Management** (45%): Comprehensive secretariat functions for D-US and D-CA interparliamentary delegations, including organization and coordination of ordinary meetings, preparatory sessions, interparliamentary assemblies and conferences, Bureau member meetings, and Steering Committee convocations; full responsibility for programming, meeting documentation production, member presentation coordination, and administrative support for all delegation governance structures.

• **Executive and Institutional Communications Support** (25%): Production of written and oral contributions for delegation leadership (Presidents, Vice-Presidents, members) including briefings, speaking notes, speeches, and press releases; collaborative development with thematic departments of analytical notes and studies; preparation of strategic briefing materials for the Office of the President, Office of the Secretary General, DG EXPO hierarchy, and parliamentary committees undertaking official visits to the United States and Canada.

• **High-Level Diplomatic Engagement and Parliamentary Reception** (10%): Organization and management of parliamentary meetings and diplomatic receptions in Brussels and Strasbourg for distinguished visitors from United States Congress, American administration, Canadian government, ambassadors, and deputy secretaries of state; comprehensive follow-up coordination of specialized meeting formats including Extended Bureau sessions, specialized member workshops, and thematic gatherings with complete administrative documentation.

• **Parliamentary Committee Coordination and Professional Development** (10%): Desk officer coordination with relevant parliamentary committees; rapporteur participation management in interparliamentary meetings; coordination and monitoring of TLD Administrators' Network and Policy Communities structures; liaison operations with European Parliament Liaison Office in Washington; organization of professional seminars and exchange programs for United States Congress staff, State Department officials, embassy personnel, and European Parliament colleagues.

• **Strategic Monitoring and Stakeholder Engagement** (9%): Monitoring and analytical support for G7 and G20 summits with strategic briefing preparation for the President's Office to facilitate institutional participation in corresponding parliamentary forums (4%); maintenance of active engagement with diplomatic missions accredited to the European Union and transatlantic policy research institutions (3%); provision of information and engagement services to transatlantic visitor groups, student delegations, and journalists through the European Union Visitors program and complementary exchange initiatives (2%).

• **Ad Hoc Institutional Missions** (1%): Execution of additional miscellaneous institutional assignments as required by senior leadership and organizational priorities.

03C.name: 03C
original_activities: Provide the Director General and the Directorates of the General Directorate with assistance in the management of human resources, financial and budgetary affairs and IT.
Ensure coordination and leadership of Directorate C units.

Ensure the preparation of Resources Management Team meetings as well as detailed reporting tasks on the staff situation (teleworking, temporary agents, contractual agent envelopes and seconded national experts).
Contribute to the coordination of PPPs as well as regular reporting on the activities of general management.
Ensure the representation of general management in inter-service and inter-DG committees and meetings.
03C.refined_description: Unit 03C functions as the strategic coordination and administrative nexus of Directorate General 03, operating at the first hierarchical level beneath the parent directorate and directly under the Secretary General's authority as a central coordinating entity for the European Parliament's administrative and operational infrastructure. The unit exercises dual responsibility for operational management and governance coordination across its organizational domain through five equally-weighted activities, each representing 20% of total workload allocation. Unit 03C provides comprehensive assistance to the Director General and all Directorates within the General Directorate across three critical domains—human resources administration, financial and budgetary management, and information technology infrastructure—while simultaneously ensuring effective coordination and strategic leadership of all subordinate units within Directorate C (comprising Units 03C10, 03C20, and 03C30) to maintain organizational coherence across human resources, administrative-financial, and IT operational pillars. The unit orchestrates Resources Management Team meetings and produces detailed analytical reporting on personnel dynamics, encompassing teleworking arrangements, temporary agent deployments, contractual agent resource envelopes, and seconded national expert assignments. Additionally, Unit 03C contributes to the coordination and oversight of Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs) while maintaining systematic activity reporting on general management operations across the institution. The unit ensures institutional representation of general management interests within inter-service committees and inter-Directorate General forums, thereby facilitating cross-organizational alignment and policy coordination. Collectively, these activities position Unit 03C as a pivotal administrative and strategic coordination hub that bridges executive leadership with operational management functions while serving as a critical nexus for resource stewardship and inter-institutional liaison within the European Parliament's executive framework.
03C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Support & Resource Management** (20%): Provision of comprehensive assistance to the Director General and all Directorates within the General Directorate across human resources administration, financial and budgetary management, and information technology infrastructure operations

• **Directorate Leadership & Internal Coordination** (20%): Coordination and strategic leadership of Directorate C subordinate units (03C10, 03C20, 03C30) to ensure operational coherence and aligned execution across human resources, administrative-financial, and IT operational pillars

• **Personnel Management & Institutional Reporting** (20%): Orchestration of Resources Management Team meetings and production of detailed analytical reporting on personnel dynamics, encompassing teleworking arrangements, temporary agent deployments, contractual agent resource envelopes, and seconded national expert assignments

• **Partnership Coordination & General Management Reporting** (20%): Coordination and oversight of Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs) coupled with systematic activity reporting on general management operations to senior institutional stakeholders

• **Inter-Institutional Representation & Governance Alignment** (20%): Institutional representation of general management interests within inter-service committees and inter-Directorate General forums to ensure cross-organizational alignment and policy coordination

03C10.name: 03C10
original_activities: Supervise personnel management: procedures for publishing and filling vacant positions, recruitment of temporary and contractual agents and monitoring of the 'contractual agents' envelope, secondments of civil servants in the interest of the service, management of Metrics and related surveys, ensuring the coordination and monitoring of the rating procedure, and the mobility exercise, the secondments of national experts to the general management.

Coordinate training in general management: training plan, management of standard and tailor-made requests, external training. Design and implement internal training and professional development initiatives, coordinate exchange programs, monitor budget lines devoted to training.
Ensure the management of office spaces (removals, furniture, telephone equipment), requests for supplies and monitoring of corresponding expenses.
Coordinate the selection of interns, ensure the design and implementation of intern reception and monitoring initiatives. Follow up on requests for study visits and exchanges with other institutions.

Manage mission orders and decentralized envelopes for missions. Monitor security issues and assistance to delegations: management of security files, participation in development work on the crisis management application.
Ensure the reception and integration of new colleagues, as well as their support and career advice and the monitoring of departures.
Manage teleworking, absences, annual and special leave, extra time, overtime.
Participate in EMAS and Waste committee meetings and coordinate activities in this sector for general management.
03C10.refined_description: Unit 03C10 functions as the integrated human resources, training, and administrative operations nucleus within Directorate General 03, operating as a second-level hierarchical specialized unit under the strategic coordination of parent unit 03C, alongside sibling units 03C20 and 03C30. As a leaf node with no subordinate entities, the unit executes a comprehensive mandate encompassing the complete lifecycle of personnel management, professional development facilitation, and operational logistics that collectively sustain the directorate's institutional functioning and enable general management operations. The unit's personnel management operations (38% of workload) represent its dominant function, encompassing the design and publication of vacant positions, competitive recruitment procedures for temporary and contractual agents, continuous monitoring of contractual agent budget envelopes, administration of civil servant secondments in service of organizational interests, management of performance metrics and associated organizational surveys, coordination and oversight of rating procedures, facilitation of mobility exercises, and coordination of national expert secondments to general management. Complementary to this core function, the unit dedicates substantial resources (30% of workload) to designing, coordinating, and implementing comprehensive training and professional development initiatives, including annual training plan development and implementation, management of both standardized and customized training requests, external training procurement, design and implementation of internal capacity-building programs, coordination of inter-institutional exchange programs, and budget monitoring for training-related expenditures. The unit further sustains organizational operations through eight integrated specialized activities: office infrastructure and logistics management (8%), encompassing removals, furniture procurement, telecommunications equipment administration, and supply chain oversight; intern program coordination and inter-institutional exchange facilitation (7%), including recruitment, selection procedures, reception and monitoring initiative implementation, and study visit coordination; mission order administration and security oversight (6%), encompassing decentralized mission envelope monitoring, delegation security file management, and crisis management application development participation; personnel reception, integration, career guidance, and departure support (6%); teleworking policy administration and comprehensive leave management including annual, special, and overtime provisions (3%); and participation in EMAS and Waste committee governance with coordinating responsibilities for general management's environmental sustainability activities (2%).
03C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Personnel Management and Recruitment Operations** (38%): Comprehensive supervision of vacancy publication and competitive position-filling procedures; recruitment protocols and envelope monitoring for temporary and contractual agents; civil servant secondment administration in service of organizational interests; performance metrics and organizational survey administration; rating procedure coordination and oversight; mobility exercise facilitation; national expert secondment coordination to general management

• **Training Coordination and Professional Development** (30%): Annual training plan development and implementation; management of standardized and customized training requests; external training procurement and coordination; design and implementation of internal capacity-building and professional development initiatives; inter-institutional exchange program coordination; training budget line monitoring and financial resource allocation

• **Operational and Facilities Administration** (8%): Office space management including removals and furniture provisioning; telecommunications equipment administration; supply request processing and expense monitoring

• **Intern Programs and Inter-Institutional Exchange Facilitation** (7%): Intern recruitment coordination and selection procedure management; reception and monitoring initiative design and implementation; study visit and inter-institutional exchange request follow-up and facilitation

• **Mission Support, Security Management, and Personnel Services** (12%): Mission order management and decentralized mission envelope administration; delegation security file management and crisis management application development participation (6%); new colleague reception, integration facilitation, career guidance provision, and departure support (6%)

• **Administrative Compliance and Organizational Sustainability** (5%): Teleworking policy administration and comprehensive leave management encompassing annual, special, and overtime provisions (3%); EMAS and Waste committee participation with coordinating responsibilities for general management's environmental sustainability activities (2%)

03C20.name: 03C20
original_activities: Coordinate budgetary management (FMS and Webcontracts), daily execution of the budget, possible transfer requests during the financial year, preparation of the annual budget, as well as closing and discharge.

Support the organization of meetings of the OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly, Eurolat, the Union for the Mediterranean and Euronest, as well as the drafting of post-event reports. Provide assistance to other important events of the Union's Directorate General for External Policies, such as Euromed, Euronest Scola and the Sakharov Prize.

Assist in the preparation and coordination of official delegations, including logistics, and travel management. Provide operational support to the organization of activities related to support for democracy, such as electoral observation missions, capacity building workshops and political dialogue initiatives, in accordance with the guidelines decided by the DEG.

Carry out public procurement procedures for the DG, including tender preparation, tender analysis and award management. Perform the helpdesk function, providing technical and administrative support to internal users. Manage contracts, ensuring compliance with contractual clauses and the proper execution of services. Prepare and verify requests for the creation of imprest accounts, as well as requests for regularization of accounts.

Contribute to the preparation of the annual report and the annual work program of the general management. Prepare interim reports and budget reports. Ensure follow-up to the internal auditor's reports as well as recommendations resulting from audits and reports from the Court of Auditors, ensuring compliance with financial regulations and the implementation of necessary corrective actions.

Assist in the preparation of financial sheets for all proposed decisions of the Bureau, the Conference of Presidents or the Quaestors. Follow up on the annual report, including collecting relevant financial data, verifying its accuracy.

Inform and train operational initiators on procedures and good practices, particularly in terms of preparing financial statements and managing the creation of imprest accounts. Organize and participate in public procurement opening and evaluation committees. Participate in inter-DG Finance coordination meetings, the Public Market Forum as well as the interpretation working group (OCG).

Coordinate the internal control of the DG. Coordinate Business Continuity Management and Risk Management activities. Serve as the main point of contact for the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF) and the European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO).
03C20.refined_description: Unit 03C20 functions as the integrated administrative and financial operations center for Directorate General 03 (External Policies), operating at the second hierarchical level within the organizational hierarchy as a subordinate entity under Direction 03C and peer unit alongside 03C10 and 03C30. The unit serves as the critical nexus between strategic policy objectives and operational execution, exercising comprehensive responsibility across eight interconnected functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of its documented workload. Unit 03C20 provides essential support across the full spectrum of the Directorate General's external relations portfolio, encompassing financial management and budgetary operations through the Financial Management System (FMS) and Webcontracts platforms; logistical and organizational coordination for major parliamentary assemblies (OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly, Eurolat, Union for the Mediterranean, Euronest) and flagship institutional initiatives (Euromed, Euronest Scola, Sakharov Prize); preparation and coordination of official delegations supporting democracy promotion activities including electoral observation missions, capacity building workshops, and political dialogue initiatives; public procurement procedures and contract management; financial reporting and audit compliance; governance support functions; capacity building and inter-institutional coordination; and internal control, risk management, and compliance frameworks. Operating in accordance with Directorate General guidance and institutional financial regulations, the unit functions as the designated liaison point with the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF) and European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO), positioning it as an essential operational and administrative enabler supporting both the immediate Direction 03C mandate and the broader strategic objectives of Directorate General 03.
03C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Management and Budgetary Operations** (25%): Coordinate comprehensive budgetary management via FMS and Webcontracts platforms; execute daily budget operations; process mid-year and annual transfer requests; prepare annual budgets; manage financial closure and discharge procedures in compliance with institutional financial regulations.

• **Parliamentary Assembly Support and Institutional Events Coordination** (20%): Organize and provide substantive logistical support for OACPS-EU Joint Parliamentary Assembly, Eurolat, Union for the Mediterranean, and Euronest meetings; coordinate major Directorate General initiatives including Euromed programs, Euronest Scola activities, and the Sakharov Prize; prepare comprehensive post-event reports and documentation.
