• **Official Delegations and Democracy Support Operations** (20%): Coordinate preparation and execution of official delegations with complete logistics and travel management; provide operational support for democracy support activities including electoral observation missions, capacity building workshops, and political dialogue initiatives aligned with Directorate General strategic guidelines.

• **Public Procurement, Contract Management, and Administrative Support** (10%): Execute full public procurement procedures including tender preparation, analysis, and award administration; provide helpdesk technical and administrative support services; manage contract compliance verification and service delivery execution; administer imprest account creation and regularization requests.

• **Financial Reporting, Audit Compliance, and Governance Support** (15%): Prepare annual reports, work programs, interim reports, and budget reports; ensure systematic follow-up to internal auditor recommendations and Court of Auditors findings; prepare financial documentation for governance bodies (Bureau, Conference of Presidents, Quaestors); deliver staff training on financial procedures and procurement processes; participate in public procurement evaluation committees and inter-DG coordination forums.

• **Internal Control, Risk Management, and Institutional Liaison** (5%): Coordinate Directorate General internal control frameworks; manage Business Continuity Management and Risk Management activities; serve as primary institutional contact point for European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF) and European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO) interactions and compliance matters.

03C30.name: 03C30
original_activities: Provide support to end users, particularly in preparation, then during and after missions, to ensure their connectivity and security at all times in sometimes hostile or critical environments: prepare equipment and train users in its use, communicate on risks, problems and solutions, urgently troubleshoot users and resolve technical problems encountered, check, erase and reinstall mission equipment upon return.

Communicate and raise awareness: Communicate, train and raise awareness among users about IT tools and provide them with the keys to becoming more autonomous and efficient in their activities. Raise awareness of risks and good practices, particularly related to missions and new uses such as AI models.

Manage Projects: Plan, execute and supervise IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality (project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management and communication with stakeholders).

Manage infrastructure, IT assets and access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management, ensuring the availability, reliability and security of IT resources. In particular, ensure the replacement of machines and the deployment of new versions of Windows or updates.

Supervise and coordinate business analysis: understand business needs and objectives, translate them into IT solutions (collection and analysis of needs, facilitation of communication between stakeholders and recommendation of effective technological solutions). Be a source of proposals to optimize and simplify work methods and tools, particularly with AI models or automation of repetitive tasks.

Manage IT and information security: ensure the approach and measures to protect the organization's IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities (risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities, awareness of cybersecurity best practices and implementation of protective measures against threats and security breaches).
Ensure liaison between staff of the Union's Directorate-General for External Policies and other Directorates-General regarding ICT activities.
03C30.refined_description:
Unit 03C30 operates as a strategically critical Information and Communications Technology (ICT) support and infrastructure management unit within Directorate General 03 (External Policies), positioned at the second hierarchical level beneath the Direction unit 03C, with no subordinate organizational structures and coordinating alongside sibling units 03C10 and 03C20. The unit's integrated mission encompasses comprehensive ICT service delivery, infrastructure governance, business-technology alignment, information security management, and inter-directorate coordination across the organization's operations, with particular emphasis on enabling field activities in challenging and hostile environments while maintaining organizational resilience and technological modernization. Operating as a leaf-node functional unit, 03C30 delivers mission-critical end-user support during all phases of field operations—from pre-deployment equipment preparation through post-mission decommissioning and data sanitization—while simultaneously advancing organizational autonomy through systematic user communication, cybersecurity awareness, and training on emerging technologies including artificial intelligence applications. The unit manages comprehensive IT project delivery and business analysis functions that translate organizational objectives and business requirements into effective technological solutions, with strategic emphasis on process optimization, automation of repetitive administrative tasks, and artificial intelligence integration opportunities. Infrastructure and asset management responsibilities encompass planning, provisioning, maintenance, and continuous monitoring of all hardware, software, network components, and user access systems, including systematic machine replacement programs and Windows operating system deployment and security patch management. Information security and IT risk governance activities include vulnerability assessments, cybersecurity best practices implementation, and protective measures against evolving threats and potential breaches. Finally, the unit maintains critical inter-directorate liaison responsibilities ensuring coordinated ICT activity communication, policy alignment, and collaborative governance between Directorate General 03 and other European Parliament organizational directorates.

---
03C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Field Operations Support & User Enablement** (35% combined allocation):
  - *Mission-Critical End-User Support* (20%): Direct technical support delivery to field personnel operating in demanding, hostile, or critical environments, encompassing equipment preparation and deployment verification, comprehensive user training on connectivity and security protocols, real-time troubleshooting and urgent problem resolution, risk assessment and communication, post-mission equipment verification and restoration, systematic equipment management throughout mission lifecycles, and security protocol assurance to ensure operational continuity.
  - *User Communication, Awareness & Training Initiatives* (15%): Organizational learning and enablement programs designed to enhance user autonomy and operational efficiency, including training on ICT tools and platforms, dissemination of cybersecurity best practices and mission-critical protocols, guidance on responsible adoption of emerging technologies (artificial intelligence models, automation solutions), and awareness-raising initiatives promoting efficient and secure work practices.

• **Infrastructure, Assets & Access Management** (15% allocation): Strategic planning, provisioning, maintenance, and continuous monitoring of comprehensive IT infrastructure encompassing hardware, software, network components, and user access systems; user access governance and identity management; operational maintenance ensuring system availability, reliability, and security; systematic management of equipment lifecycle including machine replacement cycles; and deployment of Windows operating system updates and security patches to maintain organizational IT resource resilience.

• **Project Management & Business Solution Delivery** (30% combined allocation):
  - *IT Project Delivery & Management* (15%): Structured planning, execution, and oversight of IT initiatives incorporating comprehensive project planning, implementation supervision, resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication to achieve defined organizational objectives within established scope, temporal, budgetary, and quality parameters.
  - *Business Analysis & Optimization Coordination* (15%): Translation of organizational business requirements and objectives into effective technological solutions through systematic requirements collection, multi-stakeholder facilitation, and strategic recommendations for process optimization, tool simplification, and implementation of automation and artificial intelligence-driven efficiency improvements, with particular focus on administrative process automation and emerging technology integration opportunities.

• **Information Security & IT Risk Governance** (10% allocation): Comprehensive information security strategy and implementation encompassing vulnerability and risk assessments, cybersecurity best practices awareness and training initiatives, deployment of protective measures against identified threats and security breaches, and maintenance of organizational cybersecurity posture against evolving threats to safeguard organizational IT systems and information assets.

• **Inter-Directorate ICT Coordination & Liaison** (10% allocation): Establishment and maintenance of liaison functions between Directorate General 03 (External Policies) and other European Parliament Directorates-General to ensure coordinated ICT activity communication, collaborative governance, policy alignment, and cross-institutional technology coordination.

03D.name: 03D
original_activities: Ensure the preparation and implementation of decisions of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) and adapt the Group's activities to the working environment (geopolitical situation in the neighborhood).
Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units of Directorate D, including working methods and recruitment.
Ensure the organization and assistance of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG).

Ensure monitoring and coordination of projects developed by all management units, particularly within the framework of the 'Comprehensive and Democracy Support Approach' (CDSA) concept for priority countries selected by the DEG.
Assume the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer and manage the department's budget lines.
Organize the work of Directorate D, in particular via regular meetings of the entire management, unit heads or by subject with the colleagues concerned.
Coordinate contacts with other institutions (EEAS, EC, National Parliaments, IPU, CoE, OSCE, PAP, etc.).
03D.refined_description: Unit 03D functions as the strategic Direction within the apex Directorate General (DG 03) of the European Parliament, operating under the Secretary General's direct authority as the central executive coordination hub for the institution's external relations framework and democracy support mandate. The unit's core mission encompasses the preparation, implementation, and adaptive management of decisions emanating from the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG), with particular emphasis on contextualizing Group activities to evolving geopolitical circumstances within the Parliament's neighborhood and advancing the Comprehensive and Democracy Support Approach (CDSA) framework for DEG-designated priority countries. As the directorate-level governance entity, Unit 03D exercises comprehensive strategic direction, coordination, and animation of four subordinate operational units (03D10, 03D20, 03D40, and 03D50), including oversight of working methodologies standardization and recruitment frameworks, while simultaneously providing extensive organizational logistics and substantive administrative assistance to ensure DEG operations function effectively. The unit discharges critical financial and administrative stewardship functions through subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities and departmental budget management, organizes Directorate D operational work through structured management meetings and subject-specific coordination mechanisms, and maintains systematic institutional liaison relationships with strategic external partners including the European External Action Service (EEAS), European Commission (EC), National Parliaments, Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU), Council of Europe (CoE), OSCE, and Pan-African Parliament (PAP). Through these integrated functions, Unit 03D positions itself as the critical nexus between parliamentary democracy support initiatives and the broader European institutional architecture, ensuring coherent implementation of the Parliament's external relations agenda.
03D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Democracy Support Governance & DEG Coordination** (55%): Preparation and implementation of Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) decisions with adaptive responsiveness to neighborhood geopolitical developments (35%); monitoring and coordination of cross-directorate projects advancing the Comprehensive and Democracy Support Approach (CDSA) framework for priority countries; provision of comprehensive organizational logistics and substantive administrative assistance to DEG operations (20%)

• **Directorate Strategic Management & Operational Oversight** (20%): Strategic direction, coordination, and animation of subordinate units (03D10, 03D20, 03D40, 03D50); oversight of working methods standardization and recruitment frameworks across operational units

• **Financial & Administrative Authority** (5%): Execution of subdelegated authorizing officer functions; management of departmental budget lines and financial allocations

• **Internal Operations & Organizational Coordination** (5%): Organization of Directorate D operations through regular management meetings, unit head coordination sessions, and subject-specific collaborative arrangements

• **External Institutional Relations** (5%): Systematic coordination of institutional contacts with EEAS, European Commission, National Parliaments, Inter-Parliamentary Union, Council of Europe, OSCE, Pan-African Parliament, and equivalent organizations

03D10.name: 03D10
original_activities: Organise and accompany election observation delegations, and fully implementing the 'Guidelines between European Parliament and EEAS Staff on EU Election Observation'.

Contribute to the implementation - virtually or in person (where possible) - of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) 2025 Annual Work Programme, notably to the thematic area of 'pre- and post-election activities', and to the Comprehensive Democracy Support Approach (CDSA) in Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia the Western Balkans from an election-related perspective.
Continuing implementing the working group recommendations on a revised methodology for election observation activities adopted by DEG in February 2024.
Support to the organisation of the DEG meetings, and contributing to their follow-up and the implementation of DEG decisions, in close cooperation with the other Units from the Directorate.

Enhance - in close contact with the Directorate-General for External Policies of the Union's Communication and Outreach Unit and the Directorate-General for Communication - the visibility of European Parliament's election-related and DEG activities.

Continuing providing enhanced assistance and expertise, including training activities to newly elected Members, to the DEG Co-Chairs, its Members, potential Chief Observers and potential Members of EP Election Observation Delegations.
03D10.refined_description: Unit 03D10 serves as the European Parliament's specialized operational center for election observation and democratic governance support, functioning as a critical component within Direction 03D under the Secretary General's authority and operating within the Directorate General for External Policies framework. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive management and execution of EU election observation activities through the organization and accompaniment of election observation delegations while fully implementing the jointly established Guidelines between the European Parliament and EEAS Staff on EU Election Observation, thereby establishing both the operational framework and procedural foundation for all observation missions (50% of workload). Complementing this core function, Unit 03D10 actively contributes to the implementation of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) 2025 Annual Work Programme—delivered through both virtual and in-person modalities where operationally feasible—with particular emphasis on pre- and post-election activities and the advancement of the Comprehensive Democracy Support Approach (CDSA) across Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, and the Western Balkans from an election-focused perspective (20% of workload). The unit continues operationalizing the revised election observation methodologies based on working group recommendations formally adopted by the DEG in February 2024, ensuring continuous methodological refinement, standardization, and quality assurance across all election observation initiatives (15% of workload). Unit 03D10 provides essential organizational and coordination support to DEG meetings, manages follow-up mechanisms, oversees implementation of DEG decisions through close collaboration with sibling units within the Directorate, and strategically enhances the visibility and public profile of the European Parliament's election-related and DEG activities through coordinated engagement with the Directorate-General for External Policies' Communication and Outreach Unit and the Directorate-General for Communication (10% of workload). Finally, the unit delivers continuous advanced technical assistance, specialized expertise, and comprehensive training programming to newly elected Members, DEG Co-Chairs and members, prospective Chief Observers, and designated participants in EP Election Observation Delegations, thereby building institutional capacity and ensuring high-quality observer deployment (5% of workload).
03D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Election Observation Operations and Delegation Management** (50%): Organization and accompaniment of election observation delegations with full implementation of the European Parliament-EEAS Staff Guidelines on EU Election Observation, establishing both the operational framework and procedural foundation for all observation missions and representing the unit's primary operational focus and core mandate.

• **Democracy Support Programming and Regional Coordination** (20%): Active contribution to the DEG 2025 Annual Work Programme implementation through both virtual and in-person modalities where operationally feasible, with specialized focus on pre- and post-election activities and Comprehensive Democracy Support Approach (CDSA) application across Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, and the Western Balkans from election-focused perspectives.

• **Methodology Development, Standardization, and Quality Assurance** (15%): Continued implementation of revised election observation activity methodologies and working group recommendations adopted by the DEG in February 2024, ensuring continuous methodological refinement, operational standardization, and quality assurance across all election observation initiatives.

• **Institutional Coordination and Strategic Communications** (10%): Provision of essential organizational support to DEG meetings (5%), management of follow-up mechanisms, implementation oversight of DEG decisions, and close collaboration with sibling units within the Directorate; combined with strategic enhancement of the European Parliament's election-related and DEG activities visibility through coordinated engagement with the Directorate-General for External Policies' Communication and Outreach Unit and the Directorate-General for Communication (5%).

• **Capacity Building, Technical Expertise, and Training Delivery** (5%): Delivery of enhanced technical assistance, specialized training activities, and expert guidance to newly elected Members, DEG Co-Chairs and members, prospective Chief Observers, and designated participants in EP Election Observation Delegations, ensuring institutional knowledge transfer, capacity development, and operational readiness for high-quality observer deployment.

03D20.name: 03D20
original_activities: Support and strengthen the parliaments of priority countries/regions: Western Balkans, Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, PAP, through the organization and coordination of activities, seminars, study visits, thematic meetings on a national and regional basis between members or officials of these parliaments and their EP counterparts within the framework of the support program for parliaments. Respond, through specific targeted actions, to reform needs.
Provide the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) with the necessary support for defining and monitoring the support program for the parliaments of priority countries/regions.
Strengthen the democracy support network with the national parliaments of the European Union.
Manage the program to host civil servants from parliaments from priority countries 'Democracy Fellowship Program' and 'Pre-Accession Actions Fellowship Program'.
Ensure contacts with international organizations and regional parliamentary forums for parliamentary cooperation.

Ensure contacts with the European Commission (in particular DG ENEST, DG INTPA, other directorates-general according to program needs), the EEAS, other institutions and agencies of the European Union as well as with the Council of Europe.
Ensure monitoring of the legislative and political activities of priority parliaments within the framework of their relations with the European Parliament.
03D20.refined_description:
Unit 03D20 operates as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for parliamentary democracy support and institutional strengthening, functioning as a specialized Level 2 unit within Direction 03D (the Secretary General's central executive coordination mechanism) and a critical component of the institution's democratic governance and capacity-building framework. The unit executes a comprehensive, multi-faceted mandate encompassing the systematic organization, coordination, and delivery of targeted democracy support initiatives to parliaments in strategically designated priority countries and regions—specifically the Western Balkans, Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, and the Pan-African Parliament (PAP)—through structured activities including seminars, study visits, thematic meetings, and bilateral/multilateral engagements between parliamentary members and officials of these institutions and their European Parliament counterparts, all designed to respond to identified institutional reform needs through targeted, evidence-based actions. Unit 03D20 provides essential analytical, operational, and monitoring support to the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) for the definition, implementation, and ongoing evaluation of parliament support programs across priority regions, while simultaneously strengthening and institutionalizing the democracy support network through fostered collaborative relationships with national parliaments of European Union member states and sustained engagement with international organizations and regional parliamentary forums. The unit manages specialized fellowship and professional development initiatives—including the Democracy Fellowship Program and Pre-Accession Actions Fellowship Program—designed to host and provide professional development opportunities for civil servants from priority country parliaments, and maintains critical institutional liaison and coordination functions with the European Commission (particularly DG ENEST, DG INTPA, and other relevant directorates-general), the European External Action Service (EEAS), other European Union institutions and agencies, and the Council of Europe to ensure programmatic coherence and resource optimization. Additionally, Unit 03D20 conducts systematic monitoring of the legislative and political activities of priority country parliaments within the framework of their institutional relations with the European Parliament, facilitating informed strategic engagement and responsive policy coordination. As a leaf node within Direction 03D's hierarchical structure, Unit 03D20 operates in complementary functional coordination with sibling units 03D10, 03D40, and 03D50, collectively advancing the European Parliament's institutional commitment to democratic governance, parliamentary capacity strengthening, and international democratic cooperation.

---

##
03D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Parliamentary Support and Capacity Building** (60%): Direct organization and coordination of seminars, study visits, thematic meetings, and structured bilateral/multilateral engagement activities between priority country and regional parliaments (Western Balkans, Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, Pan-African Parliament) and European Parliament counterparts, including targeted reform response mechanisms addressing identified institutional needs through evidence-based support actions.

• **Strategic Program Definition, Implementation, and Monitoring** (15%): Provision of comprehensive analytical, operational, and monitoring support to the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) for parliamentary support program strategy formulation, implementation oversight, and performance evaluation across priority regions.

• **Democratic Network Strengthening and EU Parliamentary Cooperation** (10%): Institutionalization and enhancement of the democracy support network through systematic engagement with national parliaments of European Union member states, fostering knowledge exchange and cooperative frameworks that strengthen multilateral democratic governance mechanisms.

• **International Institutional Liaison and External Relations Coordination** (8%): Maintenance of critical contact and coordination functions with international organizations, regional parliamentary forums, the European Commission (DG ENEST, DG INTPA, and other relevant directorates-general), the European External Action Service (EEAS), other European Union institutions and agencies, and the Council of Europe; ensuring programmatic coherence, resource optimization, and strategic alignment across institutional partners.

• **Professional Development and Fellowship Program Administration** (5%): Management and administration of the Democracy Fellowship Program and Pre-Accession Actions Fellowship Program, encompassing recruitment, placement coordination, and professional development facilitation for civil servants from priority country parliaments.

• **Legislative Monitoring and Institutional Intelligence** (2%): Systematic monitoring of legislative and political activities within priority country parliaments within the framework of their institutional relations with the European Parliament, facilitating informed strategic engagement and responsive policy coordination.

03D40.name: 03D40
original_activities: Assist the DEG in human rights aspects of democracy support activities; strengthen the human rights capacities of partner parliaments; promote a partnership between civil society, parliaments and government authorities, strengthen the role of women and independent media in the promotion of human rights, in particular through the capacity building program for women parliamentarians and the Triangle for Democracy.

Monitor the situation of Sakharov Prize winners and propose appropriate support actions to the political authorities if necessary; encourage joint actions between Sakharov Prize winners, finalists and fellows and MPs; strengthen their links within the framework of the Sakharov Community through the newsletter and the website.

Strengthen the capacities of human rights defenders and their links with the European Parliament through meetings and conferences; organize the 'Sakharov Fellowship for human rights defenders': selection of candidates, program, monitoring and support of fellows.

Support the European Parliament's human rights agenda and increase its visibility: organization of conferences and events in cooperation with relevant parliamentary committees, notably DROI, AFET, DEVE, LIBE, FEMM, DG EXPO COSE and DG COMM.

Assist the President of the European Parliament, AFET, DEVE, DROI and the COP in the organization of the Sakharov Prize: nomination of candidates, Prize award ceremony, program of the winner and finalists, financial aspects linked to the Prize; chair the Sakharov Task Force made up of the relevant services of the European Parliament.

Cooperate with the European External Action Service, the European Commission, the Council of Europe, the United Nations and other institutional actors with a view to increasing synergies around a common agenda for promoting the defense of human rights.
Support Members of the European Parliament in their work within the European Endowment for Democracy.
Other missions.
03D40.refined_description: Unit 03D40 operates as a specialized human rights and democracy support unit within Direction 03D under the Secretary General's strategic authority, functioning as the European Parliament's central nexus for human rights advocacy, institutional capacity-building, and multi-stakeholder coordination. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses the advancement of human rights agendas through targeted engagement with partner parliaments, civil society organizations, human rights defenders, and international institutional actors, with particular emphasis on strengthening democratic institutions and civil society capacity across multiple geographic and thematic domains. Operating across eight functionally integrated workload areas totaling 100% allocation, Unit 03D40 executes its mission through strategic initiatives including the Triangle for Democracy program, women parliamentarians capacity-building activities, the Sakharov Fellowship program, and the Sakharov Prize administration, while simultaneously coordinating with parliamentary committees (DROI, AFET, DEVE, LIBE, FEMM), relevant Directorate General services (DG EXPO COSE, DG COMM), the Directorate for External Governance, and external institutional partners including the European External Action Service, European Commission, Council of Europe, and United Nations. The unit maintains peer-level operational relationships with Units 03D10, 03D20, and 03D50 within Direction 03D while serving as the primary coordinator of the Sakharov Task Force across parliamentary services and the President's office. Through integrated community-building mechanisms encompassing newsletters, digital platforms, coordinated advocacy actions, and structured meetings and conferences, Unit 03D40 strengthens institutional linkages between human rights defenders and the European Parliament while amplifying the institution's human rights visibility and policy coherence across multiple parliamentary and governance frameworks.
03D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Democracy Support and Institutional Capacity Building** (35%): Assistance to the Directorate for External Governance in integrating human rights dimensions into democracy support activities; strengthening human rights capacities within partner parliaments and civil society organizations; promotion of multi-stakeholder partnerships between civil society, parliamentary bodies, and governmental authorities; advancement of women's parliamentary representation and independent media roles through the women parliamentarians capacity-building program and the Triangle for Democracy initiative.

• **Sakharov Prize Administration and Laureate Support** (25%): Comprehensive management of the Sakharov Prize process including candidate nomination, award ceremony coordination, and laureate/finalist program development; monitoring and support of Sakharov Prize winners and finalists; facilitation of collaborative networks among prize recipients, finalists, and fellows through the Sakharov Community infrastructure; maintenance of institutional communication channels via newsletter and website; chairing of the interdepartmental Sakharov Task Force coordinating relevant parliamentary services, the President's office, and supporting committees (AFET, DEVE, DROI, COP).

• **Human Rights Defenders Engagement and Fellowship Program** (15%): Capacity strengthening for human rights defenders through structured meetings, conferences, and institutional linkages with the European Parliament; design, implementation, and comprehensive oversight of the Sakharov Fellowship program encompassing candidate selection mechanisms, program curricula, fellow monitoring protocols, and ongoing institutional support frameworks.

• **Parliamentary Human Rights Advocacy and Institutional Visibility** (15%): Organization of thematic conferences and events in strategic cooperation with parliamentary committees (DROI, AFET, DEVE, LIBE, FEMM) and relevant Directorate General services (DG EXPO COSE, DG COMM); elevation of the European Parliament's human rights agenda prominence and institutional visibility across multiple policy domains.

• **External Institutional Coordination and Ancillary Functions** (10%): Institutional cooperation with the European External Action Service, European Commission, Council of Europe, United Nations, and other international actors to establish synergistic frameworks for human rights defense and common human rights promotion agendas; support of Members' engagement with the European Endowment for Democracy; execution of additional institutional missions as assigned within the human rights and democracy support portfolio.

03D50.name: 03D50
original_activities: Continue the implementation and development of mediation and dialogue facilitation activities with national parliaments in the priority countries of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG), in particular the Balkans, Ukraine and North Macedonia.

Continue the implementation and diversification of the Young Political Leaders programme, notably with a view to activities related to the Western Balkans and the Eastern and Southern Neighborhood of the European Union, while strengthening cooperation with other units within the European Parliament, as well as with the EUVP and other institutions.
Gradually implement the PPP on the 'creation of a pool of European Parliament mediation experts' and continue training actions in the field of mediation and dialogue for MEPs and staff.
Bring expertise in the field of mediation to the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG). Monitoring of Group decisions.

Maintain and develop contacts with other services of the European Parliament (secretariat of the Conference of Presidents, secretariats of parliamentary committees and delegations, etc.), other European institutions (EEAS, Commission, Council), international organizations (Council of Europe, OSCE, United Nations, etc.), and with non-governmental organizations.
Assist the European Parliament with policy guidance, advice and information related to mediation and dialogue and conflict prevention (including election-related conflict prevention).
Continue to contribute to other conflict prevention activities (including election-related conflict prevention and in particular, contributing to Parliamentary Electoral Dialogues).
03D50.refined_description: Unit 03D50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 03D, functioning as the European Parliament's dedicated institutional center for mediation, dialogue facilitation, and conflict prevention activities across strategic priority geopolitical regions. The unit's primary mandate encompasses the strategic implementation and continuous development of mediation and dialogue facilitation initiatives with national parliaments in Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) priority countries—specifically the Balkans, Ukraine, and North Macedonia—while simultaneously advancing the Young Political Leaders programme through systematic geographic diversification toward the Western Balkans and Eastern and Southern Neighborhood regions, strengthened through enhanced inter-unit cooperation within the European Parliament and collaboration with the European Union Visiting Programme (EUVP) and external institutional partners. Unit 03D50 progressively implements the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) framework for establishing a dedicated pool of European Parliament mediation experts and delivers comprehensive training initiatives in mediation and dialogue methodologies for both Members of the European Parliament and institutional staff, thereby building sustained institutional capacity for conflict prevention and democratic dialogue support. The unit provides specialized mediation expertise to inform and monitor the strategic decisions of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (DEG) while furnishing comprehensive policy guidance, strategic advice, and specialized information on mediation, dialogue, and conflict prevention mechanisms, including election-related conflict mitigation frameworks and Parliamentary Electoral Dialogues. Unit 03D50 maintains and develops an extensive collaborative network spanning European Parliament services (including the Conference of Presidents secretariat and parliamentary committee and delegation secretariats), European Union institutions (EEAS, European Commission, Council of the European Union), international organizations (Council of Europe, OSCE, United Nations), and non-governmental organizations, positioning the unit as a cross-institutional hub for conflict prevention and democratic dialogue support within the European Parliament's external relations and democracy support architecture.
03D50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Regional Mediation and Dialogue Facilitation with National Parliaments** (30%): Continuous strategic implementation and development of mediation and dialogue facilitation programmes with national parliaments in DEG priority countries, with particular emphasis on the Balkans, Ukraine, and North Macedonia, serving as the primary institutional mechanism for European Parliament engagement in regional democratic consolidation and conflict prevention.

• **Young Political Leaders Programme and Regional Capacity Building** (20%): Implementation, systematic diversification, and geographic expansion of the Young Political Leaders programme targeting the Western Balkans and Eastern and Southern Neighborhood regions, with strengthened coordination and cooperation across European Parliament units, the European Union Visiting Programme (EUVP), and external institutional partners.

• **Mediation Expert Development and Institutional Capacity-Building** (15%): Progressive implementation of the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) for establishing a dedicated pool of European Parliament mediation experts, coupled with continuous delivery of comprehensive training and professional development programmes in mediation and dialogue methodologies for Members of the European Parliament and institutional staff.

• **Strategic Coordination, Advisory Functions, and Policy Guidance** (20%): Provision of specialized mediation expertise to inform and monitor the strategic decisions of the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group (10%), combined with delivery of comprehensive policy guidance, strategic advice, and specialized information services on mediation, dialogue, and conflict prevention mechanisms, including election-related conflict prevention frameworks (10%).

• **Institutional Networking and Broader Conflict Prevention Support** (15%): Maintenance and development of extensive collaborative relationships across European Parliament services (including Conference of Presidents secretariat and parliamentary committee and delegation secretariats), European Union institutions (EEAS, European Commission, Council of the European Union), international organizations (Council of Europe, OSCE, United Nations), and non-governmental organizations (10%), combined with contributions to broader conflict prevention activities through participation in Parliamentary Electoral Dialogues and election-focused conflict prevention initiatives (5%).

original_activities: Define, in conjunction with the Secretary General, and apply the general guidelines of the General Directorate of Finance and report on the results obtained.
Ensure the budgetary and financial interests of the European Parliament, including in the institution's bodies, committees and working groups, as well as in interinstitutional forums.
Perform the functions of delegated or subdelegated authorizing officer.
Manage mail assigned to the General Directorate of Finance and verify all outgoing mail for the signature of the President and the Secretary General.
09.refined_description: Unit 09 serves as the executive and coordinating center of the General Directorate of Finance, operating at the apex of the European Parliament's financial administration hierarchy with direct reporting responsibility to the Secretary General. The unit's primary mandate encompasses the strategic definition, implementation, and comprehensive oversight of General Directorate-wide financial guidelines in conjunction with the Secretary General, while establishing the strategic direction and performance accountability framework for the entire financial directorate (30% of workload). Unit 09 exercises fiduciary stewardship responsibility by ensuring comprehensive protection and advancement of the European Parliament's budgetary and financial interests across all institutional bodies, standing committees, working groups, and interinstitutional forums where financial matters are deliberated, thereby maintaining financial integrity and governance alignment throughout the organization's decision-making structures (30% of workload). The unit performs delegated and subdelegated authorizing officer functions, exercising direct fiduciary responsibility for financial commitments and expenditure authorization within its delegated scope of authority (20% of workload). Additionally, Unit 09 manages all incoming and outgoing administrative correspondence assigned to the General Directorate of Finance and serves as the quality assurance mechanism for verifying all outgoing mail requiring signature authorization from both the President and Secretary General, ensuring proper administrative protocols and executive oversight of institutional communications (20% of workload). Through strategic coordination and governance oversight of six subordinate specialized units (09-10, 09-20, 09-30, 09A, 09B, and 09C), Unit 09 collectively delivers 41 distinct activities across the institution's financial control, legal-administrative frameworks, and executive support functions, thereby ensuring institutional financial integrity and operational coherence throughout the Parliament's financial administration structure.
09.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Policy Definition and Governance Oversight** (30%): Define, in conjunction with the Secretary General, and apply the general guidelines of the General Directorate of Finance while reporting comprehensively on results obtained; establish strategic direction, performance accountability, and framework implementation for the entire financial directorate.

• **Fiduciary Stewardship and Financial Protection** (30%): Ensure comprehensive protection and advancement of the European Parliament's budgetary and financial interests across all institutional bodies, standing committees, working groups, and interinstitutional forums; maintain financial integrity and governance alignment throughout the organization's decision-making structures.

• **Financial Authorization and Delegated Authority Management** (20%): Perform delegated and subdelegated authorizing officer functions; exercise direct fiduciary responsibility for financial commitments and expenditure authorization within delegated scope; maintain critical authorization powers essential to institutional budgetary execution and expenditure management.

• **Administrative Communications and Quality Assurance** (20%): Manage all incoming and outgoing correspondence assigned to the General Directorate of Finance; verify all outgoing mail for accuracy and appropriateness prior to signature authorization by the President and Secretary General; ensure proper administrative protocols and executive oversight of institutional communications.

original_activities: Carry out ex ante verification of financial transactions, including contributions and subsidies from European political parties and foundations, procurements, determinations and modifications of pension rights and transitional allowances of MPs and pre-litigation and litigation files from the Directorate General of Finance. Validate the corresponding information in the Parliament's integrated financial systems (FMS and WebContracts).

Advise the DG services on calls for tenders and administrative agreements, ensure their coordination and manage publication in the Official Journal, communication with tenderers and the opening/evaluation committees. Monitor contracts, administrative agreements and AMI lists, monitor interinstitutional contracts, draft and monitor specific contracts. Administer the WebContracts application and participate in the Public Procurement Forum and various (inter)institutional projects.
Carry out ex post controls based on a risk analysis and a multi-annual work program approved by the Director General.

Coordinate work relating to the evaluation and improvement of the internal control framework within the General Directorate of Finance, as well as the updating of the general management's risk register. Participate in the Inter-DG Steering Groups for Organizational Resilience and Finance.

Coordinate the preparation of the general management budget, monitor its execution (including requests for credit transfers) as well as prepare and communicate regular budgetary and financial reporting to financial stakeholders. Provide support to the financial actors of the general management in the use of FMS, represent the DG in the FMS key users network. Prepare the decisions of financial players. Check the delegations of the authorizing officer by delegation.

Contribute to the preparation of the annual work program and the periodic and annual activity reports of the General Directorate of Finance, as well as to the monitoring of the budgetary and financial indicators of the General Directorate (Metrics).

Manage several budget items and settle invoices for these items via FMS. Ensure reimbursement of the expenses of experts participating in hearings organized by the Bureau and the European Parliament's Observer to the EU. Participate as a member in the Public Procurement Forum.
09-10.refined_description:
Unit 09-10 functions as the Financial Control and Governance Directorate within the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), serving as the primary executive operational and coordinating center for financial integrity and regulatory compliance across the European Parliament. Reporting directly to the Secretary General through its parent unit DG 09, the unit operates as a Hierarchy Level 1 Direction with no subordinate units while maintaining functional coordination with sibling units 09-20, 09-30, 09A, 09B, and 09C to ensure comprehensive financial governance. The unit's core mission encompasses comprehensive financial transaction verification, procurement oversight, internal control coordination, and budgetary management, collectively supporting the institutional financial governance framework through systematic validation of data within the Parliament's integrated financial management systems (FMS and WebContracts). Unit 09-10 serves as the critical operational center for ex ante verification of all financial transactions, advisory services on procurement procedures, ex post control activities based on approved risk analysis, coordination of internal control framework improvements, budget preparation and execution monitoring, strategic planning contributions, and specialized administrative support functions. The unit's organizational positioning and coordinated relationships with peer units within DG 09 establish it as an essential institutional mechanism for ensuring financial transaction integrity, regulatory compliance, and governance effectiveness across the Parliament's operations.

---
09-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Transaction Verification and Validation** (54%): Execution of comprehensive ex ante verification procedures for all financial transactions including European political party and foundation contributions and subsidies, procurement activities, pension rights determinations and modifications for Members of Parliament, and pre-litigation/litigation files originating from the Directorate General of Finance, with systematic validation of corresponding transaction data within the Parliament's integrated financial management systems (FMS and WebContracts).

• **Procurement Management and Contract Administration** (14%): Provision of specialized advisory services to DG services on public tender calls and administrative agreements; coordination and Official Journal publication management; management of tenderer communications and evaluation committee operations; monitoring of contract execution and interinstitutional agreements; administration of the WebContracts application; and participation in the Public Procurement Forum and cross-institutional collaborative procurement projects.

• **Internal Control Framework and Risk Management** (8%): Execution of ex post controls based on approved risk analysis and multi-annual work programs; coordination of internal control framework evaluation and continuous improvement activities within the General Directorate of Finance; maintenance of general management's risk register; and participation in Inter-DG Steering Groups for Organizational Resilience and Finance.

• **Budget Management, Financial Reporting, and Stakeholder Communication** (7%): Preparation and monitoring of general management budget execution including credit transfer request processing; provision of regular budgetary and financial reporting to financial stakeholders; support to financial actors in FMS utilization; representation in FMS key users network; and verification of authorizing officer delegation authority.

• **Strategic Planning, Performance Monitoring, and Administrative Support** (10%): Contribution to annual work program preparation and periodic/annual activity reports; monitoring of budgetary and financial indicators (Metrics) for the General Directorate; management of specific budget items and invoice settlement via FMS; administration of expert expense reimbursement for Bureau hearings and European Parliament Observer activities; and participation in the Public Procurement Forum as an institutional member.

original_activities: Manage recovery procedures (articles 68 of the Implementing Measures of the Statute of Deputies - MAS).
Coordinate and respond to requests for access to documents as well as requests from the Press Unit and the Citizens Requests Unit.

Provide legal support to the units of the General Directorate of Finance in their various areas of intervention and to the Legal Service in the defense of European and national disputes concerning the files of the General Directorate.

Establish good administrative practices and guidelines including standard models, workflows, brochures and booklets, round tables, monitoring of case law developments and dissemination of relevant legal information.
Manage complaints from MPs (article 72 of the Implementing Measures of the Statute of MPs - MAS).
Promote compliance with the rules governing the processing of personal data and respond to requests related to the protection of this data.
Coordinate and monitor requests from national judicial authorities, OLAF and EPPO for the Directorate General of Finance.
Requests for exemption from publication of assistants' names.
09-20.refined_description: Unit 09-20 operates as a specialized legal and administrative governance directorate within the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), functioning as a Direction-level entity (Hierarchy Level 1) that serves as a critical compliance, procedural management, and legal oversight center with direct responsibility for maintaining institutional legal standards and administrative integrity across the Parliament's financial operations. As a leaf node within the DG 09 hierarchy and operating alongside sibling units (09-10, 09-30, 09A, 09B, 09C), the unit provides essential infrastructure for institutional accountability and regulatory adherence through four interconnected operational domains: disciplinary and administrative recovery procedures; comprehensive legal advisory services and dispute support; institutional governance and transparency management; and regulatory compliance including data protection and judicial authority coordination. The unit's specialized legal and administrative functions ensure adherence to the Statute of Deputies framework, protect fundamental rights including data privacy and information access, and support the operational integrity of the Finance Directorate's diverse functional areas through coordinated legal oversight. Unit 09-20 serves as a critical institutional safeguard, establishing and disseminating good administrative practices, standard operational models, workflows, and systematic legal information distribution while coordinating with the Legal Service, Press Unit, Citizens Requests Unit, and external investigative authorities (national judicial bodies, OLAF, EPPO) to ensure comprehensive financial governance and institutional compliance.
09-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Disciplinary and Administrative Recovery Procedures** (33%): Management of recovery procedures under Article 68 of the Implementing Measures of the Statute of Deputies (MAS), representing the unit's primary operational focus for institutional discipline and financial recovery processes.

• **Legal Advisory and Dispute Support Services** (14%): Provision of comprehensive legal support to all General Directorate of Finance operational units and coordination with the Legal Service on European and national dispute defense concerning DG Finance matters, ensuring legal compliance across institutional financial operations.

• **Institutional Governance, Transparency, and Administrative Standards** (27%): Establishment and dissemination of good administrative practices through development of standard operational models, workflows, and guidance documentation (12%); coordination and response to document access requests, Press Unit inquiries, and Citizens Requests Unit communications (15%); and processing of exemption requests for publication of assistants' names (1%).

• **Rights Protection and Compliance Management** (21%): Management of Member Parliament complaints under Article 72 of the MAS (10%); promotion of personal data protection compliance and processing of data protection-related requests under GDPR and institutional data governance standards (10%); and coordination and monitoring of requests from national judicial authorities, OLAF, and EPPO (5%).

• **Case Law Monitoring and Jurisprudential Analysis** (5%): Systematic monitoring of jurisprudential developments and dissemination of legal information to institutional stakeholders, supporting evidence-based administrative practice and regulatory compliance across the Finance Directorate.

original_activities: Under the supervision of the Director General of Finance, coordinate exercises relating to the definition and implementation of the strategic framework of the general management (administrative work program, annual objectives, etc.).
Define and implement the general management's policy regarding internal and external communication. Support and assist units and departments in carrying out communication projects.
Coordinate the identification of strategic innovation projects for general management. Ensure their resource planning and ensure their monitoring and change management.
Coordinate the exercise of the annual activity report of the general management.
Ensure the coordination of the monitoring of internships within the general management.
09-30.refined_description:
Unit 09-30 functions as the strategic coordination and executive support directorate within the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), operating as a first-level directional unit with direct supervisory oversight from the Director General. The unit serves as the institutional nexus for aligning financial administration operations with broader organizational objectives through five integrated functional domains that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation. Operating as a leaf node without subordinate units, Unit 09-30 functions as a peer entity alongside sibling directional units (09-10, 09-20) and specialized units (09A, 09B, 09C) within the Finance Directorate's operational structure, ensuring cohesive strategic management and institutional coordination. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive responsibilities spanning strategic framework definition and implementation, general management communication policy formulation and execution, strategic innovation project identification and oversight, institutional reporting and compliance documentation, and human capital development through internship coordination and monitoring. Unit 09-30 provides substantive support and technical assistance to other units and departments undertaking initiatives within its functional domains, positioning itself as a central coordination mechanism for the general management's strategic priorities and operational continuity.

##
09-30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Framework Coordination and Planning** (25%): Coordination of strategic framework definition and implementation exercises under Director General supervision, including development of administrative work programs and establishment of annual organizational objectives; serves as the primary mechanism for aligning institutional direction with operational execution

• **Communication Policy and Stakeholder Engagement** (25%): Definition, formulation, and execution of the general management's comprehensive internal and external communication policy; provision of substantive support and technical assistance to organizational units and departments undertaking communication initiatives and projects

• **Innovation Project Coordination and Change Management** (20%): Identification and coordination of strategic innovation projects across the general management; integrated resource planning allocation, monitoring oversight, and change management implementation to support institutional modernization and operational improvement

• **Annual Activity Reporting and Institutional Compliance** (15%): Coordination of the comprehensive preparation and compilation exercise of the general management's annual activity report; ensures documentation accuracy and institutional accountability through systematic reporting protocols

• **Human Capital Development and Internship Administration** (15%): Systematic oversight and coordination of internship monitoring activities and administrative functions across the general management structure; supports human capital development initiatives and ensures compliance with internship coordination frameworks

09A.name: 09A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the 5 units and services of the management.
Ensure relations with the budgetary bodies and financial stakeholders of the European Parliament.
Management of the EP Finance Academy.
Coordinate the management's current projects, including the institution's PPPs.
Coordinate the monitoring of authorizing officers' activity reports, audit reports and action plans.
Coordinate support activities for authorizing officers in all directorates general.
Coordinate budget preparation and execution work.
Ensure relations with the Court of Auditors.
09A.refined_description:
**

Unit 09A functions as the Direction-level executive and strategic coordination hub within the General Directorate of Finance (Unit 09), operating as the primary nexus connecting internal financial operations, institutional governance structures, and external financial oversight bodies. The unit exercises dual leadership and operational functions across five subordinate units and services (09A10, 09A20, 09A2010, 09A2020, 09A30, 09A40, and additional operational units), while maintaining supervisory relationships with sibling units 09-10, 09-20, 09-30, 09B, and 09C within the broader Finance Directorate General structure. Unit 09A's mission encompasses eight integrated activities that collectively establish organizational coherence, ensure institutional financial governance, and position the unit as the critical interface between internal management operations and external financial stakeholders. The unit directly manages the EP Finance Academy as the dedicated institutional center for financial competency development and professional training, coordinates the management directorate's portfolio of current projects including public-private partnerships, and orchestrates compliance monitoring through the tracking of authorizing officers' activity reports, audit findings, and associated remedial action plans. Through strategic engagement with the European Parliament's budgetary bodies, financial stakeholders, and the Court of Auditors, Unit 09A ensures that external audit engagement, accountability mechanisms, and financial governance negotiations function effectively while providing targeted operational support to authorizing officers across all directorates general and coordinating budget preparation and execution activities.

---

**
09A.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Strategic Direction and Internal Governance** (25%): Ensure comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of the five subordinate units and services within the management structure, establishing operational coherence and strategic alignment across the divisional portfolio while maintaining supervisory oversight of related operational units.

• **External Stakeholder Relations and Financial Governance** (30%): Maintain and strengthen relations with the European Parliament's budgetary bodies and financial stakeholders (25%), and ensure institutional relations with the Court of Auditors (5%), positioning Unit 09A as the primary liaison interface for external financial governance, audit coordination, and financial negotiations.

• **Institutional Capacity Building and Project Coordination** (25%): Manage the EP Finance Academy as the institutional center for financial competency development and professional training initiatives (15%), and coordinate the management directorate's current projects including public-private partnerships (10%), advancing strategic institutional initiatives and developing financial expertise.

• **Compliance Monitoring and Authorizing Officer Support** (15%): Coordinate the monitoring of authorizing officers' activity reports, audit reports, and corresponding action plans (10%), and coordinate targeted support activities for authorizing officers across all directorates general (5%), ensuring institutional compliance with audit findings, performance standards, and operational financial management processes.

• **Budget Administration and Execution Coordination** (5%): Coordinate budget preparation and execution work across the institution, ensuring fiscal planning and resource allocation alignment with institutional priorities and providing essential operational infrastructure for financial management processes throughout the European Parliament.

09A10.name: 09A10
original_activities: Prepare the Estimates of Parliament according to the objectives set by the political authorities and ensure the general coordination of the different stages of the budgetary procedure in liaison with the competent bodies.

Supervise the implementation of the budget in compliance with Parliament's budgetary priorities in a context of real reduction in the institution's financial resources. Prepare acts related to budget execution such as transfers, delegations from authorizing officers and financial statements in cooperation with the services and in liaison with the competent bodies.
Organize and follow budgetary and financial reporting, budget closing and discharge procedures.
Coordinate the monitoring of budgetary and discharge resolutions.

Support authorizing officers and various bodies, particularly through training in budgetary matters and contribute to the implementation of the Financial Regulations and the internal regulatory system.
Coordinate the audit work of the Court of Auditors concerning Parliament (annual report and special reports).

Contribute to the development and operation of Parliament's financial management system (FMS), in particular the modules relating to reporting, budgetary procedures and budgetary and analytical accounting.
Coordinate monitoring and advise on the financing of Parliament's real estate projects.
09A10.refined_description: Unit 09A10, the Budget Coordination and Financial Planning Unit, operates as a Level 2 operational hub within Direction 09A of the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), serving as the European Parliament's primary coordinating center for budgetary planning, execution, and financial governance. The unit exercises dual responsibility across Parliament's complete annual budgetary cycle: preparing the Estimates in alignment with political authority strategic objectives while ensuring comprehensive coordination across all budgetary procedure stages through active liaison with competent institutional bodies, and simultaneously supervising budget implementation in strict compliance with Parliament's budgetary priorities and real financial resource constraints. Unit 09A10 manages the full spectrum of budget execution instruments—including transfers, authorizing officer delegations, and financial statements—developed cooperatively with institutional services and relevant bodies, while administering comprehensive budgetary and financial reporting frameworks, managing budget closing procedures, and coordinating institutional discharge processes and monitoring mechanisms. The unit strengthens institutional capacity and regulatory compliance through specialized training for authorizing officers in budgetary matters, advancement of Financial Regulations implementation, coordination of Court of Auditors audit activities (annual and special reports), and substantive contributions to the development and operational management of Parliament's Financial Management System, particularly regarding reporting modules, budgetary procedures, and analytical accounting components. Additionally, Unit 09A10 coordinates monitoring activities and provides advisory services regarding the financing of Parliament's real estate project portfolio, operating in close coordination with sibling units within Direction 09A to ensure comprehensive financial governance across the institution.
09A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Budgetary Planning and Preparation** (28%): Prepare Parliament's Estimates according to political authority strategic objectives; ensure comprehensive coordination of all budgetary procedure stages in liaison with competent institutional bodies

• **Budget Execution and Implementation Oversight** (28%): Supervise budget implementation in compliance with Parliament's budgetary priorities amid real financial resource constraints; prepare and manage all budget execution acts including transfers, authorizing officer delegations, and financial statements in active cooperation with institutional services

• **Financial Reporting, Closure, and Discharge Management** (25%): Organize and monitor budgetary and financial reporting frameworks; manage budget closing procedures; coordinate institutional discharge processes and monitor implementation of budgetary and discharge resolutions

• **Capacity Building, Regulatory Compliance, and Audit Coordination** (10%): Support authorizing officers and institutional bodies through specialized training in budgetary matters and Financial Regulations implementation (5%); coordinate Court of Auditors audit activities concerning Parliament including annual and special reports (5%)

• **Financial Management System Development and Real Estate Project Financing** (9%): Contribute to development and operational management of Parliament's Financial Management System focusing on reporting modules, budgetary procedures, and analytical accounting components (5%); coordinate monitoring and provide advisory services on financing of Parliament's real estate project portfolio (4%)

09A20.name: 09A20
original_activities: Implement payments of the Parliament, Manage Parliament's treasury and bank accounts and optimize Parliament's cash flows.
Collect revenues and recover amounts due. Monitor recovery orders.
Supervise and enhance the accounting procedures of the Institution. Support, advise and train the financial actors, including Authorizing officers, in accounting matters.

Keep the budgetary and financial accounts of the Parliament, prepare, present and sign the annual accounts of the Parliament, and other financial reports in accordance with the Financial Regulation and Parliament's internal rules.
Open, close and control imprest account.
Manage, coordinate and animate the services and operations of the unit.
Validate the accounting systems and systems laid down by the Authorizing officers which supply or justify accounting information.
Prepare and update the rules and manuals and procedures regarding accounting and financial management and manage contacts with the Internal Auditor.
09A20.refined_description:
**

Unit 09A20 operates as a specialized financial operations and accounting unit within Direction 09A of the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), functioning as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for treasury management, payment implementation, accounting oversight, and comprehensive financial governance. The unit executes eight interconnected activities that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload, positioning it as an indispensable operational and governance mechanism supporting the Parliament's financial integrity and institutional accountability. As a leaf-node unit within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 09A20 operates under the strategic direction of its parent Direction 09A while maintaining functional coordination with sibling units (09A10, 09A2010, 09A2020, 09A30, 09A40, and one additional unit) to ensure integrated financial governance across the Parliament. The unit's mission encompasses the comprehensive execution and oversight of the Parliament's financial transactions, treasury management, accounting governance, revenue collection, and debt recovery operations, all executed in strict accordance with the Financial Regulation and the Parliament's internal governance rules. Through its multifaceted operational portfolio, Unit 09A20 delivers critical supervisory and enhancement functions, provides essential advisory services and professional development, maintains custodianship of the Parliament's budgetary and financial accounts, and administers operational support functions that collectively strengthen the institution's financial capability, compliance posture, and liquidity management efficiency.

**
09A20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Treasury and Payment Operations** (30%): Implement all Parliament payments, manage the institution's treasury and bank accounts, and execute strategic cash flow optimization measures to ensure efficient liquidity management and institutional financial efficiency.

• **Revenue Collection and Debt Recovery** (20%): Systematically collect institutional revenues and recover amounts due to the Parliament, with continuous monitoring of recovery orders to ensure comprehensive financial resource protection and accountability.

• **Accounting Governance and Professional Development** (15%): Supervise and enhance the Parliament's accounting procedures, provide comprehensive support and advisory services, and deliver training to Authorizing Officers and other financial actors in accounting methodology, best practices, and compliance matters.

• **Financial Reporting and Account Custodianship** (10%): Maintain comprehensive budgetary and financial accounts, prepare, present, and sign annual accounts and additional financial reports in strict accordance with the Financial Regulation and Parliament's internal governance rules.

• **Imprest Account Administration and Control** (10%): Execute opening, closure, and systematic control functions for imprest accounts to ensure proper account lifecycle management and institutional fund accountability.

• **Accounting Systems Validation and Information Governance** (5%): Validate accounting systems and officer-supplied information systems established by Authorizing Officers to ensure data integrity, system reliability, and compliance with institutional standards.

• **Financial Management Rules Development and Internal Audit Liaison** (5%): Develop, maintain, and enhance accounting and financial management rules, manuals, and procedures while managing ongoing liaison and coordination with the Internal Auditor to support continuous governance improvement.

• **Unit Management and Service Coordination** (5%): Execute strategic management and coordination of unit services and internal operations to ensure efficient resource utilization and organizational effectiveness.

09A2010.name: 09A2010
original_activities: Ensure the proper execution of payments and manage the Parliament's treasury operations in compliance with the Financial Regulations and Parliament's internal rules.

Create and maintain vendor master data as regards bank account information for staff members and Accredited parliamentary assistants and validate bank account information for Members and their service providers.
Optimise the Institution's cash flow (including bank supplies).
Manage the information on financial institutions.
Maintain contacts with the banks and respond to enquiries.
09A2010.refined_description: Unit 09A2010 functions as a specialized operational unit within Direction 09A (the coordinating hub of the General Directorate of Finance) and serves as the European Parliament's central treasury execution and banking operations center. The unit bears primary responsibility for comprehensive management of the Parliament's treasury operations and payment execution infrastructure, ensuring strict compliance with the Financial Regulations and internal procedural rules while orchestrating all institutional financial transactions. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate organizational structures, Unit 09A2010 collaborates with sibling units (09A10, 09A20, 09A2020, 09A30, 09A40, and additional peer units) under the coordinating direction of parent unit 09A, thereby contributing to the integrated financial governance framework of the institution. The unit's integrated operational activities span five critical domains: payment execution and treasury management operations (60% of workload); vendor master data creation, maintenance, and validation including bank account information for staff members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, Members, and their designated service providers (10% of workload); cash flow optimization and liquidity management including coordination of bank supplies and working capital efficiency (10% of workload); maintenance and management of comprehensive information systems regarding authorized financial institutions and their operational parameters (10% of workload); and cultivation and management of active banking relationships while responding to institutional enquiries regarding financial services (10% of workload). Together, these integrated activities position Unit 09A2010 as the Parliament's primary treasury execution center, directly supporting the strategic financial oversight responsibilities of Direction 09A while maintaining operational excellence across all payment and banking operations.
09A2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Treasury and Payment Execution** (60%): Ensures proper execution of all Parliament payments and comprehensive management of treasury operations in strict compliance with Financial Regulations and internal institutional procedural rules

• **Vendor and Account Data Management** (10%): Creates, maintains, and validates vendor master data and bank account information for staff members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, Members, and their designated service providers

• **Cash Flow and Liquidity Optimization** (10%): Optimizes the institution's cash flow management and working capital efficiency, including coordination and administration of bank supplies

• **Financial Institution Information Systems** (10%): Maintains and manages comprehensive information systems regarding authorized financial institutions and their operational parameters

• **Banking Relations and Stakeholder Engagement** (10%): Establishes and maintains active relationships with banking partners while providing responsive support to institutional enquiries regarding financial services and operations

09A2020.name: 09A2020
original_activities: Create, manage and control imprest accounts.
Follow-up recovery orders, manage debtor master data, issue payment reminders and contribute to litigations and other legal proceedings against the debtors.
Manage VAT reimbursement claims and recoveries.
Train, advise and support the Authorizing officers and their services in matters related to VAT, recovery orders and imprest accounts.
09A2020.refined_description: Unit 09A2020 operates as a specialized financial management and compliance unit within Direction 09A of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), functioning as a critical operational center for institutional financial accountability, debtor management, and fiscal optimization. As a leaf-node unit coordinating alongside sibling units (09A10, 09A20, 09A2010, 09A30, 09A40, and one additional unit) under Direction 09A's strategic coordination, Unit 09A2020 maintains dual operational responsibilities encompassing financial account administration and comprehensive debtor management within the Parliament's broader financial governance framework. The unit's activities are systematically structured across four integrated operational domains that collectively ensure the institution's financial integrity, regulatory compliance, and asset protection. These operational functions position Unit 09A2020 as a cornerstone of the Parliament's financial compliance infrastructure, serving simultaneously as an internal subject matter expert resource for institutional stakeholder capacity-building while protecting institutional financial interests through active recovery and legal proceedings management.
