The unit's work directly supports Authorizing Officers and their respective services in executing their financial responsibilities while optimizing the Parliament's fiscal position through systematic VAT management and recovery mechanisms.
09A2020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Account Administration & Custodial Control** (30%): Creation, management, and comprehensive control of imprest accounts to ensure proper custodial oversight, regulatory compliance, and continuous institutional oversight of advance payment mechanisms and institutional funds held in advance

• **Debtor Management & Legal Recovery Operations** (30%): Systematic follow-up of recovery orders, meticulous management of debtor master data systems, issuance of payment reminders, and active substantive support to litigations and other legal proceedings against debtors to protect and safeguard institutional financial claims and interests

• **VAT Compliance & Reimbursement Management** (25%): Comprehensive management of Value Added Tax reimbursement claims and associated recovery processes, ensuring regulatory compliance with applicable tax regulations and optimization of institutional fiscal recovery mechanisms

• **Internal Advisory Services & Institutional Capacity Development** (15%): Provision of specialized training, advisory services, and operational support to Authorizing Officers and their respective services regarding VAT administration procedures, recovery order management, and imprest account administration, thereby enhancing institutional capacity and compliance across the Parliament's financial ecosystem

09A30.name: 09A30
original_activities: Provide support and advice to Parliament's financial actors on financial management and procurement rules, including through a helpdesk and the establishment and updating of guidelines on specific subjects.
Offer training courses and workshops to Parliament's secretariat on financial management and public procurement.
Provide the secretariat of the Public Procurement Forum and facilitate (inter)institutional coordination of all procurement issues.
Prepare and facilitate the introduction of digital procurement and the innovation of procurement procedures in Parliament.

Prepare legal reporting concerning contracts awarded by Parliament, the report on payment deadlines and other reports on finances and procurement. Contribute to the analysis of the annual activity reports of the authorising officers by delegation.
Revise and update Parliament's internal financial rules.
09A30.refined_description: Unit 09A30 operates as a specialized financial management and procurement support unit within Direction 09A of the General Directorate of Finance (DG 09), serving as the European Parliament's primary institutional center of expertise for financial governance, public procurement excellence, and regulatory compliance. The unit delivers comprehensive advisory services and capacity-building support to Parliament's financial actors across multiple operational domains through an integrated service model that combines direct helpdesk support, authoritative guideline development, and professional training delivery. Unit 09A30 maintains strategic responsibility for the Public Procurement Forum's secretariat operations and facilitates inter-institutional coordination mechanisms addressing all procurement-related policy and operational matters across Parliament and partner EU institutions. The unit actively drives organizational modernization through the preparation, facilitation, and implementation of digital procurement systems and the systematic innovation of procurement procedures to enhance institutional efficiency and transparency. Unit 09A30 fulfills critical financial accountability functions by preparing legally-compliant reports on Parliament's awarded contracts, analyzing payment deadline performance metrics, generating comprehensive financial and procurement analyses, and contributing substantive analysis to annual activity reports submitted by authorizing officers by delegation. The unit maintains ongoing responsibility for the periodic revision and updating of Parliament's internal financial rules framework to ensure regulatory currency and institutional compliance. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 09A30 collaborates with sibling units (09A10, 09A20, 09A2010, 09A2020, 09A40, and others) under Direction 09A's strategic oversight to deliver integrated financial governance across the institution.
09A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Advisory Services and Institutional Guidance** (30%): Provision of direct support and specialized advice to Parliament's financial actors through dedicated operational helpdesk services; establishment, maintenance, and periodic updating of comprehensive, subject-specific guidelines on financial management and public procurement regulations to ensure institutional compliance and operational clarity

• **Capacity Building and Professional Development** (25%): Design and delivery of specialized training courses and professional development workshops to Parliament's secretariat personnel focused on financial management and public procurement competency development and procedural proficiency

• **Governance Coordination and Forum Administration** (15%): Secretariat support for the Public Procurement Forum; facilitation of inter-institutional coordination mechanisms addressing all procurement-related policy and operational matters across Parliament and partner EU institutions

• **Digital Innovation and Procurement Modernization** (15%): Strategic preparation and facilitation of digital procurement system implementation; systematic innovation and modernization of Parliament's procurement procedures to enhance operational efficiency and institutional transparency

• **Financial Accountability and Regulatory Stewardship** (10%): Preparation of legally-compliant reports on Parliament's awarded contracts and payment deadline performance analysis; substantive contribution to analysis of annual activity reports submitted by authorizing officers by delegation; continuous revision and updating of Parliament's internal financial rules framework to maintain regulatory currency (5%)

09A40.name: 09A40
original_activities: Ensure the evolutionary and corrective maintenance of IT projects put into production by the unit and manage updates linked either to upgrades of IT versions or to modifications introduced by Parliament to its processes.

Implement the IT strategy of the General Directorate of Finance with regard to the replacement of the General Directorate's tools with products based on SAP solutions similar to those used by the projects managed by the unit.
Provide user support (including training) for projects under the responsibility of the unit.
Implement new IT projects and coordinate the internal and external resources necessary for their implementation.
Prepare and manage contracts with external service providers; take care of the financial initiation of the unit.
Coordinate with the units of the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity which provide support to the unit in terms of infrastructure, IT security and interfacing.
Ensure communication and cooperation with Parliament services using applications under the responsibilities of the unit.
09A40.refined_description: Unit 09A40 operates as a specialized IT systems management and strategic implementation unit within Direction 09A of the General Directorate of Finance, serving as the primary custodian of financial application systems and their enterprise resource planning infrastructure. The unit functions as a critical enabler of the General Directorate's digital transformation objectives while maintaining operational continuity and institutional service delivery standards across the European Parliament's financial ecosystem. Operating across seven distinct functional domains that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation, Unit 09A40 manages the complete lifecycle of SAP-based solutions and related financial IT projects, from production deployment through modernization initiatives. The unit's operational mandate encompasses evolutionary and corrective maintenance of production systems, strategic implementation of legacy tool replacement with SAP-based solutions, comprehensive user support and capability development, new project implementation with coordinated resource management, vendor contract administration with financial stewardship, critical infrastructure and security coordination with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, and stakeholder communication with Parliament services. These integrated responsibilities position the unit as an essential institutional function that simultaneously delivers immediate operational support while advancing the General Directorate's strategic modernization agenda and ensuring compliance with parliamentary IT governance requirements.
09A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Systems Maintenance and Operational Management** (35%): Ensure evolutionary and corrective maintenance of all IT projects deployed into production; manage updates necessitated by IT infrastructure version upgrades and Parliament process modifications; maintain operational continuity of managed systems

• **Strategic IT Modernization and SAP Implementation** (25%): Implement the General Directorate of Finance's IT modernization strategy through systematic replacement of legacy financial tools with SAP-based product solutions; coordinate strategic implementation initiatives aligned with unit-managed infrastructure

• **User Support Services and Capability Building** (20%): Provide comprehensive user support encompassing technical assistance and training delivery for all projects under unit responsibility; develop institutional capability and user competency for managed applications

• **New Project Implementation and Resource Coordination** (10%): Implement newly mandated IT projects and coordinate both internal and external resources required for successful deployment; manage integration of new initiatives within existing operational framework

• **Vendor Management, Contract Administration, and Financial Stewardship** (5%): Prepare and manage contracts with external service providers; execute financial initiation and stewardship responsibilities for unit operations; ensure vendor accountability and institutional fiscal compliance

• **Infrastructure Coordination and IT Security Integration** (3%): Coordinate with General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity units for infrastructure support, IT security compliance, and technical system interfacing; ensure seamless integration of security protocols and infrastructure requirements

• **Stakeholder Communication and Institutional Liaison** (2%): Maintain communication and operational cooperation with Parliament services utilizing applications under unit jurisdiction; ensure institutional alignment and user satisfaction across the parliamentary ecosystem

09A50.name: 09A50
original_activities: Record invoices received by the Parliament for all the Directorates-General. Promote and on-board suppliers to use E-invoicing.

Provide central technical support to all Directorates-General in inventory management. Monitor the implementation of the inventory rules and its implementing measures and inventory data quality. Follow-up and support annual inventory tracking.

Prepare the annual financial statements including budgetary reports of the Parliament. To this end, prepare annual internal closure instructions, execute annual closure process in FMS, prepare the reporting package for the European Commission and reconcile the budgetary and general accounting of the Parliament.
Create, verify, and maintain Business Partner master data for third parties of the European Parliament, including legal entity and bank account information.

Manage accounting master data in FMS: chart of accounts, financial statement versions, set up of accounting transactions. Analyse and monitor general ledger (GL) suspense accounts, perform analytical reviews to ensure accounting data quality. Work on analytical accounting development using cost accounting module in FMS.

Support, advise and train the financial actors/end-users of the Directorates-General on asset accounting and inventory management. Supervise and enhance the accounting procedures, including supporting and advising Authorizing Officers services in accounting and inventory management.
Verify and validate budgetary and asset transactions in FMS.
Ensure contacts with the European Commission and with the European Court of Auditors concerning European Union consolidated accounts.
09A50.refined_description: Unit 09A50 operates as the specialized financial operations and accounting management backbone within Direction 09A of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Finance, serving as the central technical hub for financial administration, transactional processing, and institutional financial data governance across all Directorates-General. The unit maintains comprehensive responsibility for the complete lifecycle of financial transaction processing, accounting data integrity, and institutional financial reporting, with integrated focus on invoice management, inventory control, master data governance, and regulatory compliance. Unit 09A50 ensures the Parliament's financial transparency, data integrity, and operational efficiency by executing interconnected activities spanning transactional processing, accounting infrastructure management, financial reporting, transaction validation, and institutional liaison with external oversight bodies. Through its dual role as both operational processor and governance administrator, the unit coordinates financial management frameworks across the Parliament while maintaining regulatory alignment with the European Commission and European Court of Auditors regarding consolidated European Union accounts. The unit's mission encompasses supporting all financial actors and Directorates-General through comprehensive advisory services, training, and procedural supervision while simultaneously executing technical accounting operations that form the foundation of the Parliament's financial management system.
09A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Transactional Processing & E-Invoicing Modernization** (20%): Recording invoices received by the Parliament across all Directorates-General; promoting and onboarding suppliers to E-invoicing platforms to modernize procurement processes and enhance transactional efficiency

• **Inventory Management, Asset Accounting & Institutional Support** (20%): Providing centralized technical support for inventory management systems across all Directorates-General; monitoring implementation of inventory rules and measures; ensuring data quality standards; coordinating annual inventory tracking and follow-up activities; delivering comprehensive advisory services, training, and procedural guidance to Directorates-General and Authorizing Officers on asset accounting and inventory management procedures

• **Financial Statements Preparation, Closure & External Reporting** (15%): Preparing annual financial statements and comprehensive budgetary reports; drafting annual internal closure instructions; executing annual closure processes within the Financial Management System; preparing reporting packages for submission to the European Commission; reconciling budgetary and general accounting records

• **Master Data Governance & Accounting Infrastructure Management** (25%): Creating, verifying, and maintaining Business Partner master data for all third-party relationships including legal entity information and banking details (15%); managing accounting master data in the Financial Management System including chart of accounts configuration, financial statement versions, and general ledger suspense account analysis; developing cost accounting modules; performing analytical reviews to ensure accounting data quality

• **Financial Transaction Validation, Procedural Enhancement & Institutional Liaison** (20%): Verifying and validating budgetary and asset transactions within the Financial Management System (7%); supervising procedural enhancements and advising Authorizing Officers services on accounting procedures and controls (10%); maintaining institutional liaison and contacts with the European Commission and European Court of Auditors regarding consolidated European Union accounts (3%)

09B.name: 09B
original_activities: Provide leadership, coordination and facilitation for the four units of the Directorate, including on staff matters.

Ensure coordination, monitoring of files and management of Members' financial and social entitlements (salaries, general expenditure allowance and social entitlements; travel and subsistence expenses; local parliamentary assistance) under the Statute for Members and its Implementing Measures and in compliance with the Financial Regulation; Manage the welcome and departure of Members; Provide elements for reply and background information to the hierarchy on issues raised by Members.

Ensure the progress of digitalisation projects for the modernisation of services in relation to Members' financial and social entitlements: Develop the e-Portal into the single digital platform for Members' interaction with the administration; Biometric register for Members' attestation of attendance; Car Trip App for simplifying the reimbursement of Members' travel by car; App for consulting the e-Portal on mobile phones.

Provide information to Members and their assistants on Members' financial and social entitlements: Organise general info-sessions in cooperation with the units; Coordinate the preparation of publications for Members, such as the FAQs and various other brochures; Manage and update the relevant content of the European Parliament intranet pages; Coordinate the drafting of notices and notes for the Quaestors and the Bureau; Coordinate the production of video tutorials.
Chair the PAM Steering Committee and the e-Portal Programme Board.

Carry out consultations, studies and surveys in relation to the Directorate's remit to ensure implementation and further development of the regulatory framework and to provide guidance on its interpretation.
09B.refined_description: Unit 09B functions as the Direction-level executive and strategic coordination hub within the General Directorate of Finance (Directorate 09), operating at Hierarchy Level 1 with primary responsibility for comprehensive leadership, coordination, and facilitation across four subordinate operational units (09B10, 09B20, 09B30, and 09B40). The unit's core mandate encompasses dual operational pillars: (1) strategic directional guidance on staff and administrative matters for the Directorate, and (2) systematic administration and monitoring of all Members' financial and social entitlements—including salaries, general expenditure allowances, social entitlements, travel and subsistence expenses, and local parliamentary assistance—executed in strict compliance with the Statute for Members, its Implementing Measures, and the Financial Regulation. Unit 09B manages the complete administrative lifecycle of Members' institutional engagement through welcome and departure protocols, hierarchical support on Member-raised issues, and background information provision. The unit drives modernization of Members' services through strategic oversight of four interconnected digitalization initiatives: development and enhancement of the e-Portal as the unified digital interaction platform, implementation of biometric attendance registration systems, deployment of the Car Trip App for streamlined travel reimbursement, and creation of mobile-accessible e-Portal applications. Unit 09B ensures comprehensive stakeholder communication through organized information sessions, coordinated publication development (FAQs and informational materials), intranet content management, guidance documentation for Quaestors and the Bureau, and video tutorial production. The unit exercises governance authority through chairing both the PAM Steering Committee and the e-Portal Programme Board, while conducting consultations, analytical studies, and surveys to support continuous implementation, development, and interpretative guidance of the regulatory framework governing Members' entitlements and related administrative processes.
09B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership, Governance, and Directorate Coordination** (35%): Provide comprehensive executive leadership and cross-unit coordination for the four subordinate units of the Directorate; manage all staff-related matters and administrative governance; chair the PAM Steering Committee and e-Portal Programme Board; exercise directional oversight of integrated service delivery across the Directorate.

• **Members' Financial and Social Entitlements Administration and Compliance Management** (30%): Ensure systematic coordination and monitoring of all Members' financial and social entitlements (salaries, general expenditure allowances, social entitlements, travel and subsistence expenses, local parliamentary assistance) in full compliance with the Statute for Members, its Implementing Measures, and the Financial Regulation; manage Member welcome and departure procedures; provide hierarchical support with Member-raised issue responses and background information.

• **Digital Service Modernization and Platform Development** (15%): Direct and oversee digitalization projects modernizing Members' services and administrative interactions, including e-Portal development and enhancement as the unified digital platform, implementation of biometric member attendance registration systems, deployment of the Car Trip App for travel reimbursement simplification, and development of mobile-accessible e-Portal applications.

• **Member Information Dissemination and Communication Services** (10%): Deliver comprehensive information services to Members and their assistants through organized information sessions, coordinated publication development (FAQs and informational brochures), intranet content management and updates, coordination of guidance documentation and notices for Quaestors and Bureau, and video tutorial production.

• **Regulatory Framework Development and Interpretative Guidance** (10%): Conduct consultations, analytical studies, and comprehensive surveys relating to the Directorate's remit; ensure implementation and ongoing development of the regulatory framework governing Members' entitlements; provide authoritative guidance on framework interpretation and application.

09B10.name: 09B10
original_activities: Manage and communicate on Members' statutory entitlements related to the reimbursement of medical expenses under the EU Scheme, insurances (accident, loss and theft, and assistance insurance) and reimbursement of health insurance contributions; transmit requests and exchange information with the Commission's Settlement Office and the relevant insurance providers.

Establish statutory pension rights in accordance with the Members' Statute of 2009, liaise with the Commission's Settlement Office on pension matters and inform Members on pension rights and obligations. Handle establishment and payment of pensions prior to 2009, ensuring alignment with applicable national pension rules. Coordinate the management of the additional pension with the Additional (Voluntary) Pension Fund.
Support IT projects and innovations to streamline workflows and maximize the efficiency of processing and reporting tools.

Ensure accurate and timely salary payments, including recoveries, enforce rules prohibiting concurrent receipt of salaries; Maintain secure, up-to-date bank account details of Members, serving all units within the Directorate.

Ensure the effective monitoring and management of the general expenditure allowance by executing monthly payments, carrying out deductions for related items, verifying absences, and processing recoveries in compliance with applicable rules.

Manage and monitor the unit's budget lines; compile and provide statistical data for Eurostat and actuarial studies, prepare general reporting; ensure effective public procurement and contract management for insurance and actuarial studies.
Manage the payment of transitional allowance; ensure the offsetting of incompatible positions, and conduct compliance checks in accordance with applicable offsetting rules.
09B10.refined_description: Unit 09B10 operates as a specialized operational hub within Directorate 09B of the General Directorate of Finance, functioning as the primary administrative center for the comprehensive financial and social entitlements of European Parliament Members under the Members' Statute of 2009 and related regulatory frameworks. As a leaf-level operational unit at Hierarchy Level 2, alongside sibling units 09B20, 09B30, and 09B40 within the 09B Direction structure, Unit 09B10 manages the complete lifecycle of Members' statutory benefits and compensation through seven integrated activity streams that directly enable the Parliament's institutional operations and Member support infrastructure. The unit executes transactional and compliance services encompassing medical benefits administration, pension rights establishment and management, salary payment processing, allowance management, and operational governance—all delivered through systematic liaison with the Commission's Settlement Office, external insurance providers, and relevant regulatory bodies. Through these integrated activities, Unit 09B10 ensures transparent, compliant, and efficient administration of Members' statutory entitlements while supporting the Parliament's broader financial governance framework and institutional compliance obligations.
09B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Members' Medical Benefits and Insurance Administration** (38%): Manage and communicate Members' statutory entitlements for medical expense reimbursement under the EU Scheme; administer accident, loss, theft, and assistance insurance provisions; coordinate health insurance contribution reimbursements; systematically exchange information and transmit requests with the Commission's Settlement Office and external insurance providers

• **Pension Rights Establishment and Retirement Benefits Management** (23%): Establish statutory pension rights in accordance with the Members' Statute of 2009; liaise with the Commission's Settlement Office on pension-related matters; inform Members of pension rights and obligations; administer pre-2009 pension arrangements aligned with applicable national pension rules; coordinate management of the additional voluntary pension through the Additional (Voluntary) Pension Fund

• **Salary Administration and Member Financial Records Management** (10%): Execute accurate and timely salary payments including recoveries; enforce statutory rules prohibiting concurrent salary receipt; maintain secure, up-to-date bank account details for all Members serving all Directorate units

• **Expenditure Allowances and Transitional Payment Administration** (11%): Monitor and manage general expenditure allowance through monthly payments, deductions, absence verification, and recovery processing (6%); manage transitional allowance payments, ensure offsetting of incompatible positions, and conduct compliance verification (5%)

• **Systems Optimization, Budget Management, Reporting, and Procurement Governance** (18%): Support IT projects and innovations to streamline workflows and enhance processing and reporting tool efficiency (12%); manage unit budget lines, compile statistical data for Eurostat and actuarial studies, prepare general reporting, and oversee effective public procurement and contract management for insurance and actuarial services (6%)

09B20.name: 09B20
original_activities: Manage the parliamentary assistance allowance: inform and advise Members and their assistants; ensure that incomplete applications are completed and missing information received; ensure compliance with the rules of the files presented relating to local assistants (including annual accounting regularisation), service providers and trainees; check invoices and other supporting documents relating to service providers; carry out controls including proof of work controls.
Continue to adapt IT-tools and test new functionalities in order to maximise the efficiency of the use of IT-tools.

Use experience gained from the detailed analyses of circa 2000 Members' files/month by providing experience based replies to questions from Members and providing them with support and advice regarding the use of parliamentary assistance, especially in areas where analyses show Members have problems following the rules.

Contribute to or carry out analyses in relation to the use of parliamentary assistance allowance and provide replies and information to OLAF, national authorities, EPPO as well as to citizens regarding questions coming in via transparency requests.
Manage archiving documents in accordance with Parliament and the Directorate-General for Finance rules.
Manage Members' parliamentary assistance envelopes, and ensure that budgetary appropriations are sufficient to cover Members' requests.
Carry-out ex-post controls on already treated files in order to verify that funds have been used in accordance with rules.
Implementation of revised IMMS on time for the new legislative term.
09B20.refined_description: Unit 09B20 operates as a specialized administrative and compliance hub within Directorate 09B (Direction level) of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Finance, serving as the primary operational interface for comprehensive parliamentary assistance allowance management across all Member portfolios. The unit's core mandate encompasses a multi-layered operational framework: administration and validation of parliamentary assistance funds through information and advisory services to Members and their assistants, application completeness verification, regulatory compliance monitoring for local assistants (including annual accounting regularization procedures), service provider and trainee oversight, rigorous invoice and supporting document verification, and comprehensive proof-of-work controls. Leveraging institutional expertise derived from systematic monthly analysis of approximately 2,000 Members' files, Unit 09B20 provides evidence-based guidance to Members regarding parliamentary assistance regulations and compliance challenges while conducting analytical investigations into allowance utilization patterns and preparing responses to inquiries from external oversight bodies (OLAF, national authorities, EPPO) and citizen transparency requests. The unit additionally executes continuous IT infrastructure optimization and functionality testing to enhance operational efficiency, manages Members' parliamentary assistance budget envelopes and appropriations oversight, administers documentary archiving in compliance with institutional regulatory frameworks, performs ex-post controls on previously processed files to verify regulatory compliance, and oversees timely implementation of revised IMMS systems for new legislative terms. As a coordinated operational unit within Directorate 09B, Unit 09B20 functions as a critical compliance and administrative interface ensuring transparency, regulatory adherence, and efficient resource management across the parliamentary assistance allowance system while maintaining accountability to both internal parliamentary structures and external oversight mechanisms.
09B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Assistance Administration & Compliance Management** (60%): Core operational focus encompassing allowance administration, Member and assistant advisory services, application processing and completeness verification, regulatory compliance monitoring for local assistants (with annual accounting regularization), service provider and trainee oversight, invoice and supporting document verification, and comprehensive proof-of-work control procedures.

• **Institutional Knowledge Transfer, Investigation & External Accountability** (18%): Experience-based advisory services derived from systematic analysis of approximately 2,000 Members' files monthly; analytical examination of parliamentary assistance allowance utilization patterns; preparation of investigative responses to OLAF, national authorities, EPPO inquiries; and management of citizen transparency requests regarding parliamentary assistance fund usage.

• **IT Infrastructure Optimization & System Implementation** (10%): Continuous adaptation and functionality testing of IT-tools for operational efficiency enhancement; timely implementation of revised IMMS systems for legislative term transitions.

• **Financial & Documentary Administration** (12%): Parliamentary assistance envelope management and budgetary appropriation monitoring/adjustment (6%); documentary archiving in compliance with institutional regulations (5%); ex-post controls verification of fund utilization regulatory compliance (2%).

09B30.name: 09B30
original_activities: Payment of MPs' travel and living expenses: Receive, record, control and pay MPs' travel and living expenses, automatically by integrating data from IT systems or on the basis of expense declarations; Inform and advise MPs and their assistants; Ensure reconciliation with trips paid for by groups; Carry out the payment of invoices issued by the EP travel agency.

Managing innovation: Automating administrative processes for MPs; Projects: iTEMS, Car Trip, e-Portal, automated and paperless system for attesting the presence of MPs, 'auto-flown' system for train travel, 'fast-track' payment; SAP Concur project; Strategic reporting using Power-BI.

Payment of deputies' daily allowances: Ensure the maintenance of the central register of signatures; Manage and record the attendance of MPs entitling them to reimbursement of travel and accommodation expenses; Validate the financial forms and coordinate with other services of the General Secretariat information relating to external meetings.

Provide payment support: Manage resources, logistics and databases; Carry out the accounting management of the agency allowing reimbursements in accordance with the Financial Regulations; Monitor, evaluate, report on the evolution of expenses, budgets and risks, and collaborate with the various supervisory authorities; Ensure operational reporting via SAP-BO.

Payment of professional training costs of deputies and representation costs of Members of the Bureau: Receive, record, control and pay professional training costs of deputies; Receive, record, control and pay the expenses of Bureau Members incurred within the framework of their mandate.

Other missions: Review the organization of work in general in order to optimize procedures; Ensure good financial management of the various budget lines; Provide an induction program for new colleagues in the unit and train unit staff on an ongoing basis.
09B30.refined_description: Unit 09B30 functions as a specialized operational and financial processing center within Directorate 09B (General Directorate of Finance) of the European Parliament, serving as the primary administrative hub for the comprehensive management, validation, and payment of all financial obligations owed to Members of Parliament. Operating at Hierarchy Level 2 as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate structures, Unit 09B30's core mission encompasses the systematic receipt, recording, control, and payment of Members' travel and living expenses (39% of workload), management of daily allowances linked to verified parliamentary attendance (17% of workload), processing of professional training costs for deputies and representation expenses for Bureau Members (7% of workload), and provision of essential payment support infrastructure (14% of workload). The unit executes these functions through dual-track processing mechanisms combining automated integration with institutional IT systems and manual processing of expense declarations, while simultaneously maintaining advisory services for MPs and their assistants, reconciling group-sponsored travel arrangements, processing EP travel agency invoices, and administering centralized signature registers and attendance documentation systems. Unit 09B30 dedicates substantial resources (17% of workload) to modernizing administrative processes through implementation and maintenance of advanced technological systems—including iTEMS, Car Trip, e-Portal, automated attendance verification systems, automated train travel processing, fast-track payment mechanisms, SAP Concur project integration, and Power-BI strategic reporting capabilities—that collectively enhance transparency and streamline reimbursement workflows. The unit's operational infrastructure encompasses resource and logistics management, database administration, accounting management in compliance with Financial Regulations, systematic monitoring of expense evolution and budget allocation, risk assessment, and operational reporting via SAP-BO systems to supervisory authorities and General Secretariat services. Complementing these core functions, Unit 09B30 dedicates 6% of workload to organizational development through procedural optimization via formal reviews, ensuring sound financial stewardship of budget line allocations, and delivering comprehensive induction and ongoing professional development programs for unit personnel. As a coordinated component within the Finance Directorate's broader portfolio, Unit 09B30 operates under the strategic direction of Direction 09B while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units 09B10, 09B20, and 09B40.
09B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Members' Financial Entitlements Processing** (56%): Comprehensive receipt, recording, control, and payment of Members' travel and living expenses through both automated IT system integration and manual expense declaration processing; management and validation of daily allowances linked to verified parliamentary attendance; processing of professional training costs for deputies and representation expenses for Bureau Members; provision of advisory services to MPs and their assistants; reconciliation of group-sponsored travel arrangements; processing of EP travel agency invoices; and maintenance of centralized signature registers and attendance documentation systems.

• **Financial Management, Payment Support Services, and Operational Infrastructure** (14%): Resource and logistics management; database administration and maintenance; accounting management and reimbursement processing in strict accordance with European Parliament Financial Regulations; systematic monitoring, evaluation, and reporting on expense evolution, budget performance, and institutional financial risk exposure; operational reporting via SAP-BO systems to supervisory authorities; and coordination with General Secretariat services regarding external meeting documentation.

• **Administrative Innovation, Process Modernization, and Strategic Reporting** (17%): Development, implementation, and maintenance of process automation initiatives including iTEMS, Car Trip project, e-Portal, automated attendance verification systems, automated train travel processing, fast-track payment mechanisms, and SAP Concur project integration; deployment of Power-BI strategic reporting infrastructure for data-driven financial analytics and institutional decision-making support; systematic modernization of administrative procedures for Members.

• **Organizational Development, Procedural Optimization, and Personnel Management** (6%): Comprehensive review and optimization of unit work organization and administrative procedures to enhance operational efficiency; ensuring sound financial stewardship and management across all budget line allocations; provision of structured induction programs for new unit personnel; and ongoing professional training and capacity development initiatives for existing staff members.

09B40.name: 09B40
original_activities: Answer questions from MPs and their assistants regarding their financial and social rights as defined in the statute of MPs and its implementing measures; Receive forms, carry out preliminary checks, forward them to operational units, record any transaction in the tracking system, send acknowledgments of receipt and refusal notifications.

Organize individual and personalized advice sessions on the financial and social rights of deputies, as well as on the management of the parliamentary assistance envelope; Constantly identify and analyze the needs of MPs, in order to advise them proactively and tailor-made; Propose appropriate solutions regarding the management of their parliamentary assistance envelope over the duration of the mandate.

Organize group information sessions for MPs who request them on their various rights, in particular on the parliamentary assistance envelope; Develop support and visual material to improve MPs’ understanding of the rules.

Develop a strategic framework for the systematic organization of tools and information made available to MPs; Contribute to the development and promotion of the e-Portal; Carry out communication campaigns and produce publications in order to strengthen MPs’ understanding of their financial and social rights; Promote the single power of attorney for access by APAs and TIPAs to the sensitive information of their deputies.
09B40.refined_description: Unit 09B40 operates as a specialized advisory and administrative support function within Directorate 09B (General Directorate of Finance), serving as the primary institutional interface between European Parliament Members and the regulatory frameworks governing their financial and social entitlements. Positioned as a leaf node within the 09B structure and coordinating horizontally with sibling units 09B10, 09B20, and 09B30, Unit 09B40 executes a dual-capacity service delivery model that combines reactive constituent support with proactive institutional engagement. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses four distinct but complementary operational domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation: direct inquiry response and administrative processing (35%), personalized advisory consultation and envelope management optimization (35%), collective information dissemination and educational support (15%), and strategic information architecture development including digital portal advancement and systematic authorization mechanisms (15%). Through its integrated approach, Unit 09B40 ensures that all Members possess accurate, accessible understanding of their statutory entitlements while simultaneously providing operational infrastructure—including transaction tracking systems, acknowledgment protocols, formal notifications, and single power of attorney mechanisms—that enable authorized parliamentary assistants (APAs) and third-party information processors (TIPAs) appropriate access to sensitive member data. This comprehensive service architecture facilitates Members' autonomous financial and administrative decision-making throughout their parliamentary mandates while strengthening institutional comprehension of parliamentary assistance envelope provisions and associated regulatory frameworks.
09B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct Inquiry Response & Administrative Processing** (35%): Provision of immediate answers to Members and their assistants regarding statutory financial and social rights entitlements; receipt, preliminary validation, and forwarding of application forms to operational processing units; systematic transaction recording within comprehensive tracking infrastructure; issuance of acknowledgment receipts and formal refusal notifications.

• **Personalized Advisory Consultation & Envelope Management** (35%): Organization and delivery of individualized advisory sessions addressing financial and social rights and parliamentary assistance envelope administration; systematic identification and analysis of Member-specific needs through continuous engagement; proactive provision of tailored solutions and strategic recommendations for optimizing envelope management throughout Members' mandate periods.

• **Collective Information Dissemination & Educational Support** (15%): Organization of group information sessions addressing Members' statutory rights with particular emphasis on parliamentary assistance envelope provisions; development and production of comprehensive pedagogical support materials and visual documentation; enhancement of institutional comprehension through structured learning resources and regulatory framework clarification.

• **Strategic Information Architecture & Digital Enablement** (15%): Development of systematic frameworks for organized tool and information provision to Members; contribution to e-Portal development, enhancement, and promotion initiatives; execution of targeted communication campaigns and institutional publication programs; promotion of single power of attorney mechanisms enabling authorized parliamentary assistants (APAs) and third-party information processors (TIPAs) appropriate access to sensitive Member data while strengthening overall understanding of financial and social rights.

09C.name: 09C
original_activities: Coordinate all horizontal subjects with all units: objectives, Annual Work Programme, Annual Activity Report and intermediary reports, Discharge, Budget management, risk management, business continuity management, etc. Follow-up with the units on observations and findings from ECA and OLAF. Follow AFCO, CONT and BUDG matters in the area of the Directorate.

Provide support in the implementation and monitoring of the financing of the European political parties and foundations. Follow and provide support on the revision of the Regulation (EU, Euratom) No 1141/2014 and the revision of the Bureau Decision of 1 July 2019. Coordinate the provision of guidance and trainings to the European political parties and foundations.

Provide support in the implementation and monitoring of the financing of the political groups and non-attached Members of the European Parliament, as well as of the European Parliamentary Association and the Former Members Association. Liaise with the political groups' WG Finance, coordinating the provision of guidance and trainings to groups.

Coordinate with the Parliament's Travel Management Unit (TMU) the implementation of the contract with the external travel agency and lodge card provider and acting together with TMU as main interlocutors. Provide guidance and support to the TMU, including on simplification projects.
Provide support to the Human Resources and Organisation Unit and contribute to a better human resources management for the Directorate-General for Finance.

Ensure security and proper functioning of the IT infrastructure of the Directorate-General for Finance, which supplies assistance to end-users of different applications/IT tools as well as contributing to ongoing projects: e-Portal and dedicated IT projects for the Directorate-General.
Represent the Directorate-General in working groups on the level of the horizontal management of resources.
09C.refined_description: Unit 09C functions as the strategic coordination and governance directorate within the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), serving as the primary nexus for horizontal management, compliance oversight, and operational support across the entire financial administration of the European Parliament. The unit exercises comprehensive coordination authority over all cross-cutting institutional matters across DG Finance, encompassing strategic objectives definition, Annual Work Programme and Activity Report development, intermediary reporting cycles, Discharge procedures, budget management frameworks, risk management protocols, and business continuity management systems, while maintaining systematic follow-up on observations and findings from the European Court of Auditors (ECA) and the Office for the Prevention of Fraud (OLAF), and actively monitoring AFCO, CONT, and BUDG parliamentary committee matters affecting the Directorate. Unit 09C provides specialized implementation and monitoring support for European political parties and foundations financing mechanisms, including substantive engagement with the revision of Regulation (EU, Euratom) No 1141/2014 and the Bureau Decision of 1 July 2019, while coordinating comprehensive guidance and training programs to these external institutional stakeholders, and simultaneously administers financing mechanisms for European Parliament political groups, non-attached Members, the European Parliamentary Association, and the Former Members Association, serving as primary liaison with political groups' Finance Working Groups. The unit manages critical operational partnerships with the Parliament's Travel Management Unit (TMU) through coordinated implementation of external travel agency contracts and lodging card provider arrangements, functioning as joint principal interlocutor for TMU guidance, support provision, and participation in simplification initiatives. Unit 09C contributes substantively to enhanced human resources management practices across the Directorate-General through direct support to the Human Resources and Organisation Unit, while ensuring the security, integrity, and optimal functioning of DG Finance IT infrastructure through end-user technical assistance across multiple applications and IT tools, and contributing to critical infrastructure projects including the e-Portal and dedicated IT initiatives. As a Direction-level coordinating entity, Unit 09C supervises multiple specialized subordinate units (09C10, 09C20, 09C2010, 09C2030, 09C30, 09C3010, and three additional units) while maintaining peer coordination with sibling directions 09-10, 09-20, 09-30, 09A, and 09B under parent unit 09's strategic oversight, and maintains institutional representation within horizontal resource management working groups at the parliamentary level.
09C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Governance, Compliance & Strategic Coordination** (20%): Coordinate all horizontal institutional matters across DG Finance including strategic objectives definition, Annual Work Programmes, Annual Activity Reports, intermediary reporting mechanisms, Discharge procedures, budget management frameworks, risk management protocols, and business continuity planning; maintain systematic follow-up on European Court of Auditors (ECA) and Office for the Prevention of Fraud (OLAF) observations and findings; actively monitor AFCO, CONT, and BUDG parliamentary committee matters affecting the Directorate.

• **Political Finance & Party Support Mechanisms** (36%): Implement and monitor financing frameworks for European political parties and foundations, including substantive engagement with the revision of Regulation (EU, Euratom) No 1141/2014 and the Bureau Decision of 1 July 2019, with comprehensive guidance and training coordination to external institutional stakeholders (18%); manage financing implementation and monitoring for European Parliament political groups, non-attached Members, the European Parliamentary Association, and the Former Members Association, serving as primary liaison with political groups' Finance Working Groups and coordinating comprehensive guidance and training delivery mechanisms (18%).

• **Travel Management & Logistics Coordination** (15%): Coordinate with Parliament's Travel Management Unit (TMU) on the implementation of external travel agency contracts and lodging card provider arrangements; serve as joint principal interlocutor for TMU guidance and support provision; participate in and provide strategic guidance on simplification initiatives.

• **Human Resources & IT Infrastructure Management** (27%): Contribute substantively to enhanced human resources management practices across the Directorate-General for Finance through direct support to the Human Resources and Organisation Unit (15%); ensure security, integrity, and optimal functioning of DG Finance IT infrastructure through end-user technical assistance across multiple applications and IT tools, and contribute to critical infrastructure projects including the e-Portal and dedicated DG IT initiatives (12%).

• **Horizontal Institutional Representation** (2%): Represent the Directorate-General in working groups addressing horizontal resource management at the parliamentary institutional level.

09C10.name: 09C10
original_activities: Manage the entries and exits of non-registered Members regarding the 400 budget. Control the use of 400 appropriations by non-registered Members, carry out financial transactions and manage allocation accounting. Ensure the operation of the digital application for reimbursement requests.
Monitor the specific cases and disputes of political groups and non-registered members.

Ensure relations with the secretariats of political groups, non-attached deputies and their assistants, the Court of Auditors, internal audit, the Ex Ante Service, the Central Financial Unit, Accounting. Provide financial training and specific guidance.
Manage budget items 1007, 3020-06, 4400, 4420 (President's allowances, President's Office, associations). Ensure relations with the Office of the President and parliamentary associations.
Ensure reporting to the hierarchy (annual reports, budget reporting, data protection, risk management, business continuity plan).
Respond to requests for information (transparency, media, other institutions). Publish group accounts and other documents on the institution's internet.

Distribute the credits from budget item 400 between the political groups and non-registered deputies; monitor the annual reports of political groups; prepare the note to the Office regarding the closure of accounts. Manage budget item 400.
Monitor changes to the regulatory framework linked to the Regulations Governing the Use of Credits 400 and other rules concerning budget lines managed by the unit.
09C10.refined_description: Unit 09C10 operates as a specialized financial management and administrative unit within Directorate 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance, bearing primary responsibility for the comprehensive financial administration of non-registered Members and political groups within the European Parliament. The unit's core mission encompasses the management of budget item 400 allocations and related appropriations (44% of workload), including the critical functions of processing member entries and exits, controlling appropriations utilization, executing financial transactions, maintaining allocation accounting systems, and operating the digital reimbursement request application platform. Unit 09C10 executes the strategic distribution function of budget item 400 credits among political groups and non-registered deputies, monitors annual compliance reports from political groups, and prepares closure documentation for submission to the Office (7% of workload). The unit maintains continuous oversight of specific disputes and exceptional cases affecting political groups and non-registered members (15% of workload), while simultaneously serving as the institutional liaison point with political group secretariats, non-attached deputies and their support staff, the Court of Auditors, internal audit mechanisms, the Ex Ante Service, the Central Financial Unit, and Accounting divisions—providing comprehensive financial training and specialized guidance to these stakeholders (11% of workload). Unit 09C10 additionally manages distinct budget items including 1007, 3020-06, 4400, and 4420, which relate to Presidential allowances, the President's Office operations, and parliamentary associations, maintaining essential coordination with the Office of the President (7% of workload). The unit fulfills administrative governance responsibilities through the preparation of mandatory hierarchical reporting including annual reports, budget documentation, data protection compliance, risk management assessments, and business continuity planning (7% of workload), and meets transparency obligations by processing information requests from external parties, media outlets, and other institutions while publishing consolidated group accounts and institutional documentation on the European Parliament's official internet platforms (6% of workload). Finally, Unit 09C10 maintains regulatory vigilance by monitoring substantive changes to the Regulations Governing the Use of Credits 400 and other normative frameworks affecting the budget lines under its purview (3% of workload). As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 09C10 operates without subordinate units while functioning as a specialized financial administration component alongside its sibling units within Directorate 09C.
09C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Budget Management and Financial Operations** (44%): Comprehensive administration of budget line 400 entries and exits for non-registered Members; control and monitoring of appropriations utilization; execution of financial transactions and allocation accounting maintenance; operation of the digital reimbursement request application platform; management of budget items 1007, 3020-06, 4400, and 4420 relating to Presidential allowances, President's Office operations, and parliamentary associations.

• **Budget Distribution and Compliance Monitoring** (7%): Strategic allocation of budget item 400 credits among political groups and non-registered deputies; monitoring of annual financial compliance reports from political groups; preparation of account closure documentation for submission to the Office.

• **Case Management and Dispute Resolution** (15%): Monitoring of specific cases and exceptional circumstances affecting political groups and non-registered members; identification and management of disputes requiring specialized institutional intervention.

• **Stakeholder Relations and Financial Guidance** (11%): Establishment and maintenance of relationships with political group secretariats, non-attached deputy offices and their support staff, Court of Auditors, internal audit services, Ex Ante Service, Central Financial Unit, Accounting divisions, and the Office of the President; provision of comprehensive financial training and specialized guidance to institutional partners; coordination with parliamentary associations.

• **Governance, Reporting, and Transparency Compliance** (13%): Preparation of mandatory hierarchical reporting including annual reports, budget documentation, data protection compliance, risk management assessments, and business continuity planning (7%); processing of external information requests from transparency, media, and inter-institutional sources; publication of consolidated group accounts and institutional documents on official parliamentary internet platforms (6%).

• **Regulatory Framework Monitoring** (3%): Continuous surveillance of changes to the Regulations Governing the Use of Credits 400 and other normative frameworks governing budget lines managed by the unit; assessment of regulatory impacts on unit operations and institutional compliance obligations.

09C20.name: 09C20
original_activities: Coordinate, participate in functional analysis, follow-up implementation of IT projects including technical studies and assessments.
Participate in and represent the Directorate-General in inter-DG working groups and steering committees.
Coordinate services that are part of the unit, manage the staff, provide input to multiple dossiers to Director, Director-General and Secretary-General.
Coordinate decentralised projects (analysis, development, maintenance).
Establish the IT-Programme and managing implementation of the plan (Business Analyst), identify the needs, coordinate, analyse existing and future applications.
Other missions.
09C20.refined_description: Unit 09C20 functions as a critical information technology governance, project management, and coordination hub within Direction 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), serving as the primary operational entity responsible for IT project lifecycle management, strategic planning, and inter-institutional alignment across the European Parliament's administrative structure. The unit operates at the intersection of strategic IT governance and operational execution, managing comprehensive technical infrastructure initiatives while maintaining direct executive engagement with senior institutional leadership (Director, Director-General, and Secretary-General). Unit 09C20's operational mandate encompasses coordinating and overseeing IT project implementation through functional analysis and technical assessment activities; representing DG 09's interests and expertise within cross-functional governance structures; managing both centralized and decentralized IT service delivery; establishing institutional IT Programme frameworks; and conducting application portfolio analysis to support organizational digital transformation. The unit's work encompasses the complete spectrum of IT governance responsibilities—from identifying organizational needs and analyzing existing and future application systems to coordinating distributed IT infrastructure maintenance and supporting institutional decision-making through comprehensive technical studies. As a bridge between horizontal inter-DG coordination mechanisms and vertical executive advisory functions, Unit 09C20 ensures organizational coherence across the Parliament's technology landscape while maintaining internal service delivery and staff resource management. The unit remains responsive to ad hoc institutional missions and strategic priorities assigned by senior management, thereby supporting broader organizational objectives beyond its core portfolio.
09C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Project Governance, Coordination & Technical Implementation** (30%): Coordinate and participate in functional analysis; conduct comprehensive technical studies and assessments; oversee follow-up implementation of IT projects; ensure institutional alignment with digital transformation objectives through detailed project viability determinations

• **Inter-Institutional Representation & Cross-Functional Governance** (25%): Participate in and represent the Directorate-General within inter-DG working groups and steering committees; facilitate horizontal coordination and institutional knowledge-sharing; ensure DG 09's strategic interests and technical expertise inform cross-functional governance structures

• **Internal Management, Staff Coordination & Executive Liaison** (15%): Coordinate unit services and assigned staff resources; provide substantive multi-level dossier input to Director, Director-General, and Secretary-General; maintain executive-level engagement on key institutional matters; ensure internal organizational coherence

• **Decentralized Project Management & Distributed IT Service Delivery** (15%): Manage analysis, development, and maintenance of decentralized projects across the organization; oversee distributed IT infrastructure and application support; support organizational infrastructure resilience through coordinated service delivery

• **Strategic IT Programme Planning & Application Portfolio Management** (10%): Establish IT Programme framework and manage strategic implementation planning through business analyst functions; identify organizational needs and coordinate initiatives; analyze existing and future application systems to support organizational digital evolution

• **Additional Institutional Missions & Ad Hoc Assignments** (5%): Execute other missions and ad-hoc organizational requirements as assigned by senior management to support evolving institutional priorities

09C2010.name: 09C2010
original_activities: Deliver comprehensive IT support and assistance to all Directorate-General for Finance users in close collaboration with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity.

Provide effective first-level support for the PAM applications by serving as a liaison between the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity development teams and the Directorate-General for Finance users, facilitate smoother communication and operational flow between technical services and end-users.

Oversee the effective operation of the Directorate-General's IT infrastructure coordinated through the DG FINS LSA Team, while ensuring efficient user support for external e-Portal users such as Members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, and TIPAs, particularly concerning AD account management.

Oversee offices and workspace management, logistics services, and the provision of furniture within the scope of GBI and LPM functions, while coordinating with the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics on infrastructure-related matters.
Manage the maintenance and continuous improvement of both existing and upcoming decentralized applications.
Other missions.
09C2010.refined_description: Unit 09C2010 functions as the integrated Information Technology and Administrative Services hub within the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), operating under coordination directorate 09C and serving as a critical operational backbone that bridges technical service providers with end-user communities across the organization. The unit's primary mission encompasses delivering comprehensive IT support services through two interconnected channels: direct technical assistance to all DG Finance users (30% of workload) in close operational collaboration with the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, and first-level support for PAM applications (25% of workload) by establishing and maintaining effective liaison channels between DG ITCS development teams and DG Finance users to facilitate seamless communication and operational efficiency. Concurrently, Unit 09C2010 maintains responsibility for overseeing the Directorate-General's IT infrastructure operations (20% of workload) through coordination with the DG FINS LSA Team, with particular emphasis on managing external e-Portal user access for Members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, and TIPAs, including comprehensive Active Directory account administration and user provisioning. Beyond technical operations, the unit manages offices and workspace infrastructure (10% of workload), coordinating logistics services and furniture provision within GBI and LPM functional scopes while liaising with the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics on related infrastructure matters. Additionally, Unit 09C2010 oversees the maintenance and continuous improvement of decentralized applications (10% of workload), ensuring sustained technical functionality and user-centered enhancements, with a final 5% allocation dedicated to emerging organizational missions and additional assignments as directed by the parent directorate. Through these interconnected activities, Unit 09C2010 ensures both technological effectiveness and administrative efficiency while maintaining critical inter-directorate relationships essential to DG Finance's institutional service delivery.
09C2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT User Support Services** (55%): Delivery of comprehensive IT assistance to all DG Finance personnel (30%) and provision of first-level PAM application support with liaison functionality between DG ITCS development teams and end-users (25%), collectively representing the unit's primary service delivery interface and technical support focus

• **IT Infrastructure and Access Management** (20%): Oversight of the Directorate-General's IT infrastructure operations through DG FINS LSA Team coordination, management of external e-Portal user access for Members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, and TIPAs, and comprehensive Active Directory account administration and user provisioning

• **Facilities and Workspace Management** (10%): Administration of office and workspace management, logistics services coordination, and furniture provision within GBI and LPM functional frameworks, with cross-directorate coordination through the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics

• **Decentralized Application Maintenance and Development** (10%): Stewardship of existing and emerging decentralized applications, ensuring continuous operational functionality, technical resilience, and systematic improvement initiatives aligned with user requirements

• **Emerging Organizational Missions** (5%): Allocation reserved for additional assignments, evolving organizational requirements, and priorities as directed by parent directorate 09C

09C2030.name: 09C2030
original_activities: Analyse business needs and coordinate with the Business Units and technical teams for development. Write analysis, user stories and testing scenarios for the development of e-forms and other functionalities of the application.

Perform first and second level support for the e-Portal application to Members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, pay agents and staff on technical issues - including administering access rights. Monitor of the application use; introduce requests for corrective and evaluative maintenance, bug tracking.
Test following the development, report and bug-fix, organise testing with the operational units and Test Cell (DG ITEC).
Ensure presentations and training for e-Portal users (Members, Assistants, Paying Agents and staff) on the functioning and use of the application - including presentation of new features.
Plan and prioritise of the e-Portal development workload in accordance with the e-Portal Programme Board and PAM steering committee's decisions and in co-operation with Business Units.
Support documentation, translation of screens and documents, statistics and reporting, updating European Parliament intranet pages.
Other missions.
09C2030.refined_description: Unit 09C2030 operates as the integrated e-Portal application management and development coordination unit within Directorate 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance, functioning as the essential operational and developmental hub bridging the Parliament's financial and administrative operational requirements with their technological implementation. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the complete lifecycle management of the European Parliament's e-Portal application, spanning from initial business requirements analysis through end-user support, quality assurance, and continuous system optimization. As a critical liaison between business stakeholders (Business Units, Members, parliamentary assistants, pay agents, and institutional staff) and technical development teams, Unit 09C2030 coordinates development prioritization in alignment with the e-Portal Programme Board and PAM steering committee directives while maintaining operational excellence across multiple interconnected domains. The unit's workload distribution reflects its multifaceted responsibilities: dominant emphasis on requirements analysis and development coordination; substantial commitment to multi-tiered technical support and application administration; significant focus on quality assurance and testing operations; and supporting contributions to user enablement, governance, documentation, and institutional maintenance activities. Through these integrated activities, Unit 09C2030 ensures the e-Portal ecosystem remains responsive to parliamentary financial administration needs while maintaining system reliability, user accessibility, and continuous improvement.
09C2030.refined_activity_summary:
• **Requirements Analysis and Development Coordination** (35%): Comprehensive analysis of business needs and requirements in collaboration with Business Units and technical development teams; production of detailed analysis documentation, user stories, and comprehensive testing scenarios for e-forms and application functionality enhancements; development of specifications that inform technical implementation

• **Technical Support and Application Administration** (25%): Multi-tiered technical support delivery (first and second level) to Members, Accredited parliamentary assistants, pay agents, and institutional staff; access rights administration and management; application utilization pattern monitoring; initiation of corrective and evaluative maintenance requests; bug tracking and defect issue management

• **Quality Assurance and Testing Operations** (20%): Post-development systematic testing execution; coordination with operational units and the Test Cell (DG ITEC); defect reporting, bug-fix validation, and resolution coordination; organizational testing orchestration

• **User Enablement, Development Governance, and Stakeholder Communication** (10%): User training delivery and presentations on application functionality and newly implemented features to Members, Assistants, Paying Agents, and staff; development workload planning and prioritization in accordance with e-Portal Programme Board and PAM steering committee directives; inter-unit coordination with Business Units

• **Documentation, Translation, and Operational Support Services** (10%): Supporting documentation production and maintenance; translation of application screens and related documents; statistical analysis and reporting generation; European Parliament intranet content updates and maintenance; execution of additional assigned institutional missions

09C30.name: 09C30
original_activities: Generally supervise the implementation and correct application of contracts with travel agencies contracted by the European Parliament (individual and group reservations), with the issuer of the credit card (housed card), as well as public procurement procedures in the event of renewal of contracts.
Coordinate the two services that are part of the unit, manage staff, provide contributions on several files to the Director, the Director General and the Secretary General.
Manage budget line 03049 relating to the payment of monthly management fees.
Participate and represent the General Management in inter-DG working groups and steering committees.
Supervise negotiations with airlines and hotel groups and sign the respective agreements, as well as supervise the organization of chartered transport for plenary sessions in Strasbourg.
Monitor developments in the travel market and evaluate the possibilities of introducing new IT tools.
09C30.refined_description: Unit 09C30 operates as a specialized travel and mobility management unit within Direction 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), positioned as a second-level hierarchical entity serving as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for travel services oversight, logistics coordination, and vendor relationship management. The unit functions as a leaf node within the organizational structure, maintaining coordination with sibling units (09C10, 09C20, 09C2010, 09C2030, 09C3010, and three additional units) under the parent Direction 09C framework while operating without subordinate units of its own. Unit 09C30's comprehensive mission encompasses direct financial stewardship through budget line 03049 administration, strategic vendor negotiations and relationship optimization, internal service coordination and staff management, substantive policy contributions to senior leadership, and institutional representation across multiple governance forums. The unit's activities collectively position it as a critical support function within the DG Finance structure, directly contributing to institutional operational efficiency, procurement governance optimization, vendor relationship management, and strategic logistics coordination. Through its dual focus on contractual oversight and operational excellence, Unit 09C30 manages the institutional travel ecosystem while maintaining market intelligence capabilities and evaluating emerging information technology solutions for potential implementation.
09C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Travel Services Procurement and Contract Management** (50%): General supervision of the implementation and correct application of contracts with travel agencies providing individual and group reservation services, management of the institutional credit card issuer relationship (housed card provider), and oversight of public procurement procedures governing contract renewals

• **Internal Unit Coordination and Executive Support** (25%): Coordination of two constituent services within the unit, management of assigned staff resources, and provision of substantive policy contributions on multiple administrative files to the Director, Director General, and Secretary General

• **Financial Administration and Budget Stewardship** (10%): Dedicated management of budget line 03049 and oversight of monthly management fee payments associated with travel services

• **Strategic Vendor Negotiations and Logistics Operations** (5%): Supervision of strategic negotiations with airlines and hotel groups, execution of respective service agreements, and oversight of chartered transport organization for Strasbourg plenary sessions

• **Institutional Representation and Market Intelligence** (10%): Participation in and representation of General Management in inter-Directorate General working groups and steering committees, monitoring of travel market sector developments, and evaluation of feasibility assessments for implementing new information technology tools to enhance operational efficiency

09C3010.name: 09C3010
original_activities: Supervise and coordinate reservation files for group travel in Europe and around the world: official missions including COP, ACP, Euromed, Euronest, Eurolat and other events organized by services of the European Parliament (DG PERS/Bazoches, EPLO, DG COMM, etc.).

Carry out ex-post checks on flight/hotel reservations processed by the travel agency. Support and supervise the travel agency in the development and implementation of new tools and procedures for the group service.

Ensure coordination and cooperation with the Parliament's organizational services as well as those responsible for reimbursements, in particular with the Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit and the Missions Unit.
Establish financial estimates for travel, at the request of the political authorities of the European Parliament.
Negotiate the best rates and conditions for Brussels, Luxembourg and Strasbourg with individual hotel establishments and international hotel groups and monitor agreements.
Contribute to the implementation of contracts with travel agencies and with the issuer of the credit card (housed card) and to public procurement procedures in the event of contract renewal.
Arrange specific means of transport, for example charter planes and buses.
09C3010.refined_description:
Unit 09C3010 functions as a specialized travel coordination and logistics management unit operating at the second hierarchical level within Direction 09C of Directorate-General Finance (DG 09), positioned as a leaf node without subordinate units while maintaining peer relationships with eight sibling units across the direction.
