The unit's core mission encompasses comprehensive management of group travel reservations, logistical operations, and associated administrative procedures for the European Parliament's institutional activities across European and global destinations, serving as a critical operational support function enabling the Parliament's international engagement and institutional mobility. The unit's operations integrate five principal functional domains: supervising and coordinating comprehensive reservation files for group travel missions (35% of workload) including official delegations to COP conferences, ACP meetings, Euromed initiatives, Euronest parliamentary assemblies, Eurolat forums, and specialized events organized by DG PERS/Bazoches, EPLO, DG COMM, and equivalent Parliament services; conducting systematic ex-post quality assurance and compliance verification (20% of workload) on flight and hotel reservations processed by contracted travel agencies while simultaneously providing technical support and supervision to travel agency partners in developing and implementing enhanced tools and procedural frameworks; establishing detailed financial estimates and cost projections (10% of workload) for travel initiatives at the direction of Parliament's political authorities; negotiating and maintaining optimal accommodation rates and contractual conditions (10% of workload) with individual hotel establishments and international hotel groups across the three parliamentary seats of Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg while monitoring compliance with negotiated agreements; and arranging specialized transportation solutions (5% of workload) including charter aircraft and coach services to accommodate Parliament's unique operational requirements. The unit maintains critical horizontal coordination (10% of workload) with Parliament's organizational services, reimbursement authorities, the Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit, and the Missions Unit to ensure seamless integration of travel logistics with financial and administrative frameworks, while simultaneously contributing to contract implementation and public procurement procedures (10% of workload) for travel agency agreements and corporate credit card issuance arrangements, thereby optimizing financial resource allocation across institutional mobility operations.

##
09C3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Group Travel Reservation Management and Coordination** (35%): Supervise and coordinate comprehensive reservation files for group travel throughout Europe and internationally; manage official missions including COP, ACP, Euromed, Euronest, and Eurolat delegations; coordinate with multiple Parliament services (DG PERS/Bazoches, EPLO, DG COMM) for event-based travel requirements across all institutional activities.

• **Travel Agency Oversight and Quality Assurance** (20%): Execute ex-post verification checks and systematic audits on flight and hotel reservations processed by contracted travel agencies; provide technical support and supervision to travel agency partners in developing and implementing enhanced tools, systems, and operational procedures for group service delivery.

• **Financial Estimation and Planning** (10%): Establish detailed financial estimates and cost projections for travel initiatives and operations in response to requests from Parliament's political leadership; support resource allocation optimization across institutional mobility requirements.

• **Accommodation Rate Negotiation and Contract Management** (10%): Negotiate optimal rates and contractual conditions with individual hotel establishments and international hotel groups across Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg locations; monitor compliance with negotiated agreements and maintain active contracts.

• **Interdepartmental Coordination and Procurement Management** (10%): Maintain critical operational coordination with Parliament's organizational services, reimbursement authorities, Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit, and Missions Unit; contribute to contract implementation and public procurement procedures for travel agency agreements and corporate credit card issuance programs.

• **Specialized Transportation Arrangements** (5%): Arrange specialized means of transport including charter aircraft and coach services to accommodate Parliament's unique operational requirements for large delegations and specific institutional mobility needs.

**TOTAL WORKLOAD ALLOCATION: 100%**

09C3020.name: 09C3020
original_activities: Implement contracts with the travel agency and the credit card issuer (housed card). Coordinate the implementation of new services between the travel agency, the lodged card issuer and the various cost centers. If there is a need to renew contracts, launch the appropriate tender procedures.

Ensure the correct application of contracts between the travel agency, the issuer of the lodged card and the European Parliament (establishment of operational and technical procedures with the various authorizing services, mainly with the Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit and the Missions Unit).
Organize chartered transport during plenary sessions in Strasbourg.
Carry out ex post checks on flight/train reservations (mainly in Europe) processed by the travel agency in order to assess whether the best rates have been selected.
Ensure effective handling of complaints regarding the travel agency and proper application of data protection rules. Track statistics on transactions, number of customers and data quality.
Negotiate and monitor agreements with air and rail carriers.
Manage the arrival and departure of Members (delivery of free rail subscriptions (Libre Parcours cards), creation of new profiles).
09C3020.refined_description: Unit 09C3020 functions as the Travel Services Management and Vendor Relations Unit within Direction 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), serving as a specialized operational hub responsible for comprehensive administration of institutional travel arrangements, member mobility services, and vendor relationship management for the European Parliament. Operating as a leaf node within the coordination and support directorate, the unit manages the complete lifecycle of travel agency and payment card services, from contract procurement and implementation through operational coordination, financial oversight, and quality assurance across multiple cost centers. The unit maintains horizontal coordination with key internal stakeholders—specifically the Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit and the Missions Unit—to ensure seamless service delivery, regulatory compliance, and financial optimization across all travel and transportation services. Core responsibilities encompass contract negotiation and renewal with travel agencies and credit card issuers; establishment of operational and technical procedures with authorizing services; organization of chartered transport logistics during Strasbourg plenary sessions; execution of rigorous ex post financial audits on flight and rail reservations to verify optimal rate selection; negotiation and performance monitoring of carrier relationship agreements; administration of complaint resolution and data protection compliance while tracking transaction analytics and customer metrics; and facilitation of member mobility through arrival/departure procedures, Libre Parcours rail subscription card distribution, and member profile administration. These interconnected activities collectively support the European Parliament's institutional objectives of cost-effective operations, service excellence, regulatory adherence, and fiscal responsibility across the institution's travel procurement and reimbursement framework.
09C3020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Contract Management and Procurement** (30%): Implementation and renewal of contracts with travel agencies and credit card issuers; coordination of new service implementations across multiple cost centers; management of tender procedures for contract renewals

• **Operational Compliance and Procedure Establishment** (20%): Establishment of operational and technical procedures with authorizing services (Presence and Travel Expenses of Members Unit and Missions Unit); contractual compliance monitoring between travel agencies, card issuers, and the European Parliament; liaison and coordination with internal stakeholders to ensure correct contractual application

• **Transportation Logistics and Carrier Relations** (25%): Organization of chartered transport logistics during Strasbourg plenary sessions (15%); negotiation and monitoring of agreements with air and rail carriers to assess performance and maintain service quality standards (10%)

• **Financial Oversight and Quality Assurance** (10%): Execution of ex post checks on flight and train reservations to verify optimal rate selection; assessment of travel agency pricing performance across European routes

• **Member Services and Data Management** (15%): Management of member arrival and departure procedures; distribution of Libre Parcours rail subscription cards and creation/maintenance of member travel profiles (5%); complaint handling and resolution regarding travel agency services; data protection compliance administration; tracking of transaction statistics, customer volumes, and performance metrics (10%)

09C40.name: 09C40
original_activities: Attract, select and integrate future employees of general management according to the various selection procedures.
Identify, promote and consolidate skills and know-how to facilitate innovation, agility and resilience. Manage decentralized training actions.
Develop a human resources culture focused on efficiency, cooperation, tolerance and respect. Fight against silos, ensure staff cohesion and develop a FINS culture.
Evaluate performance, anticipate and support career development to capitalize on acquired experience and retain talent.
Promote the contribution of interns through a training experience open to the needs of general management.
Anticipate and support changes in the organization of work and the availability of resources (teleworking, absences, overtime).
Act as a partner of managers to develop a human resources policy adapted to the strategy of general management.
Manage the budget in the area of ​​human resources, reporting, internal communication, etc.
09C40.refined_description:
**

Unit 09C40 functions as the Human Resources Management and Organizational Development unit within Direction 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance (DG 09), operating as a critical enabler of institutional capacity, organizational effectiveness, and personnel administration across the general management structure. The unit's integrated mission encompasses eight interconnected operational domains that collectively optimize human capital management while supporting the broader governance and operational objectives of Direction 09C and its sibling units (09C10, 09C20, 09C2010, 09C2030, 09C30, and related units). Workforce acquisition and integration represent the dominant strategic priority, encompassing the systematic attraction, selection, and integration of future employees through adherence to established institutional selection procedures and recruitment protocols, thereby establishing the foundational talent pipeline for institutional staffing. The unit simultaneously pursues organizational development and learning through skills identification, promotion, and consolidation initiatives; decentralized training action management; and the advancement of a human resources culture anchored in efficiency, cooperation, tolerance, and mutual respect—actively combating organizational silos while fostering staff cohesion and advancing the Finance Institutional Services (FINS) culture across the directorate. Complementary operational functions include performance management and talent retention activities that leverage accumulated employee experience through systematic performance evaluation, career trajectory anticipation, and strategic career development support; structured internship program coordination responsive to general management requirements; and anticipatory support for organizational work arrangements, resource availability management, teleworking policies, absence management, and overtime coordination. The unit further functions as a strategic partner to management structures in developing human resources policies aligned with institutional strategy while ensuring appropriate financial stewardship, budget management, reporting compliance, and internal communication coordination across all human resources operations.

---

**
09C40.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Workforce Acquisition and Integration** (35%): Systematic attraction, selection, and integration of future employees across general management through established institutional selection procedures and recruitment protocols; establishment of foundational talent pipeline and assurance of quality staffing outcomes

• **Organizational Development, Skills Consolidation, and Learning** (15%): Identification, promotion, and consolidation of organizational skills and institutional know-how; decentralized training action management; facilitation of institutional innovation, agility, and organizational resilience through targeted capability development

• **Organizational Culture Development and Workforce Cohesion** (10%): Cultivation of human resources culture centered on efficiency, cooperation, tolerance, and mutual respect; active elimination of organizational silos; advancement of staff cohesion and FINS (Finance Institutional Services) culture across the directorate

• **Performance Management and Talent Retention** (10%): Systematic performance evaluation, career trajectory anticipation, and strategic career development support mechanisms; capitalization on accumulated employee experience; implementation of institutional talent retention strategies

• **Internship Program Coordination and Training Experience Management** (10%): Promotion and coordination of structured internship training experiences; alignment with general management operational requirements; facilitation of intern contribution to institutional objectives through responsive training design

• **Organizational Work Arrangements and Resource Availability Management** (10%): Anticipatory support and coordination of organizational work changes and resource availability; teleworking policy implementation and coordination; absence management administration; overtime coordination and workforce scheduling optimization

• **Strategic Human Resources Partnership and Policy Development** (5%): Advisory partnership with management structures; development of human resources policies aligned with general management institutional strategy; alignment of human resources initiatives with broader organizational objectives

• **Financial and Administrative Stewardship** (5%): Human resources budget management and financial oversight; reporting compliance and administrative coordination; internal communication infrastructure development and maintenance across human resources operations

09C50.name: 09C50
original_activities: Ensure procedures related to the financing of political parties and foundations (call for contributions/proposals, preparation of the granting of financing, control of the use of financing, recovery of amounts unduly paid). Prepare notes to the Office (allocation of funding, closing of accounts), prepare correspondence with political parties and foundations. Manage budget items 402, 403 and 501.

Monitor the evolution of the legislative framework applicable to European political parties and foundations and make proposals if necessary. Propose the revision of the Office Decision of July 1, 2019 setting out the terms of application of revised Regulation No. 1141/2014. Maintain relationships with the Legal Department, the Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit and other parties concerned.

Provide support and advice to political parties and foundations and organize bi-annual meetings including training. Ensure relations with the Authority for European Parties and Foundations, the external auditor, the Internal Audit Service, the Court of Auditors, the Budget and Verification and Public Procurement Unit, Accounting.
Monitor and analyze the specific cases and disputes of political parties and foundations. Ensure communication with the Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit and the Legal Department.
09C50.refined_description: Unit 09C50 functions as the specialized financing and regulatory compliance authority for European political parties and foundations within Direction 09C of the Directorate-General for Finance, serving as the primary administrative and governance hub for all matters related to European political party and foundation financing operations. The unit exercises comprehensive management of the complete financing lifecycle (60% of workload), encompassing the issuance of calls for contributions and proposals, preparation and execution of financing grants, systematic financial control and monitoring of fund utilization, recovery mechanisms for amounts unduly disbursed, preparation of administrative and financial notes to the Office regarding funding allocation and account closure, maintenance of correspondence with regulated entities, and direct stewardship of budget items 402, 403, and 501. Concurrently, Unit 09C50 maintains vigilant monitoring and analysis of the evolving legislative and regulatory framework applicable to European political parties and foundations (20% of workload), proposing necessary regulatory amendments and revisions to the Office Decision of July 1, 2019 implementing Regulation No. 1141/2014, while cultivating collaborative relationships with the Legal Department and Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit to ensure regulatory coherence and consistent interpretation. The unit provides substantive advisory and support services to political parties and foundations (15% of workload), organizing and facilitating bi-annual training and engagement meetings while serving as the primary operational liaison with the Authority for European Parties and Foundations, external auditors, the Internal Audit Service, the Court of Auditors, the Budget and Verification and Public Procurement Unit, and Accounting functions. Additionally, Unit 09C50 monitors, analyzes, and manages specific disputes and exceptional cases arising within the political party and foundation sector (5% of workload), maintaining coordinated communication with the Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit and Legal Department to ensure consistent dispute resolution protocols and complex compliance matter resolution.
09C50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Political Party and Foundation Financing Administration and Budget Management** (60%): Comprehensive lifecycle management of all financing procedures including issuance of calls for contributions and proposals, preparation and execution of financing grants, systematic financial control and monitoring of fund utilization, recovery of amounts unduly disbursed, preparation of administrative and financial notes to the Office regarding funding allocation and account closure, correspondence management with political parties and foundations, and direct stewardship of budget items 402, 403, and 501

• **Regulatory Framework Monitoring and Development** (20%): Continuous monitoring and analysis of the evolving legislative framework applicable to European political parties and foundations, formulation and proposal of regulatory amendments, stewardship and revision of the Office Decision of July 1, 2019 implementing Regulation No. 1141/2014, and maintenance of inter-departmental collaboration with the Legal Department and Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit to ensure regulatory coherence

• **Stakeholder Support, Training, and Inter-institutional Relations** (15%): Provision of substantive advisory and support services to political parties and foundations, organization and facilitation of bi-annual training and engagement meetings, and management of operational relationships with the Authority for European Parties and Foundations, external auditors, the Internal Audit Service, the Court of Auditors, the Budget and Verification and Public Procurement Unit, and Accounting functions

• **Dispute Resolution and Compliance Monitoring** (5%): Monitoring, analysis, and management of specific cases and disputes arising from political parties and foundations, with coordinated communication and case resolution conducted jointly with the Regulatory Compliance Monitoring Unit and Legal Department regarding compliance matters and dispute resolution protocols

original_activities: Organize the activities of the General Management.
Represent the General Directorate in the bodies of the European Parliament.
Manage General Management staff.

Supervise the 'Strategy and Innovation', 'Europa Experience and Real Estate Prospecting', 'Sustainability and Accessibility Policy' and 'Architectural Identity' units within the framework of their respective strategic missions.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
Represent and/or ensure the representation of the General Management in joint or non-joint committees, interinstitutional bodies, etc.
06.refined_description: Unit 06 functions as the apex General Management Directorate of the European Parliament, operating at the root organizational level with direct reporting responsibility to the Secretary General. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive organizational stewardship, strategic coordination, and institutional representation across the Parliament's administrative infrastructure. Unit 06 provides essential administrative leadership through five integrated operational domains: (1) organizing and coordinating multifaceted General Management activities while establishing operational frameworks and ensuring institutional continuity and strategic alignment; (2) representing the General Directorate in the Parliament's deliberative and decision-making bodies as the primary institutional voice on administrative matters, while ensuring General Management representation in joint committees, interinstitutional bodies, and collaborative governance structures; (3) managing General Management personnel resources through human resources oversight, staff recruitment, performance evaluation, and professional development to ensure operational effectiveness; (4) exercising supervisory governance authority over four specialized subordinate units—'Strategy and Innovation' (Unit 06-10), 'Europa Experience and Real Estate Prospecting' (Unit 06-20), 'Sustainability and Accessibility Policy' (Unit 06-30), and 'Architectural Identity' (Unit 06-40)—while respecting each unit's autonomous strategic missions and ensuring they operate within their respective mandates to contribute effectively to institutional objectives; and (5) performing delegated authorizing officer functions to exercise financial and administrative authorization authority on behalf of the institution, enabling efficient budget execution and commitment authorization within established parameters. Through this integrated portfolio of activities, Unit 06 coordinates nine subordinate organizational entities (06-10, 06-1010, 06-20, 06-30, 06-40, 06A, and four additional units), each with specialized mandates that collectively support the Parliament's institutional objectives and operational effectiveness.
06.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Management and Coordination** (35%): Organizing and directing all General Management activities, establishing operational frameworks, ensuring strategic alignment and institutional continuity, and coordinating multifaceted administrative functions across the directorate

• **Institutional Representation and Governance** (25%): Representing the General Directorate in European Parliament deliberative and decision-making bodies as the primary institutional voice on administrative matters (20%), and ensuring General Management representation in joint committees, non-joint committees, interinstitutional bodies, and collaborative governance structures (5%)

• **Personnel and Administrative Resource Management** (25%): Managing General Management personnel resources through human resources oversight, staff recruitment, performance evaluation, and professional development (15%), and exercising delegated authorizing officer functions for financial and administrative authorization and budget execution (10%)

• **Strategic Supervision of Subordinate Units** (15%): Providing governance oversight and strategic supervision of four specialized subordinate units—'Strategy and Innovation', 'Europa Experience and Real Estate Prospecting', 'Sustainability and Accessibility Policy', and 'Architectural Identity'—while preserving their respective strategic autonomy and ensuring they operate within their mandates to contribute effectively to institutional objectives

original_activities: Streamline and manage innovative processes and strategic planning within the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, including the coordination of preparatory work relating to the Office, its 'Buildings, Transport and Green Parliament' working group, the Quaestors and all cross-directorate-general working groups.

Establish, in the context of the strategic project of the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics 'From standard delivery to individualized service', a specific space dedicated to innovation (an autonomous space where ideas can be proposed, tested and refined on a smaller scale and with fewer obstacles) in order to encourage and foster creativity and innovation in the different areas of activity within DG INLO.

Manage the participation of the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics in the IDEA Lab and the associated working group, and coordinate the contributions of the General Directorate in this context. Serve as an interface between general management and MPs, notably through the One-Stop Shop Service.
06-10.refined_description: Unit 06-10 functions as the Strategic Innovation and Process Management directorate within the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics (DG INLO), operating as a first-level directorate under the General Management parent unit (06) with direct institutional accountability for innovation governance across the European Parliament's infrastructure and logistics operations. The unit's mission encompasses three strategically integrated functional areas that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, positioning it as a catalytic force for organizational modernization and cross-institutional coordination within the Parliament's infrastructure and logistics ecosystem. The dominant activity (75% of operational capacity) involves streamlining and managing innovative processes and strategic planning mechanisms throughout DG INLO, encompassing comprehensive coordination of preparatory work for the Office, the 'Buildings, Transport and Green Parliament' working group, engagement with the Quaestors' governance structure, and facilitation of all cross-directorate-general collaborative working groups. Concurrently, the unit dedicates 15% of its workload to establishing and operationalizing a dedicated autonomous innovation space within DG INLO's strategic transformation initiative titled 'From standard delivery to individualized service,' creating an environment where organizational ideas can be systematically proposed, tested, and refined at reduced scale with diminished procedural obstacles to foster organizational creativity across all DG INLO activity domains. The remaining 10% of workload focuses on managing DG INLO's institutional participation in the IDEA Lab initiative and its associated working group structures, while serving as a critical interface mechanism between general management leadership and Members of Parliament, notably through the One-Stop Shop Service operational framework. These activities collectively support the Parliament's strategic infrastructure modernization objectives while maintaining horizontal coordination across the General Management directorate's sibling units (06-1010, 06-20, 06-30, 06-40, 06A, and additional specialized units).
06-10.refined_activity_summary:
• Strategic Process Streamlining and Governance Coordination (75%): Streamline and manage innovative processes and strategic planning within DG INLO; coordinate comprehensive preparatory work for the Office, 'Buildings, Transport and Green Parliament' working group, and Quaestors' governance structure; facilitate all cross-directorate-general collaborative working groups; maintain horizontal coordination with sibling General Management units

• Innovation Space Development and Organizational Creativity Facilitation (15%): Establish and operate a dedicated autonomous innovation environment within the 'From standard delivery to individualized service' strategic project; enable systematic idea proposal, controlled testing, and iterative refinement mechanisms across all DG INLO activity domains; reduce procedural barriers to foster organizational creativity and innovation

• IDEA Lab Participation and Stakeholder Interface Management (10%): Manage DG INLO's institutional participation in the IDEA Lab initiative and associated working group structures; coordinate General Directorate contributions to external innovation partnerships; serve as critical interface between general management leadership and Members of Parliament; operate the One-Stop Shop Service coordination function

original_activities: Guarantee a high-level assistance and information service to Members of Parliament and their assistants by providing them with answers within the set deadlines in accordance with the decisions of the Bureau.
Be the privileged point of contact to which MPs can address any request relating to parliamentary services.
Contribute, in cooperation with other directorates general, to the simplification and harmonization of procedures related to the services offered to Members of Parliament and their assistants.
Ensure quality control and monitor customer satisfaction, with a view to constantly improving the service.
06-1010.refined_description: Unit 06-1010 operates as a specialized parliamentary support directorate within the General Management structure (DG 06) of the European Parliament, functioning as a critical interface between the institution's administrative apparatus and its Members of Parliament. The unit's primary mission centers on delivering comprehensive, high-quality assistance and information services to Members and their parliamentary assistants, with 70% of operational capacity dedicated to guaranteeing timely, accurate responses to parliamentary inquiries in full compliance with Bureau-established protocols, deadlines, and procedural standards. Functioning as the designated primary contact point and centralized access mechanism (15% of workload), Unit 06-1010 consolidates and channels all Member-related parliamentary service inquiries through a unified focal point, thereby centralizing access to institutional support, ensuring consistent information delivery, and enhancing service accessibility and accountability. The unit actively collaborates with peer directorates general and sibling units within DG 06 (including 06-10, 06-20, 06-30, 06-40, 06-A, and others) to advance procedural simplification and harmonization initiatives (10% of resources), contributing to institutional efficiency and consistency across the full range of parliamentary administrative functions. Additionally, Unit 06-1010 allocates 5% of its operational capacity to implementing systematic quality assurance mechanisms and customer satisfaction monitoring systems, enabling evidence-based continuous service improvement and responsiveness to evolving parliamentary needs. Through these integrated activities, Unit 06-1010 directly supports the European Parliament's operational effectiveness by ensuring Members and their support staff maintain optimal access to institutional resources and administrative guidance while reporting directly to its parent directorate.
06-1010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Member Support Services & Information Delivery** (70%): Provision of high-level assistance and comprehensive information services to Members of Parliament and parliamentary assistants, with guaranteed response delivery within Bureau-established timeframes and in accordance with procedural standards and Bureau decisions

• **Centralized Access Point & Member Service Coordination** (15%): Establishment and operation of Unit 06-1010 as the privileged single point of contact and unified access mechanism for all Members addressing requests relating to parliamentary services, ensuring streamlined request management, coordinated service delivery, and enhanced accessibility with improved accountability

• **Procedural Harmonization & Institutional Integration** (10%): Collaborative engagement with other directorates general and sibling units within DG 06 toward the streamlining, standardization, and simplification of parliamentary service procedures, aimed at reducing complexity, improving cross-directorate service delivery, and advancing institutional efficiency

• **Quality Assurance & Continuous Improvement** (5%): Implementation of quality control mechanisms and customer satisfaction monitoring frameworks designed to evaluate service performance, facilitate evidence-based service enhancement initiatives, and drive systematic responsiveness to evolving parliamentary needs

original_activities: Manage the development projects for Europa Experience visitor's centers and buildings accommodating the Houses of Europe/Liaison Offices, in coordination with the various stakeholders.
Carry out applicable procedures to rent or acquire premises and, where appropriate, rent a buffer building. Ensure the management of the relevant technical, economic and contractual aspects.
Analyse and prospect the property markets in the cities where the Parliament's Liaison Offices are located and study the feasibility of incorporating a Europa Experience.
Prepare the implementation of interactive installations for the Europa Experience, including future adjustments, in cooperation with the Directorate-General for Communication.
Ensure the technical, administrative and legal management of acquisitions, development and construction projects, as well as the budgetary management including tax aspects.
Negotiate with the owners of the buildings accommodating the Houses of Europe/Liaison Offices with a view to extending leases.
Keep the Bureau's Working Group on Sustainable Infrastructure and Services regularly updated on developments related to the unit's activities, and liaise with the political authorities.

Within the framework of interinstitutional cooperation of the Houses of Europe, define the financial obligations of the parties and establish administrative agreements or 'Memoranda of Understanding'.
06-20.refined_description: Unit 06-20 functions as a specialized directorate within the General Management structure (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, operating as a leaf-node organizational entity with direct reporting to the Secretary General through its parent directorate. The unit bears primary responsibility for the strategic development, acquisition, and operational management of the Parliament's physical infrastructure and visitor engagement spaces, encompassing the Europa Experience visitor centers and the geographically dispersed network of Houses of Europe/Liaison Offices across European cities. Unit 06-20 executes this institutional mandate through eight integrated activity streams that collectively advance the Parliament's core objectives of enhancing citizen engagement, strengthening decentralized representation, and ensuring sustainable infrastructure development across its distributed operations. The unit operates in systematic coordination with sibling units (06-10, 06-1010, 06-30, 06-40, 06A, and four additional units) within the General Management structure, while maintaining regular stakeholder engagement with the Bureau's Working Group on Sustainable Infrastructure and Services and relevant political authorities. All activities are executed with comprehensive technical, administrative, legal, and financial stewardship, including budgetary management, tax considerations, and the establishment of formal governance frameworks through Memoranda of Understanding within interinstitutional Houses of Europe cooperation structures.
06-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Project Development and Strategic Planning** (38%): Management of development projects for Europa Experience visitor centers and associated buildings accommodating Houses of Europe/Liaison Offices in coordination with multiple organizational stakeholders (23%); conducting comprehensive market analysis and feasibility studies for property opportunities in cities where Liaison Offices operate, with particular focus on incorporating Europa Experience components (15%)

• **Real Estate Acquisition and Lease Management** (27%): Execution of comprehensive property acquisition and rental procedures, including buffer building arrangements, while maintaining oversight of technical, economic, and contractual management (17%); negotiation of lease extensions with building proprietors accommodating the Houses of Europe/Liaison Offices (10%)

• **Technical Implementation and Financial Administration** (20%): Technical, administrative, and legal stewardship of all acquisitions, development, and construction projects, including budgetary management and tax aspect oversight (10%); preparation and implementation of interactive installations for the Europa Experience in collaboration with the Directorate-General for Communication, including management of future adjustments and modifications (10%)

• **Governance, Coordination, and Interinstitutional Relations** (15%): Regular liaison with the Bureau's Working Group on Sustainable Infrastructure and Services and political authorities regarding unit developments and strategic initiatives (10%); establishment of financial obligations and administrative frameworks within interinstitutional Houses of Europe cooperation structures through formal agreements and Memoranda of Understanding (5%)

original_activities: Provide the necessary support to the hierarchy and operational teams of the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics to facilitate the harmonious deployment of the new physical accessibility policy and the implementation of the environmental objectives for the new legislature.

Ensure the coordination of specific actions carried out in the field of physical accessibility and environmental policy in different workplaces, including the management of internal coordination networks.

Develop the necessary tools to guarantee the harmonized and coherent implementation of the physical accessibility policy within the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics, facilitate exchanges and contribute to the sharing of expertise and good practices between the different sites.
Guarantee the implementation and management of 'Buildings, Environment and Accessibility' regulatory monitoring tools in the three workplaces.
Support environmental audits and develop internal communication and monitoring tools regarding the implementation of the environmental policy.
Represent the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics in different formats and thematic structures, in its areas of expertise.
06-30.refined_description:
**

Unit 06-30 operates as a specialized directional unit within the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, functioning as the institutional nexus for policy harmonization, regulatory compliance, and cross-site coordination in support of the parent directorate's strategic objectives. The unit's primary mission encompasses systematic implementation and harmonization of physical accessibility policies and environmental objectives across the institution's operational framework for the new legislative term, positioning it as a critical enabler of institutional sustainability and accessibility goals while directly supporting the General Directorate hierarchy and operational teams. The unit serves as a strategic coordination mechanism across multiple institutional workplace locations, establishing and managing internal coordination networks while facilitating inter-site expertise sharing and best-practice dissemination. Unit 06-30 implements integrated regulatory monitoring systems for Buildings, Environment, and Accessibility compliance across three designated workplace sites and supports environmental audits through development of internal communication and monitoring infrastructure to track policy implementation effectiveness. The unit represents the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics in external forums and thematic institutional structures relevant to accessibility and environmental expertise, while collaborating with sibling units (06-10, 06-1010, 06-20, 06-40, 06A, and others) to ensure comprehensive institutional alignment and coherent policy application throughout the directorate's operational domain.

---

**
06-30.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Policy Support and Hierarchical Coordination** (25%): Provision of essential hierarchical and operational support to General Directorate leadership and operational teams to facilitate harmonious deployment of new physical accessibility policy and implementation of environmental objectives for the new legislative term

• **Cross-Site Coordination and Network Management** (20%): Coordination of specific actions in physical accessibility and environmental policy across different institutional workplace locations, including establishment, management, and facilitation of internal coordination networks and inter-site collaboration mechanisms

• **Tool Development and Policy Harmonization** (15%): Development of standardized implementation tools and frameworks guaranteeing harmonized and coherent policy application throughout the General Directorate; facilitation of inter-site expertise exchanges and best-practice dissemination mechanisms

• **Regulatory Monitoring and Compliance Management** (15%): Implementation and management of specialized integrated 'Buildings, Environment and Accessibility' regulatory monitoring tools across three designated institutional workplace sites, ensuring systematic compliance tracking and documentation

• **Environmental Audit Support and Internal Communication Infrastructure** (15%): Support for environmental audits and development of internal communication and monitoring infrastructure to track environmental policy implementation effectiveness, progress, and performance metrics

• **External Institutional Representation and Engagement** (10%): Representation of the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics in external forums, thematic institutional structures, and various institutional formats where specialized expertise in accessibility and environmental domains is required

original_activities: Develop architectural programs and preliminary draft projects in the three places of work and the Member states.
Manage the operational lease contract for furnishing the Members' working environment in Brussels.
Create an architectural portfolio of the European Parliament's buildings.
Participate in calls for tenders launched by the other directorates related to architectural interventions.
Other missions.
06-40.refined_description: Unit 06-40 functions as the Architectural Planning and Asset Management directorate within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Unit 06), operating as a specialized direction-level unit reporting through the organizational hierarchy to the Secretary General. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 06-40 bears primary responsibility for the comprehensive architectural planning, portfolio documentation, and operational management of the European Parliament's physical infrastructure across its three official places of work (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg) and all Member State facilities. The unit's core mission integrates four major functional areas that collectively ensure the Parliament maintains strategically planned, professionally managed, and comprehensively documented physical facilities supporting institutional operational requirements. Unit 06-40 operates in coordinated relationship with sibling units (06-10, 06-1010, 06-20, 06-30, 06A, and others) under the unified governance framework of the General Management directorate, particularly regarding facility-related procurement and infrastructure initiatives. The unit's specialized architectural expertise extends to providing technical support through participation in procurement processes and tender calls initiated by peer directorates concerning architectural interventions and related infrastructure projects.
06-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Architectural Planning and Development** (30%): Development of comprehensive architectural programs and preliminary draft projects across the three official places of work and Member State facilities, establishing foundational design parameters, project specifications, and ensuring compliance with institutional standards and functional requirements

• **Facilities and Environmental Management** (30%): Operational management of the furnishing and equipment lease contract governing Members' working environment in Brussels, maintaining optimal conditions and functional standards for parliamentary operations

• **Institutional Documentation and Portfolio Management** (30%): Creation, curation, and maintenance of a comprehensive architectural portfolio documenting the European Parliament's building assets, infrastructure heritage, and serving as a critical institutional reference tool and historical record

• **Interdepartmental Procurement and Cross-Directorate Support** (5%): Participation in calls for tenders and procurement processes launched by peer directorates within General Management concerning architectural interventions and related infrastructure projects, providing specialized architectural expertise

• **Supplementary Institutional Missions** (5%): Execution of additional specialized missions and assignments as directed by senior management to support the General Management directorate's broader objectives

06A.name: 06A
original_activities: Assure at central level the procurement, financial initiation and budgetary overview for the units of the directorate. Follow the budgetary cycle and coordinate and prepare the different contributions of the directorate and its units to the directorate-General in this context.
Oversee, coordinate and motivate the directorate's units and services, and ensure that information is exchanged between the various services of the directorate.
Help, as part of the management team, to draw up the directorate's strategic guidelines, and ensure that budgetary, strategic and operational decisions are taken and applied effectively.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-directorate-general committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., and represent the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those forums.
Coordinate and give legal and technical advice for matters specific to the directorate, represent Parliament as owner of its buildings in Luxembourg and European cities (except France).
Act as authorising officer by subdelegation.
06A.refined_description: Unit 06A functions as the strategic and operational management nucleus of the General Management Directorate (Unit 06) within the European Parliament's institutional hierarchy, serving as the primary organizational hub with direct coordination and oversight responsibilities across three subordinate units (06A10 Buildings Management and Maintenance, 06A20, and 06A30). Operating at the directorate level, Unit 06A executes comprehensive financial stewardship encompassing centralized procurement management, financial initiation, and budgetary oversight across all directorate units while managing the complete budgetary cycle and coordinating multi-unit contributions to Directorate-General level financial processes, ensuring rigorous alignment with budgetary requirements and resource optimization. The unit provides essential oversight, coordination, and motivational leadership to the directorate's constituent units and services while facilitating critical information exchange mechanisms and organizational cohesion across directorate functions. As an integral component of the directorate management team, Unit 06A contributes substantively to the formulation of strategic guidelines and ensures the systematic implementation and cascading of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions throughout the organizational structure with measurable effectiveness. Unit 06A maintains active institutional representation across intra-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies, serving as the directorate's primary representative forum and advocate for directorate and directorate-general positions in external governance contexts. Additionally, the unit provides specialized legal and technical counsel on directorate-specific matters and exercises stewardship over the European Parliament's real property portfolio located in Luxembourg and designated European cities outside France, while fulfilling delegated authorizing officer responsibilities through formal subdelegation arrangements that enable financial transaction authorization within established parameters.
06A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Stewardship & Budgetary Management** (30%): Assures centralized procurement, financial initiation, and comprehensive budgetary oversight for all directorate units; manages the complete budgetary cycle; coordinates and prepares directorate and unit contributions to Directorate-General financial submissions and strategic planning cycles; ensures financial coherence and resource optimization across all subordinate operational units

• **Organizational Leadership & Coordination** (25%): Oversees and coordinates all directorate units and services; motivates personnel across organizational units; facilitates structured information exchange between directorate services; ensures operational coherence and organizational cohesion

• **Strategic Leadership & Decision Implementation** (25%): Participates as core management team member in formulating directorate strategic guidelines; ensures systematic implementation and cascading of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions throughout the organizational structure with measurable effectiveness; contributes to strategic policy formulation at directorate level

• **Institutional Representation & External Engagement** (10%): Participates in and represents the directorate within intra-directorate-general committees, inter-directorate-general working groups, interinstitutional bodies, and external governance forums; advocates directorate and directorate-general positions as institutional representative

• **Legal, Technical & Property Management Services** (10%): Provides specialized legal and technical advisory counsel on matters specific to the directorate; represents the European Parliament as institutional owner of real property holdings and building assets located in Luxembourg and designated European cities (excluding France); executes delegated authorizing officer responsibilities through formal subdelegation, enabling financial transaction authorization within established parameters (2% embedded within this category)

06A10.name: 06A10
original_activities: Operate and maintain technical equipment, building envelope and finishes, exterior spaces; ensure cleaning, waste management, management of green spaces and energy management in buildings in Luxembourg.

Manage environmental aspects linked to buildings (environmental permits, EMAS actions, energy, air/water quality), audits and technical inspections of buildings, regulatory inspections of technical installations and health and safety coordination.
Maintain medium and long term planning for maintenance work in accordance with the Parliamentary Real Estate Policy.
Manage service requests sent to the Buildings Helpdesk and interface with customers for issues communicated via the Buildings Helpdesk.
Manage the administrative, financial and budgetary aspects linked to the management of buildings and related services, including the management of concessions and rents.
Manage key requests for the ADENAUER building.
06A10.refined_description: Unit 06A10 functions as the operational Buildings Management and Maintenance nucleus within Direction 06A of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as the critical infrastructure steward for the Parliament's Luxembourg-based facilities. Operating as a second-tier hierarchical unit with no subordinate divisions, Unit 06A10 bears comprehensive responsibility for the technical, environmental, administrative, and customer-facing dimensions of building operations and real estate asset management across all designated Luxembourg properties. The unit's integrated operational framework encompasses five primary functional domains: technical infrastructure operations and maintenance (45% of workload); environmental compliance and regulatory oversight (20% of workload); strategic asset planning and maintenance administration (10% of workload); customer service and helpdesk operations (10% of workload); and financial, administrative, and specialized facility management (15% of workload). Collaborating laterally with sibling units 06A20 and 06A30 under the strategic coordination of parent unit 06A, Unit 06A10 provides the essential operational foundation enabling the Parliament's institutional functioning through systematic, compliant, and efficient facility management while advancing the institution's facilities excellence and operational sustainability objectives.
06A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Technical Infrastructure Operations & Maintenance** (45%): Operate and maintain technical equipment, building envelopes, interior finishes, and exterior spaces across all Luxembourg buildings; execute cleaning services, waste management systems, green space stewardship, and energy management protocols

• **Environmental Compliance & Regulatory Oversight** (20%): Manage environmental permits, EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) certification actions, and energy auditing protocols; conduct air and water quality monitoring; perform technical building and installation inspections; verify regulatory compliance; coordinate health and safety protocols

• **Strategic Asset Planning & Maintenance Administration** (10%): Develop and maintain medium and long-term maintenance planning frameworks aligned with Parliamentary Real Estate Policy

• **Customer Service & Helpdesk Operations** (10%): Manage and process service requests through the Buildings Helpdesk system; provide direct customer interface for facility-related issue communication and resolution

• **Financial, Administrative & Specialized Facility Management** (15%): Administer financial and budgetary aspects of building operations; manage concession and rental agreements; execute specialized key management services for the ADENAUER building

06A20.name: 06A20
original_activities: Operate and maintain technical equipment, building envelope and finishes, exterior spaces; ensure cleaning, waste management, green space management and energy management in liaison offices in the Member States.

Manage environmental aspects linked to buildings (environmental permits, EMAS actions, energy, air/water quality), audits and technical inspections of buildings, regulatory inspections of technical installations and health and safety coordination.
Maintain medium and long term planning for maintenance work in accordance with the Parliamentary Real Estate Policy.
Manage service requests sent to the Buildings Helpdesk and interface with customers for issues communicated via the Buildings Helpdesk.
Manage the administrative, financial and budgetary aspects linked to the management of buildings and related services, including the management of concessions and rents.
06A20.refined_description: Unit 06A20 functions as a specialized operational division within Direction 06A of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, bearing primary responsibility for the comprehensive management and stewardship of the Parliament's liaison office buildings and facilities across all Member States. Operating as a second-level hierarchical unit coordinating with sibling units 06A10 and 06A30, Unit 06A20 executes five integrated functional domains that collectively ensure operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and sustainable asset management across the Parliament's geographically dispersed real estate portfolio. The unit's dominant operational focus (40% of workload) encompasses the direct operation, maintenance, and technical stewardship of building systems, structural envelopes, architectural finishes, and exterior spaces, alongside the delivery of cleaning services, waste management protocols, green space administration, and energy management functions across all liaison office locations. Complementing these core facilities operations, the unit allocates 20% of its resources to the management of comprehensive environmental governance and technical regulatory compliance frameworks, including environmental permitting procedures, EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) implementation and certification actions, energy performance monitoring and auditing, air and water quality management, facility and technical building inspections, regulatory compliance reviews of technical installations, and health and safety coordination responsibilities. The unit dedicates an additional 20% of its workload to the formulation and execution of medium and long-term maintenance planning strategies aligned with the Parliamentary Real Estate Policy, ensuring systematic lifecycle management and institutional asset preservation. Supporting service delivery and stakeholder engagement, the unit allocates 10% of its resources to the management of service requests channeled through the Buildings Helpdesk system, functioning as the primary customer interface for building-related operational issues and inquiries. Finally, the unit dedicates 10% of its capacity to the administrative, financial, and budgetary oversight of buildings management services, including the management of concession agreements and rental arrangements. Collectively, these integrated activity streams position Unit 06A20 as a critical operational support function that sustains the Parliament's institutional infrastructure while maintaining compliance with European environmental and safety standards.
06A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Facilities Operations and Maintenance** (40%): Direct operation, maintenance, and technical stewardship of building systems, structural envelopes, architectural finishes, and exterior spaces across all Member State liaison offices; delivery of cleaning services, waste management protocols, green space administration, and energy management functions

• **Environmental Compliance and Technical Regulatory Governance** (20%): Management of environmental permits and EMAS implementation; energy performance monitoring and auditing; air and water quality management; execution of facility and technical building inspections; regulatory compliance reviews of technical installations; health and safety coordination

• **Strategic Asset Planning and Lifecycle Management** (20%): Development and execution of medium and long-term maintenance planning strategies aligned with Parliamentary Real Estate Policy; systematic lifecycle management and institutional asset preservation

• **Customer Service and Helpdesk Operations** (10%): Management of Buildings Helpdesk service requests; primary customer interface for building-related operational issues and inquiries; stakeholder issue resolution

• **Administrative and Financial Stewardship** (10%): Administrative, financial, and budgetary oversight of buildings management services; management of concession agreements and rental arrangements

06A30.name: 06A30
original_activities: Manage development works and improve buildings located in Luxembourg in order to guarantee the continuity of the activities of the European Parliament as well as comply with the requirements of national laws and environmental and accessibility objectives.
Manage and modernize the graphic and technical archives of the Luxembourg site. Create the 3D digital model and improve building signage.
Continue to develop and deepen relationships with local authorities and other European institutions on the Luxembourg site.
Assist the various general managements in the organization of various events that they regularly organize on the Luxembourg site.

In the context of the biodiversity of the European Parliament on the three sites, continue to pilot and coordinate this multilateral project in order to preserve, promote and improve the biodiversity of the green spaces of the European Parliament.
06A30.refined_description:
**

Unit 06A30 operates as a specialized facility management and institutional coordination unit within Direction 06A of the General Management Directorate, positioned at the second hierarchical level of the organizational structure. The unit holds primary responsibility for comprehensive management, development, and modernization of the European Parliament's Luxembourg site infrastructure and facilities, executing this mandate through five integrated activity streams that collectively position it as the operational backbone for Luxembourg site management while advancing Parliament-wide sustainability and institutional excellence objectives. The unit's dominant operational focus ensures uninterrupted continuity of parliamentary activities through systematic planning, execution, and oversight of development works and building improvements across the Luxembourg campus, maintaining full compliance with applicable Luxembourg national legislation, environmental regulations, and accessibility standards while advancing sustainability objectives. Complementing this infrastructure-focused core, the unit manages and modernizes the site's graphic and technical archives, creates comprehensive three-dimensional digital building models, and enhances wayfinding and building signage systems to optimize user navigation and facility functionality. The unit further strengthens institutional positioning by cultivating collaborative relationships with Luxembourg local authorities and other European institutions co-located on the site, while providing logistical and organizational support to general management divisions for regularly scheduled events at the Luxembourg location. Additionally, Unit 06A30 serves as the coordinating pilot for the European Parliament's multilateral biodiversity initiative spanning all three institutional sites, directing systematic preservation, promotion, and enhancement of the organization's green spaces to improve ecological value and environmental quality across its geographic footprint.

**
06A30.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Infrastructure Development & Building Management** (55%): Plan, execute, and oversee development works and building improvements across the Luxembourg campus; ensure uninterrupted continuity of parliamentary activities; maintain compliance with Luxembourg national legislation, environmental regulations, and accessibility standards

• **Digital Modernization & Facility Documentation** (25%): Manage and modernize graphic and technical archives; create comprehensive three-dimensional digital building models; enhance building signage and wayfinding systems to optimize user navigation and facility functionality

• **Institutional Relations & Stakeholder Engagement** (10%): Cultivate and deepen collaborative relationships with Luxembourg local authorities; coordinate with other European institutions co-located on the Luxembourg site; strengthen institutional positioning and collaborative networks

• **Event Support Services** (5%): Provide logistical and organizational assistance to general management divisions for regularly scheduled institutional events at the Luxembourg location

• **Environmental Stewardship & Biodiversity Coordination** (5%): Pilot and coordinate the multilateral biodiversity initiative across all three European Parliament sites; preserve, promote, and systematically improve ecological value and environmental quality of the institution's green spaces across its geographic footprint

06B.name: 06B
original_activities: Oversee, coordinate and supervise the directorate's units, ensuring the continuity of operations and the teams' resilience and cohesion.
Help, as part of the management team, to draw up the directorate's strategic guidelines, and ensure that operational and strategic decisions are applied effectively.
Provide Members and staff with high-quality services in terms of catering, transport, sustainable mobility, mail distribution, purchasing, office space management and removals.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-directorate-general committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., represent the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those fora.
Act as authorising officer by subdelegation.
06B.refined_description: Unit 06B functions as the Direction-level management and coordination hub within the General Management Directorate (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, operating with direct accountability to the parent unit and the Secretary General. The unit's organizational mandate encompasses five distinct and fully documented operational and strategic functions that collectively represent 100% of workload allocation across the directorate's integrated structure. As the primary supervisory entity, Unit 06B exercises comprehensive oversight, coordination, and supervision of all subordinate directorate units (06B10, 06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B50, 06B5010, and three additional child units), while actively ensuring operational continuity, team resilience, and organizational cohesion throughout the multi-unit hierarchy. The unit functions concurrently as an integral member of the directorate's management team, contributing substantially to the formulation of strategic guidelines and ensuring the effective cascading and implementation of both operational and strategic decisions throughout the organizational structure. Unit 06B delivers essential institutional support services to Members and staff across multiple specialized domains—including catering operations, transport coordination, sustainable mobility initiatives, mail distribution systems, purchasing administration, office space management, and removals logistics—while maintaining active engagement in intra-directorate, inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies where it serves as the primary representative of the directorate and, when required, the directorate-general. Additionally, the unit exercises delegated authorizing officer responsibilities through formal subdelegation, ensuring appropriate financial and administrative authority within its operational scope and maintaining critical financial accountability functions.
06B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Management and Operational Oversight** (50%): Oversee, coordinate, and supervise all subordinate directorate units (06B10, 06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B50, 06B5010, and three additional units); ensure operational continuity; maintain team resilience and organizational cohesion across the multi-unit directorate structure.

• **Strategic Leadership and Decision Implementation** (30%): Participate as an integral member of the directorate management team in formulating strategic guidelines; ensure effective cascading and implementation of both operational and strategic decisions throughout the organizational hierarchy.

• **Institutional Support Services Delivery** (10%): Provide integrated, high-quality support services to Members and staff encompassing catering operations, transport coordination, sustainable mobility initiatives, mail distribution systems, purchasing administration, office space management, and removals logistics.

• **Interinstitutional Representation and Governance Participation** (8%): Participate actively in intra-directorate and inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies; represent the directorate and, when required, the directorate-general in these collaborative forums.

• **Financial Authorization and Administrative Authority** (2%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial transactions and commitments; maintain delegated financial and administrative authority within assigned operational scope.

06B10.name: 06B10
original_activities: Organize and ensure the transport of Members of Parliament, delegations, Presidents of political groups, directors general and other administration services.
Ensure the administrative and human resources management of the unit.
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer for the transport budget line. Prepare calls for tenders and manage framework contracts.
Contribute to the evolution of work instruments and the modernization of the unit and the services offered.
06B10.refined_description: Unit 06B10 operates as a specialized Transport Operations and Logistics Management unit within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate (DG 06), positioned as a leaf-node operational entity responsible for coordinating and executing comprehensive institutional mobility services. The unit's foundational mission, consuming 90% of its operational capacity, encompasses the organization and facilitation of transport services for Members of Parliament, parliamentary delegations, Presidents of political groups, Directors General, and other administration services—reflecting its critical role in enabling institutional mobility and diplomatic engagement across multiple operational contexts. Exercising subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities, Unit 06B10 allocates 3% of its workload to transport budget line management, including the preparation of calls for tenders and the administration of framework contracts with external transport service providers, thereby ensuring fiscal accountability and procurement governance within its delegated authority. The unit dedicates 4% of its operational capacity to internal administrative and human resources management functions, encompassing personnel administration and staff development activities essential for organizational sustainability and unit effectiveness. Additionally, Unit 06B10 commits 3% of its workload to contributing to the continuous modernization of work instruments, the evolution of operational methodologies, and the enhancement of services delivered to institutional stakeholders, supporting the broader institutional objective of continuous improvement. Operating within the Direction 06B management structure, Unit 06B10 functions in coordinated collaboration with sibling units (06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B50, 06B5010, and others) to deliver integrated general management services and support the overarching administrative and operational objectives of the European Parliament.
06B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Transport Operations and Logistics Coordination (90%)**: Organization and execution of comprehensive transport services for Members of Parliament, parliamentary delegations, Presidents of political groups, Directors General, and administration services across all institutional mobility requirements and operational contexts

• **Budget Management and Procurement Oversight (3%)**: Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer authority for transport budget line execution, preparation of calls for tenders, and management of framework contracts with external transport service providers to ensure fiscal accountability and procurement compliance

• **Administrative and Human Resources Management (4%)**: Internal unit administration, personnel management, staff development, and human resources functions to ensure organizational effectiveness and operational sustainability

• **Organizational Development and Service Enhancement (3%)**: Contribution to the modernization of work instruments, evolution of operational methodologies, and continuous improvement of services and institutional capacity offered to stakeholder beneficiaries

06B30.name: 06B30
original_activities: Organize tender procedures and manage the resulting contracts (catering concessions and central purchasing office, office furniture, technical equipment, service clothing, service vehicles, office supplies, etc.). Develop innovative public purchasing strategies.

Process financial transactions for the Logistics Department, following established procedures. Provide support to managers for budget planning and contract management. Coordinate financial and budgetary aspects of management, including communication with external parties.
Manage and plan inventory procurement across the EP's three workplaces. In accordance with EMAS policy and in order to make budgetary savings, repair, recover and clean office furniture for reuse.

Manage the inventory of goods acquired by management, in close cooperation with the Inventory Service of DG FINS (first inventory, tracking and monitoring of goods throughout their entire life cycle, decommissioning of depreciated goods).

Purchase office furniture for all EP officials and agents in the three workplaces and EPLOs across the EU. Organize delivery logistics in consultation with customer services. Financially manage the related budget lines.
Buy technical equipment, small office supplies, service clothing (tailcoats, suits, work and safety clothing). Manage the related budget lines.
Ensure the management of the unit (administrative management, human resources).
06B30.refined_description: Unit 06B30 operates as the Procurement and Inventory Management division within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, functioning as the central operational hub for institutional purchasing, asset management, and logistics coordination across the Parliament's three geographically dispersed workplaces and European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) throughout the European Union. The unit executes a comprehensive, integrated portfolio of procurement, financial management, and inventory stewardship activities representing 100% of documented organizational workload, with explicit mandate to develop and implement innovative public purchasing strategies aligned with institutional procurement objectives. Core responsibilities encompass the complete procurement lifecycle—from tender procedure organization and execution for diverse goods and services through to final asset decommissioning—while maintaining rigorous financial stewardship over all Logistics Department transactions, providing specialized budget planning and contract management support to management personnel, and coordinating comprehensive financial and budgetary aspects with external stakeholders and customer service departments. The unit maintains strategic inventory management and procurement planning across all three Parliament workplaces, incorporating sustainability objectives through the EMAS environmental policy framework and generating budgetary efficiencies via systematic repair, recovery, and refurbishment of office furniture for operational reuse, while operating in close operational coordination with the Inventory Service of DG FINS to manage the complete asset lifecycle encompassing initial inventory registration, continuous tracking and monitoring, and formal decommissioning procedures for depreciated goods. Specialized procurement operations address office furniture acquisition and delivery coordination for all Parliament officials, agents, and EPLO personnel across the three workplaces and EU-wide locations, alongside procurement of technical equipment, small office supplies, and specialized service clothing including formal attire and occupational safety garments, with integrated financial management of all associated budget allocations and logistics coordination. Administrative and human resources management functions complete the unit's operational portfolio, ensuring organizational effectiveness and personnel resource optimization within the broader institutional framework of Direction 06B.
06B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Procurement Strategy, Tender Management, and Innovation** (16%): Organization and execution of comprehensive tender procedures for catering concessions, central purchasing office operations, office furniture, technical equipment, service clothing, service vehicles, and office supplies; development and implementation of innovative public purchasing strategies to optimize procurement outcomes and align with institutional procurement objectives

• **Financial Operations and Budgetary Management** (16%): Processing of all financial transactions for the Logistics Department following established procedures; provision of specialized budget planning and contract management support to management personnel; coordination of comprehensive financial and budgetary aspects with external stakeholders and customer service departments; management of budget lines across all procurement categories

• **Inventory Procurement Planning and Sustainability Management** (16%): Strategic planning and management of inventory procurement across the Parliament's three workplaces; implementation of EMAS environmental policy objectives and sustainability compliance; systematic repair, recovery, and refurbishment of office furniture for operational reuse to achieve budgetary efficiencies and resource optimization

• **Asset Lifecycle Management and Inventory Control Coordination** (16%): Close operational coordination with the Inventory Service of DG FINS for complete institutional asset management; initial inventory registration and first-time tracking of acquired goods; continuous monitoring and tracking of goods throughout their entire operational lifecycle; formal decommissioning procedures for depreciated assets and end-of-life goods

• **Specialized Procurement Operations—Office Furniture and Equipment** (16%): Acquisition and delivery logistics coordination of office furniture for all Parliament officials, agents, and EPLO personnel across the three workplaces and EU-wide locations; integrated financial management of associated budget allocations and logistics coordination in consultation with customer service departments; procurement of technical equipment, small office supplies, and specialized service clothing including formal attire (tailcoats, suits) and occupational safety garments with corresponding budget line management
