• **Unit Administration and Human Resources Management** (4%): Administrative management and human resources functions supporting unit-level organizational effectiveness, operational continuity, and personnel resource optimization

06B3010.name: 06B3010
original_activities: Ensure the processing and monitoring of financial transactions based on elements provided by the operational units of the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department in compliance with the procedures and rules in force.
Provide monitoring, support and advice to the directorate's authorizing officers and managers in the context of budgetary planning and execution as well as for contract management.
Ensure the financial and budgetary coordination of the department, including the coordination of information exchanges from and to outside the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department.
06B3010.refined_description: Unit 06B3010 is a specialized financial transaction and budgetary management unit positioned at the second hierarchical level within the European Parliament's organizational structure, operating as a subordinate operational component of Direction 06B (the management and coordination hub within the General Management Directorate) and serving as a critical financial control mechanism for the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 06B3010 operates in coordination with its sibling units (06B10, 06B30, 06B40, 06B50, 06B5010, and three additional units) under the unified direction of Direction 06B. The unit's primary mission encompasses three interconnected financial stewardship functions that collectively ensure the integrity, compliance, and coordination of financial operations within the department while enabling the General Management Directorate to maintain transparent financial oversight and institutional compliance across Parliament's mobility and sustainable services operations. The unit systematically processes and monitors all financial transactions originating from the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department's operational units in strict adherence to established procedural frameworks and regulatory requirements; provides comprehensive monitoring, advisory support, and technical guidance to the directorate's authorizing officers and managers regarding budgetary planning, budget execution activities, and contract management oversight; and maintains financial and budgetary coordination across the department through systematic coordination of information exchanges both internally within departmental stakeholders and externally with other institutional entities beyond the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department. Through these three integrated activities, Unit 06B3010 serves as an essential operational support function that enables Direction 06B to maintain organizational financial discipline while supporting operational managers in the effective and compliant deployment of institutional resources.
06B3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Transaction Processing and Compliance Monitoring (70%)**: Systematic processing and monitoring of all financial transactions based on elements and submissions provided by operational units of the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department, maintaining strict adherence to established institutional procedures and regulatory rules in force

• **Managerial Advisory and Budgetary Support (20%)**: Provision of comprehensive monitoring, advisory support, and technical guidance to directorate authorizing officers and managers concerning budgetary planning, budget execution activities, and contract management oversight and protocols

• **Departmental Financial and Budgetary Coordination (10%)**: Comprehensive financial and budgetary coordination across the department, including systematic coordination and facilitation of information exchanges originating from and directed to both internal departmental stakeholders and external entities outside the Mobility and Sustainable Services Department

06B40.name: 06B40
original_activities: Ensure the monitoring of catering contracts in the three workplaces and Bazoches, ensure their proper execution (collective catering, à la carte, cafeterias, take-away sales and other spaces), as well as calls for tenders for concession contracts and competition between service providers. Ensure the implementation and compliance with EMAS and PMR standards.

Prepare specifications and implement administrative procedures aimed at purchasing the equipment necessary for catering. Control the services of external firms, through an external audit firm. Check deliveries and ensure inventory of newly acquired goods.

Check the compliance of requests for reservations in the 'event' area, including the application of the Office's transparency rules, and propose alternative locations if necessary. Act as a 'one-stop shop' for catering at events and conferences within the European Parliament by ensuring good coordination with DG SAFE, DG LINC and DG COMM.
Ensure the management of the unit and coordinate the various actions in the field of catering aimed at the continuity of these services and their strategic planning.
Ensure the management of credits for budget items 1652, 2140-05 and 3040, concerning catering, materials and equipment. Prepare expenditure orders (invoice control). Prepare budget forecasts.
Ensure the installation and maintenance of water fountains on the public water network, ensure the delivery of household appliances to the liaison offices.
06B40.refined_description:
Unit 06B40 operates as a specialized catering and facilities management unit within Direction 06B of the General Management Directorate (DG), functioning as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for comprehensive catering service delivery and ancillary facility management across three parliamentary workplaces and the Bazoches facility. The unit exercises complete lifecycle management of catering operations—encompassing contractual oversight and vendor performance monitoring (30% of workload), equipment procurement with technical specification development and quality assurance via external audit mechanisms (20% of workload), and centralized coordination of event catering and conference services with institutional transparency compliance verification (20% of workload)—while simultaneously ensuring organizational continuity through strategic planning, management oversight, and cross-functional coordination with DG SAFE, DG LINC, and DG COMM (15% of workload). Unit 06B40 maintains fiduciary responsibility for dedicated budget items 1652, 2140-05, and 3040 through expenditure order processing, invoice verification, and budget forecasting (10% of workload), while providing supplementary facility management services including public water network fountain installation and maintenance, alongside equipment delivery coordination to liaison offices (5% of workload). As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 06B40 operates directly under Direction 06B's coordination framework and functions in parallel with sibling units to support the Parliament's institutional operations, with particular emphasis on EMAS environmental compliance and PMR accessibility standards across all catering service delivery points, calls for tenders administration, and concession contract competition management.

---
06B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Catering Contract Management and Compliance Oversight** (30%): Comprehensive monitoring and enforcement of catering contract execution across three parliamentary workplaces and Bazoches facility; supervision of collective catering operations, à la carte services, cafeterias, take-away sales, and ancillary food service spaces; administration of calls for tenders and concession contract competitions to foster service provider competition; implementation and verification of EMAS environmental standards and PMR accessibility requirements across all catering service delivery points.

• **Equipment Procurement, Specification Development, and Quality Assurance** (20%): Development of detailed technical specifications and execution of administrative procurement procedures for catering equipment; external audit firm oversight of service provider performance and compliance; delivery verification and inventory management of newly acquired goods; quality assurance mechanisms throughout the procurement lifecycle.

• **Event Catering Coordination, Reservation Management, and Inter-Directorate Liaison** (20%): Centralized "one-stop-shop" coordination of catering services for European Parliament events and conferences; verification of event reservation requests for compliance with institutional transparency regulations; identification and proposal of alternative venue locations when necessary; coordinated collaboration and liaison with DG SAFE, DG LINC, and DG COMM to ensure integrated service delivery.

• **Strategic Management, Organizational Continuity, and Financial Administration** (25%): Operational management of the unit and coordination of catering-related initiatives to ensure service continuity and strategic development (15%); management of budget items 1652 (catering), 2140-05 (materials and equipment), and 3040 (equipment); expenditure order processing and invoice control; budget forecasting and financial planning (10%).

• **Infrastructure and Ancillary Facility Support Services** (5%): Installation and maintenance of public water network fountains; coordination of household appliance delivery to parliamentary liaison offices.

06B50.name: 06B50
original_activities: Analyze and manage spaces in all workplaces. Coordinate major moves. Ensure the 'Service Desk' for MPs and staff for the allocation of offices.
Participate in Task Forces and/or working groups concerning major moves or other projects involving space management in all workplaces. Analyze and develop strategies regarding space management.
Ensure the management and coordination of the unit (writing notes, personnel management, coordination with other services, etc.).
Write informative notes on the unit's services and prepare reports (Annual Work Program, EMAS, etc.).
06B50.refined_description: Unit 06B50 functions as a specialized operational unit within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, holding primary responsibility for comprehensive workplace space management and strategic facilities coordination across all parliamentary locations. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate structures while maintaining coordinated relationships with sibling units (06B10, 06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B5010, and related units), Unit 06B50 serves as a critical support function directly supporting the institutional needs of Parliament's membership and staff while contributing to strategic facility planning objectives. The unit's workload allocation encompasses four functionally distinct but operationally integrated activity areas that collectively constitute 100% of documented responsibilities. Unit 06B50 directly serves the institution through continuous analysis and active management of physical spaces across all European Parliament workplaces, coordination of major office relocations and relocation initiatives, and operational responsibility for the Service Desk function that processes and manages office allocation requests for Members of Parliament and parliamentary staff. The unit simultaneously engages in strategic and analytical functions through participation in Task Forces and cross-functional working groups addressing major moves and complex space management projects, while developing and analyzing comprehensive strategies to optimize space utilization across all parliamentary facilities. Additionally, the unit maintains internal organizational effectiveness through personnel management, inter-departmental coordination with other European Parliament services, and preparation of essential administrative documentation, while fulfilling institutional reporting obligations through production of technical notes on unit services and comprehensive reports including the Annual Work Program and EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) environmental compliance documentation.
06B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Workplace Space Operations & Service Delivery (40%)**: Continuous analysis and active management of physical spaces across all European Parliament workplaces; coordination of major office relocations and relocation initiatives; operation and oversight of the Service Desk function processing office allocation requests for Members of Parliament and parliamentary staff

• **Strategic Planning & Project Participation (30%)**: Participation in Task Forces and cross-functional working groups addressing major moves and complex space management projects; development and analysis of comprehensive strategies to optimize space utilization across all parliamentary facilities; analysis of existing space management conditions to inform strategic approaches

• **Unit Administration & Inter-Service Coordination (20%)**: Internal unit management including personnel administration and management; preparation of operational notes and administrative documentation; inter-departmental coordination with other European Parliament services to ensure operational effectiveness and seamless continuity

• **Institutional Reporting & Documentation (10%)**: Preparation of informative technical notes describing unit services and operational capabilities; production of institutional reports including the Annual Work Program and EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) environmental compliance documentation

06B5010.name: 06B5010
original_activities: Provide the 'Service Desk' for MPs and staff at the three workplaces for the overall management of spaces and office allocation, the organization and coordination of developments (new projects, renovation work) and moves with all the services involved.

Carry out the tasks of the 'Inventoried Assets Manager' for DG INLO as well as for DG ECTI/CASP/IUST/BUDG and DSGR-CCLA at the three workplaces: allocation of offices, organization and coordination of moves, furniture.
Update the 'Nameplates' at the three workplaces.
Coordinate the management of the unit's IT tools. Actively contribute to improving the functioning of the IT tools (applications) necessary for the proper functioning of the service.
06B5010.refined_description: Unit 06B5010 functions as the centralized Service Desk and Space Management coordination entity within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as a critical operational support service across all three parliamentary workplaces (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg). Operating as a leaf-node unit with direct accountability to its parent direction while maintaining extensive collaboration with sibling units (06B10, 06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B50, and others), the unit delivers integrated facilities and asset management services that support the full lifecycle of Member and staff workspace requirements. The unit's operational framework is structured around four primary functional areas: a Service Desk function (65% of workload) that manages comprehensive space and office allocation, coordinates facility development projects and renovation initiatives, and orchestrates multi-service coordination of relocations across all three workplaces; formal Inventoried Assets Manager responsibilities (20% of workload) executed for DG INLO and four additional Directorates-General (DG ECTI, CASP, IUST, BUDG) and DSGR-CCLA, encompassing office allocation administration, relocation coordination, and furniture asset management logistics; maintenance of nameplate systems and wayfinding identification infrastructure (10% of workload) at all three parliamentary locations; and coordination of IT tools and applications management (5% of workload) to ensure continuous system optimization and service delivery functionality. Through this integrated service delivery model, Unit 06B5010 ensures seamless workspace management integration across the institutional framework while maintaining accurate identification and administrative support infrastructure throughout the European Parliament's distributed operational footprint.
06B5010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Integrated Service Desk & Space Management** (65%): Centralized Service Desk provision for Members of Parliament and staff across three workplaces; comprehensive management of space and office allocation; organization and coordination of facility development projects, new construction initiatives, and renovation work; multi-service coordination of workplace relocations and moves

• **Inventoried Assets Management** (20%): Execution of formal Assets Manager responsibilities for DG INLO and associated Directorates-General (DG ECTI, CASP, IUST, BUDG, DSGR-CCLA); office allocation administration and coordination; organizational relocation coordination; furniture asset management and logistics across all three parliamentary workplaces

• **Workplace Identification Infrastructure** (10%): Maintenance, updating, and management of nameplate systems and wayfinding signage infrastructure at all three parliamentary locations

• **IT Systems Management & Optimization** (5%): Coordination of unit-specific IT tools and applications management; active contribution to continuous improvement of systems and applications essential to service delivery operations

06B5020.name: 06B5020
original_activities: Manage the transport of equipment via shuttles, heavy goods vehicles and vans for the plenary session in Strasbourg (canteens, audiovisual equipment, boxes, furniture, exhibition equipment, miscellaneous equipment), for external missions during meetings of political groups (canteens, documents, mobile interpretation booths, computer equipment, miscellaneous equipment), inter-site BRUL/LUX/STR moves (mail, canteens, pallet documents, sensitive documents) and any other event.
Coordinate and execute different moves (MEPs, political groups and EP staff) in the three workplaces.
Coordinate the management of calls, the TRT application, emails from the functional mailbox, daily planning, material orders (boxes, rolls, FOBU), etc.

Carry out the delivery and installation/removal of furniture in Luxembourg and Strasbourg. Carry out the installation/removal of various equipment when organizing events (e.g. podium, chairs in the hemicycle).

Manage docks and lifting and handling equipment (Clark, PAL BAC pallet truck, platforms, etc.). Monitor the service vehicle fleet (heavy goods vehicles, vans and electric vans). Manage canteens (database, distribution, recovery, labeling, cleaning). Develop BXL/STR moving schedules (weekly, monthly, annual) and during plenary session weeks in Strasbourg.
Coordinate the inter-building vehicle shuttle service (Brussels peripheral buildings, letters and parcels, meeting canteens, documents), missions in the three workplaces, and external missions.
Coordinate the delivery of packages, mail, pallets, canteens, or equipment for meetings, conferences, events such as JPO, EYE and between interconnected buildings in Brussels.
Transport and deliver furniture or other decommissioned equipment to Emmaüs and other charitable organizations.
06B5020.refined_description: Unit 06B5020 operates as a specialized logistics and transport coordination unit within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, providing essential operational support across the institution's three primary workplaces (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg). The unit's core mission encompasses comprehensive management of physical asset transportation, equipment logistics, and mobility services that enable the Parliament's legislative and administrative functions across geographically dispersed locations. The unit executes this mandate through eight integrated operational activities that collectively ensure seamless logistical support for parliamentary operations while maintaining accountability to parent unit 06B within the organizational hierarchy. These activities span specialized equipment transportation for plenary sessions, personnel and institutional relocations, administrative coordination, fleet and facility management, furniture delivery and event support, inter-building shuttle services, specialized delivery coordination for institutional events, and asset stewardship through charitable redistribution programs. The unit's operations are synchronized with the Parliament's plenary session cycles and institutional calendars, requiring coordination across multiple organizational stakeholders including Members of the European Parliament, political groups, institutional departments, and external service providers. Through these integrated functions, Unit 06B5020 maintains critical operational infrastructure and ensures continuous logistical support for the Parliament's legislative proceedings and institutional operations across all three workplaces.
06B5020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Transport & Equipment Logistics** (30%): Management of specialized equipment transportation via dedicated shuttle services, heavy goods vehicles, and vans for Strasbourg plenary sessions, encompassing canteens, audiovisual apparatus, exhibition materials, boxes, and miscellaneous equipment; external mission support during political group meetings with canteen and mobile interpretation booth transportation; and inter-site relocation movements between Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg facilities involving mail, sensitive documents, and pallet-based materials.

• **Personnel & Institutional Relocation Services** (20%): Comprehensive coordination and execution of relocation operations for Members of the European Parliament, political groups, and European Parliament staff across Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg workplaces.

• **Fleet & Operational Infrastructure Management** (10%): Dock and lifting/handling equipment management (Clark vehicles, pallet trucks, platforms); service vehicle fleet monitoring (heavy goods vehicles, vans, electric vans); canteen database administration including distribution, recovery, and cleaning operations; and development of comprehensive moving schedules (weekly, monthly, annual cycles) synchronized with Strasbourg plenary session weeks and institutional calendars.

• **Administrative Coordination & Material Management** (15%): Call coordination, TRT application administration, functional mailbox management, daily operational planning and scheduling, and procurement of operational materials (boxes, rolls, FOBU supplies, consumables).

• **Furniture Delivery, Installation & Event Support** (10%): Delivery, installation, and removal of furniture in Luxembourg and Strasbourg venues; hemicycle configuration and specialized equipment setup; equipment setup and removal for institutional events and plenary sessions.

• **Inter-Building Shuttle & Specialized Delivery Services** (10%): Inter-building Brussels shuttle service coordination for peripheral facilities serving mail, parcels, documents, and correspondence distribution across all institutional locations (5%); and package, mail, pallet, canteen, and equipment delivery coordination for institutional meetings, conferences, and high-profile events including Joint Parliamentary Occasions, Open Door Day, and European Youth Events across interconnected Brussels buildings and other venues (5%).

• **Asset Stewardship & Sustainability** (5%): Coordination of decommissioned furniture and equipment transportation and delivery to charitable organizations including Emmaüs, supporting institutional sustainability and social responsibility objectives.

06B60.name: 06B60
original_activities: Ensure the transport of equipment by shuttles, heavy goods vehicles and vans for sessions in Strasbourg, external political meetings, moves on and off three sites and other events, as well as distribution of inter-site mail.
Manage the unloading docks, lifting and handling equipment on the three sites.
Manage the vehicle fleet and canteens as well as coordinate the inter-building mail shuttle service.
Prepare and manage requests, via the TRT application, email, telephone. Develop the moving schedule as well as missions on the three work sites and external travel.

Carry out the delivery and installation of furniture and various equipment during the organization of events in the three sites. Coordinate the delivery of packages, mail, pallets, canteens and equipment for meetings, events (JPO, EYE) and between buildings in Brussels.

Ensure management of the unit. Provide occasional assistance to other units. Provide assistance to the Plenary Session Service (General Directorate of the Presidency) and the forums (General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation) in Strasbourg. Check floors and report anomalies/problems to the appropriate units.
Transport and deliver furniture or any other decommissioned equipment to Emmaüs and other charitable organizations.
06B60.refined_description:
**

Unit 06B60 is a specialized operational logistics and transport management unit within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, functioning as the central infrastructure support hub for the institution's multi-site operations across Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 06B60 collaborates with sibling units (06B10, 06B30, 06B3010, 06B40, 06B50, and others) under the strategic direction of Unit 06B while providing specialized support to the Plenary Session Service under the General Directorate of the Presidency and to logistics forums under the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation in Strasbourg. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the management of physical asset mobility, equipment handling, fleet operations, and logistical coordination that directly enable parliamentary operations across all three principal work locations. Through integrated operational activities spanning equipment transport and mobility infrastructure (45% of workload), material handling and fleet management (30% of workload), logistical request processing and movement coordination (15% of workload), event support and specialized equipment distribution (4% of workload), and unit management with institutional support services including facility monitoring and sustainable asset disposal (6% of workload), Unit 06B60 ensures seamless logistical support for parliamentary sessions, institutional events, inter-site relocations, and maintains institutional asset management standards. The unit's operational effectiveness depends on coordinated management of unloading docks and material handling equipment infrastructure, vehicle fleet and canteen operations, inter-building mail shuttle services, the TRT application system for transport request processing, and collaborative relationships with external travel coordination and charitable organizations such as Emmaüs for equipment decommissioning.

---

**
06B60.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Equipment Transport and Mobility Operations** (45%): Ensure transport of specialized equipment via shuttles, heavy goods vehicles, and vans for parliamentary sessions in Strasbourg, external political meetings, multi-site relocations, and institutional events; manage inter-site mail distribution infrastructure and systems

• **Material Handling and Fleet Infrastructure Management** (30%): Manage unloading docks and material handling/lifting equipment infrastructure across three sites (15%); oversee vehicle fleet operations, canteen management and coordination, and inter-building mail shuttle services (15%)

• **Logistical Request Processing and Movement Coordination** (15%): Process and manage transport requests via TRT application system, email, and telephone communications; develop movement schedules and coordinate missions across three work sites and external travel arrangements

• **Event Support and Specialized Equipment Distribution** (4%): Deliver and install furniture and equipment for parliamentary institutional events including Open Door Days (JPO) and European Youth Event (EYE); coordinate distribution of packages, mail, pallets, canteen deliveries, and meeting equipment between Brussels buildings

• **Unit Management and Institutional Support Services** (6%): Provide unit management oversight and operational support to Plenary Session Service and logistics forums in Strasbourg; conduct facility monitoring and anomaly reporting to relevant departments (4%); manage sustainable disposal of decommissioned furniture and equipment through charitable organizations such as Emmaüs (2%)

06B80.name: 06B80
original_activities: Implement Parliament's mobility policy by encouraging staff to use alternative modes of transport for their home-work journeys (management of annual transport subscriptions and service bicycles, awareness campaigns in favor of sustainable mobility, etc.).
Manage Parliament's parking policy by ensuring optimal and equitable use of infrastructure, in particular through the management of parking spaces and compliance with internal parking rules.
Ensure the management of electric recharge cards.
Manage framework contracts with external contractors (mobility and parking management).
Ensure contacts with other institutions, federal, regional and municipal authorities within the framework of mobility in the 3 workplaces.
Other missions.
06B80.refined_description:
**

Unit 06B80 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 06B of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, holding primary responsibility for comprehensive mobility and transportation infrastructure management across the institution's three geographically distributed operational workplaces. The unit executes an integrated dual-mandate approach: implementing Parliament's sustainable mobility policy through active promotion of alternative transportation modes (40% allocation), and optimizing parking infrastructure management to ensure equitable resource allocation and regulatory compliance (40% allocation). Sustainable mobility implementation encompasses administration of annual transport subscriptions, management of institutional service bicycle fleets, and design and execution of targeted awareness campaigns promoting sustainable commuting practices among parliamentary staff. Parking infrastructure management includes systematic space allocation and distribution, enforcement of internal parking regulations and protocols, and continuous infrastructure utilization monitoring to ensure compliant and efficient use across multiple work locations. Supporting these primary functions, Unit 06B80 administers electric vehicle recharge card systems for institutional fleet and staff vehicles (10% allocation), negotiates and manages framework contracts with external mobility and parking service providers (5% allocation), and maintains institutional liaison relationships with other EU institutions, federal authorities, regional administrations, and municipal governments regarding mobility policy coordination and initiatives across all three workplace locations (3% allocation), with residual administrative and miscellaneous operational duties comprising 2% of workload. Through this coordinated, integrated approach, Unit 06B80 directly advances the European Parliament's institutional objectives of operational efficiency, environmental sustainability, and equitable resource management.

---

**
06B80.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Sustainable Mobility Policy Implementation** (40%): Active promotion of alternative transportation modes through administration of annual transport subscriptions, management of institutional service bicycle fleets, and design/execution of targeted awareness campaigns supporting sustainable commuting practices among parliamentary staff

• **Parking Infrastructure Management** (40%): Systematic optimization and equitable allocation of parking spaces, enforcement of internal parking regulations and protocols, and continuous infrastructure utilization monitoring across Parliament's three operational workplaces

• **Electric Vehicle and Alternative Fuel Infrastructure Support** (10%): Administration and management of electric vehicle recharge card systems for institutional fleet vehicles and staff vehicles to support low-emission transportation adoption

• **External Contractor Relations and Framework Agreement Management** (5%): Negotiation, administration, and ongoing oversight of framework contracts with external service providers specializing in mobility and parking management operations

• **Institutional Coordination and Multi-Level Stakeholder Liaison** (3%): Maintenance of collaborative relationships with other EU institutions, federal authorities, regional administrations, and municipal governments regarding mobility policy coordination and initiatives across the Parliament's three operational workplaces

• **Administrative and Miscellaneous Functions** (2%): Residual operational duties, ad hoc assignments, and ancillary administrative missions supporting unit operations and institutional mobility objectives

06C.name: 06C
original_activities: Oversee, coordinate and supervise the directorate's units and departments, ensuring the continuity of operations and the teams' resilience and cohesion.
Coordinate the directorate-general's strategy.
Equip the units within the directorate-general with the necessary skills by means of recruitment and training, and ensuring the best possible use is made of them.
Ensure the sound management of financial resources, by guaranteeing proper execution of the budget and advising the teams in charge of lease and purchase projects.
Provide IT resources and monitor their use.

Develop an effective communication strategy and coordinate the directorate-general's communication, including continuous updating of the directorate-general's intranet. Coordinate public procurement in order to monitor and harmonise contract award procedure.
Mitigate risks and ensure regular and effective follow-up to the recommendations issued by the Secretary-General, the Internal Auditor and the European Court of Auditors.
Provide the European Parliament with sufficient insurance cover for its proper functioning while looking into the possibility of taking out self-insurance.
06C.refined_description: Unit 06C functions as the Direction-level strategic management and administrative coordination hub within the General Management Directorate (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, exercising comprehensive oversight and coordination across the directorate-general's operational and strategic framework. The unit serves as the central coordinating mechanism for eight interconnected functional domains that collectively represent 100% of its operational workload, positioning it as essential to institutional governance and administrative excellence. Unit 06C maintains direct supervisory oversight and operational coordination of seven child units (06C10, 06C20, 06C25, 06C30, 06C3020, 06C40, and three additional specialized units), ensuring continuous organizational functionality, resilience, and team cohesion while maintaining strategic alignment. Simultaneously, the unit coordinates the directorate-general's overarching strategy across multiple sibling units (06-10, 06-1010, 06-20, 06-30, 06-40, and four additional directional counterparts), serving as the strategic alignment mechanism that ensures institutional priorities are coherently integrated across all organizational levels. Unit 06C exercises comprehensive stewardship across human resources, financial management, digital infrastructure, communications, procurement governance, compliance, risk mitigation, and institutional insurance—collectively functioning as the primary administrative and strategic coordination mechanism that ensures institutional effectiveness, organizational coherence, and sustained operational continuity across the directorate-general's entire functional portfolio.
06C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Operational Management & Strategic Coordination** (40%): Oversee, coordinate, and supervise all directorate units and departments to ensure operational continuity, team resilience, organizational cohesion, and sustained functionality (20%); coordinate the directorate-general's overarching strategy to align institutional priorities and ensure strategic consistency across all organizational levels and sibling units (20%)

• **Human Capital Development & Organizational Capacity Building** (15%): Equip directorate-general units with necessary competencies through strategic recruitment initiatives and comprehensive training programmes; ensure optimal allocation and utilization of human resources to maximize institutional effectiveness and professional development across the organization

• **Financial Management & Resource Stewardship** (12%): Ensure sound management and proper execution of financial resources and budgets; provide advisory guidance to teams managing lease and capital procurement projects to maintain fiscal responsibility, accountability, and operational efficiency

• **Digital Infrastructure, Communications & Procurement Coordination** (20%): Provide IT resources and monitor their institutional utilization to maintain technological standards and operational effectiveness (10%); develop and implement effective communication strategy including directorate-general communications coordination, continuous intranet updates and management; oversee public procurement coordination to monitor and harmonize contract award procedures in accordance with institutional standards (10%)

• **Compliance, Risk Management & Institutional Protection** (13%): Mitigate organizational risks and ensure regular, effective follow-up to recommendations from the Secretary-General, Internal Auditor, and European Court of Auditors through systematic tracking and implementation mechanisms (7%); secure comprehensive insurance coverage for Parliament's operational continuity while investigating and evaluating self-insurance feasibility options (6%)

06C10.name: 06C10
original_activities: Ensure human resources management, including: posting of vacant positions and organization of interviews, support of the general director during the rating campaign (eRapnot supervision), recruitment of ACs, temporary workers and interns. Manage the general management ringbook. Write competition notices. Monitor related budget envelopes (contractual workers and interns). Provide career advice for senior management staff.

Define training needs in cooperation with unit heads, establish and execute the annual training plan for general management staff and manage the budget for training and related missions. Develop and organize workshops as part of the DG INLO academy. Create learning paths and populate the digital library.

Manage working time aspects, including monitoring fixed allowances for travel, overtime and shift work. Manage data corresponding to overtime, ensure management and monitoring of the overtime budget and approve payment requests by delegation from the General Director.
Manage, in compliance with financial objectives, mission organization charts, related mission orders and the related budget envelope.
Coordinate the network within the general directorate for document management (management table and classification plan) and the GIDOC (classification plan, IT system, training, management table).
Implement human resources development projects concerning the recruitment and integration of staff, skills development as well as change management.

Contribute to cross-functional reporting exercises such as risk management, the annual activity report, the annual work program, minimum internal control standards, metrics, the various advisory committees, the inter-general management Steering Group on human resources or any other body.
06C10.refined_description: Unit 06C10 functions as the Human Resources and Organizational Management operational unit within Direction 06C of the General Management Directorate (Unit 06), positioned at the second hierarchical level and operating as a leaf node within the organizational structure. The unit serves as a critical support function for senior management personnel and institutional human resources operations, with its primary mission encompassing comprehensive human resources administration, staff development, working time management, and organizational infrastructure support that directly enable the strategic objectives of general management across the European Parliament. Operating in coordination with sibling units (06C20, 06C25, 06C30, 06C3020, 06C40, and others), Unit 06C10 delivers integrated human resources services including recruitment operations, training and development programs, overtime and compensation administration, mission-related organizational support, document management governance, and strategic human resources initiatives. The unit maintains delegated approval authority from the General Director for specific operational functions and participates in cross-functional institutional reporting, risk management frameworks, and inter-directorate steering groups that inform organizational policy and governance. Through its comprehensive service delivery model, Unit 06C10 ensures cohesive general management operations and supports the professional development and administrative needs of staff across the institution.
06C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Administration & Recruitment** (35%): Posting of vacant positions and organization of recruitment interviews; support of the General Director during annual rating campaigns with eRapnot system supervision; recruitment and onboarding of Administrative Agents/Contractors (ACs), temporary workers, and interns; management of the general management ringbook; drafting of competition notices; monitoring of contractual worker and intern budget envelopes; provision of career advisory services to senior management staff

• **Staff Development & Training** (30%): Definition of training needs in cooperation with unit heads; establishment and execution of annual training plans specifically for general management staff; management of training-related budgets and mission-related expenses; development and organization of specialized workshops as part of the DG INLO academy initiative; creation of customized learning paths; population and maintenance of the institutional digital library infrastructure

• **Working Time & Compensation Management** (15%): Monitoring of fixed allowances for travel, overtime, and shift work; data management and budget monitoring for overtime records and compensation; delegation-based approval authority for overtime payment requests from the General Director

• **Mission Administration & Document Management** (10%): Management of mission-related organizational charts, mission orders, and associated budget envelopes in compliance with financial objectives (5%); coordination of document management governance protocols across the general directorate, including management tables, classification plans, and GIDOC system administration (5%)

• **Strategic Human Resources Development & Institutional Governance** (10%): Implementation of human resources development initiatives focused on staff recruitment, integration, skills enhancement, and organizational change management (5%); contribution to cross-functional institutional reporting and governance processes including risk management frameworks, annual activity reports, annual work programs, minimum internal control standards, metrics development, advisory committee participation, and inter-directorate steering group engagement (5%)

06C20.name: 06C20
original_activities: Conduct and coordinate the annual assessment of the Minimum Internal Control principles within the Directorate-General. Act as Internal Control Coordinator.

Conduct and coordinate (as Risk Coordinator) the annual risk management cycle within the Directorate-General, in collaboration with the managing entities. Implement and monitor the risk management policy of the Directorate-General.
Carry out ex post controls.

Coordinate and act as the central contact point between the Directorate-General and the outside stakeholders in the field of internal and external audits (Internal Audit Service, Court of Auditors, Directorate-General for Finance, etc.).

Conduct and coordinate the update of the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics Business Continuity Plan, to ensure the continuity of Parliament's core activities at the level of the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics' mission, in collaboration with the managing entities. Act as Business Continuity Coordinator.

Represent the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics in different inter-Directorate-General working groups (e.g. Risk coordination, Business continuity, Internal control, Finances, etc.).
Conduct transversal actions in the field of competence of the Unit: promote awareness, drive communication actions, and ensure advice.
06C20.refined_description:
Unit 06C20 functions as the integrated Internal Control, Risk Management, and Business Continuity Office within the General Management Directorate of the European Parliament, operating at the operational unit level (Level 2) under the strategic direction of Unit 06C. The unit bears primary responsibility for establishing, implementing, and continuously monitoring robust governance frameworks and compliance systems across the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics through execution of four interconnected core governance pillars. In its designated capacity as Internal Control Coordinator, Risk Coordinator, and Business Continuity Coordinator, Unit 06C20 conducts and coordinates the annual assessment of Minimum Internal Control principles, manages the comprehensive annual risk management cycle in active collaboration with managing entities, executes ex post controls to verify compliance and operational effectiveness of implemented measures, and maintains the Directorate-General's Business Continuity Plan to ensure organizational resilience and continuity of Parliament's core activities. The unit simultaneously serves as the central liaison point and designated contact point between the Directorate-General and external audit stakeholders including the Internal Audit Service, Court of Auditors, and Directorate-General for Finance, thereby facilitating transparent audit communication, institutional accountability, and coordinated internal and external audit engagement. Additionally, Unit 06C20 represents the Directorate-General across inter-Directorate-General working groups addressing risk coordination, business continuity, internal control, and financial governance, while conducting transversal awareness-building, communication initiatives, and advisory functions within its domain of competence, thereby amplifying the institutional impact of governance frameworks across the European Parliament's infrastructure and logistics operations.

---

##
06C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Control Framework Assessment and Coordination (20%)**: Conduct and coordinate annual assessment of Minimum Internal Control principles as Internal Control Coordinator; establish and monitor institutional compliance standards and frameworks across the Directorate-General

• **Risk Management Governance and Implementation (20%)**: Execute and coordinate the comprehensive annual risk management cycle as Risk Coordinator in active collaboration with managing entities; implement and monitor the Directorate-General's risk management policy to ensure institutional resilience and organizational compliance with governance policies

• **Control Verification and Operational Effectiveness Monitoring (20%)**: Carry out comprehensive ex post controls to verify compliance and effectiveness of implemented governance measures, control frameworks, and organizational activities across the Directorate-General

• **Business Continuity Planning and Institutional Resilience (15%)**: Conduct and coordinate updates to the Directorate-General's Business Continuity Plan as Business Continuity Coordinator; collaborate with managing entities to ensure organizational resilience and continuity of Parliament's core operations and critical infrastructure functions

• **Audit Liaison and External Stakeholder Coordination (15%)**: Serve as central contact point and designated liaison between the Directorate-General and external audit stakeholders including the Internal Audit Service, Court of Auditors, and Directorate-General for Finance; coordinate internal and external audit engagement, facilitate transparent audit communication, and support institutional accountability processes

• **Inter-Institutional Representation and Knowledge Dissemination (10%)**: Represent the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics in inter-Directorate-General working groups addressing risk coordination, business continuity, internal control, and financial governance (5%); promote awareness, drive communication actions, and provide advisory services within the unit's field of competence (5%)

06C25.name: 06C25
original_activities: Prepare the future budget.
Monitor the execution of the current budget and adjust the budget allocation.
Share financial expertise with budget coordinators from directorates and central units.
Facilitate informed decision-making on construction files in accordance with Article 266 of the Financial Regulation.
Report on budget execution and prepare discharge.
Coordinate the procurement procedure to guarantee financing of the SPAAK project.
Track the lifecycle of assets.
Other missions.
06C25.refined_description: Unit 06C25 is a specialized financial management and budgetary planning unit operating at the second hierarchical level within the General Management Directorate (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, positioned as a leaf node under the strategic coordination direction of Unit 06C. The unit functions as a central node in the Parliament's financial governance architecture, bearing primary responsibility for comprehensive budget stewardship across the institution through eight interconnected operational activities that collectively consume 100% of documented workload allocation. Unit 06C25's core mission encompasses the complete budget lifecycle: prospective budget preparation through comprehensive financial analysis and forward-looking institutional resource planning (20% of workload), concurrent monitoring and dynamic adjustment of budget execution against current allocations (20% of workload), and retrospective reporting on budget execution coupled with discharge preparation activities to ensure institutional financial accountability and audit compliance (10% of workload). Beyond internal budgetary stewardship, the unit serves a critical dual advisory function by disseminating specialized financial expertise to budget coordinators distributed across multiple directorates and central administrative units (15% of workload) while simultaneously facilitating evidence-based institutional decision-making regarding construction file management in strict compliance with Article 266 of the Financial Regulation (15% of workload). The unit additionally assumes responsibility for coordinating procurement procedures to ensure financing continuity for the SPAAK project (10% of workload) and maintaining systematic oversight of the complete lifecycle management of institutional assets from acquisition through disposal (5% of workload). The remaining 5% of unit capacity is allocated to supplementary missions as assigned by parent directorate Unit 06C, and the unit collaborates with sibling units including 06C10, 06C20, 06C30, 06C3020, and 06C40 to support the broader governance and administrative coordination functions of its parent Direction. Collectively, these activities ensure budgetary integrity, regulatory compliance, strategic resource allocation, and informed institutional decision-making across the Parliament's organizational structure.
06C25.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Budget Planning & Lifecycle Management** (50%): Prepare future institutional budgets through comprehensive financial analysis and forward-looking resource planning (20%); monitor current budget execution and implement dynamic budget allocation adjustments (20%); report comprehensively on budget execution outcomes and prepare discharge documentation to ensure financial accountability and audit compliance (10%)

• **Financial Advisory & Institutional Governance Support** (30%): Provide specialized financial expertise and technical advisory support to budget coordinators across all directorates and central administrative units to enhance organizational financial capacity (15%); facilitate informed institutional decision-making on construction files in strict compliance with Article 266 of the Financial Regulation (15%)

• **Capital Projects & Procurement Coordination** (10%): Coordinate procurement procedures to guarantee financing continuity for the SPAAK project

• **Asset Management & Lifecycle Oversight** (5%): Track and manage the complete lifecycle of institutional assets from acquisition through disposal

• **Additional Assigned Missions** (5%): Execute other missions as designated by parent directorate Unit 06C in collaboration with sibling units (06C10, 06C20, 06C30, 06C3020, 06C40, and additional peer units)

06C30.name: 06C30
original_activities: Ensure ex-ante control of general management budgetary transactions.
Ensure ex-ante control of general management's tender files.
Coordinate tender procedures within the general management, ensure formal and procedural uniformity of tender files, ensure monitoring of ongoing procedures / reporting.
Manage WebContracts and PMP tools and carry out centralized coding and monitoring of market programming.
Ensure the archiving of (original) documents of tender procedures and contracts, as well as bank guarantees.
Ensure the archiving of all general management budget documents.
Other missions.
06C30.refined_description:
Unit 06C30 functions as the Financial Control and Procurement Management Unit within the General Management Directorate (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, operating at Hierarchy Level 2 as a specialized leaf node under the strategic direction of parent Unit 06C. The unit bears primary institutional responsibility for ensuring comprehensive financial governance and procurement oversight through a rigorous, multi-layered operational framework that establishes preventive controls, procedural coordination, and systematic documentation management across the Parliament's budgetary and procurement processes. The unit's dominant operational focus (60% combined allocation) concentrates on ex-ante control mechanisms: validating general management budgetary transactions prior to financial commitment (35% of workload) and verifying tender file documentation integrity and regulatory conformity (25% of workload), thereby establishing institutional safeguards against financial and procurement irregularities. Complementing these control functions, the unit dedicates 17% of its operational capacity to coordinating tender procedures across general management operations, ensuring formal and procedural uniformity of all tender files while maintaining continuous monitoring and comprehensive reporting of ongoing procurement procedures to guarantee procedural compliance standards. The unit manages critical procurement infrastructure through centralized administration of WebContracts and PMP (Project Management Portal) tools (13% allocation), executing centralized coding protocols and market programming monitoring functions that support institutional procurement planning, execution, and strategic market analysis. The unit maintains essential archival and records management responsibilities, systematically preserving original tender procedure documentation, contracts, and bank guarantee files (7% allocation) while archiving general management budget documents (2% allocation) to ensure institutional record-keeping compliance. The remaining 1% of capacity addresses supplementary operational missions as required, while the unit coordinates functionally with sibling units (06C10, 06C20, 06C25, 06C3020, 06C40) to maintain coherent general management operations.

##
06C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Ex-Ante Financial and Procurement Controls** (60%): Executing preventive oversight mechanisms through ex-ante control of general management budgetary transactions (35%) to validate financial commitments prior to authorization, and ex-ante control of general management tender files (25%) to verify procurement documentation integrity, regulatory conformity, and institutional compliance before institutional commitment

• **Tender Procedure Coordination and Procedural Management** (17%): Coordinating tender procedures across general management operations, establishing formal and procedural uniformity standards for tender files, and maintaining continuous monitoring with comprehensive reporting of ongoing procurement procedures to guarantee institutional procedural compliance standards

• **Procurement Systems Management and Market Programming Infrastructure** (13%): Administering and maintaining WebContracts and PMP (Project Management Portal) tools, executing centralized coding protocols, and conducting centralized market programming monitoring functions to support institutional procurement planning, execution, and strategic market analysis

• **Institutional Records Management and Documentation Archiving** (9%): Systematically preserving and archiving original tender procedure documents, contract files, and bank guarantee documentation (7%), and maintaining comprehensive archival of general management budget documents (2%) to ensure institutional record-keeping and regulatory compliance requirements

• **Supplementary Organizational Functions** (1%): Addressing ad-hoc operational missions and organizational support requirements as directed by parent management structures

06C3020.name: 06C3020
original_activities: Ensure ex-ante control of General Management budgetary transactions.
Ensure ex-ante control of General Management’s tender files.
Ensure the archiving of all budgetary documents of the General Management.
Other missions.
06C3020.refined_description:
Unit 06C3020 is a specialized ex-ante control and compliance verification unit operating at Hierarchy Level 2 within the General Management Directorate of the European Parliament, subordinate to Direction 06C. The unit's core institutional mission centers on the systematic pre-approval review, validation, and authorization of General Management's financial transactions and procurement processes, ensuring all activities conform to European Parliament financial regulations, budgetary governance standards, and procurement compliance requirements before execution. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate organizational structures, Unit 06C3020 allocates 60% of its operational workload to ex-ante control of General Management budgetary transactions—providing critical pre-authorization review and compliance verification of all financial commitments and expenditures to ensure regulatory adherence and institutional accountability. The unit dedicates 38% of its capacity to ex-ante control of General Management's tender files, delivering pre-award scrutiny of procurement processes, competition fairness verification, contractual compliance mechanisms, and tender documentation oversight. Supporting these primary control functions, the unit maintains 1% of resources for archiving and systematic organization of all General Management budgetary documentation, thereby preserving institutional records, audit trails, and historical accountability essential for transparency and regulatory compliance. An additional 1% of workload is reserved for other missions and ad-hoc institutional assignments as designated by Direction 06C or senior management. Unit 06C3020 coordinates horizontally with sibling units (06C10, 06C20, 06C25, 06C30, 06C40, and three additional units) within Direction 06C to provide comprehensive financial governance and operational oversight infrastructure across General Management operations.

##
06C3020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Ex-Ante Financial Transaction Control** (60%): Ensure ex-ante control of General Management budgetary transactions through systematic pre-authorization review, compliance verification, and validation of all financial commitments and expenditures against European Parliament financial regulations and governance standards

• **Ex-Ante Procurement and Tender File Control** (38%): Ensure ex-ante control of General Management's tender files through pre-award scrutiny of procurement processes, competition fairness verification, contractual compliance review, and tender documentation oversight

• **Documentary Archiving and Records Management** (1%): Ensure the archiving and systematic organization of all General Management budgetary documentation, maintaining institutional records integrity, audit trails, and historical accountability requirements for transparency and regulatory compliance

• **Additional Institutional Missions and Support** (1%): Execute other missions and ad-hoc institutional assignments as designated by Direction 06C or senior management structures

06C40.name: 06C40
original_activities: Provide End User Support: Provide assistance, resolve technical issues encountered by users of IT systems, proactively communicate issues and solutions, troubleshoot hardware and software issues, offer advice, and ensure a positive user experience.
Manage applications in use: lifecycle management of custom or specialized software applications in use; scalable maintenance, training and support, testing to ensure functionality and security.

Manage Infrastructure, IT Assets and Access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management, ensuring the availability, reliability and security of IT resources.

Manage projects: planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality; project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management and communication with parties.

Supervise and coordinate business analysis: understand business needs and objectives, translate them into IT solutions; collection and analysis of needs, facilitation of communication between stakeholders and recommendation of effective technological solutions.

Manage IT and information security: approach and measures to protect the organization's IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities; designing and implementing secure IT architectures, carrying out risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities, carrying out security testing and scanning, raising awareness of cybersecurity best practices.
06C40.refined_description: Unit 06C40 operates as a comprehensive IT service delivery and infrastructure management division functioning within Direction 06C of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as a critical operational node responsible for integrated technical support, application lifecycle management, and IT security governance across the institution's digital ecosystem. The unit executes six interconnected operational domains that collectively ensure continuity, reliability, and security of the Parliament's information technology operations: direct end-user support and technical problem resolution (35%); lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications including maintenance, training, and security testing (20%); strategic planning, provisioning, monitoring, and access control administration of IT infrastructure and digital assets (15%); planning, execution, supervision, and resource allocation of technology initiatives with scope, timeline, budget, and risk management oversight (10%); business analysis supervision that translates organizational requirements into aligned IT solutions through needs assessment and stakeholder facilitation (10%); and comprehensive IT and information security governance encompassing secure architecture design, vulnerability identification through risk assessments, security testing and scanning protocols, and cybersecurity awareness promotion (10%). These six functional areas position Unit 06C40 as both a direct service delivery entity providing technical assistance and user experience optimization, and a governance entity ensuring institutional IT resource availability, operational reliability, and cybersecurity integrity through proactive infrastructure stewardship, systematic risk management, and security-centric architecture implementation.
06C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **End-User Support & Technical Service Delivery** (35%): Direct provision of comprehensive end-user assistance, technical issue resolution, proactive problem communication, hardware and software troubleshooting, user guidance, and positive user experience optimization across the Parliament's IT ecosystem

• **Application & Infrastructure Lifecycle Management** (35%): Complete lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications encompassing scalable maintenance protocols, comprehensive user training, technical support delivery, and rigorous functionality and security testing (20%); strategic planning, provisioning, continuous monitoring, and access control administration of IT infrastructure components including hardware, software, network systems, and user access controls to ensure resource availability, operational reliability, and cybersecurity integrity (15%)

• **IT Project Management & Business Analysis Coordination** (20%): Planning, execution, supervision, and oversight of technology initiatives encompassing scope, timeline, budget, resource allocation, risk mitigation, and multi-stakeholder communication frameworks (10%); business analysis supervision and coordination translating organizational business requirements and objectives into effective IT solutions through systematic needs assessment, stakeholder facilitation, and technology recommendation development (10%)

• **IT and Information Security Governance** (10%): Comprehensive cybersecurity approach encompassing threat and vulnerability protection measures, secure IT architecture design and implementation, vulnerability identification through systematic risk assessments, security testing and scanning protocols, and organizational cybersecurity awareness and best practices promotion to protect institutional systems, data, and information assets

06C50.name: 06C50
original_activities: Manage fire insurance for the European Parliament buildings, including claims management.
Manage the European Parliament's operating liability insurance, including the management of extensions and claims.
Provide advice on ad-hoc files in the insurance field.
Analyze the alternative options available, economically more favorable, in the field of insurance, for the buildings occupied by the European Parliament.
Manage insurance for the European Parliament's car fleet, including claims management.
Manage strict liability insurance for the European Parliament buildings in Belgium, including claims management.
Manage transport insurance for technical equipment, including claims management.
06C50.refined_description: Unit 06C50 operates as a specialized insurance management and risk mitigation function within the General Management Directorate (Unit 06), positioned at operational hierarchy level 2 under the strategic direction of Unit 06C. The unit holds primary responsibility for comprehensive insurance portfolio administration, claims management, and risk optimization across all European Parliament facilities, assets, and operational locations throughout multiple jurisdictions. Unit 06C50 executes this institutional mandate through seven integrated activity streams that collectively ensure complete risk coverage, regulatory compliance, and financial optimization of insurance expenditures. The unit's operational scope encompasses building-specific risk management (fire insurance and strict liability coverage for Belgian facilities), institutional liability administration (operating liability insurance with extension management), fleet and asset protection (motor vehicle insurance and transport insurance for technical equipment), and strategic portfolio optimization (economic analysis of alternative insurance solutions and specialized ad-hoc advisory services). All activities incorporate full lifecycle claims administration and resolution capabilities, enabling seamless risk mitigation from policy inception through claims settlement. These interconnected functions collectively support the broader administrative objectives of the General Management Directorate while maintaining operational continuity and institutional financial stewardship across the Parliament's complete operational footprint.
06C50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Building Risk Coverage** (35%): Fire insurance management for European Parliament buildings including comprehensive claims administration (30%), and strict liability insurance administration for Parliament facilities in Belgium with integrated claims handling (5%)

• **Operating Liability and Institutional Risk Management** (20%): Comprehensive administration of operating liability insurance portfolio encompassing extension management and full claims resolution across all Parliament operations and facilities

• **Strategic Insurance Optimization and Advisory Services** (30%): Economic analysis and evaluation of alternative insurance solutions to identify cost-advantageous options for Parliament-occupied facilities (15%), combined with provision of specialized ad-hoc advisory services addressing insurance-related institutional matters (15%)

• **Fleet and Transport Asset Protection** (15%): Management of insurance coverage for the European Parliament's automotive fleet with integrated claims administration (10%), and transport insurance management for technical equipment in transit with claims handling (5%)

06C60.name: 06C60
original_activities: Ensure that all relevant clients receive timely, relevant, precise and accurate information on the activities of the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, using all appropriate communication channels, with a focus on the needs and expectations of DG INLO clients. Monitor established key performance and results indicators to measure customer satisfaction and unit performance.

Update and develop the content of the Directorate-General's intranet on a regular basis based on current events and the needs of Members and staff of the European Parliament regarding the activities and projects of the Directorate-General. Maintain information specifically intended for DG INLO staff via the #staffonly menu for internal use.

Prepare communication projects written in the three working languages ​​(EN, FR, DE) in cooperation with the various entities of the Directorate-General and disseminate these same communications to Members and staff of the European Parliament via the most appropriate communication channels. Produce communication products, in English, French and German, on the buildings of the European Parliament and the services offered by DG INLO.

Develop and implement, in collaboration with the departments concerned, a communication strategy around the renovation of the SPAAK building in order to ensure transparent, proactive and reactive communication, during all phases of the renovation, to inform and raise awareness among internal and external audiences to promote a positive perception of the project.
Represent the general management during the organization of internal events such as open days, Europe day, inauguration of new buildings, mobility week, green week, waste week, etc.
06C60.refined_description:
**

Unit 06C60 functions as the Communications and Information Management Unit within the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics (DG INLO), operating at the second hierarchical level under Direction 06C within the General Management structure of the European Parliament. The unit serves as the central communications and stakeholder engagement hub responsible for ensuring transparent, timely, relevant, and accurate information dissemination to all relevant stakeholders—including Members, staff, and external clients—regarding DG INLO's activities, infrastructure projects, and service offerings through strategically selected communication channels. As a leaf node within the Direction 06C hierarchy, Unit 06C60 directly supports DG INLO's strategic objectives by maintaining continuous engagement with institutional audiences through tailored communications, monitoring comprehensive key performance and results indicators to assess customer satisfaction and operational effectiveness, and producing multilingual communication materials in English, French, and German that reflect the Directorate-General's infrastructure and service offerings. The unit's activities are fundamentally oriented toward enhancing organizational transparency, promoting positive stakeholder perception, and ensuring that all clients receive information aligned with their specific needs and expectations. Notably, the unit plays a critical role in managing complex, multi-phase communication strategies, exemplified by its responsibility for coordinating transparent, proactive, and reactive communications throughout the SPAAK building renovation project to foster informed decision-making and sustained organizational confidence. Additionally, the unit represents general management during the organization of internal institutional events, thereby reinforcing the Directorate-General's commitment to transparency and institutional engagement.

---

**
06C60.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **External Communications and Client Engagement** (40%): Ensure timely, relevant, precise, and accurate information dissemination to all DG INLO clients through appropriate communication channels; monitor comprehensive key performance indicators and results metrics to measure customer satisfaction and evaluate unit performance effectiveness across the Directorate-General's service delivery.

• **Internal Information Management and Digital Content Development** (25%): Regularly update and maintain the Directorate-General's intranet as a dynamic information resource responsive to current events and institutional needs of European Parliament Members and staff; manage staff-restricted content through dedicated internal access controls (#staffonly menu) for secure information repositories.

• **Multilingual Communications Production** (20%): Prepare and disseminate comprehensive communication projects in three working languages (English, French, and German) in cooperation with various DG INLO entities; produce specialized communication products addressing European Parliament building infrastructure and DG INLO service offerings for institutional audiences.

• **Strategic Project Communication and Stakeholder Engagement** (10%): Develop and implement comprehensive, phased communication strategies for major infrastructure initiatives, particularly the SPAAK building renovation project, in collaboration with relevant departments; ensure transparent, proactive, and reactive communication across all project phases to inform and raise awareness among internal and external audiences while promoting positive institutional perception.

• **Institutional Event Representation and Support** (5%): Represent general management during the organization of internal institutional events including open days, Europe Day celebrations, building inaugurations, mobility week, green week, and waste management awareness initiatives, thereby reinforcing organizational transparency and commitment to stakeholder engagement.

06C70.name: 06C70
original_activities: Conduct research on sustainability topics and study the feasibility of implementing innovations.
Disseminate knowledge on good practices and innovations in sustainability; organize meetings and exchange with experts in the field.
Set up the unit, structure its work, establish its working methods and carry out useful training.
Manage the administrative, contractual and financial aspects of the unit; prepare and carry out useful calls for tenders.
Maintain regular contact with the local environment.
06C70.refined_description: Unit 06C70 operates as a specialized sustainability research and innovation unit within the Directional framework of the European Parliament's General Management structure, positioned strategically under the coordination of parent Unit 06C. As an autonomous leaf-node unit, it functions without subordinate organizational structures while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units (06C10, 06C20, 06C25, 06C30, 06C3020, and others) to advance the institution's strategic sustainability objectives. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses five integrated operational dimensions: conducting rigorous research on sustainability topics and systematically evaluating the technical, operational, and strategic feasibility of implementing innovative solutions within European Parliament operations; serving as a knowledge dissemination hub that actively promotes evidence-based good practices and emerging sustainability innovations through the organization of specialized expert meetings, seminars, and structured knowledge exchange initiatives with both external and internal field specialists; establishing and maintaining robust organizational infrastructure by structuring operational workflows, formalizing working methodologies, defining work procedures, and delivering targeted professional training programs to enhance staff competencies and institutional effectiveness; exercising comprehensive administrative stewardship encompassing contractual management, financial oversight, and full responsibility for procurement operations including the preparation and execution of public calls for tenders in compliance with institutional regulations; and maintaining regular substantive engagement with the local institutional environment to ensure stakeholder coordination and alignment with broader European Parliament objectives. Through this integrated approach combining evidence-based research, knowledge mobilization, and institutional capacity-building, Unit 06C70 contributes strategically to the European Parliament's sustainability advancement agenda.
06C70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Research & Innovation Feasibility Analysis** (32%): Conduct comprehensive research on sustainability topics and systematically evaluate the technical, operational, and strategic feasibility of implementing innovative sustainability solutions within European Parliament operations
