• **Knowledge Dissemination & Expert Engagement** (32%): Actively disseminate evidence-based knowledge regarding established good practices and emerging sustainability innovations; organize specialized meetings, seminars, and structured knowledge exchange initiatives with external and internal sustainability experts and field specialists

• **Organizational Development & Institutional Capacity-Building** (17%): Establish unit infrastructure and operational frameworks; structure and formalize work processes and methodologies; deliver targeted professional training programs to enhance staff competencies and institutional effectiveness

• **Administrative, Contractual & Financial Management** (16%): Manage administrative procedures, contractual obligations, and financial resources; prepare and execute public calls for tenders and procurement processes in compliance with institutional procurement regulations

• **Stakeholder Relations & Local Institutional Engagement** (3%): Maintain regular substantive contact with the local institutional environment and relevant stakeholder networks to ensure coordination, awareness, and alignment with broader European Parliament objectives

06D.name: 06D
original_activities: Oversee, coordinate and motivate the directorate's units and services, ensuring that information is exchanged between the various services of the directorate and fostering inter-unit collaboration.

Oversee the technical and administrative management of Parliament's buildings in Brussels, with a particular focus on upgrading projects and the activities related to the SPAAK renovation project with connected works.
Help, as part of the management team, to draw up the directorate's strategic guidelines, and ensure that budgetary, strategic and operational decisions are applied effectively.
Manage the equipment and services departments necessary for the smooth operation of buildings in Brussels.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-directorate-general committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., and represent the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those fora.
Act as authorising officer by subdelegation.
06D.refined_description:
Unit 06D, the Directorate for Infrastructure and Building Management, functions as a first-level directional unit within the European Parliament's General Management Directorate (DG 06), operating under direct coordination with the Secretary General's office. The directorate exercises comprehensive strategic oversight and operational stewardship across Parliament's Brussels infrastructure portfolio through six interconnected functional activities that collectively position Unit 06D as the central coordinating hub for institutional facilities management. At its operational core, Unit 06D maintains dual responsibilities: overseeing inter-unit coordination and information exchange across its three subordinate units (06D10—Buildings Technical Management and Operations Unit; 06D20—Building Renovation and Compliance Management Unit; and 06D30—specialized technical units), while simultaneously managing the technical and administrative operations of Parliament's Brussels buildings with particular emphasis on capital infrastructure upgrade projects and the ongoing SPAAK renovation initiative including all associated architectural and engineering works. The directorate contributes substantively to organizational governance through participation in the management team responsible for developing and implementing directorate-wide strategic guidelines and ensuring effective application of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions in alignment with institutional priorities. Unit 06D supervises the equipment and services departments essential for optimal building operations and continuous facility functionality, while maintaining institutional connectivity through active participation in intra- and inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies where it serves as the directorate's authorized representative. The unit exercises subdelegated authorizing officer authority for financial transactions and administrative authorizations, thereby maintaining financial accountability and institutional compliance.

##
06D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Directorate Coordination and Strategic Leadership** (25%): Oversee, coordinate, and motivate subordinate units (06D10, 06D20, 06D30) and services; facilitate inter-unit information exchange and collaboration; ensure cohesive directorate operations and organizational alignment

• **Infrastructure and Building Management with Capital Projects** (25%): Direct technical and administrative management of Parliament's Brussels buildings; oversee infrastructure upgrading initiatives; manage the SPAAK renovation project and all associated connected architectural and engineering works; coordinate capital project delivery

• **Strategic Planning and Budgetary Oversight** (20%): Participate in directorate management team; develop and implement directorate-wide strategic guidelines; ensure effective application of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions in alignment with institutional priorities

• **Operational Facilities and Equipment Management** (15%): Supervise and manage equipment and services departments essential to maintaining Brussels building operations; ensure continuous functionality and service delivery for Parliament facilities

• **Institutional Representation and Governance Participation** (10%): Represent the directorate and directorate-general in intra- and inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies; serve as authorized participant in collaborative institutional forums

• **Financial Authority and Administrative Compliance** (5%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial transactions and administrative authorizations; maintain institutional financial accountability and regulatory compliance

06D10.name: 06D10
original_activities: Manage technical maintenance (electricity, fire detection, lighting, heating, air conditioning, water supply and sanitary installations, elevators, etc.); technical management as well as the structure and finishes (exterior and interior carpentry, facades, roofs, interior and exterior finishes), key management.
Clean, manage waste and maintain green spaces.

Manage environmental aspects linked to buildings (environmental permits, EMAS actions, BEACH, energy, air/water quality), audits and technical inspections of buildings in Brussels, regulatory inspections of technical installations and health and safety coordination.
Manage the Buildings Helpdesk and interface with clients, via the CLIQC team and buildings managers, for issues communicated via the Buildings Helpdesk; provide nighttime duty with the shift team.

Manage the administrative, financial and budgetary aspects linked to the management of buildings and services, including the management of concessions, rents and the carrying out of real estate prospecting.
Implement modern, customer-focused, integrated facility management; carry out proofs of concept and establish transparent monitoring and adequate reporting, including maintenance costs.
06D10.refined_description: Unit 06D10, the Buildings Technical Management and Operations Unit, operates as a second-level operational unit within Directorate 06D (Infrastructure and Building Management) of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, bearing primary responsibility for comprehensive technical stewardship and day-to-day operational management of the institution's building portfolio and facilities infrastructure across Brussels. The unit executes its mission through six interconnected operational pillars that collectively ensure operational continuity, regulatory compliance, cost efficiency, and enhanced service delivery. Technical maintenance and structural management (44% of workload) forms the cornerstone of operations, encompassing oversight of critical building systems including electrical installations, fire detection systems, lighting infrastructure, heating and air conditioning systems, water supply and sanitary installations, and elevator operations, alongside comprehensive management of structural and finishing elements such as exterior and interior carpentry, facades, roofing, and interior/exterior finishes, plus key management protocols. Environmental compliance and regulatory oversight (15% of workload) involves management of environmental permits, EMAS certification actions, BEACH compliance initiatives, energy management, air and water quality monitoring, coordination of audits and technical building inspections across Brussels facilities, regulatory inspections of technical installations, and health and safety coordination. Facilities and grounds maintenance operations (18% of workload) include cleaning services, waste management, and green space maintenance and upkeep. Customer service and emergency response operations (12% of workload) encompass Buildings Helpdesk management and client interface coordination through the CLIQC team and buildings managers, alongside provision of nighttime duty rotations with shift teams for emergency response. Administrative, financial, and strategic management activities (7% of workload) address budgetary stewardship, concession and rental agreement administration, and real estate prospecting. Modernization and performance optimization initiatives (4% of workload) implement customer-focused integrated facility management approaches, conduct proof-of-concept projects, and establish transparent monitoring and reporting mechanisms including maintenance cost analysis and tracking.
06D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Technical Infrastructure Maintenance and Structural Management** (44%): Comprehensive management of critical building systems (electricity, fire detection, lighting, heating and air conditioning, water supply, sanitary installations, elevators); technical management of structural and finishing elements (exterior and interior carpentry, facades, roofs, interior/exterior finishes); administration of key management protocols and operations

• **Environmental Compliance and Building Performance Oversight** (15%): Environmental permit management and regulatory compliance (EMAS certification actions, BEACH initiatives); energy management and air/water quality monitoring; building audits and technical inspections; regulatory inspections of technical installations; health and safety coordination

• **Facilities Operations and Grounds Maintenance** (18%): Cleaning operations and waste management; green space maintenance and upkeep; operational readiness of facilities infrastructure

• **Customer Service and Emergency Response Operations** (12%): Buildings Helpdesk management and client interface coordination through CLIQC team and buildings managers; nighttime duty provision and shift team operations for emergency response

• **Administrative, Financial, and Strategic Facility Development** (11%): Budgetary and financial management of building services; concession and rental agreement administration; real estate prospecting activities; implementation of integrated facility management systems

• **Performance Modernization and Optimization** (4%): Implementation of customer-focused integrated facility management approaches; proof-of-concept initiatives and modernization projects; transparent monitoring and reporting mechanisms; maintenance cost analysis and performance tracking

06D20.name: 06D20
original_activities: Create a master plan for the major renovation of Parliament buildings aimed at complying with current legislation regarding energy performance. Carry out large projects and heavy renovations including monitoring studies, coordination with internal services, requests for offers for services and works, monitoring the execution of the work until final acceptance, management of administrative, financial, budgetary and legal aspects.

Provide support for these same projects such as resource management (framework contracts, design office, control office) as well as logistics (secretarial work, programming, finances, graphics unit, reporting, IT management).
Participate in working groups, training, etc.
06D20.refined_description: Unit 06D20 is the specialized Building Renovation and Compliance Management operational unit within Directorate 06D (Infrastructure and Building Management), responsible for strategic planning, execution, and comprehensive administrative oversight of the European Parliament's major renovation initiatives designed to achieve full compliance with contemporary European energy performance legislation. Operating as a leaf node with no subordinate units while collaborating with sibling units 06D10 and 06D30 under unified directorate oversight, Unit 06D20 executes its mission across three integrated functional domains that collectively address Parliament's infrastructure modernization objectives. The unit's dominant activity (70% of workload) encompasses the complete lifecycle management of large-scale capital projects and heavy structural renovations, including creation of master renovation plans, execution of preliminary and ongoing monitoring studies, coordination with internal Parliament services, competitive procurement of services and works, on-site supervision of construction execution through final acceptance, and integrated management of all associated administrative, financial, budgetary, and legal dimensions. Supporting these primary renovation initiatives (25% of workload), the unit provides critical operational support through framework contract administration, specialized coordination with design offices and control offices, and essential logistics functions including secretarial services, project programming and scheduling, financial administration and accounting, graphics production and documentation, comprehensive reporting, and IT infrastructure management and systems support. Additionally, Unit 06D20 dedicates 5% of its capacity to institutional participation activities encompassing working group contributions, professional training and development initiatives, and collaborative efforts that enhance organizational effectiveness and capacity-building across the infrastructure management function.
06D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Planning and Major Project Execution** (70%): Creation and implementation of comprehensive master renovation plans for Parliament buildings; execution of large-scale capital projects and heavy structural renovations; conduct of preliminary and ongoing monitoring studies; coordination with internal Parliament services; solicitation, evaluation, and competitive procurement of services and works; supervision of construction execution and final project acceptance; comprehensive management of administrative, financial, budgetary, and legal aspects across all renovation initiatives

• **Operational Support and Resource Management** (25%): Framework contract administration and resource optimization; coordination with specialized design offices and control offices; logistics management encompassing secretarial support, project programming and scheduling, financial processing and accounting, graphics production and documentation, comprehensive reporting, and IT infrastructure management and systems support

• **Institutional Collaboration and Professional Development** (5%): Participation in working groups and collaborative institutional initiatives; engagement in professional training and capacity-building activities that enhance organizational effectiveness

06D30.name: 06D30
original_activities: Ensure support for adaptations, renovations and development work on technical installations and buildings located in Brussels, in order to meet the requirements of national laws, environmental and accessibility objectives, while guaranteeing the continuity of the activities of the European Parliament.
Respond to requests for new projects and assist departments in expressing their needs; propose sketches and the resulting feasibility studies, subject to the availability of resources and budget.
Establish the multi-annual schedule of studies and works to improve technical installations and to renovate and redevelop buildings in line with the European Parliament's buildings policy.
Establish transparent control and reporting and adequate monitoring of costs related to the unit's activities.
Manage and modernize the graphic and technical archives of the Brussels site as well as the installation of directional and safety signage in buildings.
06D30.refined_description: Unit 06D30 operates as a specialized technical and infrastructure planning unit within Directorate 06D (Infrastructure and Building Management), functioning as a critical operational component of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate (DG 06). The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses infrastructure management, planning, and oversight of technical installations and building development across the Parliament's Brussels facilities, ensuring compliance with national legislation, environmental standards, and accessibility requirements while maintaining uninterrupted parliamentary operations. As a leaf-node unit operating in coordination with sibling units 06D10 and 06D20 under unified directional oversight, Unit 06D30 balances two equally prioritized operational pillars—delivering continuous technical support through systematic adaptations and renovations (35% of workload), and providing responsive project consultation services that assist institutional departments in articulating infrastructure requirements through preliminary sketches and feasibility studies within resource and budgetary constraints (35% of workload). These core operational activities are complemented by strategic functions including the establishment and maintenance of multi-annual schedules for technical installations improvements and building renovation initiatives aligned with the European Parliament's buildings policy framework (20% of workload), the implementation of transparent financial controls, cost reporting mechanisms, and comprehensive monitoring systems specific to unit activities (5% of workload), and the management of modernized graphic and technical archives for the Brussels site alongside coordination of directional and safety signage installation throughout Parliament buildings (5% of workload). Collectively, these integrated functions ensure that Unit 06D30 maintains institutional infrastructure at optimal operational and compliance standards while supporting strategic capital planning and resource allocation decisions across the Parliament's Brussels facilities.
06D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Technical Infrastructure Maintenance and Regulatory Compliance** (35%): Ensuring continuous support for adaptations, renovations, and development work on technical installations and buildings in Brussels to meet national legal requirements, environmental objectives, and accessibility standards while guaranteeing uninterrupted European Parliament operations

• **Project Development and Departmental Support Services** (35%): Processing and responding to new project requests from parliamentary departments, providing technical assistance in infrastructure needs assessment, developing preliminary sketches, and conducting comprehensive feasibility studies subject to available resource and budget parameters

• **Strategic Planning and Policy Implementation** (20%): Establishing and maintaining multi-annual schedules for technical studies and construction works to systematically improve installations and renovate buildings in accordance with the European Parliament's overarching buildings policy framework

• **Financial Stewardship and Documentation Management** (10%): Implementing transparent cost control systems and comprehensive financial monitoring of unit activities (5%), and managing modernized technical and graphic archives for the Brussels site while coordinating directional and safety signage installation throughout Parliament buildings (5%)

06E.name: 06E
original_activities: Manage the equipment and services departments necessary for the smooth operation of buildings in Strasbourg.
Manage technically and administratively the Parliament's buildings in Strasbourg.
Oversee, coordinate and motivate the directorate's units and services, and ensure that information is exchanged between the various services of the directorate.
Help, as part of the management team, to draw up the directorate's strategic guidelines, and ensure that budgetary, strategic and operational decisions are applied effectively.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-directorate-general committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., and represent the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those fora.
Act as authorising officer by subdelegation.
06E.refined_description:
**

Unit 06E operates as a Direction-level entity within the General Management directorate (Unit 06) of the European Parliament, reporting to the Secretary General and functioning as the operational and strategic nexus for Strasbourg facilities management. The unit bears primary responsibility for comprehensive technical and administrative oversight of the Parliament's Strasbourg-based buildings and their supporting infrastructure, encompassing both equipment and services departments essential for building operations. This core facilities stewardship function constitutes 60% of the unit's resource allocation and represents its primary operational focus, bifurcated into two complementary activities: management of equipment and services departments (30%) and integrated technical and administrative management of Parliament buildings in Strasbourg (30%). Beyond direct facility management, Unit 06E exercises critical leadership and coordination functions within the directorate through oversight, coordination, and motivation of subordinate units and services (06E10, 06E20, and 06E30) while facilitating inter-service information exchange and organizational cohesion (15% of workload). The unit contributes substantively to strategic directorate governance through participation in management teams responsible for formulating strategic guidelines and ensuring effective implementation of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions (15% of workload). Unit 06E maintains institutional representation responsibilities through participation in intra- and inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies on behalf of the directorate and directorate-general (5% of workload), while exercising subdelegated authorising officer authority within its assigned domain of responsibility (5% of workload).

**
06E.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Facilities and Infrastructure Management** (60%): Direct management of equipment and services departments essential for smooth building operations (30%); integrated technical and administrative management of Parliament's Strasbourg buildings, encompassing both technical oversight and administrative stewardship functions (30%)

• **Directorate Leadership and Coordination** (15%): Oversight, coordination, and motivation of subordinate directorate units and services (06E10, 06E20, 06E30); facilitation of inter-service information exchange and organizational cohesion within the directorate

• **Strategic Planning and Governance** (15%): Participation in management team activities for development and formulation of directorate-wide strategic guidelines; oversight and effective implementation of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions across the directorate

• **Institutional Representation and Committee Participation** (5%): Participation in intra- and inter-directorate-general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies; representation of the directorate and directorate-general in external institutional fora as required

• **Financial Authority and Delegation** (5%): Exercise of authorising officer responsibilities and financial authority through subdelegation arrangements within assigned domain of responsibility

06E10.name: 06E10
original_activities: Operate and maintain technical equipment, building envelope and finishes, exterior spaces; ensure cleaning, waste management and energy management in buildings on site, in line with objective no. 1 of general management, 'Manage facilities to ensure a safe and quality working environment (...). Preserve the current real estate portfolio (...)'.
Keep the site's technical archives up to date (DOE (As-Built) file, plans, digital models, etc.) following work in the buildings.
Produce reports on maintenance operations, including financial and human resources aspects, in order to provide an overview to management.
Maintain medium and long-term planning for maintenance and renovation work in accordance with Parliament's Real Estate Policy.
Implement the Institution's policies on the Strasbourg site: Ad'AP (Accessibility for all), EMAS action plan, etc.
06E10.refined_description: Unit 06E10 operates as a specialized technical facilities management unit within Direction 06E of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as a leaf-node organizational entity with direct operational responsibility for the Strasbourg site's physical infrastructure, building systems, and operational continuity. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the strategic stewardship and daily operation of the institution's real estate assets, directly supporting General Management Objective No. 1 to "Manage facilities to ensure a safe and quality working environment" while preserving the current real estate portfolio. Unit 06E10 allocates its organizational capacity across five integrated functional domains: operational facility management (50%), technical documentation and records management (25%), maintenance operation reporting with financial and human resource analysis (10%), strategic planning and policy alignment (10%), and institutional policy implementation (5%). Operating collaboratively within Direction 06E's coordinated facility management framework alongside sibling units 06E20 and 06E30, Unit 06E10 functions as a critical operational partner that integrates daily technical operations with strategic capital planning, institutional compliance, and comprehensive documentation systems to ensure sustained facility performance and organizational knowledge preservation across the Strasbourg premises.
06E10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Operational Facility Management and Building Maintenance** (50%): Operate and maintain all technical equipment, building envelopes, finishes, and exterior spaces; implement comprehensive cleaning protocols, waste management systems, and energy management initiatives across all on-site facilities to ensure safe, quality working environments and preserve institutional real estate assets.

• **Technical Documentation and Records Management** (25%): Maintain and continuously update comprehensive site technical archives including As-Built (DOE) documentation, architectural plans, and digital building models following all maintenance, construction, and renovation interventions to ensure institutional knowledge preservation, regulatory compliance, and documentation accessibility.

• **Operational Reporting and Financial Oversight** (10%): Produce detailed maintenance operation reports integrating financial and human resource dimensions to provide management with transparent visibility of operational performance, resource utilization, and performance metrics.

• **Strategic Planning and Real Estate Policy Alignment** (10%): Develop and maintain medium and long-term maintenance and renovation planning frameworks aligned with Parliament's established Real Estate Policy and strategic capital planning objectives.

• **Institutional Policy Implementation** (5%): Implement Parliament-wide policies specific to the Strasbourg site, including Ad'AP (Accessibility for All) accessibility standards and EMAS environmental action plan commitments.

06E20.name: 06E20
original_activities: Improve Strasbourg's buildings in terms of environmental efficiency, sustainability and functionality; implement high security standards (modernization: WEISS: radio studio; TV studio; Security dispatching. CHURCHILL hall; renovation of lounges and WEISS MPs bar extension; improvement forecourt, installation of Agora WEISS secure gates; creation of direct connection between the car park and the building hall and securing access to the CHURCHILL car park).

Ensure administration as well as all financial, budgetary and contractual functions linked to projects (preparation of works procurement procedures and management of framework contracts and direct contracts).

Implement initiatives relating to accessibility in compliance with French regulations on the subject and the Vademecum of the European Parliament (improve WEISS lighting; make conference rooms and the WEISS terrace accessible; create WEISS accessible toilets; adapt the WEISS catering counters; reinforce the orientation signage on the forecourt of the WEISS building).

Conduct studies aimed at preparing the Strasbourg headquarters for the 'Fit for 55' objective: reduction in energy consumption through the installation of new equipment, adjustment and optimization of spaces, increased insulation and reduction of CO² emissions, in particular through the use of green energy.

Improve the buildings on the Bazoches site in terms of environmental efficiency, sustainability and functionality; implement high safety standards (thermal renovation of the Pommier building in BAZOCHES and creation of a safety pavilion in Bazoches).
Support ecological mobility (creation of the new WEISS bicycle parking lot and installation of CHURCHILL electric vehicle chargers).
Ensure human resources management and other missions (participate in working groups, follow training, etc.).
06E20.refined_description: Unit 06E20 operates as a specialized infrastructure stewardship entity within Direction 06E of the European Parliament's General Management directorate, bearing comprehensive responsibility for the modernization, maintenance, and sustainable optimization of the Parliament's Strasbourg headquarters complex and Bazoches satellite facilities. The unit's strategic mandate encompasses environmental sustainability aligned with the 'Fit for 55' climate objective, accessibility compliance with French regulatory requirements and European Parliament Vademecum standards, operational security enhancement, and infrastructure optimization to ensure contemporary standards across multiple facility locations. The unit executes this mandate through seven integrated activity streams that collectively advance the institution's sustainability agenda while ensuring occupant safety, regulatory compliance, and operational functionality. These activities position Unit 06E20 as a critical infrastructure stewardship entity operating in coordination with sibling units 06E10 and 06E30 within the General Management directorate structure, reporting through the organizational hierarchy to the Secretary General. The unit's portfolio spans capital modernization initiatives, comprehensive project administration and financial stewardship, accessibility and inclusive design implementation, environmental sustainability and energy efficiency programs, sustainable mobility infrastructure development, and human resources management with cross-functional institutional collaboration.
06E20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Infrastructure Modernization and Security Enhancement** (30%): Comprehensive modernization of Strasbourg headquarters encompassing WEISS radio studio, television studio, and security dispatching facility upgrades; CHURCHILL hall renovation; lounge and WEISS MPs bar extension; forecourt improvements with Agora WEISS secure gate installation; direct car park-to-building connectivity; and secured CHURCHILL car park access implementation.

• **Administrative, Financial, and Contractual Project Management** (20%): Integrated administrative oversight, financial stewardship, budgetary control, and contractual administration directly supporting all infrastructure projects; preparation of procurement procedures for works; and management of framework and direct contracts.

• **Accessibility Compliance and Inclusive Design** (15%): Implementation of accessibility enhancements across Strasbourg facilities in full compliance with French regulatory requirements and European Parliament Vademecum standards, including WEISS lighting improvements, conference room and terrace accessibility modifications, accessible sanitation facilities, catering counter adaptations, and enhanced forecourt orientation signage.

• **Environmental Sustainability and Energy Efficiency Programs** (25%): Dual-focus sustainability initiatives comprising preparatory studies aligned with 'Fit for 55' climate objective through energy consumption reduction, equipment installation, space optimization, enhanced insulation measures, and CO² emissions mitigation via renewable energy integration at Strasbourg headquarters (15%); and thermal renovation of Bazoches Pommier building with dedicated safety pavilion construction (10%).

• **Sustainable Mobility Infrastructure and Organizational Support** (10%): Promotion of ecological transportation solutions through WEISS bicycle parking facility creation and CHURCHILL electric vehicle charging infrastructure installation (5%); combined with human resources management functions, working group participation, and professional development activities supporting cross-functional institutional collaboration (5%).

06E30.name: 06E30
original_activities: Organize and monitor construction sites and operations, including financial aspects and human resources, in order to provide an overview to management.
Follow studies to carry out modernization and renovation work on installations in buildings, particularly meeting rooms.
Provide a 'customer interface' service to analyze and respond to requests from Members of Parliament and the administration.

Communicate regularly with other institutions established on the Strasbourg site (Ombudsman, Commission, etc.) and local stakeholders (Prefecture, Region, City of Strasbourg, Council of Europe, etc.).
06E30.refined_description: Unit 06E30 operates as a specialized operational management and stakeholder coordination hub within Direction 06E of the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as the critical infrastructure and facilities management unit for the institution's Strasbourg site. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive oversight of construction sites, operational activities, and facility management while maintaining transparent reporting and strategic visibility for senior management through integrated financial and human resource management. Operating as a strategic infrastructure and coordination center, the unit simultaneously advances the Parliament's modernization agenda by monitoring and supporting technical studies and implementation of renovation and building installation projects, with particular emphasis on enhancing parliamentary meeting room facilities and related infrastructure. The unit maintains direct responsiveness to internal stakeholders through a dedicated customer interface function that systematically analyzes and processes formal facility-related requests originating from Members of Parliament and administrative departments, ensuring documented and responsive service delivery. Additionally, Unit 06E30 establishes and maintains strategic communication networks with external institutional partners based at the Strasbourg site—including the European Ombudsman and European Commission offices—while coordinating with local governance and cultural stakeholders (Strasbourg Prefecture, Regional Authority, City of Strasbourg, and Council of Europe) to ensure coordinated institutional relationships and effective interagency cooperation.
06E30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Construction and Operations Management** (55%): Organize, monitor, and supervise construction sites and facility operations; manage financial resources and human resource allocations; provide comprehensive reporting and strategic visibility to senior management on project status and resource utilization.

• **Infrastructure Modernization and Facility Enhancement** (30%): Monitor and follow technical studies supporting modernization and renovation initiatives for building installations; oversee implementation of facility improvement projects with particular emphasis on parliamentary meeting room infrastructure upgrades and related operational enhancements.

• **Internal Customer Service and Request Management** (10%): Operate as dedicated customer interface; systematically analyze and respond to formal facility-related requests from Members of Parliament and administrative departments; ensure responsive, documented service delivery.

• **External Institutional and Local Stakeholder Coordination** (5%): Establish and maintain regular strategic communication with external institutional partners at the Strasbourg site (European Ombudsman, European Commission); coordinate with local governmental authorities (Prefecture, Regional Authority, City of Strasbourg) and cultural institutions (Council of Europe) to support institutional coordination and interagency cooperation.

original_activities: Provide the strategic orientations, organise and supervise the activities of the Directorate-General. Liaise with European Parliament governing bodies, supervise and coordinate relations with other Directorates-General and Political Groups.
Exercise the functions of Authorising Officer by delegation.
Manage the Directorate-General's staff policy, including staff appraisal exercises in the context of staff reports.
Meet and liaise with Members and especially with the Vice-Presidents in charge of ICT and Cybersecurity and monitor the parliamentary activities.

Ensure interinstitutional and international relations. Represent the Institution in the field of ICT Strategy and Cybersecurity, including in the context of interinstitutional coordination operations.
Hold the LCO role for the European Parliament.
Supervise internal and external communication.
10.refined_description:
Unit 10, designated as the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functions as the apex strategic leadership entity within the European Parliament's ICT governance structure, reporting directly to the Secretary General. Operating as a root-level strategic unit, it exercises comprehensive governance and operational oversight across the Institution's information and communications technology ecosystem while orchestrating specialized service delivery through seven subordinate Direction-level units (10-10, 10-20, 10-2010, 10A, 10B, 10C, and two additional units). The unit's operational mandate encompasses establishing strategic orientations and organizing the full spectrum of Directorate-General activities (35% allocation), exercising delegated Authorising Officer functions to ensure financial and administrative accountability (15% allocation), and managing comprehensive staff policy including appraisals and performance reporting administration (15% allocation). Unit 10 maintains continuous direct engagement with Members of Parliament and Vice-Presidents responsible for ICT and Cybersecurity portfolios while monitoring parliamentary activities to ensure alignment between institutional leadership and technological strategy (15% allocation). The unit serves as the official institutional representative for ICT Strategy and Cybersecurity matters, managing interinstitutional and international relations and participating in formal interinstitutional coordination frameworks (10% allocation), whilst simultaneously executing the Legislative Coordination Officer (LCO) role for the European Parliament and supervising both internal and external communication functions (10% allocation). Through these integrated activities, Unit 10 ensures coordinated institutional representation, financial stewardship, human resource management, and strategic alignment across the Parliament's technological capabilities and security posture.

---

##
10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership, Governance and Organizational Direction** (35%): Provision of strategic orientations and comprehensive organization of all Directorate-General activities; liaison with European Parliament governing bodies; supervision and coordination of inter-Directorate-General relations; coordination with Political Groups; orchestration of seven subordinate Direction-level units delivering specialized ICT and cybersecurity functions

• **Financial and Administrative Stewardship** (15%): Exercise of delegated Authorising Officer functions; budgetary and financial management accountability; resource allocation and administrative controls across the Directorate-General

• **Human Resources and Organizational Development** (15%): Management of comprehensive staff policy; implementation of staff appraisal exercises; administration of formal staff performance reporting procedures; oversight of staff evaluation frameworks

• **Parliamentary Engagement and Stakeholder Relations** (15%): Direct engagement and liaison with Members of Parliament and Vice-Presidents overseeing ICT and Cybersecurity portfolios; continuous monitoring of parliamentary activities and institutional priorities; alignment of technological strategy with parliamentary governance structures

• **External Relations, Institutional Representation and Interinstitutional Coordination** (10%): Management of interinstitutional and international relations; official institutional representation in ICT Strategy and Cybersecurity domains; participation in formal interinstitutional coordination mechanisms; representation of the Parliament's institutional interests in technology and security matters

• **Specialized Institutional Functions and Communications** (10%): Performance of Legislative Coordination Officer (LCO) responsibilities for the European Parliament; supervision of internal and external communication strategies and operations; coordinated messaging regarding institutional technological capabilities and security posture

original_activities: Support the Director-General and its Management with strategic planning monitoring and reporting activities and the implementation of Parliament's priorities.
Manage the legal advisory functions at Directorate-General level.
Develop partnerships and networks with other Directorates-General and Member States and third countries' parliaments.
Manage the advisory role core business activities of the European Parliament such as legislative and budgetary ones.
10-10.refined_description: Unit 10-10 operates as a strategic Direction-level entity within the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical coordination and advisory interface between the Director-General's office and the broader institutional framework of the European Parliament. Positioned as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy with no subordinate units, Unit 10-10 maintains functional coordination with sibling units (10-20, 10-2010, 10A, 10B, 10C, and two additional peer units) while reporting directly to parent unit DG ITEC (Unit 10). The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses four interconnected functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of its documented workload allocation, integrating executive support, legal advisory functions, external relations management, and core parliamentary business services. Unit 10-10 serves as a specialized directional component that ensures organizational alignment between ICT operations and parliamentary objectives while maintaining institutional continuity across fundamental governance and legislative processes. Through its multifaceted operational framework, the unit strengthens the European Parliament's external relations and collaborative capacity while providing essential juridical guidance and compliance oversight that underpins all major DG ITEC operations and governance decisions.
10-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Support and Strategic Governance** (30%): Provide comprehensive support to the Director-General and senior Management through strategic planning monitoring, performance reporting activities, and systematic implementation of Parliament's institutional priorities, ensuring organizational alignment between ICT operations and parliamentary objectives while enabling executive decision-making

• **Legal and Regulatory Advisory Services** (30%): Manage comprehensive legal advisory functions at the Directorate-General level, providing essential juridical guidance, regulatory compliance oversight, and legal counsel that underpins all major DG ITEC operations and governance decisions across the directorate's full operational scope

• **Institutional Partnerships and External Relations** (20%): Develop and maintain strategic partnerships and institutional networks with other Directorates-General, Member States' parliaments, and third countries' parliamentary bodies, thereby strengthening the European Parliament's external relations, collaborative capacity, and inter-parliamentary cooperation frameworks

• **Core Parliamentary Business Advisory Functions** (20%): Manage advisory role core business activities essential to the European Parliament's operational mandate, encompassing legislative support functions and budgetary advisory responsibilities, thereby ensuring institutional continuity in fundamental parliamentary processes

original_activities: Support the Director-General in the European Parliament-LCO role defined by Article 4(2) of the Bureau Decision and coordination of tasks required to ensure Parliament's compliance.
Support the Director-General as Chair of the Interinstitutional Cybersecurity Board established by Article 10 of EU Regulation 2023/2841.
Contribute to official investigations and coordinate official investigations within the Directorate-General.
Supervise Parliament's Cybersecurity Emergency Response Team (EP-CERT) and ensure Parliament's compliance with cybersecurity incidents reporting obligations.
Contribute specific cybersecurity expertise to initiatives aiming at improving Parliament's cybersecurity maturity.
10-20.refined_description:
**

Unit 10-20 operates as a specialized directional unit within DG ITEC (Unit 10), functioning as the primary executive support and governance coordination mechanism for the Director-General's institutional responsibilities across multiple regulatory and operational domains. The unit serves as a critical interface between executive leadership and Parliament's operational cybersecurity and compliance infrastructure, while maintaining peer relationships with sibling units (10-10, 10-2010, 10A, 10B, 10C, and others) that collectively constitute Parliament's comprehensive information and communications technology governance ecosystem. As a leaf node reporting directly to DG ITEC leadership, Unit 10-20 coordinates executive support for the Director-General's European Parliament-Liaison and Compliance Officer (LCO) role as defined by Article 4(2) of the Bureau Decision, ensuring comprehensive coordination of all tasks necessary to maintain Parliament's institutional compliance across regulatory frameworks and governance requirements. The unit simultaneously positions itself at the intersection of Parliament's internal cybersecurity governance and broader interinstitutional cyber-resilience frameworks through support for the Director-General's chairmanship of the Interinstitutional Cybersecurity Board established under Article 10 of EU Regulation 2023/2841. Additionally, Unit 10-20 serves as an investigative coordination hub for institutional oversight matters, conducts and coordinates official investigations within DG ITEC and across the broader Directorate-General structure, supervises Parliament's Cybersecurity Emergency Response Team (EP-CERT), enforces compliance obligations regarding cybersecurity incident reporting, and delivers specialized cybersecurity expertise to strategic initiatives advancing Parliament's cybersecurity maturity and institutional cyber-defense evolution.

---

**
10-20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Director-General Executive Support and Institutional Compliance Governance** (40%): Comprehensive support for the Director-General's European Parliament-LCO role as defined by Article 4(2) of the Bureau Decision, coordinating all tasks required to ensure Parliament's institutional compliance across regulatory frameworks and governance requirements.

• **Interinstitutional Cybersecurity Leadership and Board Chairmanship Support** (15%): Strategic support for the Director-General's chairmanship of the Interinstitutional Cybersecurity Board established by Article 10 of EU Regulation 2023/2841, facilitating cross-institutional cyber-governance coordination and oversight.

• **Investigative Coordination and Institutional Oversight** (15%): Initiation, conduct, and coordination of official investigations within DG ITEC and across the broader Directorate-General structure, serving as an investigative coordination hub for institutional accountability and governance processes.

• **Cybersecurity Operations, Incident Response, and Compliance Management** (15%): Direct supervision of Parliament's Cybersecurity Emergency Response Team (EP-CERT), enforcement of Parliament's compliance obligations regarding cybersecurity incident reporting as mandated by EU Regulation 2023/2841 and relevant institutional regulations, and management of mandatory incident reporting obligations.

• **Cybersecurity Maturity Enhancement and Strategic Capability Development** (15%): Provision of specialized cybersecurity expertise to strategic initiatives and programs designed to improve Parliament's cybersecurity maturity posture, institutional cyber-resilience, and overall defensive capabilities evolution.

original_activities: Incident Response. Respond, swiftly and effectively, to cybersecurity incidents, minimising the impact and restoring normal operations. Monitor external sources for European Parliament related data leakage. Cybersecurity Incident reporting to top management.
Expand cybersecurity services, prepare incident response processes and procedures, and strengthen incident response capabilities, promoting resilience and innovation.
Mobile device security services. Provide forensics analysis and spyware verification of mobile devices. Perform security research into mobile systems architecture.
Provide stakeholders with expert advisory services on cybersecurity risks and mitigations. Engage with the broader security community across the EU institutions.
Cyber Threat Intelligence / Cyber Threat Hunting. Manage Threat intelligence data, Threat hunting activities and Malware analysis.
Social Media Assurance Service (SMAS). Resolves incidents with regards to Social Media threats including impersonation, threat of harm, account hijacking.
Official investigations. Provide cybersecurity related assistance and facilitate Police, OLAF and other legal related investigations.
10-2010.refined_description: Unit 10-2010 operates as a specialized Directorate-level cybersecurity operations and incident management function within the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the European Parliament's primary rapid-response and threat mitigation capability for defensive security infrastructure. The unit's primary mission encompasses the detection, response, and mitigation of cybersecurity threats affecting institutional operational continuity and information security posture through swift incident containment, remediation, external source monitoring for data leakage detection, and formal executive-level incident reporting to senior management. Supporting this core operational mission, the unit expands and enhances its cybersecurity service offerings through development and refinement of incident response processes, procedures, and organizational capabilities that promote institutional resilience and foster innovation in security practices. The unit addresses emerging threat landscapes through specialized mobile device security services (including forensic analysis, spyware verification, and advanced security research into mobile systems architecture), cyber threat intelligence and proactive threat hunting operations (encompassing threat intelligence data management and malware analysis capabilities), and its Social Media Assurance Service (SMAS) which resolves impersonation, threats of harm, and account hijacking incidents targeting institutional accounts. Complementing these technical functions, the unit provides expert advisory services on cybersecurity risks and mitigation strategies to institutional stakeholders while maintaining active engagement with the broader EU institutional security community to ensure alignment with inter-institutional best practices and knowledge sharing. Finally, the unit supports official investigations by providing cybersecurity-related technical assistance and facilitating coordination with police authorities, OLAF, and other legal entities. As a leaf node within the DG ITEC structure reporting to Unit 10, the directorate operates in coordination with sibling units (10-10, 10-20, 10A, 10B, 10C, and additional units) to provide comprehensive information and communications technology governance across the institution.
10-2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Active Incident Response & Crisis Management** (40%): Rapid detection, containment, and remediation of cybersecurity incidents; continuous monitoring of external sources for European Parliament-related data leakage; formal cybersecurity incident reporting to senior management and executive leadership with emphasis on impact minimization and operational restoration

• **Capability Development & Service Expansion** (15%): Enhancement and refinement of incident response processes, procedures, and organizational capabilities; strengthening of institutional incident response infrastructure; promotion of institutional resilience and innovation in cybersecurity service delivery

• **Specialized Technical Security Services** (23%): Mobile device security services including forensic analysis, spyware verification, and advanced security research into mobile systems architecture addressing endpoint threats (15%); cyber threat intelligence data management, proactive threat hunting operations, and malware analysis capabilities (8%)

• **Stakeholder Engagement & Advisory Services** (8%): Expert cybersecurity risk advisory services to institutional stakeholders regarding risk mitigation strategies; active engagement with broader EU institutional security community; inter-institutional coordination and alignment with best practices and knowledge sharing

• **Social Media Assurance & Investigative Support** (14%): Social Media Assurance Service (SMAS) addressing incidents related to account impersonation, threats of harm, and account hijacking targeting institutional accounts (7%); cybersecurity-related technical assistance for official investigations; facilitation of inquiries and coordination with police authorities, OLAF, and other legal entities (7%)

10A.name: 10A
original_activities: Provide customer care and user experience, including service desks and proximity teams, the provision of the Digital Workplace, its configuration and support, and management of applications and IT logistics.
Act as the single point of contact for Members and staff for services and support.
Provide direct facing user support services, including logistics team installing and repairing equipment. Provide support for videoconferencing systems and telephony.
Acquire and provide IT equipment and manage just-in-time stock of individual IT equipment and videoconferencing systems.
Design the Digital Work Place related services and standard configuration. Manage and acquire Customers and DWP related licenses. Support and automatise e-mail services.
Be responsible for unified printing, customer relations and customer care and for negotiation of SLAs/MoUs with customers.
10A.refined_description: Unit 10A is a Direction-level organizational unit within DG ITEC that functions as the primary operational hub for end-user services and digital workplace enablement across the European Parliament, serving as the critical interface between ICT infrastructure and the institution's Members and staff. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses six interconnected activity streams that collectively represent a customer-centric service delivery model totaling 100% of operational workload. Unit 10A maintains hierarchical oversight of nine child units (10A10, 10A1010, 10A1020, 10A20, 10A2010, 10A30, and six additional specialized units) that operationalize these functions at granular levels, while operating as a peer unit alongside 10B, 10C, and other directional counterparts within the DG ITEC structure to ensure coordinated ICT service delivery across the institution. The unit's mission encompasses delivering comprehensive customer care and user experience services through dedicated service desks and proximity support teams with integrated Digital Workplace provisioning, application management, and IT logistics (30%); functioning as the unified single point of contact for all Members and staff requiring support and services (30%); providing direct-facing user support services through specialized logistics teams responsible for equipment installation, repair, videoconferencing system support, and telephony infrastructure assistance (15%); acquiring, provisioning, and managing IT equipment inventory with just-in-time stock management capabilities (10%); designing and maintaining Digital Workplace-related service architectures, standard configurations, and managing customer and DWP-related licensing portfolios while automating email infrastructure (10%); and overseeing unified printing services, customer relations governance, and negotiating Service Level Agreements and Memoranda of Understanding with institutional customers (5%). These activities collectively ensure seamless technology integration, comprehensive user support, and optimized resource management across the Parliament's institutional operations.
10A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Customer Care and Service Delivery (60%)**: Provision of comprehensive customer care and user experience management through dedicated service desks, proximity support teams, Digital Workplace provisioning with full configuration and technical support, and integrated application and IT logistics management (30%); establishment and maintenance of a unified single point of contact for all Members and staff requiring support and services across the Parliament (30%)

• **Direct Technical Support and Infrastructure (25%)**: Delivery of direct-facing user support services through specialized logistics teams responsible for equipment installation, repair operations, videoconferencing system support, and telephony infrastructure assistance (15%); acquisition, provisioning, and just-in-time inventory management of IT equipment and videoconferencing systems to ensure optimal resource availability (10%)

• **Digital Workplace Design and Systems Management (15%)**: Design and standardization of Digital Workplace-related service architectures and configurations (5%); management and acquisition of customer and DWP-related software licensing portfolios with automation of email service delivery infrastructure (5%); maintenance of unified printing services, customer relations governance protocols, and negotiation of Service Level Agreements and Memoranda of Understanding with internal and external institutional customers (5%)

10A10.name: 10A10
original_activities: Provide high quality IT support services to Members and their offices with particular emphasis on user experience and the identification of their emerging IT needs.

Provide in person assistance in various dedicated venues (IT Lounge, IT Corner, and Members' Trainees Welcome Office), including specific support for Members' private devices and first line spyware verifications.
Focus on building trusted partnerships, giving special attention to the Digital Accredited Parliamentary Assistant community and the IT Security Correspondents.
Ensure the implementation of the Rules on the provision of IT equipment.
10A10.refined_description: Unit 10A10 operates as the dedicated end-user support and digital workplace enablement hub within Direction 10A (DG ITEC), functioning as the critical operational interface between the European Parliament's Information Technology and Communications infrastructure and its Members, parliamentary staff, and associated stakeholder communities. The unit's integrated mission encompasses four strategically aligned operational pillars that collectively position it as an essential component within the institution's broader end-user services ecosystem and digital transformation objectives. Unit 10A10 delivers high-quality primary IT support services directly to Members and their offices with systematic emphasis on user experience optimization and proactive identification of emerging IT requirements to inform strategic digital workplace planning. The unit provides on-site technical assistance across three strategically located dedicated physical venues—the IT Lounge, IT Corner, and Members' Trainees Welcome Office—where specialized first-line support is delivered, including technical assistance for Members' privately-owned devices and preliminary cybersecurity threat verification protocols. Additionally, Unit 10A10 actively cultivates and maintains trusted partnerships with key stakeholder communities, specifically the Digital Accredited Parliamentary Assistant community and the IT Security Correspondents network, thereby strengthening institutional security posture, operational resilience, and knowledge-sharing networks. As a subordinate operational unit within Direction 10A's hierarchical structure, Unit 10A10 ensures full implementation and compliance with the established institutional Rules governing IT equipment provision, thereby guaranteeing standardized, governance-aligned technology distribution across the Parliament while collaborating with sibling units to deliver cohesive end-user services.
10A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary IT Support Services Delivery** (50%): High-quality IT support provision to Members and their offices with emphasis on user experience optimization and systematic identification of emerging IT requirements to inform strategic digital workplace planning

• **On-Site Technical Assistance Provision** (25%): In-person support delivery across three dedicated physical venues (IT Lounge, IT Corner, Members' Trainees Welcome Office), encompassing specialized technical assistance for Members' privately-owned devices and first-line cybersecurity threat verification services

• **Strategic Stakeholder Partnership Development** (15%): Cultivation and maintenance of trusted partnerships with the Digital Accredited Parliamentary Assistant community and IT Security Correspondents network to strengthen institutional security posture, operational resilience, and organizational knowledge-sharing capabilities

• **IT Equipment Governance and Compliance** (10%): Implementation and oversight of institutional Rules governing IT equipment provision to ensure standardized, compliant technology distribution and allocation standards across the European Parliament

10A1010.name: 10A1010
original_activities: Provide multilingual ICT support services to handle IT requests from Members and their offices.
Deliver IT equipment in accordance with the IT provision rules.
Provide first level support for cybersecurity issues (spams and phishing e-mails, viruses) and contributing to Members' cybersecurity awareness in collaboration with the CISO.
Provide support for iOS and corporate Android devices, delivering advice and assistance for personal devices.
Organise and deliver support for Webex meetings.
Provide extended support service during Saturdays and European Parliament closing days when required.
Deliver IT equipment for missions when required and advised by the crisis cell.
Provide guidance on the use of European Parliament applications and delivering advice on the use of third-party tools.
10A1010.refined_description: Unit 10A1010 functions as a specialized end-user support operational unit within Direction 10A of DG ITEC, serving as the primary multilingual ICT assistance hub for Members of the European Parliament and their offices. The unit's core mission encompasses delivering comprehensive first-line and extended technical support services across the Parliament's digital infrastructure, with multilingual ICT support services (40% of workload) representing the dominant operational focus for managing IT requests from Members and their parliamentary offices. Unit 10A1010 maintains critical cybersecurity resilience through first-level incident response addressing spam, phishing emails, and virus threats (10% of workload), executed in direct collaboration with the Chief Information Security Officer to enhance Members' cybersecurity awareness and institutional threat resilience. The unit's technical portfolio encompasses IT equipment provision in strict accordance with established IT provision rules (15% of workload); comprehensive mobile device support for iOS and corporate Android devices including personal device advisory services (10% of workload); dedicated technical support for Webex meeting platforms to facilitate parliamentary communications infrastructure (10% of workload); guidance on European Parliament applications and third-party tool implementation (5% of workload); extended support services during non-standard operational periods including Saturdays and European Parliament closing days when operationally required (5% of workload); and mission-critical IT equipment deployment for parliamentary missions coordinated through the crisis cell (5% of workload). Collectively, these activities position Unit 10A1010 as a critical operational component within the Direction 10A structure, ensuring seamless digital workplace functionality, robust technological accessibility, and operational continuity for parliamentary Members across all institutional contexts and contingency scenarios.
10A1010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary User Support Services** (50%): Provision of multilingual ICT support services to Members and their offices (40%), supplemented by guidance on European Parliament applications and third-party tool implementation (5%), and IT equipment delivery in accordance with established provision rules (15%), collectively representing the core customer-facing support infrastructure

• **Device and Platform Management** (20%): Technical support for iOS and corporate Android devices including personal device advisory services (10%) and organization/delivery of Webex meeting platform support (10%), ensuring unified communication and mobile device ecosystem functionality

• **Cybersecurity and Threat Mitigation** (10%): First-level cybersecurity incident response addressing spam, phishing emails, and viruses (10%), with collaborative engagement with the Chief Information Security Officer to strengthen Members' cybersecurity awareness and institutional threat resilience

• **Extended Operations and Crisis Support** (10%): Provision of extended support services during non-standard operational periods including Saturdays and European Parliament closing days when required (5%) and mission-critical IT equipment delivery for parliamentary missions coordinated through the crisis cell (5%), ensuring operational continuity across all institutional scenarios

10A1020.name: 10A1020
original_activities: Organise and deliver multilingual IT workshops, webinars, info sessions and demonstrations for assistants, trainees, and constituency-based local assistants.
Run and promote the Digital Accredited Parliamentary Assistant Community and the IT Security correspondents network.
Provide technical support in specialised fields such as social media and Members' self-recorded videos.
Provide short-term loan of audiovisual equipment for self-recorded videos of Members, and manage the self-recording room for Bureau members.
Arrange tailored IT-consultancy services for Members to discover the European Parliament IT environment and get the best out of the IT tools and applications.
Create dedicated materials, targeted campaigns and official communications to Members to support their use of technologies.
10A1020.refined_description: Unit 10A1020 functions as a specialized digital enablement and technical support service within Direction 10A of DG ITEC, serving as the critical operational bridge between the Parliament's technological infrastructure and its end-users—Members, parliamentary staff, assistants, trainees, and constituency-based local personnel. The unit's primary mission encompasses democratizing access to and proficiency with the European Parliament's IT infrastructure and digital tools through four complementary operational domains that collectively support organizational efficiency, digital competency development, and institutional communication objectives. Operating as a leaf node within the Direction 10A hierarchy, Unit 10A1020 delivers integrated end-user digital services through comprehensive training and capacity-building initiatives, community management and professional network coordination, specialized technical expertise in emerging digital domains, personalized IT consultancy services, and targeted communications and change management activities. The unit's strategic positioning enables Members and parliamentary staff to navigate and optimize the Parliament's IT environment while simultaneously fostering institutional knowledge-sharing, peer learning, and coordinated security practices across the organization. Through these interconnected service delivery mechanisms, Unit 10A1020 directly supports Members' and staff proficiency with European Parliament information technology systems and digital workplace tools, positioning itself as an essential enabler of institutional digital transformation and operational effectiveness.
10A1020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Digital Skills Development and Capacity-Building** (25%): Organization and delivery of multilingual IT workshops, webinars, information sessions, and technical demonstrations targeting parliamentary assistants, trainees, and constituency-based local assistants; comprehensive educational programming designed to build foundational and advanced IT competencies across the institution's support staff and membership.

• **Community Management and Professional Network Coordination** (20%): Establishment, operation, active promotion, and administrative support of the Digital Accredited Parliamentary Assistant Community and the IT Security correspondents network; facilitation of peer-to-peer knowledge exchange, professional community engagement, ongoing professional development, and coordinated institutional security practices.

• **Specialized Technical Support Services and Equipment Provisioning** (30%): Provision of expert technical assistance in specialized digital domains including social media management and Members' self-recorded video production (15%); short-term audiovisual equipment loan services and dedicated self-recording facility management for Members and Bureau officials (15%); comprehensive support enabling Members to discover and optimize utilization of institutional digital tools and applications.

• **Personalized IT Consultancy and Guidance Services** (15%): Arrangement of tailored, one-on-one IT-consultancy engagements enabling Members to develop comprehensive understanding of the European Parliament's IT environment, navigate the institution's technology infrastructure, and achieve optimal utilization of available digital tools and applications ecosystem.

• **Communications, Guidance Development, and Technology Advocacy** (10%): Development and dissemination of dedicated materials, targeted awareness campaigns, official guidance documents, and promotional communications directed toward Members and staff to facilitate technology adoption, support institutional digital transformation, and promote effective utilization of parliamentary digital systems.

10A20.name: 10A20

Ensure proximity services providing onsite support in staff offices related to workplace requests and incidents, as well as assistance with mobile devices (BYOD). Walk in experience at Welcome Desks.
Manage the telephone switchboard (Parliament Call Centre service) providing direct contact for citizens to the European Parliament.
Support videoconferences for staff in the three working places.
Ensure a timely and appropriate communication on IT matters to the European Parliament staff.
Manage European Parliament Directory database.
10A20.refined_description: Unit 10A20 operates as a critical operational component within Direction 10A of DG ITEC, functioning as the European Parliament's primary end-user IT support and digital workplace services hub serving the institution's distributed workforce across three working locations. The unit fulfills its core mission of enabling institutional staff productivity and seamless citizen engagement by delivering comprehensive first-line technology support and integrated workplace services across multiple operational dimensions. As the single point of contact for all staff technology-related inquiries and incidents, Unit 10A20 allocates 50% of its workload to primary-level IT support delivered through multiple communication channels (telephone, email, chat platforms, and the ServiceNow ticketing system), while simultaneously dedicating 30% of its capacity to proximity services, providing on-site technical assistance directly within staff office environments for workplace-specific requests, incident resolution, bring-your-own-device (BYOD) mobile device support, and Welcome Desk walk-in user experiences across parliamentary locations. Beyond direct user support, the unit maintains critical institutional infrastructure and communications responsibilities that collectively represent 20% of operational capacity: managing the Parliament Call Centre switchboard service (7% of workload) as the direct public interface for citizen inquiries and contact with the European Parliament, providing videoconference technical support and coordination for staff across all three working locations (5% of workload), ensuring timely and appropriate IT communications and updates to Parliament staff (5% of workload), and administering the European Parliament Directory database system (3% of workload). These integrated activities collectively enable Unit 10A20 to deliver seamless digital workplace services, maintain essential communication infrastructure, and sustain institutional operational continuity across the Parliament's organizational structure.
10A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary IT Support Services** (50%): First-level IT helpdesk functioning as single point of contact for all staff technology inquiries and incident resolution, delivered through telephone, email, chat platforms, and ServiceNow ticketing system

• **On-Site Proximity and Workplace Support Services** (30%): On-site technical assistance delivered directly in staff office environments, including workplace-specific incident management, bring-your-own-device (BYOD) mobile device support, and Welcome Desk walk-in user support across parliamentary locations

• **Parliament Call Centre Switchboard and Public Interface Services** (7%): Operation and management of the Parliament Call Centre switchboard serving as the direct public contact channel for citizens seeking to reach the European Parliament

• **Videoconference Infrastructure Support** (5%): Technical coordination and support for videoconferencing systems and infrastructure utilized by staff across the Parliament's three working locations

• **Internal IT Communications and Institutional Messaging** (5%): Dissemination of timely and appropriate IT-related communications, updates, and institutional messaging to parliamentary staff

• **European Parliament Directory Database Administration** (3%): System administration and maintenance of the European Parliament Directory database serving as the institutional staff contact and organizational reference resource

original_activities: Ensure first level IT support for staff acting as a single point of contact. Handle user requests, IT support for all European Parliament staff for corporate services and other IT issues via telephone, email, chat and ServiceNow application.

• **Multi-Tiered Application Support Services** (35% combined): Direct technical support delivery for proprietary in-house software applications and standardized desktop applications deployed across Parliament (20%), combined with specialized second-level escalation support services that resolve complex technical issues referred from first-level support functions and provide expert technical assistance for critical system impediments (15%)

10A50.name: 10A50
original_activities: Define relevant criteria and standards for purchasing IT equipment. Manage budget, acquisition and logistic activities related to IT equipment and videoconferencing systems, manage the entire lifecycle of products from ordering to decommissioning.
Manage multifunctional copiers ensuring printing, copying and scanning facilities, optimising printing resources.
Manage just-in-time stock of IT equipment and videoconferencing systems. Configure new IT equipment.
Manage A4 paper contract for European Parliament. Distribute A4 paper for Members in Brussels and Strasbourg.
Manage IT equipment inventory tracking with 02PI* numbers.
Manage copyrights contracts in Luxembourg and Belgium.
10A50.refined_description: Unit 10A50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10A of DG ITEC, functioning as the European Parliament's central hub for information technology asset management, office equipment procurement, and comprehensive lifecycle operations. The unit's primary mandate encompasses the definition and implementation of procurement criteria and standards for IT equipment, coupled with complete budget management, acquisition coordination, and logistical administration spanning the entire product lifecycle from initial ordering through final decommissioning. Unit 10A50 maintains integrated responsibility for multifunctional copier systems providing printing, copying, and scanning capabilities while implementing resource optimization strategies across the institution, alongside management of just-in-time inventory systems for IT equipment and videoconferencing technologies with configuration protocols for newly deployed hardware.
