The unit administers the centralized A4 paper supply contract for the European Parliament with direct distribution coordination to Members' offices in both Brussels and Strasbourg locations, maintains comprehensive IT equipment inventory tracking systems utilizing 02PI* numerical designation protocols, and manages copyright licensing contracts across Luxembourg and Belgium jurisdictions. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 10A50 directly supports the operational efficiency of the European Parliament's digital workplace infrastructure while ensuring cost-effective resource utilization, regulatory compliance, and seamless end-user services across all member locations.
10A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Equipment Procurement and Lifecycle Management** (50%): Definition and establishment of purchasing criteria and standards; comprehensive budget administration and allocation; acquisition coordination and logistics operations; complete product lifecycle management from procurement planning through final decommissioning for IT equipment and videoconferencing systems

• **Office Equipment Operations and Resource Optimization** (20%): Multifunctional copier system management; administration of printing, copying, and scanning facility availability; implementation of organizational printing resource optimization strategies and efficiency initiatives

• **Inventory Management and Equipment Configuration** (18%): Just-in-time inventory system operations for IT equipment and videoconferencing systems; configuration protocols and deployment preparation for newly acquired hardware; comprehensive IT equipment inventory tracking systems utilizing 02PI* numerical designation protocols

• **Consumables and Supply Chain Administration** (10%): Centralized A4 paper contract administration for the European Parliament; coordination and distribution of paper supplies to Members' offices across Brussels and Strasbourg operational locations

• **Legal and Contractual Compliance** (2%): Copyright licensing contract management and administration across Luxembourg and Belgium jurisdictions

10A5010.name: 10A5010
original_activities: Manage multifunctional copiers (MFDs) ensuring printing, copying and scanning facilities, optimising printing resources. Manage budget, leasing and logistic activities related to MFDs for the European Parliament.
Modernise and optimise the multifunction device fleet and improve the digital work environment with digital scanning workflows.
Manage A4 paper contract for the European Parliament. Order and distribute A4 paper for Members in Brussels and Strasbourg.
Manage copyrights contracts in Luxembourg and Belgium.
Produce statistics, including relevant indicators. Collaborate with EMAS in the field of MFDs printing.
Facilitate printing in mobile working environment.
10A5010.refined_description: Unit 10A5010 operates as a specialized operational management unit within Direction 10A of DG ITEC, serving as the European Parliament's primary hub for multifunctional device (MFD) infrastructure, printing services, and associated resource optimization across all institutional locations. The unit functions as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while maintaining direct coordination with sibling units (10A10, 10A1010, 10A1020, 10A20, 10A2010, and others) to deliver integrated end-user services and digital workplace solutions. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the full lifecycle management of the Parliament's MFD fleet—including provision, maintenance, and optimization of printing, copying, and scanning facilities—coupled with complete responsibility for budget allocation, equipment leasing agreements, and logistical coordination of these critical resources across Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg operational sites. Beyond core infrastructure management, Unit 10A5010 actively drives strategic modernization initiatives through implementation of advanced digital scanning workflows that enhance operational efficiency and reduce paper dependency, while simultaneously managing the Parliament's A4 paper supply contract and coordinating the ordering and distribution of materials to Members across multiple locations. The unit additionally administers copyright and licensing contracts applicable to Luxembourg and Belgium jurisdictions, produces comprehensive statistical analyses and performance indicators for MFD operations, collaborates with the EMAS environmental management framework on sustainability metrics, and facilitates printing capabilities within the Parliament's mobile working environment to support flexible workforce arrangements. Through these integrated activities, Unit 10A5010 directly advances DG ITEC's mission of delivering efficient end-user services and digital workplace management while maintaining operational continuity and environmental responsibility across the Parliament's multi-site infrastructure.
10A5010.refined_activity_summary:
• **MFD Fleet Management & Infrastructure Operations** (60%): Comprehensive management of multifunctional copiers ensuring printing, copying, and scanning facility provision across all institutional locations; optimization of printing resource allocation; administration of budgets, leasing agreements, and logistical coordination of MFD infrastructure

• **Digital Transformation & Fleet Modernization** (20%): Modernization and optimization of the multifunction device fleet; advancement of the digital work environment through implementation of advanced digital scanning workflows that reduce paper dependency and enhance operational efficiency

• **Consumables & Materials Management** (10%): Management of A4 paper contracts for the European Parliament; coordination of ordering and distribution of paper supplies to Members in Brussels and Strasbourg locations

• **Regulatory Compliance & Rights Management** (5%): Administration of copyright and licensing contracts applicable to Luxembourg and Belgium jurisdictions

• **Analytics, Environmental Integration & Mobile Workforce Support** (5%): Production of statistical analyses and performance indicators relating to MFD operations; collaborative engagement with EMAS environmental management framework on sustainability metrics; facilitation of printing capabilities within the Parliament's mobile working environment

10A5020.name: 10A5020
original_activities: Support all users in MUST for the request related to the IT equipment.
Manage the full cycle of IT assets from the acquisition to the final delivery in the European Parliament.
Support other activities related to end-users (like comoves) in close collaboration with other teams.
Manage the inventory of the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, in particular supporting the decommissioning of obsolete assets.
10A5020.refined_description: Unit 10A5020 operates as a specialized IT asset management and end-user support function within Direction 10A of the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity (DG ITEC), serving as a critical operational component for the European Parliament's digital infrastructure and workplace technology services. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management of information technology resources throughout their complete lifecycle—from initial acquisition through final disposition—while simultaneously providing direct technical support to end-users across the institution. Unit 10A5020 functions as a foundational enabler of institutional IT service delivery, systematically managing user support requests, coordinating asset procurement and deployment, facilitating workplace transitions, and stewarding technology inventory governance. The unit's integrated approach to asset stewardship and end-user services positions it as essential to DG ITEC's broader organizational goals, with all activities executed through close collaboration with peer units within the Direction to maintain seamless, integrated service delivery across the European Parliament's technology infrastructure.
10A5020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct User Support and Equipment Services** (40%): Provision of comprehensive technical assistance to all MUST (Management of User Service Technologies) platform users regarding IT equipment requests, ensuring responsive resolution and seamless access to technology resources across the European Parliament

• **IT Asset Lifecycle Management** (30%): Administration of the complete technology asset management cycle encompassing acquisition, procurement coordination, deployment logistics, and institutional distribution of IT equipment throughout the Parliament's operational footprint

• **End-User Mobility and Collaborative Support Services** (20%): Facilitation of workplace-related activities including employee relocations, office moves (comoves), and associated end-user transitions through coordinated collaboration with complementary organizational teams within Direction 10A

• **Inventory Management and Asset Decommissioning Governance** (10%): Stewardship of DG ITEC's technology inventory systems with specialized focus on the planned decommissioning, retirement, and responsible disposition of obsolete and end-of-life IT assets

10B.name: 10B
original_activities: Manage, coordinate and animate the life, work, activities and projects of Directorate and its units in line with mission, strategy and priorities. Define governance frameworks, project management methodologies. Ensure cybersecurity resilience and compliance.

Manage and coordinate relations with internal/external stakeholders and ensure the Directorate's contribution to the various internal and external ICT bodies (Bureau, ICT Working Group, Cabinet of the SG, ICDT, IICB etc.).
Organise, coordinate, monitor and support transversal IT Governance tasks of ITEC Change Management Board (CMB), Investment Panel (IP) and manage the Architecture Standards Design Board (ASB).
10B.refined_description: Unit 10B functions as the strategic directorate management and ICT governance hub within the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), operating at the first hierarchical level beneath the Directorate-General and exercising supervisory oversight across four subordinate operational units (10B10, 10B20, 10B30, and 10B40). The unit serves as the foundational governance architecture that enables its constituent units to execute specialized functions while maintaining alignment with DG ITEC's strategic directives and the European Parliament's institutional objectives. Unit 10B's integrated mission encompasses three interconnected functional domains that collectively operationalize coherent ICT decision-making and architectural standards across the Parliament's information technology ecosystem. Through these coordinated activities—encompassing comprehensive directorate lifecycle management, stakeholder relations and interinstitutional coordination, and transversal IT governance functions—Unit 10B establishes governance frameworks, maintains cybersecurity resilience, ensures regulatory compliance, facilitates strategic alignment, and enables integrated governance mechanisms that support the European Parliament's ICT infrastructure and organizational priorities.
10B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Management, Strategic Governance & Cybersecurity Oversight** (50%): Comprehensive management, coordination, and animation of the Directorate's lifecycle, work, activities, and projects in alignment with institutional mission, strategy, and organizational priorities; definition and implementation of governance frameworks and project management methodologies; establishment and maintenance of cybersecurity resilience protocols; assurance of full regulatory compliance across all ICT operations and subordinate units (10B10, 10B20, 10B30, 10B40).

• **Stakeholder Relations & Interinstitutional Coordination** (30%): Management and coordination of relations with both internal and external stakeholders; ensuring the Directorate's substantive contribution to and active participation in multiple critical institutional ICT governance bodies, including the Bureau, ICT Working Group, Cabinet of the Secretary-General, ICDT (Interinstitutional ICT Committee), and IICB (Interinstitutional Information and Communication Technology Board); facilitating inter-institutional engagement and collaborative decision-making processes.

• **Transversal IT Governance & Architectural Standards Administration** (20%): Organization, coordination, monitoring, and provision of operational support for the ITEC Change Management Board (CMB), Investment Panel (IP), and Architecture Standards Design Board (ASB); ensuring consistent application of architectural standards and change management protocols; managing integrated governance mechanisms across the European Parliament's technology infrastructure and subordinate operational units.

10B10.name: 10B10
original_activities: Coordinate cybersecurity efforts across the European Parliament to safeguard ICT systems, monitor emerging cyber threats and implement effective response mechanisms. Ensure the secretariat of the InterDG on Cybersecurity.
Develop, revise and maintain a coherent cybersecurity policy framework that aligns with the latest regulations and Bureau decisions, transforming policies into practical standards and guidelines.
Design and deliver targeted training programs, awareness campaigns and engagement activities to equip all personnel with the necessary knowledge and skills to recognise and respond to cyber threats.
Work closely with the CISO and proximity support services to mentor IT professionals and champion cybersecurity best practices within the Parliament community.
10B10.refined_description:
**

Unit 10B10 operates as the Cybersecurity Coordination and Policy Unit within Direction 10B (Strategic Directorate Management and ICT Governance) of DG ITEC, serving as the European Parliament's central hub for integrated cybersecurity governance and institutional resilience. As a specialized leaf node functioning without subordinate units, 10B10 executes a strategically balanced mission across four interconnected operational domains that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation. The unit's primary responsibilities encompass: (1) coordinating comprehensive cybersecurity efforts across the Parliament's ICT infrastructure through real-time threat monitoring and effective response mechanism implementation, including stewardship of the InterDG on Cybersecurity secretariat—a critical cross-institutional coordination body; (2) developing, revising, and maintaining coherent cybersecurity policy frameworks that translate regulatory requirements and Bureau decisions into actionable standards and guidelines applicable institution-wide; (3) designing and delivering targeted training programs, awareness campaigns, and engagement initiatives that equip all Parliament personnel with requisite knowledge and threat-response capabilities; and (4) collaborating with the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and proximity support services through mentoring activities that foster cybersecurity best practices and professional development within the Parliament community. Organizationally, Unit 10B10 operates collaboratively with sibling units 10B20, 10B30, and 10B40 under the strategic direction of parent unit 10B to advance cohesive institutional ICT governance objectives.

**
10B10.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Cybersecurity Coordination, Threat Management & Institutional Governance** (40%): Coordinate comprehensive cybersecurity efforts across the European Parliament to safeguard ICT systems; monitor emerging cyber threats and implement effective response mechanisms in real-time; serve as secretariat operations steward for the InterDG on Cybersecurity cross-institutional coordination body

• **Policy Framework Development, Regulatory Compliance & Standards Translation** (30%): Develop, revise, and maintain coherent cybersecurity policy frameworks aligned with latest regulatory requirements and Bureau decisions; transform strategic institutional policies into practical operational standards and guidelines applicable across all Parliament operations

• **Personnel Training, Security Awareness & Capability Development** (25%): Design and deliver targeted training programs and awareness campaigns; conduct engagement activities that equip all institutional personnel with essential knowledge and skills to recognize and respond to cyber threats

• **Professional Mentorship, Best Practice Leadership & CISO Collaboration** (5%): Collaborate with the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and proximity support services; mentor IT professionals; champion and advance cybersecurity best practices throughout the Parliament community

10B20.name: 10B20
original_activities: Unify, manage and evolve the IT governance framework, policies and processes providing central IT governance services for how the European Parliament manages, and uses its information and communication technology resources.

Ensure a better coordination of architectural standards, obsolescence management, pragmatic assessment of the impact of the various technological decisions and strengthening the coordination between domains.
10B20.refined_description: Unit 10B20 functions as the central IT governance and standards coordination hub within Direction 10B of DG ITEC, positioned at the second hierarchical level of the European Parliament's information and communication technology governance structure. Operating as a leaf node without subordinate units while maintaining lateral coordination with sibling units 10B10, 10B30, and 10B40 under the strategic oversight of parent Direction 10B, the unit bears primary responsibility for unifying, managing, and continuously evolving the comprehensive IT governance framework, policies, and processes that establish the institutional standards and foundational procedures through which the European Parliament manages and utilizes its information and communication technology resources. Allocating 56% of its documented operational workload to this core mission, the unit provides centralized IT governance services essential to institutional operations. Complementing this foundational governance mission, the unit dedicates 44% of its capacity to ensuring enhanced coordination of architectural standards across technological domains, systematically managing technology obsolescence through structured assessment protocols, conducting pragmatic evaluations of technological decision impacts, and strengthening inter-domain coordination mechanisms to facilitate integrated technology governance. These two complementary and interdependent operational domains work synergistically to establish coherent IT governance architecture while maintaining operational flexibility, cross-functional alignment, and strategic coherence across all institutional technology infrastructure and decision-making processes.
10B20.refined_activity_summary:
• IT Governance Framework Development and Management (56%): Unification, management, and continuous evolution of the comprehensive IT governance framework, policies, and processes; provision of centralized IT governance services establishing institutional standards and foundational procedures for the European Parliament's management and utilization of information and communication technology resources

• Architectural Coordination and Technology Lifecycle Management (44%): Enhanced coordination of architectural standards across technological domains; systematic management of technology obsolescence through structured assessment protocols; pragmatic evaluation of technological decision impacts; strengthening of inter-domain coordination mechanisms to ensure coherent, strategically aligned technology governance and integrated infrastructure optimization across institutional operations

10B30.name: 10B30
original_activities: Serve as the central IT Program and Project Management Office, oversee the global portfolio of IT programs and projects, including demand management, capacity assessment, budgetary monitoring, and provide the secretariat of the Investment Panel.
Govern the IT assets portfolio, ensure that software assets are compliant, optimised and well-governed, manage cloud assets transparently according to FinOps principles.
Develop and apply the IT compliance framework of the Parliament, ensuring compliance across compliance domains (data protection, cybersecurity, AI, digital accessibility and interoperability).
Ensure framework, Enterprise Licence Agreement (ELA) and other contract management for interinstitutional and European Parliament-specific contracts for software licences and cloud subscriptions.
Host the ITEC Data Protection Coordination cell, support ITEC controllers in ensuring the fulfilment of their legal obligations in terms of transparency and risk assessments.
Ensure effective strategic IT change management through providing the professional secretariat of the Change Management Board.
Develop and evangelise the ENGAGE project and portfolio management methodology of the European Parliament, provide training and certification.
10B30.refined_description: Unit 10B30 functions as the central governance, strategic management, and coordination hub for information technology operations within the European Parliament, operating as a second-level organizational unit under Directorate 10B within DG ITEC. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses IT portfolio governance and investment management, IT assets and contract lifecycle management, compliance framework development and data protection coordination, and strategic change management with standardized methodology institutionalization—collectively representing a 100% workload allocation across seven interconnected functional domains. As the central IT Program and Project Management Office (20% allocation), Unit 10B30 serves as the primary steward of IT investment prioritization and resource allocation, overseeing the global portfolio of IT programs and projects through demand management, capacity assessment, budgetary monitoring, and provision of essential secretariat services to the Investment Panel. The unit simultaneously governs the IT assets portfolio (15% allocation) by ensuring software assets achieve compliance standards and optimization targets while managing cloud assets with transparency according to FinOps principles, thereby establishing cost-effective and accountable cloud infrastructure management. Unit 10B30 develops, applies, and maintains the Parliament's comprehensive IT compliance framework (15% allocation) across critical domains including data protection, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence governance, digital accessibility, and interoperability standards—ensuring regulatory alignment and institutional risk mitigation. The unit manages and administers framework agreements, Enterprise Licence Agreements, and specialized contract governance (15% allocation) for both interinstitutional and Parliament-specific software licences and cloud subscriptions, positioning itself as the central procurement governance authority for technology services. Unit 10B30 hosts the ITEC Data Protection Coordination cell (15% allocation), providing specialized support to ITEC data controllers in fulfilling legal obligations regarding transparency and risk assessments under applicable data protection regulations. The unit ensures effective strategic IT change management (10% allocation) through professional secretariat services to the Change Management Board, enabling coordinated technology transformation initiatives across the institution, while developing, promoting, and certifying the ENGAGE project and portfolio management methodology (10% allocation) through institutional training programs to institutionalize standardized management practices. These activities collectively position Unit 10B30 as a critical governance intermediary and strategic management function within the Parliament's digital transformation infrastructure, operating in coordination with sibling units 10B10, 10B20, and 10B40 under the strategic direction of parent unit 10B.
10B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Portfolio and Investment Governance (20%)**: Central IT Program and Project Management Office operations providing global portfolio oversight, demand management, capacity assessment, budgetary monitoring, and Investment Panel secretariat provision—establishing strategic control over IT resource allocation, project prioritization, and investment stewardship

• **Asset and Contract Lifecycle Management (30%)**: IT assets portfolio governance ensuring software compliance, optimization standards, and cloud asset management under FinOps principles (15%), combined with development and administration of Enterprise Licence Agreements and interinstitutional contracts for software licenses and cloud subscriptions (15%)—creating comprehensive technology procurement governance, asset optimization, and cost accountability

• **Compliance Framework and Data Protection Coordination (30%)**: Development and application of comprehensive IT compliance framework spanning data protection, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence governance, digital accessibility, and interoperability standards (15%), alongside hosting the ITEC Data Protection Coordination cell to support data controllers in fulfilling transparency and risk assessment obligations under applicable regulations (15%)—establishing institutional regulatory alignment and risk mitigation infrastructure

• **Strategic Change Management and Methodology Institutionalization (20%)**: Strategic IT change management through professional secretariat services to the Change Management Board, enabling coordinated technology transformation initiatives (10%), combined with development, promotion, and certification of the ENGAGE project and portfolio management methodology through institutional training programs (10%)—institutionalizing structured change governance and standardized management practices across the organization

10B40.name: 10B40
original_activities: Support the identification and management of risks and the maintenance of key DG ITEC risks in a dedicated register - embed risks into management practices.
Ensure the regular reviews of cybersecurity controls at Institution-level and ensure that gaps identified regarding targets are addressed timely.
Ensure the DG ITEC ex-post control and internal control functions and ensure regular reporting of the exceptions identified and the implementation of mitigating measures.
Support the identification and management of cyber risks and the maintenance of key Parliament's cyber risks in a dedicated register.
Develop Business Continuity arrangements of DG ITEC, including the maintenance of the organisational Business Continuity Plan and the continuity exercising.
Ensure the management, coordination and animation of the unit, and the coherence of the activities of the unit with the mission of the Directorate.
10B40.refined_description: Unit 10B40 is the Risk Management, Cybersecurity Governance, and Business Continuity Centre of Excellence within DG ITEC, operating at the second hierarchical level under Direction 10B, which provides strategic directorate management and ICT governance oversight for the European Parliament. The unit functions as a specialized Centre of Excellence responsible for establishing and maintaining comprehensive risk governance frameworks and institutional control mechanisms across the Directorate-General, while operating as a peer entity alongside sibling units 10B10, 10B20, and 10B30 within the broader strategic governance framework of Direction 10B. Unit 10B40's primary mission encompasses the systematic identification, assessment, and management of institutional risks across both general organizational and cybersecurity domains, with particular emphasis on embedding risk management practices into operational frameworks and maintaining dedicated institutional risk registers to ensure compliance with institutional governance requirements. The unit's activities collectively address enterprise-wide and specialized cybersecurity risk landscapes, ensure regular Institution-level cybersecurity control reviews with timely gap remediation, execute ex-post and internal control functions with systematic exception reporting and mitigation implementation, develop and maintain Business Continuity arrangements including organizational continuity planning and exercising protocols, and ensure unit management and strategic coordination to maintain coherence with Direction 10B's mission objectives. These integrated governance and control activities embed risk management into operational practices and ensure institutional operational resilience across the Parliament's ICT infrastructure and organizational framework.
10B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Risk Identification and Register Management** (46%): Support identification and management of organizational risks with dedicated DG ITEC risk register maintenance to embed risks into management practices (30%), and identification and management of Parliament-wide cyber risks with dedicated cyber risk register maintenance (16%), establishing systematic risk governance across the institution.

• **Cybersecurity and Control Assurance** (37%): Ensure regular reviews of cybersecurity controls at Institution-level with timely gap remediation addressing identified targets (20%), and execute DG ITEC ex-post control and internal control functions with systematic exception reporting and implementation of mitigating measures (17%), maintaining institutional compliance and control effectiveness.

• **Business Continuity Planning and Organizational Resilience** (12%): Develop and maintain DG ITEC Business Continuity arrangements, including organizational Business Continuity Plan maintenance and continuity exercising protocols, ensuring organizational operational resilience and institutional continuity capacity.

• **Unit Management and Strategic Alignment** (5%): Manage unit operations, coordinate activities with sibling units, and ensure organizational coherence with Direction 10B's mission objectives and broader institutional governance framework.

10C.name: 10C
original_activities: Ensure the management and coordination of the units and services within the Directorate, in alignment with our mission. Ensure the continuity of service operations and team cohesion.
Ensure the effective implementation of operational and strategic decisions, as well as the coherence of the Directorate's internal processes, and oversee organizational changes.
Maintain relationships with client Directorates-General as well as with political and administrative authorities. Represent the Directorate in inter-institutional committees or working groups.
Participate in defining the strategic direction of the Directorate-General, and make decisions regarding strategic IT choices on both managerial and technical levels.
Provide support for technology watch, the initiation and monitoring of innovative projects, and the exploitation of information derived from technological innovation.
Manage the risks inherent to the Directorate's activities.
Fulfil the role of delegated authorizing officer.
10C.refined_description:
Unit 10C functions as the strategic Direction within the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the primary operational, governance, and coordination hub for the European Parliament's ICT infrastructure, digital transformation initiatives, and institutional technology strategy. The unit exercises hierarchical oversight of 18 subordinate units organized across two primary branches (10C1x and 10C2x series)—specifically encompassing Units 10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, 10C2010, and twelve additional specialized units—while maintaining lateral coordination with sibling units (10-10, 10-20, 10-2010, 10A, 10B, and two additional units) within the broader DG ITEC structure. Unit 10C's core operational mandate encompasses comprehensive internal directorate management and coordination, effective implementation of operational and strategic decisions, maintenance of critical stakeholder relationships with client Directorates-General and political/administrative authorities, participation in strategic governance and institutional decision-making regarding ICT direction, support for technology innovation and advancement initiatives, management of operational risks, and fulfillment of delegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial and administrative accountability. The unit serves as the official representative of the Directorate in inter-institutional committees and working groups that shape European Parliament ICT policy and governance frameworks, ensuring alignment of all subordinate units with the Parliament's ICT mission objectives while maintaining service operation continuity, team cohesion, and coherence of internal processes across all organizational domains. Through its multifaceted governance role, Unit 10C ensures institutional competitiveness by exploiting technological innovation information, initiating and monitoring innovative projects, and overseeing organizational transformation initiatives that impact the Directorate's structural and functional architecture.

---

##
10C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Directorate Management & Organizational Coordination** (30%): Ensures comprehensive management and coordination of all 18 subordinate units and services within the Directorate (Units 10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, 10C2010, and twelve additional units); maintains alignment with organizational mission objectives; preserves continuity of service operations and team cohesion across all operational domains; coordinates with lateral sibling units (10-10, 10-20, 10-2010, 10A, 10B, and additional units) within DG ITEC.

• **Operational & Strategic Implementation** (25%): Guarantees effective execution of operational and strategic decisions; ensures coherence of internal processes; oversees organizational changes and transformation initiatives affecting the Directorate's structural and functional architecture; manages organizational transformation to maintain institutional effectiveness.

• **External Stakeholder Relations & Institutional Representation** (15%): Maintains strategic relationships with client Directorates-General; engages with political and administrative authorities; serves as official Directorate representative in inter-institutional committees and working groups; facilitates inter-organizational coordination; shapes European Parliament ICT policy and governance frameworks.

• **Strategic Governance & Innovation Leadership** (20%): Participates in defining the Directorate-General's long-term strategic direction and institutional vision (10%); determines authoritative strategic IT choices at both managerial and technical levels; supports technology watch activities (10%); initiates and monitors innovative projects; exploits information derived from technological innovation to maintain institutional competitiveness and advance institutional capability.

• **Risk Management & Financial Accountability** (10%): Manages risks inherent to the Directorate's operational activities (7%); fulfills delegated authorizing officer responsibilities ensuring financial and administrative accountability throughout the organization (3%).

10C10.name: 10C10
original_activities: Ensure the business and technical analysis of new demands, align solutions with the ITEC IT Application Strategy Framework (e.g. reuse/buy/build, cybersecurity by design).
Guarantee quality assurance through functional testing, ensure compliance with software development standards, cybersecurity by design, and digital accessibility requirements.
10C10.refined_description: Unit 10C10 operates as a specialized analytical and quality assurance unit within Direction 10C of DG ITEC, functioning as a critical intermediary component within the European Parliament's information and communications technology governance structure. The unit maintains dual primary responsibilities that operate in coordinated sequence: conducting comprehensive business and technical analysis of emerging organizational IT demands while ensuring strategic alignment with the ITEC IT Application Strategy Framework, whereby solutions are rigorously evaluated and classified according to strategic acquisition methodologies—including reuse of existing systems, procurement of commercial solutions, or custom development—with cybersecurity-by-design principles systematically embedded throughout the analytical and solution-design process (60% of documented workload); and executing rigorous quality assurance mechanisms through functional testing protocols and systematic verification of compliance with established software development standards, cybersecurity-by-design requirements, and digital accessibility mandates applicable across the Parliament's digital infrastructure (40% of documented workload). As a leaf-node operational unit with no subordinate organizational structures, 10C10 collaborates laterally with sibling units (including 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, 10C2010, and additional peer units) under the strategic direction of parent Direction 10C, collectively contributing to DG ITEC's overarching governance and operational objectives. This dual focus on strategic demand analysis and quality validation ensures that IT investments align with institutional priorities while maintaining technical excellence, security standards, and institutional conformity across the Parliament's digital infrastructure.
10C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Demand Analysis and Solution Architecture** (60%): Comprehensive business and technical analysis of emerging organizational IT demands with explicit alignment to the ITEC IT Application Strategy Framework; systematic evaluation and classification of acquisition strategies (reuse, procurement, or custom development methodologies); integration of cybersecurity-by-design principles throughout the analytical phase and solution-design process.

• **Quality Assurance and Compliance Verification** (40%): Execution of functional testing protocols and quality validation mechanisms; systematic verification of compliance with established software development standards, cybersecurity-by-design requirements, and digital accessibility mandates; assurance of institutional conformity and user inclusivity across the Parliament's digital infrastructure.

10C1010.name: 10C1010
original_activities: Perform business analysis for proposed applications and application changes.
Provide consultations on applicable policies in the design phase of new solutions and identify gaps or overlaps of business requirements.
Design functional architecture of applications and application changes.
Integrate accessibility into the design process following the accessible by design principle.
Attribute execution to the right implementation unit.
Liaise with the GOV and CARE directorates on implementing the architectural governance in the IT landscape.
Coordinate transformations across the IT portfolio with the implementation units in BUSOL and INSO.
10C1010.refined_description: Unit 10C1010 operates as a specialized architectural and business analysis center of excellence within Direction 10C of DG ITEC, functioning as the critical bridge between business requirements and IT solution implementation across the European Parliament's information systems landscape. The unit's core mission encompasses comprehensive business analysis for proposed applications and application changes (20% of workload), which serves as the foundational analytical activity informing all downstream architectural and implementation decisions, complemented by strategic policy consultations during the design phase of new solutions (15% of workload) that explicitly identify gaps and overlaps in business requirements to prevent redundancy and ensure regulatory compliance. The unit simultaneously dedicates 15% of its capacity to designing the functional architecture of applications and application changes, translating business requirements into technically coherent system designs that align with organizational objectives. Demonstrating institutional commitment to inclusive digital services, the unit allocates 15% of its workload to integrating accessibility principles into all design processes through the accessible-by-design methodology, ensuring compliance with European accessibility standards and inclusive service delivery. The unit further assumes critical governance and coordination responsibilities by attributing execution responsibilities to appropriate implementation units (15% of workload) to ensure proper resource allocation and accountability across the IT portfolio, liaising with the GOV and CARE directorates on implementing architectural governance frameworks (10% of workload), and coordinating portfolio-wide transformations with implementation units in the BUSOL and INSO directorates (10% of workload) to ensure synchronized execution and cross-functional integration. As a leaf node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 10C1010 operates as a specialized governance and coordination mechanism within the broader Direction 10C framework, collaborating with sibling units (10C10, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, 10C2010, and twelve additional units) to deliver integrated IT governance and architectural services across the European Parliament.
10C1010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Business Analysis and Requirements Definition** (35%): Perform comprehensive business analysis for proposed applications and application changes (20%); provide strategic policy consultations during solution design phases to identify critical requirements gaps, overlaps, and regulatory compliance risks (15%)

• **Architectural Design and Accessibility Integration** (30%): Design functional architecture of applications and application changes, translating business requirements into technically coherent system designs (15%); integrate accessibility requirements into the design process following accessible-by-design principles to ensure compliance with European accessibility standards and inclusive service delivery (15%)

• **Implementation Governance, Attribution, and Cross-Directorate Coordination** (35%): Attribute execution responsibilities to appropriate implementation units to ensure proper resource allocation and accountability across the IT portfolio (15%); liaise with GOV and CARE directorates to implement architectural governance frameworks within the IT landscape (10%); coordinate portfolio-wide transformations with implementation units in BUSOL and INSO directorates to ensure synchronized execution and cross-functional integration (10%)

10C1020.name: 10C1020
original_activities: Identify candidate business commercial of the shelf solutions, integrate them into the IT landscape, and ensure handover.
Produce and update technical analyses of applications and application changes, ensuring technical coherence between solutions in each domain.
Harmonise user interface experience across the application portfolio.
Provide consultancy services to Directorates-General for project management activities for the implementation of new projects/studies.
Support decision process in the context of Reuse before Buy, before Build policy by performing preliminary market research.
Support to managing business software budget.
10C1020.refined_description:
**

Unit 10C1020 operates as a specialized technical solutions integration and portfolio management unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical institutional bridge between commercial solutions and the European Parliament's IT infrastructure. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy with no subordinate units, 10C1020 executes a multifaceted mission encompassing the identification and integration of candidate commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) business solutions into the institutional IT landscape while ensuring comprehensive handover protocols to operational teams (36% of workload). The unit produces and continuously updates technical analyses evaluating applications and application changes to maintain technical coherence and consistency across solution domains (25% of workload), while simultaneously standardizing user interface experience across the application portfolio to enhance institutional consistency and end-user accessibility (15% of workload). Operating collaboratively with sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1030, 10C20, 10C2010, and others) under the strategic direction of parent unit 10C, the unit provides specialized consultancy services to other Directorates-General regarding project management activities for new project and study implementations (12% of workload), supports institutional procurement decision-making through preliminary market research activities aligned with the European Parliament's "Reuse before Buy, before Build" policy framework (6% of workload), and provides essential administrative and financial support for managing business software budgetary allocations and oversight (6% of workload). Through these interconnected technical governance, integration, and advisory activities, Unit 10C1020 ensures technical coherence, cost optimization, and standardized user experience across the institutional application ecosystem.

**
10C1020.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Commercial Solutions Integration & Deployment** (36%): Identify candidate commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) business solutions, integrate them into the European Parliament's IT landscape, and ensure comprehensive handover protocols to operational teams

• **Technical Governance & Portfolio Coherence** (25%): Produce and continuously update technical analyses of applications and application changes to ensure technical coherence and consistency across solution domains

• **User Experience Standardization & Harmonization** (15%): Harmonize user interface experience across the application portfolio to enhance institutional consistency and end-user accessibility

• **Strategic Consultancy & Advisory Services** (12%): Provide specialized consultancy services to other Directorates-General regarding project management activities for new project and study implementations

• **Procurement Strategy & Policy Support** (6%): Support institutional procurement decision-making through preliminary market research activities aligned with the "Reuse before Buy, before Build" policy framework

• **Budget Administration & Financial Support** (6%): Manage and provide administrative and financial support for business software budgetary allocations and oversight

10C1030.name: 10C1030
original_activities: Deliver specific reports to the software responsible (functional, security and accessibility).
Ensure continuous enhancing of quality of proposed IT solutions.
Help make applications safe and accessible by design by giving security and accessibility advice during the design phase.
Run systemic analyses of solutions.
10C1030.refined_description: Unit 10C1030 operates as a specialized quality assurance and governance unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical control and advisory mechanism for ensuring the integrity, security, and accessibility of IT solutions across the European Parliament's institutional infrastructure. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, it serves as an essential governance mechanism within the broader IT service delivery framework, working in close coordination with its parent Direction 10C and alongside parallel units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C20, 10C2010, and additional sibling units) to ensure comprehensive institutional compliance and technological excellence. The unit's comprehensive quality management framework encompasses four core activities that collectively represent the full spectrum of quality oversight throughout the software development lifecycle: delivering detailed functional, security, and accessibility reports to software-responsible parties (50% of workload), which constitutes the unit's dominant operational focus and primary accountability mechanism; continuously enhancing and advancing the quality standards of proposed IT solutions through systematic evaluation and optimization (20% of workload); providing proactive security and accessibility guidance during the design phase to embed safety and accessibility principles from inception and prevent downstream remediation requirements (20% of workload); and conducting systemic analyses of IT solutions to identify structural vulnerabilities, interdependencies, and optimization opportunities across the organization's technology portfolio (10% of workload). Through this evidence-based, comprehensive allocation of operational capacity, Unit 10C1030 ensures that all information systems meet stringent European institutional standards for functionality, cybersecurity, digital accessibility, and systemic coherence while promoting "secure and accessible by design" principles throughout the technology infrastructure.
10C1030.refined_activity_summary:
• **Quality Assurance and Reporting** (50%): Delivery of comprehensive functional, security, and accessibility reports to software-responsible parties, establishing documented compliance, performance accountability, and critical oversight mechanisms across the IT solution portfolio—representing the unit's primary operational focus and dominant workload allocation.

• **Quality Enhancement and Continuous Improvement** (20%): Systematic enhancement of IT solution quality standards through ongoing evaluation, optimization, and advancement of technical and operational parameters, maintaining institutional technological excellence through refinement and best-practice implementation.

• **Design-Phase Security and Accessibility Advisory Services** (20%): Provision of specialized security and accessibility guidance during the application design phase to embed safety and accessibility principles from inception, ensuring "secure and accessible by design" principles are architected into solutions and preventing post-implementation remediation requirements.

• **Systemic Solution Analysis and Pattern Identification** (10%): Execution of comprehensive systemic analyses of IT solutions to evaluate structural integrity, identify interdependencies, structural vulnerabilities, and optimization opportunities across the technology portfolio, generating evidence-based insights for organizational learning and portfolio-wide improvement initiatives.

10C20.name: 10C20
original_activities: Ensure centralized financial initiation of DG ITEC: expenditure commitments on specific contracts and PRESTO purchase orders, issuance of purchase orders, VAT certificates, logistics for approval of payment of invoices.

Ensure the financial and budgetary coordination of general management: establishment and monitoring of the budget, reporting, follow-up with the Court of Auditors, transfers and transfers, authorizations and subdelegations.
Ensure the centralized operational initiation of the Strategic Procurement and Organizational Management Department (SOMA).
Ensure the logistics of signing specific contracts and their amendments: verification of the conformity of documents, the quality of the signatories, bank guarantee requests.
10C20.refined_description: Unit 10C20 functions as the centralized financial and administrative operations hub within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as a critical leaf-node operational unit that provides essential transactional and administrative infrastructure for DG ITEC's financial governance, procurement compliance, and operational sustainability. The unit's primary mission encompasses four integrated functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, ensuring seamless coordination between financial management, budgetary compliance, and procurement operations across the organization. Operating within the broader Direction 10C framework and coordinating with thirteen sibling units (including 10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C2010, and eight additional units), Unit 10C20 serves as a critical coordination point that integrates financial initiation processes with strategic procurement objectives and general management operations. The unit's activities span from transactional processing of expenditure commitments and invoice approvals through to high-level budgetary governance, audit coordination, and contract administration logistics, ensuring that all financial operations maintain both operational efficiency and regulatory compliance. Through its interconnected functional responsibilities, Unit 10C20 ensures the financial integrity of DG ITEC's procurement and management activities while maintaining alignment between centralized procurement strategies, organizational management objectives, and established budgetary frameworks.
10C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Initiation and Expenditure Commitment Processing** (43%): Centralized management of DG ITEC expenditure commitments on specific contracts and PRESTO purchase orders; issuance of purchase orders and VAT certificates; comprehensive logistics coordination for invoice approval and payment processing workflows

• **Financial and Budgetary Governance** (25%): Establishment and continuous monitoring of budget frameworks; generation and analysis of financial reporting; systematic coordination of follow-up activities with the European Court of Auditors; administration of budget transfers, reallocations, authorizations, and subdelegations

• **Strategic Procurement Operational Initiation** (17%): Centralized operational initiation and coordination services provided to the Strategic Procurement and Organizational Management Department (SOMA), ensuring alignment between procurement strategies and organizational management objectives

• **Contract Administration and Compliance Logistics** (15%): Management of contract signing and amendment processes; verification of document conformity; validation of signatory credentials and authority; processing and management of bank guarantee requests for specific contracts

10C2010.name: 10C2010
original_activities: Serve as the central hub for AI expertise and AI innovation within the European Parliament. Drive AI adoption across the European Parliament, fostering a culture of AI and data analysis literacy and ensure inter-institutional collaboration.

Ensure that Parliament's services receive technical guidance in implementing AI related projects. Set up suitable oversight, coordination and assessment practices when developing or acquiring AI models or solutions.
Guide, validate, promote and monitor any initiatives using Artificial intelligence carried out across Parliament's administration.
Support Parliament's Artificial intelligence Governance Board and ensure its secretariat.
10C2010.refined_description:
Unit 10C2010 serves as the European Parliament's dedicated centre of excellence for artificial intelligence governance, strategy, and implementation, operating as a specialized operational leaf-node unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC). The unit functions as an integrated, autonomous team with no subordinate organizational structures while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, and twelve additional units) within the Direction 10C portfolio. Unit 10C2010 executes four interconnected strategic and operational functions that collectively drive Parliament's comprehensive AI transformation agenda: establishing and maintaining the institution's central knowledge repository and innovation catalyst for AI expertise (40% of workload); providing specialized technical guidance and implementing robust oversight frameworks, coordination mechanisms, and assessment protocols for Parliament's services undertaking AI-related projects, including the development and acquisition of AI models and solutions (25% of workload); actively guiding, validating, promoting, and monitoring all artificial intelligence initiatives executed throughout Parliament's administrative structures to ensure strategic alignment and institutional compliance (25% of workload); and providing essential secretariat and administrative support functions to Parliament's Artificial Intelligence Governance Board to ensure operational continuity of formal decision-making processes (10% of workload). Through these integrated activities, Unit 10C2010 fosters institutional AI and data analysis literacy, facilitates inter-institutional collaboration on AI matters, establishes suitable coordination and assessment practices for AI implementation, positions the Parliament as a leader in AI-informed governance, and ensures coherent governance of artificial intelligence deployment across the institution's operations. The unit's work directly supports the strategic direction established by its parent unit 10C and operates in full coordination with the broader technology and innovation portfolio of DG ITEC.

##
10C2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic AI Leadership, Innovation Enablement, and Institutional Transformation** (40%): Establish and maintain the European Parliament's central expertise hub and knowledge repository for artificial intelligence; drive institutional AI adoption and innovation across all parliamentary services; cultivate organizational capacity in AI and data analysis literacy; facilitate inter-institutional collaboration on AI initiatives; position Parliament as a leader in AI-informed governance

• **Technical Guidance, Implementation Support, and Project Oversight** (25%): Deliver specialized technical guidance to Parliament's services implementing AI-related projects; establish and maintain robust oversight mechanisms, coordination frameworks, and assessment protocols; provide governance structures for AI model development and acquisition processes; ensure institutional compliance and quality standards in AI solution implementation

• **AI Initiative Governance, Validation, and Performance Monitoring** (25%): Guide, validate, and promote artificial intelligence initiatives across Parliament's administrative structure; monitor ongoing AI deployment and performance; ensure strategic alignment with institutional standards and governance requirements; maintain comprehensive oversight of all AI-related activities throughout the Parliament

• **Governance Board Support and Institutional Coordination** (10%): Provide administrative and secretariat support to Parliament's Artificial Intelligence Governance Board; facilitate board operations and formal decision-making processes; ensure operational continuity of institutional AI governance mechanisms

10C2020.name: 10C2020
original_activities: Develop and maintain applications built on top of the supported platforms (e.g. SharePoint, SAP, Jahia, Power Platform, UiPath, xWiki, EP GenAI Hub).
Ensure the implementation, and lifecycle management of business and automation platforms (e.g. SharePoint, SAP, Jahia, Power Platform, UiPath, xWiki, EP GenAI Hub).
Collaborate with stakeholders and partner services to align platform capabilities with institutional priorities and evolving needs.
Act as the Power Platform Centre of Excellence to establish best practices, govern usage, and enable colleagues to build secure, compliant, and efficient solutions.
Collaborate with other services to ensure smooth deployment, operation, and support of the platforms and applications managed by AUTOPLAT.
Monitor platform evolution and vendor roadmaps to anticipate changes and guide strategic decisions related to lifecycle and future-proofing.
10C2020.refined_description: Unit 10C2020 (AUTOPLAT) functions as a specialized platform engineering, lifecycle management, and governance unit within Direction 10C of DG ITEC, serving as the European Parliament's primary operational hub for enterprise application development, business and automation platform stewardship, and digital transformation enablement. The unit operates as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy without subordinate structures, while maintaining collaborative relationships with six sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20) and twelve additional peer units under Direction 10C governance. Unit 10C2020's comprehensive mission encompasses the full spectrum of platform operations across a sophisticated technology ecosystem, including strategic development, implementation, operational governance, and continuous modernization of supported enterprise platforms (SharePoint, SAP, Jahia, Power Platform, UiPath, xWiki, and the EP GenAI Hub). The unit functions simultaneously as a cross-institutional technology enabler and governance authority, establishing best practices, controlling usage patterns, and empowering colleagues across the Parliament to develop secure, compliant, and operationally efficient solutions. Through integrated stakeholder collaboration, inter-service coordination, and continuous technology intelligence activities, Unit 10C2020 ensures platform capabilities remain synchronized with institutional priorities, enabling seamless deployment and operational continuity while anticipating technological evolution and informing strategic lifecycle decisions that future-proof the Parliament's technology infrastructure.
10C2020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Application Development and Platform Engineering** (41%): Develop and maintain custom applications and solutions built on supported enterprise platforms (SharePoint, SAP, Jahia, Power Platform, UiPath, xWiki, EP GenAI Hub), delivering institutional business value through technological implementation and continuous enhancement.

• **Platform Implementation and Lifecycle Management** (18%): Ensure comprehensive implementation, deployment, lifecycle management, and operational support of business and automation platforms; coordinate platform and application deployment, operation, and technical support from initial deployment through operational maturity and eventual modernization.

• **Strategic Stakeholder Alignment and Collaborative Governance** (23%): Collaborate with institutional stakeholders and partner services to align platform capabilities with institutional priorities and evolving organizational needs (15%); coordinate cross-service collaboration ensuring seamless deployment, operational continuity, and comprehensive technical support of all managed platforms and applications (8%).

• **Power Platform Centre of Excellence and Governance Leadership** (12%): Establish and maintain Power Platform Centre of Excellence function, defining best practices, governing platform usage patterns, establishing compliance standards, and enabling colleagues to develop secure, compliant, and operationally efficient solutions aligned with institutional standards.

• **Technology Intelligence and Strategic Foresight** (6%): Monitor platform evolution and vendor roadmaps, anticipate technological changes, analyze vendor strategic directions, and provide informed strategic guidance regarding platform lifecycle decisions and institutional future-proofing strategies to ensure long-term technology sustainability and competitiveness.

10C2030.name: 10C2030
original_activities: Design, develop, and maintain custom workflows and applications to support case, service, and operations management.
Administer and configure native ServiceNow modules (e.g. ITSM, Service Catalogue, Flow Designer) and ensure integration with other systems.
Ensure the full lifecycle management of the ServiceNow cloud platform as an enterprise service management solution.
Collaborate with business units to analyse, model, and automate processes using ServiceNow capabilities.
Monitor platform updates and vendor roadmap to guide strategic decisions and align with evolving organisational needs.
Provide technical support and guidance to users and functional administrators.
10C2030.refined_description:
**

Unit 10C2030 operates as a specialized technical unit and enterprise service management platform custodian within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the innovation hub and administration centre for the European Parliament's ServiceNow ecosystem. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive design, development, and maintenance of custom workflows and applications to support case, service, and operations management (25% of workload), representing its core strategic focus area for delivering tailored solutions that directly enable institutional operations. Complementing this development capability, the unit administers and configures native ServiceNow modules—including ITSM, Service Catalogue, and Flow Designer—whilst ensuring seamless integration with complementary organizational systems to maintain technical coherence and interoperability (20% of workload). The unit ensures full lifecycle management and governance of the ServiceNow cloud platform as an enterprise service management solution, maintaining continuous availability, security, and performance optimization (15% of workload), whilst actively monitoring platform updates, vendor roadmap developments, and emerging technological trends to inform strategic decision-making and guide organizational adaptation to evolving service management best practices (15% of workload). The unit actively collaborates with business units across the Parliament to analyse, model, and automate processes leveraging ServiceNow capabilities, thereby translating organizational requirements into technical solutions that drive operational efficiency (15% of workload), and provides essential technical support and guidance to end-users and functional administrators to ensure effective platform adoption and operational continuity (10% of workload). As a leaf-node unit within Direction 10C's governance structure, Unit 10C2030 functions in coordination with its sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, and twelve additional units) to collectively advance DG ITEC's digital transformation and integrated information and communications technology service delivery mandate across the European Parliament.

**
10C2030.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Platform Development and Customization** (25%): Design, develop, and maintain custom workflows and applications supporting case management, service delivery, and operational processes—representing the unit's core strategic focus area for delivering tailored solutions that directly enable institutional operations

• **Platform Administration and Integration** (20%): Administer and configure native ServiceNow modules (ITSM, Service Catalogue, Flow Designer) and ensure seamless integration with complementary organizational systems to maintain technical coherence and interoperability

• **Platform Lifecycle Governance and Strategic Alignment** (30%): Ensure full lifecycle management and governance of the ServiceNow cloud platform as an enterprise service management solution, maintaining continuous availability, security, and performance optimization (15%); monitor platform updates, vendor roadmap developments, and emerging technological trends to inform strategic decision-making and guide organizational adaptation to evolving service management best practices (15%)

• **Business Process Collaboration and Automation** (15%): Collaborate with business units across the Parliament to analyse, model, and automate processes leveraging ServiceNow capabilities, thereby translating organizational requirements into technical solutions that drive operational efficiency

• **User Support and Technical Guidance** (10%): Provide technical support and guidance to end-users and functional administrators to ensure effective platform adoption and operational continuity

10C30.name: 10C30
original_activities: Develop and maintain an extensive portfolio of applications supporting various administrative and corporate processes in the European Parliament (document, conference and interpretation management, human resources, administrative support for Members).
Design, maintain, and modify technical environments for application development (EP-Foundry).
Develop, maintain, and upgrade reusable solutions and components.
Maintain, modify, and upgrade legacy applications, and refactor them using reusable components.
10C30.refined_description: Unit 10C30 functions as a specialized Applications and Development Services unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the European Parliament's primary operational custodian of enterprise application development, deployment, and comprehensive lifecycle management. The unit's integrated mission encompasses the strategic development, deployment, and maintenance of a diverse portfolio of mission-critical applications that directly support the institution's administrative, corporate, and parliamentary operations, including document management systems, conference and interpretation management platforms, human resources information systems, and administrative support tools specifically designed for Members of the European Parliament. Operating as both a service provider and strategic technology steward, Unit 10C30 designs, establishes, maintains, and continuously modifies the technical development environments and infrastructure frameworks collectively known as EP-Foundry, which provides the foundational platform and tooling ecosystem upon which all application development and deployment activities are executed across the institution. The unit concurrently develops, maintains, and progressively enhances standardized reusable solutions and modular architectural components that establish institutional technology standards, promote consistency, reduce redundancy, and facilitate efficiency across the Parliament's information systems landscape. Additionally, Unit 10C30 serves as the steward of legacy applications, encompassing their maintenance, modification, systematic upgrade, and strategic refactoring through the integration of reusable components and contemporary architectural approaches, thereby modernizing the Parliament's technology infrastructure while preserving critical institutional functionality. Through these coordinated and integrated activities, Unit 10C30 directly contributes to the strategic objectives of Direction 10C and the broader DG ITEC mission to provide reliable, efficient, user-centered, and technologically advanced solutions that enable the European Parliament's legislative, administrative, and operational excellence.
10C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Enterprise Application Portfolio Management** (50%): Develop, deploy, and maintain a comprehensive suite of mission-critical applications supporting document management, conference and interpretation services, human resources administration, and administrative support functions for Members of the European Parliament, directly enabling the institution's administrative and parliamentary operations

• **Development Infrastructure and Platform Services** (20%): Design, establish, maintain, and continuously modify the technical development environments and infrastructure frameworks (EP-Foundry) that provide the foundational platform, tooling ecosystem, and technological infrastructure necessary for all application development and deployment activities across the institution

• **Reusable Solutions and Component Architecture** (20%): Develop, maintain, and progressively enhance standardized reusable technological solutions and modular components that establish institutional architectural standards, promote consistency, reduce redundancy, and facilitate operational efficiency across the Parliament's information systems landscape

• **Legacy Application Stewardship and Modernization** (10%): Maintain, modify, systematically upgrade, and strategically refactor legacy applications through the integration of reusable components and contemporary architectural approaches, ensuring continued institutional functionality while advancing technological modernization initiatives

10C3010.name: 10C3010
original_activities: Maintain existing applications and carry out development projects that will directly serve Members of Parliament administrative and financial processes.
Design, maintain, and modify technical environments for application development (EP-Foundry).
Develop, maintain, and upgrade reusable solutions and components.
Maintain, modify, and upgrade legacy applications, and refactor them using reusable components.
10C3010.refined_description: Unit 10C3010 operates as a specialized technical delivery and operations unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical operational nucleus for application development and technical infrastructure supporting the European Parliament's administrative and financial operations. The unit maintains a dual mandate as both a primary operational execution unit and a strategic enabler of parliamentary processes, serving Members of Parliament and institutional operations through comprehensive lifecycle management of applications and technical infrastructure. Positioned as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 10C3010 coordinates with twelve sibling units (including 10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, and 10C20) to collectively deliver comprehensive information and communications technology services across the Parliament, while simultaneously operating within the broader Direction 10C framework to ensure integrated service delivery. The unit's operational model integrates four interconnected functional domains: maintaining and enhancing core parliamentary business systems, establishing foundational development infrastructure through EP-Foundry, developing standardized reusable components to promote architectural consistency, and systematically modernizing legacy systems while preserving institutional knowledge. This multi-dimensional approach ensures continuity of critical parliamentary systems, establishes standardized development practices, reduces technological redundancy across the organization, and balances operational stability with technical modernization objectives.
10C3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Application Delivery and Member Support** (50%): Maintain existing applications and execute development projects that directly serve Members of Parliament administrative and financial processes, representing the unit's primary operational focus and ensuring uninterrupted access to critical parliamentary business systems

• **Technical Infrastructure and Development Environment Management** (20%): Design, maintain, and modify technical development environments encompassed within the EP-Foundry platform initiative, establishing and sustaining foundational infrastructure and standardized development practices necessary for application development initiatives across the organization

• **Reusable Solutions and Components Architecture** (20%): Develop, maintain, and upgrade reusable solutions and components serving as standardized building blocks across the DG ITEC technology portfolio, promoting architectural consistency, reducing system redundancy, and establishing standardized components for organizational use

• **Legacy System Modernization and Refactoring** (10%): Maintain, modify, and upgrade legacy applications while systematically refactoring them to incorporate reusable components developed by the unit, extending operational lifespan, modernizing technical foundations, and leveraging institutional knowledge while addressing technical debt

10C3020.name: 10C3020
original_activities: Maintain and evolve IT solutions automating the core processes of the DG for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences related to organisation and interpretation of meetings, conferences and events.
Maintain and evolve IT solutions that automate processes for governance, long-term planning and crisis management.
Deliver and maintain an enterprise records management system (ERMS) that automates the management of administrative records and documents.
Promote, facilitate and streamline the digitalisation efforts of European Parliament by offering IT solutions for digital signature and electronic documents exchange.
Offer advice and consultation to all European Parliament services related to electronic signatures and trust services in general.
10C3020.refined_description:
Unit 10C3020 operates as a specialized technological solutions unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical operational and strategic enabler of institutional digitalization, operational efficiency, and digital governance across the European Parliament. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, the unit reports directly to Direction 10C and coordinates with its sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, and 12 additional units) to deliver comprehensive information and communications technology governance and infrastructure solutions. The unit's integrated mission encompasses the design, deployment, continuous evolution, and maintenance of enterprise-level IT systems that automate core institutional processes across multiple operational domains, while simultaneously serving as a trusted advisory resource for digital transformation initiatives. Through its multifaceted service portfolio, Unit 10C3020 directly supports the strategic objectives of Direction 10C and contributes substantially to institutional modernization, operational efficiency, compliance frameworks, and digital governance capabilities across the Parliament. The unit's workload allocation reflects its comprehensive scope: 25% dedicated to conference and event logistics automation; 25% to governance, strategic planning, and crisis management systems; 25% to enterprise records and document management infrastructure; 20% to digital signature and electronic documents exchange solutions; and 5% to advisory and consultation services regarding trust services and electronic signature frameworks. By integrating these operational and strategic functions, the unit positions itself as a key institutional enabler of the Parliament's digital transformation agenda while maintaining robust compliance and institutional memory systems.

---

##
10C3020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Conference Logistics and Event Management IT Solutions** (25%): Maintain and evolve IT solutions that automate core processes of the DG for Logistics and Interpretation for Conferences, directly supporting the organization and interpretation of parliamentary meetings, conferences, and events while ensuring seamless institutional event operations.
