• **Governance, Strategic Planning, and Crisis Management Systems** (25%): Maintain and evolve IT solutions that automate institutional governance processes, long-term strategic planning frameworks, and crisis management capabilities, supporting executive decision-making and institutional resilience.

• **Enterprise Records and Document Management Infrastructure** (25%): Deliver and maintain a comprehensive enterprise records management system (ERMS) that automates the management of administrative records and documents across Parliament services, establishing institutional memory and compliance frameworks.

• **Digital Signature and Electronic Documents Exchange Solutions** (20%): Promote, facilitate, and streamline the Parliament's broader digitalization agenda by offering, maintaining, and evolving IT solutions specifically designed for digital signature functionality and secure electronic documents exchange capabilities.

• **Trust Services and Electronic Signature Advisory and Consultation** (5%): Provide strategic advice, consultation, and guidance to all European Parliament services regarding electronic signatures, trust services frameworks, digital authentication matters, and related electronic trust infrastructure.

10C3030.name: 10C3030
original_activities: Develop, enforce, and continuously update development standards and guidelines to ensure adherence to industry best practices, including adoption of DevSecOps principles and practices.
Provide dedicated support to European Parliament development teams throughout Development Support Front Office.
Design, implement, and maintain a robust developer desktop environment to enhance productivity and efficiency.
Maintain and update a portfolio of critical tools used by DG for Infrastructure and Logistics (GPI application portfolio and CoMoVe).
Maintain and update EditCat, a reusable framework to build applications that manages catalogue (EditCat, Closer, European Parliament Library).
Maintain and update a portfolio of critical tools used by Members and Legal Service (CarMEP, SIB, WebAjur).
Define opportunities to re-use developed applications and backends.
10C3030.refined_description: Unit 10C3030 operates as a critical technical enablement and standards governance unit within Direction 10C of DG ITEC, functioning as the European Parliament's primary custodian of development excellence, infrastructure tooling, and application portfolio stewardship across the institution's information systems ecosystem. As a leaf-node operational unit collaborating with sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, and others) within the hierarchical structure, Unit 10C3030 delivers integrated development governance services that establish foundational quality and security frameworks cascading throughout the Parliament's development community. The unit's mission encompasses establishing and continuously evolving comprehensive development standards and DevSecOps best practices that ensure adherence to industry best practices and govern all technology initiatives institution-wide. Complementing this standards leadership, the unit provides direct operational support through dedicated development team assistance integrated within the Development Support Front Office and maintains a robust developer desktop environment infrastructure that enhances organizational productivity and technical efficiency. Unit 10C3030 maintains active stewardship over three distinct application portfolios serving critical institutional functions: the GPI application portfolio and CoMoVe platform utilized by DG Infrastructure and Logistics; the EditCat reusable framework for catalogue-management applications including Closer and the European Parliament Library; and specialized mission-critical tools serving Members and the Legal Service (CarMEP, SIB, and WebAjur). The unit additionally identifies and facilitates opportunities for application and backend component reusability across the institution, promoting architectural coherence and reducing redundant development efforts while maximizing technological investments.
10C3030.refined_activity_summary:
• **Standards Development & Governance** (25%): Develop, enforce, and continuously update comprehensive development standards and guidelines ensuring adherence to industry best practices; establish and cascade DevSecOps principles and practices implementation across all Parliament development activities, creating foundational quality and security governance frameworks

• **Direct Development Support & Infrastructure** (30%): Provide dedicated technical support to European Parliament development teams through the Development Support Front Office (15%); design, implement, and maintain robust developer desktop environment infrastructure (15%) that enhances organizational productivity and operational efficiency for development personnel

• **Critical Application Portfolio Stewardship** (40%): Maintain and update the GPI application portfolio and CoMoVe platform serving DG Infrastructure and Logistics (15%); maintain and update the EditCat reusable framework for catalogue-management applications including Closer and European Parliament Library implementations (15%); maintain and update mission-critical tools serving Members and the Legal Service including CarMEP, SIB, and WebAjur (10%)

• **Architectural Optimization & Application Reusability** (5%): Identify and promote opportunities to re-use developed applications and backend components across the institution, promoting architectural coherence, maximizing technological investments, and reducing redundant development efforts

10C40.name: 10C40
original_activities: Develop and maintain a number of information systems to support legislative processes at every stage.
Enhance existing applications, develop new solutions — prioritizing off-site bespoke development where feasible — and facilitate the integration of acquired applications.
Provide the eLegislate building blocks necessary for the end-to-end digitalisation of legislative workflows.
10C40.refined_description: Unit 10C40 is a specialized technical unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy without subordinate units while collaborating alongside sibling units (10C10, 10C1010, 10C1020, 10C1030, 10C20, and twelve additional peer units). The unit serves as the primary custodian of legislative information systems infrastructure for the European Parliament, with a core mandate encompassing three interconnected functional areas representing 100% of its operational workload. Unit 10C40's foundational responsibility (70% allocation) involves the comprehensive development, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of multiple integrated information systems designed to provide essential technological support for legislative processes across all procedural stages, from initial proposal through final adoption, thereby establishing the critical infrastructure necessary for parliamentary operations. Complementing this infrastructure role, the unit dedicates 20% of its capacity to strategic enhancement of existing applications, development of innovative solutions with prioritization of external bespoke development arrangements where operationally feasible, and facilitation of seamless integration of newly acquired or third-party applications into the Parliament's technological ecosystem. The unit further allocates 10% of its resources to delivering eLegislate building blocks—standardized, reusable digital components and architectural foundations—that serve as essential prerequisites for achieving end-to-end digitalization of legislative workflows throughout the institution. Through these three integrated activities, Unit 10C40 directly supports the strategic objectives of its parent Direction 10C and contributes to DG ITEC's overarching mission of modernizing parliamentary digital infrastructure and enhancing legislative efficiency through technology-enabled processes.
10C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Process Information Systems Infrastructure Development and Maintenance** (70%): Comprehensive development, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of multiple integrated information systems providing essential technological support for legislative processes at every procedural stage from initial proposal through final adoption, establishing foundational infrastructure for parliamentary operations

• **Application Enhancement, Solution Development, and Integration Services** (20%): Strategic enhancement of existing applications through iterative improvement cycles, development of innovative technological solutions with prioritization of external bespoke development delivery models where operationally feasible, and facilitation of seamless integration of newly acquired or third-party applications into the institutional technology ecosystem

• **Digital Workflow Standardization and eLegislate Architecture Delivery** (10%): Provision of standardized eLegislate building blocks, reusable digital components, and architectural foundations essential for achieving comprehensive end-to-end digitalization of legislative workflows across the European Parliament

10C4010.name: 10C4010
original_activities: Develop, maintain, and support IT applications in the legislative domain.
Ensure all applications stay aligned with the European Parliament's technology clock and DG ITEC's strategies on reuse before buy before build, cloud adoption and cybersecurity.
Provide 3rd level support for applications within the service's scope.
Design and implement new solutions to meet emerging business needs or replace obsolete systems, aligned with defined investment priorities.
Drive the evolution of the eLegislate programme by delivering building blocks, shared services, and technical coordination across stakeholders.

Unit 10C50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as the European Parliament's primary nexus for integrated data management, digital governance, and institutional information architecture. The unit's strategic mission encompasses four interconnected operational pillars that collectively address the Parliament's critical information technology and communications infrastructure requirements. Unit 10C50 dedicates 50% of its workload to establishing and maintaining coherent data governance frameworks and stewarding specialized data platforms—specifically allocating 25% to implementing corporate data governance policies, roles, and processes across organizational stakeholders, while dedicating an additional 25% to overseeing data management operations for mission-critical platforms including the European Parliament Data Platform (EPDP), Open Data Services/Portal, and Codict system. The unit commits 25% of its capacity to maintaining and continuously evolving the European Parliament's extensive public-facing digital infrastructure through comprehensive support of the Europarl website portfolio, ensuring institutional accessibility and information dissemination to external audiences and stakeholders. Additionally, Unit 10C50 allocates 30% of resources to technical document management and institutional compliance functions—specifically 15% supporting document indexing and transformation processes to enhance information accessibility and organizational knowledge management, and 15% ensuring adherence to inter-institutional commitments regarding data and document interoperability standards with external institutional partners. As a leaf-node unit within Direction 10C's organizational structure, Unit 10C50 operates collaboratively with 15+ sibling specialized units to deliver comprehensive information technology and communications services, positioning itself as a critical infrastructure component for both internal governance operations and external institutional communication objectives.

The unit's mission encompasses five interconnected operational domains that collectively establish robust institutional data practices and enable evidence-based decision-making: (1) maintaining and continuously developing the European Parliament's comprehensive data catalogue while actively supporting both data users and data owners to enhance content richness and platform functionality; (2) establishing and operationalizing the corporate data governance framework through defined policies, role assignments, and standardized processes that provide the institutional foundation for consistent data management; (3) overseeing the strategic development and management of the European Parliament's data platform to institutionalize strong data practices and foster strategic data usage across the institution; (4) developing and maintaining coherent data architecture foundations while ensuring seamless interoperability across institutional systems and technical infrastructure; and (5) ensuring continuous stakeholder alignment and involvement across business Directorates-General through structured engagement with local data correspondents serving as distributed governance liaison points. Additionally, the unit advances open data principles by developing semantic vocabularies, defining standardized document formats, and promoting alignment with other European institutions' data initiatives, while participating in inter-institutional working groups addressing data governance challenges and emerging standards to contribute to broader EU institutional coordination on information management practices.

10C5020.name: 10C5020
original_activities: Support the maintenance and evolution of a wide portfolio of official websites (Europarl) to facilitate the search, access, and track parliamentary activities.

Design, develop, and deploy content management systems (CMS) and back-office platforms to facilitate the creation, publication, and monitoring of online content.
Process and convert documents: index and transform parliamentary documents into structured formats, such as XML, to support legislative workflows and publication processes.

10C5020.refined_description: Unit 10C5020 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10C of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as the European Parliament's central hub for digital information management, public access infrastructure, and content delivery systems. The unit maintains comprehensive responsibility for sustaining and advancing the Parliament's official web portfolio (15% of workload), encompassing website maintenance and evolution to facilitate seamless search, access, and tracking of parliamentary activities for both internal stakeholders and the general public, while simultaneously supporting and maintaining websites dedicated to National Parliaments activities (15% of workload) to enable inter-parliamentary cooperation and information sharing. Unit 10C5020 manages and develops the Parliament's open data services and centralized open data portal (15% of workload), positioning itself as the authoritative provider of structured, publicly accessible parliamentary data across the institution. The unit operates the reference database system CODICT/MADIS (15% of workload), which functions as the centralized repository for referential data dissemination across the Parliament's IT ecosystem, and designs, develops, and deploys sophisticated content management systems and back-office platforms (15% of workload) that enable efficient creation, publication, and monitoring of online parliamentary content. Additionally, Unit 10C5020 executes critical processing and document conversion activities (15% of workload) whereby parliamentary documents are indexed and transformed into structured XML formats to support legislative workflows and publication processes. The unit extends its digital infrastructure mandate by providing operational support and maintenance for the Visiting Booking platform (10% of workload), managing reservation systems for all visitor group categories, both sponsored and self-guided. Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 10C5020 as the Parliament's primary custodian of digital information architecture, public data accessibility, and content delivery infrastructure, thereby supporting the institution's transparency, accountability, and public engagement objectives while serving as a foundational service provider to other DG ITEC units.
10C5020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Public Web Infrastructure & Digital Public Interface** (30%): Maintenance and evolution of official Europarl website portfolio to facilitate parliamentary activity search, access, and tracking (15%); support and maintenance of National Parliaments activity websites to enable inter-parliamentary cooperation and information sharing (15%)

• **Data Management & Reference Systems Infrastructure** (30%): Management and development of open data services and the centralized open data portal as the authoritative provider of structured public data (15%); operation and administration of the CODICT/MADIS reference database system for centralized storage and dissemination of referential data across the Parliament's IT ecosystem (15%)

• **Content Management Systems & Document Processing** (30%): Design, development, and deployment of content management systems (CMS) and back-office platforms for online content creation, publication, and monitoring workflows (15%); processing, indexing, and conversion of parliamentary documents into structured XML formats to support legislative workflows and publication processes (15%)

10D.name: 10D

Participate in the definition and strategic orientation of the Directorate-General, apply the operational and strategic decisions. Supervise and coordinate the organisational changes.
Manage the relations with the user Directorates-General and the political and administrative authorities. Represent the Directorate in the Committees and Groups of the interinstitutional works.

10D.refined_description: Unit 10D functions as the Direction-level operational, administrative, and strategic hub within the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the critical coordinating entity between DG leadership and its subordinate technical and operational units (10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, 10D2020, and twelve additional specialized units). The unit discharges integrated dual-track responsibilities: providing comprehensive administrative support encompassing mission order creation, external access management and control, leave request administration via HRM systems, and official document management and archival; simultaneously delivering integrated financial support including budgetary planning and continuous monitoring, financial transaction initiation and processing, CAF (Commitment Authorisation Form) provision, and exercise of subdelegated authorising officer responsibilities for financial commitments. Unit 10D ensures continuous directorate operation and service delivery through direction-setting and coordination of all Units and Services with consistency to institutional mission, while contributing substantively to DG-level strategic orientation by implementing operational and strategic decisions and supervising organizational changes across the Directorate. The unit maintains essential stakeholder relationships with user Directorates-General and political/administrative authorities, representing the Directorate in interinstitutional committees and working groups, and participates in the complete management cycle encompassing objective definition, administrative and Annual Work Programme development, and identification, assessment, and management of risks inherent to directorate activities. • **Network Architecture & Infrastructure Design** (25%): Architect, engineer, and establish comprehensive secure network connectivity solutions across all institutional network domains, creating the foundational technical framework and infrastructure architecture for Parliament's digital operations

• **Network Security & Protection** (20%): Design, implement, deploy, and maintain comprehensive edge and internal network protection mechanisms through firewalls and advanced protection systems, ensuring perimeter security and internal threat mitigation across all network domains

10D2020.name: 10D2020
original_activities: Design, implement, and maintain robust structured cabling and wireless infrastructure solutions—including IT cabling systems, Wi-Fi coverage, GSM connectivity, radio spectrum management, and data centres.
Manage and deliver large-scale, network-related projects by meeting scope, time, budget, and quality objectives, while ensuring a seamless service transition into operational environments.
Procure, purchase, and implement communication services, network equipment and associated services that align with the highest industry standards.
Proactively manage technological obsolescence by identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks associated with aging infrastructure and equipment.
Manage vendor relationships by fostering strategic partnerships, ensure accountability, and maintain alignment with organisational goals.
Provide infrastructure support to remote offices through effective coordination with relevant Directorates-General.
Manage network-related licenses to optimise cost and maximize value, drive modernisation initiatives, and oversee efficient asset and stock management.
10D2020.refined_description: Unit 10D2020 functions as a specialized infrastructure and network management unit within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as a critical operational pillar responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining the European Parliament's comprehensive communications and connectivity ecosystem. The unit's foundational mission—representing 35% of its operational workload—encompasses the engineering and operational stewardship of structured cabling systems, wireless infrastructure solutions, Wi-Fi coverage networks, GSM connectivity systems, radio spectrum management, and data centre operations across the institution. Complementing this core infrastructure responsibility, Unit 10D2020 dedicates 30% of its capacity to the strategic management and delivery of large-scale network-related projects, ensuring adherence to scope, timeline, budgetary, and quality parameters whilst orchestrating seamless transitions into operational service environments. The unit further allocates 10% of resources to the procurement, purchase, and implementation of communication services and network equipment in accordance with industry-leading standards, while maintaining strategic vendor partnerships and ensuring organizational accountability (5% of this allocation). Additionally, the unit dedicates 10% to proactive technological obsolescence management through comprehensive risk identification, assessment, and mitigation strategies targeting aging infrastructure, complemented by network license optimization and modernization initiatives (5% of this allocation). The remaining 5% of resources supports coordination of infrastructure services to remote offices through collaborative engagement with peer Directorates-General, coupled with efficient asset and stock management across the network ecosystem. Through these integrated activities, Unit 10D2020 serves as the essential technical custodian of the Parliament's network infrastructure, ensuring the institution's technological ecosystem remains current, reliable, operationally continuous, and strategically aligned with institutional digital transformation objectives.
10D2020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Infrastructure Design, Engineering & Operations** (35%): Design, implement, and maintain structured cabling and wireless infrastructure solutions, including IT cabling systems, Wi-Fi coverage networks, GSM connectivity systems, radio spectrum management, and data centre operations across the institution

• **Project & Service Delivery Management** (30%): Manage large-scale network-related projects to meet scope, time, budget, and quality objectives while ensuring seamless service transitions into operational environments

• **Procurement & Standards Compliance with Vendor Management** (10%): Procure, purchase, and implement communication services and network equipment aligned with industry-leading standards; foster strategic vendor partnerships and maintain accountability to organizational goals (5% vendor relations; 5% procurement and standards)

• **Infrastructure Modernization & Technological Obsolescence Management** (10%): Proactively manage technological obsolescence through comprehensive risk identification, assessment, and mitigation of aging infrastructure; optimize network-related licenses and drive modernization initiatives (10% obsolescence management; 5% license optimization and modernization initiatives integrated within this allocation)

• **Extended Infrastructure Support & Asset Management** (5%): Coordinate infrastructure support services to remote offices through effective collaboration with relevant Directorates-General; oversee efficient asset and stock management across the network ecosystem

10D2030.name: 10D2030
original_activities: Architect and engineer modern device configuration management (MDCM) for the European Parliament and private devices, replacing standard configuration run from the premises.
Architect and engineer the Exchange infrastructure, including load balancing, spam defence, and malware defence.
Architect and engineer unified communication services at the European Parliament, including IP based telephony, voice gateways, audio/videoconferencing solutions, WebEx and Teams.
Architect and engineer virtual desktop environments for Directorate-General specific needs, for administrators and end users.
Architect and engineer streaming and IPTV services including EU multilingual TV channels. Record and distribute internal and public video content for the European Parliament Plenary meetings.
Conduct technology watch. Provide 3rd level support to operational units of INSO, including for Office 365 (Teams, SharePoint online, Power Platform).
10D2030.refined_description:
**

Unit 10D2030 operates as a specialized technical architecture and engineering unit within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, functioning as the European Parliament's primary center of excellence for enterprise infrastructure modernization, unified communications systems, and digital service delivery. Positioned as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structure, 10D2030 collaborates strategically within the broader Direction 10D framework alongside twelve sibling units to advance institutional technology modernization objectives. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the design, implementation, and ongoing maintenance of critical infrastructure services that enable the European Parliament's operational continuity and multi-directional digital communications across its diverse stakeholder base. Through its specialized technical capabilities, Unit 10D2030 architects and engineers modern, cloud-centric technology systems that fundamentally replace legacy on-premises methodologies, while simultaneously providing advanced third-level technical support and strategic technology surveillance to ensure alignment with evolving institutional requirements. The unit's portfolio spans five interconnected technical domains—device and endpoint management, messaging and email infrastructure security, unified communications services, virtual desktop and streaming services, and strategic technology governance—collectively positioning 10D2030 as a strategic enabler of the Parliament's digital infrastructure and communications capabilities.

---

**
10D2030.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Device and Endpoint Management** (33%): Architect and engineer modern device configuration management (MDCM) systems for both European Parliament-issued and private devices, establishing strategic replacement for traditional on-premises configuration methodologies—representing the unit's primary focal responsibility area

• **Messaging and Email Infrastructure Security** (26%): Architect and engineer comprehensive Exchange infrastructure ecosystem incorporating sophisticated load balancing mechanisms, advanced spam defense systems, and multi-layered malware defense protocols—the unit's secondary major responsibility domain

• **Unified Communications Services** (15%): Design and implement integrated unified communication platforms encompassing IP-based telephony systems, voice gateway infrastructure, and audio/videoconferencing solutions including WebEx and Microsoft Teams deployments across the institution—supporting Parliament-wide communication requirements

• **Virtual Desktop and Media Services** (22%): Architect and engineer virtualized desktop environments tailored to specific Directorate-General operational requirements for both administrative personnel and end-user populations (11%), alongside streaming and IPTV service delivery infrastructure including EU multilingual television channels with responsibility for recording and distributing both internal parliamentary proceedings and public video content from European Parliament Plenary sessions (11%)

• **Strategic Technology Governance and Advanced Support** (4%): Conduct continuous technology watch activities and provide third-level technical support to INSO operational units, with specialized expertise in Office 365 ecosystem components including Teams, SharePoint Online, and Power Platform implementations

10D30.name: 10D30
original_activities: Ensure the hosting, running, and operation of the Institution's, large commercial off the shelf and custom built applications, including lifecycle management and performance testing prior to go-live.
Ensure availability and management of the electronic voting system in the hemicycles for plenary sessions, committee rooms for committee meetings, political group meetings and special events.

Move a number of the Institution's applications to the cloud, through the provision of the necessary artefacts and advice to allow development teams to successfully develop for and deploy on the Cloud.
Perform Load Testing and introspection of the Applications. Support the application teams with troubleshooting and Performance improvement.
Identify and implement process improvements across the Directorate, including the management of projects (migration of servers, containers platforms implementation, automation of cloud migration).
Develop and deliver Enhanced Hosting Services, focussing on enhanced monitoring and observability, with a view to provide more stable applications.
Ensure availability and management of the SMIAP screens in the hemicycles for plenary sessions and special events.
10D30.refined_description: Unit 10D30 operates as a critical operational hub within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, functioning as the European Parliament's primary custodian of application infrastructure management and institutional digital systems availability. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the hosting, operation, and full lifecycle management of the Institution's extensive portfolio of commercial off-the-shelf and custom-built applications, while ensuring continuous availability of parliamentary operational systems including electronic voting infrastructure across hemicycle chambers, committee meeting spaces, and political group assemblies, alongside SMIAP display systems in hemicycle and special event environments. Unit 10D30 actively advances the Parliament's cloud transformation agenda by providing technical artifacts, architectural guidance, and implementation support to development teams for successful cloud migration and deployment, while simultaneously conducting rigorous load testing, application performance introspection, and troubleshooting support to optimize system resilience and reliability. The unit enhances institutional service delivery through the development and implementation of Enhanced Hosting Services featuring advanced monitoring capabilities and observability frameworks designed to improve application stability and performance. Additionally, Unit 10D30 drives continuous operational excellence through the identification and implementation of directorate-wide process improvements and strategic infrastructure projects, including server migrations, containerization platform implementations, and cloud migration automation initiatives. As a leaf node within the Direction 10D hierarchy, the unit operates in close coordination with its sibling units to ensure cohesive IT service delivery across the Institution.
10D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Application Infrastructure & Lifecycle Management** (35%): Comprehensive hosting, operation, and lifecycle management of the Institution's commercial off-the-shelf and custom-built applications, including mandatory performance testing and validation prior to production deployment

• **Parliamentary Operational Systems** (27%): Ensuring continuous availability and technical management of electronic voting systems across hemicycle chambers, committee rooms, and political group meeting spaces for plenary sessions, committee meetings, and special parliamentary events (24%), plus stewardship of SMIAP information display infrastructure in hemicycle and special event environments (3%)

• **Cloud Transformation & Digital Migration** (10%): Provision of technical artifacts, architectural guidance, and deployment support enabling development teams to successfully migrate and deploy applications to cloud platforms and adopt cloud-native practices

• **Performance Optimization & Reliability Enhancement** (18%): Rigorous load testing and application performance introspection to support troubleshooting and performance improvement (10%), combined with development and delivery of Enhanced Hosting Services featuring advanced monitoring and observability capabilities to ensure application stability and reliability (8%)

• **Infrastructure Modernization & Process Improvement** (10%): Identification and implementation of directorate-wide process improvements and management of strategic infrastructure projects including server migrations, containerization platform implementations, and cloud migration automation initiatives

10D3010.name: 10D3010
original_activities: Ensure the hosting, running, and operation of the Institution's standard, custom-built and commercial off the shelf (COTS) applications, throughout their lifecycle (from initial on-boarding to decommissioning), both on-premises and in the Cloud.
Perform load testing prior to go-live and regularly during the applications' operational phase. Offer introspection services and support troubleshooting and performance improvement.
Offer Extended Hosting Services (EHS) to the Application Responsibles, in the form of standard or custom monitoring dashboards, metrics, alerting and proactive failure prevention.
Implement, maintain and operate the operational platforms and tools required to deliver the operational activities (Deployment, Automation, Testing), on-premises and in the Cloud.
10D3010.refined_description: Unit 10D3010 functions as the Application Operations and Infrastructure Management unit within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, serving as the critical operational backbone for the European Parliament's application portfolio lifecycle management. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive hosting, deployment, operation, and lifecycle management of the Institution's diverse technology portfolio—encompassing standard institutional applications, custom-built solutions, and commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) systems—across both on-premises and cloud-based deployment environments, constituting 50% of the unit's workload allocation. Complementing this foundational responsibility, the unit dedicates 20% of its operational capacity to delivering essential performance assurance services, including systematic pre-production load testing prior to application go-live, continuous performance monitoring during operational phases, introspection capabilities, and technical troubleshooting support to optimize application performance and enable performance improvement recommendations. Additionally, the unit allocates 15% of its workload to delivering Extended Hosting Services (EHS) to Application Responsibles through provisioning of customizable and standard monitoring dashboards, comprehensive metrics collection and aggregation, intelligent alerting mechanisms, and proactive failure prevention strategies. The remaining 15% of the unit's effort focuses on implementing, maintaining, and operating the foundational operational platforms, automation frameworks, and tooling infrastructure—encompassing deployment orchestration, continuous automation capabilities, and testing frameworks—required across both on-premises and cloud environments to enable the delivery of all aforementioned operational activities. As a leaf-node unit within the Direction 10D hierarchy, Unit 10D3010 operates collaboratively with its sibling units (10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, and twelve additional units) to collectively fulfill DG ITEC's strategic objectives and deliver integrated information and communications technology services to the Institution.
10D3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Application Hosting and Lifecycle Management** (50%): Ensure comprehensive hosting, deployment, running, and operational management of the Institution's complete application portfolio—including standard institutional applications, custom-built solutions, and commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) systems—throughout their entire lifecycle from initial onboarding through decommissioning, across both on-premises and cloud deployment environments.

• **Performance Assurance and Optimization Services** (20%): Conduct systematic pre-go-live load testing and continuous performance validation during operational phases; provide introspection services, technical troubleshooting support, and performance improvement recommendations to enable optimization across hosted applications.

• **Extended Hosting Services and Proactive Monitoring** (15%): Deliver Extended Hosting Services (EHS) to Application Responsibles through provisioning of standard and customizable monitoring dashboards, comprehensive metrics collection and aggregation, intelligent alerting systems, and proactive failure prevention mechanisms and strategies.

• **Operational Infrastructure, Automation, and Enablement Platforms** (15%): Implement, maintain, and operate the foundational operational platforms, automation frameworks, and tooling infrastructure—encompassing deployment orchestration, continuous automation capabilities, and testing frameworks—required to support operational activities across on-premises and cloud environments.

10D3020.name: 10D3020
original_activities: Coordinate project services throughout the Directorate.
Manage non-standard application infrastructure layers and standardise them.
Identify and implement process and services improvements across infrastructure, security operations and Directorate.
Receive, analyse and manage new requests, needs and complaints from the clients, and match them to the right IT service or process within the Unit or Directorate.
Support engineering with the implementation of new processes/technologies in the hosting environment.
10D3020.refined_description:
**

Unit 10D3020 functions as a critical operational and coordination hub within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as a leaf-level organizational unit with comprehensive responsibility for infrastructure service delivery, continuous improvement initiatives, and strategic intermediation between client requirements and technical delivery capabilities. Operating at the second hierarchical level of the European Parliament's information technology governance structure, the unit maintains collaborative relationships with sibling units (including 10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, and additional peer units within DG ITEC) while serving as a vital bridge between the parent Direction 10D's strategic direction and operational execution across the Directorate. The unit's workload is systematically distributed across five integrated functional domains: coordinating project services and technology initiatives across organizational boundaries (25%); managing non-standard application infrastructure layers and implementing standardization protocols to enhance system consistency, reliability, and reduce technical debt (25%); identifying and executing process and service improvements spanning infrastructure operations, security operations, and broader Directorate functions to drive organizational modernization and efficiency gains (20%); receiving, analyzing, and systematically managing new client requests, needs assessments, and complaints while matching these demands to appropriate IT services and processes within both the Unit and parent Directorate (20%); and supporting engineering teams in implementing new processes and technologies within the hosting environment to operationalize technical innovations (10%). Collectively, these activities position Unit 10D3020 as a strategic intermediary that simultaneously optimizes the infrastructure and process foundations upon which DG ITEC's services depend while ensuring seamless integration and delivery of technology initiatives across the organization.

**
10D3020.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Project and Service Coordination** (25%): Coordinate project services throughout the Directorate, ensuring seamless integration and aligned delivery of technology initiatives across organizational boundaries with cross-functional coordination.

• **Infrastructure Standardization and Management** (25%): Manage non-standard application infrastructure layers and implement standardization protocols to enhance system consistency, reliability, operational efficiency, and reduce technical debt across the IT estate.

• **Process and Operational Improvement** (20%): Identify and implement process and service improvements spanning infrastructure operations, security operations, and Directorate-wide functions to drive organizational modernization, efficiency gains, and operational excellence.

• **Client Request Management and Service Matching** (20%): Receive, analyze, and systematically manage new client requests, needs assessments, and complaints from internal stakeholders, with responsibility for routing demands to appropriate IT services and processes within the Unit and parent Directorate.

• **Engineering Support and Technology Implementation** (10%): Support engineering teams in implementing new processes and technologies within the hosting environment to operationalize technical innovations and ensure effective translation into operational capability.

10D3030.name: 10D3030
original_activities: Ensure the availability of the electronic voting system in the hemicycles and committee rooms for Plenary sessions, committee meetings, Political Groups meetings and special events. Ensure the availability of SMIAP screens.
Manage the electronic voting operations and the SMIAP screens during the Plenary sessions/special events and ensure interoperability with other services and applications.

Ensure the maintenance of the system, technical assistance and support. Analyse related technology trends and business needs. Maintain and ensure the technological evolution of the complete electronic voting infrastructure.
Provide training for clients and users (Members, DG PRES, Political Groups, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG, DG EXPO, organizers). Provide operational contract and supplier management.
10D3030.refined_description: Unit 10D3030 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), bearing primary responsibility for the comprehensive management, technological integrity, and operational delivery of the European Parliament's electronic voting infrastructure and associated digital display systems across all parliamentary venues. The unit operates under a dual-focused mission combining infrastructure availability and real-time operational execution: it ensures continuous availability and operational readiness of electronic voting systems deployed across hemicycles and committee rooms, alongside the provisioning and maintenance of SMIAP (Smart Member Information and Assistance Platform / Système de Multimédias d'Affichage et d'Information Parlementaire) display screens for Plenary sessions, committee meetings, Political Groups assemblies, and special parliamentary events (35% of workload); simultaneously, it manages real-time electronic voting operations and SMIAP screen functionality during active parliamentary sessions and special events while maintaining seamless interoperability with other institutional services and applications across the Parliament's technology ecosystem (35% of workload). The unit sustains technological excellence through systematic preventive and corrective maintenance protocols, technical assistance provision, user support services, technology trend analysis, and strategic planning and implementation of infrastructure modernization to ensure the electronic voting system remains contemporary and aligned with emerging institutional business requirements (15% of workload). Additionally, the unit delivers comprehensive training programs to diverse stakeholder groups—including Members of Parliament, DG PRES, Political Groups, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG, DG EXPO, and event organizers—while managing operational contracts and supplier relationships to ensure sustained capability development and service delivery (15% of workload). As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 10D3030 functions as a critical service delivery entity supporting institutional parliamentary operations under Direction 10D's strategic guidance while collaborating with peer units across the DG ITEC structure to deliver integrated information and communications technology services.
10D3030.refined_activity_summary:
• **Infrastructure Availability and Operational Provisioning** (35%): Ensure continuous availability and operational readiness of electronic voting systems across hemicycles, committee rooms, and designated parliamentary venues; provision, deploy, and maintain SMIAP display screens for Plenary sessions, committee meetings, Political Groups assemblies, and special parliamentary events

• **Real-Time Operational Management and System Integration** (35%): Manage real-time electronic voting operations and SMIAP screen functionality during active Plenary sessions and special events; ensure seamless interoperability with institutional services and applications; coordinate system performance and institutional service integration during parliamentary proceedings

• **Maintenance, Technical Support, and Technological Evolution** (15%): Provide preventive and corrective system maintenance; deliver technical assistance and user support services; analyze emerging technology trends and institutional business requirements; plan and implement infrastructure modernization and technological advancement to maintain contemporary alignment with institutional needs

• **Stakeholder Training, Contract Management, and Supplier Relations** (15%): Deliver comprehensive training programs to Members of Parliament, DG PRES, Political Groups, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG, DG EXPO, and event organizers; manage operational contracts and supplier relationships; ensure sustained stakeholder capability development and service continuity

10D40.name: 10D40
original_activities: Manage the lifecycle of infrastructure (servers, storage, network, security) and platforms (operating systems, databases, email, unified communication), including installation, maintenance, upgrade, decommissioning.
Operate secure hosting, networking and central services solutions both on-site (data centres) and in the cloud.
Manage procurement and asset management (hardware, licences and subscriptions) of hosting solutions equipment.
Run the Operations Centre supporting data centres, security and network.
Manage incidents.
10D40.refined_description: Unit 10D40 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), serving as the primary custodian of the European Parliament's critical information technology infrastructure and hosting services ecosystem. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the complete lifecycle management of enterprise infrastructure components—including servers, storage systems, network architecture, and security frameworks—alongside platform stewardship for operating systems, databases, email systems, and unified communication solutions, encompassing installation, maintenance, upgrade procedures, and decommissioning activities (40% of operational workload). Unit 10D40 operates and maintains secure hosting, networking, and centralized service solutions deployed across hybrid environments, comprising both on-premises data centre facilities and cloud-based infrastructure platforms, ensuring continuous availability and security of institutional digital services (30% of workload). The unit manages strategic procurement workflows and asset lifecycle administration for hosting equipment, including hardware acquisition, software licensing, and subscription management, thereby ensuring cost-effective resource utilization and compliance with institutional procurement frameworks (10% of workload). Unit 10D40 operates a dedicated Operations Centre providing continuous monitoring, support, and oversight of data centre operations, network infrastructure, and security systems (10% of workload), while maintaining comprehensive incident management capabilities to detect, escalate, and remediate service disruptions with minimal institutional impact (10% of workload). As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 10D40 operates under the strategic direction of Direction 10D while functioning in close coordination with multiple sibling units (10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, and additional units) to deliver integrated information technology services across the institution.
10D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Infrastructure Lifecycle and Platform Management** (40%): Comprehensive administration of the complete lifecycle for enterprise infrastructure components (servers, storage systems, network architecture, security frameworks) and platform technologies (operating systems, databases, email systems, unified communication solutions), encompassing installation, maintenance, upgrade, and decommissioning activities

• **Hosting and Centralized Services Operations** (30%): Operation and maintenance of secure hosting, networking, and centralized service solutions deployed across hybrid environments, including both on-premises data centre facilities and cloud-based infrastructure platforms, ensuring continuous availability and security of institutional digital services

• **Strategic Procurement and Asset Management** (10%): Management of procurement workflows and asset lifecycle administration for hosting equipment, including hardware acquisition, software licensing, and subscription lifecycle management, ensuring cost-effective resource utilization and institutional procurement compliance

• **Operations Centre Monitoring and Support** (10%): Continuous operation of the dedicated Operations Centre providing real-time monitoring, support, and oversight of data centre operations, network infrastructure, and security systems

• **Incident Management and Service Restoration** (10%): Detection, escalation, and remediation of service incidents to restore normal operations and minimize institutional service disruption

10D4010.name: 10D4010
original_activities: Provision, maintain and troubleshoot of databases and operating systems.
Monitor 24/7 the infrastructure of the European Parliament, on-call teams for the resolution of incidents out of office hours.
Operate the corporate email service.
Dispatch efficiently the requests and incidents to the right groups (ITOC).
10D4010.refined_description: Unit 10D4010 operates as a critical technical operations and infrastructure management unit within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a foundational operational component of the European Parliament's information technology ecosystem. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive provision, maintenance, and technical troubleshooting of databases and operating systems, which constitutes the dominant focus of organizational effort at 60% of total workload, ensuring the stability, availability, and continuity of core computational infrastructure essential to parliamentary operations. Complementing this primary function, Unit 10D4010 maintains continuous 24/7 infrastructure monitoring and surveillance through dedicated on-call teams positioned to respond to critical incidents occurring outside standard office hours, representing 20% of allocated resources and providing essential continuity of service assurance and operational resilience across the institution. The unit additionally operates the corporate email service infrastructure, accounting for 15% of workload allocation, which represents a mission-critical communications backbone essential to institutional functioning. As a leaf-node unit reporting directly to Direction 10D, Unit 10D4010 also fulfills a critical coordination and triage function by efficiently dispatching requests and incidents to appropriate specialized technical groups within the IT Operations Center (ITOC), comprising 5% of documented workload and ensuring optimal resource allocation and response effectiveness across the broader IT service delivery ecosystem. Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 10D4010 as a cornerstone of DG ITEC's operational resilience, service delivery capabilities, and institutional support infrastructure.
10D4010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Infrastructure Management** (60%): Comprehensive provision, maintenance, and technical troubleshooting of databases and operating systems, representing the primary operational focus area and foundational responsibility ensuring stability and continuity of parliamentary IT infrastructure

• **Continuous Monitoring and Incident Response** (20%): Round-the-clock (24/7) infrastructure surveillance and monitoring with dedicated on-call teams providing out-of-hours emergency incident resolution and critical response capabilities, ensuring uninterrupted operational resilience

• **Critical Communications Infrastructure Operations** (15%): Management and operation of the corporate email service platform, supporting essential institutional communication infrastructure and parliamentary operations

• **Service Coordination and Request Dispatch** (5%): Efficient triage, categorization, and routing of technical requests and incidents to appropriate specialized groups within the IT Operations Center (ITOC), optimizing resource allocation and response effectiveness across the broader IT operations structure

10D4020.name: 10D4020
original_activities: Run the day-to-day operations of data centres and network infrastructures, including monitoring, proactive maintenance, and incident resolution across hybrid and distributed environments.
Keep the corporate network infrastructure reliable and secure, including LANs, WANs, wireless networks, and unified communications systems.
Ensure the security and integrity of our infrastructure through proactive measures such as vulnerability management, regular patching, privilege access management, and business continuity testing.
Protect data through the management of backup and recovery systems, ensuring data availability, integrity, and disaster recovery.
Collaborate with other teams to ensure alignment and secure operation of the infrastructure.
10D4020.refined_description: Unit 10D4020 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, serving as the critical infrastructure backbone for the European Parliament's information and communications technology ecosystem. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management and operational stewardship of the institution's data centre and network infrastructure assets across hybrid and distributed environments, positioning it as an essential operational component supporting the Parliament's digital infrastructure resilience and institutional continuity. As a leaf-node unit within the Direction structure, Unit 10D4020 directly supports the strategic objectives of its parent Direction 10D while working in coordination with its sibling operational units (10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, and related units) to deliver integrated technology services. The unit's operational capacity is systematically distributed across five integrated functional areas that collectively ensure uninterrupted service delivery, infrastructure security, and organizational data protection. Through continuous monitoring, proactive maintenance protocols, vulnerability management programmes, privilege access controls, and business continuity testing regimes, the unit sustains the connective tissue of parliamentary operations while maintaining robust disaster recovery capabilities. Cross-functional collaboration with complementary teams ensures coordinated alignment and secure operational execution across all infrastructure domains.
10D4020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Infrastructure Operations & Maintenance** (45%): Execute day-to-day operations of data centres and network infrastructures across hybrid and distributed environments, encompassing continuous monitoring, proactive maintenance protocols, and systematic incident resolution to ensure uninterrupted service delivery

• **Network Infrastructure Management** (20%): Maintain the reliability and security posture of corporate network infrastructure through administration of local area networks (LANs), wide area networks (WANs), wireless network systems, and unified communications platforms

• **Security & Vulnerability Management** (15%): Ensure infrastructure security and integrity through proactive vulnerability management programmes, regular security patching cycles, privilege access management controls, and business continuity testing initiatives

• **Data Protection & Recovery** (10%): Protect organizational data through operational management of backup and recovery systems, ensuring continuous data availability, integrity assurance, and robust disaster recovery capabilities

• **Cross-functional Coordination** (10%): Collaborate with complementary teams and sibling operational units to ensure coordinated alignment, secure operational execution, and integrated infrastructure service delivery across the European Parliament

10D50.name: 10D50
original_activities: Ensure the project management role for DevSecOps and coordinate the different activities with special focus on the 'Sec' part.
Ensure the protection of end points by operating the standard solution and providing support to LSAs and project teams (especially in the context of the migration to Windows 11 and MDCM projects).
Ensure the direction, the coordination and the animation of the unit, and the coherence of the activities of the unit within the mission of the Directorate.
Ensure the ICT Security technology watch, follow the evolution of the threat, the evaluation of new products and new technologies that can be applied in the field of operational security.
Support the implementation of the new Cybersecurity regulation.
Manage the administrative and practical aspects of the reorganisation.
10D50.refined_description: Unit 10D50 functions as a specialized operational security and cybersecurity management unit within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, serving as a critical organizational nexus for cybersecurity and infrastructure protection initiatives across the European Parliament's information and communications technology ecosystem. As a leaf-node unit operating collaboratively with sibling units (10D10, 10D1010, 10D1020, 10D20, 10D2010, and twelve additional units) under the strategic oversight of Direction 10D, Unit 10D50 maintains a comprehensive portfolio encompassing five core functional domains with clearly defined workload allocations and integrated responsibilities. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive cybersecurity operations, technology governance, and strategic security initiatives, with particular emphasis on the "Sec" (Security) dimension of DevSecOps methodologies and direct technical support to Local System Administrators (LSAs) and project teams engaged in critical institutional initiatives including the Windows 11 migration program and MDCM (Mobile Device and Client Management) projects. Unit 10D50 provides directional oversight, coordination, and organizational animation of unit activities while maintaining vigilant oversight of emerging cybersecurity threats and technological innovations applicable to operational security frameworks. The unit's operational structure integrates project management leadership, endpoint protection operations, strategic governance, technology surveillance, regulatory compliance implementation, and administrative coordination—collectively advancing Direction 10D's institutional mission to ensure organizational ICT security posture and operational resilience.
10D50.refined_activity_summary:
• **DevSecOps Project Management & Security Leadership** (25%): Assumes primary project management responsibility for DevSecOps initiatives with concentrated focus on security components, coordinating cross-functional security activities and ensuring security integration throughout the development lifecycle

• **Endpoint Protection Operations & Infrastructure Support** (20%): Operates standard endpoint protection solutions, delivers technical support services to LSAs and project teams, and provides specialized assistance for critical infrastructure transition projects including Windows 11 migration and MDCM implementations

• **Strategic Direction, Unit Leadership & Organizational Coherence** (20%): Provides directional oversight, coordination, and organizational animation of unit activities while ensuring alignment with broader Direction 10D mission objectives and institutional strategic goals

• **ICT Security Technology Surveillance & Threat Intelligence** (15%): Conducts continuous ICT security technology watch, monitors threat landscape evolution, evaluates emerging products and technologies for operational security applications, and maintains systematic awareness of industry developments and threat landscape evolution

• **Regulatory Compliance Implementation & Administrative Operations** (15%): Supports implementation of new cybersecurity regulatory frameworks and manages administrative and logistical aspects of organizational restructuring initiatives, ensuring institutional compliance and operational readiness

10D5010.name: 10D5010
original_activities: Perform a continuous cyber security attack surface monitoring service for assets belonging to the European Parliament.
Conduct on-demand application or infrastructure penetration tests.
Operate a vulnerability management process.
Provide network, infrastructure, and application vulnerability evaluation using technical audits, configuration reviews, pen-tests, and regular vulnerability assortment scans.
Conduct cyber audits by using acquired data to independently identify and assess vulnerabilities and gaps in Detection, Response, and Prevention capabilities.
Coordinate with CERT-EU external Penetration tests and Red Team activities as part of the standard CERT-EU services to European Parliament.
10D5010.refined_description:
Unit 10D5010 operates as a specialized cybersecurity assurance, threat assessment, and vulnerability management function within Direction 10D of DG ITEC, serving as the European Parliament's primary internal capability for continuous security validation, threat intelligence, and independent security posture evaluation. The unit executes a comprehensive, multi-layered cybersecurity defense strategy encompassing persistent attack surface monitoring, proactive and reactive vulnerability identification, formalized risk management, and strategic capability assessment across Detection, Response, and Prevention functions. As a leaf node within the DG ITEC organizational hierarchy, Unit 10D5010 maintains continuous visibility into the Parliament's evolving threat landscape and security exposure while collaborating with sibling units across the broader cybersecurity ecosystem and coordinating with CERT-EU on advanced threat simulation activities. The unit's six interconnected operational activities collectively represent 100% of workload allocation and directly support institutional resilience, compliance objectives, and alignment of security capabilities with identified vulnerabilities and emerging threats. Through integrated assessment methodologies spanning technical audits, configuration reviews, penetration testing, vulnerability scanning, and cyber audits, the unit delivers comprehensive risk quantification and strategic security insights that inform institutional-level security planning and remediation prioritization.

---

##
10D5010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Continuous Security Monitoring & Attack Surface Surveillance** (25%): Perform continuous cyber security attack surface monitoring service for all European Parliament assets, maintaining persistent real-time visibility into the institution's security exposure, threat landscape evolution, and security configuration drift across the digital estate.

• **Offensive Security Assessment & Penetration Testing** (40%): Conduct on-demand application and infrastructure penetration tests (25%) combined with provision of comprehensive vulnerability evaluation through technical audits, configuration reviews, penetration tests, and regular vulnerability scans across network, infrastructure, and application domains (15%), delivering both reactive and systematic security validation and active threat simulation across institutional systems.

• **Vulnerability & Risk Management** (20%): Operate a formalized, comprehensive vulnerability management process ensuring systematic identification, classification, remediation tracking, lifecycle management, and integration of findings from all assessment activities into a structured remediation and tracking framework for security gaps identified across the Parliament's technical infrastructure.

• **Strategic Security Capability Assessment & Cyber Audits** (13%): Conduct cyber audits utilizing acquired security data to independently identify and assess vulnerabilities and capability gaps in Detection, Response, and Prevention functions, enabling institutional-level security posture evaluation, strategic capability planning, and identification of security weaknesses through independent analysis.

• **External Security Coordination & Red Team Operations** (2%): Coordinate with CERT-EU on external penetration tests and Red Team activities as part of standard CERT-EU services delivered to the European Parliament, facilitating advanced threat simulation, institutional security validation through external expertise, and alignment with EU-wide cybersecurity initiatives.

10D5020.name: 10D5020
original_activities: Design, implement, host and administer cybersecurity solutions.
Produce cybersecurity threat detection content. Perform initial review of security alerts, based on the threat detection content.

Design, implement and administer the public key infrastructure of the Parliament. Deliver services in the field of digital signatures and data encryption. Support the Competence Centre on Trust Services (CCTS).
Deliver tools and services to EP-CERT to support their mission. Assist EP-CERT in incident investigations by correlating security events.
Provide strategic input and organisational support on security log collection.
10D5020.refined_description: Unit 10D5020 is a specialized cybersecurity operations and infrastructure unit positioned within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical second-level component of the European Parliament's organizational hierarchy and serving as an essential technical enabler for the institution's comprehensive information security framework. The unit's operational mandate encompasses five integrated functional domains: the design, implementation, hosting, and administration of enterprise-wide cybersecurity solutions that establish the foundational protective infrastructure for parliamentary IT systems (35% of workload); the production of specialized cybersecurity threat detection content coupled with initial review, triage, and analysis of security alerts derived from threat intelligence mechanisms to enable proactive identification of emerging security incidents (25% of workload); the design, implementation, administration, and operational maintenance of the European Parliament's public key infrastructure (PKI) while delivering critical cryptographic services in digital signatures and data encryption and providing technical and operational support to the Competence Centre on Trust Services (CCTS) to ensure secure authentication and data protection across institutional operations (25% of workload); the delivery of specialized tools, services, and technical support to EP-CERT in fulfillment of their cybersecurity incident response mission, including assistance with forensic investigations through systematic security event correlation and analysis (10% of workload); and the provision of strategic guidance and organizational coordination regarding security log collection initiatives, methodologies, and governance frameworks across the institution (5% of workload). Through these coordinated and integrated activities totaling 100% of documented workload, Unit 10D5020 collectively delivers essential cybersecurity infrastructure, threat intelligence capabilities, cryptographic and trust services, and incident response support that strengthen the Parliament's overall information security posture, operational resilience, and institutional protection while working in concert with sibling units within Direction 10D to ensure comprehensive information security coverage across the parliamentary infrastructure.
10D5020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Cybersecurity Infrastructure and Solutions Management** (35%): Design, implement, host, and administer comprehensive enterprise-wide cybersecurity solutions establishing the foundational protective infrastructure for parliamentary IT systems

• **Threat Detection, Intelligence, and Alert Analysis** (25%): Produce specialized cybersecurity threat detection content and conduct initial review, triage, and analysis of security alerts derived from threat detection mechanisms to enable proactive identification of emerging security incidents

• **Public Key Infrastructure, Cryptographic Services, and Trust Services Support** (25%): Design, implement, and administer the European Parliament's public key infrastructure (PKI); deliver critical digital signature and data encryption services; provide technical and operational support to the Competence Centre on Trust Services (CCTS) to ensure secure authentication and data protection across institutional operations

• **Incident Response Support, Forensic Assistance, and Security Event Analysis** (10%): Deliver specialized tools and services to EP-CERT; assist with security incident investigations and forensic analysis through systematic security event correlation and analysis

• **Security Log Management Governance and Strategic Advisory** (5%): Provide strategic guidance and organizational coordination regarding security log collection initiatives, methodologies, and governance frameworks across the institution

10D5030.name: 10D5030
original_activities: Design and maintain the Identity and Access Management (IAM) architecture. It includes multi-factor and password-less authentication solutions to enhance security and support the Parliament's hybrid multi-cloud strategy.
Provide level 3 support to ensure the resolution of complex identity-related issues and security incidents. Support the integration of applications into the IAM system.
Define and enforce access policies, including role-based access control and least privilege principles, in combination with federated identity integration (e.g. EU Login).
Enable visibility, compliance, and continuous improvement by documenting IAM processes, integrating with monitoring systems, and improving automation in identity management.
10D5030.refined_description: Unit 10D5030 operates as a specialized Identity and Access Management (IAM) operational unit within Direction 10D of the Directorate-General for Information and Communications Technology (DG ITEC), functioning as a critical enabler of the European Parliament's secure digital infrastructure and organizational resilience. The unit bears comprehensive responsibility for designing, implementing, and maintaining enterprise-grade IAM architecture that incorporates advanced multi-factor and passwordless authentication solutions, directly supporting the Parliament's hybrid multi-cloud strategic deployment while enhancing overall security posture. Unit 10D5030 simultaneously delivers specialized level 3 technical support for the resolution of complex identity-related incidents and security vulnerabilities, while facilitating seamless integration of institutional applications into the IAM ecosystem and ensuring alignment with organizational security standards. The unit defines, implements, and enforces sophisticated access control policies—including role-based access control (RBAC) frameworks and least privilege principles—in conjunction with federated identity management systems such as EU Login, thereby ensuring compliance with European institutional governance standards. Additionally, the unit establishes comprehensive process documentation, integrates IAM operations with organizational monitoring systems, and advances automation capabilities within identity management workflows to enable continuous visibility, regulatory compliance, and operational improvement. Collectively, these interconnected activities position Unit 10D5030 as an essential component of the Parliament's broader digital infrastructure strategy, balancing security excellence with operational efficiency.
10D5030.refined_activity_summary:
• **IAM Architecture Design and Enhancement** (35%): Design, development, and ongoing maintenance of enterprise-grade Identity and Access Management architecture incorporating advanced multi-factor authentication and passwordless authentication solutions; direct support of the Parliament's hybrid multi-cloud strategic infrastructure and security posture enhancement

• **Technical Support and Application Integration** (35%): Delivery of specialized level 3 technical support for complex identity-related incidents and security vulnerability resolution; facilitation of seamless integration of institutional applications into the IAM ecosystem; alignment with organizational security standards

• **Access Control Policy Definition and Governance** (20%): Definition, implementation, and enforcement of sophisticated access control policies utilizing role-based access control (RBAC) frameworks and least privilege principles; integration with federated identity management systems including EU Login; compliance assurance with European institutional governance standards

• **Process Documentation, Monitoring Integration, and Automation Advancement** (10%): Establishment of comprehensive IAM process documentation; integration of IAM operations with organizational monitoring and compliance systems; advancement of automation capabilities within identity management workflows to enable continuous visibility, regulatory compliance, and continuous operational improvement

10E.name: 10E
original_activities: Provide strategic oversight, manage and coordinate DG ITEC's and SOMA Directorate's resources, ensuring alignment with their mission and strategies.
Provide oversight on budget allocation and spending, procurement and contract management for DG ITEC as well as chairing the investment panel.
Oversee and provide communication and engagement services for the whole Directorate-General.
Represent of the Directorate, and when appropriate the Directorate-General, in the inter-DG and inter-institutional committees or working groups.
Oversee and provide Learning and Development (L&D) services for the whole Directorate-General and for the end users of ITEC services.
Provide administrative support to the Units via the administrative cell.
Oversee and provision of property management / GBI (real estate management) services.
10E.refined_description: Unit 10E serves as the apex strategic Direction within DG ITEC, functioning as the principal governance and coordination hub for the Directorate-General's comprehensive operational, administrative, and institutional functions. Exercising comprehensive strategic oversight and resource coordination (25% of workload), the unit manages the complex matrix of subordinate operational units—including Human Resources (10E10), Financial and Administrative Operations (10E20), Procurement and Contract Management (10E30), and specialized support entities (10E0010, 10E2010, 10E3010, and four additional units)—while ensuring organizational alignment with institutional missions and strategic objectives across both DG ITEC and the SOMA Directorate. The unit maintains rigorous financial and procurement governance (15% of workload) through comprehensive oversight of budget allocation, expenditure monitoring, procurement operations, contract management, and chairmanship of the investment panel to ensure optimal capital deployment for DG ITEC. Unit 10E directs comprehensive organizational communication and engagement services across the entire Directorate-General (15% of workload), positioning the organization effectively within the European Parliament's institutional framework while simultaneously representing the Directorate and Directorate-General in inter-DG and inter-institutional committees, working groups, and stakeholder engagement forums (15% of workload). The unit enhances organizational capability through Learning and Development service provision (10% of workload) extending to both Directorate-General personnel and end users of ITEC services, while delivering foundational administrative support to all subordinate units via its dedicated administrative cell (10% of workload) and overseeing property management and GBI (real estate management) services to ensure optimal facility utilization (10% of workload).
10E.refined_activity_summary:
