• **Strategic Governance and Resource Coordination** (25%): Comprehensive strategic oversight and coordinated management of DG ITEC and SOMA Directorate resources; supervision of subordinate operational units including Human Resources, Financial and Administrative Operations, Procurement and Contract Management, and specialized support entities; ensuring organizational alignment with institutional missions and strategic objectives

• **Financial and Procurement Stewardship** (15%): Oversight of budget allocation and expenditure management; procurement operations and contract administration; chairmanship of the investment panel for DG ITEC ensuring optimal capital deployment

• **Organizational Communication and Stakeholder Engagement** (15%): Direction of comprehensive communication and engagement services across the entire Directorate-General; representation of the Directorate and Directorate-General in inter-DG and inter-institutional committees, working groups, and stakeholder engagement forums

• **Workforce Development and Organizational Capacity** (10%): Oversight and provision of Learning and Development services for DG ITEC personnel and external end users of ITEC services; fostering organizational capability and professional competency advancement

• **Administrative and Facility Operations** (20%): Provision of administrative support to all subordinate units through the dedicated administrative cell (10%); oversight and provision of property management and GBI (real estate management) services ensuring optimal facility utilization (10%)

10E0010.name: 10E0010
original_activities: Manage, coordinate and decide on ITEC internal communication, communication towards the European Parliament, external communication, European Parliament Intranet, ITECnet, video production and communication orientated visuals.
Update and manage the European Parliament Intranet and preserve a coherent information architecture.
Provide a 1st level support and train the European Parliament intranet webmasters (technical and editorial) for the creation and maintenance of local intranets.
Write technical specifications for new developments of the Jahia platform.
Train, help and steer the DG ITEC staff in their pursuit of effective and service-oriented communication.
Simplify complex IT topics into understandable, engaging messages for various audiences.
Provide the right information at the right time, to the right people, in the right format and on the right channel.
10E0010.refined_description:
Unit 10E0010 operates as the integrated Communications and Digital Infrastructure Management Division within DG ITEC's Direction (Unit 10E), functioning as the critical institutional nexus between the Directorate-General's technical operations and the European Parliament's diverse stakeholder ecosystem. The unit exercises comprehensive management, coordination, and decision-making authority over ITEC's multifaceted communication infrastructure, encompassing internal organizational communications, European Parliament-directed communications, external institutional messaging, the European Parliament Intranet platform, ITECnet infrastructure, video production initiatives, and communication-oriented visual design—establishing the foundational communication and information architecture upon which institutional knowledge accessibility depends. Operationally, Unit 10E0010 maintains direct stewardship of the European Parliament Intranet through ongoing platform updates, content management, and preservation of coherent information architecture standards, while simultaneously serving as the primary support and training provider for European Parliament intranet webmasters across both technical and editorial competencies, thereby enabling the creation and maintenance of distributed local intranet ecosystems in compliance with institutional standards. The unit contributes specialized technical expertise through the development and documentation of technical specifications for new Jahia platform enhancements, while providing strategic guidance, mentoring, and professional development support to DG ITEC staff in advancing service-oriented and effective communication practices. Additionally, Unit 10E0010 specializes in translating complex IT concepts into accessible, engaging messaging tailored to diverse audience segments, and ensures optimal information delivery through strategic assessment and deployment across appropriate channels, formats, temporal frameworks, and recipient designations to maximize institutional effectiveness and organizational alignment with broader DG ITEC strategic objectives.

##
10E0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Communications Management and Infrastructure Coordination** (30%): Manage, coordinate, and exercise decision-making authority over ITEC internal communications, European Parliament-directed communications, external institutional communications, European Parliament Intranet platform operations, ITECnet infrastructure, video production initiatives, and communication-oriented visual design

• **Digital Platform Stewardship and Information Architecture Management** (25%): Update, manage, and maintain the European Parliament Intranet platform while preserving coherent information architecture standards; ensure institutional knowledge accessibility and optimal user navigation effectiveness across the digital ecosystem

• **Technical Development and Platform Enhancement Specifications** (10%): Develop and document comprehensive technical specifications for new Jahia platform developments and enhancements to enable platform evolution and enhanced institutional functionality

• **Stakeholder Support, Training, and Capacity Building** (20%): Provide first-level technical and editorial support with comprehensive training programs to European Parliament intranet webmasters for intranet creation, maintenance, and standards compliance (10%); train, mentor, and steer DG ITEC staff toward effective, service-oriented communication practices and professional development (10%)

• **Message Translation and Information Delivery Optimization** (15%): Translate complex IT concepts into understandable, engaging messages tailored for diverse audience segments (10%); ensure optimal information delivery through strategic assessment and deployment to designated recipients via appropriate channels, formats, and temporal frameworks (5%)

10E10.name: 10E10
original_activities: Provide comprehensive HR services to DG ITEC and its staff, ensuring professional and people-centric approach to HR management; recruitment activities (staff, trainees, contract agents).
Manage traineeships cycle, onboarding programmes (Buddy Programme and mentoring programme for new managers), mobility, annual assessment, non-recruitment staff files.
Provide HR reporting and statistical analyses.
Participate in working groups and special projects.
Ensure Career advisory services (RELOP), exit interviews (staff, trainees).
Provide training to managers, staff and trainees on HR matters (ad hoc workshops and presentations).
Provide outreach activities (participation in job fairs, in person and online).
Ensure budgetary resources planning and management (contract agents, part time compensation agents, extra time, traineeships, missions).
10E10.refined_description:
**

Unit 10E10 is the dedicated Human Resources Unit within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, functioning as the primary organizational entity responsible for comprehensive HR service delivery to the Directorate-General and its entire workforce. The unit operates as a leaf-node entity within the 10E Direction hierarchy, coordinating strategically with sibling units (10E0010, 10E20, 10E2010, 10E30, 10E3010, and additional units) to ensure integrated HR governance and administrative support across the entire DG ITEC structure. The unit's core mission encompasses the delivery of comprehensive, people-centric HR services through a professional management approach, including full-cycle recruitment activities for permanent staff positions, traineeship placements, and contract agent engagements, which collectively constitute 50% of the unit's total workload. Complementing this primary focus, Unit 10E10 dedicates 30% of its workload to the complete management of the traineeships lifecycle and implementation of structured onboarding programmes (including the Buddy Programme and specialized mentoring initiatives for newly appointed managers), while simultaneously overseeing staff mobility processes, conducting annual performance assessments, and maintaining non-recruitment personnel files to ensure effective workforce integration and development. The unit further allocates 20% of its workload across seven complementary and strategically integrated functions: HR reporting and statistical analyses (3%), participation in cross-organizational working groups and special projects (3%), delivery of career advisory services through the RELOP framework and execution of exit interviews for departing staff and trainees (3%), provision of targeted training and development workshops for managers, staff, and trainees on HR-related matters (3%), execution of outreach and recruitment promotion activities including participation in job fairs through both in-person and digital formats (3%), and strategic budgetary resources planning and management for contract agents, part-time compensation arrangements, overtime compensation, traineeship programmes, and official missions (3%).

---

**
10E10.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Core HR Service Delivery & Recruitment** (50%): Provision of comprehensive, people-centric HR services to DG ITEC and its workforce; full-cycle recruitment activities encompassing permanent staff positions, traineeship placements, and contract agent engagements; professional HR management approach

• **Personnel Development, Lifecycle Management & Onboarding** (30%): Complete management of traineeships cycle; implementation of structured onboarding programmes including the Buddy Programme and specialized mentoring initiatives for newly appointed managers; oversight of staff mobility processes; administration of annual performance assessments; maintenance of non-recruitment personnel files

• **HR Analytics, Reporting & Advisory Services** (6%): HR reporting and statistical analyses; delivery of career advisory services through the RELOP framework; execution of exit interviews for departing staff and trainees; provision of professional guidance on career progression and institutional transitions

• **Training, Capacity Building, Organizational Support & Outreach** (6%): Provision of targeted training and development workshops for managers, staff, and trainees on HR-related matters; participation in cross-organizational working groups and special projects; execution of outreach and recruitment promotion activities including participation in job fairs through in-person and digital formats

• **Budgetary Planning & Resource Management** (3%): Strategic planning and management of budgetary resources for contract agents, part-time compensation arrangements, overtime compensation, traineeship programmes, and official mission-related expenditures

10E20.name: 10E20
original_activities: Ensure centralised financial initiation for DG ITEC: expenditure commitments on direct/specific contracts and PRESTO purchase orders, issuance of purchase orders, VAT certificates, processing payment of invoices, preparation of recovery orders.

Ensure the financial and budgetary coordination of the Directorate-General: drawing up and monitoring the budget, reporting, follow up on Court of Auditors requests, budget transfer requests, empowerments and authorising officer sub-delegations.
Ensure the centralised operational initiation of the Directorate for Sourcing and Organisational Management (SOMA).
Ensure the logistics of signing specific contracts and their amendments: verification of document compliance, signatory compliance, and bank guarantee requests.
10E20.refined_description: Unit 10E20 functions as the centralized Financial and Administrative Operations Hub within Direction 10E of the Directorate-General for Innovation, Technology and Cybersecurity (DG ITEC), operating as a critical execution entity at the second hierarchical tier of the organizational structure. Reporting to Direction 10E and coordinating with sibling units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E2010, 10E30, 10E3010, and others), Unit 10E20 bears primary responsibility for centralizing financial initiation, administrative coordination, and operational compliance functions that underpin the entire Directorate-General's operational capacity and financial governance. The unit's integrated portfolio encompasses four interconnected functional domains that collectively ensure comprehensive financial control, budgetary transparency, and operational compliance across DG ITEC's diverse activities. Through its dual role as both a specialized operational unit serving DG ITEC's internal financial infrastructure and a centralized service provider to the Sourcing and Organizational Management Directorate (SOMA), Unit 10E20 serves as the administrative backbone enabling effective resource management, procurement coordination, and regulatory adherence across the organization.
10E20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Initiation and Payment Processing** (43%): Centralized management of DG ITEC expenditure commitments on direct and specific contracts; PRESTO purchase order processing and issuance; VAT certificate generation; invoice payment processing; and recovery order preparation—representing the unit's primary operational focus area.

• **Budgetary Coordination and Financial Governance** (25%): Financial and budgetary oversight for the Directorate-General including budget formulation and continuous monitoring; regulatory and financial reporting; Court of Auditors request management and follow-up; budget transfer request processing; and authorization of empowerments and officer sub-delegations.

• **Sourcing and Organizational Operations Support** (17%): Centralized operational initiation services provision to the Directorate for Sourcing and Organizational Management (SOMA), ensuring alignment between financial operations and organizational procurement and management objectives.

• **Contract Administration, Compliance Verification, and Logistics Coordination** (15%): Logistics coordination for specific contract execution and amendment processes, encompassing verification of document compliance with regulatory requirements, validation of signatory authority and authorization, and administration of bank guarantee requests.

10E2010.name: 10E2010
original_activities: Ensure verification of procurement documents before publication and award.
Verify the DG ITEC's financial transactions.
Verify the DG ITEC's draft contracts and order forms.
Perform checks on inventory write-offs.
10E2010.refined_description: Unit 10E2010 functions as a specialized verification and compliance unit within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, serving a critical internal oversight mandate across the Directorate-General's procurement, financial, and contractual operations. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy and functioning as a peer entity alongside sibling units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E20, 10E30, 10E3010, and four additional units), Unit 10E2010 collectively contributes to Direction 10E's comprehensive governance and control framework. The unit's primary mission encompasses systematic pre-publication and pre-award verification of procurement documentation, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and institutional procedures before public dissemination and contract award decisions. Concurrently, the unit executes detailed scrutiny of DG ITEC's financial transactions and draft contracts, maintaining rigorous quality controls and ensuring fiscal accuracy, legitimacy, and adherence to budgetary protocols and European institutional financial standards. Additionally, Unit 10E2010 performs specialized auditing functions regarding inventory write-offs, maintaining accountability over asset management and decommissioning processes. Through these integrated verification mechanisms, the unit establishes systematic quality assurance across all phases of procurement execution, financial management, contractual obligations, and asset disposition.
10E2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Procurement Document Verification and Pre-Execution Quality Assurance** (40%): Comprehensive verification of procurement documents prior to publication and formal award, establishing rigorous pre-execution quality controls and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and institutional procedures

• **Financial Transaction Oversight and Compliance** (30%): Systematic verification of DG ITEC's financial transactions to ensure accuracy, legitimacy, adherence to budgetary protocols, and compliance with European institutional financial standards

• **Contractual and Order Form Documentation Review** (25%): Detailed scrutiny of DG ITEC's draft contracts and order forms prior to finalization and implementation, ensuring legal sufficiency, procedural correctness, and contractual term adequacy

• **Asset Management Controls and Inventory Write-Off Auditing** (5%): Specialized checks on inventory write-offs to ensure proper documentation, justification of asset dispositions, and accountability over asset decommissioning and disposal processes

10E30.name: 10E30
original_activities: Monitor, manage and coordinate the call for tenders in DG ITEC.
Establish specific contracts based on framework contracts.
Provide guidance to the authorising officers by delegation in the domains of calls for tender and framework contract execution.
Ensure the digital archiving of call for tender documents and contractual documents.
10E30.refined_description: Unit 10E30 is a specialized procurement and contract management unit operating within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, functioning as a critical operational component of the Directorate-General's procurement governance framework and serving the European Parliament's information technology procurement processes. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the full procurement lifecycle through dual primary responsibilities: monitoring, managing, and coordinating calls for tenders across DG ITEC, and establishing specific contracts derived from pre-negotiated framework agreements, together consuming 80% of its operational capacity (40% each). As a leaf-node unit within the Direction 10E hierarchy, Unit 10E30 operates under strategic oversight of its parent Direction while coordinating laterally with seven sibling procurement and administrative units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E20, 10E2010, 10E3010, and four additional units) to ensure coherent procurement practices and support the Directorate-General's broader administrative and operational objectives. To maintain institutional compliance, operational consistency, and regulatory adherence, Unit 10E30 dedicates 10% of its workload to providing authoritative guidance and advisory support to authorising officers by delegation regarding best practices and procedural compliance in both tender call procedures and framework contract execution matters. The unit further allocates 10% of its resources to ensuring systematic digital archiving and long-term preservation of all call for tender documentation and contractual records, thereby maintaining institutional memory, transparency, and audit readiness throughout DG ITEC's complete procurement lifecycle.
10E30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Procurement Process Management** (80%): Monitor, manage, and coordinate calls for tenders across DG ITEC; establish specific contracts based on pre-negotiated framework agreements

• **Institutional Guidance & Compliance Support** (10%): Provide authoritative guidance and advisory support to authorising officers by delegation on procedural compliance and best practices in tender calls and framework contract execution

• **Records Management & Digital Archiving** (10%): Ensure comprehensive digital archiving and long-term preservation of all call for tender documents and contractual records for institutional continuity, transparency, and audit purposes

10E3010.name: 10E3010
original_activities: Assist the operational units in the domain of contract execution through the establishment of specific contracts and order forms.
Provide assistance and guidance to the Authorising officers in the above mentioned areas.
Execute quota transfers according to the instructions of Authorising officer.
Coordinate the DG ITEC's response to surveys to participate in inter-institutional procurement procedures.
Provide information to the Directorates-General and EUI using the DG ITEC's contracts.
Ensure the lead of the working group for procurement and contract execution.
10E3010.refined_description: Unit 10E3010 operates as a specialized procurement and contract management unit within DG ITEC's Direction 10E, functioning as a second-tier hierarchical entity without subordinate organizational components that serves as the primary operational support mechanism for contract execution across the Directorate-General. The unit's integrated mission encompasses establishing and managing specific contracts and order forms that enable operational activities (65% of workload), providing comprehensive technical guidance and authoritative assistance to Authorising Officers in contract execution and procurement-related decision-making (25% of workload), and executing three specialized procedural functions: quota transfers under Authorising Officer instructions (3%), coordinating DG ITEC's institutional participation in inter-institutional procurement surveys and procedures (3%), and disseminating contract-related information to other Directorates-General and the European University Institute to facilitate utilization of DG ITEC's established contractual frameworks (3%). Additionally, Unit 10E3010 dedicates 1% of its resources to leading the cross-organizational working group responsible for procurement and contract execution governance, providing strategic direction for institutional procurement policies and practices. Through these integrated activities, the unit ensures efficient and compliant procurement processes, maintains robust contract execution infrastructure, facilitates inter-institutional collaboration, and provides critical advisory support to senior administrative decision-makers within the DG ITEC organizational framework.
10E3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Contract Establishment and Execution Support (65%)**: Establish and manage specific contracts and order forms for operational units, serving as the principal mechanism for contract execution infrastructure and enabling DG ITEC's operational activities across the Directorate-General.

• **Authorising Officer Guidance and Advisory Services (25%)**: Provide comprehensive technical guidance and authoritative assistance to Authorising Officers in contract execution and procurement-related decision-making, ensuring proper administrative oversight and institutional compliance.

• **Administrative and Procedural Operations (6%)**: Execute quota transfers according to Authorising Officer directives (3%) and coordinate DG ITEC's institutional participation in inter-institutional procurement surveys and procedures (3%), ensuring procedural adherence and institutional engagement.

• **Information Dissemination and Inter-Institutional Coordination (3%)**: Disseminate contract-related information to other Directorates-General and the European University Institute, facilitating knowledge-sharing and utilization of DG ITEC's established contractual resources and capabilities.

• **Governance and Strategic Leadership (1%)**: Lead the cross-organizational working group responsible for procurement and contract execution governance, providing strategic direction and coordination for institutional procurement policies, standards, and organizational practices.

10E40.name: 10E40
original_activities: Advise, guide and support clients on visual choices, products and services, deadlines and costs; contribute to the definition and design of clients' internal communication strategies, propose creative ideas and develop them.

Ensure continuous innovation by transitioning from traditional print-focused graphic design to an agile, customer-focused, in-house creative agency focused on digital multi-channel publishing and distribution.

Maintain various applications and provide support and training to customers. Develop and maintain the range of products offered on the publishing order portal. Automate and maintain document production workflows.
Ensure the management and the coordination of the unit, staff, machinery and finances.
10E40.refined_description: Unit 10E40 operates as a specialized creative services and publishing operations unit within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, functioning as a leaf-node operational entity and in-house creative agency that strategically bridges visual communication design with operational publishing infrastructure. The unit serves the European Parliament by delivering integrated graphic design, digital publishing, and communications support services across the institution, enabling both internal and external communication objectives through four complementary and interconnected functional areas. As a critical enabler within the Parliament's organizational framework, Unit 10E40 maintains collaborative relationships with sibling units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E20, 10E2010, 10E30, and four additional units) to ensure cohesive and coordinated service delivery across the institutional communication ecosystem. The unit's operational model reflects a strategic transformation from traditional print-focused graphic design methodologies toward an agile, customer-centric approach emphasizing digital multi-channel publishing and distribution capabilities, thereby driving continuous organizational innovation. Through its four primary functional areas—encompassing client advisory services, digital transformation leadership, technical infrastructure management, and unit governance—Unit 10E40 positions itself as an indispensable strategic partner to Parliament stakeholders seeking comprehensive communication design and publishing solutions.
10E40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Client Advisory & Creative Strategy Services** (38%): Provide comprehensive guidance on visual communication choices, product and service offerings, project deadlines, and cost implications; contribute substantively to the definition and design of clients' internal communication strategies; propose and develop creative ideas and ideation

• **Digital Transformation & Innovation Leadership** (32%): Drive continuous organizational innovation through systematic transition from traditional print-focused graphic design methodologies to an agile, customer-focused, in-house creative agency model; establish and advance digital multi-channel publishing and distribution capabilities

• **Technical Infrastructure, Systems Management & Portal Operations** (22%): Maintain diverse applications and deliver ongoing user support and training to customers; develop and sustain the product range accessible through the publishing order portal; automate and maintain document production workflows to enhance operational efficiency

• **Unit Governance, Resource Management & Financial Stewardship** (8%): Ensure administrative coordination, staff management, machinery oversight, and financial stewardship of unit operations

10E4010.name: 10E4010
original_activities: Manage front-desk of all DG ITEC's publishing products and services.
Advise, guide and support clients on visual choices on publishing products and services.

Contribute to the definition and design of strategies for internal communication with clients, proposing creative design ideas, and ensuring the quality of graphics and final publishing products and services.
Lead digital transformation project to enhance client service delivery.
10E4010.refined_description: Unit 10E4010 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, functioning as the primary interface and integrated service delivery mechanism for the Directorate-General's comprehensive publishing products and services portfolio. As a leaf-node operational entity with no subordinate units, the unit serves as a dedicated service delivery hub that directly supports the strategic coordination and operational oversight functions of its parent Direction while maintaining seamless client access and operational continuity across all DG ITEC publishing operations. The unit's core mission encompasses four functionally distinct but strategically interconnected operational domains that collectively constitute a comprehensive client-facing and strategic communications operation, with documented workload allocations totaling 100%. Through coordinated execution of these integrated activities—encompassing front-desk client interface management, specialized visual design advisory services, strategic communication strategy development with creative design contributions, and digital transformation leadership—Unit 10E4010 directly enhances DG ITEC's operational effectiveness and contributes substantively to the Directorate-General's strategic objectives regarding accessible, high-quality institutional communications, client engagement, and modernized service delivery mechanisms.
10E4010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Client Service Interface & Front-Desk Operations** (30%): Comprehensive management of the front-desk function serving as the primary contact point for all DG ITEC publishing products and services inquiries, requests, and client access, ensuring seamless operational continuity and centralized service coordination

• **Design Advisory & Visual Guidance Services** (25%): Provision of specialized advisory, guidance, and support services to clients regarding visual design choices, aesthetic considerations, and design-related decision-making across the full spectrum of publishing products and services offerings

• **Strategic Communication & Creative Design Strategy** (25%): Substantive contribution to the definition, design, and implementation of internal communication strategies with clients, including generation and proposal of creative design concepts and maintenance of rigorous graphic quality standards and assurance throughout the publishing production lifecycle and final deliverables

• **Digital Transformation & Service Enhancement** (20%): Leadership and management of critical digital transformation initiatives specifically designed to enhance, modernize, and optimize client service delivery mechanisms, operational efficiency, and institutional communication accessibility

10E50.name: 10E50
original_activities: Ensure the provision of a wide range of high quality printed products, including legislative and protocol documents, on-demand printing services and stationery.

Process and produce proofs of various legislative and parliamentary documents such as minutes of sittings, verbatim reports of debates, texts adopted, written questions and answers; ensuring publication in the Official Journal.
Ship and disseminate printed materials.
Prepare, edit, proofread and format proofs in all the official languages of the European Union.
Ensure the management and the coordination of the unit, allocating and monitoring its finances and contracts.
10E50.refined_description: Unit 10E50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 10E of DG ITEC, serving as the European Parliament's primary hub for legislative document production, multilingual editorial services, and materials management. The unit bears primary responsibility for the comprehensive management of the Parliament's document production and dissemination ecosystem, integrating five core operational functions that collectively constitute 100% of its workload allocation. As a leaf-node unit within the directorate hierarchy, Unit 10E50 coordinates with sibling units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E20, 10E2010, 10E30, and others) to fulfill Direction 10E's strategic objectives for information technology and communications support. The unit's integrated activities encompass the provision of high-quality printed products spanning legislative documents, protocol materials, on-demand printing services, and institutional stationery; the processing and production of formally proofed parliamentary documents including minutes of sittings, verbatim debate reports, adopted texts, and written questions with answers, with subsequent publication coordination in the Official Journal; multilingual editorial services ensuring linguistic accuracy and institutional compliance across all official European Union languages; the physical logistics of shipping and disseminating printed materials to designated recipients and stakeholders; and comprehensive administrative and financial stewardship encompassing management coordination, budget allocation, contract monitoring, and resource oversight. Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 10E50 as an essential support mechanism within the broader DG ITEC operational framework, contributing directly to the Parliament's legislative documentation and institutional communication objectives.
10E50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Document Production and Provision** (45%): Ensuring the provision of an extensive range of high-quality printed products, including legislative documents, protocol materials, on-demand printing services, and institutional stationery to support parliamentary operations and administrative functions

• **Legislative Document Processing and Official Journal Publication** (40%): Processing and producing formally proofed parliamentary documents (minutes of sittings, verbatim debate reports, adopted texts, written questions and answers) with coordination of publication in the Official Journal

• **Multilingual Editorial Services and Quality Assurance** (5%): Preparation, editing, proofreading, and formatting of documentary proofs across all official European Union languages to ensure institutional and linguistic compliance and accuracy

• **Physical Distribution and Logistics Management** (7%): Shipping and dissemination of printed materials to Parliament members, institutions, designated recipients, and external stakeholders

• **Unit Management and Financial Governance** (3%): Strategic coordination of unit operations, budget allocation, contract management, and resource monitoring to ensure operational efficiency and institutional compliance

10E5010.name: 10E5010
original_activities: Produce the Institution's printed materials and publications (printing for the work of Members of Parliament, political groups, protocol service and the Administration).
Produce the Institution's working documents (pure legislative printing).
Provide direct customer service (shipping printed materials or making them available via delivery lockers).

Instant printing of documents and distribution of standard documents (calendars, regulations, consolidated treaties, technical data sheets, etc.), speaking cards, diaries and other items to Members and European Parliament staff.
10E5010.refined_description: Unit 10E5010 functions as the European Parliament's centralized printing and document production facility, operating as a specialized operational leaf-node unit within Direction 10E of the Directorate-General for Innovation, Technology and Institutional Change (DG ITEC). The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive production and distribution of institutional printed materials across all parliamentary functions and multi-stakeholder environments, including Members of Parliament, political groups, the protocol service, and administrative departments. Operating independently without subordinate organizational structures while collaborating with sibling units (10E0010, 10E10, 10E20, 10E2010, 10E30, and four additional units), Unit 10E5010 delivers integrated printing services and document distribution infrastructure that supports the broader institutional printing and documentation requirements of the Parliament. The unit's operational portfolio is strategically structured around four complementary activities: producing the Institution's diverse printed materials and publications (65% of workload); generating specialized legislative working documents through dedicated legislative printing processes (15% of workload); providing direct customer service functions encompassing physical delivery of printed materials and management of accessible delivery locker systems (10% of workload); and delivering rapid instant printing services alongside the distribution of standardized institutional documents such as calendars, regulations, consolidated treaties, technical data sheets, speaking cards, diaries, and other customized items to Members and European Parliament staff (10% of workload). This integrated operational structure ensures both accessibility and efficiency across the Parliament's comprehensive printing and distribution needs.
10E5010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Publication Production** (65%): Comprehensive production of the Institution's diverse printed materials and publications, including specialized customized printing for Members of Parliament, political groups, protocol service, and administrative departments

• **Legislative Document Production** (15%): Specialized production of the Institution's working documents through dedicated legislative printing processes supporting parliamentary legislative functions

• **Distribution and Customer Service Operations** (10%): Direct customer service provision encompassing physical delivery of printed materials through traditional and modern distribution infrastructure, including management of accessible delivery locker systems

• **Standardized Materials and Instant Printing Services** (10%): Rapid production and distribution of standardized institutional documents (calendars, regulations, consolidated treaties, technical data sheets) and personalized items (speaking cards, diaries) for Members and European Parliament staff, providing on-demand printing capacity for immediate institutional needs

original_activities: Ensure the organization and proper functioning of all activities linked to parliamentary committees and thematic departments in the areas of citizens' rights, justice and institutional affairs.
Provide institutional assistance and support to the parliamentary committees responsible for citizens' rights, justice and institutional affairs.

Ensure personnel management; organize and chair several management meetings (directors, unit heads) per month. Manage mail assigned to general management and verify all outgoing mail for the signature of the Secretary General and the President.
Define the strategy of the general management, organize and coordinate its activities.
Advise the Secretary General and cooperate with the jurisconsult and other directors general.

Provide committees, other parliamentary bodies as well as the President of Parliament with specialist advice and know-how using summary notes, in-depth analyzes and studies on all aspects of the general management's areas of competence.

Represent and/or ensure the representation of the general management in the bodies of Parliament, in internal meetings, as well as in interinstitutional forums and in joint or other committees and ensure their monitoring.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
23.refined_description: Unit 23 functions as the apex General Management directorate of the European Parliament, reporting directly to the Secretary General and serving as the strategic and operational nerve center for parliamentary governance. The unit bears primary responsibility for ensuring the comprehensive organization and proper functioning of all parliamentary committees and thematic departments operating within the citizens' rights, justice, and institutional affairs domains, while simultaneously providing institutional assistance and specialized support services to these same parliamentary entities. Unit 23 exercises critical personnel management functions encompassing the organization and chairing of multiple monthly management meetings with directors and unit heads, alongside comprehensive mail management responsibilities that include processing incoming general management correspondence and verifying all outgoing communications requiring the signature authority of the Secretary General and the President of Parliament. The unit defines and implements strategic direction for general management operations, organizing and coordinating all associated activities to ensure institutional coherence, while providing direct advisory counsel to the Secretary General and maintaining cooperative relationships with the jurisconsult and other Directors General on matters of institutional significance. Through its specialized analytical capacity, Unit 23 delivers expert advisory services to parliamentary committees, other parliamentary bodies, and the President via summary notes, in-depth analyses, and comprehensive studies, while representing general management across Parliament's internal bodies, interinstitutional forums, and joint committees with systematic monitoring of all engagements. The unit directly supervises three subordinate organizational entities—Unit 23-10 (integrated General Management directorate for DG Citizens' Rights), Unit 23A (primary directional and managerial hub), and Direction 23B (specialized management and coordination directorate)—thereby establishing itself as the foundational governance structure upon which the Parliament's administrative and institutional framework depends.
23.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Committee Coordination & Institutional Support** (45%): Ensures organization and proper functioning of all parliamentary committees and thematic departments in citizens' rights, justice, and institutional affairs domains (25%); provides institutional assistance and specialized support services to parliamentary committees responsible for these policy areas (20%)

• **Strategic Management & Personnel Administration** (30%): Defines general management strategy and organizes/coordinates all associated strategic activities to ensure institutional coherence (10%); manages personnel administration, organizes and chairs multiple monthly management meetings with directors and unit heads, and oversees all general management correspondence including verification of outgoing communications for Secretary General and President signatures (20%)

• **Executive Advisory & Representational Functions** (15%): Advises the Secretary General and cooperates with the jurisconsult and other Directors General on institutional matters (10%); represents general management in Parliament's internal bodies, interinstitutional forums, and joint committees while maintaining systematic monitoring oversight of all engagements (5%)

• **Specialized Analysis & Financial Authority** (10%): Provides specialist analytical support to parliamentary committees, bodies, and the Parliament President through summary notes, in-depth analyses, and comprehensive studies addressing general management competencies (5%); performs delegated authorizing officer functions, exercising financial authorization and accountability responsibilities within delegated parameters (5%)

original_activities: HR: Manage the recruitment of staff for DG IUST (civil servants, temporary, contractual, END); manage the organization chart of permanent and temporary positions; manage the mobility exercise; manage the selection of interns; monitor recruitment/reintegration; manage rights and obligations (absences, illnesses, leave, CCP, retirement, external activities); assist during the grading exercise; assist in the organization of teleworking.

PURPOSE: Coordinate the preparation and monitoring of the establishment of the budget, monitor budget execution, follow the various budgetary procedures and distribute credits to budget lines; prepare periodic and annual activity reports, reports to the hierarchy on budget execution and management, as well as financial and budgetary reports to the central services of the European Parliament.

PURPOSE: Carry out ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded in general management; provide assistance and advice on all questions relating to budget execution, calls for tenders and financial procedures and circuits, including the regular updating of the financial and budgetary procedures of the general management.

HR: Manage the budget envelope for missions (3 locations and outside locations); manage and control the organization charts for the missions in Strasbourg; control and monitor 3-site and non-3-site missions (compliance with procedures, exemptions, Back to School, social events such as EYE); develop human resources statistics.

HR: Provide internal training; organize and finance external training and missions for external training, including Away Days; manage study visits, secondments and fellowships. Manage conflicts and provide advice (e.g. after long-term absences, illness, CCP).

COMM: Manage the internal communication of DG IUST via the intranet site and by collaborating with the EP intranet service and Newshound to promote the activities of the DG; participate in several networks to discuss communication at EP level; liaise and collaborate with the COMS unit (CCLA); organize our participation in annual events such as Europe Day and EYE; develop a newsletter for DG IUST; advise the DG units on their communication.

ALL: Ensure coordination and representation of the General Directorate of Citizens' Rights, Justice and Institutional Affairs in various committees and working groups in the areas of human resources, budgetary and financial management, and communication.
Logistics: identify and manage the present and future spaces of the general management.
23-10.refined_description:
Unit 23-10 functions as the integrated General Management directorate of DG Citizens' Rights, Justice and Institutional Affairs (DG IUST), operating as a Direction-level organizational unit (Level 1) directly subordinate to the General Management parent directorate (Unit 23) within the European Parliament's administrative hierarchy, and structurally coordinating with sibling units 23A and 23B. The unit serves as the operational backbone of DG IUST, delivering comprehensive administrative stewardship across four primary functional domains: human resources management, budgetary and financial administration, internal communications, and strategic coordination. Unit 23-10's mandate encompasses the complete personnel lifecycle administration—including recruitment management for civil servants, temporary agents, contractual staff, and external national experts (END); organizational chart maintenance for permanent and temporary positions; mobility exercise coordination; intern selection management; recruitment and reintegration monitoring; and comprehensive rights and obligations administration covering absences, illnesses, leave entitlements, career classification procedures (CCP), retirement processes, and external activity authorizations. The unit simultaneously exercises rigorous budgetary stewardship through strategic budget preparation, establishment coordination, execution monitoring, procedural oversight of budgetary mechanisms, and systematic credit distribution to designated budget lines, complemented by preparation of periodic and annual activity reports and hierarchical reporting to European Parliament central services, while conducting ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded within general management scope. Beyond these core functions, Unit 23-10 manages mission-specific human resources operations across three designated locations and external venues, maintains mission organizational chart compliance in Strasbourg, provides technical assistance during grading exercises and teleworking implementation, delivers internal and external training coordination and financing, manages study visits and fellowships, organizes professional development events (Away Days, EYE participation, Back to School initiatives), provides conflict resolution and advisory services for complex personnel situations, generates comprehensive human resources statistics, and maintains dedicated mission budget envelopes. The unit actively manages DG IUST internal communications through intranet stewardship, collaboration with European Parliament intranet services and Newshound platforms, participation in institutional communication networks, liaison with the COMS unit (CCLA), coordination of annual institutional event participation (Europe Day, European Youth Event), development and dissemination of DG-specific newsletters, and provision of communication advisory services to individual DG units. Unit 23-10 ensures systematic institutional representation and advocacy for DG IUST across multiple Parliament committees and working groups addressing human resources, budgetary and financial management, and communication domains, while simultaneously identifying, assessing, and administering current and future physical workspace requirements and facilities for the General Directorate.

---

##
23-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Management and Personnel Administration** (40% combined allocation): Comprehensive recruitment lifecycle management across all personnel categories (civil servants, temporary agents, contractual staff, END, interns); organizational chart maintenance and administration for permanent and temporary positions; mobility exercise coordination; recruitment and reintegration monitoring; comprehensive rights and obligations administration (absences, illnesses, leave entitlements, CCP, retirement processes, external activity authorizations); technical assistance during grading exercises and teleworking implementation; mission-specific human resources budget envelope management across three designated locations and external venues; mission organizational chart compliance monitoring in Strasbourg; three-site and non-three-site mission procedure compliance verification and exemptions oversight; social and professional development event coordination (European Youth Event, Back to School initiatives, special institutional events); comprehensive human resources statistics development and reporting; internal training provision; external training program coordination and financing; study visits, secondments, and fellowships management; Away Days organization and financing; conflict management and advisory services for complex personnel situations (post-absence reintegration, post-illness reintegration, other personnel matters).

• **Budgetary and Financial Administration** (40% combined allocation): Strategic budget preparation, establishment coordination, and execution monitoring; procedural oversight of budgetary mechanisms and systematic credit distribution to designated budget lines; preparation of periodic and annual activity reports; hierarchical reporting on budget execution and management status; submission of financial and budgetary reports to European Parliament central services; comprehensive ex-ante verification of all financial transactions and contracts awarded within general management scope; technical assistance and comprehensive advisory services on budget execution questions, tender call procedures, public procurement procedures, and financial circuit compliance; regular updates and maintenance of financial and budgetary procedural documentation; mission budget envelope management and oversight.

• **Internal Communications and Institutional Visibility** (10% allocation): DG IUST intranet presence management and content promotion through active collaboration with European Parliament intranet services and Newshound platform; participation in Parliament-level communication networks; direct liaison and collaboration with COMS unit (CCLA); coordination of DG participation in annual institutional events (Europe Day, European Youth Event); development and dissemination of DG-specific newsletters; provision of communication advisory services to individual DG units.

• **Cross-Institutional Coordination and Strategic Representation** (5% allocation): Systematic presence and advocacy for DG IUST across multiple Parliament committees and working groups addressing human resources, budgetary and financial management, and communication domains; coordination of General Directorate participation in institutional governance structures and Parliament-level coordination mechanisms.

• **Logistics, Facilities Management, and Administrative Support** (5% allocation): Identification, assessment, and administration of current and future physical workspace requirements, space allocation, and facilities planning for the General Directorate across designated operational locations.

23A.name: 23A
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of the directorate, as well as the management of personnel and the general organization of the directorate.
Provide parliamentary committees with high-level expertise, up-to-date analysis and independent research in areas covered by the directorate.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Ensure project management in the areas of expertise of the management units and services.

Advise the deputies of Parliament in their areas of expertise and assist them as members of the parliamentary management committees, and in particular the presidents and rapporteurs, in the exercise of their functions.
Represent the institution in various internal committees and interinstitutional forums.
23A.refined_description: Unit 23A is a Direction-level organizational unit within the General Management directorate (Unit 23) of the European Parliament, positioned at the first hierarchical level beneath the Secretary General's office and functioning as the strategic management interface and operational nerve center for directorate-wide governance. The unit's integrated mission encompasses six functionally distinct but complementary activities that collectively establish it as both the administrative and strategic backbone of the General Management directorate, ensuring coherent organizational performance while maintaining institutional representation and supporting evidence-based parliamentary decision-making. The dominant function (60% of workload) involves exercising comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all four subordinate units and services (Units 23A10, 23A20, 23A30, and 23A40) alongside full personnel management and general organizational administration—responsibilities that establish 23A as the critical coordination hub between the Secretary General's office and the broader directorate structure. Complementary to this administrative leadership, Unit 23A allocates 10% of its capacity to delivering high-level expertise, contemporary policy analysis, and independent research to parliamentary committees across the directorate's substantive domains, thereby enhancing evidence-based parliamentary decision-making and supporting Members of Parliament and parliamentary management committee leadership, particularly presidents and rapporteurs, in matters within its competence. The unit exercises subdelegated authorizing officer authority (10% of workload), managing financial and administrative delegation responsibilities within its designated scope, while simultaneously providing project management oversight across the expertise areas of subordinate management units and services (10% of effort), ensuring coordinated implementation of directorate initiatives. Finally, Unit 23A dedicates 5% of its workload to institutional representation across internal committees and interinstitutional forums, ensuring coordinated external and internal institutional engagement. Through this integrated portfolio of activities, Unit 23A maintains the strategic management interface essential to directorate operations while supporting parliamentary bodies requiring institutional expertise and coordination.
23A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directional Management and Administrative Governance** (60%): Exercise comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services (Units 23A10, 23A20, 23A30, and 23A40); manage personnel administration; oversee general organizational structure and operational administration of the directorate

• **Financial and Fiduciary Responsibilities** (10%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer functions for budgetary and administrative authorization within delegated authority parameters; manage financial and administrative delegation responsibilities

• **Project and Operational Management** (10%): Provide project management oversight and implementation coordination across the specialized expertise domains of subordinate management units and services; ensure coordinated delivery of directorate initiatives

• **Parliamentary Support and Expert Advisory Services** (10%): Deliver high-level expertise, contemporary analysis, and independent research to parliamentary committees across directorate substantive domains; provide specialized advisory support to Members of Parliament and parliamentary management committee leadership, including presidents and rapporteurs, in matters within unit competence

• **Institutional Representation and External Coordination** (5%): Represent the institution across internal committees and interinstitutional forums; ensure coordinated external and internal institutional engagement

23A10.name: 23A10
original_activities: Provide assistance to parliamentary bodies and the secretariats of parliamentary committees (internal and external studies and notes, workshops, notes for the office of the President and the Secretary General, etc.) in the field of Citizens' Rights and Constitutional Affairs (Relevant Commissions: PETI, FEMM, CULT).

Make external expertise work available to parliamentary committees (identification of needs, calls for tenders, evaluation); develop exchanges with external experts, research institutes and universities (relations with external sources of expertise constitute a main activity, linked to the purchase of external expertise).
Develop relations with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, the dissemination of the work of the Thematic Department, the working tools of the Thematic Department.
Participate in the implementation of a modern IT environment for knowledge management.
Ensure the updating of technical sheets.
23A10.refined_description: Unit 23A10 functions as a specialized thematic research and analytical support unit positioned at the second hierarchical level within Direction 23A of the European Parliament's General Management structure, operating under the Secretary General's office. The unit's primary mission is to provide comprehensive analytical, research, and administrative assistance to parliamentary bodies and their secretariats, with particular emphasis on Citizens' Rights and Constitutional Affairs domains, specifically serving the Committee on Petitions (PETI), Committee on Women's Rights and Gender Equality (FEMM), and Committee on Culture and Education (CULT). Operating as a critical knowledge intermediary within the Parliament's legislative support ecosystem, Unit 23A10 executes a multifaceted operational portfolio that encompasses the production and delivery of internal and external studies, analytical notes, research briefings, workshop facilitation, and specialized policy documentation for the offices of the President and Secretary General. The unit simultaneously functions as an expertise broker by identifying parliamentary research requirements, designing and managing competitive procurement processes for external consultants, evaluating external deliverables, and cultivating strategic relationships with external research institutes, universities, and specialized expert networks. Beyond its core parliamentary support functions, the unit strengthens institutional knowledge networks through collaborative relationships with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, promotes the dissemination and visibility of the Thematic Department's research outputs and working methodologies, participates in the implementation and modernization of contemporary knowledge management information technology infrastructure, and maintains current and accurate technical documentation and reference materials. Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 23A10 as an essential operational and strategic resource supporting the Parliament's legislative analysis and policy development capacity.
23A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Support and Policy Analysis** (55%): Comprehensive provision of analytical and research assistance to PETI, FEMM, and CULT committees and their secretariats through the production of internal and external studies, analytical notes, research briefings, workshop facilitation, and specialized documentation for the offices of the President and Secretary General, exclusively focused on Citizens' Rights and Constitutional Affairs domains

• **External Expertise Acquisition and Strategic Management** (35%): Identification and assessment of specialized parliamentary research requirements, design and management of competitive procurement processes and tender calls, systematic evaluation of external expertise deliverables, and cultivation and maintenance of sustained collaborative relationships with external research institutes, universities, and subject-matter expert networks as primary sources of specialized knowledge deployment

• **Institutional Knowledge Management, Coordination, and Dissemination** (5%): Enhancement of inter-directorate coordination with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, promotion and dissemination of the Thematic Department's research outputs and analytical tools throughout the institutional structure, and optimization of departmental working methodologies and resources

• **Knowledge Infrastructure Development and Documentation Maintenance** (5%): Participation in the implementation and modernization of contemporary information technology systems for institutional knowledge management infrastructure (3%) and systematic maintenance, updating, and accuracy assurance of technical reference materials and resource documentation (2%)

23A20.name: 23A20
original_activities: Summarize, manage and follow up on petitions; preparation of mail, e-Petition, website and relations with petitioners.

Ensure the secretariat of the commission: organization of PETI meetings, Coordinator meetings, shadow rapporteur meetings, network meetings, public hearings, information missions, programming, drafting of minutes and feedback.

Prepare working documents, draft reports (PETI annual activity reports and European Ombudsman annual activity reports), draft opinions, oral questions and draft resolutions, amendment documents, voting lists.
Ensure IT projects (Peti Portal, ePetition).

Ensure contacts with other institutions (General Secretariat of the European Commission, European Ombudsman office, the petitions committees of national parliaments, Permanent Representations) and civil society.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion.
23A20.refined_description: Unit 23A20 functions as a specialized administrative and secretariat unit within Direction 23A of the European Parliament's General Management structure, operating directly beneath the Secretary General's office and serving as a critical operational backbone for the Committee on Petitions (PETI). The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the complete lifecycle management of citizen petitions submitted to the European Parliament, systematic institutional coordination across multiple European bodies, and full secretariat services for PETI committee operations. Positioned as a leaf-node unit within the hierarchical structure and functioning alongside sibling units 23A10, 23A30, and 23A40, Unit 23A20 bridges citizen engagement mechanisms with institutional decision-making processes through five integrated functional areas: petition management and citizen engagement (55%), commission secretariat and meeting coordination (25%), legislative documentation and reporting (10%), technology infrastructure and institutional relations management (5%), and direct parliamentary support services (2%). The unit maintains responsibility for comprehensive summarization and follow-up of petitions via postal correspondence, the e-Petition platform, and dedicated website interfaces; logistical coordination and documentation of PETI committee meetings, coordinator assemblies, shadow rapporteur consultations, inter-parliamentary network gatherings, public hearings, and information missions; preparation of substantive working documents and legislative outputs including annual activity reports, committee opinions, and draft resolutions; oversight of IT projects related to the Peti Portal and e-Petition systems; and formal institutional liaison with the European Commission General Secretariat, the European Ombudsman office, national parliamentary petitions committees, Permanent Representations, and civil society organizations. Through these integrated activities, Unit 23A20 enables the European Parliament's direct engagement with citizen petitions while supporting parliamentary committee functionality and the roles of MEPs serving as commission presidents, vice-presidents, rapporteurs, and opinion draftsmen.
23A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Petition Management and Citizen Engagement** (55%): Complete lifecycle management of citizen petitions including comprehensive summarization, administrative management, systematic follow-up coordination, and multi-channel communication with petitioners via postal correspondence, e-Petition platform administration, website content maintenance, and direct stakeholder relations

• **Commission Secretariat and Meeting Coordination** (25%): Full secretariat service delivery encompassing organizational coordination and logistical support for PETI committee meetings, coordinator assemblies, shadow rapporteur consultations, inter-parliamentary network meetings, public hearings, and information missions; meeting programming, scheduling, detailed minutes preparation, and feedback documentation

• **Legislative Documentation and Reporting** (10%): Preparation of substantive working documents and legislative outputs including PETI annual activity reports, European Ombudsman annual activity reports, committee opinions, oral questions, draft resolutions, amendment documentation, and voting list compilation to inform parliamentary decision-making processes

• **Technology Infrastructure and Institutional Relations Management** (5%): Oversight and management of IT projects related to the Peti Portal and e-Petition system operations combined with institutional liaison and stakeholder engagement with the European Commission General Secretariat, European Ombudsman office, national parliament petitions committees, EU Permanent Representations, and civil society organizations

• **Parliamentary Support Services** (2%): Provision of written and oral technical support to Members of the European Parliament in execution of their committee roles including presidential, vice-presidential, rapporteur, and opinion draftsman positions

23A30.name: 23A30
original_activities: Ensure the organization of commission meetings, coordinator meetings, organization of public hearings and ICMs, including the IJF, and the organization of trilogues and interinstitutional negotiations.

Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion; prepare working documents/draft reports/opinions/resolutions/questions (legislative and non-legislative procedures).
Organize missions outside the workplace and ad hoc delegations.
Ensure coordination with other units, services and General Directorates as well as contacts with other institutions, international organizations and civil society.
Organize the commission's communication activities (FEMM website, newsletter, etc.).
23A30.refined_description: Unit 23A30 functions as a specialized parliamentary support unit operating at the second hierarchical level within Direction 23A of the European Parliament's General Management structure, serving as a critical operational backbone for committee administration and parliamentary procedure facilitation. Positioned alongside sibling units 23A10, 23A20, and 23A40 within the broader Direction 23A framework, Unit 23A30 provides essential institutional continuity and administrative capacity that directly supports the Parliament's legislative and representative functions. The unit's primary mission encompasses the full lifecycle management of parliamentary commission proceedings and legislative support functions, with comprehensive responsibility for secretariat and coordination services across multiple operational domains. Through its diversified workload allocation, Unit 23A30 ensures seamless organization of formal commission proceedings while simultaneously providing substantive parliamentary assistance to Members exercising their designated institutional roles, thereby positioning itself as a foundational administrative and procedural support mechanism within the Parliament's committee system. The unit maintains essential liaison with parallel units, services, other General Directorates, and external stakeholders including other EU institutions, international organizations, and civil society actors, ensuring coordinated institutional functioning across the broader European Parliament ecosystem.
23A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Proceedings Management and Interinstitutional Facilitation** (37%): Comprehensive organization and logistical support for commission meetings, coordinator meetings, public hearings, Intergovernmental Meetings (IGMs) including the Inter-Parliamentary Forum (IJF), trilogues, and interinstitutional negotiations—activities that directly enable the Parliament's legislative and supervisory functions

• **Legislative and Parliamentary Member Support** (37%): Substantive written and oral contributions assisting Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) in exercising their designated institutional roles (president, vice-president, rapporteur, draftsman for opinion); comprehensive preparation of working documents, draft reports, opinions, resolutions, and parliamentary questions across both legislative and non-legislative procedures

• **External Representation and Delegation Coordination** (15%): Organization and management of external missions and ad hoc delegations extending parliamentary activities beyond Brussels and Strasbourg

• **Institutional Coordination and External Relations** (7%): Inter-unit coordination with other services and General Directorates; liaison and stakeholder engagement with other EU institutions, international organizations, and civil society entities

• **Communications Infrastructure and Digital Content Management** (4%): Management of commission communication activities including website maintenance (FEMM), newsletter production, and digital information dissemination mechanisms

23A40.name: 23A40
original_activities: Prepare working documents/draft reports (legislative and non-legislative procedures), monitoring 'comitology' acts.
Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings, coordinators' meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Organize public hearings, workshops, working groups and commission missions outside the workplace, etc.
Assist, through written and oral contributions, MEPs in the exercise of their functions as president, vice-president, rapporteur, etc.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society.
Site Web, Newsletter, eMeeting et eCommittee.
Other missions.
23A40.refined_description: Unit 23A40 operates as a specialized secretariat and legislative support unit positioned at the second hierarchical level within the European Parliament's organizational structure, directly subordinate to Direction 23A and functioning as a leaf node without subordinate units. The unit delivers comprehensive administrative and legislative support services to parliamentary commissions through a strategically diversified portfolio of activities designed to facilitate the legislative process, enhance institutional coordination, and strengthen stakeholder engagement. Operating in coordinated collaboration with sibling units 23A10, 23A20, and 23A30 under the unified Direction 23A framework, Unit 23A40 manages both core secretariat functions and specialized legislative documentation services while maintaining critical relationships with other European institutions, professional circles, and civil society organizations. The unit's mission encompasses the complete operational support of commission activities—from formal meeting organization and strategic programming to comprehensive documentation protocols—while simultaneously preparing legislative and non-legislative parliamentary documentation and systematically monitoring regulatory compliance through comitology act tracking. Beyond these primary functions, the unit facilitates stakeholder engagement through public hearings, workshops, and external commission missions; provides direct assistance to Members of the European Parliament in their exercise of institutional roles; manages digital communications infrastructure; and executes additional specialized missions as required by institutional priorities.
23A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Documentation and Regulatory Monitoring** (35%): Preparation of working documents and draft reports for both legislative and non-legislative parliamentary procedures; systematic monitoring and tracking of comitology acts to ensure regulatory compliance, procedural accuracy, and institutional accountability.

• **Commission Secretariat Operations** (30%): Organization and coordination of formal commission meetings, coordinators' meetings, and strategic programming; comprehensive documentation through minute-taking protocols and official record maintenance.

• **External Engagement, Stakeholder Consultation, and Institutional Relations** (19%): Organization of public hearings, workshops, specialized working groups, and external commission missions conducted outside standard workplace locations (10%); maintenance of institutional contacts and relationship development with other European institutions, interested professional circles, and civil society organizations (9%).

• **Parliamentary Member Support Services** (10%): Direct provision of written and oral assistance to Members of the European Parliament in the exercise of institutional functions including roles as president, vice-president, rapporteur, and other designated positions.

• **Digital Communications and Platform Management** (5%): Management of institutional website content and maintenance, newsletter production and distribution, electronic meeting infrastructure, and eCommittee platform functionality.

• **Specialized Institutional Missions** (1%): Implementation of additional ad-hoc assignments and specialized tasks as required by Direction 23A or broader parliamentary institutional priorities.

23B.name: 23B
original_activities: Ensure the supervision and coordination of management units, namely the secretariats of the JURI, LIBE and AFCO committees and the EUDS special committee, as well as the thematic department.
Ensure the provision to parliamentary committees of independent analyzes and research in the areas covered by the department.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Advise the Institution, its bodies and its members, as well as the hierarchy.
Represent management in various internal committees and interinstitutional forums.
23B.refined_description:
Unit 23B functions as a Direction-level management and coordination entity within the General Management directorate (Unit 23) of the European Parliament, operating under the direct authority of the Secretary General. As a critical nexus between parliamentary committees and institutional management, Unit 23B exercises comprehensive supervisory and coordinating authority over four distinct parliamentary committee secretariats—the Committee on Legal Affairs (JURI), the Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs (LIBE), the Committee on Constitutional Affairs (AFCO), and the European Union Delegations Special Committee (EUDS)—while maintaining operational coordination with its thematic department through a network of five specialized child units (23B10, 23B20, 23B30, 23B40, and 23B0050) that collectively manage 33 distinct operational activities. The unit allocates 45% of its workload to ensuring comprehensive supervision and coordination of these management units and their secretariats, supporting their substantive parliamentary functions. Unit 23B dedicates 30% of its capacity to providing independent analytical research and evidence-based policy analyses across the specialized subject areas within its mandate—encompassing legal affairs, civil liberties, constitutional matters, and external relations domains—thereby ensuring parliamentary committees receive rigorous analytical support. The unit exercises delegated financial authority as a subdelegated authorizing officer (10% of workload), enabling efficient budget execution and financial management across its portfolio. Additionally, Unit 23B allocates 10% of its resources to providing strategic advisory services to the Institution's bodies, hierarchical leadership, and individual Members on matters within its purview. Finally, the unit dedicates 5% of its workload to representing management interests and perspectives across various internal parliamentary committees and interinstitutional forums, ensuring alignment between operational management and broader institutional governance structures.

##
23B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Secretariat Supervision & Coordination** (45%): Comprehensive supervision and coordination of management units and secretariats serving the JURI, LIBE, AFCO committees and EUDS special committee, alongside thematic departmental operations, facilitated through five specialized child units managing 33 operational activities

• **Parliamentary Research & Analysis Support** (30%): Provision of independent analyses and specialized evidence-based policy research to parliamentary committees across legal affairs, civil liberties, constitutional affairs, and external relations domains

• **Financial & Budgetary Authority** (10%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer functions enabling efficient budget execution and financial management responsibilities within the unit's purview

• **Strategic Advisory & Institutional Guidance** (10%): Provision of strategic advisory services and institutional counsel to the European Parliament's bodies, hierarchical leadership, and individual parliamentary Members on matters within competence
