• **Management Representation & Interinstitutional Engagement** (5%): Representation of management interests and institutional positions across internal parliamentary committees and interinstitutional forums to ensure alignment between operational management and broader institutional governance structures

23B10.name: 23B10
original_activities: Provide assistance to parliamentary bodies and the secretariats of parliamentary committees (internal and external studies and notes, workshops, notes for the office of the President and the Secretary General) in the field of legal affairs, civil liberties, justice, constitutional affairs (Relevant committees: AFCO, JURI, LIBE, EUDS).

Make external expertise work available to parliamentary committees (identification of needs, calls for tenders, evaluation); develop exchanges with external experts, research institutes and universities (relations with external sources of expertise constitute a main activity, linked to the purchase of external expertise).
Develop relations with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, the dissemination of the work of the Thematic Department and the working tools of the Thematic Department.
Participate in the implementation of a modern IT environment for knowledge management.
Ensure the updating of technical sheets.
23B10.refined_description: Unit 23B10 functions as a specialized thematic department within Direction 23B, serving as the primary analytical and advisory resource for the European Parliament's legislative and policy work in legal affairs, civil liberties, justice, and constitutional affairs. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate structures, Unit 23B10 delivers comprehensive support to four substantive parliamentary committees—AFCO (Constitutional Affairs), JURI (Legal Affairs), LIBE (Civil Liberties and Justice), and EUDS (EU Defence and Security)—along with their associated secretariats and senior institutional leadership including the offices of the President and Secretary General. The unit's operational framework is built upon four integrated functional pillars: (1) production of internal and external studies, analytical notes, workshop materials, and specialized briefing documents; (2) identification and procurement of external expertise through structured calls for tenders and expert evaluation processes, coupled with cultivation of strategic relationships with research institutes, universities, and specialized knowledge centers; (3) coordination with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services to ensure coherent deployment of thematic department methodologies and working tools across the institution; and (4) participation in knowledge management IT environment implementation and maintenance of technical documentation and reference materials. Unit 23B10 operates in lateral coordination with sibling units 23B20, 23B30, 23B40, and 23B0050 within the Direction 23B framework, collectively delivering comprehensive parliamentary support services under the governance structure of Unit 23 and the Secretary General's authority.
23B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Advisory and Research Support** (55%): Comprehensive assistance to parliamentary bodies, committee secretariats (AFCO, JURI, LIBE, EUDS), and senior institutional leadership through production of internal and external studies, analytical notes, workshop development and facilitation, and specialized briefing documents addressing legal affairs, civil liberties, justice, and constitutional affairs matters

• **External Expertise Acquisition and Strategic Relationship Management** (35%): Identification of specialized knowledge requirements, development and management of external expertise procurement processes including calls for tenders and expert evaluation mechanisms, and cultivation of sustained relationships with external research institutes, universities, and specialized knowledge centers to enhance parliamentary analytical capacity with cutting-edge external expertise

• **Institutional Knowledge Coordination and Dissemination** (5%): Development and maintenance of inter-directorate relations with the General Directorate of Parliamentary Research Services, and active promotion of thematic department outputs, methodologies, and working tools throughout the parliamentary institution to ensure coherent institutional knowledge deployment

• **Infrastructure and Documentation Management** (5%): Participation in implementation of modern knowledge management information technology environments (3%), and maintenance and regular updating of current technical reference materials and documentation sheets (2%)

23B20.name: 23B20
original_activities: Assist the Chair with the planning and the organisation of committee meetings, coordinators' meetings, LIBE working groups; prepare draft agendas, chairs notes, minutes, register incoming and draft outgoing letters for the Chair; liaise with the secretariats of the Conference of Committee Chairs, Conference of Presidents and the Bureau, with other European Parliament services and other European Union institutions, etc.

Support the work of LIBE Members in their capacity of Chair, Vice-Chairs, rapporteurs; prepare documents related to the committee's legislative and non-legislative activities (draft reports and opinions, resolutions, amendments and compromise amendments, voting lists) and ensure the follow up and finalisation in view of the plenary stage.

Prepare and participate in interinstitutional legislative negotiations (organise trilogues, shadows and internal and interinstitutional preparatory meetings); assist with the preparation of the European Parliament position in the context of other legislative procedures (consent, consultation).

Organize public hearings, seminars, missions or ad-hoc delegations, and workshops in cooperation with the thematic departments, and meetings with national parliaments jointly with the country holding the Presidency, provide the secretariat for the Joint Parliamentary Scrutiny Group on Europol (JPSG).

Liaise with European Union agencies and bodies within the area of responsibility of LIBE - European Union Agency for Asylum (EUAA), the Fundamental Rights Agency (FRA), the European Union Agency for Drugs (EUDA), EUROJUST, EUROPOL, FRONTEX, CEPOL, eu-LISA, the Anti-money Laundering Agency (AMLA), European Data Protection Supervisor, the European Public Prosecutor (EPPO).

Contribute to the oversight and follow-up on delegated acts and draft implementing acts through a regular newsletter; organise briefings with the Commission at technical and political level, prepare committee resolutions to react to specific acts or draft acts.
Prepare Committee contributions to the annual budgetary and discharge procedures.

Communicate the activities of the Committee through electronic means, including through the European Parliament's web pages and a regular newsletter informing about the latest developments, initiatives and legislative actions within the Committee.
23B20.refined_description:
Unit 23B20 functions as the dedicated secretariat and primary administrative-operational hub for the Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs (LIBE), operating as a Level 2 unit within Direction 23B under the European Parliament's General Management directorate. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses eight integrated activity streams representing 100% of documented workload, all of which directly enable LIBE's legislative, oversight, and inter-institutional functions while maintaining coordination with sibling units 23B10, 23B30, 23B40, and 23B0050 to deliver integrated secretariat services. Core responsibilities encompass committee governance and chairperson support (20%), including planning and organization of committee meetings, coordinators' meetings, and LIBE working groups; preparation of draft agendas, chairs' notes, minutes, and correspondence management with the Conference of Committee Chairs, Conference of Presidents, the Bureau, and other European Parliament and EU institutional services. The unit provides substantive legislative documentation and member support (20%), preparing comprehensive legislative and non-legislative materials including draft reports, opinions, resolutions, amendments, compromise amendments, and voting lists while providing support to Members in leadership capacities (Chair, Vice-Chairs, rapporteurs) and ensuring follow-up and finalization through the plenary stage. Unit 23B20 actively facilitates interinstitutional legislative negotiations (20%), organizing and participating in trilogues, shadow meetings, and preparatory sessions while supporting the European Parliament's position development across consent and consultation procedures. The unit organizes specialized parliamentary activities and external engagement (15%), including public hearings, seminars, missions, ad-hoc delegations, workshops in cooperation with thematic departments, coordination of meetings with national parliaments in collaboration with Presidency countries, and provision of secretariat services for the Joint Parliamentary Scrutiny Group on Europol (JPSG). Institutional liaison and oversight responsibilities (25%) span liaison activities with EU agencies and bodies within LIBE's sectoral remit—including EUAA, FRA, EUDA, EUROJUST, EUROPOL, FRONTEX, CEPOL, eu-LISA, AMLA, the European Data Protection Supervisor, and EPPO—alongside delegated and implementing acts oversight through regular newsletters and technical/political briefings; organization of Commission briefings and preparation of reactive committee resolutions; contributions to budgetary and discharge procedures; and strategic communication of committee activities, legislative developments, and institutional visibility through European Parliament web pages and regular newsletters.

---

##
23B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Administration, Governance, and Chairperson Support** (20%): Coordinate planning and organization of committee meetings, coordinators' meetings, and LIBE working groups; prepare draft agendas, chairs' notes, minutes, and correspondence management; liaise with Conference of Committee Chairs, Conference of Presidents, Bureau, and other EP/EU institutional services; manage incoming and outgoing correspondence for the Chair and institutional stakeholders.

• **Legislative Documentation and Member Support** (20%): Prepare and finalize comprehensive legislative and non-legislative documentation including draft reports, opinions, resolutions, amendments, compromise amendments, and voting lists; provide substantive support to Members serving as Chair, Vice-Chairs, and rapporteurs; ensure follow-up and completion through plenary proceedings.

• **Interinstitutional Legislative Negotiations and Position Development** (20%): Organize and participate in trilogues, shadow meetings, and internal/interinstitutional preparatory meetings; support European Parliament position development for consent and consultation legislative procedures; advance Parliament's negotiating positions across EU institutional channels.

• **Parliamentary Activities, External Engagement, and Specialized Functions** (15%): Organize public hearings, seminars, missions, ad-hoc delegations, and workshops in cooperation with thematic departments; coordinate meetings with national parliaments in collaboration with Presidency countries; provide secretariat services for the Joint Parliamentary Scrutiny Group on Europol (JPSG).

• **Institutional Liaison and Sectoral Agency Coordination** (10%): Maintain liaison relationships with EU agencies and bodies within LIBE's remit (EUAA, FRA, EUDA, EUROJUST, EUROPOL, FRONTEX, CEPOL, eu-LISA, AMLA, EDPS, EPPO); coordinate ongoing engagement across asylum, fundamental rights, drugs, justice, border management, and data protection policy domains.

• **Delegated Acts Oversight and Commission Briefings** (5%): Oversee delegated and implementing acts through regular newsletters and technical/political briefings; organize Commission briefings at technical and political levels; prepare reactive committee resolutions based on delegated acts developments.

• **Budgetary and Discharge Procedure Contributions** (5%): Prepare committee contributions to annual budgetary procedures and discharge procedures; coordinate institutional financial accountability processes.

• **Strategic Communications and Digital Engagement** (5%): Manage committee communications through European Parliament web pages and regular newsletters; document and disseminate committee developments, legislative initiatives, and institutional activities; maintain public transparency of LIBE's work through electronic and institutional publication channels.

23B30.name: 23B30
original_activities: Assist MEPs in the exercise of their functions by preparing various documents: working documents/draft reports/opinions (legislative and non-legislative procedures), written and oral contributions, resolutions, including opinions on legal bases, proposals for reasoned decisions in the context of the procedure on the lifting or defense of immunity, verifications of credentials and litigation.
Ensure the secretariat of the commission (organization of meetings: of commission, of coordinators, with political groups, trilogues, working group meetings, programming, minutes, etc.).
Prepare and monitor votes (drafting and formatting amendments, voting lists and adopted documents).
Write and manage (receive, record) mail for the President of the Commission and other entities.
Other missions (internal contributions, CCC contributions etc., internal training).
Organize public hearings, workshops, working groups and commission missions outside the workplace, etc.
Ensure the monitoring of delegated acts, implementing measures, as well as the processing of reasoned opinions from national parliaments.
Ensure contacts with other institutions (in particular the Court of Justice and the OUEPI), interested circles and civil society.
23B30.refined_description:
Unit 23B30 operates as a specialized support and administrative unit within Direction 23B of the European Parliament's General Management structure, functioning as a Hierarchy Level 2 operational hub that serves directly under Direction-level management while coordinating with sibling units (23B10, 23B20, 23B40, 23B50) to ensure comprehensive institutional support. The unit's primary mission encompasses providing substantive legislative and administrative support to Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) in the exercise of their parliamentary functions while simultaneously ensuring the effective operational management of commission structures, procedures, and institutional relationships. Unit 23B30 integrates five core operational domains: legislative document preparation (30%), commission secretariat and meeting management (20%), parliamentary voting procedures and document administration (15%), administrative correspondence and institutional communications (10%), and a consolidated portfolio of institutional monitoring, external relations, and supplementary governance functions (25%). Through this integrated portfolio of activities, Unit 23B30 functions as an essential administrative and support infrastructure that directly enables parliamentary work, maintains procedural integrity, ensures compliance with EU legislative procedures, and sustains institutional relationships with external bodies including the Court of Justice, the European Union and European Parliament Interinstitutional Relations Office (OUEPI), and civil society stakeholders.

---

##
23B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Support and Documentation Services** (30%): Preparation of comprehensive parliamentary documentation including working documents, draft reports, legislative and non-legislative opinions, written and oral contributions, resolutions, legal basis opinions, reasoned decisions regarding immunity procedures, credential verifications, and litigation-related materials to directly assist MEPs in executing their parliamentary mandates and legislative functions.

• **Commission Operational Management and Secretariat Functions** (20%): Organization and coordination of all commission-related meetings (plenary commission sessions, coordinator meetings, political group consultations, trilogue negotiations, working group sessions), programming of commission activities, and maintenance of official meeting minutes and comprehensive documentation to ensure seamless procedural operations.

• **Voting Procedures and Document Management** (15%): Preparation, drafting, formatting, and monitoring of amendments; maintenance of voting lists; and management of officially adopted legislative documents to ensure procedural accuracy, transparency, and compliance in parliamentary decision-making processes.

• **Administrative Support and Institutional Communications** (10%): Reception, recording, and management of correspondence directed to the Commission President and other institutional entities, ensuring proper administrative documentation, institutional communication protocols, and mail management functions.

• **Institutional Monitoring, External Relations, and Supplementary Governance Functions** (25%): Monitoring of delegated acts and implementing measures (5%), processing and management of reasoned opinions from national parliaments pursuant to the subsidiarity control mechanism (5%), maintenance of institutional contacts with the Court of Justice and OUEPI (5%), engagement with civil society and interested stakeholder circles, organization of public hearings, workshops, external working groups, and commission missions (5%), and provision of internal institutional contributions to the Coordination Committee on Cooperation (CCC), inter-directorate coordination initiatives, and internal staff training activities (5%).

23B40.name: 23B40
original_activities: Ensure the secretariat of the committee: organization of AFCO meetings, Coordinator meetings, shadow rapporteur meetings, trilogues and technical meetings, working group meetings, public hearings, missions, exchanges of views with experts/eminent guests, programming, drafting of presidency sheets, minutes, feedback, letters from the President and various letters, contacts with the CoP and the Bureau, interinstitutional contacts.

Assist, through written and oral contributions, the deputies in the exercise of their functions (President, Vice-presidents, coordinators, rapporteurs, rapporteurs for opinions), prepare documents related to the activities of the committee: draft reports, opinions, resolutions, oral questions, amendments, voting lists for legislative and non-legislative procedures.

Assist, through written and oral contributions, the President and Members of AFCO in their work on the interpretation, application and modification of the Rules of Procedure of the European Parliament.

Ensure management and internal communication (department meetings, reports, website, e-Committee, statistics, training), participation in the various internal project teams and projects of the General Directorate of Citizens' Rights, Justice and Institutional Affairs.
Ensure the monitoring of relations with national Parliaments, joint interparliamentary meetings with representatives of national Parliaments, parliamentary meetings and COSAC.
Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society.
23B40.refined_description:
Unit 23B40 functions as the primary secretariat and comprehensive administrative support structure for the Committee on Constitutional Affairs (AFCO) within the European Parliament, operating as a Level 2 organizational entity under Direction 23B within the General Management hierarchy. The unit's integrated mission encompasses six functionally distinct yet operationally interconnected domains totaling 100% of documented workload allocation, reflecting both top-down strategic direction and bottom-up operational synthesis. As a leaf-node unit within the Direction 23B structure—operating alongside sibling units 23B10, 23B20, 23B30, and 23B0050—Unit 23B40 serves as a critical institutional node supporting the European Parliament's constitutional and institutional governance architecture while maintaining active participation in cross-directorate project teams within the General Directorate of Citizens' Rights, Justice and Institutional Affairs. The unit's organizational positioning and functional scope reflect the constitutional significance of AFCO's work on European Parliament Rules of Procedure, interinstitutional relations, and institutional development, requiring specialized expertise in both parliamentary administration and constitutional affairs. Through its differentiated functional allocation, Unit 23B40 simultaneously manages operational continuity of committee processes, substantive support to parliamentary decision-makers, constitutional interpretation and advisory functions, institutional integration within the General Directorate, and external relational engagement with national parliaments and civil society stakeholders.

---

##
23B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Secretariat and Administrative Operations** (40% of workload): Organization and logistical coordination of all AFCO meetings including plenary committee sessions, Coordinator meetings, shadow rapporteur meetings, interinstitutional trilogues, technical working group meetings, and formal working group sessions; facilitation of public hearings, official parliamentary missions, and expert/eminent guest exchanges; comprehensive administrative documentation management encompassing presidency sheets, official meeting minutes, feedback mechanisms, agenda programming, and presidential correspondence; maintenance of interinstitutional liaison relationships with the College of Presidents, the Bureau, and external institutional partners.

• **Parliamentary Support and Legislative Documentation** (25% of workload): Written and oral assistance to AFCO Members and Officers in the exercise of their parliamentary functions, including direct support to the AFCO President, Vice-presidents, coordinators, rapporteurs, and opinion rapporteurs; preparation of all legislative and non-legislative documentation including draft reports, opinions, resolutions, oral questions, amendments, voting lists, and related parliamentary procedure materials across all committee work streams.

• **Constitutional and Procedural Expertise** (15% of workload): Specialized written and oral advisory contributions to the AFCO President and Members on the interpretation, application, and modification of the European Parliament's Rules of Procedure, reflecting the unit's constitutional affairs function and AFCO's distinctive institutional role in parliamentary governance.

• **Internal Management and Institutional Integration** (10% of workload): Department coordination and internal communications; website and e-Committee digital platform administration and maintenance; statistical analysis and reporting; staff professional training and development; active participation in cross-directorate project teams and initiatives within the General Directorate of Citizens' Rights, Justice and Institutional Affairs.

• **Interparliamentary Relations and External Stakeholder Engagement** (10% of workload): Monitoring and facilitation of relations with national parliaments (5%); coordination of joint interparliamentary meetings, parliamentary exchanges, and COSAC participation (5%); management of institutional contacts and engagement with external EU institutions, professional stakeholder circles, and civil society organizations (5%).

23B0050.name: 23B0050
original_activities: Ensure the organization of parliamentary committee meetings (preparation of the agenda, presidency sheet, preparation of exchanges of views, logistical organization, preparatory meeting with political groups, minutes and feedback notes).

Ensure the organization of public hearings: coordination with political groups to identify speakers, preparation of the program, financial sheet and presidency sheet, logistical organization (accreditation, remote participation, preparation of the room, etc.), communication on the hearing (web, poster).

Ensure the organization of the commission's missions outside the workplace with the preparation of the program in connection with the decisions of the coordinators and in coordination with the political groups, financial sheet, logistical organization (hotel, transport, MCM), preparation of the information document (info pack) containing all the information necessary for the smooth running of the mission, drafting of the mission report, presidency sheet and press release.

Ensure the organization of coordinators' meetings (preparation of the agenda, notes for coordinators, annexes, logistical organization, preparatory meeting with political groups, decisions of coordinators).

Assist MEPs in the exercise of their duties by preparing the working document and the draft EUDS report and setting up a work schedule with the rapporteur. Ensure the organization of technical meetings and meetings with shadow rapporteurs. Prepare meetings with the various institutional actors and stakeholders.

Ensure management and internal communication (department meetings, reports, website, eCommittee, statistics, training). Ensure contacts with other institutions, interested circles and civil society. Manage mail (geda, letters).
23B0050.refined_description: Unit 23B0050 operates as a specialized parliamentary secretariat and coordination entity positioned at the second hierarchical level within Direction 23B, functioning under General Management directorate oversight as a critical operational support backbone for European Parliament committee governance and legislative functions. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses logistical, administrative, and organizational support across all phases of parliamentary committee activities, political group coordination, institutional engagement, and external stakeholder relations. Operating collaboratively with sibling units 23B10, 23B20, 23B30, and 23B40, Unit 23B0050 provides seamless facilitation of formal parliamentary governance structures while maintaining robust support for individual MEP legislative duties and institutional representation. The unit's workload distribution reflects a strategic emphasis on committee operations and external engagement, with proportionate allocations ensuring both operational excellence in formal parliamentary processes and responsive support for rapporteur activities, coordinator decision-making mechanisms, and institutional stakeholder relations. Through integrated coordination of political group liaisons, documentation production, logistical execution, and multi-channel communications dissemination, the unit positions itself as an essential operational entity supporting the Parliament's legislative, representational, and institutional functions across diverse operational contexts.
23B0050.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Committee and Meeting Organization** (60%): Organization of parliamentary committee meetings (25%) encompassing agenda preparation, presidency documentation, facilitation of inter-group exchanges of views, logistical coordination, preparatory consultations with political groups, and production of comprehensive minutes and feedback documentation; coordination and execution of public hearing initiatives (25%) including speaker identification and coordination through political group liaison, comprehensive hearing program development, financial and presidency documentation production, logistical arrangements (accreditation systems, remote participation infrastructure, venue preparation), and multi-channel communication dissemination across web platforms and physical media; and organization of coordinators' meetings (10%) requiring agenda preparation, coordinator briefing materials and supporting documentation, logistical arrangements, political group liaison, and formal documentation of coordinator decisions.

• **External Missions and Stakeholder Engagement** (25%): Comprehensive planning, execution, and documentation of external commission missions (25%) involving program development aligned with coordinator decisions and political group coordination, detailed financial management and logistical arrangements (accommodation, transportation, media and communication management), preparation of comprehensive information documentation and info packs, mission reporting, and institutional communications including presidency sheets and press releases.

• **Parliamentary Support and Rapporteur Coordination** (10%): Direct assistance to MEPs in legislative parliamentary duties (10%) through working document preparation, draft European Union Delegated Statute (EUDS) report development, rapporteur work scheduling, technical and shadow rapporteur meeting organization, and facilitation of meetings with institutional actors and stakeholder constituencies.

• **Internal Administration and Institutional Communications** (5%): Management of departmental operations including internal meetings and reporting procedures, website and eCommittee platform maintenance and administration, statistical analysis and reporting, staff training and development coordination, external institutional liaison and civil society engagement, and comprehensive mail management systems (geda systems and correspondence).

original_activities: Provide leadership to the general management, supervise and coordinate its activities. Ensure the continuity of operation of the operations of the general management, ensuring the resilience and cohesion of the various services of the general management.
Design and coordinate the strategic direction of general management; define your objectives.
Represent the general management within the European Parliament.
Organize the activities of general management.
Represent the general management and coordinate its activities in interinstitutional and international fora, including CITI, CEI, IAMLADP and HINTS.
Provide legal advice within general management and manage questions relating to the protection of personal data.
Contribute to the management of the General Secretariat of the European Parliament.
Exercise the function of delegated authorizing officer.
08.refined_description: Unit 08 functions as the apex strategic leadership and executive coordination directorate of the European Parliament's General Secretariat, operating at the root organizational level with direct reporting to the Secretary General. The unit serves as the primary architect and coordinator of comprehensive executive leadership across the general management function, exercising supervisory and coordinating authority to ensure operational continuity, institutional resilience, and service cohesion across all constituent activities and diverse services within the general management structure (35% of workload). Unit 08 bears primary responsibility for designing and coordinating the strategic direction of general management, defining organizational objectives and institutional priorities while contributing to the broader management framework of the General Secretariat (20% of workload). The unit maintains institutional representation within the European Parliament's internal governance structures and exercises delegated representation of general management in interinstitutional and international forums including CITI, CEI, IAMLADP, and HINTS, thereby coordinating institutional activities across these external engagement platforms (20% of workload). Additionally, Unit 08 organizes and systematizes the operational activities of general management to ensure efficient institutional functioning (10% of workload), provides specialized legal counsel and manages personal data protection compliance matters within the organization (5% of workload), and exercises delegated authorizing officer functions for budgetary, contractual, and administrative authorization matters (3% of workload). Through its hierarchical structure encompassing six subordinate operational units (08-50, 08-60, 08A, 08B, 08C, and 08D), Unit 08 ensures integrated strategic management, operational effectiveness, and institutional coordination across the Parliament's administrative and management infrastructure.
08.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Leadership and Operational Management** (35%): Provision of comprehensive executive leadership to general management with supervisory and coordinating authority; supervision and coordination of all management activities; ensuring operational continuity, organizational resilience, and service cohesion across general management services; and maintaining functional effectiveness across the general management structure.

• **Strategic Direction and Institutional Governance** (20%): Design and coordination of strategic direction for general management; definition of organizational objectives and institutional priorities; establishment of policy frameworks; and contribution to the broader management framework of the General Secretariat of the European Parliament.

• **Institutional and External Representation** (20%): Representation of general management within the European Parliament's internal governance structures (10%); representation and coordination of general management activities in interinstitutional and international forums including CITI, CEI, IAMLADP, and HINTS (10%); and maintenance of institutional relationships across external engagement platforms.

• **Administrative Organization and Coordination** (10%): Organization and systematization of general management operational activities to ensure efficient institutional functioning and coordinated service delivery.

• **Legal Counsel and Data Protection Compliance** (5%): Provision of specialized legal advice and counsel within general management; management of personal data protection matters and compliance with data protection regulations.

• **Delegated Financial and Administrative Authority** (3%): Exercise of delegated authorizing officer functions for budgetary, contractual, and administrative authorization matters.

original_activities: Develop the strategic planning and reporting cycle for senior management and provide strategy advice.

Carry out data collection and analysis; develop indicators and carry out performance measurements. Manage and improve the collection of feedback from customers and team leaders to ensure a service feedback loop and progressive service improvement.

Ensure the design, support and coordination of the strategic project portfolio of general management; represent the general management and its needs within the European Parliament working group which is preparing the transitional phase between the closure of the SPAAK building and its reopening after the renovation work.
Carry out process analyses, monitor internal controls and carry out occasional ex-post checks on transactions.
Coordinate risk management and business continuity plan (including input to relevant inter-DG groups).
Promote an innovative work environment through new projects. Ensure that infrastructure, systems and tools comply with the physical and digital accessibility rules defined by the European Parliament.
Ensure internal management (unit management/administration including staff skills development).
Coordinate EMAS actions within general management.
08-50.refined_description: Unit 08-50 functions as the strategic management and institutional governance directorate within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Unit 08), operating as a critical nexus between executive leadership and operational governance while subordinate to Unit 08 and collaborating horizontally with sibling units 08-60, 08A, 08B, 08D, and 08C. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses eight interconnected functional areas that collectively constitute an integrated management support and oversight framework designed to enable senior management decision-making across the Parliament. Unit 08-50 develops and executes strategic planning frameworks, performance management systems, and institutional governance mechanisms through systematic execution of its core functional responsibilities, which include: developing and managing the strategic planning and reporting cycle for senior management while providing strategic advisory services (20% of workload); conducting systematic data collection, analysis, and performance measurement activities including the design and maintenance of key performance indicators and implementation of customer feedback and team leader feedback mechanisms to facilitate progressive service improvement (20% of workload); designing, supporting, and coordinating the strategic project portfolio on behalf of general management, including active representation of institutional needs within the European Parliament's inter-departmental working group overseeing the SPAAK building closure and renovation transition phases (18% of workload); undertaking process analysis and internal control monitoring functions with periodic ex-post transaction verification (15% of workload); coordinating enterprise-wide risk management and business continuity planning initiatives through participation in relevant inter-directorate general collaborative groups (10% of workload); promoting innovative work environments and ensuring organizational compliance with physical and digital accessibility standards established by the European Parliament (7% of workload); managing internal unit administration, staff skills development, and professional development activities (5% of workload); and coordinating environmental management system (EMAS) actions and sustainability initiatives across general management operations (5% of workload). Collectively, these activities position Unit 08-50 as the institutional intelligence and governance coordination center within the General Management structure, providing comprehensive strategic support, performance oversight, and compliance assurance mechanisms.
08-50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Planning and Executive Guidance** (20%): Develop and manage the strategic planning and reporting cycle for senior management; provide strategic advisory services to institutional leadership to inform institutional decision-making

• **Performance Management and Service Excellence** (20%): Conduct systematic data collection and performance analysis; develop and maintain key performance indicators; establish and manage customer feedback and team leader feedback mechanisms; implement progressive service improvement frameworks and initiatives
