Coordinate and manage the unit.
08A30.refined_description: Unit 08A30 is a specialized operational directorate within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Unit 08A) that serves as the institutional nexus for professional interpreter development, academic partnership coordination, and workforce continuity planning. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while coordinating with 26 sibling units, the unit implements and sustains a comprehensive cooperation strategy with universities and schools training professional interpreters, managing the full spectrum of partnership activities including general management coordination, grant program administration, Memoranda of Understanding (MoU) negotiation and maintenance, and student visit program facilitation. The unit extends the Parliament's training capacity through the organization and delivery of virtual classes and specialized lecture courses conducted in partnership with accredited universities, ensuring continuous professional development and knowledge transfer to successive generations of interpreters while transcending geographic and temporal constraints. Unit 08A30 maintains operational responsibility for the complete technical management lifecycle of the Multipurpose Speech Bank (MSB)—encompassing speech recording, quality evaluation, archival storage systems, and institutional deployment—thereby preserving institutional linguistic and professional knowledge resources for training and reference purposes. Additionally, the unit develops and implements succession planning strategies aligned with the European Parliament's specific operational requirements and contributes to institutional strategic reflection on innovative virtual collaboration models with universities and accredited independent interpreters. These integrated functions position Unit 08A30 as a critical contributor to the Parliament's human resource development objectives and institutional continuity.
08A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Academic Institutional Partnerships & Educational Cooperation Management** (35%): Implementation and strategic management of cooperation strategy with universities and schools training professional interpreters; coordination of general management activities within this domain; administration of grant programs; negotiation, establishment, and maintenance of Memoranda of Understanding (MoU) with partner institutions; facilitation and coordination of student visit programs

• **Virtual Professional Development & Distance Learning Delivery** (35%): Organization and management of virtual classes and specialized lecture courses conducted in partnership with accredited universities; delivery of continuous professional development programming through digital platforms; expansion of training accessibility beyond traditional geographic and temporal constraints

• **Speech Resources Management & Multipurpose Speech Bank Operations** (15%): Management of parliamentary speech recording processes; evaluation and quality assurance of speech materials; implementation of archival storage and institutional deployment systems; technical infrastructure management of the Multipurpose Speech Bank (MSB); coordination of institutional access and utilization of recorded materials for training and reference purposes

• **Strategic Workforce Planning & Innovation Development** (10%): Development and implementation of succession planning strategies for the interpreting profession aligned with the European Parliament's specific operational requirements and evolving needs; strategic reflection on and development of innovative virtual cooperation models with universities and accredited independent interpreters; contribution to institutional frameworks for future professional development

• **Unit Coordination & Administrative Operations** (5%): Internal management and coordination of Unit 08A30 operational functions, resources, and administrative activities

08A3010.name: 08A3010
original_activities: Organize interpreter selection tests (accreditation, competition, language addition).
Cooperate at the interinstitutional level and develop technological solutions facilitating the selection procedure.
Work closely with the Interpreting Academy Service of the Multilingualism and Succession Planning Unit (MSPU) to organize exit assessments from the academy.
Manage the service.
08A3010.refined_description: Unit 08A3010, designated as the Interpreter Selection and Accreditation Unit, operates as a specialized operational component within the European Parliament's General Management directorate (Unit 08A), where it functions as a leaf-node unit coordinating with 26 sibling units while maintaining strategic alignment with parent directorate objectives. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive management of interpreter professional qualification and recruitment processes across all language combinations required by the institution, organizing and administering interpreter selection tests through three distinct procedural pathways: accreditation assessments for external interpreters, competitive examinations for permanent staff recruitment, and language addition qualifications for existing personnel—activities constituting 75% of its operational workload. Complementing this core function, Unit 08A3010 dedicates 15% of its resources to fostering interinstitutional cooperation and developing technological innovations that enhance the efficiency, accessibility, and effectiveness of selection procedures, positioning the European Parliament at the forefront of institutional best practices in interpreter recruitment while ensuring procedural harmonization across European institutions. The unit allocates an additional 5% of its capacity to collaborative engagement with the Interpreting Academy Service of the Multilingualism and Succession Planning Unit (MSPU), specifically coordinating exit assessments that validate academy graduates' competency levels and readiness for operational deployment into professional roles. The remaining 5% of the unit's workload is devoted to essential internal service management, administrative functions, and operational continuity measures necessary to sustain institutional effectiveness and compliance. Through these integrated activities, Unit 08A3010 directly supports the Parliament's institutional capacity to maintain a highly qualified, multilingual interpreting workforce while advancing interinstitutional standards for professional selection and development.
08A3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Interpreter Selection and Accreditation Management** (75%): Comprehensive organization and administration of interpreter selection tests encompassing three procedural pathways—professional accreditation assessments for external interpreters, competitive examinations for permanent staff recruitment, and language addition qualifications for existing personnel—across all language combinations required by the institution

• **Interinstitutional Cooperation and Technological Innovation** (15%): Development and implementation of technological solutions and collaborative frameworks that enhance the efficiency and accessibility of selection procedures while ensuring procedural harmonization and best practice alignment across European institutions

• **Academy Exit Assessment Coordination** (5%): Collaborative engagement with the Interpreting Academy Service (MSPU) to organize and conduct exit assessments validating academy graduate competency levels and readiness for operational deployment

• **Service Management and Administration** (5%): Internal operational management, administrative support, and service continuity functions necessary for sustained unit effectiveness and institutional compliance

08A3020.name: 08A3020
original_activities: Organize academies (selection and confirmation tests, programming, implementation) and reflect on strategies for priority languages.
Organize the virtual coaching program.
Manage the service.
08A3020.refined_description: Unit 08A3020 operates as a specialized operational unit within the General Management directorate (Unit 08A) of the European Parliament, positioned at hierarchy level 2 and functioning as an independent leaf node without subordinate units. The unit's primary mission centers on the strategic development and comprehensive delivery of language training and professional development programs that support institutional capacity-building objectives and ensure staff possess requisite linguistic competencies aligned with the Parliament's multilingual operational requirements. The unit's operational workload is predominantly allocated (80%) to the full lifecycle management of language academies, encompassing candidate selection and confirmation testing administration, curriculum programming and scheduling, implementation oversight, and critical strategic reflection on priority language designation and pedagogical approaches within institutional frameworks. Complementing this core function, Unit 08A3020 dedicates 15% of its operational capacity to the organization, coordination, and delivery of virtual coaching programs utilizing digital platforms and remote engagement modalities to extend professional development accessibility and flexibility for Parliament staff. The remaining 5% of the unit's documented workload is dedicated to essential operational and administrative management activities, including service management, administrative coordination, and operational continuity functions that support the efficiency and sustainability of all programmatic activities. Through these integrated functions, Unit 08A3020 contributes directly to the broader institutional objectives of Units 08A and 08 while advancing the European Parliament's commitment to multilingual professional development and institutional language capacity.
08A3020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Language Academy Development and Delivery** (80%): Comprehensive organization and management of language academies encompassing the complete operational lifecycle including candidate selection and confirmation testing administration, curriculum programming and scheduling, implementation execution, and strategic reflection on priority language identification and pedagogical approaches within institutional capacity-building frameworks

• **Virtual Professional Development Programs** (15%): Organization, coordination, and delivery of virtual coaching programs utilizing digital platforms and remote engagement modalities to enhance accessibility, flexibility, and professional development opportunities for Parliament staff

• **Operational and Administrative Management** (5%): Service management, administrative coordination, and operational continuity functions ensuring the efficiency and sustainability of all programmatic activities across the unit

08A40.name: 08A40
original_activities: Make meeting documentation (and possibly other types of information) available to help Directorate-General interpreters provide a quality service. Make documentation available to external interpretation providers.
Ensure the implementation of specific thematic training for interpreters.
Ensure the implementation of language training, including the management of external courses for interpreters.
Produce 'Enhanced Meeting Digests', including their terminology component, to ensure the quality of the interpretation provided in the legislative process.
Monitor market developments in the field of new technologies and interpretation support tools; organize training on this subject.
Implement special training measures for auxiliary conference interpreters (AIC).
Implement the general management's accessibility policy regarding products and services for interpreters.
Coordinate and manage the unit.
08A40.refined_description: Unit 08A40 functions as the Interpretation Support and Training Centre within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Directorate 08), operating as a specialized Level 2 operational leaf node under the strategic direction of Unit 08A. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses eight interconnected operational activities designed to enhance the quality and effectiveness of interpretation services across the institution by providing integrated support infrastructure to both internal Directorate-General interpreters and external interpretation service providers. Operating collaboratively with 26 sibling units within the 08A directorate hierarchy, Unit 08A40 delivers specialized support across documentation provision, professional development, quality assurance, technology innovation, and accessibility implementation. The unit's operational focus prioritizes the curation and dissemination of meeting documentation and information resources (30% of workload) to enable quality service delivery and operational consistency across parliamentary proceedings. Complementing this documentation function, the unit commits substantial resources to professional development activities encompassing thematic interpreter training, language training program management with external course coordination, specialized auxiliary conference interpreter training, and the production of Enhanced Meeting Digests with integrated terminology components to directly support interpretation quality during legislative processes. Additional operational responsibilities include monitoring emerging interpretation support technologies while organizing associated training initiatives, implementing accessibility policies for interpreter-facing products and services, and executing unit coordination and general management functions. Collectively, these activities represent a comprehensive support ecosystem that maintains institutional interpretation service excellence while contributing to broader General Management operational objectives and institutional transparency goals.
08A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Documentation and Information Services Provision** (30%): Curation and dissemination of meeting documentation and information materials to both internal Directorate-General interpreters and external interpretation service providers to facilitate quality service delivery, operational consistency, and institutional transparency across parliamentary proceedings.

• **Professional Development and Training Implementation** (45%): Implementation of thematic training programs for interpreters (15%), management and coordination of language training programs including external course provision (15%), production of Enhanced Meeting Digests with integrated terminology components to support interpretation quality during legislative processes (15%), and delivery of specialized training measures for auxiliary conference interpreters (5%).

• **Technology Monitoring and Innovation Support** (10%): Market surveillance of emerging technologies and interpretation support tools with organization of associated staff training initiatives to ensure institutional proficiency with evolving solutions and maintain competitive service delivery capabilities.

• **Accessibility Policy Implementation** (5%): Implementation of General Management accessibility policies specifically tailored to interpreter-focused products and services to ensure equitable access to unit resources and support infrastructure.

• **Unit Administration and Coordination** (5%): Overall coordination and management of unit operations, resource allocation, inter-unit collaboration within the 08A directorate structure, and administrative functions to support operational effectiveness.

08B.name: 08B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of the Directorate. In collaboration with other departments, ensure the continuity and efficiency of operations in a context of growing and changing activity, while taking into account the resilience and cohesion of teams.

Participate in the definition of the strategic orientations of the general management as part of the management team and guarantee the effective application of budgetary, strategic and operational decisions, in particular relating to the management of requests for interpretation and events according to institutional priorities and rules relating to integrity and transparency.

Provide the interpretation service for the bodies of the European Parliament, political groups and other users, in accordance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism. Ensure the availability of resources for priority legislative meetings, in particular through the renewal of the AIC pool and supplement them, if necessary, in strict compliance with the principles of good financial management and the environmental objectives of the institution.

Ensure regular dialogue with interpretation user services, particularly within the framework of the OCG. Coordinate consultation with the professional delegation of AICs to the European Parliament and contribute to consultation with the delegation of civil servant interpreters to the European Parliament.
Guarantee interinstitutional coordination in the areas under the responsibility of the Directorate and ensure cooperation with interpretation services at international level.
Participate in general management's information, communication and awareness campaigns.
Monitor the evolution of the IT tool and, where appropriate, provide the necessary expertise and support to projects aimed at its renewal, in cooperation with the Resources Department.
08B.refined_description: Unit 08B functions as the strategic direction and operational coordination nucleus within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Unit 08), serving as the primary executive body responsible for directing six subordinate operational units (08B10, 08B20, 08B30, 08B3010, 08B40, and 08B90) that collectively deliver 40 distinct interpretation and multilingual service activities across the institution. The unit's operational framework encompasses four interconnected strategic pillars integrated across directorate-level governance, strategic decision-making, core service delivery, and stakeholder management. Unit 08B ensures comprehensive directional oversight, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services (30% of workload), maintaining operational continuity and efficiency within contexts of expanding institutional demands while preserving team resilience and organizational cohesion. As an active constituent member of the management team, Unit 08B participates in defining general management's strategic orientations and guarantees effective implementation of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions—particularly those governing interpretation request management and institutional event coordination in strict adherence to integrity and transparency protocols (25% of workload). The unit delivers direct multilingual interpretation services to all European Parliament bodies, political groups, and authorized users in compliance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism, with explicit responsibility for ensuring resource availability for priority legislative meetings through systematic renewal and supplementation of the Accredited Interpreter Community (AIC) pool in accordance with sound financial management principles and institutional environmental sustainability objectives (21% of workload). Unit 08B maintains continuous stakeholder engagement through regular dialogue with interpretation user services via the OCG framework and coordinates professional consultations with both the AIC professional delegation and the civil servant interpreter delegation to the European Parliament (13% of workload), while additionally ensuring interinstitutional coordination across its areas of responsibility, fostering cooperation with international interpretation services (5% of workload), contributing to general management communication and awareness initiatives (3% of workload), and monitoring the evolution of interpretation management IT tools in cooperation with the Resources Department to support necessary system renewal projects (3% of workload). Through this comprehensive portfolio of activities, Unit 08B operationalizes strategic institutional direction while maintaining coordination with sibling units (08-50, 08-60, 08A, 08D, 08C) within the broader General Management framework.
08B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Leadership and Operational Coordination** (30%): Ensures directional oversight, coordination, and animation of all six subordinate operational units and Directorate services; maintains operational continuity and efficiency within expanding institutional demands; prioritizes team resilience, cohesion, and organizational sustainability across evolving service contexts.

• **Strategic Governance and Decision Implementation** (25%): Participates actively in management team's definition of general management strategic orientations; guarantees effective implementation of budgetary, strategic, and operational decisions; oversees interpretation request management and institutional event coordination in strict compliance with institutional integrity and transparency protocols.

• **Interpretation Service Delivery and Resource Management** (21%): Provides multilingual interpretation services to European Parliament bodies, political groups, and authorized users under the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism; ensures systematic resource availability for priority legislative meetings; manages Accredited Interpreter Community (AIC) pool renewal and supplementation in accordance with sound financial management principles and institutional environmental sustainability objectives.

• **Stakeholder Engagement and Professional Consultation** (13%): Maintains regular dialogue with interpretation user services through the OCG framework; coordinates professional consultations with the AIC professional delegation and civil servant interpreter delegation to the European Parliament; ensures continuous stakeholder alignment with service delivery priorities.

• **Interinstitutional Coordination, International Cooperation, and Technology Infrastructure Oversight** (11%): Ensures interinstitutional coordination across Directorate responsibility areas and fosters cooperation with international interpretation services (5%); participates in general management information, communication, and awareness initiatives (3%); monitors evolution of interpretation management IT tools and provides expertise supporting system renewal projects in cooperation with the Resources Department (3%).

08B10.name: 08B10
original_activities: Lead recruitment operations for conference interpreters (AICs) in the long, medium and short term, for in situ, remote and mission assignments outside three locations, by applying principles of good financial management.
Organize and manage the unit, including rotations, secondments and replacements. Participate in projects such as the Next Generation Professionals program.

Contribute to maintaining a high level of quality of interpretation services provided by AICs and good integration of recently tested AICs, in consultation with the heads of linguistic units and interpreter training.
Cooperate with other management services with a view to a good estimation of long and medium-term recruitment needs, particularly from the perspective of good financial management.
Contribute to the necessary adaptations of IT tools with a view to greater efficiency of the unit's operations.
Provide, in a concise and regular manner, statistics, analyses, as well as productivity measurements concerning AIC contracts.
Contribute to social dialogue with AIC representatives for all questions relating to the management of AIC contracts.
08B10.refined_description: Unit 08B10 functions as the primary operational management nucleus for Accredited Interpreter Contractor (AIC) recruitment and personnel administration within the European Parliament's General Management Directorate, serving as a specialized component of Direction 08B's strategic coordination framework. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility for leading recruitment operations for conference interpreters across all assignment horizons—long-term, medium-term, and short-term—encompassing in situ assignments, remote delivery modalities, and mission-based work outside the three primary Parliament locations, while applying rigorous principles of sound financial management (65% of workload). Unit 08B10 simultaneously manages its own organizational operations through rotation cycles, secondment arrangements, and staff replacement procedures, while actively participating in institutional development initiatives such as the Next Generation Professionals program (10% of workload). The unit maintains elevated quality standards for interpretation services and ensures optimal integration of newly tested AICs into operational structures through direct collaboration with heads of linguistic units and interpreter training divisions (9% of workload). In coordination with other management services across the Parliament, Unit 08B10 conducts systematic, evidence-based estimation of long-term and medium-term recruitment requirements with explicit attention to financial sustainability, budgetary alignment, and resource optimization (8% of workload). The unit contributes to continuous process improvement through adaptation of information technology tools supporting unit operations to increase overall efficiency (3% of workload), while producing regular statistical analyses, analytical assessments, and productivity measurements concerning AIC contract management and performance (3% of workload). Additionally, Unit 08B10 participates in ongoing structured social dialogue with AIC representative organizations regarding all contractual and employment-related matters affecting the specialized interpreter workforce (2% of workload).
08B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Recruitment Operations & Financial Management** (65%): Lead comprehensive recruitment management for Accredited Interpreter Contractors across all assignment modalities—in situ, remote, and mission-based deployments outside the three primary Parliament locations—across long, medium, and short-term horizons, with full application of rigorous sound financial management principles.

• **Organizational Management & Institutional Development** (10%): Manage internal unit operations including staff rotations, secondment arrangements, and replacement procedures; actively participate in institutional advancement programs such as the Next Generation Professionals initiative.

• **Quality Assurance & Professional Integration** (9%): Maintain high-quality standards for interpretation service delivery and ensure effective integration of newly tested AICs through direct consultation with linguistic unit heads and interpreter training service leadership.

• **Strategic Planning & Inter-Service Coordination** (8%): Cooperate with institutional management services to develop evidence-based long and medium-term recruitment forecasts and requirement estimations, with emphasis on financial sustainability, budgetary alignment, and operational capacity planning.

• **Operational Efficiency & Performance Analytics** (6%): Adapt information technology tools and systems to enhance unit operational efficiency (3%) and provide regular statistical analyses, analytical assessments, and productivity metrics concerning AIC contract performance and management (3%).

• **Labor Relations & Stakeholder Engagement** (2%): Conduct ongoing structured social dialogue with AIC representative organizations regarding all questions pertaining to contract management, employment conditions, and workforce-related matters affecting the interpreter workforce.

08B20.name: 08B20
original_activities: Ensure the composition and management of teams of interpreters for the European Parliament and other institutions (EC, CoR, EESC) according to the needs of users: development of the program and management of it during the current week by integrating hybrid and non-hybrid meetings in a balanced manner. Ensure the integration of KPIs and predefined groups while respecting the limitations of the rules in force for the assignment of interpreters. Carry out administrative follow-up.

Manage the medium-term program; carry out the evaluation of the feasibility of requests based on the availability of resources in compliance with the regulatory framework. Estimate the recruitment needs for interpreters for the AIC recruitment unit. Follow up on requests to ensure the provision of the service. Manage and coordinate meetings provided in cooperation with the Multilingual Solutions Unit as well as 3-worksite or hybrid assignments.
Ensure coordination and management of the unit.

Ensure the rebalancing of the workload during the week and in the medium term, and check all parameters with particular attention to the rebalancing between meetings with or without remote participation as well as for the contributions of the heads of linguistic units.

Work on the technical optimization and automation of the interpreter assignment system in projects managed in cooperation with the IT services of the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation and the General Directorate for Information Technology and Cybersecurity.
Ensure communication and coordination with all relevant services regarding (un)availability for training/virtual classes/tests/competitions; ensure coding and monitoring.

Manage absences of civil servant and AIC interpreters/inform and communicate regarding potential replacements. Coordinate and communicate with the AIC Recruitment Unit, the AIC Payments Unit and the Interpreter Training and Support Unit.

Manage contacts with DG SCIC and, in particular, the resumption of civil servant contracts/loans in order to provide the service as well as the loan of AICs to DG SCIC in cooperation with the AIC Management Service.
08B20.refined_description: Unit 08B20 functions as the critical operational hub for interpreter team composition, scheduling, and resource management within the European Parliament's multilingual interpretation infrastructure, serving as a Level 2 unit under Direction 08B. The unit's primary mission encompasses the comprehensive coordination and real-time deployment of multilingual interpreter teams across the European Parliament, the European Commission, the Committee of the Regions, and the European Economic and Social Committee through dynamic weekly scheduling that integrates hybrid and non-hybrid meeting modalities while maintaining strict compliance with established regulatory frameworks and key performance indicators. Unit 08B20 concurrently executes medium-term program planning and resource feasibility assessments, conducting recruitment needs analysis in coordination with the AIC Recruitment Unit and managing inter-institutional meeting provisions through cooperation with the Multilingual Solutions Unit for multi-site and hybrid assignments. The unit maintains strategic coordination and management responsibilities while implementing continuous workload rebalancing mechanisms across weekly and medium-term cycles, with particular attention to equilibrating remote participation configurations and ensuring equitable linguistic unit contributions. Unit 08B20 drives technical optimization and systems automation initiatives in partnership with the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation and the General Directorate for Information Technology and Cybersecurity, manages interpreter availability communications for professional development activities including training, virtual classes, tests, and competitions, administers civil servant and AIC interpreter absence management with cross-unit communication to the AIC Recruitment Unit, Payments Unit, and Training and Support Unit, and maintains institutional liaison with DG SCIC regarding civil servant contract resumptions and AIC loan arrangements. Collectively, these activities position Unit 08B20 as an indispensable operational linchpin ensuring continuous, compliant, and efficient multilingual interpretation service delivery across the European Parliament's institutional ecosystem.
08B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Weekly and Medium-Term Interpreter Team Composition and Scheduling Management** (75%): Ensures composition and deployment of multilingual interpreter teams across the European Parliament and partner institutions (EC, CoR, EESC) through dynamic weekly scheduling programs that integrate hybrid and non-hybrid meeting modalities in balanced proportion while maintaining strict compliance with regulatory assignment rules and key performance indicators (55%); executes medium-term program development and planning with feasibility assessment of interpretation requests based on resource availability within regulatory compliance frameworks, recruitment needs estimation in coordination with the AIC Recruitment Unit, and management of multi-site and hybrid meeting assignments in cooperation with the Multilingual Solutions Unit (20%)

• **Workload Optimization, System Balancing, and Rebalancing** (6%): Ensures systematic rebalancing of interpreter workload distribution across weekly and medium-term cycles; monitors and adjusts parameters with particular attention to equilibrating meetings with and without remote participation modalities; coordinates contributions from heads of linguistic units to maintain equitable resource distribution and compliance with established frameworks

• **Technical Systems Development and Modernization** (5%): Collaborates on technical optimization and automation initiatives for the interpreter assignment system through partnerships with the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation and the General Directorate for Information Technology and Cybersecurity to improve operational efficiency and system performance

• **Interpreter Availability Management and Professional Development Coordination** (3%): Manages and ensures communication regarding interpreter (un)availability for training, virtual classes, tests, and competitions; implements coding and monitoring protocols for these professional development activities to maintain current capability levels

• **Personnel Management and Inter-institutional Coordination** (6%): Administers absence management for both civil servant and AIC interpreters with coordinated replacement communications to the AIC Recruitment Unit, AIC Payments Unit, and Interpreter Training and Support Unit; maintains institutional liaison with DG SCIC regarding civil servant contract resumptions and AIC loan arrangements through the AIC Management Service; executes unit-level coordination and strategic management functions to ensure operational coherence

08B30.name: 08B30
original_activities: Manage meeting requests, process room requests for meetings of European Parliament bodies, political groups, directorates-general and other European Parliament entities. Code plenary sessions, manage room emergencies and resolve room availability issues.

Ensure the management of requests for interpretation in accordance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism, in interaction with DGs and political groups. Manage requests for interpretation outside the framework and from administrative bodies as well as contacts with interpretation clients and services of DG LINC, and the coordination of services in the DG. Manage last-minute language regime changes based on resource availability.

Be the single point of contact (within the European Parliament) to ensure the processing and monitoring of requests for exhibitions from MEPs, EP services and other European institutions. Ensure the proper application of the rules and prepare the files submitted for approval to the responsible Quaestor or the Secretary General, depending on the applicant. Prepare files for the College of Quaestors for all other ad-hoc requests; advise on the rules on events in the EP.

Be the single point of contact to process, manage and track conference, meeting and event requests from other institutions and external clients. For the use of the Chamber in Brussels, coordinate with requesters and Parliament services, establish financial statements and provide any relevant information to facilitate the Bureau's decision-making. Prepare administrative responses in close collaboration with the Office of the Secretary General.

Ensure the proper application of transparency rules to requests for exhibitions, meetings and events organized at the European Parliament in collaboration with the Transparency Unit of the Directorate General of the Presidency. Manage and regularly provide unit-monitored data on meeting room usage and other statistics and analysis of different types of department-managed events that are necessary for business management.

Provide internal support within the European Parliament, including providing advice on meeting rules and procedures. Ensure regular coordination with political and administrative stakeholders involved in managing meeting requests. Intervenes, as necessary, to resolve overlaps, conflicting requests or other meeting issues.

Be the single point of contact to prepare and monitor requests for organizing events on the Esplanade Solidarność 1980. Inform stakeholders and clients/event organizers, internal or external to the EP. Manage requests under the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for a dedicated space for long-term exhibitions of rotating presidencies on the Esplanade. Maintain contacts and negotiate with Belgian regional and local authorities.
Ensure coordination and management of the unit.
08B30.refined_description: Unit 08B30 functions as the European Parliament's central administrative hub for meeting coordination, multilingual services, and institutional events management, operating as a critical operational support function within the General Management directorate (Unit 08) under the strategic direction of Unit 08B. The unit serves as the primary operational interface between parliamentary bodies (including political groups and directorates-general), external institutional clients, and internal administrative services, executing eight integrated activity streams that collectively ensure institutional transparency, multilingual accessibility, and efficient resource utilization across the Parliament's meeting and event infrastructure. Unit 08B30 maintains operational coordination with sibling units 08B10, 08B20, 08B3010, 08B40, and 08B90 within the Direction 08B structure to support overarching General Management directorate objectives. The unit's comprehensive portfolio encompasses meeting logistics and room administration, multilingual interpretation services coordination, exhibition request processing and approval workflows, external conference and event hosting arrangements, institutional transparency compliance, operational analytics on facility usage patterns, internal procedural guidance and stakeholder coordination, and specialized event operations on the Esplanade Solidarność 1980 venue under established Memoranda of Understanding with Belgian regional authorities. Through these interconnected functions, Unit 08B30 bridges parliamentary bodies, external stakeholders, and internal administrative services while maintaining institutional compliance standards and optimizing the Parliament's physical and administrative infrastructure.
08B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Meeting and Room Management Operations** (20%): Comprehensive administration of meeting requests and room allocation for all European Parliament bodies, political groups, and directorates-general; coding of plenary sessions; emergency room issue resolution; management of facility availability conflicts and scheduling complications; and optimization of parliamentary facility utilization.

• **Multilingual Services Coordination** (20%): Strategic management of interpretation services in strict accordance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism; coordination with DGs, political groups, and DG LINC; administration of non-framework and non-standard interpretation requests; real-time adjustment of language regimes based on available resources; and liaison with external interpretation clients and service providers.

• **Exhibitions and Internal Events Processing** (15%): Single-point-of-contact responsibility for exhibition request processing, monitoring, and approval workflows from MEPs and EP services; application of institutional rules and procedures; preparation of approval files for Quaestors or Secretary General; processing of ad-hoc requests for the College of Quaestors; and advisory services on event regulations within the European Parliament.

• **External Institutions and Events Management** (15%): Centralized processing and tracking of conference, meeting, and event requests from external institutions and clients; coordination with requesters and Parliament services regarding Chamber usage in Brussels; preparation of financial statements and administrative responses in collaboration with the Office of the Secretary General; and provision of relevant information to support Bureau decision-making processes.

• **Transparency Compliance, Data Management, and Specialized Venue Operations** (10%): Enforcement of transparency rules for all exhibitions, meetings, and events through collaboration with the Presidency's Transparency Unit; collection, monitoring, and regular provision of meeting room usage statistics, facility usage data, and analytical reporting on departmentally-managed events for institutional business management and decision-support purposes; management of Esplanade Solidarność 1980 venue operations including rotating presidency exhibition coordination under the Memorandum of Understanding; and stakeholder communication and negotiation with Belgian regional and local authorities.

• **Internal Advisory Support and Stakeholder Coordination** (10%): Provision of expert guidance to European Parliament stakeholders on meeting rules, procedures, and organizational requirements; regular coordination with political and administrative entities involved in request management; resolution of scheduling conflicts, overlapping requests, and facility availability disputes; and maintenance of internal procedural frameworks for operational efficiency.

• **Unit Leadership and Operational Management** (5%): Overall operational coordination, management responsibilities, and internal coordination functions ensuring seamless integration of all unit activities and alignment with Direction 08B strategic objectives.

08B3010.name: 08B3010
original_activities: Ensure the management of interpretation reservations - as a single point of contact within the Directorate-General for logistics and conference interpretation - for all meetings of the bodies of the European Parliament, the committees and delegations of DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG and DG EXPO, political groups, DGs. Advise and guide clients on their interpretation needs.

Ensure the management of interpretation requests in accordance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism. Ensure compliance with the rules governing meetings of political groups as well as plenary sessions. Ensure the counting of time slots for meetings with parallel interpretation. Ensure a fair weekly distribution of meetings with interpretation.

Ensure contacts with the services of the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation regarding interpretation requests. Manage last-minute language regime changes based on resource availability.

Monitor and coordinate last minute changes during the current week in close collaboration with the Programming Unit/Directorate B, the IPXP Calendar Unit, political groups as well as all other operational entities.
Ensure monitoring of the progressive development of the MRS application for service activities.
Define, write and standardize guidelines and procedures. Provide statistics and analyzes in a concise and regular manner.
08B3010.refined_description: Unit 08B3010 operates as the centralized interpretation reservation and management hub within the European Parliament's organizational framework, functioning as a critical operational unit at hierarchical level 2 under Direction 08B within the General Management structure (Unit 08). The unit serves as the single point of contact for all interpretation logistics and conference coordination across multiple Directorates-General—specifically DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG, and DG EXPO—and institutional bodies including parliamentary committees, delegations, and political groups, managing comprehensive interpretation reservations and providing client advisory services on interpretation requirements (30% of workload). The unit ensures rigorous compliance with the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism and parliamentary meeting regulations through dedicated management of interpretation requests, including time-slot accounting for parallel interpretation sessions and equitable weekly distribution of interpretation-resourced meetings across all parliamentary bodies (20% of workload). Operationally, Unit 08B3010 maintains continuous direct liaison with the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation, managing dynamic language regime adjustments in response to real-time resource availability constraints and operational requirements (20% of workload). The unit demonstrates operational agility through close coordination with the Programming Unit/Directorate B, the IPXP Calendar Unit, political groups, and all stakeholder entities to monitor and coordinate last-minute scheduling changes and interpretation modifications during active parliamentary weeks (15% of workload). Additionally, the unit oversees the progressive development and implementation of the MRS application system supporting service delivery operations (10% of workload), while establishing standardized procedural guidelines, documentation protocols, and providing regular statistical analysis and performance metrics to organizational leadership (5% of workload). As a leaf node unit without subordinate organizational structures, 08B3010 operates collaboratively with sibling units 08B10, 08B20, 08B30, 08B40, and 08B90 within the broader General Management framework, positioning itself as an essential operational coordinator ensuring seamless multilingual communication infrastructure across the Parliament's diverse institutional functions.
08B3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Interpretation Reservation Management and Client Advisory Services** (30%): Serves as the centralized single point of contact for all interpretation reservation management across DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, DG BUDG, DG EXPO, and other parliamentary bodies; provides comprehensive client guidance and advisory services on interpretation needs and requirements for all committees, delegations, and political groups.

• **Regulatory Compliance, Resource Allocation, and Governance Management** (40%): Ensures strict adherence to the Code of Conduct for Multilingualism and parliamentary meeting regulations; manages interpretation request processing, time-slot accounting for parallel interpretation sessions, and equitable weekly distribution of interpretation-resourced meetings; maintains direct liaison with the General Directorate for Logistics and Conference Interpretation and manages dynamic language regime adjustments based on real-time resource availability constraints.

• **Operational Coordination and Real-Time Adaptation** (15%): Monitors and coordinates last-minute scheduling changes and interpretation modifications during active parliamentary weeks through close collaboration with the Programming Unit/Directorate B, the IPXP Calendar Unit, political groups, and all operational stakeholder entities; maintains operational agility in response to emerging requirements.

• **Systems Development, Process Standardization, and Institutional Reporting** (15%): Oversees the progressive development and implementation of the MRS application system supporting service delivery operations (10%); defines, establishes, standardizes, and documents operational guidelines and procedural protocols while generating regular statistical analysis and performance metrics to support institutional decision-making (5%).

08B40.name: 08B40
original_activities: Plan and manage the provision of interpretation (provided remotely, or locally) and technical services for official missions of the European Parliament outside the three workplaces, as well as for external meetings of political groups, according to user needs; in coordination with the conference programming and coordination units, as well as the conference technicians and AIC recruitment units.

Manage the provision of complementary multilingual services (e.g. the provision of external remote interpretation for non-essential meetings), including the preparation and management of related financial procedures (public procurement) in cooperation with the Budget Unit.

Ensure the management of requests for interpretation emanating from the Presidency of the European Parliament as well as individual requests from Members (IAP) in accordance with the applicable rules; by proceeding with the creation of the meeting in the PERICLES database, the possible recruitment of AICs with the requested linguistic profile as well as the assignment of interpreters to the program.
Coordinate and manage the unit; ensure the implementation and compliance with internal control standards within the unit.

Recruit, upon specific request, international sign language interpreters for European Parliament meetings; manage lists of international sign language interpreters; act as a lead on any questions regarding sign language interpretation.
Intervene, if necessary, on other ad hoc interpretation requests requiring special attention.
08B40.refined_description: Unit 08B40 functions as the specialized operational services coordination hub for interpretation and multilingual communication within Direction 08B of the European Parliament's General Management structure, responsible for orchestrating comprehensive interpretation service delivery across the institution's diverse operational contexts and external engagements. The unit's core mission encompasses the planning, coordination, and delivery of interpretation services—delivered through remote, local, or hybrid modalities—for official European Parliament missions conducted outside the three principal workplaces and for external meetings convened by political groups, executed in close coordination with conference programming units, conference technicians, and AIC (Accredited Interpreter Contractor) recruitment resources to ensure alignment with user requirements. Complementing this primary function, Unit 08B40 manages the provision of supplementary multilingual services, including external remote interpretation for non-essential meetings, while assuming full responsibility for associated financial procedures and public procurement processes conducted in operational partnership with the Budget Unit. The unit processes and fulfills interpretation requests originating from the Presidency of the European Parliament and individual Members (IAP—Individual Access Provision), managing the complete request lifecycle from PERICLES database entry through interpreter assignment and AIC recruitment with specified linguistic profiles. Additionally, Unit 08B40 maintains specialized competency in international sign language interpretation services, including recruiter responsibilities for sign language interpreters, maintenance of qualified interpreter rosters, and institutional advisory capacity on sign language accessibility matters. Administrative and governance functions encompass unit coordination, internal control compliance, management of ad hoc interpretation requests requiring specialized attention, and operational oversight mechanisms, ensuring service delivery integrity and institutional responsiveness across all service categories.
08B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Interpretation Services Delivery** (55%): Plan and manage provision of interpretation services (remote, local, or hybrid modalities) for official European Parliament missions outside primary workplaces and external political group meetings, coordinated with conference programming units, technical services, and AIC recruitment mechanisms according to user requirements

• **Complementary Multilingual Services and Financial Administration** (20%): Manage provision of external remote interpretation for non-essential meetings; prepare and administer associated financial procedures and public procurement processes in cooperative partnership with the Budget Unit

• **Presidency and Member Interpretation Requests Processing** (15%): Manage interpretation requests from the Presidency and individual Members (IAP); administer PERICLES database entries; recruit AICs with specified linguistic profiles; assign interpreters to scheduled programs

• **Specialized Accessibility Services** (5%): Recruit and manage international sign language interpreters; maintain and manage sign language interpreter rosters; provide institutional leadership and advisory capacity on sign language interpretation accessibility matters

• **Unit Coordination, Governance, and Ad Hoc Support** (5%): Coordinate unit operations; ensure implementation and compliance with internal control standards; manage ad hoc interpretation requests requiring specialized attention; oversee operational quality assurance mechanisms

08B90.name: 08B90
original_activities: Process requests for prepaid external interpreter (AIC) tickets issued by the European Parliament's travel agency.

Prepare and communicate to the hierarchy reliable information and various statistics on the financial management of external interpretation, including the analysis of the costs of the different interpretation methods implemented as well as the development of statistical and financial forecasting tools (DEFIN).

Ensure the implementation (planning, execution, monitoring) of the budget intended for external interpretation by ensuring compliance with the principle of good financial management, in particular by collaborating with other management units in the establishment and revision of procedures having a financial impact.

Ensure, in collaboration with the European Commission - DG SCIC (Joint Payment Office of AICs), the liquidation of financial rights under the contracts of external interpreters (AIC) and ensure the quality of the services provided to them (exemptions, article 11, Financial Helpdesk, reconciliation of GRIF/Pericles data, etc.).
Ensure the coordination and management of the unit as well as the implementation and compliance with internal control standards within the unit.
08B90.refined_description:
Unit 08B90 functions as the integrated Financial Management and Administrative Services hub for external interpretation within the European Parliament's General Management directorate (Unit 08), operating under the strategic oversight of Direction 08B while maintaining autonomous operational authority as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility across five interconnected operational domains that collectively ensure the efficient, compliant, and cost-effective delivery of external interpretation services (AIC contractor services) to the institution. Unit 08B90 maintains essential collaborative relationships with sibling units (08B10, 08B20, 08B30, 08B3010, 08B40) while serving as the critical operational and financial steward within the Parliament's interpretation management ecosystem. The unit's integrated activities encompass direct service procurement administration, authoritative financial analysis and strategic reporting, budget stewardship with rigorous compliance oversight, contractual liquidation with structured quality assurance mechanisms, and comprehensive unit governance with internal control compliance. Through these five operational functions, Unit 08B90 ensures systematic resource allocation, institutional decision-making support, financial transparency, service quality maintenance, and adherence to sound financial management principles across all external interpretation expenditures and methodologies.

---
08B90.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct Service Administration & Procurement Operations** (30%): Processing and administration of prepaid external interpreter (AIC) tickets issued through the European Parliament's designated travel agency; represents the direct operational interface with service procurement mechanisms and ensures seamless logistical support for interpreter deployment.

• **Financial Analysis, Strategic Reporting & Forecasting** (24%): Preparation and hierarchical communication of authoritative financial management information, statistical analyses concerning external interpretation costs across varying implementation methodologies, and development of advanced statistical and financial forecasting analytical tools (DEFIN system) to support institutional decision-making and resource optimization.

• **Budget Stewardship, Implementation & Compliance Management** (19%): Systematic planning, execution, and monitoring of external interpretation budget appropriations; collaborative engagement with other management units on establishment and revision of procedures with financial implications; ensuring compliance with sound financial management principles and institutional governance frameworks.

• **Contractual Liquidation, Financial Settlement & Service Quality Assurance** (19%): Joint management with the European Commission's DG SCIC (Joint Payment Office) of financial rights liquidation under external interpreter contracts; quality assurance encompassing exemptions processing, Article 11 applications, Financial Helpdesk operations, regulatory compliance maintenance, and systematic reconciliation of GRIF and Pericles database records to ensure accuracy and service quality.

• **Unit Governance, Coordination & Internal Control Compliance** (8%): Coordination and management of unit operations; implementation and compliance with established internal control standards and institutional governance frameworks.

08D.name: 08D
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the management units and services, guaranteeing continuity of service, operational resilience and team cohesion in a context of intense activity and changing practices, including preparation for the renovation of the SPAAK building. Exercise the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer by ensuring rigorous and efficient management of the financial resources allocated to the department.

Supervise the evolution of conference organization and support professions and services, ensuring their overall coherence and their adaptation to hybrid and remote formats, in consultation with the services of the General Directorate for Conference Logistics and Interpretation and the other general directorates.

Participate in defining the strategic orientations of general management, within the management team, and guarantee the effective application of operational and strategic decisions, in a context of technological innovation and optimization of resources.

Support technology monitoring, the launch and monitoring of innovative projects, as well as the capture and exploitation of information on technological and organizational innovations in the field of conference services, in particular remote multilingual participation, remote interpretation and remote and hybrid conference services.

Represent management in various intra- and inter-general management committees or working groups, interinstitutional and international forums, and, where appropriate, also represent general management.
Participate in social dialogue with delegations of technicians and conference ushers. Contribute to the social dialogue of interpreters.
08D.refined_description: Unit 08D functions as the strategic Direction and executive coordination hub within the European Parliament's General Management structure (Unit 08), operating as a Level 1 directorate with direct supervisory authority over six specialized operational child units (08D10, 08D20, 08D30, 08D40, 08D50, and 08D60) that collectively deliver 39 distinct activities. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive directional leadership, financial stewardship through subdelegated authorizing officer functions, and operational oversight across conference services and management infrastructure, ensuring service continuity, operational resilience, and team cohesion during periods of intense activity and organizational transformation. Unit 08D exercises rigorous and efficient management of all departmental financial resources while ensuring the direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate management units and services, with particular strategic focus on preparation for the SPAAK building renovation project. The unit supervises the systematic evolution and coherence of conference organization professions and support services, guaranteeing their systematic adaptation to hybrid and remote operational delivery formats through coordinated consultation with the General Directorate for Conference Logistics and Interpretation and peer general directorates. Within the management team, Unit 08D participates actively in defining strategic organizational orientations and guarantees effective implementation of operational and strategic decisions within contexts of technological innovation and resource optimization, while supporting technology monitoring and the systematic launch, implementation, and oversight of innovative projects—particularly those addressing remote multilingual participation, remote interpretation services, and hybrid conference delivery models. The unit captures and exploits organizational and technological innovations in conference service provision and provides institutional representation across intra-institutional and inter-general directorate committees, working groups, interinstitutional forums, and international bodies, while contributing to social dialogue engagement with technician and conference usher delegations and supporting interpreter-related social dialogue initiatives.
08D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction, Executive Coordination, and Financial Management** (35%): Ensures comprehensive directional leadership, coordination, and animation of all subordinate management units and services; guarantees operational continuity, resilience, and team cohesion; exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions with rigorous and efficient management of departmental financial resources; coordinates strategic preparation for SPAAK building renovation project

• **Conference Services Evolution, Coherence, and Format Adaptation** (25%): Supervises the systematic evolution of conference organization professions and support services; ensures overall coherence and systematic adaptation to hybrid and remote delivery formats; maintains coordinated consultation with General Directorate for Conference Logistics and Interpretation and peer general directorates; guarantees professional service continuity through format transitions

• **Strategic Orientation, Technological Innovation, and Operational Decision Implementation** (20%): Participates actively in defining strategic organizational orientations within management team structures; guarantees effective application of operational and strategic decisions in contexts of technological innovation and resource optimization; supports technology monitoring and surveillance activities; launches, implements, and oversees innovative projects addressing remote multilingual participation, remote interpretation services, and hybrid conference delivery modalities; captures and exploits organizational and technological innovations in conference service provision

• **Technology Monitoring and Innovative Project Development** (10%): Supports systematic technology monitoring and surveillance; launches and implements innovative projects; captures and exploits intelligence on technological and organizational innovations; ensures systematic oversight and integration of innovations into operational frameworks

• **Institutional Representation and Social Dialogue Engagement** (10%): Represents management across intra-institutional and inter-general directorate committees, working groups, interinstitutional forums, and international bodies; extends representation on behalf of general management where appropriate; participates in social dialogue with technician and conference usher delegations; contributes to interpreter-related social dialogue initiatives (7% institutional representation; 3% social dialogue activities)

08D10.name: 08D10
original_activities: Provide technical support for all parliamentary meetings, including VIP meetings and complex events, in cooperation with other relevant departments.
Prepare and provide technical assistance during missions outside the three locations.
Prepare and provide technical assistance for all other events (exhibitions, cultural and social events, etc.).
Manage assignments of conference operators and technicians, including external staff.
Ensure the management of the workshop in Brussels.
Coordinate and manage the unit, ensure human resources management, including administrative management of external resources.
08D10.refined_description: Unit 08D10 operates as the Technical Support and Conference Operations Centre within Directorate 08D of the European Parliament's General Management structure, functioning as a specialized technical services unit that serves as the primary operational hub for audio-visual and technical infrastructure delivery across all parliamentary venues and institutional activities. As a Level 2 operational unit collaborating with sibling units 08D20, 08D30, 08D40, 08D50, and 08D60, Unit 08D10 delivers integrated institutional support services through five interconnected operational domains: comprehensive technical support for parliamentary proceedings, VIP meetings, and complex institutional events (55% of workload); technical assistance and operational readiness for official missions and activities conducted outside the three primary Parliament locations (20% of workload); technical support for supplementary institutional events including exhibitions, cultural initiatives, and social functions (10% of workload); strategic management and deployment of conference operators and technician personnel, encompassing both permanent staff and external contractual arrangements (5% of workload); and dual responsibility for operational stewardship of the Brussels technical workshop facility alongside unit-level strategic coordination, comprehensive human resources administration, and oversight of external contractual resources (5% of workload). These interconnected activities ensure seamless technical execution and professional standards across all European Parliament venues, external engagements, and institutional functions.
08D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary and High-Priority Event Technical Support** (55%): Comprehensive technical infrastructure and expertise delivery for all parliamentary meetings, VIP meetings, and complex institutional events, executed in systematic cooperation with other relevant European Parliament departments

• **External Operations and Field Technical Services** (20%): Preparation and delivery of specialized technical assistance during official missions and activities outside the three primary Parliament locations, ensuring consistent technical capability across dispersed operational venues

• **Supplementary Events and Cultural Programming** (10%): Technical assistance provision for all institutional events beyond core parliamentary functions, including exhibitions, cultural initiatives, and social programming

• **Personnel Management and Deployment** (5%): Strategic assignment, management, and deployment of conference operators and technician personnel, including permanent staff and external contractual arrangements, with comprehensive human resources administration

• **Facility Operations and Unit Coordination** (5%): Operational management of the Brussels technical workshop facility, executive coordination of unit operations, and administrative oversight of external resources and contractual arrangements

08D20.name: 08D20
original_activities: Assist the bodies of the European Parliament, political groups and Members of Parliament in the preparation and logistical arrangements, as well as in the smooth running of their meetings, plenary sessions and external missions, ensure room service for participants, and contribute to passive security while respecting the rules in force.
Provide assistance during visits and high-level events organized at the European Parliament, in close cooperation with the organizers and other services.

Ensure the coordination and management of the unit, the organization of daily work, the supervision of field activities during meetings, plenary sessions, high-level events and external missions as well as the drafting of reports relating to their progress; ensure the management of human resources, the organization of specific professional training and the structured sharing of information.
Implement and monitor projects directly involving the unit (MRS, TUSC, ePoster, electronic seating chart); implement the Digital Signage project.

Ensure the validation of requests and the operational management of the ePoster electronic display in cooperation with the Quaestors' secretariat, as well as the operational management of the ePoster content and technical infrastructure; ensure the placement of exhibition visuals and signage; ensure compliance with the regulations regarding signage in the premises of the European Parliament.
08D20.refined_description:
**

Unit 08D20 operates as a specialized operational support and events management unit within Direction 08D of the European Parliament's General Management structure, functioning as a Level 2 leaf node that serves as a critical facilitator for parliamentary institutional functions and integrated general management services. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses logistical and operational support across three interconnected domains: parliamentary proceedings, institutional events, and digital infrastructure management, executed in close coordination with sibling units (08D10, 08D30, 08D40, 08D50, 08D60) to deliver integrated services across the institution. Unit 08D20 provides multifaceted assistance to all parliamentary bodies, political groups, and Members of Parliament, encompassing preparation and seamless execution of meetings, plenary sessions, and external missions, including participant room service provision and passive security implementation in compliance with established regulations.
