**
08D20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Parliamentary Meeting Support and Logistical Operations** (60%): Comprehensive assistance to all European Parliament bodies, political groups, and Members of Parliament in preparation and logistical arrangements for meetings, plenary sessions, and external missions; provision of room services for all participants; implementation and maintenance of passive security measures in full compliance with established institutional regulations.

• **Institutional Events and Visits Coordination** (18%): Specialized operational support for parliamentary visits and high-level events organized at the European Parliament, executed through close collaborative coordination with event organizers and other institutional services.

• **Unit Management and Human Resources Administration** (15%): Strategic coordination and daily work organization; supervision of field activities during all parliamentary proceedings, events, and external missions; preparation of progress reports and documentation; human resources management; organization of professional training programs; and structured information-sharing and knowledge management protocols.

• **Digital Infrastructure and Technology Projects Implementation** (4%): Implementation and monitoring of technology systems including Meeting Room System (MRS), TUSC platform, ePoster electronic displays, electronic seating chart applications, and Digital Signage project infrastructure.

• **ePoster Electronic Display System Operations and Compliance Management** (3%): Operational and compliance management of the ePoster electronic display system in partnership with the Quaestors' secretariat, encompassing request validation, technical infrastructure oversight, operational content management, exhibition visual placement coordination, signage placement management, and regulatory compliance assurance for all signage within European Parliament premises.

08D30.name: 08D30
original_activities: Provide assistance in the management of H3L meetings, events and missions with remote multilingual participation and/or remote simultaneous interpretation. This support covers platform moderation, technical support for remote participants, including pre-testing and delivery of microphones, coordination with organizers during the meeting, and technical coordination and support for interpreters working remotely.

Assist clients in the preparation of H3L meetings, events and missions in collaboration with the General Directorate's services for logistics and conference interpretation. Create and send links for the remote participation platform and manage the allocation of support human resources during meetings.
Organize pre-testing of external participants and distribution of microphones, coordination with organizers and internal communication.
Coordinate and manage the unit: human resources management and budget, including administrative and budgetary management of external resources. Prepare statistics.
Monitor the performance of support agents and coordinate internal training.
Ensure management of the platform within the framework of remote participation (technical development, relations with the subcontractor, etc.).
Organize and implement awareness-raising actions to improve the sound quality of remote participants during meetings.
Manage the framework contract EP-LINC/2024/OP/0002 - EUVC LOT 1 for the provision of multilingual videoconferencing platform services in cooperation with national parliaments.
08D30.refined_description:
**

Unit 08D30 is a specialized operational support unit within Direction 08D of the European Parliament's General Management structure, functioning as a critical infrastructure node for enabling inclusive, multilingual remote participation across parliamentary meetings, events, and official missions. Operating as a leaf unit alongside sibling units 08D10, 08D20, 08D40, 08D50, and 08D60, the unit provides comprehensive technical and logistical support for hybrid and fully remote proceedings with particular emphasis on simultaneous interpretation services and remote participation platform management. The unit's mission encompasses direct real-time assistance during H3L meetings and events featuring remote multilingual participation and/or remote simultaneous interpretation, collaborative preparation activities with clients and the General Directorate's logistics and conference interpretation services, technical infrastructure oversight of the remote participation platform, quality assurance and participant engagement initiatives, and strategic contract management for multilingual videoconferencing services. Through these coordinated operational, technical, and administrative functions, Unit 08D30 ensures the European Parliament maintains institutional capacity to conduct accessible, multilingual remote proceedings while sustaining quality standards and operational efficiency across the organization.

---

**
08D30.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Core Remote Meeting Support Operations** (50%): Provision of comprehensive real-time assistance in managing H3L meetings, events, and missions with remote multilingual participation and/or remote simultaneous interpretation, encompassing platform moderation, technical troubleshooting for remote participants, pre-testing protocols, microphone delivery coordination and logistics, real-time coordination with meeting organizers, and dedicated technical support for remotely-based interpreters.

• **Meeting Preparation and Resource Coordination** (20%): Collaborative assistance with clients and the General Directorate's logistics and conference interpretation services in preparing H3L meetings and events; generation and distribution of remote participation platform links; coordination and allocation of human resource support throughout meeting execution.

• **Participant Pre-Testing and Microphone Distribution Management** (5%): Organization of external participant pre-testing protocols; coordination of microphone distribution logistics; liaison with meeting organizers and internal stakeholders regarding technical readiness.

• **Performance Monitoring and Internal Training Coordination** (5%): Oversight and performance monitoring of support agents; coordination and delivery of internal training programs to maintain operational capability and service quality standards.

• **Platform Technical Infrastructure and Subcontractor Management** (5%): Technical development and operational management of the remote participation platform; maintenance of relationships with external subcontractors; oversight of platform compliance with remote participation requirements and service specifications.

• **Quality Assurance and Awareness-Raising Initiatives** (5%): Organization and implementation of awareness-raising campaigns and initiatives designed to enhance audio quality standards among remote participants; coordination of quality improvement activities across the participant base.

• **Strategic Contract and Administrative Management** (5%): Management of framework contract EP-LINC/2024/OP/0002 - EUVC LOT 1 for multilingual videoconferencing platform services provision in cooperation with national parliaments; human resources management and administration; budgetary administration of external resources; preparation of performance statistics and organizational reporting.

08D40.name: 08D40
original_activities: Organize in advance and manage on site conferences and events held in the three workplaces of the bodies of the European Parliament, for external bodies, committees, delegations as well as for high-level conferences.

Provide assistance and manage the use of IT tools intended to facilitate the organization of events, now 'EP Events' (management of participant registration requests, support for requesting services, organization of training, etc.). Identify potential integrations to support the organization of events.
Organize in advance and manage on-site meetings outside the three workplaces of the bodies of the European Parliament and political groups.
Ensure coordination, operational and strategic management of the unit, optimize internal and interdepartmental processes, and guarantee the achievement of common objectives.
Organize logistical support and control of exhibition venues that are authorized for Members as well as events at Esplanade Solidarność 1980 in cooperation with applicants and relevant services.
Establish a framework contract to facilitate contract award procedures for the organization of conferences outside the three workplaces.
08D40.refined_description: Unit 08D40 functions as the European Parliament's specialized Events and Conferences Management Unit, operating as a leaf-node organizational entity within the General Management Directorate (08D) and serving as a critical operational hub for comprehensive event coordination across the Parliament's institutional structure. The unit's integrated mission encompasses the advance planning, organization, and on-site management of conferences and events held at the three official workplaces (Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg) for external bodies, parliamentary committees, delegations, and high-level political conferences (37% of workload), complemented by the planning and management of external meetings and off-site gatherings held outside these primary institutional locations on behalf of political groups and affiliated stakeholders (22% of workload). Unit 08D40 maintains strategic responsibility for administering and supporting the 'EP Events' information technology platform, which facilitates participant registration management, service request processing, organizational training delivery, and the identification of technological integration opportunities to enhance event administration efficiency (22% of workload). The unit simultaneously manages logistical support and operational oversight of authorized exhibition venues accessible to Members, as well as coordinating events at Esplanade Solidarność 1980 in partnership with applicants and relevant institutional services (7% of workload). Unit 08D40 dedicates resources to operational and strategic unit coordination, internal and interdepartmental process optimization, and the establishment of framework contracts to streamline procurement procedures for conference organization activities conducted outside the three primary institutional workplaces (12% combined workload). Functioning without subordinate organizational units, Unit 08D40 collaborates with sibling units (08D10, 08D20, 08D30, 08D50, 08D60) to support the Parliament's broader administrative, operational, and democratic engagement objectives.
08D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Institutional Events Management** (37%): Comprehensive advance planning, organization, and on-site management of conferences and events held across the three official workplaces of the European Parliament bodies, serving external organizations, parliamentary committees, delegations, and high-level political conferences

• **External & Off-Site Meetings Coordination** (22%): Advance planning and on-site management of meetings and events organized outside the three primary institutional workplaces on behalf of parliamentary political groups and external stakeholders

• **Digital Events Infrastructure & Technology Support** (22%): Administration and operational support of the 'EP Events' information technology platform encompassing participant registration management, service request facilitation, organizational training coordination, and identification of technological integration opportunities to enhance event organization capabilities

• **Exhibition & Specialized Venue Management** (7%): Logistical organization, support provision, and operational oversight of authorized exhibition venues accessible to Members, including event management at Esplanade Solidarność 1980 in cooperative coordination with applicants and institutional services

• **Strategic Unit Operations & Procurement Framework Development** (12%): Operational and strategic unit coordination, internal and interdepartmental process optimization, achievement of common institutional objectives, and establishment and management of framework contracts to facilitate and streamline procurement procedures for external conference organization activities

08D50.name: 08D50
original_activities: Develop innovative technical solutions allowing the institution to function well, particularly when organizing meetings with remote participation and/or interpretation. Ensure project management and monitoring as well as supervision of subcontractors for the modernization of conference facilities in the three workplaces, with particular attention to the robustness and resilience of the equipment.
Ensure procurement as well as management of stocks and inventory of materials.
Ensure the supervision of subcontractors in charge of preventive and corrective maintenance of technical installations in the three workplaces.
Develop specifications and implement all administrative procedures for purchases of equipment and services from external firms.
Prepare, maintain and modernize equipment for missions outside three locations.
Ensure coordination and management of the unit.
Ensuring the highest quality and reliability of our facilities, either at the end of each project or during the operation and maintenance of our conference rooms.
08D50.refined_description:
**

Unit 08D50 operates as a specialized technical and facilities management unit within the European Parliament's General Management directorate (Unit 08D), positioned at the second hierarchical level to ensure institutional operational continuity across the Parliament's three primary workplaces while coordinating alongside sibling units 08D10, 08D20, 08D30, 08D40, and 08D60. The unit's primary mission (50% of workload) encompasses the development and implementation of innovative technical solutions that enable institutional functioning, with particular emphasis on systems supporting remote meeting participation and simultaneous interpretation capabilities, while simultaneously managing comprehensive modernization projects for conference facilities across all three workplace locations through full project management oversight, rigorous subcontractor supervision, and specialized attention to equipment robustness and operational resilience standards. Supporting this core mission, Unit 08D50 executes four complementary operational functions: procurement operations and materials inventory management (15%), which includes managing vendor relationships and controlling materials stocks; supervision of preventive and corrective maintenance subcontractors across all three locations (10%), which ensures technical installation integrity; development of detailed technical specifications and implementation of administrative procurement procedures governing equipment and external service acquisitions (10%), which ensures regulatory compliance and vendor coordination; and preparation, maintenance, and modernization of equipment for external mission deployments (5%), which extends institutional capabilities beyond primary workplaces. The unit further allocates 5% of resources to assurance of facility quality and reliability standards throughout both project lifecycle execution and ongoing conference room operations, maintaining state-of-the-art technical capabilities and infrastructure performance standards. Finally, 5% of resources support internal unit coordination and management functions that facilitate all primary and secondary activities. Collectively, these activities—totaling 100% of documented workload—position Unit 08D50 as an essential operational support structure that maintains institutional infrastructure excellence within the Parliament's General Management framework.

**
08D50.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Technical Innovation, Conference Infrastructure Development & Modernization** (50%): Develop innovative technical solutions for remote participation and simultaneous interpretation systems; manage comprehensive modernization projects for conference facilities across three workplaces; provide full project management oversight and rigorous subcontractor supervision; ensure equipment robustness, resilience, and operational continuity

• **Procurement Operations & Materials Inventory Management** (15%): Execute procurement processes and manage materials stocks and inventory systems; coordinate vendor relationships for supplies and equipment; control inventory allocation and distribution

• **Maintenance Supervision & Quality Assurance** (15%): Supervise subcontractors responsible for preventive and corrective maintenance of technical installations across three workplaces (10%); ensure highest quality and reliability standards for facility operations, project completion, and ongoing conference room performance (5%)

• **Technical Specifications & Administrative Procurement Procedures** (10%): Develop detailed technical specifications for all equipment and service acquisitions; implement administrative procedures governing external vendor purchases; ensure procurement regulatory compliance and standardization

• **External Mission Equipment Support & Unit Coordination** (10%): Prepare, maintain, and modernize equipment for external mission deployments beyond primary workplaces (5%); coordinate internal unit operations and management functions supporting all primary and secondary activities (5%)

08D60.name: 08D60
original_activities: Support external partners (civil society and non-governmental organizations) and local and European institutions in the organization of conferences and events - at the European Parliament in Strasbourg, outside plenary sessions - in compliance with the rules on the use of European Parliament premises by external organizations. Manage high-level and cultural events outside of sessions, including protocol aspects when protocol is not involved.

Manage requests for events at the European Parliament in Strasbourg: prepare and monitor files relating to new requests for events with a view to approval by the Secretary General. Advise, guide and direct external organizations in their needs related to events organized outside of plenary sessions.

Provide logistical support before and during events or conferences, including during the plenary session or during events organized at the level of the European Parliament, such as the European Youth Event, Europe Day etc.
Ensure the application of all regulations related to the activity such as transparency rules and others; ensure compliance in all aspects of events.

Develop internal and external communication and awareness strategies: promote the European Parliament site in Strasbourg as a 'citizen hub' and prospect for new partners in a targeted manner. Communicate about the activity and services offered both internally and via social networks, including partners at the time of events.

Ensure strategic points, coordination and management of the unit; optimize workflows between the different divisions within the unit and in relation to other services involved in the organization of conferences and events at the European Parliament in Strasbourg outside sessions.

Coordinate reservations for all rooms in the premises of the European Parliament in Strasbourg outside sessions in order to have a complete and exhaustive overview of all activities at the European Parliament in Strasbourg.
08D60.refined_description:
Unit 08D60 functions as the specialized Events and Conferences Management Unit within Direction 08D of the European Parliament's General Management structure, operating at hierarchical level 2 as a support directorate responsible for coordinating and facilitating all non-plenary session activities at the Strasbourg premises. The unit serves as a critical institutional interface between external partners—including civil society organizations, non-governmental institutions, and local and European authorities—and the Parliament's infrastructure, providing comprehensive support throughout the complete lifecycle of event requests from initial processing through execution and post-event management. Unit 08D60's integrated operational portfolio encompasses direct event support and partner guidance, logistical coordination across all event phases, systematic regulatory compliance monitoring, strategic communication and stakeholder engagement initiatives, and facilities coordination that collectively ensure institutional accessibility, democratic engagement, and efficient premises utilization. The unit maintains strict adherence to all institutional regulations governing external use of parliamentary facilities while managing protocol coordination for high-level and cultural events, and actively positions the Strasbourg site as a prominent citizen hub through multi-channel awareness activities including social media engagement and targeted partnership prospecting. Through executive oversight of unit strategy, inter-divisional workflow optimization, and cross-service coordination with other Parliament departments, Unit 08D60 directly contributes to the European Parliament's institutional objectives while operating in coordinated alignment with sibling units 08D10, 08D20, 08D30, 08D40, and 08D50 under the governance of parent Direction 08D.

##
08D60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Event Organization and External Partner Support** (20%): Direct facilitation and support to civil society organizations, non-governmental institutions, and local/European authorities in organizing conferences, high-level events, and cultural activities at the Strasbourg Parliament premises outside plenary sessions; management of protocol coordination where applicable; full compliance with institutional regulations governing external use of parliamentary facilities.

• **Event Request Management and Advisory Services** (15%): Systematic processing and monitoring of event requests with Secretary General approval oversight; comprehensive advisory guidance to external organizations regarding event organization requirements, logistics, institutional procedures, and regulatory frameworks for non-plenary session activities; directional support throughout the request lifecycle.

• **Logistical Operations and Event Execution** (20%): Provision of integrated pre-event, on-site, and post-event logistical support for all conferences and events; operational coordination and seamless implementation during both plenary and non-plenary periods; management of major institutional initiatives including the European Youth Event and Europe Day celebrations; comprehensive event delivery across all Parliament activity periods.

• **Regulatory Compliance and Institutional Governance** (15%): Systematic application, monitoring, and enforcement of all regulatory frameworks governing event activities, including transparency requirements and institutional compliance standards; comprehensive adherence assurance across all operational dimensions of event organization and lifecycle management.

• **Strategic Communication, Stakeholder Engagement, and Partnership Development** (15%): Development and implementation of internal and external communication strategies; active positioning of the Strasbourg premises as a citizen-centered institutional hub; targeted prospecting for new institutional partnerships; multi-channel awareness activities including social media engagement, partner communications during events, and service promotion through internal and external channels.

• **Strategic Management, Operational Coordination, and Facilities Administration** (15%): Executive oversight of unit strategy and inter-divisional workflow optimization; cross-service coordination with other Parliament departments involved in event organization and premises management; centralized coordination and monitoring of all room reservations across the Strasbourg premises outside sessions; comprehensive and exhaustive visibility of all institutional activities and space utilization to ensure efficient facility management and operational coherence.

08C.name: 08C
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the management units and services. Ensure the continuity of operations, while taking into account resilience, team cohesion and the evolution of the regulatory framework.

Continue to develop the partnerships established with the three operational departments and within the Resources Department by governing the human resources policy of the general management: allocation of resources and planning for future needs (succession planning, development of key skills and monitoring of their acquisition through training or recruitment), optimal use of temporary and contractual staff.
Participate in defining the strategic orientations of general management as part of the management team and guarantee the effective application of operational and strategic decisions.

Represent the Directorate-General at inter-DG meetings and ensure that other service Directorates-General take into account the needs of DG LINC while liaising with the DGs involved: FINS, INLO, SAFE and PERS.
Oversee budgetary and financial procedures, including in cooperation with the General Directorate of Finance.

Continue to work, in cooperation with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, on the upgrade of IT tools supporting the activities and the modernization process of the General Directorate.

Coordinate risk management and business continuity. Manage and coordinate the EMAS actions of general management. Improve the adequate distribution of workspaces and their maintenance taking into account the needs in a hybrid work environment.
08C.refined_description: Unit 08C functions as the strategic coordination and executive management directorate within the General Management structure of the European Parliament, operating as a first-level direction under Unit 08 with direct functional responsibilities to the Secretary General. The unit serves as the institutional continuity hub and executive coordination center, responsible for ensuring operational resilience, cross-functional alignment, and cascading strategic directives across the Directorate-General through oversight of four subordinate units (08C10, 08C20, 08C2010, and 08C30) and coordination with sibling units 08-50, 08-60, 08A, 08B, and 08D. Unit 08C's comprehensive management portfolio encompasses seven interconnected operational and strategic domains executed through collaborative partnerships with the three operational departments, the Resources Department, the General Directorate of Finance, and the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, alongside inter-DG coordination with peer Directorates-General (FINS, INLO, SAFE, and PERS) to ensure institutional awareness of DG LINC's specific requirements and priorities. The unit maintains dual accountability for both executive stewardship of organizational performance and strategic alignment with institutional priorities, ensuring that operational decisions reflect broader institutional orientations while maintaining organizational resilience, team cohesion, and adaptive capacity to evolving regulatory frameworks. Through its integrated management approach, Unit 08C ensures institutional effectiveness by balancing immediate operational continuity with long-term strategic positioning, human capital optimization, financial governance, technological modernization, and comprehensive risk and sustainability management tailored to hybrid work environment requirements.
08C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Operational Leadership and Organizational Stewardship** (25%): Ensure directional leadership, coordination, and animation of all subordinate management units and services; maintain operational continuity while fostering organizational resilience, team cohesion, and adaptive capacity to regulatory framework evolution; provide overarching management stewardship across the Directorate-General and cascade strategic directives through subordinate units.

• **Human Resources Strategy and Workforce Optimization** (25%): Develop and implement integrated human resources policies governing resource allocation, succession planning, strategic skills development, recruitment initiatives, and optimal utilization of temporary and contractual personnel; establish partnerships with the three operational departments and Resources Department; plan for future organizational needs through comprehensive workforce planning and key competencies cultivation.

• **Strategic Planning, Decision Implementation, and Inter-Institutional Coordination** (25%): Participate in the formulation of strategic organizational orientations as a member of the general management team; guarantee rigorous implementation of both operational and strategic institutional decisions; represent the Directorate-General at inter-DG coordination meetings and serve as primary liaison with peer Directorates-General (FINS, INLO, SAFE, PERS) to ensure institutional alignment with DG LINC's specific requirements and priorities.

• **Financial Governance and IT Modernization** (20%): Oversee budgetary and financial procedures in collaborative partnership with the General Directorate of Finance to ensure fiscal compliance and resource optimization (10%); advance the modernization agenda of the General Directorate through cooperative engagement with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity to upgrade IT infrastructure and digital tools supporting operational activities (10%).

• **Risk Management, Business Continuity, Environmental Compliance, and Workspace Optimization** (5%): Coordinate comprehensive risk management frameworks and business continuity protocols; manage and coordinate EMAS environmental compliance actions; optimize workspace allocation and maintenance strategies tailored to hybrid work environment requirements and institutional infrastructure needs.

08C10.name: 08C10
original_activities: Ensure the management of the DG's human resources (e.g. planning of needs, filling of positions, annual rating procedure, management of leave and absences, management of working time) and that of decentralized budget envelopes and contract staff budget; provide analysis and advice on strategic or regulatory issues relating to HR and their development; provide HR-related performance metrics; provide a career advice service (RELOP).

Develop, implement and monitor professional training for civil servants (excluding interpreters), particularly through the management of the training plan, budgetary monitoring and the implementation of strategic actions. Organize the reception and training of new arrivals, as well as the supervision of interns within general management.

Develop and implement a talent management and succession planning strategy adapted to the needs of general management in the context of mobility. Represent general management in various internal or interinstitutional working groups. Coordinate one-off projects on instructions from the hierarchy and in areas linked to the unit's activities (for example, strengthening teams as part of the renovation work on the Spaak building).

Ensure the management of the administrative archives of the general management and implement the institutional document management policy (classification plan, conservation lists and files). Contribute to the optimal use of the dedicated tool. Represent DG LINC within the Interdepartmental Group of Document Administration Officers (GIDOC). Manage all DG infrastructure in terms of space and inventory.

Manage mission orders for interpreters and ensure validation of mission orders for general management. Contribute to the implementation of the EMAS policy by optimizing business travel. Participate in the modernization of mission management applications.
Ensure the coordination and management of the unit as well as the implementation and compliance with internal control standards within the unit.
08C10.refined_description:
Unit 08C10 functions as the integrated Human Resources, Administrative Services, and Organizational Development hub within Direction 08C (Strategic Coordination and Executive Management) of the European Parliament's General Management structure. As a second-level operational unit with no subordinate branches, 08C10 operates as a leaf node within the 08C directorate hierarchy while maintaining collaborative relationships with sibling units 08C20, 08C2010, and 08C30. The unit serves as the institutional backbone for comprehensive personnel administration, strategic HR governance, professional development, talent management, records management, and infrastructure administration across the General Management directorate. 08C10's mission encompasses six interconnected functional domains that collectively address the complete spectrum of human resources strategy, administrative operations, and organizational development requirements. The unit's activities are distinguished by their dual operational character: foundational administrative execution (workforce planning, recruitment, leave administration, budget management, document classification, and infrastructure oversight) combined with strategic advisory and analytical functions (HR regulatory analysis, performance metrics generation, talent strategy development, succession planning, and organizational capacity building). Through its integrated service delivery model, 08C10 ensures institutional accountability, regulatory compliance, and strategic alignment across all general management personnel and administrative operations while maintaining established internal control standards and contributing to broader institutional initiatives including EMAS environmental policy optimization and mission management system modernization.

---

##
08C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Administration and Strategic HR Management** (45%): Comprehensive personnel administration encompassing workforce planning, position recruitment and filling procedures, annual performance rating cycles, leave and absence administration, working time management, decentralized budget envelope oversight, and contract staff budget administration; provision of strategic analysis and advisory services on HR regulatory matters and institutional developments; delivery of HR-related performance metrics; operation of the RELOP career guidance and advisory service.

• **Professional Development and Staff Integration Programs** (20%): Design, implementation and monitoring of professional training programs for civil servants (excluding interpreter training); management of institutional training plans and budgetary oversight of training initiatives; execution of strategic training actions; organization of reception and integration programs for newly recruited personnel; supervision of internship placements and coordination within general management.

• **Talent Management, Succession Planning, and Organizational Development** (13%): Development and implementation of talent management and succession planning strategies aligned with organizational mobility requirements; representation of general management in internal and interinstitutional working groups; coordination of strategic projects and initiatives including team strengthening and infrastructure enhancement projects (such as Spaak building renovation).

• **Records Management, Document Governance, and Infrastructure Administration** (10%): Management of general management administrative archives and implementation of institutional document management policies; oversight of classification plans, conservation protocols, retention schedules, and file management; optimization of dedicated document management tools; representation of DG LINC within the Interdepartmental Group of Document Administration Officers (GIDOC); comprehensive directorate infrastructure management encompassing space allocation and inventory administration.

• **Mission Management, Environmental Policy Optimization, and System Modernization** (7%): Administration and validation of mission orders for interpreters; implementation and optimization of EMAS environmental policy through business travel management and optimization; participation in modernization of mission management applications and systems.

• **Unit Coordination, Management Oversight, and Internal Control Compliance** (5%): Internal unit operational coordination and management implementation; monitoring and maintenance of compliance with established institutional internal control standards; ensuring organizational accountability and operational effectiveness across all unit activities.

08C20.name: 08C20
original_activities: Manage Projects: Planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality: project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management and communication with stakeholders.
Manage applications in use: Lifecycle management of custom or specialized software applications in use: rolling maintenance, training and support, testing to ensure functionality and security.

Manage infrastructure, IT assets and access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management, ensuring the availability, reliability and security of IT resources.
Manage human and contractual resources, including coordination of IT budget lines. Ensure coordination and management of the unit.

Provide end-user support: resolve technical issues encountered by users of IT systems, proactively communicate issues and solutions, troubleshoot hardware and software issues, offer advice, and ensure a positive user experience.

Supervise and coordinate business analysis: understand business needs and objectives, translate them into IT solutions: collection and analysis of needs, facilitation of communication between stakeholders and recommendation of effective technological solutions.
Manage computer and information security: approach and measures to protect the organization's IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities.
08C20.refined_description: Unit 08C20 operates as a specialized operational and technical management unit within Direction 08C (Strategic Coordination and Executive Management) of the European Parliament's General Management structure, serving as a critical second-level hierarchical entity responsible for comprehensive information technology service delivery, infrastructure stewardship, and project portfolio management. The unit's primary mission encompasses the full lifecycle management of the Parliament's IT systems, applications, and technological infrastructure while ensuring robust resource coordination, end-user satisfaction, and organizational IT governance across the institution. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 08C20 functions under the strategic direction of parent Unit 08C while maintaining operational coordination with sibling units 08C10, 08C2010, and 08C30 to ensure integrated service delivery across the General Management directorate. The unit's balanced portfolio workload distribution reflects seven integrated operational activities that collectively position Unit 08C20 as a critical operational enabler within the Parliament's IT service delivery ecosystem: strategic project planning and execution (25%), dual-track lifecycle management of applications in use and IT infrastructure (46% combined: 23% applications, 23% infrastructure), human and contractual resource management with budget coordination (13%), comprehensive end-user support services (11%), business analysis supervision translating institutional needs into IT solutions (4%), and computer and information security management protecting organizational IT assets (1%). This integrated operational structure ensures that Unit 08C20 simultaneously manages technology portfolios, maintains system availability and reliability, coordinates resources, supports end-user productivity, aligns business requirements with technical capabilities, and safeguards organizational information security across all Parliament IT systems and data assets.
08C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Project and Portfolio Management** (25%): Comprehensive IT project planning, execution, and supervision encompassing scope, timeline, budget, and quality parameter management; stakeholder communication and coordination; resource allocation; risk management; and project governance to achieve defined organizational objectives within established constraints.

• **Application Lifecycle Management** (23%): Full lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications in operational use, including rolling maintenance operations, end-user training and technical support services, functionality and security-focused testing protocols, and continuous operational optimization to ensure application reliability and performance.

• **Infrastructure and IT Asset Stewardship** (23%): Comprehensive IT infrastructure planning, provisioning, maintenance, and monitoring encompassing hardware and software resource management, network component administration, user access management systems, and security-focused access controls to guarantee organizational IT resource availability, reliability, and security across the institution.

• **Organizational and Resource Governance** (13%): Human resource coordination and contractual resource management, IT budget line coordination, unit-level operational management, and administrative oversight to support operational effectiveness and resource optimization.

• **End-User Services and Technical Support** (11%): Comprehensive end-user support services including technical issue resolution and troubleshooting for hardware and software systems, proactive issue communication and solution delivery, user advisory services, and positive user experience management to enhance Parliament IT system utilization.

• **Business-Technology Alignment and Security Management** (5%): Supervision and coordination of business analysis activities translating institutional business requirements and needs into effective technological solutions through requirements collection, stakeholder facilitation, and technological recommendations (4%), combined with computer and information security management implementing protective measures and approaches safeguarding IT systems, data, and information assets against emerging threats and vulnerabilities (1%).

08C2010.name: 08C2010
original_activities: Manage and ensure the proper functioning and development of IT applications specific to general management, such as the PERICLES suite of tools, chatbots and EP Events.
Manage general management projects for logistics and conference interpretation with an IT component.
Carry out needs and feasibility studies, submit and monitor IT projects for the agile IT program.
Coordinate and manage the service. Ensure the management of human and contractual resources.
08C2010.refined_description: Unit 08C2010 operates as a specialized technology management unit within Direction 08C (General Management directorate) of the European Parliament, positioned as a second-hierarchical-level leaf node that provides essential IT infrastructure, application support, and strategic technology enablement services. The unit's integrated mission encompasses four primary operational domains: the management, maintenance, and continuous development of critical general management IT applications (60% of workload); the delivery of general management projects incorporating IT components across logistics and conference interpretation operations (20%); the execution of strategic IT planning activities including needs assessments, feasibility studies, and project proposal submission within the European Parliament's agile IT program framework (15%); and the provision of essential service coordination and resource stewardship functions encompassing human resources and contractual resource management (5%). The unit's core technical portfolio includes the comprehensive PERICLES suite of tools, institutional chatbots, and EP Events platforms—all mission-critical systems supporting parliamentary administrative functions and institutional digital transformation objectives. Operating as a peer unit alongside sibling units 08C10, 08C20, and 08C30 under the strategic coordination of Direction 08C, Unit 08C2010 functions without subordinate organizational units while maintaining collaborative relationships that collectively enable the European Parliament's operational excellence and technology governance infrastructure.
08C2010.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Application Management and Operational Support (60%)**: Comprehensive management, maintenance, and continuous development of general management IT applications including the PERICLES suite of tools, institutional chatbots, and EP Events platform, ensuring optimal functionality and sustained institutional service delivery

• **IT-Enabled Project Management (20%)**: Oversight and delivery of general management projects with integrated IT components, specifically addressing logistics operations and conference interpretation requirements to extend technological support across critical institutional administrative operations

• **Strategic IT Planning and Portfolio Governance (15%)**: Execution of comprehensive needs assessments and feasibility studies; submission and monitoring of IT project proposals within the European Parliament's agile IT program framework to ensure strategic technology alignment with institutional digital transformation initiatives and organizational governance objectives

• **Service Coordination and Resource Stewardship (5%)**: Unit coordination and management functions including human resource stewardship and contractual resource administration to maintain operational effectiveness, institutional accountability, and organizational compliance

08C30.name: 08C30
original_activities: Perform financial initiation and verification in dedicated FMS and Webcontracts IT systems, financial stakeholder management, archiving as well as other back-office functions.

Ensure the planning of public contracts, provide advice on procurement procedures, manage the encoding of contracts in dedicated applications (e-Tendering, e-submission). Participate in the opening and evaluation committees. Ensure contract management.

Participate in various working groups in the field of budget management such as the Inter-DG Finance Group and the Public Procurement Forum. Meet the needs of DG LINC units in terms of budgetary management and financial procedures; ensure internal training for these units. Organize and lead LINC Financial Community meetings. Manage the Budget Unit website.

Coordinate, establish and draft the budget for DG LINC N+2. Manage the budgets for the current year, transfer requests, as well as the budget procedure for year N+1. Ensure monitoring of budgetary transfers, discharge, reports from the Court of Auditors in collaboration with the DG units. Write periodic activity reports.
Develop and monitor new projects in the financial field such as e-Tendering for low and medium value contracts, e-Grants. Review workflows and the new financial control strategy of the DG.
Provide support for DG LINC grant management: programming, publication, evaluation and monitoring of grants.
08C30.refined_description:
Unit 08C30 operates as a specialized financial management and procurement operations unit within Direction 08C of the European Parliament's General Management structure, functioning as the operational backbone for DG LINC's budgetary, contractual, and financial governance infrastructure. The unit executes comprehensive financial administration, procurement coordination, and budgetary oversight across the directorate general while maintaining institutional financial compliance and audit readiness. As a leaf-node unit within the 08C directorate alongside sibling units 08C10, 08C20, and 08C2010, Unit 08C30 provides essential operational support that enables the broader strategic and executive management functions of the parent direction. The unit's mission encompasses six interconnected activity streams: financial transaction processing and back-office operations; procurement planning and contract lifecycle management; budgetary coordination and inter-institutional collaboration; budget planning, monitoring, and financial reporting; financial systems innovation and process improvement; and grant administration support. These integrated functions position Unit 08C30 as both an essential operational asset and a strategic contributor to the Parliament's financial governance infrastructure, serving as a critical support function for DG LINC's financial and budgetary operations through dedicated engagement with financial stakeholders, inter-directorate working groups, operational units, and external audit bodies.

##
08C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Transaction Processing & Back-Office Operations** (46%): Perform financial initiation and verification within dedicated Financial Management Systems (FMS) and Webcontracts IT platforms; manage financial stakeholder relationships; execute essential archiving, record management, and back-office administrative functions ensuring institutional financial compliance and audit readiness

• **Procurement Planning & Contract Management** (18%): Conduct public contract planning and provide expert procurement procedure advisory services; manage contract encoding within e-Tendering and e-Submission applications; participate in opening and evaluation committees; oversee comprehensive contract lifecycle management and contractual oversight

• **Budgetary Coordination, Planning, Monitoring & Inter-Institutional Collaboration** (28%): Coordinate and draft DG LINC's N+2 budget framework and manage current-year budgets (13%); administer N+1 budget procedures, process transfer requests, and monitor budgetary transfers and discharge processes; coordinate with the Court of Auditors regarding audit compliance and prepare periodic activity reports; participate actively in Inter-DG Finance Group and Public Procurement Forum working groups (15%); deliver specialized budgetary management training and support to DG LINC operational units; organize and lead LINC Financial Community meetings; maintain the Budget Unit website as a central information resource

• **Financial Systems Innovation & Workflow Optimization** (5%): Develop and monitor innovative financial projects including e-Tendering extensions for low and medium-value contracts and e-Grants initiatives; conduct comprehensive operational workflow reviews; implement the DG's updated financial control strategies and methodologies

• **Grant Administration Support** (3%): Provide grant management support functions encompassing programming, publication facilitation, evaluation coordination, and ongoing monitoring of grant awards and activities

original_activities: Advise the Secretary General, the President and the Vice-Presidents of Parliament in the areas of competence of the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy.

Organize the activities of the General Directorate from a strategic perspective, in accordance with the objectives and strategy of Parliament, as well as the Annual Work Program of the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, by determining the objectives and means, and adapting them to the increase in the activity of the General Directorate.
Represent the general management in meetings of the bodies of the European Parliament and in internal meetings of the General Secretariat. Cooperate with other general managers.
Exercise the function of delegated authorizing officer.
Contribute to preparing, attending, and following up meetings of the CPG and the Bureau, for matters within the competence of the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy.
13.refined_description: Unit 13, designated as the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, operates at the apex of the European Parliament's organizational hierarchy with direct reporting accountability to the Secretary General. This strategic organizational unit functions as the institutional apex and coordinating mechanism for the entire Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy operational framework, exercising supervisory authority over four subordinate operational units (13-10, 13A, 13B, and 13C) that collectively encompass 27 distinct activities. Unit 13 bears primary responsibility for providing specialized high-level advisory counsel to the Secretary General, the President, and the Vice-Presidents of Parliament across all matters falling within the Directorate General's competence domain, while simultaneously orchestrating comprehensive strategic organization, management, and direction of all General Directorate activities through systematic objective-setting, resource allocation, and adaptive capacity management in response to evolving operational demands and institutional priorities. The unit maintains institutional representation and collaborative coordination functions, serving as the principal representative of general management in all formal bodies of the European Parliament and internal General Secretariat meetings while actively fostering inter-directorate cooperation and coordination with peer general managers across the institution. Additionally, Unit 13 exercises delegated financial authorization authority as an authorizing officer and contributes substantively to the preparation, attendance, and follow-up of meetings convened by the Conference of Presidents of Political Groups (CPG) and the Bureau, addressing matters within the General Directorate's jurisdictional scope and ensuring strategic coherence with Parliament's overarching institutional objectives and the General Directorate's Annual Work Program.
13.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Advisory & Strategic Leadership Engagement** (40%): Provision of specialized advisory guidance to the Secretary General, President, and Vice-Presidents of Parliament on all matters within the Directorate General's domain of competence

• **Strategic Organizational Management & Direction** (30%): Comprehensive strategic planning, objective-setting, resource allocation, and adaptive management of General Directorate activities; determination of organizational objectives and dynamic adaptation of means to accommodate evolving activity volumes in accordance with Parliament's institutional strategy and the General Directorate's Annual Work Program

• **Institutional Representation & Inter-Organizational Coordination** (20%): Representation of general management in European Parliament bodies and General Secretariat meetings; active cooperation and coordination with peer general managers across the institution to foster inter-directorate collaboration

• **Financial Authorization & Governance Functions** (5%): Exercise of delegated authorizing officer responsibilities for financial and administrative matters within the General Directorate's purview

• **Political Governance Engagement & High-Level Meeting Support** (5%): Substantive preparation, participation, and follow-up coordination for Conference of Presidents of Political Groups (CPG) and Bureau meetings addressing Directorate General competency areas

original_activities: Propose communication priorities and promote the activities of the general management, within Parliament and outside; supervise the process of developing a communication strategy via social networks for the DG. Produce content for different platforms. Ensure the proper implementation of the DG's communication strategy, and the harmonization of content created within the DG, in accordance with the European Parliament's Brand Book.

Define and implement the internal communication activities of the General Management, among other things through its intranet site. Strengthen cooperation, communication and knowledge sharing between general management services through the intranet and the organization of internal events.

Contribute to the organization and development of general management activities from a strategic perspective by proposing objectives and projects. Define event management guidelines and ensure their proper implementation. Assist the Director General in her outreach activities, by proposing various types of content, for example: speeches, notes, articles, scripts. Provide advice regarding media relations.

Assist the General Director and her team in monitoring the activities of the general management services, in particular by setting up and monitoring the planning of the general management's activities; contribute to the definition of new initiatives for the promotion of partnerships and other initiatives for parliamentary democracy, in collaboration with the Innovation and Partnership Development Unit.

Coordinate and prepare the work of drafting the work program and the annual activity report of the general management. Monitor the implementation of the priorities and objectives of the units of the General Directorate and liaise with the Strategy and Innovation Office of the General Secretariat.

Link the communication of the general management to that of the other institutions of the European Union, particularly during international days and awareness campaigns. Establish a network of communication and awareness-raising professionals on parliamentary democracy, in the European Union and beyond.
Coordinate the activities of general management in the area of ​​organizational risk.
13-10.refined_description: Unit 13-10 functions as the strategic communications and general management coordination directorate within the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy (DG 13), operating at the Directional level with direct accountability to the parent unit's leadership while serving as the operational hub for institutional messaging, strategic planning, and cross-directorate coordination. Positioned as a critical liaison between the Director General's office and all general management services, Unit 13-10 orchestrates a comprehensive portfolio spanning institutional communications strategy, internal organizational coordination, strategic planning development, directorial support services, inter-institutional networking, and organizational risk management. The unit operates without subordinate units while functioning as a peer to sibling units 13A, 13B, and 13C within the broader DG 13 structure, engaging in continuous collaboration with the Innovation and Partnership Development Unit on democratic partnership initiatives and maintaining liaison with the Strategy and Innovation Office of the General Secretariat. All communications activities are executed in strict compliance with the European Parliament's Brand Book standards, ensuring consistent institutional messaging across multi-platform channels and inter-institutional networks. The unit's workload allocation reflects its dual focus on external institutional visibility and internal organizational cohesion, positioning it as the central node for coordinating the DG's strategic priorities, annual planning cycles, and general management service oversight.
13-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **External Communications & Strategic Brand Management** (40%): Proposes and implements DG communication priorities and develops overarching communication strategy across all platforms including social media (20%); produces and harmonizes platform-specific content in compliance with European Parliament Brand Book standards; establishes inter-institutional communication networks with other European Union bodies, particularly during international observances and awareness campaigns (10%); develops professional networks focused on parliamentary democracy promotion within and beyond the EU; provides strategic advisory support to the Director General regarding media relations and creation of specialized content including speeches, articles, notes, and scripts (10%)

• **Internal Communications & Organizational Cooperation** (20%): Defines and implements general management internal communication activities through intranet infrastructure administration (20%); strengthens inter-service cooperation and knowledge sharing among general management services through digital platforms and organizational events; facilitates communication flows between general management services to enhance institutional collaboration

• **Strategic Planning & Program Development** (30%): Coordinates drafting of the DG's work program and annual activity report while monitoring implementation of priorities and objectives across all units (15%); assists Director General in monitoring general management service activities and activity planning; collaborates with the Innovation and Partnership Development Unit on partnership and democratic promotion initiatives (15%); contributes to strategic development of general management activities by proposing objectives and projects

• **Event Management & Directorial Support Services** (10%): Designs and executes event management frameworks and guidelines ensuring proper implementation (10%); provides specialized content assistance and media relations advisory support for Director General outreach activities including speeches, notes, articles, and scripts

• **Risk Management & Institutional Governance** (5%): Coordinates organizational risk management activities across general management functions (5%)

13A.name: 13A
original_activities: Ensure the direction and coordination of the main activities of the Directorate, in particular contacts and monitoring with the political and administrative bodies of the European Parliament (Cabinets, Vice-Presidents, Political Groups, Directorates-General, etc.).
Maintain and develop relationships with representatives of national parliaments in Brussels and capitals.
Prepare background notes and analyzes for MPs.
Participate in CERDP and IPEX activities (CERDP co-direction - member of the IPEX Board).
Ensure administrative management as well as monitoring of files (objectives, activity reports, PPP, personnel, etc.).
Perform the function of budget authorizing officer.
13A.refined_description: Unit 13A functions as the strategic Direction within the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy (DG 13), serving as the primary coordination and liaison hub for all major Directorate activities and inter-institutional relationships. The unit's core mission encompasses ensuring comprehensive direction and coordination of Directorate functions through systematic engagement with the European Parliament's political and administrative bodies—including Parliamentary Cabinets, Vice-Presidential offices, Political Groups, and peer Directorates-General—thereby establishing itself as a central nexus for institutional coordination (30% of workload). Unit 13A simultaneously develops and sustains substantive bilateral relationships with national parliament representatives operating in both Brussels-based and capital-city locations (20% of workload), while producing analytical background notes and policy documentation to support Members of the European Parliament in their legislative and institutional functions (20% of workload). The unit actively participates in governance structures of transnational parliamentary cooperation, specifically co-directing the Conference of European Directors of Parliaments (CERDP) and serving as Board member of the Inter-Parliamentary Exchange (IPEX) platform, thereby advancing multilateral parliamentary cooperation frameworks and institutional exchange mechanisms (17% of workload). Administrative and fiduciary stewardship constitutes a significant responsibility, encompassing comprehensive file and objective monitoring, activity reporting, performance planning processes (PPP), and personnel administration (8% of workload), complemented by execution of budget authorizing officer functions ensuring fiscal oversight and resource allocation accountability (5% of workload). Through oversight of three subordinate operational units (13A10, 13A20, and 13A30), Unit 13A implements these integrated strategic and operational mandates while maintaining collaborative alignment with sibling directional units (13-10, 13B, and 13C) within the broader Directorate structure.
13A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction and Internal Institutional Coordination** (30%): Ensure comprehensive direction and coordination of all Directorate activities; establish and maintain critical institutional relationships with European Parliament's political bodies (Parliamentary Cabinets, Vice-Presidential offices, Political Groups) and administrative structures (peer Directorates-General); function as central liaison hub for inter-institutional engagement

• **Inter-Parliamentary Relations Development and Representation** (40%): Develop and maintain bilateral and multilateral relationships with national parliament representatives in Brussels and respective capital cities (20%); prepare substantive analytical background materials, policy analyses, and briefing documentation supporting Members of the European Parliament in legislative decision-making and institutional functions (20%)

• **Transnational Parliamentary Governance and Multilateral Networks** (17%): Co-direct the Conference of European Directors of Parliaments (CERDP) operations; serve as Board member of the Inter-Parliamentary Exchange (IPEX) platform; represent the European Parliament in multilateral parliamentary cooperation mechanisms and institutional exchange frameworks

• **Administrative Management and File Stewardship** (8%): Manage comprehensive organizational file systems including strategic objectives tracking, activity reporting, performance planning processes (PPP), and personnel administration across the Directorate

• **Budget Authorization and Fiscal Oversight** (5%): Execute budget authorizing officer responsibilities ensuring fiscal accountability, resource allocation oversight, and financial stewardship across Directorate operations

13A10.name: 13A10
original_activities: Assist the European Parliament delegation to COSAC (preparation of meetings, drafting notes, monitoring, etc.) and participate in the work of the COSAC secretariat.

Assist the President, Vice-Presidents and political bodies of the European Parliament in their relations with national Parliaments, including the Conference of Presidents of Parliaments (EUSC), meetings of Secretaries General (EUSG), informal meetings of Secretaries General, European Conference of Presidents of Parliament (organized by the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe - PACE), Summits of Presidents of Parliament, and others.
Organize bilateral visits by deputies and officials of national Parliaments, organize and develop relations with representatives of national Parliaments to the European Parliament.
Ensure co-secretariat and monitoring of ECPRD activities, including the publication 'Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe'. Cooperate with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services.
Ensure the participation of the European Parliament in the IPEX platform.

Cooperate with other general directorates, in particular for events with PNs in the area of ​​Democracy Support, Capacity Building, Election Observation and Pre-Accession Assistance. Assist the President, Vice-Presidents and political bodies of the European Parliament in meetings of the 'Crimea Platform' and the Union for the Mediterranean.
Organize the 'Presidency Parliament Support Program' (PPSP). Organize and coordinate thematic seminars for the administrations of national parliaments and the European Parliament.
Coordinate and write the annual report of the Directorate. Ensure budgetary management of the credits made available to management.
13A10.refined_description:
Unit 13A10 functions as a specialized operational unit within Direction 13A of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, serving as the primary administrative and coordinating mechanism for inter-parliamentary relations and multilateral parliamentary engagement at the European level. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses facilitating substantive diplomatic and working relationships between the European Parliament's senior leadership (President, Vice-Presidents, and political bodies) and their counterparts in national parliaments, while simultaneously managing the European Parliament's participation across multiple inter-parliamentary forums and governance structures. Unit 13A10 allocates 45% of its workload to direct support of European Parliament leadership in managing high-level diplomatic relations with national parliaments, including coordination of the Conference of Presidents of Parliaments (EUSC), the European Conference of Presidents of Parliament (organized under PACE auspices), Secretaries General meetings (EUSG and informal variants), and Parliamentary Summits, complemented by substantial support (23%) to the European Parliament's delegation to COSAC (Conference of Parliamentary Committees of the European Union), encompassing comprehensive meeting preparation, technical note drafting, ongoing monitoring activities, and active participation in COSAC secretariat operations. An additional 20% of unit activities are dedicated to capacity-building, knowledge-exchange, and institutional development initiatives, comprising: organization and coordination of the Presidency Parliament Support Program (PPSP) and thematic seminars for national parliament administrations (10%); co-secretariat functions and monitoring responsibilities for the European Centre for Parliamentary Research and Documentation (ECPRD), including editorial oversight of the 'Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe' publication in close cooperation with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services, alongside organization of bilateral parliamentary visits and cultivation of direct working relationships between deputies and officials of national parliaments with European Parliament representatives (10%); and European Parliament participation in the IPEX (Inter-Parliamentary EU Information Exchange) platform to facilitate inter-parliamentary information exchange and digital connectivity (10%). The unit dedicates 5% of its workload to inter-directorate cooperation on democracy support initiatives, capacity building programs, election observation missions, and pre-accession assistance activities, while simultaneously coordinating European Parliament engagement in the Crimea Platform and Union for the Mediterranean parliamentary meetings. Administrative and budgetary oversight, including preparation of the annual Directorate report and financial management of allocated credits, comprises the remaining 5% of unit operational capacity. Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 13A10 as the central operational nexus and primary coordination hub for parliamentary diplomacy, inter-parliamentary institutional cooperation, and multilateral parliamentary engagement within the European Parliament's broader partnerships framework.

##
13A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Leadership Support & Multilateral Forum Coordination** (45%): Provide comprehensive assistance to the European Parliament President, Vice-Presidents, and political bodies in managing high-level diplomatic relations with national parliaments; coordinate the Conference of Presidents of Parliaments (EUSC), Secretaries General meetings (EUSG and informal variants), European Conference of Presidents of Parliament (PACE), and Parliamentary Summits; support European Parliament delegation to COSAC with meeting preparation, technical note drafting, monitoring activities, and active COSAC secretariat participation

• **Capacity-Building, Knowledge-Exchange & Institutional Development Programs** (20%): Organize and coordinate the Presidency Parliament Support Program (PPSP) and thematic seminars for national parliament administrations; organize bilateral parliamentary visits and cultivate direct working relationships between deputies and officials of national parliaments with European Parliament representatives; manage ECPRD co-secretariat functions and editorial oversight of 'Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe' publication in cooperation with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services; ensure European Parliament participation in the IPEX platform for inter-parliamentary information exchange and digital connectivity

• **Democracy Support, Capacity Building & Regional Parliamentary Engagement** (5%): Cooperate with other general directorates on democracy support, capacity building, election observation missions, and pre-accession assistance initiatives; coordinate European Parliament engagement in the Crimea Platform and Union for the Mediterranean parliamentary structures

• **Administrative & Financial Management** (5%): Coordinate and draft the annual report of Direction 13A; manage budgetary oversight and financial administration of allocated credits and resources

13A20.name: 13A20
original_activities: Ensure legislative dialogue and parliamentary control: organize interparliamentary committee meetings (ICMs) in cooperation with the committee secretariats, including specialized interparliamentary conferences such as the Art Conference. 13 TSCG, the European Semester (EPW), the EUROPOL JPSG and support for the CFSP/CSDP Conference. Organize bilateral visits of PN deputies and officials to the European Parliament.

Ensure subsidiarity control: process documents from national Parliaments, manage the 'CONNECT' database and prepare a monthly note for the attention of the Conference of Committee Chairs and Members of Parliament on the documents sent by national Parliaments within the meaning of Protocols 2 (reasoned opinions and contributions). Update (in collaboration with Representatives of national Parliaments) the electronic Directory of the corresponding PE/PN committees (CORCOM).

Ensure the coordination of interparliamentary activities: develop the biannual calendar of EP/PN interparliamentary activities for the attention of the Conference of Presidents (in coordination with the former DG IPOL and the Parliament of the Presidency of the Council) and ensure its internal and external transmission (Presidents of national Parliaments). Distribute each week within Parliament the 'Weekly Agenda' of activities organized with national Parliaments.

Ensure interparliamentary cooperation at administrative level: organize meetings in the form of seminars or working groups between the administrations of national Parliaments and the secretariat of the European Parliament on thematic issues in cooperation with the different directorates-general.
Organize high-level interparliamentary meetings between the President and Vice-Presidents of the European Parliament and the Presidents of national Parliaments.

Providing support to rapporteurs: assisting MEPs, in particular rapporteurs, with regard to Union legislative projects that have attracted a considerable number of reasoned opinions and contributions from national Parliaments (notes to rapporteurs, ad hoc meetings between rapporteurs and national Parliaments, including video conferences).

Providing support to parliamentary bodies: assisting the bodies and administration of the European Parliament by providing expertise in the field of interparliamentary cooperation, in particular by providing thematic briefings and notes on the political and socio-economic situation of a country for bilateral visits and parliamentary committee delegations.
13A20.refined_description:
Unit 13A20 functions as the operational core of the European Parliament's interparliamentary relations framework, reporting to Direction 13A within the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. The unit serves as the primary mechanism for orchestrating comprehensive legislative dialogue and democratic accountability between the European Parliament and national parliaments across all EU member states. With a dominant operational focus of 65% on legislative dialogue and parliamentary control, the unit organizes and facilitates interparliamentary committee meetings (ICMs) in systematic cooperation with committee secretariats, coordinates specialized interparliamentary conferences (Art Conference, Thirteen Member States Convergence Group [13 TSCG], European Semester Parliamentary Working Group [EPW], and EUROPOL Joint Parliamentary Scrutiny Group [JPSG]), supports CFSP/CSDP Conference activities, and manages bilateral visits by national parliament deputies and officials to the European Parliament. Concurrently, the unit exercises subsidiarity control mechanisms (20% of workload) by processing legislative documents submitted by national parliaments, administering the CONNECT database, preparing monthly analytical notes for the Conference of Committee Chairs regarding Protocol 2 submissions (reasoned opinions and contributions), and collaboratively maintaining and updating the electronic Directory of corresponding PE/PN committees (CORCOM) in partnership with national parliament representatives. The unit further coordinates interparliamentary activities (5% of workload) through development of the biannual calendar of EP/PN interparliamentary activities for the Conference of Presidents, coordinating with relevant directorates-general and the Council Presidency, and distributing weekly agendas of national parliament-related activities throughout the Parliament. Supporting administrative and high-level functions (6% combined workload) include organizing administrative-level interparliamentary cooperation seminars and working groups between institutional secretariats on thematic matters, and facilitating high-level meetings between European Parliament President/Vice-Presidents and national parliament presidents. The unit additionally provides targeted support services (4% of workload) to MEPs and rapporteurs on Union legislative projects receiving substantial national parliament engagement through technical assistance notes and ad hoc meetings, and delivers specialized expertise to parliamentary bodies and administrations through thematic briefings and country-specific socio-economic analysis supporting bilateral visits and parliamentary committee delegations.

##
13A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Dialogue and Parliamentary Control** (65%): Organize and manage interparliamentary committee meetings (ICMs) in systematic cooperation with committee secretariats; coordinate specialized interparliamentary conferences (Art Conference, Thirteen Member States Convergence Group [13 TSCG], European Semester Parliamentary Working Group [EPW], EUROPOL Joint Parliamentary Scrutiny Group [JPSG]); support CFSP/CSDP Conference activities; arrange and manage bilateral visits of national parliament deputies and officials to the European Parliament.
