• **Subsidiarity Control and Document Management** (20%): Process and manage legislative documents submitted by national parliaments; administer the CONNECT database; prepare monthly analytical notes for the Conference of Committee Chairs and Members of Parliament regarding Protocol 2 submissions (reasoned opinions and contributions); collaboratively maintain and update the electronic Directory of corresponding PE/PN committees (CORCOM) in partnership with national parliament representatives.

• **Interparliamentary Coordination and Calendar Management** (5%): Develop and coordinate biannual calendars of EP/PN interparliamentary activities for the Conference of Presidents; liaise with relevant directorates-general and Council Presidency; distribute weekly internal and external agendas of national parliament-related activities throughout the Parliament.

• **Administrative Cooperation and High-Level Engagement** (6%): Organize administrative-level interparliamentary cooperation seminars and working groups between national parliament administrations and European Parliament secretariat on thematic issues (3%); facilitate high-level interparliamentary meetings between European Parliament President/Vice-Presidents and national parliament presidents (3%).

• **Rapporteur and Parliamentary Body Support Services** (4%): Provide targeted technical assistance to MEPs and rapporteurs on Union legislative projects attracting substantial national parliament engagement, including analytical notes and ad hoc meetings/video conferences (2%); deliver specialized expertise and thematic briefings to parliamentary bodies and administrations regarding interparliamentary cooperation frameworks and country-specific socio-economic analysis supporting bilateral visits and parliamentary committee delegations (2%).

13A30.name: 13A30
original_activities: Ensure co-secretariat and monitoring of ECPRD activities, including the publication 'Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe'. Cooperate with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services.
Organize seminars between officials of the European Parliament and officials of national Parliaments in several thematic and technical areas.
Ensure the participation of the European Parliament in the IPEX platform.
Organize bilateral visits by deputies and officials of national Parliaments, organize and develop relations with representatives of national Parliaments to the European Parliament.
Organiser le Presidency Parliament Support Programme (PPSP).
13A30.refined_description: Unit 13A30 functions as a specialized operational unit within Direction 13A of the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, serving as the European Parliament's primary institutional interface for inter-parliamentary cooperation, knowledge exchange, and diplomatic engagement across the European Union's parliamentary systems. Operating as a critical nexus for inter-institutional coordination, the unit maintains a balanced and comprehensive portfolio of five interconnected core activities that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, each designed to strengthen horizontal cooperation and enhance knowledge-sharing mechanisms among parliamentary systems. The unit executes dual roles as co-secretariat and monitoring body for the European Centre for Parliamentary Research and Documentation (ECPRD) while ensuring publication and dissemination of the "Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe" series in systematic coordination with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services, thereby advancing comprehensive parliamentary research accessibility and institutional visibility across the EU framework. Beyond research coordination, Unit 13A30 organizes and facilitates structured seminars and professional development initiatives that bring together European Parliament officials and counterparts from national parliaments across multiple thematic and technical domains, fostering institutional learning, cross-parliamentary policy dialogue, and capacity-building. The unit ensures substantive European Parliament participation and engagement through the IPEX (Inter-Parliamentary EU Information Exchange) digital platform, maintaining institutional presence within critical inter-parliamentary information-sharing infrastructure while simultaneously orchestrating bilateral parliamentary visits, developing reciprocal diplomatic relations between deputies and officials, and cultivating sustained working relationships with national parliamentary representatives accredited to or engaging with the European Parliament. Additionally, Unit 13A30 administers and coordinates the Presidency Parliament Support Programme (PPSP), providing specialized institutional capacity-building support mechanisms during rotating parliamentary presidencies. Collectively, these interconnected activities position Unit 13A30 as a central facilitator of horizontal inter-parliamentary cooperation, institutional relationship management, and knowledge transfer that supports democratic governance across multiple parliamentary systems within the broader European parliamentary democracy partnership framework.
13A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Inter-Parliamentary Research, Documentation, and Publication Exchange** (25%): Co-secretariat and comprehensive monitoring of ECPRD activities; publication and systematic dissemination of "Spotlight on Parliaments in Europe" series; institutional coordination with the Directorate General of Parliamentary Research Services to ensure comprehensive parliamentary research accessibility and cross-parliamentary visibility.

• **Institutional Knowledge-Sharing, Professional Development, and Capacity-Building** (25%): Organization and facilitation of structured thematic and technical seminars between European Parliament officials and national parliament counterparts; fostering cross-institutional learning, policy dialogue, and professional development initiatives; administration of the Presidency Parliament Support Programme (PPSP) providing targeted institutional capacity-building support during rotating parliamentary presidencies.

• **Bilateral Parliamentary Relations and Official Diplomatic Engagement** (20%): Organization and facilitation of bilateral parliamentary visits for deputies and officials from national parliaments; development, cultivation, and maintenance of sustained formal relationships and diplomatic networks with national parliamentary representatives accredited to or engaging with the European Parliament; diplomatic liaison and institutional partnership functions.

• **Digital Inter-Parliamentary Engagement and Information Exchange Infrastructure** (20%): Ensuring substantive European Parliament participation and engagement within the IPEX (Inter-Parliamentary EU Information Exchange) platform; maintenance of institutional presence within critical inter-parliamentary information-sharing and coordination systems.

13B.name: 13B
original_activities: Provide leadership and coordination of the Directorate's units, in particular the Parliamentary Liaison Offices and representations outside the European Union as well as the European Union Visitors Program (EUVP), while consolidating the role of the European Parliament in parliamentary and public diplomacy and promoting parliamentary democracy.

Consolidate and develop existing structures, skills, procedures and activities; identify synergies; explore new possible structures; finalize the implementation of new structures, activities and procedures as well as promote cooperation, knowledge sharing and dialogue between units.

Contribute to improving legislative dialogue and building partnerships for democracy with countries and international organizations, promoting relations and links between the European Parliament and the Parliaments of strategically important countries as well as multilateral actors.

Contribute to the promotion of European Union values ​​and the facilitation of exchanges by organizing visit programs and follow-up activities for young political leaders and opinion multipliers, while exploring possibilities for cooperation with similar people-to-people policy programs and other institutional partners.
Contribute to establishing and strengthening relationships with a wide range of relevant civil society interlocutors, stakeholders and multipliers in strategically important countries.
Provide assistance to political and administrative bodies: draft proposals for action to the hierarchy and political authorities of the Institution.

Ensure communication and cooperation with other services within the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy and the European Parliament as well as interinstitutional cooperation.
13B.refined_description:
Unit 13B functions as the strategic Direction-level operational hub within the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy (DG 13), exercising comprehensive executive leadership and coordination across the European Parliament's external parliamentary engagement infrastructure while consolidating the institution's role in parliamentary and public diplomacy initiatives that advance democratic governance. The unit provides integrated strategic direction and operational oversight of nine subordinate units encompassing the Parliamentary Liaison Offices (13B10, 13B20, 13B30), regional representations external to the European Union (13B40, 13B50, 13B60, and three additional specialized units), and the European Union Visitors Program (EUVP), thereby serving as the primary coordinating and policy-development body for the European Parliament's external parliamentary engagement and democratic promotion initiatives. Unit 13B undertakes substantial organizational development responsibilities by consolidating and advancing existing institutional structures, technical capabilities, and procedures; identifying cross-functional synergies among subordinate units; exploring innovative organizational configurations; implementing new structures and activities; and fostering inter-unit cooperation, knowledge exchange, and strategic dialogue to enhance institutional effectiveness and promote inter-unit cooperation across the Directorate. The unit strengthens legislative dialogue and builds democratic partnerships with strategically important countries and multilateral international organizations, actively promoting substantive relations and institutional linkages between the European Parliament and the parliaments of key geopolitical actors and multilateral institutional partners, while simultaneously facilitating people-to-people diplomatic engagement through targeted visit programs and follow-up activities designed for emerging political leaders and opinion multipliers. Unit 13B establishes and reinforces relationships with diverse civil society interlocutors, stakeholders, and influential multipliers operating in strategically important countries; provides strategic advisory assistance to political and administrative authorities through the development of action proposals for institutional hierarchy; and ensures systematic communication and operational cooperation with complementary services within the Directorate-General and across the European Parliament while coordinating interinstitutional engagement mechanisms with external institutional partners.

---
13B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership, Network Coordination, and Diplomatic Consolidation** (30%): Provide comprehensive executive direction and operational oversight of the Directorate's Parliamentary Liaison Offices and external representations; manage the European Union Visitors Program (EUVP); consolidate and advance the European Parliament's institutional role in parliamentary and public diplomacy while promoting parliamentary democracy advancement and reinforcing the institution's position in democratic governance promotion.

• **Organizational Development, Structural Optimization, and Internal Synergy Enhancement** (25%): Consolidate and develop institutional structures, technical capabilities, and operational procedures; identify and exploit cross-functional synergies among subordinate units; explore and implement innovative organizational models and configurations; implement new structures and activities; and promote inter-unit cooperation, knowledge sharing, strategic dialogue, and institutional effectiveness across the Directorate.

• **Legislative Dialogue, Democratic Partnership Building, and Multilateral Engagement** (20%): Advance legislative cooperation frameworks and democracy-building partnerships with strategically important countries and multilateral international organizations; promote substantive relations and institutional linkages between the European Parliament and parliaments of key geopolitical actors; engage with multilateral institutional partners to strengthen democratic partnerships.

• **People-to-People Diplomacy, Civic Engagement, and Capacity Building** (15%): Organize targeted visit programs and coordinated follow-up activities for emerging political leaders and opinion multipliers; explore cooperative opportunities with analogous democratic promotion and institutional exchange programs; establish and reinforce relationships with civil society actors, stakeholders, and influential multipliers in strategically important countries.

• **Strategic Advisory Support and Interinstitutional Coordination** (10%): Provide analytical and administrative assistance to political and administrative authorities through development and submission of action proposals to institutional hierarchy; ensure systematic communication and operational cooperation with complementary services within DG 13 and across the European Parliament; manage interinstitutional coordination mechanisms with external institutional partners.

13B10.name: 13B10
original_activities: Contribute to the development of relations between the European Parliament, the British Parliament, national, regional and local British authorities. Provide information on the political situation in the United Kingdom to political authorities and hierarchy.

Stimulate and ensure public debate on the work of the European Parliament using all appropriate means, in particular towards citizens of the European Union, young people and pro-European Britons by organizing debates and political and cultural events. Run campaigns on values, such as cultural diversity and human rights. Deepen the network of 'together.eu' volunteers.
Provide logistical support to the President, MPs and parliamentary delegations carrying out activities in the Member State where the Office is established.
Provide information to national, regional and specialized media (written press, TV, radio, online, social media) on the positions taken by the European Parliament. Manage the website.
Ensure the financial management of the activities carried out by the Office; manage its human resources; manage the logistics of the 'House of Europe'.
Implement interinstitutional relations with the European External Action Service, including joint management of public space.
13B10.refined_description:
Unit 13B10 is a specialized parliamentary liaison and public engagement office operating as a Level 2 organizational unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, functioning as the European Parliament's primary institutional interface with the United Kingdom. The unit operates as a leaf-node structure with no subordinate units but maintains parallel coordination with sibling units 13B20, 13B30, 13B40, 13B50, 13B60, and three additional units within Direction 13B to deliver comprehensive parliamentary partnership and democratic engagement objectives. Unit 13B10 executes a multifaceted mandate integrating diplomatic engagement, political intelligence provision, public communication, logistical support, media relations, and administrative operations, with particular strategic focus on developing and maintaining institutional relations between the European Parliament and the British Parliament, national, regional, and local British authorities while simultaneously serving as the primary information conduit to parliamentary leadership and organizational hierarchy regarding the evolving United Kingdom political situation. The unit stimulates sustained public debate on European Parliament activities through diverse communication channels and organized political-cultural events, targeting European Union citizens, young people, and pro-European British constituencies while deepening the 'together.eu' volunteer network; provides comprehensive logistical support to the President, Members of Parliament, and parliamentary delegations conducting activities within the United Kingdom; manages media engagement across national, regional, and specialized outlets including written press, television, radio, digital platforms, and social media while maintaining institutional web presence; oversees financial management and human resources administration of office operations and the 'House of Europe' logistics infrastructure; and implements interinstitutional coordination with the European External Action Service, including joint management of public space. This comprehensive operational structure ensures the European Parliament maintains strategic presence, effective communication capacity, and sustained institutional engagement within the United Kingdom across diplomatic, public, administrative, and interinstitutional dimensions.

##
13B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Relations and Political Intelligence** (50%): Development and maintenance of bilateral relations between the European Parliament and the British Parliament, national, regional, and local British authorities; provision of systematic political analysis and situational intelligence regarding United Kingdom political developments to parliamentary leadership and organizational hierarchy.

• **Public Engagement and Civic Mobilization** (15%): Stimulation of public debate on European Parliament activities through organized political and cultural events; implementation of public campaigns advancing fundamental values including cultural diversity and human rights; development and expansion of the 'together.eu' volunteer network targeting European Union citizens, young people, and pro-European British constituencies.

• **Media Relations and Communications** (15%): Dissemination of European Parliament institutional positions to national, regional, and specialized media outlets across all platforms (written press, television, radio, digital platforms, and social media); management of institutional website and digital communications infrastructure.

• **Operational Support Services** (15%): Provision of comprehensive logistical support to the President, Members of Parliament, and parliamentary delegations conducting activities within the United Kingdom jurisdiction.

• **Administrative and Interinstitutional Management** (5%): Financial management and human resource administration of office operations, including 'House of Europe' facility logistics coordination; interinstitutional coordination with the European External Action Service and joint management of shared public space resources.

13B20.name: 13B20
original_activities: Facilitate parliamentary dialogue with the United States Congress and other authorities, by preparing visits or virtual meetings of TLDs or Delegations for the USA, delegations from parliamentary committees or other bodies, authorities and EP deputies traveling to the USA. Strengthen ties with congressional bodies at all levels. Prepare and assist contacts and mutual visits of parliamentarians/staff of the EP and the Congress in order to improve mutual understanding.

Deepen contacts with think tanks, universities, academic institutions and think tanks as well as with NGOs and other stakeholders. Develop the participation of the EPLO in seminars and conferences of interest, as well as the promotion of information on the EU and awareness-raising actions on the work of the European Parliament in forums and in university and academic circles.

Provide useful information on developments in the United States, and in particular in Congress, to all actors in Parliament, as well as implement means to concretely identify the main legislative issues of common interest.
Implement the communication strategy for EPLO (social media, EPLO briefings). Prepare materials explaining the European Parliament and its policies to the interested American public.

Maintain and develop 'communities' in the European population in the United States in order to prepare the campaign for the next European elections, with particular emphasis on maintaining the activation of volunteers and young Europeans.
Ensure the proper functioning and financial management of EPLO Washington.
Strengthen interinstitutional collaboration with the EU delegation in Washington (EEAS and European Commission).
Expand networks, including EUVP Alumni and Programs, Congressional Research Service alumni, Government Accountability Office, Congressional Budget Office, etc.
13B20.refined_description:
Unit 13B20 operates as the European Parliament's Liaison Office (EPLO) in Washington, D.C., functioning as a strategic operational unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. As a leaf-node unit collaborating with sibling units (13B10, 13B30, 13B40, 13B50, 13B60, and three additional units), Unit 13B20 executes a comprehensive mission to strengthen transatlantic parliamentary relations, advance European Union interests, and deepen democratic cooperation between the European Parliament and United States governmental institutions, particularly Congress, through multifaceted engagement with congressional bodies, federal agencies, and American civil society stakeholders. The unit's operational framework integrates high-level parliamentary diplomacy with strategic intelligence analysis, stakeholder relationship development, and public communication initiatives, all supported by robust administrative and interinstitutional coordination mechanisms. Unit 13B20 maintains coordinated engagement with the European External Action Service (EEAS) and European Commission delegations in Washington to ensure coherent EU institutional messaging and operational efficiency, while simultaneously expanding professional networks encompassing Congressional Research Service, Government Accountability Office, Congressional Budget Office, and EUVP alumni programs. The unit's activities directly support the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy's broader strategic objectives while maintaining operational sustainability through comprehensive financial management and administrative oversight of the Washington office.

---

##
13B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Diplomacy, High-Level Engagement, and Institutional Exchange** (45%): Facilitate and prepare high-level visits, virtual meetings, and delegations for European Parliament top-level delegations (TLDs), parliamentary committee delegations, and individual EP deputies traveling to the United States; coordinate reciprocal visits of U.S. parliamentarians and congressional staff to the European Parliament; organize meetings with United States Congress bodies and governmental authorities across all congressional levels; strengthen institutional ties, mutual understanding, and parliamentary cooperation through coordinated exchange programs between EP and congressional counterparts.

• **Stakeholder Engagement and Academic Outreach** (15%): Develop and maintain substantive collaborative relationships with American think tanks, universities, academic institutions, and non-governmental organizations; promote European Union awareness and European Parliament visibility through active participation in seminars, conferences, and academic forums; facilitate EPLO participation in university and academic circles; coordinate information dissemination to American stakeholder communities.

• **Intelligence Analysis, Legislative Monitoring, and Policy Coordination** (15%): Provide strategic intelligence and continuous analytical reporting on United States political developments, congressional legislative agendas, activities, and policy trends to all European Parliament stakeholders; systematically monitor and identify priority legislative issues of mutual interest between the EP and Congress; disseminate relevant policy information to all EP actors to inform parliamentary decision-making.

• **Communications, Public Engagement, and Electoral Mobilization** (16%): Implement EPLO strategic communications strategy across social media platforms and briefing materials; develop and prepare educational resources explaining European Parliament structures and policies to American audiences; activate and maintain European diaspora communities within the United States population to support European Parliament election campaigns; coordinate volunteer engagement networks and emphasize participation of young European residents in campaign mobilization efforts.

• **Institutional Operations, Interinstitutional Coordination, and Professional Network Development** (9%): Ensure proper financial stewardship and operational functioning of the Washington office through comprehensive administrative management; strengthen interinstitutional collaboration with EU representation structures, including the European External Action Service (EEAS) and European Commission delegations in Washington, to ensure coordinated EU institutional presence and messaging; expand professional networks through alumni programs and specialized connections with U.S. governmental research institutions (Congressional Research Service, Government Accountability Office, Congressional Budget Office) and EUVP program alumni.

13B30.name: 13B30
original_activities: Contribute to the dissemination of the European Parliament's positions, to knowledge of its activities and to strengthening the visibility of the European Parliament. Likewise, disseminate appropriate information on UN activities more widely within the European Parliament. Organize the concrete activities necessary to accomplish these objectives.
Ensure the preparation, organization and follow-up of the European Parliament's visits to the UN and other interlocutors in New York.

Fulfill the leadership role on a certain number of files of the Delegation of the European Union to the UN, which are of interest to the European Parliament (logic of burden-sharing as seconded civil servants); participate in service and management meetings of the Delegation of the European Union.
Identify and intensify contacts with key interlocutors of the UN Secretariat (programs and agencies), as well as other interlocutors in New York (city, civil society, universities and think tanks).
Contribute to the activities of the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, participate in internal meetings of the Directorate-General and the European Parliament.
13B30.refined_description: Unit 13B30 functions as the European Parliament's dedicated liaison and coordination office for United Nations engagement, operating as a specialized unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy at the second hierarchical level of the organization. The unit serves as the central institutional mechanism for strengthening parliamentary-UN relations and advancing the European Parliament's multilateral engagement through five integrated functional pillars that collectively represent 100% of documented workload. These activities encompass strategic communications and institutional visibility; comprehensive preparation, organization, and follow-up of European Parliament delegations to the United Nations and New York-based interlocutors; substantive leadership on designated priority files within the Delegation of the European Union to the UN operating under burden-sharing principles through seconded civil servants; identification and intensification of strategic contacts with UN Secretariat leadership, programs, specialized agencies, and broader New York-based stakeholders including municipal authorities, civil society organizations, academic institutions, and policy research organizations; and participation in internal coordination mechanisms of the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. Through these interconnected operational activities, Unit 13B30 enables the European Parliament to maintain effective institutional presence at the United Nations while strengthening democratic dialogue, parliamentary cooperation frameworks, and multilateral engagement capacity.
13B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Communications, Visibility, and Information Dissemination** (25%): Disseminate European Parliament positions, institutional activities, and parliamentary visibility regarding UN engagement; disseminate UN activities and operational information throughout the European Parliament; organize and implement concrete dissemination activities and initiatives to enhance internal Parliament knowledge of UN operations and strengthen parliamentary visibility within UN contexts

• **Parliamentary Delegation Coordination and Visit Management** (25%): Prepare, organize, conduct follow-up, and ensure logistical and diplomatic coordination for all European Parliament official delegations visiting the United Nations and engaging with key New York-based interlocutors; manage substantive and administrative aspects of parliamentary missions

• **EU Delegation Leadership and Institutional Participation** (20%): Exercise substantive leadership responsibility on designated priority files within the Delegation of the European Union to the UN that align with European Parliament interests; participate in Delegation service and management meetings; fulfill burden-sharing responsibilities as seconded civil servants

• **Strategic Stakeholder Engagement and Network Development** (20%): Identify and intensify strategic contacts with UN Secretariat leadership, programs, and specialized agencies; develop and maintain relationships with New York-based stakeholders including municipal authorities, civil society organizations, academic institutions, and policy research organizations to enhance parliamentary dialogue channels and multilateral engagement capacity

• **Directorate-General Coordination and Internal Institutional Alignment** (10%): Contribute to broader activities of the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy; participate in Directorate-General service, management, and coordination meetings; engage in Parliament-wide coordination mechanisms to ensure alignment with institutional priorities and governance frameworks

13B40.name: 13B40
original_activities: Promote the exchange of knowledge and good practices with the ASEAN Inter-Parliamentary Assembly (AIPA) and the National Parliaments of ASEAN Member States.

Contribute to the development of a permanent EU-ASEAN multilateral dialogue, based on the agreement reached in 2021 between the President of DASE and the Secretary-General of the ASEAN Inter-Parliamentary Assembly (AIPA), by supporting the work and in close cooperation with the ASEAN-Australia-New Zealand unit.
Work in coordination with all EU institutional actors involved in EU-ASEAN relations, for example other institutions, including the EEAS and its delegations in the region.

Raise awareness of the institutional role and policies of the European Parliament and improve its visibility in the region; promote assistance in the preparation and monitoring of the activities of the European Parliament in the region; promote the exchange of good practices in legislation in areas of common interest.

Keep staff and Members of the European Parliament regularly informed of political and legislative developments in the ASEAN region and vice versa; facilitate working relationships between ASEAN National Parliamentary Research Services and EPRS and thematic departments.
Develop a network of think tanks and policy foundations in the ASEAN region.
Create a set of dedicated communication tools and communication activities to raise awareness of cooperation between regions and facilitate interpersonal relationships.
13B40.refined_description: Unit 13B40 operates as the dedicated European Parliament liaison and strategic engagement unit for Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) relations, functioning as a specialized operational component within Direction 13B under the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. The unit serves as the European Parliament's primary institutional mechanism for advancing multilateral parliamentary dialogue, institutional coordination, and knowledge-sharing mechanisms with ASEAN stakeholders, positioning itself as a critical bridge institution between European and Southeast Asian parliamentary ecosystems. Unit 13B40's comprehensive mandate encompasses seven fully integrated activity streams representing 100% of documented operational workload, strategically designed to deepen institutional relationships, facilitate evidence-based knowledge exchange, enhance parliamentary visibility, and strengthen interpersonal relationships across the ASEAN region. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 13B40 functions within a broader constellation of sibling units (13B10, 13B20, 13B30, 13B50, 13B60, and three additional units) under Direction 13B's strategic coordination, collectively advancing the European Parliament's comprehensive external relations portfolio. The unit's activities are strategically distributed across foundational relationship-building, institutional coordination and visibility enhancement, and knowledge infrastructure development, with particular emphasis on implementing the permanent EU-ASEAN multilateral dialogue framework established through the 2021 agreement between the President of DASE and the AIPA Secretary-General.
13B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Multilateral Dialogue Development and Institutional Partnerships** (40%): Promote knowledge exchange and good practice sharing with the ASEAN Inter-Parliamentary Assembly (AIPA) and National Parliaments of ASEAN Member States (20%); develop and maintain the permanent EU-ASEAN multilateral dialogue framework through close cooperation with the ASEAN-Australia-New Zealand unit, implementing the 2021 DASE-AIPA Secretary-General agreement (20%).

• **Institutional Coordination and Parliamentary Visibility** (30%): Coordinate engagement with all relevant EU institutional actors involved in EU-ASEAN relations, including the European External Action Service (EEAS) and its regional delegations, ensuring coherent European institutional positioning (15%); enhance the European Parliament's institutional visibility, role awareness, and policy profile within the region while promoting legislative good practice exchanges in areas of mutual interest (15%).

• **Knowledge Management and Information Flows** (10%): Maintain regular communication channels and briefings with European Parliament staff and Members regarding ASEAN political and legislative developments; facilitate reciprocal information exchange and working relationships between ASEAN National Parliamentary Research Services and the European Parliament Research Service (EPRS) and thematic departments.

• **Strategic Network Development** (10%): Establish and cultivate a dedicated network of think tanks and policy foundations throughout the ASEAN region to support evidence-based parliamentary cooperation and dialogue.

• **Communications and Stakeholder Engagement** (10%): Develop and implement dedicated communication tools and targeted engagement activities to strengthen regional cooperation awareness, amplify inter-regional dialogue visibility, and foster sustainable interpersonal relationships between European and ASEAN parliamentary actors.

13B50.name: 13B50
original_activities: Ensure the full functioning and visibility of the EP-African Union Antenna, including in logistical and personnel aspects.

Contribute to the dissemination of the European Parliament's positions, to knowledge of its activities and to strengthening the visibility of the Parliament. Also disseminate appropriate information more widely on the activities of the African Union and the Pan-African Parliament within the European Parliament. Organize the concrete activities necessary to accomplish these objectives.

Contribute to the preparation, organization and follow-up of visits by official bodies of the European Parliament to the African Union, the Pan-African Parliament, regional and national Parliaments and other interlocutors in Addis Ababa and Africa.

Contribute to the activities of the General Management, participate in internal meetings of the General Management and Parliament. Contribute within Parliament to the reflection on relations and cooperation, including as future potential, between the European Union and the African Union.

Strengthen the relations of the European Parliament with the Pan-African Parliament. Identify and intensify contacts with key interlocutors from the African Union, the Pan-African Parliament, regional organizations and their Parliaments.

Work in coordination with other institutions, including the EEAS and the European Union Delegations in Africa. Develop a network of contacts (civil society, think tanks, universities) in Europe and Africa.

Take the lead role on a number of files of the European Union Delegation to the African Union, which are of interest to the European Parliament. Participate in service and management meetings of the European Union Delegation as well as in meetings organized by the Delegation with the Heads of Mission of the Member States present in Addis Ababa and with the political advisors of these Missions.

Ensure a valuable contribution to the EUVP Program by ensuring a selection of program participants that can ensure closer links between the Parliament and the African Union and with the Pan-African Parliament.
13B50.refined_description:
**

Unit 13B50 functions as the European Parliament's specialized operational hub for institutional engagement with the African Union, Pan-African Parliament, and continental African structures, positioned within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. The unit's mission encompasses the full operational management and institutional visibility of the EP-African Union Antenna based in Addis Ababa (20% of workload), including comprehensive logistical and personnel administration necessary for on-site representation and effectiveness. The unit advances the European Parliament's strategic communication objectives by disseminating parliamentary positions and activities within African institutional networks while simultaneously promoting knowledge of the African Union and Pan-African Parliament's work throughout the Parliament, organizing all concrete implementation activities required to achieve these visibility and communication goals (15% of workload). Unit 13B50 orchestrates the logistical, diplomatic, and follow-up preparations for official parliamentary delegations visiting the African Union, Pan-African Parliament, regional and national parliaments, and key interlocutors throughout Addis Ababa and the African continent (15% of workload). The unit strengthens bilateral parliamentary relations with the Pan-African Parliament through systematic identification and intensification of strategic contacts across African Union structures, regional organizations, and their respective parliamentary bodies (10% of workload), while simultaneously serving as a critical coordination nexus with external institutional partners—including the European External Action Service (EEAS) and European Union Delegations throughout Africa—and developing comprehensive networks encompassing civil society, academic institutions, and think tanks across both European and African contexts (10% of workload). Unit 13B50 contributes substantively to General Management activities and internal parliamentary deliberations on EU-AU relations and future cooperation potential (15% of workload), while assuming a leadership role on designated files of the European Union Delegation to the African Union that align with European Parliament interests, actively participating in service management meetings and high-level diplomatic engagements with Member State Heads of Mission and political advisors stationed in Addis Ababa (10% of workload). The unit further enhances EU-AU institutional linkages through strategic curation of the EUVP Program's participant selection, ensuring participants possess the capacity to deepen institutional connections between the Parliament and both the African Union and Pan-African Parliament (5% of workload).

---

**
13B50.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **EP-African Union Antenna Operations & Infrastructure Management** (20%): Ensure full functioning and institutional visibility of the EP-African Union Antenna in Addis Ababa through comprehensive management of logistical requirements, personnel administration, and on-site operational capacity necessary for sustained institutional representation and engagement effectiveness.

• **Strategic Communication & Visibility Enhancement** (15%): Disseminate European Parliament positions and activities throughout African institutional networks; promote knowledge of African Union and Pan-African Parliament activities within the Parliament; organize and implement all concrete activities required to achieve communication and visibility objectives across both institutional contexts.

• **Official Delegation Support & Diplomatic Coordination** (15%): Prepare, organize, and conduct follow-up activities for official European Parliament delegations visiting the African Union, Pan-African Parliament, regional and national parliaments, and key interlocutors in Addis Ababa and across the African continent, managing all logistical and diplomatic preparations.

• **Bilateral Relations & Strategic Contact Development** (10%): Strengthen EP-Pan-African Parliament relations through systematic identification and intensification of strategic contacts across African Union structures, regional organizations, and their respective parliamentary bodies to deepen institutional partnerships.

• **Inter-institutional Coordination & Network Development** (10%): Coordinate with the European External Action Service (EEAS) and European Union Delegations throughout Africa; develop and maintain comprehensive contact networks encompassing civil society, academic institutions, and think tanks across European and African contexts to enhance institutional connectivity.

• **General Management & Diplomatic Engagement** (15%): Contribute to General Management activities and internal parliamentary deliberations on EU-AU relations and future cooperation frameworks; assume leadership role on designated European Union Delegation to the African Union files aligned with European Parliament interests; participate actively in service management meetings and high-level diplomatic engagements with Member State Heads of Mission and political advisors in Addis Ababa.

• **Capacity-Building & Program Enhancement** (5%): Ensure strategic selection of EUVP Program participants possessing demonstrated capacity to deepen institutional connections between the European Parliament and both the African Union and Pan-African Parliament, strengthening long-term relational foundations.

13B60.name: 13B60
original_activities: Prepare, based on the specific interests of visitors, programs of visits and meetings, within the Institutions, but also with external contacts (in practice, this translates on average, per visitor, into +/-40 contacts, for the organization of +/- 10/15 meetings/visitors/week). Organize virtual visits (at least 6 virtual meetings/year), if necessary.

Maintain direct, effective and daily cooperation with European Union delegations and Liaison Offices and Antennas of the European Parliament located outside the EU in order to improve the preparation, selection and monitoring of candidates for the study visits program.

Ensure the management of the interinstitutional unit (EP/Commission) responsible for coordinating individual study visits to European institutions, in Brussels and/or Strasbourg, of around 270 non-national visitors from European Union countries per year (five-day working study visits or virtual visits).
Ensure budgetary management and administrative coordination of all of these study visits.

Develop and ensure communication activities as well as regular updating of the web page, Facebook page and information publications on the program. Develop relationships with think tanks and universities.
Develop regular relationships with the services of the different institutions: EP, CE, Council, CoR, CES, EEAS, etc.
Prepare itineraries and book flights and hotels (prepare insurance and visas, when necessary).
13B60.refined_description:
**

Unit 13B60 operates as a specialized second-level operational unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, serving as the European Parliament's primary administrative and coordinating hub for structured study visit programs targeting non-national visitors from European Union member states. The unit manages approximately 270 annual study visits (encompassing both five-day in-person working visits and virtual engagement formats) to EU institutions in Brussels and/or Strasbourg, executing this responsibility through an interinstitutional coordination framework shared with the European Commission that ensures seamless program delivery across multiple institutional locations. Core operations are systematically organized across six integrated functional domains: personalized visitor program preparation leveraging approximately 40 institutional contacts per visitor to coordinate 10-15 meetings weekly supplemented by minimum 6 annual virtual sessions; strategic partnership management with EU delegations, Liaison Offices, and European Parliament Antennas outside EU territory to enhance candidate preparation, selection refinement, and ongoing program monitoring; comprehensive interinstitutional coordination and budgetary administration ensuring fiscal accountability and operational efficiency; targeted communication strategy development with digital platform maintenance (web and social media channels) and relationship cultivation with academic institutions and think tanks; systematic engagement with institutional services across the European Parliament, Commission, Council, Committee of the Regions, Economic and Social Committee, and European External Action Service; and logistical operations encompassing itinerary development, flight and hotel reservations, insurance documentation processing, and visa arrangement coordination. This integrated operational structure reinforces the interconnected nature of EU institutional operations while advancing democratic participation objectives within the broader parliamentary partnership framework.

**
13B60.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Visitor Program Customization and Meeting Coordination** (30%): Personalized preparation of visit programs based on individual visitor interests and institutional engagement requirements; organization of approximately 40 institutional contacts per visitor; coordination of 10-15 meetings weekly across Brussels and Strasbourg; facilitation of minimum 6 annual virtual visit sessions to ensure comprehensive accessibility across geographic and scheduling constraints

• **Strategic Partnership Management and International Network Coordination** (20%): Direct operational cooperation with EU delegations, Liaison Offices, and European Parliament Antennas located outside EU territory; enhancement of candidate preparation standards and selection processes; continuous monitoring of study visit program effectiveness and participant outcomes throughout the program lifecycle

• **Interinstitutional Program Administration and Financial Management** (20%): Management of the EP/Commission coordinating unit responsible for executing approximately 270 annual study visits from EU member states; comprehensive budgetary oversight and administrative coordination protocols; coordination across both Brussels and Strasbourg institutional locations; fiscal accountability and operational efficiency assurance

• **Communications Strategy, Digital Platform Management, and Stakeholder Relations** (10%): Development and execution of targeted communication initiatives; digital platform maintenance across web and social media channels (including Facebook); publication of informational materials; cultivation of strategic relationships with academic institutions and think tanks; systematic engagement with services across EP, Commission, Council, CoR, CES, and EEAS

• **Logistical Operations and Travel Facilitation** (5%): Detailed itinerary preparation; flight and hotel booking arrangements; insurance documentation processing and coordination; visa preparation procedures ensuring seamless visitor travel and accommodation management throughout institutional engagement

13B70.name: 13B70
original_activities: Enhance European Parliament relations with the Latin American Parliament (Parlatino), the regional and national Parliaments in the area, and with civil society across the region.
Prepare and follow-up on visits by Parliament's official bodies, in cooperation with the political Directorates-General of the House.

Strengthen the cooperation and outreach capacity of the European Parliament to relevant institutional and civil society interlocutors in Latin America and the Caribbean, through events on issues of common interest and other activities that promote the values of the European Union.
Monitor the regional political, security and economic developments in the area.
13B70.refined_description: Unit 13B70 is a specialized parliamentary relations and strategic engagement unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, functioning as a leaf-node operational unit responsible for advancing the European Parliament's institutional presence, influence, and diplomatic engagement across Latin America and the Caribbean. Operating in coordination with sibling units (13B10, 13B20, 13B30, 13B40, 13B50, and three additional units) within Direction 13B, Unit 13B70 executes a comprehensive four-stream regional engagement strategy that collectively represents 100% of documented workload and positions the unit as a critical interface for parliamentary diplomacy and institutional cooperation. The unit's integrated mission encompasses enhancing bilateral and multilateral relations with the Latin American Parliament (Parlatino), regional parliaments, and national parliaments throughout Latin America and the Caribbean while simultaneously cultivating partnerships with civil society organizations across the region; preparing, organizing, and coordinating comprehensive follow-up activities for official parliamentary visits and delegations in close cooperation with political Directorates-General to ensure strategic alignment with House priorities and institutional objectives; strengthening the Parliament's outreach and cooperative capacity through targeted events, seminars, and thematic discussions addressing issues of mutual institutional interest while advancing European Union principles and values to institutional and civil society stakeholder networks; and maintaining systematic monitoring and analysis of regional political, security, and economic developments to inform parliamentary strategy, policy positioning, and institutional decision-making processes. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 13B70 advances the European Parliament's global parliamentary network development while serving as an essential mechanism for sustained diplomatic engagement and institutional cooperation with the Latin American region.
13B70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Relations & Institutional Partnerships** (50%): Enhancement of bilateral and multilateral relations with the Latin American Parliament (Parlatino), regional parliaments, and national parliaments throughout Latin America and the Caribbean, coupled with systematic cultivation of civil society organization partnerships across the region through sustained diplomatic engagement and institutional cooperation mechanisms

• **Official Visit Coordination & Strategic Management** (30%): Preparation, organization, and comprehensive follow-up management of official parliamentary visits and delegations conducted by Parliament's bodies in direct cooperation with political Directorates-General to ensure strategic alignment, institutional continuity, and House priority achievement

• **Outreach, Engagement & Values Promotion** (10%): Development and implementation of targeted events, seminars, and collaborative activities addressing issues of common institutional interest while promoting European Union values, principles, and institutional visibility to relevant institutional and civil society audiences in Latin America and the Caribbean

• **Regional Intelligence & Strategic Monitoring** (10%): Systematic analysis and monitoring of political, security, and economic developments across Latin America and the Caribbean to provide institutional intelligence and inform parliamentary strategy, policy positioning, and institutional decision-making processes

13B80.name: 13B80
original_activities: Enhance the relations and the cooperation between the European Parliament and the Verkhovna Rada on the basis of the existing Memorandum of Understanding in particular with a view to the ongoing Ukraine's EU accession process.

Support the work of the European Parliament regarding the interaction with the Verkhovna Rada, Ukrainian authorities and civil society. Provide guidance regarding all political and practical aspects of missions to Ukraine.
Provide to the Members and the hierarchy timely information and analysis about important developments relevant to Ukrainian politics.
Cooperate with the other EPLOs, Antennas, services of the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships, to enhance the relevance of the European Parliament's parliamentary diplomacy.

Undertake outreach activities in Ukraine and in the European Union (mainly Brussels and Strasbourg) to enhance the understanding of the European Union and in particular the European Parliament on the one hand, and to raise awareness about the situation in Ukraine.
Contribute to the democracy support efforts of the European Parliament for Ukraine in close coordination with the lead Member for Ukraine at the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group.
Support the work of the European Union Visitors Programme (EUVP).
13B80.refined_description: Unit 13B80 operates as the European Parliament's specialized operational and liaison mechanism for parliamentary relations with Ukraine, positioned within Direction 13B of the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy. As a dedicated institutional interface between the European Parliament and the Verkhovna Rada, the unit serves as a critical bridge facilitating parliamentary dialogue, democratic consolidation support, and strategic partnership development during a period of significant geopolitical and institutional transformation. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses six interconnected functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, spanning bilateral institutional cooperation, operational support for parliamentary engagement, intelligence and analytical services, diplomatic coordination with parallel institutional networks, outreach and democracy support programming, and administrative support for the European Union Visitors Programme. Unit 13B80's activities are strategically calibrated to advance Ukraine's European Union accession process while simultaneously strengthening the coherence and effectiveness of the European Parliament's broader parliamentary diplomacy architecture. The unit functions as a leaf-node entity at hierarchy level 2, directly supporting Members and institutional leadership through timely information delivery, mission facilitation, and substantive coordination across multiple stakeholder constituencies including the Verkhovna Rada, Ukrainian governmental authorities, civil society organizations, and European Union institutional partners.
13B80.refined_activity_summary:
• **Bilateral Institutional Relations & Cooperation Framework** (25%): Enhancement of relations and institutional cooperation with the Verkhovna Rada through the existing Memorandum of Understanding, with particular strategic emphasis on supporting Ukraine's ongoing EU accession process, institutional alignment requirements, and sustained parliamentary partnership development.

• **Operational Support for Parliamentary Engagement & Mission Facilitation** (25%): Comprehensive operational and strategic support for European Parliament activities involving the Verkhovna Rada, Ukrainian governmental authorities, and civil society organizations; provision of substantive guidance on all political and practical aspects of parliamentary missions to Ukraine; coordination of Member participation and logistical arrangements.

• **Intelligence, Analysis & Information Services** (15%): Delivery of timely information and analytical assessments to Members and institutional hierarchy regarding significant developments in Ukrainian political affairs, institutional developments, and strategic context relevant to parliamentary relations.

• **Parliamentary Diplomacy Coordination & Network Integration** (10%): Cooperation and coordination with other European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs), Antennas, and services within the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships to strengthen strategic coherence and enhance the effectiveness of the European Parliament's parliamentary diplomacy initiatives.

• **Outreach, Awareness-Raising, Democracy Support & Institutional Programming** (25%): Implementation of targeted outreach and public diplomacy activities in both Ukraine and European Union venues (primarily Brussels and Strasbourg) designed to deepen European Parliament understanding of Ukraine's strategic context while enhancing Ukrainian stakeholder comprehension of EU institutions and processes; substantive contribution to the European Parliament's democracy support programming for Ukraine in coordinated partnership with the designated lead Member within the Democracy Support and Election Coordination Group; administrative and operational support to the European Union Visitors Programme (EUVP) to facilitate broader institutional engagement with Ukrainian and European audiences.

13B85.name: 13B85
original_activities: Strengthen relations between the European Parliament and the national parliaments of the Western Balkans, with particular emphasis on strengthening political and legislative dialogue, supporting parliamentary democracy and strengthening parliamentary capacities, as part of their efforts to join the European Union.
Raise awareness and improve the visibility of the European Parliament's official positions and activities, while monitoring local and regional developments and ensuring a regular flow of information.

Contribute to the organization, preparation and monitoring of visits by official bodies of Parliament and Members of Parliament as part of their institutional functions, while supporting exchanges at the political and administrative level.
Take the initiative in parliamentary matters of interest to the European Parliament within the European Union delegations in the Western Balkans.
Strengthen Parliament's engagement with civil society groups at local and regional level.

Contribute to the activities of the General Directorate, participate in internal meetings of the General Directorate and the European Parliament. Contribute to the internal reflections of the European Parliament on cooperation with the national parliaments of the Western Balkans.
Contribute to the EUVP program by facilitating the selection of participants who can work to strengthen links between the European Parliament and the national parliaments of the Western Balkans.
13B85.refined_description: Unit 13B85 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 13B of the Directorate General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy, serving as the European Parliament's dedicated institutional focal point for engagement with Western Balkan national parliaments pursuing European Union membership. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses strengthening bilateral parliamentary relations and supporting democratic institutional development across the region through integrated political, legislative, and capacity-building mechanisms. Unit 13B85 executes a multifaceted portfolio of activities spanning parliamentary dialogue and democracy support, communications and regional monitoring, official visits and delegations coordination, civil society engagement, and internal strategic coordination, with these activities collectively advancing both the accession aspirations of Western Balkans countries and the European Parliament's institutional interests in the region. The unit maintains proactive engagement postures across multiple channels: directly with Western Balkan national parliaments and their legislative processes; through European Union delegations stationed throughout the region; with local and regional civil society organizations; and within internal General Directorate coordination mechanisms. Through these interconnected activities, Unit 13B85 reinforces democratic governance structures, enhances institutional capacity, ensures informed and timely communication flows, and strengthens Parliament's institutional connections and visibility within the Western Balkans, while simultaneously supporting European Union-wide capacity development initiatives such as the European Union Visitors Programme.
13B85.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Relations, Democracy Support and Institutional Capacity Building** (30%): Strengthen bilateral relations between the European Parliament and Western Balkans national parliaments through enhanced political and legislative dialogue mechanisms, targeted democratic governance support, and parliamentary capacity development initiatives aligned with European Union accession processes.

• **Communications, Visibility, Information Management and Regional Monitoring** (20%): Raise awareness of and improve visibility of the European Parliament's official positions and activities; conduct continuous monitoring of local and regional political developments; ensure regular, structured information flows to stakeholders and maintain informed institutional positioning.

• **Official Visits, Exchanges, Delegations and Administrative Cooperation** (15%): Organize, prepare, and monitor official visits by European Parliament bodies and individual Members of Parliament; facilitate political-level and administrative-level exchanges; support institutional functions and cooperation frameworks.

• **Parliamentary Engagement within EU Delegations and Civil Society Outreach** (20%): Initiate and coordinate parliamentary matters of strategic institutional interest to the European Parliament through engagement with European Union delegations across the Western Balkans (10%); strengthen Parliament's direct institutional engagement with civil society organizations at local and regional levels (10%).

• **Strategic Coordination, Institutional Reflection and Capacity Development Programs** (15%): Contribute to General Directorate strategic coordination mechanisms and internal institutional reflections on Western Balkans cooperation frameworks (10%); support the European Union Visitors Programme through participant identification, selection, and facilitation to reinforce parliamentary linkages between the European Parliament and Western Balkans national legislatures (5%).

13C.name: 13C
original_activities: Provide the Director General, Directors and Heads of Units with advice, assistance and support in the areas of human resources, secondment to other institutions, finance, logistics, missions, professional training, internships.
Direct, coordinate and ensure the development of the Resources Department, including the development of new partnerships.

Represent the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy in various forums of Parliament (such as meetings of Resource Directors) and with specific directorates general (PERS, FINS, ITEC, EXPO, etc.) as well as with other institutions (EEAS).
Supervise and support innovation and development projects for parliamentary democracy.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer for the budget lines allocated to the General Directorate.
Contribute to strategic thinking on the evolution and transformation of administration and resource management.

Contribute to the annual work program, the activity reports of the general directorate, responses to control bodies and specific projects specific to the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy.
13C.refined_description: Unit 13C functions as the strategic Resources and Administration Directorate within the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy (DG 13), serving as a first-level hierarchical unit with direct operational accountability to senior institutional leadership. The unit operates as the essential administrative and strategic coordination nexus for the General Directorate, delivering comprehensive resource management, inter-institutional coordination, and organizational development across seven integrated activity streams. Unit 13C maintains direct supervisory relationships with two subordinate operational units—13C10 (Strategic Partnerships and Events Coordination) and 13C20 (Integrated Resources and Administration)—while operating alongside sibling units (13-10, 13A, 13B) within the DG 13 structure. The unit's organizational mission encompasses providing executive-level advisory services to senior leadership, directing Resources Department development and partnership cultivation, representing the General Directorate across parliamentary governance forums and inter-directorate coordination mechanisms, exercising subdelegated budgetary authorization functions, supervising innovation and parliamentary democracy advancement projects, and contributing to strategic administrative transformation and institutional control compliance. Through these integrated functions, Unit 13C positions itself as the critical administrative and strategic coordination mechanism ensuring operational alignment across the European Parliament's institutional ecosystem while maintaining direct engagement with external partners including the European External Action Service (EEAS).
13C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Advisory and Administrative Support** (30%): Delivery of specialized advice, assistance, and comprehensive operational support to the Director General, Directors, and Heads of Units across human resources administration, inter-institutional secondment facilitation, financial management and advisory services, logistics coordination, official mission organization, professional development programming, and internship administration.

• **Departmental Leadership and Strategic Development** (20%): Direction and coordination of Resources Department operational evolution, institutional capacity building, partnership development initiatives, and enhancement of external collaboration networks to strengthen departmental effectiveness and organizational resilience.

• **Inter-Institutional Liaison and Coordination** (20%): Representation of the General Directorate within Parliament's internal governance forums (particularly Resource Directors' meetings), formal engagement with peer directorates general (PERS, FINS, ITEC, EXPO), and external institutional partnerships (notably the European External Action Service/EEAS) to ensure strategic alignment across the European Parliament's operational ecosystem.

• **Budgetary Authority and Project Oversight** (20%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer functions for all General Directorate budget line allocations (10%) and provision of supervisory oversight and developmental support for innovation and parliamentary democracy advancement projects (10%).

• **Strategic Planning, Administrative Transformation, and Institutional Reporting** (10%): Contribution to administrative evolution and resource management transformation initiatives (5%), support for annual work program development, activity reporting, institutional control body engagement and compliance documentation, and execution of General Directorate-specific strategic projects and institutional initiatives (5%).

13C10.name: 13C10
original_activities: Reinforce the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships's role as a hub for promoting parliamentary democracy to partners inside and outside the European Union by organising democracy-related events and establishing partnerships with key democracy fora, organisations and networks.
Identify and launch innovative activities and parliamentary partnerships. In particular, explore opportunities to build inter-parliamentary alliances on key European Parliament priorities.

Contribute to parliamentary democracy outreach by exploring initiatives to engage with regional stakeholders, civil society and European Union citizens, inter alia via closer cooperation with the CoR and EESC.

Ensure the coordination at the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships level for high-profile cross-DG events like the Gender Equality Week and the European Youth Event, and contribute to broader communication initiatives such as the Europe Day and relevant international day campaigns.

Foster innovation, facilitate knowledge sharing, and contribute to enhancing horizontal cooperation and synergies amongst the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships's Directorates. Support DG PART's Directorates on administrative level through targeted tools, including legislative monitoring and a comprehensive calendar of key events.

Serve as a focal point within the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships for business continuity, IT-related initiatives, EMAS actions, document management and digital accessibility practices.
Contribute to the preparation of notes, meetings, and activities for the Bureau Working Group on Communication, Outreach and Research.
13C10.refined_description: Unit 13C10 operates as the Strategic Partnerships and Events Coordination Unit within Direction 13C (Resources and Administration) of the Directorate-General for Parliamentary Democracy Partnerships (DG PART), functioning at the second hierarchical level as a leaf node without subordinate units. The unit's dual mandate positions it as both an external-facing strategic engagement entity and an internal organizational catalyst, serving to strengthen DG PART's positioning as a central hub for advancing parliamentary democracy while simultaneously enhancing horizontal integration and operational excellence across the Directorate-General. Externally, the unit drives democracy promotion through organizing democracy-focused events, establishing strategic partnerships with key democracy forums and networks within and external to the European Union, and identifying innovative parliamentary activities and inter-parliamentary alliances aligned with European Parliament priorities. Concurrently, the unit advances parliamentary democracy outreach by developing engagement strategies with regional stakeholders, civil society organizations, and EU citizens through enhanced cooperation with the Committee of the Regions (CoR) and the European Economic and Social Committee (EESC). Internally, Unit 13C10 serves as a critical coordination mechanism by facilitating high-profile cross-Directorate-General events including Gender Equality Week and the European Youth Event, supporting institutional communication initiatives such as Europe Day and relevant international day campaigns, fostering innovation and knowledge-sharing mechanisms across DG PART's Directorates, and providing specialized administrative support including legislative monitoring tools and comprehensive event calendars. The unit additionally functions as the focal point for business continuity management, IT-related strategic initiatives, EMAS environmental compliance actions, document management systems, and digital accessibility practices within DG PART, while contributing preparatory support to the Bureau Working Group on Communication, Outreach and Research. This integrated operational structure enables Unit 13C10 to enhance DG PART's institutional visibility and effectiveness while maintaining close collaborative relationships with its sibling unit 13C20 within the organizational structure.
13C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **External Partnership Development and Democracy Promotion Hub Function** (45%): Reinforce DG PART's positioning as a central platform for parliamentary democracy advancement by organizing democracy-focused events and establishing strategic partnerships with key democracy forums, organizations, and networks both within and external to the European Union (25%); identify and launch innovative parliamentary activities and inter-parliamentary alliances that address core European Parliament priorities (20%)

• **Parliamentary Democracy Outreach and Stakeholder Engagement** (15%): Develop and implement parliamentary democracy outreach initiatives by engaging regional stakeholders, civil society organizations, and EU citizens through enhanced cooperation with the Committee of the Regions (CoR) and the European Economic and Social Committee (EESC)

• **Cross-Institutional Event Coordination and Communication Support** (15%): Coordinate high-profile cross-Directorate-General events including Gender Equality Week and European Youth Event at the DG PART level; support broader institutional communication initiatives including Europe Day and designated international day campaigns

• **Internal Innovation, Knowledge-Sharing, and Administrative Support** (10%): Foster innovation and facilitate horizontal knowledge-sharing mechanisms across DG PART's Directorates; provide comprehensive administrative support through legislative monitoring tools and detailed event calendars to enhance synergies and horizontal cooperation

• **Operational Infrastructure, Business Continuity, and Compliance Management** (10%): Serve as the focal point for business continuity initiatives, IT-related strategic actions, EMAS environmental compliance actions, document management systems, and digital accessibility practices within DG PART

• **Governance and Policy Advisory Support** (5%): Provide preparatory support including meeting preparation and activity coordination for the Bureau Working Group on Communication, Outreach and Research

13C20.name: 13C20
original_activities: Ensure the strategic and operational management of human resources within DG PART throughout the professional career of each staff member, regardless of their status, and create and maintain a positive working environment and a healthy culture. This includes selection, recruitment, integration, training and development, mobility, evaluation, as well as rigorous management of budgetary envelopes (contract agents, missions, interns).

Ensure the planning, preparation and execution of the annual budget of the Directorate General of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy by guaranteeing good financial management. Ensure compliance of transactions with the Financial Regulations and the integrity of financial actors in particular, during ex-ante verifications and monitoring of delegations granted to financial actors.

Provide support to the Director of Resources in her responsibilities related to the management of human and budgetary resources. Represent the Directorate-General for Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy in inter-DG steering groups concerning human resources, finance, equality and diversity and 'facilities', as well as within the public procurement forum.

Guarantee the effective management of general management assets as well as logistical functions by creating a pleasant physical working environment and offering well-organized, equipped and functional spaces.

Contribute with regard to the parts relating to resources and the internal control system to the drafting of activity reports and the administrative work program (AWP), and draft responses to requests from political authorities, the General Directorate of Finance, the Internal Auditor or the Court of Auditors.
13C20.refined_description: Unit 13C20 functions as the integrated Resources and Administration operational hub within the Resources and Administration Directorate (13C) of the General Directorate of Partnerships for Parliamentary Democracy (DG PART), serving as a critical second-level hierarchical entity responsible for comprehensive organizational support across human resources, financial management, and administrative operations. The unit maintains dual strategic and operational focus on human resources management (30% of workload), exercising comprehensive lifecycle management of all staff members regardless of employment status—encompassing selection, recruitment, integration, training and development, mobility management, performance evaluation, and rigorous stewardship of budgetary envelopes for contract agents, missions, and interns—while cultivating a positive working environment and healthy organizational culture. In parallel, the unit ensures meticulous planning, preparation, and execution of DG PART's annual budget (30% of workload), guaranteeing sound financial management practices, strict compliance with Financial Regulations, and the integrity of financial actors through ex-ante verifications and systematic monitoring of delegations granted to financial stakeholders. Operating as a direct support mechanism to the Director of Resources, Unit 13C20 provides essential administrative and strategic assistance in managing both human and budgetary resources while representing DG PART across inter-DG steering groups addressing human resources, finance, equality and diversity, facilities management, and public procurement coordination (20% of workload). The unit guarantees effective management of general administrative assets and logistical functions (10% of workload) by creating and maintaining functional, well-equipped, well-organized, and pleasant physical working environments. Finally, the unit contributes substantively to organizational accountability and governance (10% of workload) through drafting activity reports and administrative work programs with particular focus on resources and internal control frameworks, and by preparing responses to inquiries from political authorities, the General Directorate of Finance, the Internal Auditor, and the Court of Auditors.
13C20.refined_activity_summary:
