• **Human Resources Strategic Management** (30%): Comprehensive lifecycle management of all staff members across all employment statuses including selection, recruitment, integration, training and development, mobility management, and performance evaluation; rigorous management of budgetary envelopes for contract agents, missions, and interns; cultivation and maintenance of positive working environment and healthy organizational culture

• **Financial Planning and Budget Execution** (30%): Annual budget planning, preparation, and execution for DG PART; guarantee of sound financial management practices; strict compliance verification with Financial Regulations; ex-ante verification protocols and systematic monitoring of delegations granted to financial stakeholders; maintenance of financial actor integrity

• **Strategic Representation and Director Support** (20%): Direct operational support to the Director of Resources regarding human and budgetary resource management; representation of DG PART in inter-DG steering groups addressing human resources, finance, equality and diversity initiatives, facilities management, and public procurement coordination

• **Facilities and Logistical Asset Management** (10%): Effective management of general administrative assets and logistical functions; creation and maintenance of functional, well-equipped, well-organized, and pleasant physical working environments

• **Administrative Compliance and Governance Reporting** (10%): Drafting of activity reports and administrative work programs (AWP) with emphasis on resource-related and internal control system components; formulation of responses to inquiries from political authorities, General Directorate of Finance, Internal Auditor, and Court of Auditors

original_activities: Formulate the strategy of the general management for approval by the administrative and political authorities.
Represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament.
Communicate with senior management staff and requesting departments on policies and strategy.
Organize the activities of general management within the framework of the formulated strategy and manage projects.
Facilitate the continuous improvement of working methods and promote clear and understandable procedures.
Implement tools to enhance performance and quality of service and apply control procedures in the General Directorate of Personnel.
Implement Parliament's policy linked to the duty of care.

Exercise the powers of appointing authority, exercise the function of delegated authorizing officer and represent and/or ensure the representation of the general management in joint or non-joint committees, interinstitutional bodies, etc.
05.refined_description: Unit 05 functions as the apex strategic management entity within the European Parliament, operating at the Directorate General level and reporting directly to the Secretary General with primary responsibility for comprehensive personnel management strategy and institutional governance. The unit formulates general management strategy requiring approval by administrative and political authorities while simultaneously representing general management interests and positions across all European Parliament institutional bodies, maintaining continuous strategic dialogue with senior management personnel and departmental leadership regarding policy direction and organizational positioning. Unit 05 orchestrates the implementation of formulated strategy through active project management and coordination of general management activities within the established strategic framework, while championing organizational excellence by facilitating methodological improvement initiatives, establishing transparent procedural frameworks, and implementing performance enhancement tools, quality assurance mechanisms, and formal control procedures within the General Directorate of Personnel. The unit ensures institutional compliance with Parliament's duty of care policy framework and exercises critical governance functions including appointing authority powers, delegated authorizing officer responsibilities, and representation within joint committees and interinstitutional bodies. Unit 05 provides overarching strategic direction and governance coordination through direct supervisory responsibility over six subordinate units (05-0010, 05-10, 05A, 05B, 05C, and 05D), collectively encompassing 39 distinct activities that operationalize specific functional domains within the broader organizational mandate.
05.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Planning and Governance Authority** (25%): Formulation of general management strategy for approval by administrative and political authorities (20%); exercise of appointing authority powers, delegated authorizing officer functions, and representation in joint committees and interinstitutional bodies (5%)

• **Institutional Representation and Stakeholder Communication** (30%): Representation of general management across European Parliament institutional bodies (15%); strategic communication with senior management staff and requesting departments regarding policies and organizational direction (15%)

• **Operational Coordination and Project Management** (15%): Organization and coordination of general management activities within established strategic framework; management of institutional projects and initiatives (15%)

• **Organizational Excellence and Performance Enhancement** (20%): Facilitation of continuous improvement in working methods and establishment of clear, transparent procedural standards (10%); implementation of performance enhancement tools, quality assurance mechanisms, and formal control procedures within the General Directorate of Personnel (10%)

• **Institutional Policy Compliance** (10%): Implementation and oversight of Parliament's duty of care policy framework across the organization (10%)

original_activities: Ensure the weekly publication of the European Parliament's internal magazine, Newshound: preparation, programming, documentation, writing and verification of texts, research of illustrations, dispatch to the Parliament's population.

Manage the editorial animation and ongoing maintenance of the Intranet site of the General Directorate of Personnel, PERSnet, as well as its technical development, in tandem with the IT unit of the General Directorate of Personnel.
Check the communications sent by the General Directorate of Personnel, both linguistically and in terms of their presentation.
Manage translations: send via Gepro+, carry out translations internally and check the translations.
Provide webmaster and graphics editing duties: upload and publish weekly in publishing software used for both Newshound and PERSnet.
Maintain internal and external contacts (other institutions) necessary to carry out the above tasks.
Contribute to other tasks related to publication, communication, editorial animation and maintenance of the Intranet site of the General Directorate of Personnel.
05-0010.refined_description: Unit 05-0010 operates as a specialized Direction-level editorial and communications directorate within the European Parliament's General Directorate of Personnel (DG 05), functioning as the institutional hub for internal information dissemination, personnel-focused digital communications infrastructure, and editorial quality assurance. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses the complete management of the European Parliament's internal publication ecosystem, including strategic oversight of content creation, digital platform administration, multilingual communications services, and stakeholder relationship management essential to sustaining organizational information flows across the Parliament's personnel population and institutional partners. Operating under the strategic oversight of DG 05's leadership structure, Unit 05-0010 executes its mission through an integrated portfolio of interconnected editorial, technical, and quality assurance functions that collectively position the unit as a critical facilitator of personnel communication strategy and organizational transparency within the European Parliament's administrative framework. The unit coordinates extensively with the General Directorate of Personnel's IT division to ensure seamless technical development and platform functionality across both publication and intranet systems, while simultaneously managing external relationships with other European institutions and Parliament departments necessary for fulfilling its institutional mandate. Through the systematic allocation of operational capacity across distinct functional domains—each supported by dedicated technical expertise, established workflows, and quality control mechanisms—Unit 05-0010 ensures consistent delivery of high-standard communications products and services that sustain institutional credibility and personnel engagement.
05-0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Internal Publication Management** (50%): Weekly production and distribution of Newshound internal magazine, encompassing complete editorial lifecycle including content preparation, programming, documentation, text composition and verification, illustration research, and distribution to Parliament personnel and stakeholders

• **Digital Platform Administration and Technical Development** (20%): Editorial animation and ongoing technical maintenance of PERSnet intranet portal through collaborative coordination with General Directorate of Personnel IT unit; content management and platform functionality assurance; webmaster oversight and graphics editing services ensuring weekly content upload and publication across both Newshound and PERSnet platforms using institutional publishing software

• **Quality Assurance and Communications Review** (10%): Comprehensive linguistic and presentational verification of all General Directorate of Personnel communications to ensure institutional standards and organizational consistency

• **Translation and Multilingual Services** (8%): Management of complete translation workflow including Gepro+ system submission, internal translation execution, and quality verification of translated materials across institutional communications

• **Stakeholder Relations and Ancillary Support Services** (12%): Maintenance of strategic internal and external institutional relationships with other European institutions and Parliament departments essential for operational continuity (5%); contribution to ancillary publication, communication, and intranet maintenance initiatives supporting broader personnel administration objectives (2%); technical publishing support services (5%)

original_activities: Provide legal advise to the Directorate-General for Personnel and act as support with compliance in general and on specific projects. Coordinate with the Legal Service.

Contribute to the development and implementation of data protection policies and procedures within the Directorate-General for Personnel. Assist controllers with data protection notices and records as well as with data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) to identify and mitigate potential risks to personal data. Liaise with the Parliament's DPO service. Raise awareness of data protection with staff and inform about the correct procedure for handling data breaches.

Assist the Director-General in the Meeting of the Heads of Administrations and the CA Lux and ensure their follow-up, inter alia by requesting the publication of any conclusion adopted by the former. Represent the Parliament at the CPQS / Preparatory Committee for questions concerning the Staff Regulations and the COSTAT / Staff Regulations Committee.
Provide support to the Director-General in her role as Appointing Authority for assistance under Article 24 of the Staff Regulations.

Assist with the formal and informal discussions, negotiations, and consultations between the European Parliament's administration, staff representatives and trade unions inter alia preparing the Contact Committee meetings. Prepare the decisions on the composition of the joint and consultative committees and liaise with the Webmaster team to keep the relevant pages updated.

Handle requests for documents held by the Directorate-General for Personnel (Regulation 1049/2001, OLAF, EPPO and National Authorities) and coordinate with other services outside the Directorate-General on those requests.
05-10.refined_description: Unit 05-10 functions as the specialized legal and compliance directional entity within the Directorate-General for Personnel (Unit 05), serving as the critical institutional interface between personnel administration and governance frameworks while operating as a leaf node with no subordinate organizational structures. The unit reports to its parent Directorate-General and collaborates with sibling units (05-0010, 05A, 05B, 05C, and 05D) to ensure integrated personnel management across the institution. Unit 05-10's mission encompasses five interconnected operational domains that collectively concentrate specialized expertise essential to personnel administration governance: delivering comprehensive legal counsel and compliance support to the Directorate-General for Personnel and coordinating with the Parliament's Legal Service on institutional matters and compliance initiatives; developing, implementing, and maintaining data protection compliance infrastructure including Data Protection Impact Assessments, personal data handling procedures, data breach protocols, staff awareness-raising, and liaison with the Parliament's Data Protection Officer; representing the Parliament's personnel administration at senior intergovernmental forums through support for the Director-General's participation in the Meeting of the Heads of Administrations and CA Lux deliberations, as well as institutional representation at CPQS (Preparatory Committee for Staff Regulations questions) and COSTAT (Staff Regulations Committee) with publication coordination of adopted conclusions; providing administrative and procedural support to the Director-General in her statutory capacity as Appointing Authority under Article 24 of the Staff Regulations; and facilitating formal and informal social dialogue between institutional administration, staff representatives, and trade unions through Contact Committee preparation and joint/consultative committee composition management. Additionally, the unit manages document access requests from external stakeholders including OLAF, EPPO, and national authorities under Regulation 1049/2001 protocols, coordinating inter-service responses to ensure compliance with EU transparency and accountability requirements.
05-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legal Advisory and Compliance Support** (35%): Provision of comprehensive legal counsel to the Directorate-General for Personnel; general compliance support across institutional operations and projects; coordination with the Parliament's Legal Service on institutional matters and compliance initiatives

• **Data Protection and Information Governance** (25%): Development and implementation of data protection policies and procedures; execution and facilitation of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) for risk identification and mitigation; management of personal data handling procedures and protection notices; data breach protocol development and management; staff awareness-raising initiatives; liaison with the Parliament's Data Protection Officer service

• **Intergovernmental Relations and Staff Regulations Representation** (15%): Administrative and substantive support to the Director-General's participation in the Meeting of the Heads of Administrations and CA Lux deliberations; institutional representation at CPQS (Preparatory Committee for Staff Regulations questions) and COSTAT (Staff Regulations Committee); publication coordination of adopted conclusions; liaison on Staff Regulations matters

• **Social Dialogue and Labor Relations** (10%): Facilitation of formal and informal discussions, negotiations, and consultations between Parliament administration, staff representatives, and trade unions; preparation of Contact Committee meetings; management of joint and consultative committee composition decisions; coordination of website content maintenance with Webmaster services

• **Appointing Authority Support and Document Access Management** (10%): Administrative assistance to the Director-General in her statutory role as Appointing Authority under Article 24 of the Staff Regulations; management of document access requests from external stakeholders (Regulation 1049/2001, OLAF, EPPO, national authorities) with inter-service coordination on document access matters

05A.name: 05A
original_activities: Oversee and coordinate the directorate's units, and ensure staff management and the organisation of the directorate as a whole.
Oversee and coordinate human resources policies in the directorate's and its units' areas of responsibility.
Analyse, plan and assess the needs of Parliament's Secretariat in terms of human resources, talent selection and outreach, staff recruitment, training, career development, and ethics.
Manage learning and development for Members and staff.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-Directorate-General committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., and represent the directorate and, if necessary, the directorate-general within those fora.
Manage human resources development projects.
Exercise the powers of the appointing authority and of the authorising officer by sub-delegation.
05A.refined_description:
**

Unit 05A functions as the strategic Direction-level organizational entity within the Directorate General for Personnel and Administration, serving as Hierarchy Level 1 reporting to parent unit 05 and exercising comprehensive executive oversight and coordination responsibilities across the European Parliament's human resources management framework. As the principal administrative and policy-setting body for personnel matters institution-wide, Unit 05A operates as the primary management hub cascading strategic HR and administrative functions throughout the institutional framework via five subordinate units (05A10, 05A20, 05A40, 05A50, and 05A60), while functioning as a coordinating peer to sibling units 05B, 05C, 05D, and specialized units 05-0010 and 05-10 within the broader directorate structure. The unit dedicates 30% of its workload to overseeing and coordinating all directorate subordinate units while ensuring comprehensive staff management, integrated organizational coherence, and the directorate's function as a comprehensive operational whole. Unit 05A commits 20% of its capacity to overseeing and coordinating human resources policies applicable to both the directorate and its subordinate units' areas of responsibility, establishing consistent HR governance frameworks and ensuring policy compliance across organizational levels. Additionally, Unit 05A allocates 20% of its workload to conducting strategic analysis, planning, and needs assessment regarding Parliament's Secretariat human resources requirements, encompassing talent identification and recruitment strategies, professional development pathways, ethics frameworks, outreach initiatives, and strategic talent selection processes. The unit dedicates 10% of its resources to managing comprehensive learning and development programmes for both Members and staff, supporting institutional capacity building and professional advancement. Unit 05A allocates 10% of its workload to participating in intra- and inter-Directorate-General committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies, representing the directorate and directorate-general in these strategic forums. The remaining 10% addresses human resources development project management and the exercise of appointing authority and authorizing officer powers through sub-delegation mechanisms, ensuring operational implementation of personnel policies and decisions.

**
05A.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Directorate Oversight and Coordination** (30%): Oversee and coordinate all subordinate directorate units (05A10, 05A20, 05A40, 05A50, 05A60); ensure comprehensive staff management, integrated organizational coherence, and the directorate's function as a comprehensive operational whole; maintain coordination with peer units 05B, 05C, 05D and specialized units 05-0010 and 05-10

• **Human Resources Policy and Governance** (20%): Oversee and coordinate human resources policies across the directorate and all subordinate units' areas of responsibility; establish consistent institutional HR governance frameworks; ensure policy compliance and coherence across organizational levels

• **Strategic HR Planning and Talent Management** (20%): Analyze, plan, and assess Parliament's Secretariat human resources needs; develop and implement talent identification, recruitment strategies, outreach initiatives, and professional development pathways; establish ethics frameworks and strategic talent selection processes

• **Learning, Development, and Institutional Capacity Building** (10%): Manage comprehensive learning and development programmes serving both Members and staff; support institutional capacity-building and professional advancement initiatives

• **Interinstitutional Representation and Strategic Governance** (10%): Participate in intra- and inter-Directorate-General committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies; represent the directorate and directorate-general within these strategic forums

• **HR Project Management and Delegated Authority** (10%): Manage human resources development projects; exercise appointing authority and authorizing officer powers through sub-delegation mechanisms; ensure operational implementation of personnel policies and institutional decisions

05A10.name: 05A10
original_activities: Develop and produce human resources metrics and studies and make recommendations for planning those activities in order to achieve the institution's strategic objectives.

Coordinate the Directorate-General for Personnel liaison with the Resource Directorates on human resources strategy and planning issues, including promoting best practices in these areas for the whole institution.
Develop, coordinate and apply the European Parliament's staff mobility policy and monitor accompanying measures.
Provide career guidance through the Central Career Guidance Service (SCOP) and coordinate the Local Career Guidance Network (RELOP).
Manage job descriptions at the European Parliament.
Develop management tools for Heads of Unit, in particular through the Middle Management Action Programme (MM-AP).
Coordinate the Directorate-General for Personnel's participation in the selection procedures for Heads of Unit and provide procedural and administrative support to the Directorates-General.
05A10.refined_description: Unit 05A10 is a strategic human resources planning and development unit operating at the second hierarchical level within the Directorate-General for Personnel and Administration (DG 05) as a specialized operational component of the Direction-level parent unit 05A. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, 05A10 functions collaboratively with sibling units 05A20, 05A40, 05A50, and 05A60 to deliver comprehensive personnel and administration functions across the European Parliament. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses the comprehensive development, coordination, and implementation of human resources strategy, mobility policies, career management frameworks, and workforce planning systems that directly support the European Parliament's strategic objectives and organizational management excellence. Unit 05A10 exercises critical cross-institutional coordination responsibilities by liaising between the Directorate-General for Personnel and Resource Directorates on human resources strategy and planning matters while actively promoting evidence-based best practices throughout the Parliament's organizational structure. Through its integrated portfolio of analytical, developmental, and operational activities—encompassing strategic HR metrics and workforce planning analysis, staff mobility policy frameworks, career guidance services, job description management, management development programming, and senior management selection procedures—Unit 05A10 functions as a strategic enabler of human resource policy implementation and institutional organizational management excellence.
05A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Strategy, Metrics, and Workforce Planning Analysis** (25%): Development and production of advanced human resources metrics, analytical studies, and strategic recommendations to inform workforce planning decisions and support the European Parliament's achievement of institutional strategic objectives

• **Inter-Directorate HR Strategy Coordination and Best Practice Promotion** (15%): Coordination of liaison activities between the Directorate-General for Personnel and Resource Directorates on human resources strategy and planning; active promotion and dissemination of evidence-based best practice methodologies across the institution

• **Staff Mobility Policy Development and Implementation** (15%): Development, coordination, and implementation of the European Parliament's comprehensive staff mobility policy framework; monitoring of associated supportive measures and institutional mechanisms that facilitate employee movement and career progression

• **Career Development and Guidance Services** (15%): Provision of comprehensive career guidance through the Central Career Guidance Service (SCOP); coordination of the Local Career Guidance Network (RELOP) to ensure accessible career guidance accessibility across all parliamentary organizational structures

• **Job Description Framework Management** (10%): Development, maintenance, coordination, and application of the institution's job description frameworks across the European Parliament

• **Management Development Programming and Leadership Tools** (10%): Development and delivery of structured management development tools and training programmes for Heads of Unit through the Middle Management Action Programme (MM-AP) to enhance leadership effectiveness and management capacity

• **Senior Management Selection Procedures and Administrative Support** (10%): Coordination of the Directorate-General for Personnel's participation in selection procedures for senior management positions (Heads of Unit); provision of comprehensive procedural and administrative support to all Directorates-General throughout the selection process

05A20.name: 05A20
original_activities: Plan and organise Parliament's own competitions and selection procedures (for officials/temporary agents/contract agents); manage all stages of those competitions and selection procedures, including the drawing up of lists of suitable candidates for Parliament; liaise with EPSO regarding competitions that only concern Parliament.

Coordinate with EPSO and the other institutions regarding EPSO competition planning and design; provide EPSO with members of selection boards, markers and subject matter experts for competitions in which Parliament participates.

Develop the European Parliament career outreach policy and undertake outreach activities to promote Parliament's Secretariat as an employer of choice and to attract qualified candidates in order to meet the institution's needs.
Manage Parliament's reserve lists and its quotas on EPSO lists; assist and advise the Directorates-General recruiting from those lists.

Manage the certification procedure, the Positive Action Programme, the tests for the Schuman Trainee Recruitment and Development Programme and other specific procedures including Calls for expressions of interest.

Manage the budget for budget line 1610: reimbursement of travel expenses (medical appointments, interviews, written and oral tests) and management of costs and procurement procedures related to other unit activities, particularly outreach activities.
05A20.refined_description:
Unit 05A20 operates as a Level 2 specialized operational unit within Direction 05A of the Directorate General for Personnel and Administration, serving as the European Parliament's primary organizational entity responsible for recruitment governance, talent acquisition, and selection administration across all staff categories. The unit exercises comprehensive authority over Parliament's independent recruitment framework while maintaining coordinated engagement with the European Personnel Selection Office (EPSO) and other EU institutions, thereby operationalizing Parliament's human resources strategy while preserving institutional autonomy in recruitment governance. Unit 05A20's mandate encompasses the complete lifecycle of Parliament-specific recruitment processes—from strategic design through candidate list development—encompassing competitions and selection procedures for officials, temporary agents, and contract agents, complemented by inter-institutional coordination on multi-institutional competition architecture and the provision of specialized personnel including selection board members, assessment markers, and subject matter experts. The unit simultaneously develops and implements strategic career outreach initiatives to establish Parliament's Secretariat as an employer of choice, manages Parliament's reserve candidate lists and EPSO quota allocations with advisory support to recruiting Directorates-General, administers specialized recruitment procedures including certification protocols, the Positive Action Programme, the Schuman Trainee Recruitment and Development Programme, and alternative mechanisms such as calls for expressions of interest, and oversees budget line 1610 encompassing candidate travel expense reimbursement and procurement management. Through these integrated activities, Unit 05A20 positions itself as a critical enabler of Parliament's human resources strategy within the broader personnel administration framework.

##
05A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Recruitment Administration and Competition Management** (70%): Design, plan, organize, and execute Parliament's independent competitions and selection procedures for officials, temporary agents, and contract agents; manage all procedural stages from architecture through candidate list compilation; develop merit-based candidate lists; maintain direct liaison with EPSO regarding Parliament-exclusive recruitment initiatives and Parliament-specific competition planning.

• **Inter-Institutional Coordination and Expert Provision** (8%): Coordinate with EPSO and other EU institutions on multi-institutional competition planning and design architecture; provide specialized personnel including selection board members, assessment markers, and subject matter experts for competitions involving Parliament participation; support competition design across the EU institutional framework.

• **Talent Acquisition Strategy and Employer Branding** (7%): Develop and execute Parliament's strategic career outreach policy; conduct targeted outreach activities to position Parliament's Secretariat as an employer of choice; implement candidate attraction initiatives designed to establish qualified candidate pipelines aligned with institutional staffing requirements.

• **Reserve List and Quota Management** (7%): Manage Parliament's reserve candidate lists and EPSO list quota allocations; provide advisory support and recruitment guidance to Directorates-General conducting hiring decisions from these resources; administer quota allocation procedures.

• **Specialized Recruitment Procedures and Programs** (5%): Administer certification procedures, Positive Action Programme implementation, Schuman Trainee Recruitment and Development Programme assessments and administration, and alternative recruitment mechanisms including calls for expressions of interest; manage targeted recruitment pathways.

• **Budget and Procurement Administration** (3%): Manage budget line 1610 encompassing candidate travel expense reimbursement for medical appointments, interviews, and written/oral assessment sessions; oversee procurement procedures and cost management associated with outreach activities and unit operations.

05A40.name: 05A40
original_activities: Manage the stages in the career and administrative life of European Parliament staff, in particular the various procedures relating to the career development of officials and other staff (transfers, changes of administrative position, terminations of service, extensions of service, leaves on personal grounds and reinstatements).
Manage the procedures relating to staff assessment, the award of merit points, the ability to work in a third language, and promotions.

Manage ethics-related procedures, in particular requests concerning the acceptance of gifts and honours, the exercise of paid or unpaid outside activities, the activity of a spouse, standing for election, and the publication of texts.
Inform staff about administrative procedures and respond to client queries.
Update and create internal procedures, directives and regulations relating to careers, ethics and the management of the unit's activities.
05A40.refined_description: Unit 05A40 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 05A of the Directorate General for Personnel and Administration, functioning as the European Parliament's primary administrative hub for comprehensive staff career management and institutional ethics compliance. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate organizational structures, Unit 05A40 coordinates strategically with sibling units 05A10, 05A20, 05A50, and 05A60 under the direction of parent Unit 05A to support the Parliament's integrated human resources and administrative governance objectives. The unit exercises coordinated responsibility across five integrated functional areas that collectively constitute 100% of its documented workload, spanning the complete lifecycle of personnel administration from initial appointment through service termination and reinstatement, while simultaneously administering performance evaluation systems, ethics governance frameworks, stakeholder communication services, and regulatory development activities. Unit 05A40's integrated approach ensures institutional compliance with established standards while supporting staff development and organizational integrity through its dual focus on career progression management and ethical conduct oversight. The unit's operational mandate reflects the Parliament's commitment to transparent, equitable personnel administration coupled with rigorous institutional ethics governance across all staff categories and employment modalities.
05A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Career Lifecycle and Administrative Management** (25%): Comprehensive administration of all career and administrative life stages for European Parliament staff, including official appointments, lateral transfers, administrative position modifications, service terminations, contract extensions, personal leave arrangements, and reinstatement procedures

• **Performance Evaluation and Professional Advancement** (25%): Management of staff assessment protocols, merit point attribution, multilingual competency verification (third-language proficiency), and promotion procedures

• **Ethics Compliance and Institutional Governance** (25%): Administration of institutional ethics procedures including gift and honours acceptance requests, authorization of paid and unpaid external activities, spousal employment oversight, electoral candidacy notifications, and publication authorization

• **Client-Facing Administrative Services and Information Provision** (15%): Provision of staff information services regarding administrative procedures and comprehensive response to client inquiries concerning personnel matters across all functional areas

• **Regulatory Framework Development and Procedure Management** (10%): Development, updating, and creation of internal procedures, directives, and regulations governing career management, ethics administration, and unit operational activities

05A50.name: 05A50
original_activities: Organise, select and run mandatory and non mandatory professional training activities (catalogue and tailor-made courses, team buildings, away days) for Members of the European Parliament, staff of the Secretariat-General and Accredited parliamentary assistants in languages, IT and professional development fields, through the medium of in presence, virtual, hybrid or e-learning courses; manage logistical aspects and checking the quality of training.

Update the training offer according to the institution's current and future needs (upskilling and reskilling), assist and advise the Directorates-General in analysing their staff training needs and filling in their Annual Learning Plans.
Create and run learning events at the Jean Monnet Academy in Houjarray for initial training, lifelong learning and team events.

Manage the unit's budget in close cooperation with the Financial Resources Unit of the Directorate-General for Personnel; organise and monitor the public procurement required for setting up training contracts.
Cooperate with the other institutions and contribute to the development of the European School of Administration's training programmes.
05A50.refined_description: Unit 05A50, a specialized operational unit positioned within Direction 05A of the Directorate General for Personnel and Administration, functions as the European Parliament's institutional hub for comprehensive professional development and learning governance. The unit strategically designs, procures, delivers, and ensures quality assurance of mandatory and non-mandatory professional training programmes tailored to three distinct stakeholder populations: Members of the European Parliament, Secretariat-General staff, and Accredited parliamentary assistants. Operating across multiple organizational functions simultaneously, Unit 05A50 manages a diversified portfolio of training activities spanning catalogue-based courses, bespoke customized programmes, and team development events delivered through in-person, virtual, hybrid, and e-learning modalities, with specialized subject matter coverage encompassing languages, information technology competencies, and professional development. The unit extends its institutional influence through strategic learning needs analysis and curriculum development activities that proactively align training offerings with current and emerging organizational requirements, whilst providing analytical and advisory services to peer Directorates-General in conducting staff training needs assessments and developing Annual Learning Plans. Unit 05A50 operates the Jean Monnet Academy in Houjarray, a dedicated institutional learning facility supporting initial professional training, continuous lifelong learning initiatives, and team development activities. The unit maintains comprehensive financial stewardship through close collaboration with the Financial Resources Unit of the Directorate-General for Personnel, encompassing budget management and oversight of public procurement processes essential to establishing and maintaining training service contracts. Additionally, Unit 05A50 contributes substantively to inter-institutional collaboration by cooperating with counterpart European institutions and actively developing training programmes through the European School of Administration, thereby positioning itself as a critical strategic partner in the Parliament's human capital development ecosystem.
05A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Training Programme Delivery, Design, and Quality Assurance** (60%): Organization, selection, and execution of mandatory and non-mandatory professional training activities encompassing catalogue-based courses and bespoke customized programmes; delivery through multiple modalities (in-person, virtual, hybrid, e-learning); subject matter coverage including languages, information technology, and professional development; comprehensive logistical management and quality verification protocols for Members of the European Parliament, Secretariat-General staff, and Accredited parliamentary assistants.

• **Strategic Learning Needs Analysis, Curriculum Development, and Institutional Planning Support** (20%): Continuous assessment and dynamic updating of training offerings aligned with institution's current and future skill requirements; proactive upskilling and reskilling programme development; provision of analytical and advisory services to all Directorates-General regarding staff training needs identification and assessment; support for Annual Learning Plan development and implementation across organizational units.

• **Institutional Learning Facilities and Specialized Events Management** (10%): Operation and administration of the Jean Monnet Academy in Houjarray as a dedicated venue; creation and delivery of learning events encompassing initial professional training, continuous lifelong learning programmes, and institutional team development activities within the dedicated learning environment.

• **Financial Management and Public Procurement Oversight** (5%): Unit budget management conducted in strategic coordination with the Financial Resources Unit of the Directorate General for Personnel; organization and monitoring of public procurement processes required for training service contract establishment, maintenance, and compliance.

• **Inter-Institutional Collaboration and Cooperative Programme Development** (5%): Cooperative engagement with counterpart European institutions and peer organizations; substantive contribution to the development and enhancement of training programmes delivered through the European School of Administration, extending institutional influence beyond Parliament's organizational boundaries.

05A60.name: 05A60
original_activities: Recruit and onboard officials, probationary officials, temporary agents and contract agents for all entities of the Secretariat-General of the European Parliament and the political groups by applying the statutory and regulatory provisions and following the guidance on recruitment in force, in close cooperation with the client services.
Manage contracts for interim staff and decisions for seconded national experts.
Develop tools, including IT solutions, for the unit's operations.
Monitor current temporary and contract agent contracts and arrange any extensions/renewals as requested by client services.

Ensure the administrative management of the careers of contract agents (ends of contracts, extensions of probationary periods, authorisations to work beyond retirement age, careers in the European institutions, certificates for former session assistants).
Publish vacant posts internally and on the interinstitutional portal; receive applications and check their eligibility; send eligible applications to the Directorates-General concerned.
Contribute to strategic considerations in the unit's field of work.
Manage the budget lines for contract agents, interim staff and seconded national experts.
05A60.refined_description: Unit 05A60 is a core operational unit within Direction 05A of the Directorate General for Personnel and Administration, serving as a critical administrative and operational hub for comprehensive human resources recruitment, contract management, and personnel administration across all entities of the European Parliament's Secretariat-General and political groups. The unit's dominant mission encompasses the complete recruitment lifecycle and onboarding of officials, probationary officials, temporary agents, and contract agents (60% of workload), executed through rigorous application of statutory and regulatory provisions and current recruitment guidance in close operational cooperation with client services throughout the institution. Complementing its core recruitment function, Unit 05A60 manages the contractual frameworks for interim staff and seconded national expert arrangements (12%), monitors and administers contract renewals and extensions for temporary and contract agents (5%), and ensures comprehensive administrative career management for contract agents including contract terminations, probationary period extensions, post-retirement work authorizations, and inter-institutional mobility certifications (5%). The unit fulfills critical operational enablement activities by publishing vacant positions on internal and interinstitutional portals, receiving applications, verifying eligibility, and distributing qualified candidates to relevant Directorates-General (4%), managing dedicated budget lines for contract agents, interim staff, and seconded national experts (4%), and developing specialized operational tools and IT solutions to enhance unit efficiency and institutional capability (6%). Additionally, Unit 05A60 contributes to strategic workforce planning and policy development within its functional domain (4%), thereby ensuring the European Parliament maintains a compliant, efficient, and strategically aligned human resources management framework supporting the institution's staffing infrastructure and operational requirements.
05A60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Recruitment and Onboarding Operations** (60%): Recruit and onboard officials, probationary officials, temporary agents, and contract agents for all Secretariat-General entities and political groups through rigorous application of statutory and regulatory provisions and current recruitment guidance in close operational cooperation with client services

• **Contract and Personnel Administration Management** (22%): Manage interim staff contracts and seconded national expert arrangements (12%); monitor current temporary and contract agent assignments and coordinate extensions/renewals per client service requests (5%); administer the full career lifecycle for contract agents including contract terminations, probationary period extensions, post-retirement work authorizations, inter-institutional mobility certifications, and session assistant documentation (5%)

• **Vacancy Management and Application Processing** (4%): Publish vacant positions internally and on interinstitutional portals; receive and verify application eligibility; transmit eligible applications to relevant Directorates-General

• **Financial and Operational Resource Management** (10%): Manage dedicated budget lines for contract agents, interim staff, and seconded national experts (4%); develop and maintain specialized operational tools and IT solutions supporting unit functions and institutional capability (6%)

• **Strategic Planning and Institutional Development** (4%): Contribute to strategic workforce planning and policy considerations within the unit's human resources recruitment and contract management domain

05B.name: 05B
original_activities: Oversee, coordinate and motivate the directorate's units, and ensuring staff management and organisation across the directorate as a whole.

Oversee and coordinate human resources policies in relation to individual entitlements, remuneration, front office services for staff and Accredited parliamentary assistants, missions, pensions and social insurance, trainee recruitment, working time and crèches.
Manage human resource administration projects.
Provide effective administrative management, tailored to the individual needs of staff, while respecting the rules in force and budget availability.
Exercise the powers of the appointing authority and the authorising officer by sub-delegation.

Participate in meetings of intra- and inter-DG committees and working groups, interinstitutional bodies, etc., representing the directorate and, where applicable, the directorate-general within those fora.
05B.refined_description: Unit 05B functions as the Direction-level strategic and operational management entity within the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), reporting directly to the Secretary General and exercising comprehensive oversight, coordination, and motivational leadership across ten subordinate specialized units (05B10, 05B20, 05B30, 05B40, 05B50, 05B60, and four additional units) while maintaining alignment with sibling directional units (05-0010, 05-10, 05A, 05C, 05D). The unit serves as the strategic nexus ensuring staff management cohesion and organizational alignment throughout the entire directorate structure through its primary supervisory and coordination functions. Unit 05B administers and coordinates a comprehensive portfolio of human resources policies encompassing individual staff entitlements, remuneration systems, front office services for both permanent staff and Accredited parliamentary assistants, official missions management, pension administration, social insurance arrangements, trainee recruitment protocols, working time regulations, and childcare facility operations. The unit actively manages strategic human resource administration projects designed to enhance organizational capability, operational efficiency, and policy implementation across the directorate while providing individualized administrative management services tailored to staff needs. Unit 05B exercises delegated authority as both the appointing authority and authorizing officer through established sub-delegation mechanisms, and participates in intra- and inter-directorate general committees, working groups, and interinstitutional collaborative bodies, representing directorate and directorate-general interests in these forums. Through these integrated activities, Unit 05B maintains strict compliance with institutional regulations and budgetary constraints while coordinating the work of all subordinate units and ensuring effective organizational performance within the broader European Parliament personnel management framework.
05B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Directorate Strategic Leadership, Oversight & Organizational Coordination** (50%): Exercise supervisory authority and provide strategic direction to ten subordinate specialized units; oversee, coordinate, and motivate all directorate units; ensure comprehensive staff management and organizational cohesion across the entire directorate structure; manage cross-directorate coordination mechanisms

• **Human Resources Policy Administration & Coordination** (20%): Oversee and coordinate comprehensive HR policies encompassing individual staff entitlements, remuneration systems, front office services for permanent staff and Accredited parliamentary assistants, official missions management, pension administration, social insurance provisions, trainee recruitment protocols, working time regulations, and crèche facility operations

• **Strategic HR Administration Project Management** (15%): Manage human resource administration projects designed to enhance directorate operational capability, efficiency, and policy implementation across the personnel management framework

• **Individualized Administrative Services & Compliance Management** (10%): Provide effective, tailored administrative management responsive to individual staff needs while ensuring strict compliance with institutional regulations and budgetary constraints

• **Delegated Authority & Institutional Representation** (5%): Exercise appointing authority and authorizing officer powers through established sub-delegation mechanisms (3%); participate in intra- and inter-DG committees, working groups, and interinstitutional bodies representing directorate and directorate-general interests (2%)

05B10.name: 05B10
original_activities: Determine and manage the individual entitlements of officials and other staff when they enter the service, are transferred to a new place of work or following a change in their family situation.
Determine and manage the individual rights of officials and other staff when they leave the service or change their place of work.
Verify decisions and data entered into the HRM portal.
Determine and manage the individual entitlements of pensioners.
Determine and manage the rights provided for in Annex X to the Staff Regulations for staff serving in a third country.
Manage the rights of officials on secondment.
Manage the reimbursement of transport costs for children attending Type I European Schools; manage contributions to the education costs of children attending Type II European Schools.
Prepare and encode decisions concerning duty, shift, overtime and travel allowances.
05B10.refined_description:
Unit 05B10 operates as a specialized operational leaf-node unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), functioning as the primary administrative hub for the comprehensive determination and management of individual staff entitlements, statutory rights, and benefits throughout the complete employment lifecycle within the European Parliament—from initial service entry through post-employment pensioner administration. The unit's core operational mandate encompasses the determination and administration of personnel entitlements during critical employment transitions, including service entry, workplace transfers, and family status changes (60% of workload), alongside the management of exit procedures and relocation rights when staff members depart the organization or change duty stations (10% of workload). Unit 05B10 exercises essential quality assurance functions by verifying administrative decisions and validating data entries within the Human Resources Management portal system (10% of workload), thereby ensuring the institutional integrity and accuracy of personnel records across the institution. The unit extends its specialized mandate to encompass the administration of entitlements for retired officials (4% of workload), the determination and management of rights for staff deployed to third-country locations as defined under Annex X of the Staff Regulations (4% of workload), and the oversight of rights and benefits for officials on secondment arrangements (4% of workload). Additionally, Unit 05B10 administers family support provisions through the management of reimbursement mechanisms for children attending Type I European Schools (including transport cost reimbursements) and the coordination of financial contributions toward education costs for children in Type II European Schools (4% of workload), while simultaneously preparing and encoding administrative decisions concerning duty allowances, shift premiums, overtime compensation, and travel expense reimbursements (4% of workload). As a leaf-node operational unit without subordinate entities, Unit 05B10 collaborates with sibling units (05B20, 05B30, 05B40, 05B50, 05B60, and additional units) under the strategic direction of Direction 05B to deliver comprehensive personnel administration services that directly support the institution's human resources governance framework and regulatory compliance obligations.

##
05B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Entitlements Management** (60%): Determination and administration of individual staff entitlements upon service entry, workplace transfers, and changes in family circumstances—representing the unit's dominant operational focus and most resource-intensive function affecting benefit allocations.

• **Personnel Transition and Exit Administration** (10%): Management and determination of individual rights during staff departures and workplace relocations, including the processing, documentation, and administration of all separation and reassignment procedures.

• **Data Quality Assurance and Portal Verification** (10%): Verification of administrative decisions and validation of data entries within the HRM portal system to maintain institutional record integrity, accuracy, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

• **Specialized Personnel Categories and Rights Administration** (12%): Determination and management of entitlements for pensioners/retired officials (4%), administration of rights for staff serving in third-country locations under Annex X provisions of the Staff Regulations (4%), and oversight and management of rights and benefits for officials on secondment arrangements (4%).

• **Family Support and Statutory Allowance Administration** (8%): Management of educational support mechanisms through reimbursement of transport costs for Type I European Schools and coordination of financial contributions toward Type II European School education costs (4%), combined with the preparation and encoding of administrative decisions concerning duty allowances, shift premiums, overtime compensation, and travel expense reimbursements (4%).

05B20.name: 05B20
original_activities: Calculate and codify the remuneration and allowances of officials, temporary staff, contract staff and parliamentary assistants.
Check the data entered into the NAP application, which calculates staff members' pay on a monthly basis and automatically updates their individual entitlements.
Issue salary statements; carry out attachments or assignments of staff members' earnings.
Manage the transfer abroad of part of a staff member's salary; determine and pay annual travel, entry into service, internal transfer, interinstitutional transfer and termination of service expenses.
Manage social and financial assistance in line with Article 76 of the Staff Regulations on the granting of gifts, loans and advances to staff who are in a particularly difficult situation.
Check that payslips are correct.
Interpret appointing authority decisions that have an impact on salaries.
Manage advances on, and deductions from, salaries.
05B20.refined_description: Unit 05B20 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05) that functions as the European Parliament's primary remuneration and payroll administration center, serving as a critical leaf-node operational entity within the broader personnel management structure. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility for the calculation, codification, and management of compensation structures for all categories of parliamentary personnel—including officials, temporary staff, contract staff, and parliamentary assistants—while maintaining direct oversight of the NAP (payroll calculation) application system to ensure data entry accuracy and the integrity of monthly salary calculations and automatic entitlement updates for all staff members. Unit 05B20 executes a broad spectrum of salary administration and financial transaction functions, encompassing the issuance of salary statements, processing of earnings attachments and assignments, management of international salary transfers, and the determination and payment of all categories of staff-related expenses including annual travel allowances, entry-into-service costs, internal transfer expenses, interinstitutional transfer costs, and termination-of-service payments. The unit administers social and financial assistance programs under Article 76 of the Staff Regulations, managing discretionary grants, loans, and advances for staff experiencing exceptional financial hardship, while simultaneously performing specialized quality assurance and regulatory interpretation functions including payslip verification, interpretation of appointing authority decisions with salary implications, and management of salary advances and deductions. Operating in coordination with sibling units 05B10, 05B30, 05B40, 05B50, 05B60, and four additional units under Direction 05B, Unit 05B20 delivers comprehensive human resources and compensation services that directly support the Personnel Directorate General's strategic objectives regarding staff administration and institutional financial management.
05B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Remuneration Calculation and Codification** (52%): Calculate and codify the remuneration and allowances of officials, temporary staff, contract staff, and parliamentary assistants—representing the unit's primary operational focus and core institutional responsibility.

• **Payroll System Management and Data Verification** (15%): Perform data validation and verification activities within the NAP application system to ensure accurate monthly salary calculations and automatic updates of individual staff entitlements, maintaining system integrity and payment accuracy across all personnel categories.

• **Salary Administration and Financial Transactions** (20%): Issue salary statements; execute attachments or assignments of staff earnings; manage international salary transfers; and determine and pay all categories of staff-related expenses including annual travel allowances, entry-into-service costs, internal transfer expenses, interinstitutional transfer costs, and termination-of-service payments.

• **Staff Financial Assistance and Support** (10%): Manage social and financial assistance programs in accordance with Article 76 of the Staff Regulations, administering discretionary gifts, loans, and advances to staff members experiencing particularly difficult financial situations.

• **Quality Assurance, Verification, and Regulatory Interpretation** (3%): Verify payslip accuracy; interpret appointing authority decisions with salary implications; and manage salary advances and deductions to ensure regulatory compliance and proper implementation of personnel decisions.

05B30.name: 05B30
original_activities: Create and approve certain mission orders. Verify the mission expense declarations. Settle and pay the missions - implement ex-ante controls, manage the controls. Ensure the follow-up (invoicing, revision of the invoices, etc), the coordination with the Travel agency and archiving. Quality and risk management. As the guardian of the legal framework, ensure the revision of the European Parliament Mission rules (GIP and IR).

Participate, as business owner, in the developments of the new IT application eMiss - manage the eMiss project in cooperation with the Steering Committee and the Working Groups. Manage and ensure the maintenance of IT applications MISS et MISDECFR - provide support, Helpdesk to the users.

Manage the Imprest account, ensure the follow-up of the budget items and sub-items - manage the appropriations, commitments, payments, recoveries, transfers. Prepare the necessary reports, ensure the follow-up of the FMS application. Manage the debts and litigation procedures, participate in the work of the Debt Committee.

Create and approve the mission orders of Accredited parliamentary assistants and Members' Trainees. Answer the requests for information concerning the European Parliament Mission rules and procedures, the creation and approval of mission orders, the submission of expense declarations and the calculation and payment of settlements, fill in obligatory administrative forms. Provide training courses and information sessions, establish reports and statistics, etc.

Provide assistance and information to the Directorates-Generals, Services of Parliament as well as to the staff members concerning the EP Mission rules and procedures, the creation and approval of mission orders, the submission of expense declarations and the calculation and payment of settlements. Provide training courses and information sessions, establish reports and statistics, etc.

Manage the Unit's projects and changes, ensure innovation and continuous improvement in the functioning of the Unit as well as in the work organisation and methods. Plan, manage and ensure the follow-up of the Unit's activities, prepare reports, statistics as appropriate, etc.
Manage and coordinate the travel-related files and questions with the Travel agency - such as invoicing, reinvoicing, quality control, regular update of the Travel policy.
Manage the various service contracts linked to missions, ensure the follow-up of the files and of the individual cases - Travel insurance and Professional credit cards.
05B30.refined_description: Unit 05B30, operating as a specialized operational unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), functions as the European Parliament's central administrative, financial, and compliance hub for comprehensive mission management and travel expense governance across the entire institutional framework. The unit exercises complete responsibility over the mission lifecycle, encompassing creation, approval, and settlement of mission orders; rigorous implementation of ex-ante financial controls; verification of expense declarations; payment processing; coordination with external travel agencies; archival management; and systematic custodianship of the institutional legal framework governing missions (General Internal Provisions and Internal Rules). Unit 05B30 manages critical information technology infrastructure through its role as business owner for eMiss application development—coordinating with steering committees and working groups—while simultaneously maintaining operational support, helpdesk services, and technical assistance for legacy systems MISS and MISDECFR. The unit provides specialized administrative support services to accredited parliamentary assistants and members' trainees, delivering mission order creation and approval, regulatory guidance, administrative form completion, comprehensive training programs, and statistical reporting on mission procedures and settlement calculations. Unit 05B30 extends institutional advisory capacity across all Directorates-General and Parliament services, furnishing comprehensive assistance regarding mission rules, procedures, financial settlements, and continuous training delivery supplemented by systematic statistical analysis. The unit actively manages financial stewardship through imprest account administration, budget appropriation tracking, commitment and payment management, recovery procedures, FMS application oversight, and participation in debt committee proceedings and litigation management. Operationally, Unit 05B30 coordinates travel-related activities with external service providers, manages travel agency invoicing and reinvoicing, administers travel policy updates and quality assurance mechanisms, oversees travel insurance and professional credit card service contracts, and drives continuous organizational improvement through project management and innovation initiatives. Through these integrated activities, Unit 05B30 ensures seamless, compliant, and efficient mission management operations supporting the Parliament's institutional mobility and financial accountability objectives.
05B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Mission Administration & Legal Framework Governance** (43%): Creation, approval, verification, and settlement of mission orders; implementation and management of ex-ante financial controls; expense declaration verification and payment processing; travel agency coordination and archival management; systematic custodianship and revision of European Parliament Mission rules (GIP and IR); quality and risk management oversight

• **Information Technology Infrastructure & Application Management** (14%): Business ownership and project management of eMiss application development in cooperation with steering committees and working groups; maintenance, operational support, and comprehensive helpdesk services for legacy systems MISS and MISDECFR; technical assistance and user support

• **Financial & Budget Administration** (10%): Imprest account management; budget item and sub-item tracking; appropriation, commitment, payment, and recovery management; FMS application oversight; debt administration, litigation procedure management, and Debt Committee participation

• **Stakeholder Support & Capacity Building** (18% combined): Mission order creation, approval, and regulatory guidance for accredited parliamentary assistants and members' trainees with administrative form completion support and training programs (10%); comprehensive information services to all Directorates-General, Parliament services, and staff regarding mission rules, procedures, and financial settlements, supplemented by training delivery and statistical reporting (8%)

• **Operational Excellence & External Service Coordination** (15% combined): Unit project management and continuous organizational improvement initiatives (6%); travel agency coordination, invoicing, reinvoicing, and travel policy administration (5%); service contract administration for travel insurance and professional credit cards with quality control mechanisms (4%)

05B40.name: 05B40
original_activities: Establish pension rights, disability benefits and allowances for staff assigned to non-active status, leave in the interests of the service and retirement in the interests of the service, pursuant to Articles 41, 42c and 50 of the Staff Regulations.

Manage pension rights, disability benefits, and allowances (modification, suspension and termination, address changes, personal data updates, and bi-annual declarations) and the issuing of certificates.
Provide staff with information on pension rights, including pension simulations.
Calculate the salary percentage payable to staff members working half-time in preparation for retirement.
Determine the amount of severance grants.
Transfer pension rights acquired during previous employment.
Prepare transfers of acquired pension rights to other schemes.

Manage social security contributions for EU staff taking unpaid leave on personal grounds (CCP). Ensure cooperation with entities representing retired EU staff, in particular AIACE. Participate in retirement preparation seminars.
05B40.refined_description: Unit 05B40, positioned as a specialized operational center within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), serves as the European Parliament's primary administrative entity responsible for comprehensive pension and retirement benefits management throughout the complete staff lifecycle. The unit exercises statutory responsibility for establishing pension rights, disability benefits, and allowances for staff transitioning to non-active status—including leave in the interests of the service and retirement in the interests of the service pursuant to Articles 41, 42c, and 50 of the Staff Regulations—while simultaneously maintaining continuous stewardship of these entitlements through active management of modifications, suspensions, terminations, address changes, personal data updates, bi-annual declarations, and official certificate issuance. Operating in coordination with sibling units 05B10, 05B20, 05B30, 05B50, and 05B60 within Direction 05B, Unit 05B40 delivers essential informational services including personalized pension simulations and advisory support to facilitate informed retirement planning decisions by staff members. The unit executes specialized technical and financial functions encompassing the calculation of salary percentages applicable to half-time working arrangements during pre-retirement preparation phases, determination of severance grant amounts according to regulatory frameworks, and the technical transfer and preparation of pension rights acquired during previous EU institutional employment for portability to other pension schemes. Additionally, Unit 05B40 administers social security contribution continuity for EU staff on unpaid personal leave (CCP), fosters cooperative relationships with retired staff representation entities—particularly AIACE—and actively participates in retirement preparation seminars, thereby ensuring statutory compliance, financial accuracy, and comprehensive support for all staff members navigating transitions to retirement within the institutional framework.
05B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Pension Rights Establishment and Initial Administration** (25%): Establishment of pension rights, disability benefits, and allowances for staff transitioning to non-active status, including leave in the interests of the service and retirement in the interests of the service, pursuant to Staff Regulations Articles 41, 42c, and 50.

• **Ongoing Pension Management and Administrative Modifications** (20%): Active management of pension rights modifications, suspensions, terminations, address changes, personal data updates, bi-annual declarations, and issuance of official certificates maintaining continuous stewardship of entitlements.

• **Staff Information Services and Retirement Planning Support** (15%): Provision of comprehensive staff information regarding pension rights entitlements and calculation of personalized pension simulations to support informed retirement planning and decision-making.

• **Technical Pension Calculations and Financial Determinations** (22%): Calculation of salary percentages applicable to half-time employment arrangements in pre-retirement preparation phases (12%) and determination of severance grant amounts according to applicable regulatory frameworks (10%).

• **Pension Rights Transfers, Social Security Administration, and Stakeholder Engagement** (18%): Transfer and preparation of pension rights acquired during previous EU institutional employment for portability to other pension schemes (6%); management of social security contributions for EU staff on unpaid personal leave (CCP); maintenance of cooperative relationships with retired staff representation entities, particularly AIACE; and active participation in retirement preparation seminars and institutional staff support initiatives (12% combined).

05B50.name: 05B50
original_activities: Inform and advise staff members about administrative procedures; welcome new recruits in Luxembourg, Brussels and Strasbourg and help them settle in, provide support to colleagues leaving the European Parliament; organise specific initiatives (ad hoc conferences, visits, Back to School programme, retirement preparation seminars); manage the Starting@EP and Leaving@EP applications, manage the Interinstitutional Accommodation Office in Luxembourg.

Handle administrative procedures, provide information when taking up new duties; assist staff with relations with the authorities in the Member States, including special residence permits, single permits, EP Digital key service, VAT exemption, administrative formalities, certificates on request: employment, tax, schools, unemployment benefits, drawing up Laisser-passer, 136F procedure etc. Sign contracts electronically (APAs and Contractual Agents) for the 3 workplaces.
Ensure the planning, organisation and follow-up of the unit's activities; manage human and financial resources.
05B50.refined_description:
Unit 05B50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), functioning as a critical administrative services hub and institutional interface serving the European Parliament's multinational workforce across all three official workplaces (Luxembourg, Brussels, and Strasbourg). The unit executes three coordinated functional domains that collectively ensure seamless staff lifecycle management, administrative-legal compliance, and operational sustainability. As a leaf-node unit within the 05B Direction's organizational structure—alongside sibling units 05B10, 05B20, 05B30, 05B40, 05B60, and four additional units—05B50 contributes essential administrative infrastructure that enables the broader Personnel Directorate General to fulfill its institutional mandate. The unit's workload allocation reflects its tripartite mission: 35% dedicated to comprehensive staff lifecycle and institutional integration services, 35% allocated to complex administrative procedures and inter-institutional regulatory compliance, and 30% devoted to operational planning, activity coordination, and resource stewardship. Through this balanced functional architecture, Unit 05B50 maintains compliance with interinstitutional and Member State regulatory requirements while delivering integrated support throughout the complete staff employment journey across the three parliamentary locations.

---

##
05B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Staff Lifecycle Integration and Institutional Onboarding (35% workload allocation):** Provision of comprehensive information and advisory services regarding administrative procedures to all staff members; orchestration of formal welcome and integration programs for newly recruited personnel across Luxembourg, Brussels, and Strasbourg; delivery of settlement support and transition assistance for departing colleagues; organization and implementation of specialized initiatives including ad hoc conferences, institutional visits, Back to School programmes, and retirement preparation seminars; management of Starting@EP and Leaving@EP digital applications; operational administration of the Interinstitutional Accommodation Office in Luxembourg

• **Administrative Compliance and Inter-Institutional Relations (35% workload allocation):** Administration of procedures associated with assumption of new duties and staff transitions; facilitation of Member State authority relations and regulatory compliance guidance; management of special residence permits and single permit applications; coordination of EP Digital key service access and administration; processing of VAT exemption documentation and administrative formalities; issuance of specialized certificates (employment verification, tax documentation, school enrollment documentation, unemployment benefit documentation); preparation of Laisser-passer documentation; execution of 136F procedures; exercise of electronic contract signature authority for Auxiliary Personnel and Contractual Agents across all three parliamentary workplaces

• **Operational Management and Resource Stewardship (30% workload allocation):** Strategic planning and organization of unit activities; systematic follow-up and monitoring of operational execution; management of allocated human resources; administration of financial resources, budget allocation, and fiscal stewardship

05B60.name: 05B60
original_activities: Ensure recruitment operations, renewals and modifications to the contracts of accredited parliamentary assistants at the request of MPs (preparation, production and verification of the contract until the signing of the AHCC).

Provide MPs and accredited parliamentary assistants (APAs) with a single point of contact in Brussels (APA Desk) in order to provide information and advice on APA contracts (recruitments, amendments, modifications, resignations and dismissals) and on other related administrative procedures. To be a privileged point of orientation for APAs regarding their administrative life in the European Parliament.

Ensure the updating and development of applications and IT tools necessary for processing APA files in close collaboration with the services concerned with the aim of simplifying and automating procedures.

Ensure budgetary management of the part of the 'Parliamentary Assistance' allowance for MPs relating to the contracts of accredited parliamentary assistants, requests for external professional training and installation or reinstallation compensation.
Manage the end of APA contracts: end of mandate of MPs, resignations and termination requests from MPs. Hold pre-dismissal interviews.
05B60.refined_description: Unit 05B60 operates as the specialized operational and administrative hub within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), exercising comprehensive responsibility for the complete lifecycle management of Accredited Parliamentary Assistants (APAs) serving Members of the European Parliament.
