The unit's integrated mission encompasses end-to-end administration of APA employment relationships—spanning recruitment, contract administration, advisory services, financial stewardship, and separation management—while simultaneously functioning as the primary institutional interface for both MPs and APAs within the European Parliament's human resources framework. The unit delivers these services through five strategically balanced operational activities: managing the full spectrum of recruitment operations, contract renewals, and contractual modifications (35%); operating the APA Desk in Brussels as a unified point of contact providing comprehensive information, advisory guidance, and administrative orientation (25%); overseeing continuous development of applications and IT tools for APA file processing to advance procedure simplification and automation (15%); exercising financial administration over the Parliamentary Assistance allowance component designated for APA contracts, external professional training, and installation/reinstallation compensation (15%); and administering contract terminations resulting from MP mandate conclusions, voluntary APA resignations, and MP-initiated dismissal requests, including pre-dismissal interview procedures (10%). Together, these integrated activities enable Unit 05B60 to deliver seamless, efficient, and compliant APA employment administration while serving as an essential advisory and support interface for all stakeholders engaged in parliamentary assistance within the institution.
05B60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Contract Administration & Recruitment Operations** (35%): Execute the complete lifecycle of APA recruitment processes, contract renewals, and contractual modifications initiated by MPs, encompassing preparatory documentation, contract production, verification procedures, and finalization through AHCC signature

• **Stakeholder Advisory Services & Single Point of Contact** (25%): Operate the APA Desk in Brussels as the centralized point of contact providing MPs and accredited parliamentary assistants with comprehensive information, guidance, and administrative orientation regarding contract management, recruitments, amendments, modifications, resignations, dismissals, and broader administrative procedures within the European Parliament

• **Systems Development & Process Automation** (15%): Oversee continuous updating and development of applications and IT tools required for APA file processing through collaborative engagement with relevant institutional services, with explicit objectives of simplifying procedures and advancing automation capabilities

• **Financial Management & Budgetary Oversight** (15%): Exercise financial stewardship over the Parliamentary Assistance allowance component designated for APA contracts, external professional training requests, and installation or reinstallation compensation provisions

• **Contract Termination & Exit Management** (10%): Administer the conclusion of APA employment contracts resulting from MP mandate terminations, voluntary APA resignations, and MP-initiated termination requests, including responsibility for conducting pre-dismissal interview procedures

05B70.name: 05B70
original_activities: Manage the recruitment of Members' trainees and Schuman trainees; manage extensions, temporary suspensions and early terminations of traineeships; ensure geographical balance when recruiting Schuman trainees.
Provide personalised assistance to trainees and Members before, during and after traineeships.

Conduct outreach activities to promote the traineeship opportunities offered by the European Parliament, thereby enhancing the institution's reputation as a quality employer and increasing its attractiveness.
Manage Members' study visits and study visits to the Secretariat of the European Parliament.
Implement the Schuman Recruitment and Development Programme (SRDP); implement the Positive Action Scheme (PAS) for trainees living with disabilities.

Prepare the budgets for trainees allocated to directorates-general and monitor their use; monitor the use of the budgets for Members' trainees; ensure proper management of the appropriations under the relevant budget headings.
Deploy the mechanism for monitoring the quality of Schuman traineeships with a view to improving the services offered to trainees.
Continue to develop the tools necessary for the selection, recruitment and management of trainees (T-People and HRM portal); monitor current trainee health and accident insurance policies.
05B70.refined_description: Unit 05B70 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), serving as the European Parliament's central administrative hub for trainee recruitment, lifecycle management, and professional development initiatives. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility across the complete trainee continuum, encompassing recruitment administration for both Members' trainees and Schuman trainees—including management of extensions, temporary suspensions, early terminations, and maintenance of geographical balance in Schuman trainee selection—while simultaneously delivering intensive personalized assistance to trainees and Members throughout pre-trainee, during-trainee, and post-trainee phases. Unit 05B70 executes strategic outreach and promotional activities designed to enhance the Parliament's institutional reputation as a quality employer and increase competitive attractiveness in the talent market, while administering Members' study visits and Secretariat-hosted institutional orientation visits. The unit implements specialized programmes including the Schuman Recruitment and Development Programme (SRDP) and the Positive Action Scheme (PAS) for trainees with disabilities, demonstrating the institution's commitment to inclusive professional development. Unit 05B70 maintains rigorous budget oversight and appropriations management for trainee allocations across directorates-general, deploys quality assurance mechanisms for continuous service improvement in Schuman traineeships, and develops advanced digital infrastructure including T-People and HRM portal systems while overseeing trainee health and accident insurance policies. Through these eight interconnected activity streams, Unit 05B70 directly supports DG 05's strategic mission of human resources excellence and positions itself as a critical enabler of the Parliament's institutional capacity building, talent acquisition and retention strategy, and commitment to inclusive employment practices within the European institutional framework.
05B70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Trainee Recruitment and Lifecycle Management** (30%): Manage comprehensive recruitment cycles for Members' trainees and Schuman trainees; administer contract extensions, temporary suspensions, and early terminations; ensure geographical balance in Schuman trainee selection processes

• **Trainee and Member Support Services** (20%): Deliver personalized, multi-phase assistance to trainees and Members spanning pre-trainee, during-trainee, and post-trainee periods; facilitate professional development and institutional integration throughout the trainee experience continuum

• **Institutional Outreach and Employer Branding** (20%): Conduct targeted outreach activities promoting European Parliament traineeship opportunities; enhance institutional reputation and employer attractiveness; strengthen recruitment pipelines through strategic marketing and engagement initiatives

• **Specialized Programmes, Study Visits, and Quality Assurance** (15%): Implement Schuman Recruitment and Development Programme (SRDP) and Positive Action Scheme (PAS) for trainees with disabilities; administer Members' study visits and Secretariat institutional orientation visits; deploy quality monitoring mechanisms for Schuman traineeships to drive continuous service improvement

• **Budget Management, Digital Infrastructure, and Compliance Systems** (15%): Prepare and monitor trainee budgets allocated across directorates-general; ensure proper appropriations management and Members' trainee budget utilization oversight; develop and maintain T-People and HRM portal systems; oversee trainee health and accident insurance policies and compliance provisions

05B80.name: 05B80
original_activities: Manage, oversee and monitor the work of the unit; exercise the Appointing Authority's powers as regards special leave and 95% work arrangements for all members of staff; exercise the Appointing Authority's powers as regards part-time work / parental leave / family leave for Accredited parliamentary assistants; exercise the Appointing Authority's powers with a view to settling compensation for untaken or excessive leave for members of staff who are leaving the service.

Manage the budgets of the crèches (responsibility of the European Parliament), day nurseries and study centres (responsibility of the European Commission (OIB/OIL)), and act as the authorising officer by subdelegation for those budgets. Plan calls for tender, draw up specifications and monitor contracts (external contractors: operating companies, caterers, paediatricians, etc.).
Monitor teleworking in Parliament's Secretariat: advise managers and users, carry out regulatory monitoring, draft reports and statistics, liaise with the IT Unit on technical aspects.
Chair, provide the secretariat for and take part in a number of Interinstitutional joint committees; manage the Interinstitutional Leisure Library.
05B80.refined_description: Unit 05B80 operates as a specialized administrative unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), functioning as a critical operational hub for comprehensive human resources management, institutional welfare services, and workplace policy implementation across the European Parliament. The unit's primary mission encompasses the exercise of delegated Appointing Authority powers and the management of personnel-related services that directly impact the institutional workforce and their families, while simultaneously overseeing Parliament's essential social infrastructure and cross-institutional coordination responsibilities. Through its integrated portfolio of functions, Unit 05B80 manages all unit operations while administering special leave arrangements, implementing and monitoring 95% work arrangements for all staff members, managing part-time work, and processing parental leave and family leave provisions for Accredited parliamentary assistants, alongside settling compensation claims for untaken or excessive leave for departing personnel. The unit exercises authorizing officer responsibilities by subdelegation for budget management and contract oversight of Parliament-operated crèches, Commission-operated day nurseries, and study centres, directing tender processes, specification development, and comprehensive contractor performance monitoring for external service providers including operating companies, catering services, and medical personnel. Additionally, Unit 05B80 provides regulatory monitoring, compliance management, and advisory support for teleworking arrangements throughout Parliament's Secretariat, generating statistical reports and maintaining technical coordination with the IT Unit on implementation aspects. The unit further contributes to cross-institutional cooperation by chairing and providing secretariat services for multiple Interinstitutional joint committees while managing the Interinstitutional Leisure Library collection and services. Collectively, these integrated functions position Unit 05B80 as an essential operational support structure within the Personnel Directorate General framework, ensuring effective personnel administration, safeguarding employee welfare provisions, and facilitating institutional cooperation on social and workplace matters.
05B80.refined_activity_summary:
• **Personnel Administration & Appointing Authority Functions (60%)**: Management and monitoring of all unit operations; exercise of delegated Appointing Authority powers regarding special leave arrangements, 95% work arrangements for all staff members, part-time work arrangements, parental leave, and family leave for Accredited parliamentary assistants; processing and settlement of compensation for untaken or excessive leave upon staff departure

• **Institutional Childcare & Welfare Infrastructure Management (20%)**: Budget administration and authorizing officer responsibilities by subdelegation for Parliament-operated crèches, Commission-operated day nurseries, and study centres; planning and execution of tender calls, specification drafting, and comprehensive contract monitoring for external service providers (operating companies, catering contractors, paediatricians, and related medical personnel)

• **Teleworking Policy Oversight & Compliance (10%)**: Regulatory monitoring and compliance management of teleworking arrangements throughout Parliament's Secretariat; advisory services to managers and end-users; generation of statistical reports and regulatory documentation; technical liaison coordination with IT Unit on teleworking infrastructure and implementation

• **Interinstitutional Coordination & Staff Leisure Services (10%)**: Chairmanship and secretariat provision for multiple Interinstitutional joint committees; management and oversight of the Interinstitutional Leisure Library collection and services

05B8010.name: 05B8010
original_activities: Manage the different forms of leave and absences of civil servants, interns, other agents and accredited parliamentary assistants, and initiate procedures for payment of untaken leave or recovery of excess leave taken.
Manage the various procedures related to requests for parental leave, family leave and part-time work.
Respond to inquiries and provide advice on leave and working hours.
Carry out operations to control the data entered in the HRM Portal and check their consistency in relation to the service's own data.
05B8010.refined_description:
**

Unit 05B8010 is a specialized operational unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), functioning as a leaf-node administrative entity that serves as the European Parliament's primary administrative body responsible for comprehensive leave and absence management across all personnel categories. The unit exercises primary responsibility for administering leave entitlements and work-time arrangements for civil servants, interns, other agents, and accredited parliamentary assistants through systematic management of all forms of leave and absence procedures, while simultaneously initiating and processing procedures for payment of untaken leave and recovery of excess leave taken—activities that collectively constitute 40% of its operational workload. Complementing this core function, the unit dedicates 25% of its operational capacity to managing specialized procedures encompassing parental leave requests, family leave arrangements, and part-time work modifications, thereby supporting work-life balance, flexible working conditions, and ensuring compliance with institutional and legislative requirements for family-responsive human resources policies within the institution. The unit further allocates 25% of its workload to providing responsive advisory services and addressing inquiries regarding leave entitlements and working hours regulations, furnishing expert guidance to internal stakeholders throughout the Parliament and ensuring transparent communication and consistent interpretation of personnel policies across the institution. Additionally, the unit dedicates 10% of its capacity to performing critical data governance operations, including systematic control operations and verification of information entered into the HRM Portal and validation of data consistency against the service's own records, thereby maintaining data integrity, ensuring administrative accuracy, and supporting evidence-based personnel administration. As a leaf-node operational unit within the Direction-level structure, Unit 05B8010 operates in coordination with sibling units (05B10, 05B20, 05B30, 05B40, 05B50, and four additional units) to deliver integrated personnel management services and contribute essential administrative continuity to the Personnel Directorate General's strategic human resource management objectives across the European Parliament.

**
05B8010.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Leave and Absence Administration** (40%): Comprehensive management of all forms of leave and absences for civil servants, interns, other agents, and accredited parliamentary assistants; systematic administration of leave and absence procedures; initiation and processing of procedures for payment of untaken leave and recovery of excess leave taken

• **Family-Responsive Work Arrangements and Specialized Leave Procedures** (25%): Management of parental leave requests, family leave procedures, and part-time work arrangements; processing and administration of work arrangement modifications; support for work-life balance and flexible working conditions while ensuring compliance with institutional and legislative requirements

• **Advisory and Inquiry Services** (25%): Provision of responsive inquiry responses and expert advice concerning leave entitlements, working hours regulations, and related personnel policies; furnishing guidance to internal stakeholders throughout the Parliament; ensuring transparent communication and consistent interpretation of personnel policies

• **Data Quality and System Integrity** (10%): Systematic control operations and verification of information entered into the HRM Portal; validation of data consistency against internal service records; maintenance of data integrity to ensure administrative accuracy and support evidence-based personnel administration

05B8020.name: 05B8020
original_activities: Manage the administration of the CPE 2 nursery and the La Cigogne family room. Manage contracts for Wayenberg and CPE 5 nurseries and catering contracts (CPE 2; Strasbourg) and other stakeholders (3 sites). Ensure operational and financial management: admissions, parental and institutional contributions, children's files, advice to parents, contacts with nursery management. Manage the partnership with the Council of Europe crèche (Strasbourg).

Ensure financial monitoring: calculation and recovery of parental and institutional contributions, purchases, ex-post and audits. Manage the administration of the European Parliament's budgetary shares linked to crèches and associated structures. Establish specifications, ensure the administration, monitoring and evaluation of calls for tenders (purchase/services linked to Wayenberg, CPE 5, the catering of CPE 2 and Strasbourg, as well as the services of pediatricians and psychomotor therapists).

Ensure the representation of the 'Crèches' service within the Joint Management Committee (COGE): validation of admissions, regulations, participation in working groups, etc. Represent the European Parliament in joint and interinstitutional committees (COCEPE, CCPE and CAS). Participate proactively and contributorily in interinstitutional work of common interest: regulations, logistics, social actions, activity reports, surveys, etc.

Prepare activity reports for each of the entities (Brussels, Luxembourg and Strasbourg), for the attention of the control bodies (COGE, CCPE). Write summary documents for the attention of the hierarchy on any subject involving nurseries (buildings, financial implications, relations with national authorities, etc.).
05B8020.refined_description:
Unit 05B8020 operates as a specialized childcare and family services management unit within Direction 05B of the Personnel Directorate General (DG 05), exercising comprehensive operational and administrative authority over the European Parliament's crèche infrastructure and family support facilities across all three institutional seats (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg). As a leaf-node unit within Direction 05B's structure, Unit 05B8020 collaborates with sibling units (05B10, 05B20, 05B30, 05B40, 05B50, and others) to support the Parliament's broader personnel management and institutional support objectives. The unit's integrated mission encompasses direct administration of childcare facilities (CPE 2 nursery and La Cigogne family room), contractual oversight of additional nursery sites (Wayenberg and CPE 5), coordination of catering services across CPE 2 in Strasbourg and three additional stakeholder sites, and strategic partnership management with the Council of Europe crèche in Strasbourg—including admissions processing, parental advisory services, institutional and parental contribution collection, and comprehensive children's file administration. Complementing operational responsibilities, the unit exercises rigorous financial stewardship encompassing calculation and recovery of parental and institutional contributions, ex-post audits and financial monitoring, budgetary administration of Parliament's crèche-related allocations, and establishment and administration of tender specifications for services and supplies including pediatric and psychomotor therapy provision. The unit fulfills critical governance functions through representation in the Joint Management Committee (COGE), the Coordinated Committee of Childcare Provision of European Public Institutions (COCEPE), the Common Childcare Policy Committee (CCPE), and the Childcare and Social Action Committee (CAS), actively contributing to regulatory development, working group participation, and collaborative initiatives addressing regulations, logistics, and social actions. The unit additionally prepares comprehensive activity reports for each institutional location for submission to control bodies and produces hierarchical summary documentation addressing substantive matters with nursery implications, including facility infrastructure, financial considerations, and relations with national authorities.

---
05B8020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Operational Childcare Facility Management** (60%): Direct administration of CPE 2 nursery and La Cigogne family room; contractual management and oversight of Wayenberg and CPE 5 nurseries; catering contract administration for CPE 2 (Strasbourg) and three additional stakeholder sites; strategic partnership coordination with Council of Europe crèche (Strasbourg); admissions processing and validation; parental and institutional contribution coordination; comprehensive children's file administration; parental advisory services; and nursery management liaison

• **Financial Monitoring and Procurement Administration** (30%): Calculation and recovery of parental and institutional contributions; ex-post audits and comprehensive financial monitoring; European Parliament budgetary administration for crèche-related allocations and associated structures; expenditure and purchase management; tender specification development and administration for Wayenberg, CPE 5, CPE 2 catering services, and ancillary professional services (pediatricians and psychomotor therapists); procurement monitoring and evaluation

• **Interinstitutional Representation and Collaborative Governance** (5%): Representation within Joint Management Committee (COGE) including admissions validation and regulatory oversight; European Parliament representation in joint and interinstitutional committees (COCEPE, CCPE, CAS); proactive participation in interinstitutional working groups and collaborative initiatives addressing regulations, logistics, social actions, activity reporting, and institutional surveys

• **Reporting and Strategic Documentation** (5%): Preparation of comprehensive activity reports for each institutional location (Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg) for submission to control bodies (COGE, CCPE); production of hierarchical summary documentation addressing nursery-related substantive matters including facility infrastructure, financial implications, and relations with national authorities

05C.name: 05C
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the management units and services.
Develop the regulations, processes and practices relating to the field according to the needs of the Institution and according to the needs of customer services.
Exercise the powers of the appointing authority (medical part-time, article 60 of the statute, reasonable accommodation, invalidity procedures, etc.).
Develop relationships with partners in the Institution in terms of well-being and health at work.
Manage projects assigned to management within the framework of the SEF.
Represent the Institution in various committees and interinstitutional forums; participate in working groups and committees on specific themes.
Perform the functions of principal subdelegate authorizing officer, including in matters of purchases and markets.
Manage social and well-being infrastructure, including sports centers in the three workplaces.
05C.refined_description:
Unit 05C functions as a Direction-level strategic management and coordination hub within the European Parliament's personnel administration structure, reporting directly to the parent Directorate (Unit 05) and exercising comprehensive oversight authority over six subordinate operational units (05C0010, 05C10, 05C20, 05C30, 05C40, and 05C50). The unit serves as the institutional nexus for personnel management governance through four integrated functional domains: executive direction and coordination of management units and services; development and implementation of institutional regulations, processes, and practices governing personnel administration; exercise of appointing authority powers encompassing medical part-time arrangements, Article 60 statutory provisions, reasonable accommodation determinations, and invalidity procedures; and stewardship of institutional partnerships, project management, interagency representation, procurement authority, and workplace well-being infrastructure. Unit 05C operates as a peer entity alongside sibling directional units (05-0010, 05-10, 05A, 05B, 05D) within the broader personnel management framework, positioning it as the central coordinating mechanism for personnel administration, regulatory development, workplace well-being governance, and operational management across all three parliamentary workplaces. The unit's portfolio encompasses both strategic policy formulation aligned with institutional and customer service needs and tactical execution of administrative procedures, financial authorities, and infrastructure management responsibilities that collectively establish Unit 05C as the organizational foundation for personnel management excellence throughout the Institution.

---
05C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Direction and Organizational Coordination** (35%): Ensure the direction, coordination, and animation of management units and services; provide strategic oversight and operational leadership across the directorate's functional domains and subordinate units

• **Regulatory Development and Institutional Processes** (20%): Develop comprehensive regulations, processes, and practices aligned with institutional needs and customer service requirements; establish procedural frameworks governing personnel management operations and institutional practices

• **Personnel Administration and Statutory Authority** (15%): Exercise appointing authority powers including medical part-time arrangements, Article 60 statutory provisions, reasonable accommodation determinations, and invalidity procedures administration

• **Institutional Partnership and Interagency Relations** (10%): Develop relationships with institutional partners regarding occupational well-being and health initiatives; represent the Institution in committees and interinstitutional forums; participate in specialized working groups

• **Operational and Financial Stewardship** (20%): Manage assigned projects within SEF framework (5%); perform principal subdelegated authorizing officer functions for procurement and market operations (5%); manage social and well-being infrastructure including sports facilities across three parliamentary workplaces (5%); coordinate organizational well-being initiatives and occupational health partnerships (5%)

05C0010.name: 05C0010
original_activities: Gather, analyze and encode medical certificates from Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg and field offices.
Ensure medical monitoring of agents who are unable to work for more than 14 days in order to support them and help them prepare for their return at the right time and in good conditions.
Carry out, or have carried out, medical checks, if necessary, and manage the related invoices.
Manage the absence of Ombudsman staff and process medical certificates submitted by MEPs to justify their absence from plenary sessions.
Ensure contact with resource management and line managers in the context of managing long-term absences.
Analyze requests to spend sick leave in a place other than the place of employment (article 60 of the Statute).
Check and monitor the accounting of days of absence and inform the appointing authority if necessary (article 59 of the Statute).
Establish sick leave statistics, including statistics allowing general management to monitor their absences.
05C0010.refined_description: Unit 05C0010 is a specialized medical administration and absence management unit operating at the secondary hierarchical level within Direction 05C of the European Parliament's personnel management structure. The unit bears comprehensive institutional responsibility for medical documentation processing, health-related absence administration, and statutory compliance across all Parliament locations (Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg, and field offices). The unit's operational scope encompasses the systematic gathering, analysis, and encoding of medical certificates; proactive medical monitoring and return-to-work support for agents with extended work incapacity exceeding 14 days; procurement and financial administration of medical examinations; processing of absence documentation for Ombudsman personnel and Members of the European Parliament; coordination with resource management and line managers regarding long-term absence administration; evaluation of statutory requests for alternative sick leave locations under Article 60 of the Staff Statute; validation of absence accounting records with appointing authority notification of statutory deviations under Article 59; and production of comprehensive absence statistics for organizational management oversight. These integrated activities collectively establish Unit 05C0010 as an essential operational entity ensuring statutory compliance, employee welfare support, operational continuity, and institutional transparency in health-related absence management across the Parliament's institutional structure.
05C0010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Medical Documentation Processing & Analysis** (50%): Systematically gather, analyze, and encode medical certificates from Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg, and field offices, providing comprehensive institutional coverage and establishing the foundational administrative activity supporting all downstream medical and absence management functions.

• **Medical Monitoring & Return-to-Work Support** (15%): Ensure proactive medical monitoring of agents unable to work for periods exceeding 14 days, providing coordinated support mechanisms to facilitate appropriate return-to-work conditions and timely reintegration into active employment.

• **Medical Services Administration & Financial Management** (10%): Procure or conduct necessary medical examinations and manage associated invoicing, accounting procedures, and financial administration to maintain institutional health monitoring infrastructure.

• **Absence Management & Statutory Compliance** (17%): Process absence documentation for Ombudsman staff and Members of the European Parliament requiring plenary session justification (7%); evaluate statutory requests for sick leave taken outside the place of employment under Article 60 of the Staff Statute (5%); and monitor, validate, and report absence accounting accuracy to appointing authorities regarding statutory deviations under Article 59 (5%).

• **Stakeholder Coordination & Statistical Reporting** (8%): Maintain ongoing coordination with resource management and line managers regarding long-term absence administration (5%) and establish comprehensive sick leave statistics enabling organizational management to monitor absence patterns, institutional trends, and policy evaluation (3%).

05C10.name: 05C10
original_activities: Provide occupational medicine and preventive medicine.
Carry out medical-administrative activities (doctors, managers).
Provide care and advice (nursing).
Providing social assistance and addictions advice (other than the assistance and advice included in points 1, 2 and 5).
Provide emergency services.
05C10.refined_description: Unit 05C10 functions as a specialized occupational health and wellness services unit positioned at the second hierarchical level within Direction 05C of the European Parliament's personnel management framework, operating as an independent leaf-node entity without subordinate divisions while maintaining functional coordination with sibling units (05C0010, 05C20, 05C30, 05C40, 05C50) to ensure comprehensive institutional personnel support. The unit's integrated mission centers on comprehensive employee health protection and welfare through a strategically prioritized, multifaceted service delivery model that establishes foundational health safeguard infrastructure for parliamentary personnel. Occupational medicine and preventive medicine services constitute the unit's dominant operational focus at 40% of total workload, ensuring proactive health risk management, disease prevention, and clinical health protection across the institution. Supporting this primary function, medical-administrative activities performed by qualified doctors and managers account for 27% of workload and encompass clinical documentation, health records management, regulatory compliance, clinical governance, and coordination of medical services delivery. Direct clinical care and health guidance delivered through nursing personnel represent 19% of operational capacity, providing frontline clinical support and health counseling to beneficiaries. The unit dedicates 11% of resources to specialized social assistance and addictions advisory services, extending its mandate beyond purely clinical functions to address broader psychosocial employee welfare needs distinct from core medical interventions. Emergency services provision constitutes a focused 3% allocation, maintaining dedicated rapid response capability for acute health incidents and crisis situations requiring immediate clinical intervention. Through this integrated five-stream service portfolio, Unit 05C10 directly contributes to the European Parliament's institutional objectives of maintaining a healthy, productive workforce while fulfilling its comprehensive duty of care obligations to personnel and supporting organizational operational continuity.
05C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Occupational Medicine & Preventive Health Services** (40%): Provision of occupational medicine and preventive medicine programs representing the unit's primary strategic focus area, establishing foundational health safeguard infrastructure and ensuring proactive health risk management and disease prevention across parliamentary personnel

• **Medical-Administrative Operations & Clinical Governance** (27%): Execution of medical-administrative activities by physicians and management personnel, encompassing clinical documentation, health records management, regulatory compliance, clinical governance, and supervisory coordination of medical services delivery

• **Direct Clinical Care & Nursing Support** (19%): Delivery of nursing care services and health advice providing frontline clinical support, patient-facing wellness guidance, and health counseling to parliamentary personnel

• **Specialized Social & Psychosocial Support Services** (11%): Provision of social assistance and addiction advisory services offering targeted intervention and specialized counseling distinct from primary medical functions, addressing broader employee welfare needs

• **Emergency Medical Response Services** (3%): Maintenance of emergency medical services capability ensuring rapid response capacity for acute health incidents and crisis situations requiring immediate clinical intervention

05C20.name: 05C20
original_activities: Providing occupational medicine, preventive medicine as well as travel medicine for MEPs and staff members. Organize and carry out annual visits. Implement vaccination campaigns against influenza and COVID-19. Supporting mental health through the Mind Matters program.

Provide statutory medicine in relation to the Civil Servants' Statute, the RAA, the Members' Statute and the internal regulations of the European Parliament. Carry out recruitment visits – Task Force Elections 2024.
Providing general medicine for MEPs (Medical service in Brussels and Strasbourg).
Provide emergency medicine and hotlines for MEPs, staff and visitors in Brussels and Strasbourg.
Provide general medicine for staff members on mission to Strasbourg during parliamentary sessions.
Provide social assistance (other than that included in missions 1 to 5).
Collaborate with the Medical Crisis Anticipation and Management Unit.
05C20.refined_description: Unit 05C20 operates as the specialized Medical Services operational unit within the European Parliament's Personnel and Administration Directorate (Direction 05C), functioning as a leaf node within the broader personnel management structure alongside sibling units 05C0010, 05C10, 05C30, 05C40, and 05C50. The unit delivers comprehensive, integrated occupational, preventive, statutory, and emergency medical services to Members of the European Parliament (MEPs), staff members, and institutional visitors across all parliamentary locations (Brussels and Strasbourg), thereby establishing a coordinated healthcare delivery system that supports the Parliament's human resources objectives and institutional continuity. Unit 05C20's mission encompasses seven distinct operational activities: occupational and preventive medicine services (including annual medical visits, influenza and COVID-19 vaccination campaigns, travel medicine, and mental health support through the Mind Matters program); statutory medical compliance services mandated by the Civil Servants' Statute, RAA, Members' Statute, and internal European Parliament regulations (including recruitment medical examinations and Task Force Elections 2024 support); general medicine consultations for MEPs in both Brussels and Strasbourg locations; emergency medicine services with 24-hour operational hotlines for MEPs, staff, and visitors across both parliamentary seats; targeted general medicine services for staff members on official mission assignments to Strasbourg during parliamentary sessions; supplementary social assistance functions beyond primary mission areas; and collaborative coordination with the Medical Crisis Anticipation and Management Unit to ensure integrated institutional response capacity for health emergencies. This comprehensive service portfolio, underpinned by strategic direction from Direction 05C, ensures continuity of care and institutional preparedness across the European Parliament's distributed operational structure while maintaining full compliance with applicable statutory and regulatory frameworks.
05C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Occupational, Preventive, and Travel Medicine Services** (30%): Provision of occupational medicine and preventive medicine services; organization and execution of annual medical visits; implementation of influenza and COVID-19 vaccination campaigns; delivery of travel medicine services; mental health support through the Mind Matters program for MEPs and staff members

• **Statutory and Regulatory Medical Compliance** (25%): Delivery of statutory medicine services in accordance with the Civil Servants' Statute, RAA (Règlement Administratif et Financier), Members' Statute, and European Parliament internal regulations; execution of recruitment medical examinations; specialized support for Task Force Elections 2024

• **General Medicine Services for MEPs** (15%): Provision of general medicine consultations and clinical services for Members of the European Parliament in both Brussels and Strasbourg parliamentary locations

• **Emergency Medicine and Crisis Response Services** (15%): Provision of emergency medicine services and 24-hour operational hotline coverage for MEPs, staff members, and visitors across Brussels and Strasbourg; integrated coordination with the Medical Crisis Anticipation and Management Unit (1% embedded); collaborative institutional crisis management support

• **Mission-Based Medical Support for Staff** (10%): Provision of general medicine services for staff members on official mission assignments to Strasbourg during parliamentary sessions, ensuring continuity of care during extended institutional operations

• **Supplementary Social Assistance Services** (4%): Provision of social assistance functions and welfare support services beyond those encompassed in primary mission activities

05C30.name: 05C30
original_activities: Coordinate preparedness for major medical incidents. Assure the secretariat and coordination of the Interdisciplinary Working Group, and ensure strategic readiness through planning, exercises, stockpiles, and integration into institutional emergency frameworks in cooperation with the Directorate-General for Security and Safety and other key services.

Develop general medical preparedness and resilience, including for large events and missions abroad. Provide medical threat analysis, ensure support for events and delegations beyond usual workplaces, and strengthen cooperation for early response and continuity of care.

Contribute to the delivery of clinical medical services. Provide medical advice and support for travel medicine, consultations, emergency response, and statutory medical checks, in collaboration with the medical services.
Promote occupational health and wellbeing. Support mental health, contribute to workplace health promotion and safety policy, and advise on data-driven health initiatives across the institution.

Ensure coordination among the Parliament's medical services. Harmonise medical guidelines, promote digital tools and training, and ensure consistent standards while safeguarding medical confidentiality and data protection.

Contribute medical expertise to internal and interinstitutional bodies. Participate in boards and committees such as the IMB, JSIS, Crisis Cell, harassment committee, and health and safety groups and provide expert advice on medical policy and prevention. Participate in the EU27+ network of parliamentary medical services.

Manage ad hoc medical crises and support emergency response. Respond to outbreaks, provide medical advice in urgent incidents, and support decision-making bodies, staff, and delegations through agile medical coordination.
Mind Matters support programme, Authorising officer and administrative support in budgetary matters, Financial procedures and selection boards.
05C30.refined_description:
**

Unit 05C30 functions as the European Parliament's specialized Medical Services and Health Preparedness unit, operating as a critical Level 2 organizational component within Direction 05C's personnel and institutional wellbeing mandate. The unit executes a comprehensive, integrated portfolio spanning emergency medical preparedness, clinical service delivery, occupational health promotion, and medical services coordination—totaling 100% workload allocation across eight distinct activity streams. Operating in close coordination with the Directorate-General for Security and Safety, internal medical services, and parallel sibling units (05C0010, 05C10, 05C20, 05C40, 05C50), Unit 05C30 establishes seamless institutional health governance through strategic readiness planning, medical threat analysis, digital tool implementation, data-driven health initiatives, and active participation in institutional bodies including the IMB, JSIS, Crisis Cell, harassment committees, and the EU27+ parliamentary medical services network. The unit's operational framework ensures that medical preparedness, clinical excellence, occupational wellbeing, and emergency response capabilities are comprehensively integrated throughout the Parliament's institutional framework, thereby strengthening resilience and staff protection across all Parliament locations and external missions. This positioning enables Unit 05C30 to serve as a critical institutional resource for medical governance, emergency preparedness, personnel health management, and continuity of care across the organization.

**
05C30.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Emergency Preparedness and Crisis Management** (30%): Coordinate preparedness for major medical incidents through Interdisciplinary Working Group secretariat and strategic readiness planning, including planning exercises, stockpiles, and integration into institutional emergency frameworks (25%); manage ad hoc medical crises, outbreak response, and emergency coordination support including urgent incident medical advice (5%)

• **Medical Preparedness, Resilience, and Threat Analysis** (25%): Develop general medical preparedness and resilience infrastructure for large-scale events and missions abroad; conduct comprehensive medical threat analysis; ensure delegation support and early response coordination systems; strengthen continuity of care frameworks

• **Clinical Medical Service Delivery and Occupational Health** (22%): Deliver clinical medical services including travel medicine consultations, emergency response protocols, statutory medical checks, and medical advice in active collaboration with institutional medical services (12%); promote occupational health, mental health support, workplace health promotion, safety policy development, and evidence-driven health initiatives (10%)

• **Medical Services Coordination and Standardization** (10%): Harmonize and standardize medical guidelines across Parliament's medical services; promote digital tools and training program implementation; ensure consistent medical standards while safeguarding medical confidentiality and data protection compliance

• **Interinstitutional Medical Expertise, Governance, and Administrative Functions** (10%): Contribute specialized medical expertise to internal and interinstitutional bodies including IMB, JSIS, Crisis Cell, harassment committees, and health and safety groups; participate actively in the EU27+ network of parliamentary medical services (7%); provide Mind Matters programme support, authorizing officer functions, and budgetary and selection board administration (3%)

05C40.name: 05C40
original_activities: Concerning risks linked to the physical working environment: advise the European Parliament in the development, proposal and implementation of prevention and protection measures linked to safety and health at work, based on internal rules and in accordance with European and national legislation.
Develop psychosocial risk management systems at work (including risk analyzes as well as assistance in the design and implementation of action plans).

Play a role in promoting a healthy lifestyle and providing sporting activities to staff in the three main workplaces. Participate in health prevention programs in collaboration with the Medical Service.
Carry out and follow up on workplace risk assessments and workplace accident analyses.

Develop programs, policies and mechanisms intended to support or improve health and well-being at work, particularly in the psychosocial area, mental health at work, prevention of conflict and harassment at work, mentoring mechanisms, etc. Design and implement awareness campaigns in these areas.
Establish an occupational health and safety (OHS) management system in Parliament and manage contacts with other administration services in this area. Establish an OHS data collection system.
Conduct investigations into professional circumstances in the context of requests for recognition of illnesses of occupational origin, including in certain cases of disability.
05C40.refined_description: Unit 05C40 functions as a specialized occupational health and safety (OHS) operational unit positioned at the second hierarchical level within Direction 05C of the European Parliament's personnel management structure, serving as a critical institutional component in the organization's comprehensive employee welfare and workplace protection framework. Operating across all three main parliamentary workplaces, the unit develops, implements, and oversees integrated occupational health and safety systems that address both physical and psychosocial workplace hazards while ensuring compliance with European and national legislation. Unit 05C40 executes seven interconnected functional activities that collectively constitute the complete scope of its operational mandate: advisory services on physical working environment risks and prevention measures; psychosocial risk management system design and implementation; health promotion and sporting activities coordination; workplace risk assessments and accident investigation and follow-up; comprehensive health and well-being program development with emphasis on mental health, conflict prevention, harassment prevention, and mentoring mechanisms; establishment and management of integrated OHS governance systems with inter-service coordination infrastructure; and occupational illness recognition investigations. The unit coordinates strategically with its sibling units (05C0010, 05C10, 05C20, 05C30, 05C50) within Direction 05C to ensure coherent personnel management policies while collaborating with the Medical Service on integrated health prevention initiatives. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 05C40 positions itself as the primary institutional architect for parliamentary OHS governance, advancing the Parliament's institutional commitment to employee protection, workplace dignity, and organizational health standards.
05C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Physical and Occupational Risk Management** (34%): Provide expert advisory services on physical working environment safety, health risk prevention, and protection measures aligned with European and national legislation (19%); conduct and monitor workplace risk assessments, conduct accident analyses, and execute follow-up investigations (15%)

• **Psychosocial Health and Well-being Programs** (34%): Design and implement comprehensive psychosocial risk management systems including detailed risk analyses and evidence-based action plan development (19%); develop and implement integrated health and well-being policies, programs, and targeted awareness campaigns addressing mental health promotion, conflict prevention, harassment prevention, mentoring mechanisms, and psychosocial support structures (15%)

• **Health Promotion and Lifestyle Support** (17%): Promote healthy lifestyle initiatives and coordinate sporting activities across all three main parliamentary workplaces; participate in collaborative health prevention programs in partnership with the Medical Service

• **OHS Governance and Systems Management** (10%): Establish and manage the institution-wide integrated occupational health and safety management system; develop OHS data collection and reporting infrastructure; establish and maintain inter-service coordination mechanisms with other administration services

• **Occupational Health Recognition and Investigation** (5%): Conduct professional circumstance investigations in the context of occupational illness recognition requests, including disability-related cases and workplace condition assessments

05C50.name: 05C50
original_activities: Assist the Vice-President responsible for the High Level Group on Gender Equality and Diversity, particularly in the drafting of the new gender action plan and the resulting roadmap. Promote gender equality in all aspects of human resources policy; develop studies, reports and statistics, notably the annual brochure 'Women in the European Parliament' as well as the half-yearly data published on the intranet.

Prepare and organize monthly meetings of the Acora committee; issue recommendations addressed to the appointing authority. Prepare and organize meetings of the ad hoc Finaid committee for additional assistance to disabled colleagues and their families; issue consequent recommendations to the appointing authority and make payments. Coordinate the affirmative action program for disabled contract agents. Ensure compliance with the CRDPH convention and monitoring of ECA audits.

Organize awareness-raising events, in particular to mark key international dates relating to diversity issues, such as International Women's Day; the Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination; the Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia, etc. Maintain a dialogue with the three relevant intergroups (ARDI, LGBTIQ and Disability) and the three relevant staff associations (DSG, Equality and PoC).

Organize information sessions regarding measures to strengthen diversity and inclusion for key staff groups, including front-line staff (security officers, accreditation center staff, medical practices, etc.) and trainee supervisors.

Prepare the drafting of the new roadmap on diversities other than gender. Meet and inform gender coordinators and diversity coordinators from general management and political groups (HR). Support the coordination of political groups (Personal Group) in their efforts to implement diversity policies.

Serve as a point of contact for staff on all matters relating to the LGBTIQ+ community, anti-racism and disability. Provide the requested information and alert the competent bodies in the event of possible discrimination.
Advise the AIPN, maintain a dialogue with COPEC and Inter-copec, with other institutions (in particular via the network of advisors) as well as the staff committee in various areas of expertise.
05C50.refined_description: Unit 05C50 functions as the European Parliament's specialized operational unit dedicated to gender equality, diversity, and inclusion, positioned as a Level 2 entity within the Direction 05C personnel management structure. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses advancing gender equality across all institutional human resources policies while simultaneously promoting diversity and inclusion across multiple protected characteristics including disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, and racial/ethnic background. Operating across six integrated functional domains, the unit provides substantive analytical and policy support to the Vice-President responsible for the High Level Group on Gender Equality and Diversity; exercises formal governance and compliance functions through committee administration and financial assistance programs; orchestrates awareness-raising campaigns and stakeholder engagement with parliamentary intergroups and staff associations; delivers targeted capacity-building activities to frontline institutional staff; develops strategic policy frameworks addressing diversity dimensions; and serves as the institutional point of contact for diversity-related staff inquiries with discrimination escalation authority. The unit maintains strategic coordination relationships with gender and diversity specialists across general management and political groups, the AIPN, COPEC networks, peer institutions through the advisors network, and the staff committee, thereby positioning itself as a central institutional mechanism for advancing the Parliament's gender equality and diversity objectives across all organizational dimensions.
05C50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Gender Equality Policy Development and Institutional Benchmarking** (30%): Provision of substantive analytical and drafting support to the Vice-President and High Level Group on Gender Equality and Diversity; development and publication of authoritative gender equality studies, comprehensive statistical reports, and analytical assessments; production of the annual flagship publication "Women in the European Parliament"; maintenance of comprehensive institutional demographic data published half-yearly on the institutional intranet; drafting of the new gender action plan and associated strategic roadmap; integration of gender equality considerations across all HR policy domains.

• **Governance, Compliance Administration, and Financial Support Mechanisms** (20%): Preparation and organization of monthly Acora committee meetings with formal recommendations to the appointing authority; administration of ad hoc Finaid committee sessions providing financial assistance to disabled colleagues and their families; coordination and implementation of affirmative action programs for disabled contract agents; institutional compliance monitoring with the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRDPH); oversight and follow-up of European Court of Auditors (ECA) audit recommendations and institutional audit processes.

• **Awareness-Raising, Commemoration, Stakeholder Engagement, and Capacity-Building** (30%): Organization of awareness-raising campaigns and commemorative events aligned with international observances including International Women's Day, Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination, and Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia; active dialogue maintenance with three designated parliamentary intergroups (ARDI, LGBTIQ, Disability); coordination with three staff associations (DSG, Equality, People of Colour); design and delivery of targeted information sessions on diversity and inclusion measures for key staff cohorts including security personnel, accreditation center staff, medical practice employees, and trainee supervisors; development of roadmaps addressing diversity dimensions beyond gender with coordination support for political group diversity policy implementation.

• **Advisory Services, Point-of-Contact Functions, and Inter-Institutional Coordination** (10%): Provision of specialized point-of-contact services for staff inquiries regarding LGBTIQ+ community matters, anti-racism initiatives, and disability accommodations; authority to escalate potential discrimination cases to competent bodies; delivery of advisory services to the AIPN; maintenance of strategic dialogue with COPEC, Inter-COPEC, peer institutions through the advisors network, and the staff committee; coordination with gender and diversity specialists across general management and political groups to support institutional diversity policy implementation.

05D.name: 05D
original_activities: Provide the Director General, directorates and staff of the General Directorate of Personnel with the necessary assistance and support in terms of human resources, financial, IT, logistics, professional training, career advice and document management.
Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units of Directorate D.

Ensure the representation of the General Directorate of Personnel in internal and external committees and meetings and the Office's working groups (WG Digital transformation, Cybersecurity and Information Security and WG Sustainable infrastructure and Services).

Ensure monitoring of the Annual Work Program (AWP), the Annual Activity Report (AAR), the IT plan. Manage specific projects such as Temporary placement, ensure the implementation of Business Continuity and EMAS Policies in the General Personnel Department.
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer.
05D.refined_description: Unit 05D functions as the strategic Direction and primary operational hub within the General Directorate of Personnel (Directorate General 05), exercising dual responsibility as both a Level 1 hierarchical entity and the central coordinating and support centre for the entire directorate structure. The unit serves as the institutional nexus for comprehensive administrative and operational support services, directorate-wide governance and coordination functions, external and internal representation, strategic programme and project management, and delegated financial authorization. Operating across five integrated functional areas that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation, Unit 05D maintains coherent directorate-wide performance and alignment with institutional objectives through strategic direction and animation of its three subordinate operational units (05D10: human resources operations and administrative management; 05D20: financial operations and compliance; 05D30: IT service delivery and infrastructure management). The unit exercises institutional representation of the General Directorate of Personnel across internal and external committees, meetings, and Office-level working groups, specifically including participation in the WG Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity and Information Security, and WG Sustainable Infrastructure and Services initiatives. Additionally, Unit 05D assumes responsibility for monitoring and managing critical strategic programmes including the Annual Work Programme (AWP), Annual Activity Report (AAR), and IT planning frameworks, while directing implementation of specific operational projects such as Temporary Placement initiatives and ensuring compliance with Business Continuity and EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) Policies throughout the General Personnel Department. The unit head exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions, enabling financial and administrative decision-making authority within established European Parliament governance parameters.
05D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Support and Administrative Services** (33%): Comprehensive provision of human resources management, financial administration, information technology systems, logistics coordination, professional development and training programmes, career guidance services, and document management operations to the Director General, fellow directorates, and General Directorate of Personnel personnel

• **Directorate Governance, Coordination, and Leadership** (25%): Strategic direction, coordination, and animation of three subordinate operational units (05D10, 05D20, 05D30) to ensure organizational cohesion, operational synergy, coherent directorate-wide performance, and alignment with institutional objectives

• **Institutional Representation and Strategic Engagement** (20%): Representation of the General Directorate of Personnel as primary interface in internal and external committees, meetings, and Office-level working groups, including participation in WG Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity and Information Security, and WG Sustainable Infrastructure and Services initiatives

• **Strategic Programme Management and Policy Implementation** (20%): Monitoring and oversight of Annual Work Programme (AWP), Annual Activity Report (AAR), and IT strategic planning frameworks; management of Temporary Placement programmes; implementation and compliance oversight of Business Continuity and EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) Policies across the General Personnel Department

• **Financial and Administrative Authority** (2%): Exercise of subdelegated authorizing officer functions enabling delegated financial and administrative decision-making capacity within established European Parliament governance structures and parameters

05D10.name: 05D10
original_activities: Ensure the management of human resources at the disposal of the Directorate-General for Personnel to enable the achievement of its strategic and operational objectives: manage the selection and the recruitment of staff, carry out the continuous screening of the workforce and provide guidance and support on human resources matters.

Manage the personal files of agents in the European Parliament. Implement the corporate project on the management of personal files to streamline, automate and digitalise the related procedures and work processes.

Coordinate and implement human resources processes: Schuman traineeships, mobility exercises, staff assessment procedure, gender equality action plan, tutoring programme and exit interviews. Follow up the implementation of budget envelopes for contract agents and missions.
Coordinate the corporate planning exercises: annual work programme, annual learning plan, HR Forward Planning.

Ensure the role of GBI: manage office spaces, coordinate individual and collective moves, carry out the tracking of assets. Liaise with the Directorate-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, the Directorate-General for Information Technologies and Cybersecurity and the Directorate-General for Finance on infrastructure and logistics topics.
Implement the corporate document management policy and contribute to and establish related rules and procedures. Contribute to the development of the new document management tool 'BRICKS'.
05D10.refined_description: Unit 05D10 functions as the core human resources operations and strategic administrative management hub within Directorate 05D of the European Parliament's Directorate-General for Personnel, serving as the primary operational centre that enables the achievement of institutional human resources objectives across multiple interconnected domains. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive human resources management including staff selection and recruitment processes, continuous workforce screening, and the provision of authoritative guidance and support on human resources matters to facilitate the organization's strategic and operational objectives. Unit 05D10 administers the complete lifecycle management of personal files for all European Parliament agents whilst implementing the corporate digitalization project designed to streamline, automate, and modernize associated administrative procedures and work processes. The unit coordinates and operationalizes critical human resources processes encompassing Schuman traineeships, staff mobility exercises, staff assessment procedures, gender equality action plans, tutoring programmes, exit interviews, and budget envelope oversight for contract agents and missions. Unit 05D10 serves as the institutional coordinator for corporate planning exercises including the annual work programme, annual learning plan, and HR Forward Planning initiatives that align departmental activities with organizational strategy, whilst simultaneously fulfilling General Budget Item (GBI) management responsibilities encompassing office space allocation, coordination of individual and collective relocations, and asset tracking with active liaison relationships maintained across the Directorates-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, and Finance. The unit additionally implements the corporate document management policy framework, contributes to the establishment of related institutional rules and procedures, and participates in the development of the new institutional document management system 'BRICKS'.
05D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Human Resources Administration and Strategic Workforce Management** (45%): Direct management of human resources resources available to the Directorate-General for Personnel; comprehensive administration of staff selection and recruitment processes; continuous workforce screening activities; provision of authoritative guidance and support on all human resources matters to achieve strategic and operational objectives

• **Personnel File Administration and Corporate Digitalization** (25%): Complete lifecycle management of personal files for all European Parliament agents; implementation of corporate digitalization project designed to streamline, automate, and modernize related administrative procedures and work processes across the institution

• **Human Resources Process Coordination and Implementation** (10%): Coordination and operationalization of specialized human resources initiatives including Schuman traineeships, staff mobility exercises, staff assessment procedures, gender equality action plans, tutoring programmes, exit interviews, and budget envelope management for contract agents and missions

• **Corporate Planning and Strategic Coordination** (10%): Institutional coordination of corporate planning exercises encompassing annual work programmes, annual learning plans, and HR Forward Planning initiatives to ensure alignment of departmental activities with organizational strategic objectives

• **Infrastructure Management and Institutional Services** (7%): General Budget Item (GBI) management including office space allocation, coordination of individual and collective relocations, asset tracking, and inter-directorate liaison on infrastructure and logistics matters with Directorates-General for Infrastructure and Logistics, Information Technologies and Cybersecurity, and Finance

• **Document Management Policy Implementation and Systems Development** (3%): Implementation of corporate document management policy framework; establishment of related institutional rules and procedures; participation in development of new institutional document management system 'BRICKS'

05D20.name: 05D20
original_activities: Supervise the financial initiation of budgetary operations (commitments, payment orders, recovery orders, regularization, liquidation), including payroll and missions.
Develop and manage open/negotiated public procurement procedures, record, monitor contracts and advise operational units.
Ensure ex-ante verification of financial operations, individual staff rights and public procurement, as well as ex-post controls.

Supervise the financial management of the General Directorate of Personnel: budgetary monitoring, drafting of periodic and annual activity reports, financial closing, budget transfers, debt management, monitoring of delegations, access to financial applications (FMS and WebContracts), and advice to operational units.
Coordinate the internal controls and risk management of the General Personnel Directorate.
Monitor and process requests from supervisory authorities.

Represent the Directorate-General for Personnel within the inter-DG Finance steering group, the Public Procurement Forum, the risk management working group and in the network of internal control coordinators.
05D20.refined_description:
Unit 05D20 functions as the Financial Operations and Compliance Hub within Directorate 05D of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Personnel (DG 05), serving as the central financial control and administrative oversight mechanism for the entire Personnel Directorate. Operating as a level-2 operational unit within the hierarchical structure and collaborating with sibling units 05D10 and 05D30 under parent unit 05D coordination, Unit 05D20 exercises comprehensive financial stewardship encompassing the complete lifecycle of budgetary operations, including supervision of financial initiation for commitments, payment orders, recovery orders, regularization, liquidation procedures, payroll processing, and mission-related expenditures. The unit maintains dual responsibility for procurement governance and financial control by developing and managing both open and negotiated public procurement procedures, recording and monitoring contracts, and providing advisory support to operational units, while simultaneously executing rigorous ex-ante verification of financial operations, individual staff rights determinations, and public procurement activities alongside comprehensive ex-post controls to ensure compliance and operational integrity. Unit 05D20 provides specialized financial management oversight of the General Directorate of Personnel through budgetary monitoring, periodic and annual activity reporting, financial closing procedures, budget transfer authorization, debt management, delegation monitoring, and facilitation of access to critical financial applications including FMS and WebContracts systems, with concurrent advisory services to operational units. The unit coordinates internal control frameworks and risk management mechanisms across the General Personnel Directorate, monitors and processes supervisory authority requests, and represents the Directorate-General for Personnel within inter-DG Finance steering groups, the Public Procurement Forum, risk management working groups, and the network of internal control coordinators, thereby serving as the critical interface between personnel operations and institutional financial governance structures.

##
05D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Financial Operations Initiation, Payroll & Budgetary Stewardship** (34%): Supervises the complete financial lifecycle of budgetary operations including commitments, payment orders, recovery orders, regularization, and liquidation procedures; manages payroll processing and mission-related expenditures with full oversight authority and integrated financial responsibility.

• **Procurement Management & Contract Governance** (20%): Develops and manages open and negotiated public procurement procedures; records, monitors, and maintains comprehensive contract documentation; provides specialized advisory guidance to operational units on procurement compliance and requirements.

• **Financial Verification & Control Mechanisms** (20%): Executes ex-ante verification of financial operations, individual staff rights determinations, and public procurement documentation; conducts systematic ex-post controls to ensure compliance, accuracy, and operational integrity across all financial transactions.

• **Directorate Financial Management & Systems Administration** (15%): Provides specialized financial management oversight of the General Directorate of Personnel including budgetary monitoring, preparation of periodic and annual activity reports, financial closing procedures, budget transfers, debt management, delegation oversight, and grants operational units technical access and advisory services related to FMS and WebContracts financial applications.

• **Governance, Risk Management & Inter-Institutional Coordination** (11%): Coordinates internal controls and risk management frameworks across the General Personnel Directorate (4%); monitors and processes supervisory authority requests (4%); represents the Directorate-General for Personnel within inter-DG Finance steering groups, Public Procurement Forum, risk management working groups, and internal control coordinator networks (3%).

05D30.name: 05D30
original_activities: Manage the lifecycle of custom or specialised software applications in use: implement enhancements, provide training and support, and conduct testing to ensure functionality and cybersecurity.

Plan, execute, and oversee IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints such as scope, time, budget, and quality: schedule projects, implement solutions, allocate resources, manage risks, and communicate with stakeholders.

Understand business needs and objectives and translate them into IT solutions: gather and analyse requirements, facilitate communication between stakeholders, and recommend effective technological solutions.

Provide support and resolve technical issues encountered by human resources applications users; proactively communicate problems and solutions; troubleshoot hardware and software issues; offer guidance and ensure a positive user experience.

Plan, provision, maintain, and monitor IT infrastructure deployed for the Directorate-General for Personnel users - including hardware, software, network components, and user access management - to ensure the availability, reliability, and security of IT resources.
Ensure the smooth operation and maintenance of the HRM Portal and its satellite applications.
Take measures to protect human resources applications, data, and informational assets against threats, vulnerabilities, and other cybersecurity risks.
05D30.refined_description: Unit 05D30 operates as a specialized IT service delivery and infrastructure management centre within Directorate 05D of the General Directorate of Personnel, functioning as the critical operational hub for human resources technology systems serving the entire Directorate-General for Personnel user base. As a leaf-node unit operating in coordinated alignment with sibling units 05D10 and 05D20 under the strategic direction of Directorate 05D, Unit 05D30 executes a comprehensive mandate encompassing the complete lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications, structured IT project delivery, business-to-technology requirements translation, dedicated user support operations, IT infrastructure provisioning and management, operational continuity assurance, and cybersecurity risk mitigation. The unit's service delivery model integrates technical excellence, strategic project execution, and organizational alignment through systematic requirements analysis, multi-stakeholder facilitation, rigorous testing protocols, proactive user communication, and continuous infrastructure monitoring. All activities are executed within defined parameters of scope, timeline, budget, and quality standards, ensuring sustained functionality, cybersecurity compliance, resource availability, reliability, and positive user experience outcomes across the Directorate-General for Personnel technology ecosystem.
05D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Software Application Lifecycle Management & Portal Operations** (31%): Comprehensive management of custom and specialized software applications encompassing enhancement implementation, end-user training delivery, technical support provision, and rigorous functionality and cybersecurity testing protocols (25%); operational management and maintenance of HRM Portal and associated satellite applications ensuring uninterrupted service delivery and operational stability (6%)

• **IT Project Management & Infrastructure Delivery** (35%): Structured IT project planning, execution, and oversight encompassing scheduling, solution implementation, resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication to achieve defined objectives within scope, timeline, budget, and quality constraints (20%); comprehensive IT infrastructure planning, provisioning, maintenance, and continuous monitoring of hardware assets, software systems, network components, and user access management protocols to ensure availability, reliability, and security (15%)

• **Requirements Analysis, Business-IT Alignment & User Support** (30%): Systematic translation of business needs and organizational objectives into IT solutions through requirements gathering and analysis, multi-stakeholder facilitation, and evidence-based technological recommendations (15%); direct technical support and issue resolution for human resources applications users including proactive problem communication, hardware and software troubleshooting, user guidance, and assurance of optimal user experience outcomes (15%)
