• **Cybersecurity Risk Mitigation & Operational Assurance** (4%): Implementation of protective measures and risk mitigation activities safeguarding human resources applications, data assets, and informational resources against identified threats, vulnerabilities, and emerging cybersecurity risks

original_activities: Advise the Secretary General, the President, the political bodies - in particular the Conference of Presidents - as well as the Members of the European Parliament in their areas of competence, including for the tasks devolved to the Deputy Secretary General (in particular legislative programming and the 'Better law-making' agenda).
Ensure the organization, modernization and proper functioning of all activities linked to the Plenary, the Protocol, legislative acts and document management.
Organize the activities of general management from a strategic perspective; define objectives and means, and propose areas of development.
Represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament, including joint committees, cooperate with other directors-general.
Represent and/or ensure representation of the general management and the institution in interinstitutional forums and negotiations, etc.
Exercise the function of delegated authorizing officer.
If necessary, act as interim Secretary General and represent the institution.
Other missions.
01.refined_description: Unit 01 functions as the apex strategic and operational command center of the European Parliament, operating at the Directorate-General level and reporting directly to the Secretary General. This unit serves as the institutional nerve center responsible for translating the Parliament's political direction into coherent organizational strategy while ensuring seamless institutional functioning across all operational domains. Unit 01 operationalizes its mandate through eight core activity streams across the institution's administrative architecture, encompassing specialized advisory services to senior leadership and Members of Parliament, comprehensive management of institutional operations and document systems, strategic organizational planning and development, institutional representation within governance bodies and interinstitutional forums, and fiduciary continuity-of-operations functions. The unit exercises delegated authorizing officer responsibilities and maintains interim Secretary General capacity, while remaining flexible for other assigned institutional missions. Through its seven subordinate operational units (01-01, 01-30, 01-40, 01A, 01B, 01C, and three additional units), Unit 01 operationalizes these strategic mandates and functions as a specialized Direction-level command center enabling the institution's legislative, administrative, and strategic objectives. This organizational structure ensures both forward-looking strategic perspective management and immediate operational responsiveness across all areas of institutional competence.
01.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Advisory and Legislative Governance** (25%): Provision of comprehensive specialized counsel to the Secretary General, President, Conference of Presidents, and Members of the European Parliament on all institutional competency areas, including legislative programming and Better Law-Making agenda implementation, with support for Deputy Secretary General-delegated responsibilities

• **Institutional Operations and Document Management** (25%): Organization, modernization, and operational oversight of all Plenary activities, Protocol procedures, legislative acts processing, and comprehensive document management systems ensuring institutional continuity, procedural integrity, and seamless administrative functioning

• **Strategic Management and Organizational Development** (15%): Strategic-level organization of general management functions, including institutional objective formulation, resource allocation definition, means specification, and identification of organizational development and improvement opportunities

• **Institutional Representation and Interinstitutional Relations** (20%): Representation of general management within European Parliament governance structures, joint committees, and inter-directorate coordination mechanisms (10%); representation of general management and the institution in interinstitutional forums, joint committees, and formal negotiations (10%)

• **Fiduciary, Continuity, and Discretionary Functions** (15%): Exercise of delegated authorizing officer authority (5%); assumption of interim Secretary General responsibilities and institutional representation capability when required (5%); execution of other assigned institutional missions as directed (5%)

original_activities: Assist the Deputy Secretary General / Director General of the Presidency in the exercise of his duties and in the management and monitoring of the activities of the various services of the general management.
01-01.refined_description: Unit 01-01 operates as the Executive Support and Strategic Coordination Office within Directorate-General 01 of the European Parliament, functioning as a first-level Direction and leaf node within the organizational hierarchy. This unit is exclusively dedicated to providing comprehensive administrative, operational, and strategic support to the Deputy Secretary General and/or Director General of the Presidency, encompassing 100% of its documented workload allocation. Unit 01-01 serves as the institutional nexus through which senior leadership maintains executive authority and institutional effectiveness, accomplishing this through dual integrated functions: direct assistance in the exercise of constitutional duties and responsibilities, and active management, coordination, supervision, and performance monitoring of activities across the multiple specialized services and departments that constitute the Parliament's general management apparatus. By consolidating these executive support functions at the apex administrative level, the unit enables senior leadership to maintain strategic oversight and operational coherence across the organizational portfolio, ensuring seamless integration between the Presidency's executive priorities and the implementation capacity of subordinate administrative services. Unit 01-01 operates in structural coordination with its sibling Direction units (01-30, 01-40, 01A, 01B, 01C, and three additional peer units), all reporting to the Secretary General, thereby contributing to the integrated functioning of the Parliament's command and control infrastructure.
01-01.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Support and Constitutional Duty Assistance** (100%): Provision of comprehensive administrative, operational, and strategic support to the Deputy Secretary General and/or Director General of the Presidency in the exercise of all assigned executive duties, institutional responsibilities, and constitutional functions

• **General Services Management, Coordination, and Oversight** (100%): Management, coordination, supervision, and continuous performance monitoring of activities, operations, and service delivery functions across the various specialized services and departments within the Parliament's general management structure

• **Strategic Institutional Coherence and Operational Control** (100%): Facilitation of strategic alignment and operational coherence between senior leadership priorities and the implementation activities of subordinate administrative services, ensuring integrated policy implementation and institutional effectiveness across the administrative apparatus

**Primary Focus:** All activities (100% allocation) concentrate exclusively on high-level executive support and cross-organizational management coordination functions within the apex administrative structure.

original_activities: Ensure the organisation of the regular and additional Conference of Presidents meetings (as well as of related preparatory meetings) in Brussels, Strasbourg and countries holding the European Union Council Presidency). Prepare and follow up of agendas, dossiers, notes, minutes, letters, etc.
Serve as competence center for colleagues in the various Directorates-General on procedures and matters falling under the remit of the Conference of Presidents.
Ensure the handling of questions concerning the Rules of Procedure within the competences of the Conference of Presidents as well as related sensitive political questions.
Contribute to the elaboration and adoption of relevant rules (Rules of Procedure, implementing measures).
Others.
01-30.refined_description: Unit 01-30 functions as the dedicated secretariat, administrative support office, and procedural competence center for the Conference of Presidents within the European Parliament's apex strategic command structure, operating under the direct authority of the Secretary General through Directorate-General 01. As a first-level directional unit, it bears primary responsibility for the comprehensive organizational management and substantive support of the Conference of Presidents—the institution's highest collegial decision-making body—which constitutes the core of its operational mission. The unit organizes and manages all regular and extraordinary Conference of Presidents meetings convened across multiple venues (Brussels, Strasbourg, and countries holding the rotating European Union Council Presidency), encompassing complete preparation and follow-up activities including agenda development, policy dossier compilation, analytical note preparation, preparatory meeting coordination, official minutes documentation, and formal correspondence. Beyond its primary secretariat function, Unit 01-30 operates as an institutional competence center providing expert procedural guidance and substantive advisory support to colleagues across all Directorates-General on matters and procedures falling within the Conference of Presidents' organizational and decision-making authority. The unit additionally manages sensitive procedural and political questions concerning the Rules of Procedure within the Conference of Presidents' competencies while actively contributing to the elaboration, amendment, and formal adoption of relevant institutional rules and implementing measures. Through these integrated activities, Unit 01-30 ensures the effective functioning of the Conference of Presidents as a critical inter-institutional coordination mechanism while maintaining institutional procedural integrity and supporting the broader governance objectives of the European Parliament.
01-30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Conference of Presidents Meeting Organization and Secretariat Support** (60%): Comprehensive organizational management of regular and extraordinary Conference of Presidents meetings convened in Brussels, Strasbourg, and countries holding the rotating European Union Council Presidency; complete preparation and follow-up of agendas, policy dossiers, analytical notes, preparatory meeting coordination, official minutes, correspondence, and all associated administrative materials and documentation.

• **Institutional Advisory and Competence Center Functions** (20%): Operation as a specialized competence center providing expert procedural guidance and substantive advisory support to colleagues across all Directorates-General regarding procedures, policies, and institutional matters falling under the Conference of Presidents' organizational and decision-making authority.

• **Rules of Procedure and Sensitive Governance Matters** (10%): Specialized handling and expert management of procedural questions and sensitive political matters concerning the Rules of Procedure within the Conference of Presidents' competencies, requiring institutional discretion and procedural expertise.

• **Regulatory Development and Institutional Rule-Making** (5%): Active contribution to the elaboration, development, and formal adoption of relevant institutional rules, Rules of Procedure amendments, and associated implementing measures.

• **Ancillary Operational Functions** (5%): Performance of miscellaneous operational and administrative activities supporting the Conference of Presidents' operational effectiveness and the unit's broader institutional objectives.

original_activities: Coordinate the horizontal and strategic aspects of the Directorates-General activities and projects, including in areas relating to simplification.
Prepare the Directorates-General strategic documents, in particular the annual activity reports and work programmes.
Assist the Director-General in specific projects, tasks and administrative requirements.
Support the management in the implementation of organisational change, as well as in the introduction of new tools and innovative working methods.
01-40.refined_description: Unit 01-40 operates as a strategic coordination and support directorate functioning as a critical institutional bridge within the European Parliament's apex administrative structure, reporting to Unit 01 (the Directorate-General apex) while serving as an essential intermediary between the Director-General and the broader Directorates-General organizational framework. The unit's integrated mission encompasses four interconnected domains of institutional governance and strategic management that collectively ensure organizational coherence, strategic alignment, and responsive executive capacity across the institution's operational landscape. At its core (60% combined allocation), Unit 01-40 coordinates the horizontal and strategic dimensions of all Directorates-General activities and projects—including those relating to simplification initiatives designed to enhance operational efficiency—while simultaneously preparing and maintaining essential strategic documentation, particularly annual activity reports and comprehensive work programmes that establish organizational priorities and accountability frameworks. The unit further dedicates 25% of its workload to providing direct administrative and project-based support to the Director-General, addressing specific institutional requirements, specialized tasks, and high-priority executive initiatives that require centralized coordination. Additionally, Unit 01-40 allocates 15% of its resources to supporting and facilitating management-level implementation of organizational change initiatives, enabling the introduction of innovative tools and modern working methodologies, thereby positioning itself as a catalyst for institutional modernization and operational excellence. Functioning as a leaf node alongside sibling units (01-01, 01-30, 01A, 01B, 01C, and three additional units) within the hierarchical structure, Unit 01-40 collectively delivers the comprehensive strategic and operational governance framework essential to the European Parliament's institutional functioning.
01-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Coordination and Simplification (30%)**: Coordinate horizontal and strategic dimensions of all Directorates-General activities and projects with explicit focus on simplification-related initiatives that enhance operational efficiency and institutional coherence across the organization

• **Strategic Documentation and Planning (30%)**: Prepare, develop, and maintain essential strategic institutional documents including annual activity reports and comprehensive work programmes that establish organizational priorities, guide institutional direction, and provide accountability frameworks for the Directorates-General

• **Executive Support Services (25%)**: Provide specialized administrative and project-based support directly to the Director-General, addressing specific institutional requirements, high-priority executive initiatives, specialized tasks, and administrative needs requiring centralized coordination at the apex level

• **Organizational Change Management and Innovation Implementation (15%)**: Support and facilitate management-led implementation of organizational transformation initiatives, enable introduction of innovative operational tools and modern working methodologies, and advance institutional modernization to enhance operational effectiveness

01A.name: 01A
original_activities: Supervise the preparation and distribution of plenary session agendas.

Supervise the organization and monitoring of plenary sessions: ensure presidential turns and assistance in plenary; prepare and manage votes on texts submitted to plenary; publish the minutes, the Election Report (CRE) and the voting results; transmit the adopted texts.

Oversee the secretarial functions of the Advisory Committee on the Conduct of Members of the European Parliament and provide administrative support regarding their mandate, transparency and integrity obligations, parliamentary immunities, sanctions and requests for certificates.
Supervise the management and monitoring related to the receipt and return of official documents to the relevant parliamentary committees, including legislative and non-legislative texts.
Supervise the management of written and oral questions, interpellations and proposals for Union acts.
Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of Directorate A.
01A.refined_description: Unit 01A functions as the strategic Direction-level command center within the European Parliament's apex administrative structure, operating under direct oversight of the Secretary General (via Unit 01) and exercising supervisory authority over six specialized operational units (01A10, 01A20, 01A40, 01A50, 01A70, and 01A80) that collectively operate forty activities. The unit maintains a comprehensive portfolio encompassing six core functional domains representing 100% of its organizational workload, positioning it as the essential nexus between parliamentary governance mechanisms and the broader institutional framework. Unit 01A executes dual supervisory responsibilities for plenary session management through the preparation and distribution of plenary agendas and the comprehensive organization and monitoring of plenary sessions, encompassing presidential protocol management, plenary assistance provision, vote preparation and management on submitted texts, publication of minutes and Electoral Reports (CRE), dissemination of voting results, and transmission of adopted legislative texts to relevant recipients. The unit concurrently exercises significant institutional oversight responsibilities through its management of the Advisory Committee on the Conduct of Members, providing comprehensive administrative support for mandate execution, transparency and integrity compliance, parliamentary immunity matters, sanctions administration, and certificate issuance. Additionally, Unit 01A supervises the complete lifecycle of official parliamentary document receipt, management, and return to relevant committees across both legislative and non-legislative texts, manages the full administrative processing of written and oral questions, interpellations, and Union act proposals, and provides strategic direction, coordination, and animation of all subordinate units and services within Directorate A. As a Level 1 Direction, Unit 01A operates as a coordinated peer within the broader sibling unit network (01-01, 01-30, 01-40, 01B, 01C, and three additional units), ensuring integrated operational performance across the Parliament's apex administrative structure.
01A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Plenary Session Governance and Operations Management** (40%): Supervision of plenary agenda preparation and distribution (20%); comprehensive organization and monitoring of plenary sessions including presidential protocol management, plenary assistance provision, vote preparation and management on submitted texts, publication of minutes and Electoral Reports (CRE), dissemination of voting results, and transmission of adopted legislative texts to relevant recipients (20%)

• **Parliamentary Integrity and Conduct Oversight** (20%): Oversight and administrative support for the Advisory Committee on the Conduct of Members of the European Parliament, encompassing mandate administration, transparency and integrity obligations management, parliamentary immunities administration, sanctions procedures, and certificate issuance

• **Legislative Document and Parliamentary Questions Management** (28%): Supervision of official document receipt, management, and return protocols for both legislative and non-legislative texts distributed to parliamentary committees (16%); management of the complete administrative lifecycle of written and oral questions, interpellations, and proposals for Union acts (12%)

• **Directorate-Level Strategic Leadership and Coordination** (12%): Direction, coordination, and animation of six subordinate units (01A10, 01A20, 01A40, 01A50, 01A70, 01A80) and all services within Directorate A to ensure integrated operational effectiveness, strategic alignment, and coordinated performance across functional domains

01A10.name: 01A10
original_activities: Design, manage and control the CRE of the plenaries published gradually on Europarl and the next day in paper version 'Arc-en-ciel' and in electronic version on Epades, Europarl and Register of documents. Coordinate with the services of the General Directorate of Translation the transcription and revision work of the CRE. Check and insert the written declarations (article 178.13 of the regulations). Check the written EdVs and publish them on each Member's page on Europarl.

Write the minutes (PV) of the sessions (includes annexes: results of votes, RCV, attendance lists), in French which, after translation into all official languages, are available online (Epades, Europarl, Register of documents). Coordinate with the services of the General Directorate of Translation the translation work of the minutes. Collate the minutes and prepare the version published in electronic form in the Official Journal.

Contribute to projects aimed at replacing obsolete applications used by the unit (ACTA project) with the aim of improving access to the proceedings of the Plenary (Bespoke). Identify improvements and make necessary changes to the Verba application, following its release into production in September 2024.
Ensure the parallelism and complementarity of the PV, CRE and VOD during collation, revision and control. Participate in the evolutionary maintenance of applications.

Design, manage and monitor the CRE of debates in other meetings (statement of the European Central Bank before the ECON committee, hearings of designated commissioners, commissions of inquiry). Prepare the minutes in French of the Conference of Presidents open to Members.
Answer questions from Members, assistants, officials and citizens regarding the PV, the CRE and the VOD. Train the staff of the General Directorate of Translation in PV and CRE applications.
Feed and control the VOD-VERBA index during sessions. Ensure control and post-editing of the VOD after the session.
01A10.refined_description: Unit 01A10 operates as the European Parliament's central operational hub for the complete lifecycle management, production, and dissemination of official parliamentary records, functioning as a specialized Level 2 unit within Direction 01A. The unit bears primary responsibility for designing, managing, and controlling the Compte Rendu Étendu (CRE—Complete Record of Events) of plenary sessions, which are published progressively across multiple platforms including Europarl, the paper-based "Arc-en-ciel" publication, and electronic versions via Epades and the Register of Documents, whilst simultaneously authoring the Procès-Verbal (PV—official minutes) of parliamentary sessions in French with comprehensive annexes containing voting results, recorded votes (RCV), and attendance documentation, subsequently translated into all official languages and made available across Epades, Europarl, the Register of Documents, and the Official Journal in electronic form, collectively representing 72% of operational capacity. The unit maintains critical coordination relationships with the General Directorate of Translation services to oversee transcription, revision, and translation workflows, and manages the publication of written declarations (article 178.13 of regulations) and written editorial versions (EdVs) on Members' Europarl pages. Unit 01A10 contributes substantially to digital modernization initiatives through participation in the ACTA project aimed at replacing obsolete applications and enhancing access to plenary proceedings via the Bespoke system, whilst identifying improvements and implementing necessary modifications to the Verba application following its production release in September 2024. The unit ensures operational coherence and complementarity across the trilogy of parliamentary documentation—minutes (PV), complete records (CRE), and video-on-demand (VOD)—during collation, revision, and quality control phases, manages CRE documentation for specialized parliamentary forums including European Central Bank statements before the ECON committee, commissioner hearings, commissions of inquiry, and Conference of Presidents sessions (with French-language minutes preparation for the latter), provides comprehensive user support to Members, parliamentary assistants, officials, and citizens regarding parliamentary documentation access and functionality, and maintains operational responsibility for feeding, controlling, and post-editing VOD-VERBA indexing during and following sessions. Collectively, these activities position Unit 01A10 as an indispensable operational support mechanism ensuring the integrity, accessibility, and modernization of the European Parliament's documentary and record management infrastructure.
01A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Parliamentary Proceedings Documentation & Publication** (72%): Design, management, and control of the Compte Rendu Étendu (CRE) of plenary sessions with progressive publication across Europarl, paper "Arc-en-ciel," Epades, and the Register of Documents; authoring and production of the Procès-Verbal (PV—official minutes) in French with comprehensive annexes (voting results, recorded votes, attendance documentation) followed by translation into all official languages and publication across Epades, Europarl, Register of Documents, and the Official Journal; coordination of all transcription, revision, and translation workflows with the General Directorate of Translation services; management of written declarations (article 178.13) and written editorial versions (EdVs) on Members' Europarl pages.

• **Digital Systems Modernization & Application Management** (11%): Contribution to the ACTA project for replacing obsolete applications and enhancing access to plenary proceedings through the Bespoke system; identification and implementation of improvements to the Verba application following its September 2024 production release; participation in evolutionary maintenance of parliamentary record applications; feeding, controlling, and post-editing of VOD-VERBA indexing during live sessions and post-session control phases.

• **Specialized Parliamentary Documentation** (6%): Design, management, and monitoring of CRE documentation for non-plenary parliamentary forums including European Central Bank statements before the ECON committee, designated commissioner hearings, commissions of inquiry, and Conference of Presidents sessions; preparation of French-language minutes for Conference of Presidents meetings open to Members.

• **Records Coherence & Quality Assurance** (7%): Ensuring parallelism and complementarity across the trilogy of parliamentary documentation—minutes (PV), complete records (CRE), and video-on-demand (VOD)—during collation, revision, and control phases; participation in evolutionary maintenance of supporting applications to sustain operational integrity.

• **User Support & Training** (4%): Responding to inquiries from Members, parliamentary assistants, officials, and citizens regarding parliamentary records (PV, CRE, and VOD) access and functionality; providing specialized training to General Directorate of Translation personnel on PV and CRE applications and systems.

01A20.name: 01A20
original_activities: Manage questions with request for written response (priority and non-priority) to the Commission and the Council, the President of the European Council and the Vice-President/High Representative, including processing of corresponding responses; ensure the QP+ application is updated.

Check the admissibility of questions with a request for a written response, ensuring in particular compliance with data protection; advise the authors and ensure monitoring of the circuit of decisions concerned.

Check the precedents of questions requiring written answers, questions with answers already available from the European Parliament's research services and follow the entire associated decision-making process.

Manage questions with a request for an oral response followed by a debate, addressed to the Council, the Commission and the Vice-President/High Representative; verify admissibility; ensure the QP+ application is updated.

Monitor the development of computer applications used for the management of written and oral questions, major interpellations and proposals for Union acts; prepare statistics regarding parliamentary matters; propose improvements to procedures, including regulatory ones; participate in various working groups; carry out management and secretarial tasks for the Unit.
Manage proposals for Union acts: check admissibility; advise the authors and ensure the monitoring of the circuit of related decisions.

Manage major arrests: check admissibility; advise the authors and ensure monitoring of the circuit of corresponding decisions; update the QP+ application; monitor and manage written responses and their possible inclusion on the agenda.
01A20.refined_description: Unit 01A20 operates as a specialized parliamentary procedures and legislative proposals management unit within Direction 01A of the European Parliament, functioning as the critical administrative hub for processing and administering parliamentary instruments directed toward the Commission, Council, President of the European Council, and Vice-President/High Representative. The unit's comprehensive operational mandate encompasses the complete lifecycle management of written questions (30% of workload)—including priority and non-priority submissions, response processing, and QP+ application maintenance—coupled with rigorous admissibility verification (30% of workload) that prioritizes data protection compliance, provides substantive author guidance, and maintains oversight of decision-making circuits. The unit systematically conducts precedent analysis and research coordination (15% of workload), cross-referencing questions against existing European Parliament research materials while managing associated decision workflows, and administers oral questions with debate provisions (12% of workload) directed to the Council, Commission, and Vice-President/High Representative, including admissibility verification and application record maintenance. Additionally, Unit 01A20 assumes responsibility for digital infrastructure stewardship (10% of workload), monitoring and developing computer applications governing written and oral question management, major interpellations, and Union act proposals; generating parliamentary statistics; recommending procedural and regulatory improvements; and executing essential management and secretarial functions. The unit further processes Union act proposals (2% of workload) and major interpellations (1% of workload) through comprehensive admissibility assessment, author consultation, decision circuit monitoring, application updates, response management, and potential agenda inclusion coordination. As a leaf-node unit operating alongside sibling units 01A10, 01A40, 01A50, 01A70, and 01A80 within Direction 01A, Unit 01A20 directly supports the European Parliament's democratic accountability mechanisms by ensuring systematic, compliant, and transparent processing of legislative instruments and parliamentary questions.
01A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Written Questions Management and Administration** (30%): Comprehensive lifecycle management of written response questions to the Commission, Council, President of the European Council, and Vice-President/High Representative, encompassing priority and non-priority submissions, response processing, QP+ application maintenance and updates

• **Admissibility Verification and Legal Compliance** (30%): Systematic admissibility checking of written questions with particular emphasis on data protection compliance; substantive author advisory services; decision-making circuit monitoring and oversight

• **Research Coordination and Precedent Analysis** (15%): Examination of precedent cases for written answer questions; cross-referencing against existing European Parliament research materials; comprehensive management of complete decision-making processes for established precedents

• **Oral Questions and Debate Administration** (12%): Management of oral response questions with debate provisions submitted to Council, Commission, and Vice-President/High Representative; admissibility verification; QP+ application updates and record maintenance

• **Digital Systems Stewardship, Parliamentary Analytics, and Procedural Development** (10%): Monitoring and development of computer applications for written and oral question management, major interpellations, and Union act proposals; parliamentary statistics generation; procedural and regulatory improvement proposals; participation in cross-functional working groups; unit management and secretarial operations

• **Union Act Proposals and Major Interpellations Processing** (3%): Union act proposal management including admissibility assessment, author consultation, and decision circuit monitoring (2%); major interpellation administration including admissibility verification, substantive author guidance, response management, application updates, and potential agenda inclusion coordination (1%)

01A40.name: 01A40
original_activities: Planning and agendas for plenary sessions: ensure contacts with political groups, deputies, other services of the Secretariat, other European Institutions and bodies; plan of the hemicycle; prepare the timetable for session periods; manage the pronunciation guide; establish the rotation of session chairs as well as that of assistants to session chairs; respond to requests regarding the progress of the Plenary.

Management of the Plenary: prepare speaking time, lists of speakers, one-minute interventions, explanations of vote, announcements by the President in collaboration with other services; assist the session chairs; prepare session files; ensure the management of access badges to the hemicycle; organize the elections of the President, Vice-Presidents, Quaestors and Mediator; ensure the management of Plenary ceremonies and solemn sessions.

Monitoring session periods: handling questions about the progress of sessions, analyzing problems, defining the follow-up to be given to interventions in collaboration with the President's Office, analyzing precedents. Statistical monitoring of sessions (reports, amendments, resolutions, declarations from the Council and the Commission, other interventions). In cooperation with other units, ensure the notification, monitoring and dissemination of texts adopted by the European Parliament.

Information policy on the Plenary: ensure the provision and monitoring of the Plenary website in collaboration with DG ITEC; coordinate the contributions of the different information providing services; respond to any request for internal or external information and advice. Provide information on the activities and organization of the Plenary, particularly in the context of internal and external training.

Prepare and organize meetings of the Conference of Presidents open to all MPs. Participate in various working groups (collection of models, document management, implementation of the Rules, IT applications, web accessibility task force, etc.), as well as in the development of IT applications (list of speakers, tracking of proceedings, ELVIIS, PPMS - management of session precedents, management of explanations of vote, etc.).
Internal management: provide specific training linked to the needs of the unit, organize internal meetings, update databases; support the trainees.
01A40.refined_description:
Unit 01A40 functions as the operational command center and essential administrative foundation of the European Parliament's Plenary Session administration, serving as a critical second-level operational unit within Direction 01A's governance framework. The unit maintains a comprehensive dual-focused mandate encompassing strategic planning, real-time management, continuous monitoring, and institutional coordination functions that collectively ensure the Parliament's plenary operations maintain procedural integrity, institutional transparency, and administrative efficiency. Through integrated coordination with political groups, parliamentary deputies, Secretariat services, and external European Institutions, Unit 01A40 orchestrates hemicycle logistics, session timetabling, pronunciation protocols, chair rotation systems, and responsive communication mechanisms that establish the operational foundation for all plenary proceedings. The unit simultaneously operationalizes these strategic plans through real-time management of speaking time allocation, speaker lists, one-minute interventions, voting explanations, presidential announcements, session chair support, badge access administration, and the organization of institutional elections for the President, Vice-Presidents, Quaestors, and Mediator, alongside ceremonial and solemn session coordination. Complementing these core operational functions, Unit 01A40 dedicates substantial capacity to continuous session monitoring and statistical analysis—tracking procedural issues, documenting amendments and resolutions, managing notifications of adopted texts in cooperation with peer units, and maintaining institutional precedent analysis—while simultaneously managing information dissemination through website provision in collaboration with DG ITEC, coordinating information service contributions, and providing internal and external advisory services. The unit additionally participates in strategic institutional coordination through Conference of Presidents organization and cross-functional working groups addressing document management, Rules implementation, IT applications (including speaker lists, proceedings tracking, ELVIIS, PPMS systems), and web accessibility, while maintaining internal organizational capacity through specialized staff training, database maintenance, and trainee professional development. Through these integrated activities, Unit 01A40 maintains essential alignment with sibling units (01A10, 01A20, 01A50, 01A70, 01A80) within Direction 01A's hierarchical structure.

---

##
01A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Plenary Session Planning and Strategic Coordination** (30%): Comprehensive establishment of session agendas, hemicycle configurations, and timetables; strategic coordination with political groups, parliamentary deputies, Secretariat services, and external European Institutions; management of pronunciation guides and protocols; establishment of session chair and assistant rotation systems; responsive communication regarding plenary progress, request handling, and logistical coordination

• **Active Plenary Operations and Session Management** (30%): Real-time administration of speaking time preparation and allocation; preparation and management of speaker lists and one-minute interventions; drafting of voting explanations and presidential announcements; direct session chair support and session file preparation; hemicycle badge access administration and control; organization of institutional elections for President, Vice-Presidents, Quaestors, and Mediator; coordination of ceremonial and solemn session proceedings

• **Session Monitoring, Statistical Analysis, and Documentation** (20%): Continuous monitoring and tracking of session progress; analysis of procedural issues and problem identification; coordination with President's Office regarding intervention follow-up; precedent analysis and institutional memory documentation; statistical monitoring and comprehensive reporting on amendments, resolutions, and Council and Commission declarations; notification, monitoring, and dissemination of adopted parliamentary texts in cooperation with peer units

• **Information Management and External Communication** (10%): Plenary website provision, maintenance, and monitoring in collaboration with DG ITEC; coordination of information service contributions; provision of internal and external information and advisory services regarding plenary activities and organizational structures; training support on plenary operations and institutional organization

• **Institutional Coordination and Systems Development** (5%): Conference of Presidents meeting preparation and organization; participation in cross-functional working groups addressing document management, Rules implementation, IT applications (speaker lists systems, proceedings tracking, ELVIIS, PPMS, voting explanation systems), and web accessibility; development and enhancement of specialized parliamentary IT applications

• **Internal Administrative and Human Resource Management** (5%): Unit-specific specialized staff training aligned with operational requirements; internal meeting coordination and organization; database maintenance and periodic updates; trainee support and professional development programming

01A50.name: 01A50
original_activities: Prepare voting lists in plenary session: receive requests for separate votes, by division and by roll call; brief and assist the session chairs.
Manage the official tabling of amendments and resolution proposals: check, record, send for translation and ensure follow-up.
Support and advise the President and Members of the European Parliament, committees and political groups on all procedural matters.
Manage the official tabling of reports adopted by parliamentary committees: check, record, send for translation and ensure follow-up.
Check draft reports for compliance with parliamentary templates and advise authors.

Participate in inter-service working groups (IT projects, regulations, Collection of Models) and in training activities aimed at Members and their assistants, political groups and the general secretariat.
Develop statistics on some of the activities listed above.
01A50.refined_description: Unit 01A50 functions as the European Parliament's central procedural operations and legislative documentation management unit, operating as a specialized administrative interface within Direction 01A's governance infrastructure. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the full spectrum of parliamentary legislative processes, from the preparation and administration of plenary voting procedures through the formal tabling, verification, and tracking of legislative instruments and reports. Serving as a critical functional node coordinating with sibling units 01A10, 01A20, 01A40, 01A70, and 01A80, Unit 01A50 provides integrated procedural guidance and advisory services to the President, Members of the European Parliament, parliamentary committees, and political groups across all procedural matters. The unit simultaneously contributes to institutional modernization and evidence-based management through participation in inter-service working groups addressing IT infrastructure projects, regulatory frameworks, and institutional model development, while delivering capacity-building training activities and developing statistical analyses on core operational performance. All activities are executed in strict compliance with institutional standards and legislative pipeline requirements, ensuring seamless coordination between parliamentary bodies and stakeholders throughout the legislative process.
01A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Voting Procedures and Plenary Session Management** (25%): Preparation and administration of voting lists during plenary sessions, including receipt and processing of requests for separate votes, divisions, and roll-call voting procedures; provision of briefing materials and direct operational assistance to session chairs during legislative sessions.

• **Legislative Document Tabling and Processing** (37%): Official tabling management of amendments and resolution proposals (22%), encompassing documentation verification, formal recording, translation coordination, and comprehensive follow-up procedures; official tabling management of committee-adopted reports (15%), including compliance verification, registration, translation services coordination, and administrative tracking to ensure institutional compliance and legislative continuity.

• **Procedural Advisory and Guidance Services** (28%): Provision of specialized procedural counsel to the President, Members of the European Parliament, parliamentary committees, and political groups on all procedural and legislative process matters (20%); template compliance review of draft reports against established parliamentary standards and provision of technical guidance to document authors (8%).

• **Institutional Development, Training, and Analytics** (10%): Participation in inter-service working groups focused on information technology infrastructure projects, regulatory frameworks, and institutional model development (5%); delivery of training and capacity-building activities for Members, parliamentary assistants, political groups, and general secretariat personnel; development of statistical analyses and performance metrics on core operational activities (5%).

01A70.name: 01A70
original_activities: Register and refer to the competent parliamentary committee(s) legislative, budgetary and non-legislative official documents received from other European Union institutions and bodies, national Parliaments, and Members (motions for resolution pursuant to Rule 149 of European Parliament Rules of Procedure).
Assess citizens' submissions and register petitions pursuant to Art. 227 TFEU, and in accordance with operating procedures, and transmit them to the Committee on Petitions.
Refer to the relevant committees delegated acts and implementing acts and measures, as well as other comitology related documents, and participate in the DIA network.
Maintain and develop the Legislative Observatory (OEIL) and contribute to the improvement of digital tools supporting parliamentary work.
Contribute to the development of the inter-institutional website EU Law-tracker.
01A70.refined_description: Unit 01A70 functions as the European Parliament's central document management and parliamentary procedure coordination hub, serving as a critical operational component within Direction 01A. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 01A70 coordinates with sibling units (01A10, 01A20, 01A40, 01A50, and 01A80) within Direction 01A's integrated administrative framework to facilitate comprehensive parliamentary workflow and institutional transparency. The unit's primary mission encompasses the systematic intake, classification, registration, and procedural routing of all legislative, budgetary, and non-legislative official documents flowing through the Parliament from European Union institutions, bodies, national Parliaments, and Members to competent parliamentary committees—allocating 50% of its workload to this core operational priority, which includes processing motions for resolution pursuant to Rule 149 of the European Parliament Rules of Procedure. Unit 01A70 administers the formal citizen petition process under Article 227 TFEU by assessing submissions and registering petitions for transmission to the Committee on Petitions (20% of workload), thereby ensuring direct democratic participation mechanisms operate effectively within the Parliament's institutional framework. The unit manages the Parliament's engagement with delegated and implementing acts through comitology documentation referral and active participation in the DIA network (15% of workload), maintaining inter-institutional coordination across the European regulatory framework. Unit 01A70 maintains operational responsibility for the Legislative Observatory (OEIL) system and contributes to the enhancement of digital parliamentary tools and infrastructure (10% of workload), while simultaneously collaborating on the development and maintenance of the inter-institutional EU Law-tracker website (5% of workload). Collectively, these integrated activities position Unit 01A70 as an essential facilitator of parliamentary workflow, institutional transparency, regulatory coordination, and digital infrastructure supporting the Parliament's legislative and administrative functions.
01A70.refined_activity_summary:
• **Document Registration and Parliamentary Referral** (50%): Systematic intake, classification, and registration of legislative, budgetary, and non-legislative official documents received from EU institutions, bodies, national Parliaments, and Members; procedural routing to competent parliamentary committees; processing of motions for resolution pursuant to Rule 149 of European Parliament Rules of Procedure

• **Citizens' Petitions and Submissions Management** (20%): Assessment and registration of citizens' submissions and formal petitions pursuant to Article 227 TFEU in accordance with established operating procedures; transmission of validated petitions to the Committee on Petitions for institutional processing

• **Comitology and Delegated Acts Coordination** (15%): Referral of delegated acts, implementing acts, measures, and comitology-related documentation to relevant committees; active participation in the DIA (Delegated and Implementing Acts) network for coordinated inter-institutional regulatory oversight

• **Digital Infrastructure and Parliamentary Tools Development** (10-15%): Maintenance and systematic development of the Legislative Observatory (OEIL) digital platform; contribution to improvement of digital tools and infrastructure supporting parliamentary work; collaborative development of the inter-institutional EU Law-tracker website (5%)

01A80.name: 01A80
original_activities: Manage Members' declarations of private interests, declarations of assets and conflicts of interest, the gift register and invitations to events organized by third parties, within the framework of the Code of Conduct for Members of the European Parliament. Monitor compliance with the rules. Provide training, assistance and advice to deputies and their assistants.
Ensure the Secretariat of the Advisory Committee on the conduct of deputies (organization of meetings, preparation of files, follow-up to decisions taken by the Committee).

Receive and ensure the procedures for recording requests for lifting and defense of parliamentary immunity (information of the MP concerned, preparation of the announcement in session, transmission of the file to the Legal Affairs Committee).
Establish passes. Assist MPs in the procedures with the Belgian Administration to obtain the special identity card. Manage the personal data of MPs and their curriculum vitae.

Prepare the procedures relating to the start and end of the mandate of MEPs (establishment of resignation minutes and transmission to the JURI, announcement during the meeting, information to the services, contact with the competent national authorities, preparation of the file relating to the verification of the credentials of incoming MEPs).

Manage the composition of parliamentary committees and delegations (announcements during sessions, contacts with political groups and secretariats of committees and delegations, registration in MADIS).
Manage and save meeting posts. Manage the register of intergroups and unofficial groups.
Ensure the secretariat of the interinstitutional body of ethical standards (organization of meetings, preparation of files).
01A80.refined_description: Unit 01A80 functions as the European Parliament's specialized administrative unit responsible for managing the comprehensive legal, ethical, and procedural compliance framework governing Members of the European Parliament (MEPs). Operating as a second-level operational unit within Direction 01A, it serves as the critical institutional mechanism ensuring compliance with parliamentary governance standards, administrative protocols, and institutional integrity across all conduct, immunity, and membership administration matters. The unit maintains essential liaison relationships with sibling units 01A10, 01A20, 01A40, 01A50, and 01A70 within the parent Direction 01A structure. Through five integrated functional domains encompassing ethical conduct oversight, governance committee support, parliamentary immunity administration, Members' administrative status management, and mandate lifecycle/parliamentary composition management, Unit 01A80 ensures institutional transparency, proper immunity procedures, accurate membership records, and rigorous ethical standards while providing comprehensive administrative support to all MEPs and their support staff. The unit's operational framework integrates both top-down hierarchical accountability and bottom-up administrative synthesis to deliver seamless compliance monitoring, procedural administration, and institutional governance across the full spectrum of parliamentary conduct and membership administration responsibilities.
01A80.refined_activity_summary:
• **Ethical Conduct, Governance & Transparency Management** (20%): Administration of Members' declarations of private interests, asset declarations, conflict-of-interest documentation, gift registers, and invitations from third parties within the Code of Conduct framework; provision of comprehensive training, assistance, and advisory services to deputies and their assistants regarding compliance obligations and ethical standards

• **Governance Committee Support & Institutional Oversight** (20%): Secretariat operations for the Advisory Committee on the conduct of deputies, encompassing meeting organization, file preparation, and decision implementation (15%); secretariat support for interinstitutional ethical standards body with equivalent meeting and file management responsibilities (5%)

• **Parliamentary Immunity Administration & Legal Procedures** (15%): Reception, processing, and management of requests for lifting and defense of parliamentary immunity; notification of affected Members; preparation of session announcements; transmission of files to the Legal Affairs Committee; coordination with competent authorities on all related procedural documentation

• **Members' Administrative Status, Credentials & Documentation Management** (15%): Establishment of parliamentary access passes; coordination with Belgian Administration for special identity card acquisition; management of MEP mandate commencement and termination procedures including resignation documentation, JURI transmission, credentials verification of incoming members, and liaison with national authorities; maintenance and management of comprehensive personal data, curriculum vitae records, and identity documentation for all Members

• **Parliamentary Composition & Institutional Registry Management** (15%): Administration of parliamentary committee and delegation composition through coordination with political groups, session announcements, and MADIS registration systems; management and maintenance of meeting post allocation records and archiving; administration of intergroup and unofficial group registry maintenance

01B.name: 01B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination and animation of the units and services of the Legislative Acts Directorate.
Oversee the quality of legislative texts and the delivery of other legislative services, as well as legislative planning.
Perform ad hoc tasks at the request of the Office of the President, the Secretary General and the Deputy Secretary General.
Manage projects.
01B.refined_description: Unit 01B functions as the Direction-level strategic command and coordination center within the Legislative Acts Directorate of the European Parliament, operating at Hierarchy Level 1 and serving as the primary operational hub for all subordinate units and services within its domain. Positioned as the critical interface between senior institutional leadership and the specialized legislative support infrastructure, Unit 01B exercises supervisory authority over seven subordinate specialized units (01B05, 01B10, 01B20, 01B30, 01B40, 01B50, and one additional unit) while operating within the broader organizational framework of the parent strategic unit (01) and alongside multiple sibling directional units (01-01, 01-30, 01-40, 01A, 01C, and three additional units). The unit's core mission encompasses four interconnected functional areas that collectively represent its comprehensive operational scope: ensuring comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all units and services within the Legislative Acts Directorate; overseeing quality assurance and institutional standards of legislative texts while managing delivery of supplementary legislative services and coordinating legislative planning initiatives; executing ad hoc specialized tasks and assignments as formally requested by the Office of the President, the Secretary General, and the Deputy Secretary General; and managing organizational projects across the directorate. Through these functions, Unit 01B maintains institutional legislative standards, procedural efficiency, and consistency while serving as a responsive instrument for senior institutional leadership and enabling coordinated execution of strategic and operational initiatives throughout the directorate.
01B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction and Organizational Coordination** (50%): Ensure comprehensive direction, coordination, and animation of all units and services within the Legislative Acts Directorate, serving as the primary operational command center for all subordinate organizational entities and the directorate's functional activities.

• **Legislative Quality Assurance and Services Management** (25%): Oversee quality standards and institutional consistency of legislative texts, manage delivery of supplementary legislative services, and coordinate legislative planning activities to maintain procedural excellence and institutional standards.

• **Executive-Level Support and Ad Hoc Responsiveness** (15%): Execute specialized ad hoc tasks and assignments as formally requested by the Office of the President, the Secretary General, and the Deputy Secretary General, functioning as a responsive resource for senior institutional leadership.

• **Project Management** (10%): Manage organizational projects across the directorate, ensuring coordinated execution of strategic and operational initiatives.

01B05.name: 01B05
original_activities: Ensure ex ante the legislative quality of texts by establishing, identifying and documenting editorial and procedural rules, models and precedents to be used in management, as well as by providing colleagues with tailor-made training, recommendations and opinions.
Make models of parliamentary and legislative texts available to the services of the European Parliament, as well as advise these services on the application of these models.
Ensure and supervise the preparation, finalization and publication of the rules of procedure of the European Parliament, as well as amendments to these rules.

Contribute to inter-general directorate and interinstitutional work in areas falling within the unit and promote interinstitutional cooperation in legal matters; supervise the representation of management in the relevant bodies.
Ensure and supervise the publication in the Official Journal of texts relating to management.
Control and ensure ex post the legislative quality of texts (targeted verifications, corrections, monitoring of case law).
01B05.refined_description: Unit 01B05 functions as the Legislative Quality Assurance and Rules Management Unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, operating at the operational unit level (Hierarchy Level 2) as a leaf node without subordinate units. The unit bears primary responsibility for maintaining the highest standards of legislative drafting and procedural compliance across parliamentary documentation through a comprehensive, dual-phase quality control framework encompassing both ex ante verification mechanisms (implemented through standards development, training, and advisory services) and ex post verification mechanisms (implemented through targeted corrections, verifications, and case law monitoring). Operating as a peer to sibling units 01B10, 01B20, 01B30, 01B40, 01B50, and one additional unit, Unit 01B05 serves as a critical quality control and standards-setting function supporting the broader legislative documentation and procedural framework of the European Parliament. The unit's core mission encompasses five equally weighted primary activity clusters (100% of total workload), each representing 20% of operational capacity: establishing and documenting editorial and procedural rules, models, and precedents while providing colleagues with tailored training, recommendations, and specialized opinions to ensure ex ante legislative quality; making parliamentary and legislative text models available to European Parliament services and advising on their application to promote institutional consistency; ensuring comprehensive oversight of the preparation, finalization, and publication of the Rules of Procedure of the European Parliament alongside all amendments thereto; contributing to inter-general directorate and interinstitutional cooperation in legal matters while supervising management representation in relevant bodies to strengthen the Parliament's position within the EU institutional framework; and maintaining ex post legislative quality through targeted verifications, corrections, and case law monitoring while ensuring and supervising the publication of management-related texts in the Official Journal.
01B05.refined_activity_summary:
• **Ex Ante Legislative Quality Assurance & Institutional Standards Development** (20%): Establish, identify, and document comprehensive editorial and procedural rules, models, and precedents for parliamentary management; provide colleagues with tailored training, recommendations, and specialized opinions to ensure compliance with legislative quality standards before text finalization.

• **Legislative Text Model Management & Advisory Services** (20%): Develop, maintain, and make standardized parliamentary and legislative text models available across European Parliament services; provide specialized advisory guidance on the application and consistent implementation of these institutional models throughout the organization.

• **Rules of Procedure Governance & Administration** (20%): Ensure comprehensive oversight of the preparation, finalization, and publication of the Rules of Procedure of the European Parliament; manage all procedural amendments and regulatory updates to maintain the institutional framework governing parliamentary operations and legitimacy.

• **Interinstitutional Coordination & Legal Cooperation** (20%): Facilitate inter-general directorate collaboration and promote interinstitutional cooperation in legal matters within the unit's domain; supervise management representation in relevant interinstitutional bodies to strengthen the Parliament's position within the broader EU institutional framework.

• **Ex Post Quality Control & Official Journal Publication** (20%): Conduct targeted ex post legislative quality verifications, corrections, and case law monitoring to maintain documentary standards; ensure and supervise the publication of management-related texts in the Official Journal to maintain institutional transparency and compliance.

01B10.name: 01B10
original_activities: Coordinate IT projects, database and other technical support activities for the entire Directorate, as well as its representation in the context of eLegislate and its relations with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity regarding specific questions of the Directorate.
Coordinate the management's operational processes, including flows relating to the Plenary and adopted texts.
Coordinate the distribution, and technical assistance if necessary, of texts received from parliamentary committees and plenary services.

Ensure the rereading ('quality checks') of texts, legislative and non-legislative, in the different stages of the decision-making process in the European Parliament, as well as the technical finalization of the definitive legislative texts and their transmission to the Council and the Commission.
Coordinate the distribution to Legislative Quality units of texts received from MPs and parliamentary committees for editorial assistance ('Drafting Support tool').
Coordinate the efforts of the Directorate with a view to implementing the European Parliament's document management policy.
01B10.refined_description: Unit 01B10 functions as the integrated Technical Operations and Quality Assurance hub within Direction 01B of the Legislative Acts Directorate, operating as a leaf-node unit at Hierarchy Level 2 with primary responsibility for coordinating comprehensive information technology infrastructure, document management, and legislative text processing across the entire Directorate. The unit serves as the critical operational and technical coordination center that ensures seamless integration between IT project management, database administration, and technical infrastructure support while maintaining strategic representation in eLegislate initiatives and serving as the liaison interface with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity on Directorate-specific technical and cybersecurity matters. Unit 01B10 orchestrates management's operational processes encompassing Plenary-related workflows and adopted text management procedures, while simultaneously coordinating the distribution and provision of technical assistance for texts originating from parliamentary committees and plenary services, and managing the distribution of texts from Members and parliamentary committees to Legislative Quality units for editorial support services via the Drafting Support tool. The unit maintains rigorous quality assurance protocols through comprehensive rereading and quality checks of both legislative and non-legislative texts throughout all decision-making process stages, coupled with technical finalization and transmission of definitive legislative texts to the Council and Commission. Additionally, Unit 01B10 coordinates the Directorate's implementation of the European Parliament's document management policy framework, enabling Direction 01B to maintain operational excellence while supporting its sibling units (01B05, 01B20, 01B30, 01B40, 01B50, and one additional unit) in their respective legislative and administrative functions under the strategic direction of parent unit 01B.
01B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Information Technology Infrastructure Coordination & External Relations (50%)**: Coordinate comprehensive IT projects, database management, and technical support services across the entire Directorate; ensure strategic representation in eLegislate platform activities; maintain liaison relationships with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity regarding Directorate-specific technical requirements and cybersecurity matters.

• **Operational Process Management (15%)**: Coordinate management's operational workflows and procedural flows directly relating to Plenary activities and the handling of adopted legislative texts.

• **Text Distribution & Technical Support Services (20%)**: Coordinate the distribution and provision of technical assistance for texts received from parliamentary committees and plenary services (10%); coordinate distribution of texts from Members and parliamentary committees to Legislative Quality units for editorial support through the Drafting Support tool (10%).

• **Legislative Quality Assurance & Finalization (10%)**: Ensure comprehensive rereading and quality checks of both legislative and non-legislative texts throughout all European Parliament decision-making process stages; manage technical finalization of definitive legislative texts and coordinate their formal transmission to the Council and Commission.

• **Document Management Policy Implementation (5%)**: Coordinate Directorate-wide efforts and initiatives to implement and advance the European Parliament's document management policy framework.

01B20.name: 01B20
original_activities: Provide committee secretariats and MPs with legislative drafting assistance, particularly for draft reports and opinions and amendments in committee and plenary sessions.
Provide assistance at plenary session level (briefings, votes, adopted texts).
Provide procedural and legislative drafting assistance during negotiations with the Council.
Finalize legislative texts.
Finalize the regulation, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements and other texts of a legal nature.
01B20.refined_description:
Unit 01B20 operates as a specialized legislative support unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, functioning at Hierarchy Level 2 with responsibility for comprehensive technical and procedural assistance throughout the Parliament's complete legislative lifecycle. The unit delivers expert legislative drafting support and procedural guidance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament across all stages of legislative processes, from initial committee-level work through final text finalization, while simultaneously ensuring Parliament's institutional credibility and legislative effectiveness. Operating as a leaf node within the 01B Direction structure alongside sibling units 01B05, 01B10, 01B30, 01B40, 01B50, and others under unified directional oversight, Unit 01B20 provides five integrated and complementary service functions that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload. The unit's technical expertise encompasses both standard legislative procedures and specialized instruments requiring enhanced precision, including regulatory frameworks, Union budget provisions, and interinstitutional agreements. Through coordinated collaboration with peer units and strategic support across committee, plenary, and interinstitutional contexts, Unit 01B20 functions as a foundational element within the Parliament's legislative support infrastructure, ensuring technical coherence, procedural compliance, and strategic articulation of Parliament's legislative positions throughout the institutional negotiation process.

---

##
01B20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee and Plenary Legislative Drafting Support** (50%): Comprehensive provision of legislative drafting assistance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament for the preparation of draft reports, opinions, and amendments at both committee and plenary session levels (25%); concurrent delivery of plenary session operations support encompassing briefing materials, vote analysis and preparation, coordination of adopted texts, and management of finalized legislative documents (25%)

• **Interinstitutional Negotiation Assistance** (20%): Provision of specialized procedural and legislative drafting support during formal negotiations with the Council, ensuring technical soundness, strategic positioning, procedural compliance, and coherent legislative positioning throughout the trilogue process and related interinstitutional dialogue

• **Legislative Text Finalization** (20%): Finalization and technical refinement of legislative texts across standard legislative procedures and instruments to ensure compliance with institutional standards and quality requirements

• **Specialized Legal Instrument Finalization** (10%): Specialized finalization of regulatory instruments, Union budget framework provisions, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally binding texts requiring enhanced technical precision and procedural expertise

01B30.name: 01B30
original_activities: Provide committee secretariats and MPs with legislative drafting assistance, particularly for draft reports and opinions and amendments in committee and plenary sessions.
Provide assistance at plenary session level (briefings, votes, adopted texts).
Provide procedural and legislative drafting assistance during negotiations with the Council.
Finalize legislative texts.
Finalize the regulation, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements and other texts of a legal nature.
01B30.refined_description: Unit 01B30 operates as a specialized legislative support unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, functioning at the operational (Hierarchy Level 2) tier to provide comprehensive technical, procedural, and drafting assistance across the complete legislative lifecycle. The unit's integrated mission encompasses delivering specialized legislative drafting and procedural support services to parliamentary committees, individual Members of Parliament, and plenary sessions, while simultaneously managing the finalization of all legislative instruments and legal texts to ensure technical accuracy and formal compliance. As a leaf-node unit without subordinate structures, Unit 01B30 coordinates horizontally with sibling units (01B05, 01B10, 01B20, 01B40, 01B50, and one additional unit) under the unified Direction 01B framework to collectively support Parliament's legislative production capacity and institutional decision-making processes. The unit's workload encompasses four primary functional domains that directly enable Parliament's legislative function: committee and plenary-level drafting support, plenary operations management, interinstitutional negotiation assistance, and comprehensive legislative text finalization across standard and specialized legal instruments. This integrated operational structure ensures Parliament maintains optimal technical positioning throughout the entire legislative process, from initial committee deliberations through final text adoption and interinstitutional bargaining with the Council of the European Union.
01B30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee and Parliamentary Drafting Support** (25%): Provision of specialized legislative drafting assistance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament, including preparation of draft reports, opinions, and amendments for consideration in both committee and plenary deliberations

• **Plenary Session Operations Support** (25%): Delivery of comprehensive plenary-level operational assistance encompassing substantive briefing materials, voting guidance, and management of formally adopted legislative texts

• **Interinstitutional Negotiation Support** (20%): Provision of procedural and legislative drafting assistance during formal negotiations and trilogue discussions with the Council of the European Union, ensuring Parliament's legislative positions are technically refined, procedurally sound, and optimally presented throughout the bargaining process

• **Legislative Text Finalization** (30%): Completion and formalization of standard legislative texts (20%) and specialized legal instruments including regulatory instruments, Union budget documentation, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally binding documents requiring heightened technical expertise and formal legal compliance verification (10%)

01B40.name: 01B40
original_activities: Provide committee secretariats and MPs with legislative drafting assistance, particularly for draft reports and opinions and amendments in committee and plenary sessions.
Provide assistance at plenary session level (briefings, votes, adopted texts).
Provide procedural and legislative drafting assistance during negotiations with the Council.
Finalize legislative texts.
Finalize the regulation, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements and other texts of a legal nature.
01B40.refined_description: Unit 01B40 functions as a specialized legislative support and technical drafting unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, operating at Hierarchy Level 2 to provide comprehensive procedural and technical assistance throughout the complete legislative cycle. As a leaf-node unit coordinating with sibling units (01B05, 01B10, 01B20, 01B30, 01B50, and one additional unit), Unit 01B40 serves as a critical technical backbone supporting the Parliament's legislative competencies and institutional coordination responsibilities. The unit delivers expert assistance to parliamentary committees, individual Members of Parliament, and institutional negotiating bodies, ensuring technical precision and procedural compliance across all legislative deliberations. Through its structured resource allocation across five integrated activity domains, the unit maintains continuity and institutional coordination from initial legislative drafting through final text adoption and specialized legal instrument finalization, encompassing both standard legislative processes and complex intergovernmental negotiations with the Council of the European Union.
01B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee and Amendment Support** (25%): Provision of legislative drafting assistance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament for the preparation of draft reports, opinions, and amendments during both committee and plenary session deliberations

• **Plenary Session Support** (25%): Delivery of comprehensive plenary-level assistance services encompassing briefing materials, vote analysis and voting documentation, and coordination of adopted legislative texts

• **Interinstitutional Negotiation Support** (20%): Provision of procedural guidance and legislative drafting assistance during formal negotiations and trilogic discussions with the Council of the European Union, ensuring institutional coordination and technical precision throughout intergovernmental legislative deliberations

• **Legislative Text Finalization** (20%): Completion and technical finalization of standard legislative texts to ensure conformity with parliamentary drafting standards and requirements prior to adoption

• **Legal Instruments Finalization** (10%): Specialized finalization of regulatory instruments, Union budget framework provisions, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally binding texts requiring advanced institutional expertise and technical review

01B50.name: 01B50
original_activities: Provide committee secretariats and MPs with assistance in drafting legislative and budgetary texts, particularly for draft reports and opinions and amendments in committee and plenary sessions.
Provide assistance at plenary session level (briefings, votes, adopted texts).
Finalize legislative texts.
Provide procedural and legislative drafting assistance during negotiations with the Council.
Coordinate the preparation, signature and publications of the budget, decisions to mobilize special instruments not included in the Multiannual Financial Framework (MFF) and discharge decisions.
