Finalize the regulation, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements and other texts of a legal nature.
01B50.refined_description: Unit 01B50 functions as a specialized legislative drafting and procedural support unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, operating at Hierarchy Level 2 as a leaf node unit with no subordinate entities. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses providing integrated technical and procedural assistance across the complete legislative lifecycle, from initial text preparation through final adoption, publication, and implementation. Unit 01B50 delivers targeted drafting support and procedural guidance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament throughout all phases of the legislative process, while simultaneously ensuring compliance with parliamentary and institutional requirements at each stage. The unit operates collaboratively with six sibling units (01B05, 01B10, 01B20, 01B30, 01B40, and one additional unit) within the Direction 01B structure to provide comprehensive coverage of the Parliament's legislative support requirements. Operationally, the unit engages across multiple dimensions of parliamentary activity: committee-level deliberations, plenary session operations, interinstitutional negotiations with the Council, and budget administration, thereby fulfilling the Parliament's co-legislative responsibilities and financial oversight functions.
01B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Text Drafting and Committee Support** (50%): Provision of substantive drafting assistance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament for legislative and budgetary texts, including draft reports, opinions, and amendments at both committee and plenary session stages (25%); delivery of comprehensive plenary session support through provision of briefing materials, voting guidance, and finalized adopted text compilation (25%)

• **Text Finalization and Legal Formalization** (30%): Finalization of legislative texts to ensure compliance with institutional standards and procedural requirements (20%); finalization of regulatory instruments, Union budget provisions, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally binding documents of institutional significance (10%)

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Budget Administration** (20%): Provision of procedural and legislative drafting assistance during interinstitutional negotiations with the Council of the European Union to facilitate the Parliament's co-legislative role (10%); coordination of the preparation, signature, and publication of budget documents, decisions to mobilize special financial instruments outside the Multiannual Financial Framework (MFF), and discharge decisions (10%)

01B60.name: 01B60
original_activities: Provide procedural and legislative drafting assistance during negotiations with the Council.
Finalize legislative texts.
Provide committee secretariats and MPs with legislative drafting assistance, particularly for draft reports and opinions and amendments in committee and plenary sessions.
Provide assistance at plenary session level (briefings, votes, adopted texts).
Finalize the regulation, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements and other texts of a legal nature.

Coordinate and plan the finalization of legislative texts with the Council, based on the work of lawyer-linguists in the decision-making process in parliamentary committee, as well as coordinate and plan the adoption of legislative texts by the Plenary, in collaboration with other services of DG PRES and DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST and DG BUDG.

Ensure the analysis and synthesis of information collected in the management database following the work of parliamentary committees, with a view to preparing briefings and notes on legislative planning for the President, the Secretary General and the Deputy Secretary General; coordinate the transmission to the Legislative Quality units and to the Council of texts in the process of joint finalization with the Council.
01B60.refined_description: Unit 01B60 operates as a specialized legislative drafting and procedural support unit within Direction 01B of the European Parliament's Legislative Acts Directorate, functioning at Hierarchy Level 2 as a leaf node organizational entity that collaborates with sibling units (01B05, 01B10, 01B20, 01B30, 01B40, and one additional unit) to ensure coherent legislative support across the institution. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses providing integrated legislative drafting assistance and procedural coordination throughout all stages of the European Parliament's legislative decision-making process, from initial committee work through final adoption and formal finalization of legal texts. The unit's workload is strategically distributed across five primary functional domains: text finalization and interinstitutional negotiation (40%), direct parliamentary body support (30%), specialized legal instrument processing (10%), interinstitutional coordination and planning (10%), and strategic information management for senior leadership (10%). Core to the unit's operations is the provision of procedural and legislative drafting assistance during Council negotiations (20%) and the finalization of legislative texts (20%), which together form the backbone of interinstitutional legislative coordination and represent the unit's primary operational focus. Complementing this core function, the unit delivers specialized support to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament with legislative drafting assistance for draft reports, opinions, and amendments in both committee and plenary sessions (15%), while simultaneously providing plenary-level operational support through briefings, vote preparation, and management of adopted texts (15%). The unit further specializes in finalizing regulatory instruments, the Union budget, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally-binding texts (10%), while coordinating and planning legislative text finalization with the Council through integrated lawyer-linguist collaboration and coordinating Plenary adoption in partnership with DG PRES, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, and DG BUDG (10%). Through analysis and synthesis of parliamentary committee work data from management databases, the unit prepares executive briefings and legislative planning documents for the President, Secretary General, and Deputy Secretary General (10%), while coordinating transmission of jointly-finalized texts to Legislative Quality units and the Council. Through this comprehensive portfolio of integrated activities, Unit 01B60 ensures legislative quality, procedural compliance, and effective interinstitutional coordination throughout the European Parliament's legislative processes.
01B60.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Text Finalization and Council Negotiation Support** (40%): Provision of procedural and legislative drafting assistance during interinstitutional negotiations with the Council (20%) and comprehensive finalization of legislative texts across all institutional stages (20%), representing the unit's primary operational focus and backbone of interinstitutional legislative coordination.

• **Committee and Plenary-Level Parliamentary Support** (30%): Delivery of specialized legislative drafting assistance to committee secretariats and Members of Parliament for draft reports, opinions, amendments, and legislative documents processed during both committee and plenary sessions (15%), combined with plenary session-level operational support including briefings, vote preparation, and management of adopted texts (15%).

• **Legal Instruments and Regulatory Text Finalization** (10%): Specialized finalization of regulatory instruments, Union budget provisions, interinstitutional agreements, and other legally-binding texts requiring formal institutional processing and legal precision across the parliamentary decision-making process.

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Plenary Adoption Planning** (10%): Coordination and planning of legislative text finalization with the Council through integrated lawyer-linguist collaboration throughout the parliamentary decision-making process, combined with coordination of Plenary adoption in partnership with DG PRES, DG ECTI, DG CASP, DG IUST, and DG BUDG to ensure synchronized institutional adoption.

• **Strategic Information Management and Senior Leadership Support** (10%): Analysis and synthesis of parliamentary committee work data from management databases to prepare executive briefings and legislative planning documents for the President, Secretary General, and Deputy Secretary General, with coordinated transmission of jointly-finalized texts to Legislative Quality units and the Council.

01C.name: 01C
original_activities: Supervise and manage the preparation and implementation of the protocol activities of the President and the political bodies of the European Parliament and in particular the official and working visits of Heads of State and Government and institutional ceremonies. Welcome invited personalities (Heads of State and Government, ministers, Presidents of Parliaments and ambassadors).
Ensure the administrative management of the Protocol Directorate as well as the coordination of the two units.

Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer for the budget lines allocated to the Protocol Directorate. Prepare and supervise public procurement contracts. Initiate commitments and payments.
Other missions.
01C.refined_description: Unit 01C functions as the Direction-level strategic leadership and administrative command center of the European Parliament's Protocol Directorate, operating under the institutional authority of Unit 01 and the Secretary General. The unit exercises comprehensive supervisory responsibility over protocol operations across the institution's highest-level diplomatic and ceremonial engagements, serving as the central operational hub ensuring institutional representation standards. Unit 01C's integrated functional mandate encompasses four primary domains: (1) directing the preparation and implementation of comprehensive protocol activities for the President and all political bodies, including orchestrating official and working visits of Heads of State and Government, organizing institutional ceremonies, and managing reception and welcome protocols for invited high-level personalities (Heads of State, Government ministers, Presidents of national and international Parliaments, and accredited ambassadors); (2) exercising administrative stewardship over the Protocol Directorate's operational management and coordinating integrated service delivery across its two subordinate specialized units (Unit 01C10 and Unit 01C20, which collectively manage 15 distinct activities); (3) functioning as the subdelegated authorizing officer for all Protocol Directorate budget allocations, exercising fiduciary responsibility through preparation and supervision of public procurement contracts and initiating all financial commitments and payment authorizations across protocol-related budget lines; and (4) addressing discretionary institutional missions and emerging organizational requirements as assigned. These integrated activities position Unit 01C as the essential operational nexus for European Parliament protocol functions, ensuring coherent institutional representation and financial stewardship across the organization's diplomatic and ceremonial engagements.
01C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Protocol Operations & Ceremonial Management** (40%): Supervise and manage preparation and implementation of comprehensive protocol activities for the President and all political bodies; orchestrate official and working visits of Heads of State and Government; organize institutional ceremonies of significance; manage reception and welcome protocols for high-level personalities including Heads of State and Government, government ministers, Presidents of national and international Parliaments, and accredited ambassadors

• **Directorate Administration & Inter-Unit Coordination** (35%): Ensure comprehensive administrative management and operational coherence of the Protocol Directorate; coordinate integrated service delivery across subordinate units Unit 01C10 and Unit 01C20 (collectively managing 15 distinct activities); maintain organizational alignment and inter-unit functionality

• **Financial Management & Procurement Oversight** (20%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer authority for all Protocol Directorate budget allocations and budget lines; prepare and supervise public procurement contracts; initiate financial commitments and authorize payment processing

• **Discretionary Institutional Missions** (5%): Address additional missions and emerging organizational requirements as assigned by institutional needs and organizational priorities

01C10.name: 01C10
original_activities: Assist the President and the political bodies of the European Parliament in all matters relating to the protocol activities of the Institution, in particular official and working visits, including visits from outside the European Union.

Assist interparliamentary delegations with all matters relating to protocol aspects (program, reception, meals, VIP services, protocol lounges, diplomatic stands, flags, etc.), including external meetings of interparliamentary assemblies.
Assist Members, officials and agents in obtaining VISAs for official visits. Coordinate relations with the diplomatic corps; drafting notes verbales.
Preparation of notes verbales for official visits and external trips of the President and logistical aspects, such as booking VIP services and transport, also including financial aspects.
Ensure the management of official gifts offered on behalf of the European Parliament by the President and her official representatives and interparliamentary delegations.
Ensure e.g. drafting of death notices for deputies and the preparation of condolence books.
Other missions.
01C10.refined_description: Unit 01C10 functions as the Protocol Operations Unit within the Protocol Directorate (01C) of the European Parliament, operating at the second hierarchical level as the primary operational entity responsible for executing comprehensive protocol and diplomatic support services to the Institution's highest political bodies and interparliamentary structures. The unit's core mission encompasses four interconnected operational domains that collectively ensure seamless diplomatic engagement and institutional representation: executive protocol assistance to the President and political bodies for all official and working visits (both intra-EU and external), comprehensive protocol coordination for interparliamentary delegations including programmatic design, reception arrangements, meal planning, VIP services, protocol lounge management, diplomatic stands, flag protocols, and external interparliamentary assembly meetings, visa facilitation and diplomatic coordination for Members, officials, and agents undertaking official visits coupled with direct liaison management with the diplomatic corps, and logistical and administrative coordination for presidential and delegation travel encompassing VIP service booking, transport arrangements, and financial management. Additionally, Unit 01C10 maintains custodial responsibility for the institutional management of official gifts presented on behalf of the European Parliament by the President, official representatives, and interparliamentary delegations to foreign partners, performs ceremonial administrative functions including the drafting of death notices for deceased deputies and preparation of institutional condolence documentation, and executes other specialized protocol missions as assigned. A critical cross-cutting function throughout these activities involves the preparation and drafting of formal diplomatic notes verbales for presidential official visits, external parliamentary missions, and diplomatic coordination with the international diplomatic corps. Collectively, these integrated activities ensure the European Parliament maintains proper diplomatic protocols, institutional dignity, effective international parliamentary relations, and comprehensive support infrastructure for external engagement.
01C10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Presidential and Institutional Protocol Support** (30%): Direct executive assistance to the President and political bodies of the European Parliament regarding all protocol dimensions of official and working visits, encompassing both intra-EU visits and visits from third countries outside the European Union framework

• **Interparliamentary Delegation Protocol Management** (25%): Comprehensive protocol coordination for interparliamentary delegations encompassing program development, official reception arrangements, meal planning, VIP services, protocol lounge management, diplomatic stands, flag protocols, and external interparliamentary assembly meeting logistics

• **Visa Administration and Diplomatic Relations** (15%): Visa acquisition assistance for Members, officials, and agents undertaking official visits; direct liaison management with the international diplomatic corps; and preparation of formal diplomatic notes verbales for diplomatic coordination purposes

• **Travel Logistics, Diplomatic Correspondence, and Official Gifts Management** (20%): Preparation of formal diplomatic notes verbales for presidential visits and external parliamentary missions; booking and coordination of VIP services; transport logistics and arrangements; financial administration of travel-related matters; and institutional custodial management of official gifts presented by the President, official representatives, and interparliamentary delegations to foreign parliamentary partners

• **Ceremonial and Miscellaneous Functions** (10%): Drafting of death notices for deceased Members, preparation of institutional condolence books, and execution of other assigned specialized protocol missions within the institutional framework

01C20.name: 01C20
original_activities: Organize, coordinate and provide protocol support in the context of major institutional ceremonies, one-off and recurring ceremonies in plenary sessions, inauguration ceremonies, receptions, ad hoc events organized by the President and the political bodies of the European Parliament.

Assist the President of the European Parliament, and the political bodies of the European Parliament in everything relating to the protocol activities of the Institution, and in particular, official and working visits (organization, program, reception, protocol lounges, diplomatic forums, flags).
Provide protocol assistance to Vice-Presidents when they represent the President during an event or visit within or outside the Institution.
Provide protocol support for events organized within the European Parliament by other internal or external services.

Prepare the musical dimension of the official ceremonies organized at the European Parliament, conceptualize and organize the annual series of concerts as part of protocol and cultural diplomacy activities.

Organize recognition measures for civil servants who have completed 15 and 25 years of service (diplomas, medals, letters). Assist European Parliament staff in preparing for their retirement (departure cocktail, institutional gifts).
Participate in the establishment of the European Order of Merit.
Provide protocol assistance during visits by interparliamentary delegations and interparliamentary assemblies.
01C20.refined_description:
**

Unit 01C20 operates as the Protocol Support and Ceremonial Services unit within the Protocol Directorate (01C) of the European Parliament, functioning as the specialized institutional hub for comprehensive protocol execution, ceremonial coordination, diplomatic relations support, and honors management. The unit's integrated mission encompasses the organization and coordination of all major institutional ceremonies—including plenary session ceremonies, recurring institutional observances, presidential inaugurations, official receptions, and ad hoc events commissioned by the President and political bodies (25% of workload)—while simultaneously providing extensive protocol assistance to the President and Institution's political bodies regarding official and working visits, program development, reception logistics, protocol lounge management, diplomatic forum coordination, and ceremonial flag protocols (25% of workload). Beyond executive leadership support, Unit 01C20 extends specialized protocol assistance to Vice-Presidents when they represent the President at institutional or external events (10% of workload) and facilitates protocol support for events organized by other internal or external Parliament services (10% of workload). The unit manages the cultural and artistic dimensions of official ceremonies through the preparation of musical programming and the conceptualization and organization of the annual concert series as integrated components of Protocol and Cultural Diplomacy initiatives (10% of workload). Additionally, Unit 01C20 administers comprehensive staff recognition and transition programs, including the organization of service milestone celebrations for civil servants completing 15 and 25 years of tenure (through diplomas and medals), retirement preparation assistance with departure ceremonies and institutional gifts (10% of workload), participation in the establishment of the European Order of Merit (5% of workload), and the provision of specialized protocol assistance during visits by interparliamentary delegations and interparliamentary assemblies (5% of workload). Collectively, these integrated activities enable the Institution to project diplomatic excellence, maintain ceremonial traditions, recognize institutional contributions, support external relations, and reinforce internal cohesion.

---

**
01C20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Institutional Ceremony and Event Coordination** (50%): Organization, coordination, and comprehensive protocol support for major institutional ceremonies, plenary session ceremonies, recurring institutional observances, presidential inaugurations, official receptions, and ad hoc events commissioned by the President and political bodies; provision of protocol support for events organized by other internal or external Parliament services

• **Presidential and Political Leadership Protocol Support** (35%): Comprehensive assistance to the President and political bodies regarding official and working visits, including program development, reception logistics, protocol lounge management, diplomatic forum coordination, and ceremonial flag protocols; specialized protocol assistance to Vice-Presidents when representing the President at institutional or external events

• **Cultural Diplomacy and Artistic Programming** (10%): Preparation of musical dimensions for official ceremonies; conceptualization and organization of the annual concert series as integrated components of Protocol and Cultural Diplomacy initiatives

• **Staff Recognition, Honors, and Transition Management** (10%): Administration of service recognition measures for civil servants completing 15 and 25 years of tenure through diplomas and medals; coordination of retirement transition support including departure ceremonies and institutional gifts; participation in the establishment of the European Order of Merit; provision of specialized protocol assistance during visits by interparliamentary delegations and interparliamentary assemblies

01D.name: 01D
original_activities: Assist the Deputy Secretary General in the exercise of his duties.
Ensure the direction, coordination and leadership of the units of the Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination Department.

Ensure interinstitutional relations, in particular with the European Commission, the Council, the Council Presidencies, the Committee of the Regions and the Economic and Social Committee. Contribute to the negotiation and implementation of interinstitutional agreements.
Ensure legislative coordination and plenary planning, both within Parliament and with other institutions, in particular with regard to the implementation of Article 17 TEU.

Ensure the implementation of the European Parliament's transparency policy regarding the institution's relations with interest representatives, in accordance with the interinstitutional agreements relating to the transparency register.
Ensure the implementation of public access to documents, in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001.

Propose reflection and contributions on future interinstitutional developments and horizontal subjects, linked to enlargement and internal reform of the Union, as well as citizen participation and follow-up to the Conference on the Future of Europe.
Other missions.
01D.refined_description: Unit 01D, the Direction of Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination, functions as a first-level hierarchical unit within the European Parliament's apex strategic command structure, operating directly under the Directorate-General (Unit 01) and exercising coordinated oversight of four specialized operational child units (01D10, 01D30, 01D40, and 01D50). The unit serves as the institutional nexus bridging the Parliament's legislative and strategic functions with the broader European institutional architecture, managing the Parliament's external relations and internal legislative processes through eight distinct, fully-documented activity streams. Unit 01D's mandate encompasses executive support functions, departmental governance and leadership, comprehensive interinstitutional relations and agreement management, legislative coordination and plenary planning, transparency and document access compliance, strategic institutional development, and residual assigned missions, collectively representing 100% of documented workload allocation. These activities position Unit 01D as the institutional bridge between Parliament's operations and the European institutional ecosystem, working in concert with sibling units under unified strategic direction while exercising hierarchical authority over subordinate operational units that operationalize its strategic mandates across multiple institutional domains and policy horizons.
01D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Executive Support and Departmental Leadership** (30%): Direct executive assistance to the Deputy Secretary General in the discharge of his institutional responsibilities (15%); comprehensive directional oversight, strategic coordination, and leadership of all subordinate units within the Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination Department (15%)—establishing the unit's governance and operational framework.

• **Interinstitutional Relations and Agreement Management** (15%): Establishment, maintenance, and strategic coordination of formal institutional relations with the European Commission, the Council, successive Council Presidencies, the Committee of the Regions, and the Economic and Social Committee; negotiation, implementation, and monitoring of binding interinstitutional agreements.

• **Legislative Coordination and Plenary Planning** (15%): Internal coordination of legislative scheduling and plenary organization within Parliament; coordination with external institutions regarding legislative processes; specific implementation of Article 17 TEU provisions concerning Commission investiture and accountability mechanisms.

• **Transparency, Access, and Institutional Accountability** (20%): Implementation of the European Parliament's transparency policy framework regarding interest representative relations and interinstitutional transparency register compliance (10%); administration of public document access rights under Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 (10%).

• **Strategic Institutional Development and Citizen Engagement** (10%): Development of institutional reflection and strategic contributions addressing prospective interinstitutional developments, Union enlargement considerations, internal institutional reform initiatives, citizen participation enhancement mechanisms, and substantive follow-up to the Conference on the Future of Europe.

• **Residual Institutional Missions** (10%): Execution of additional assigned missions and responsibilities as determined by institutional leadership.

01D10.name: 01D10
original_activities: Carry out programming activities: plan the agendas of the plenary sessions and produce related documents (short, medium and long-term programming tables, memos, priority files, prepare and implement the Joint Declaration, analyze and monitor the Commission's annual work program).

Ensure coordination activities: monitor the work of the plenary session (including assistance to the Vice-Presidents) and the parliamentary committees. Write notes on these topics, and contribute to the development of the briefing for the President for the plenary sessions. Participate in internal and interinstitutional coordination meetings.

Supply and ensure the administration of the ITER application with debates, votes, plenary session procedures as well as all Council decisions (Coreper I & II). Ensure synchronization between SCHED and ITER for variation notes and updates according to the OJ project. Open procedures at the request of parliamentary committees and political groups. Produce and provide statistical analyzes on the work of the Plenary.

Organize seminars and workshops in collaboration with other directorates general for national civil servants, with a view to future Council presidencies. Provide notes and organize meetings for the preparation of future Council presidencies, particularly on their priorities. Give seminars on the work of the legislative planning unit to other services of the European Parliament.
Provide tables and notes on current legislative and non-legislative procedures with plenary programming.
01D10.refined_description: Unit 01D10, the Legislative Programming and Interinstitutional Coordination Unit, operates as a critical operational nexus within the Direction of Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination (01D) of the European Parliament, functioning as an essential infrastructure component for the institution's strategic planning and procedural management apparatus. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the systematic planning, coordination, and administration of plenary session activities while maintaining full synchronization with interinstitutional processes and Council decision-making mechanisms at all levels (Coreper I & II). As a leaf-node unit functioning alongside sibling units 01D30, 01D40, and 01D50, Unit 01D10 serves as the primary nexus for legislative coordination, procedural management, and institutional planning, ensuring the Parliament's legislative calendar operates with maximum efficiency and interinstitutional alignment. The unit integrates sophisticated programming methodologies with administrative stewardship of critical systems (ITER and SCHED platforms), while simultaneously generating analytical intelligence, presidential briefing materials, and institutional knowledge-sharing initiatives that support both internal parliamentary operations and cross-institutional coordination forums. Through its multifaceted operational framework, Unit 01D10 ensures comprehensive integration of executive branch priorities (Commission annual work programs), legislative procedures, plenary operations, and Council decisions into a coherent institutional planning and execution strategy that positions the European Parliament as a fully coordinated legislative actor within the interinstitutional framework.
01D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Legislative Programming and Agenda Planning** (50%): Comprehensive planning of plenary session agendas across multiple timeframes (short, medium, and long-term); systematic production of programming documentation including priority files and planning tables; preparation and implementation of Joint Declarations; continuous analysis and monitoring of the European Commission's annual work program to ensure parliamentary alignment with executive priorities; development of strategic planning materials

• **Institutional Coordination and Briefing Support** (20%): Continuous monitoring and coordination of plenary session operations including direct assistance to Vice-Presidents; parliamentary committee oversight and analysis; production of analytical notes on institutional developments; substantive contribution to presidential briefing materials for plenary session preparation; active participation in both internal Parliament coordination mechanisms and interinstitutional coordination forums

• **ITER System Administration and Procedure Management** (15%): Comprehensive administration and maintenance of the ITER application system managing debates, votes, and all plenary procedures; full integration of Council decisions including Coreper I and II deliberations; systematic synchronization between SCHED and ITER platforms for variation notes and updates aligned with Official Journal project specifications; processing of procedure opening requests submitted by parliamentary committees and political groups; production of detailed statistical analyses of plenary work and institutional activities

• **Capacity-Building and Council Presidency Preparation** (10%): Organization of seminars and specialized workshops in collaborative partnership with other Directorates-General designed to prepare national civil servants for future Council presidencies; coordination of preparatory meetings and documentation regarding upcoming presidency priorities; delivery of institutional knowledge seminars on legislative planning methodologies to European Parliament services; knowledge-sharing initiatives across the institution

• **Legislative Procedure Documentation and Reference Materials** (5%): Production of comprehensive tables and analytical notes documenting current legislative and non-legislative procedures; integration of procedural documentation with plenary programming schedules; ongoing provision of reference materials supporting institutional decision-making processes

01D30.name: 01D30
original_activities: Contribute to interinstitutional dialogue: monitoring the follow-up given by the Commission to EP resolutions; participate in the negotiations of interinstitutional agreements; TF secretariat for the negotiation of the EP-EC Framework Agreement; monitoring the application of cooperation agreements with the Consultative Committees; information seminars for Council Presidencies; monitoring of general files of an interinstitutional nature; database development Interinstitutional Agreements.

Ensure monitoring of the activities of other institutions: monitor and disseminate regular notes on the activities of the European Council, the Council, the Commission, the Court of Auditors, the EESC, the CoR and the EEAS; manage the interinstitutional calendar on the European Parliament intranet.

Ensure the organization, preparation and monitoring of the Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG): supervise the GCI as a forum for interinstitutional dialogue, coordination with a view to plenary sessions as well as monitoring the implementation of the 'Better Lawmaking' agreement.

Support the SGA in the implementation of the 'Better regulation' agreement, in particular §10 (follow-up given by the EC to non-legislative resolutions of the EP); §50 monitoring (monitoring the implementation of other Institutions; annual report and high-level meeting); contribute to the preparation and monitoring of the Joint Declaration on annual and multi-annual legislative programming; assist in the negotiation of interinstitutional agreements following the 'Better lawmaking' agreement.

Manage work related to the plenary session: prepare briefings for the President in Plenary; assist in planning Plenary agendas and regular dialogue with other institutions on their support; ensure monitoring of the presence of other institutions.
01D30.refined_description: Unit 01D30 functions as the European Parliament's specialized operational nucleus for interinstitutional coordination and legislative dialogue, serving as a critical component within Direction 01D (Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination) at the second hierarchical tier. Operating as an essential interface within the institutional architecture, the unit orchestrates the Parliament's strategic engagement with all EU institutions through five integrated functional areas totaling 100% documented workload allocation, while maintaining operational coordination with sibling units 01D10, 01D40, and 01D50 to deliver comprehensive interinstitutional affairs management. The unit's mission encompasses systematic monitoring of Commission follow-up actions to EP resolutions, active participation in interinstitutional agreement negotiations, secretariat management for EP-EC Framework Agreement negotiations, oversight of cooperation agreements with Consultative Committees, facilitation of information seminars for Council Presidencies, tracking of interinstitutional files, and maintenance of comprehensive databases documenting interinstitutional agreements. Unit 01D30 simultaneously maintains continuous institutional surveillance and intelligence dissemination through regular monitoring and circulation of analytical activity notes from the European Council, Council, Commission, Court of Auditors, EESC, CoR, and EEAS, alongside management of the interinstitutional calendar on the European Parliament intranet as a central coordination resource. The unit positions the Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG) as a formal dialogue forum for coordinated institutional functioning, ensures implementation of the Better Lawmaking agreement framework, and supports the Secretary-General's implementation of the Better Regulation agreement (§10 and §50 monitoring), while providing essential plenary session support through presidential briefing preparation, agenda coordination, and systematic monitoring of institutional participation. Collectively, these activities establish Unit 01D30 as an indispensable operational hub ensuring legislative coherence and coordinated functioning across the European institutional architecture.
01D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Interinstitutional Dialogue & Agreement Management** (30%): Monitor Commission follow-up to EP resolutions; actively participate in interinstitutional agreement negotiations; provide secretariat functions for EP-EC Framework Agreement negotiations and Task Force operations; oversee cooperation agreements with Consultative Committees; facilitate information seminars for Council Presidencies; track and monitor interinstitutional files; develop and maintain comprehensive databases documenting interinstitutional agreements.

• **Institutional Activity Monitoring & Intelligence Dissemination** (30%): Conduct systematic monitoring and regular circulation of analytical activity notes from the European Council, Council, Commission, Court of Auditors, EESC, CoR, and EEAS; manage and maintain the interinstitutional calendar on the European Parliament intranet as a central coordination resource for institutional activities and scheduling.

• **Interinstitutional Coordination Group Governance & Better Lawmaking Implementation** (20%): Organize, prepare, and monitor the Interinstitutional Coordination Group (ICG) as a dedicated forum for interinstitutional dialogue and coordination; coordinate ICG activities with plenary session preparation requirements; ensure implementation of the Better Lawmaking agreement framework across participating institutions; position ICG as a central mechanism for institutional coordination in advance of parliamentary proceedings.

• **Better Regulation Agreement & Legislative Programming Support** (10%): Support the Secretary-General's implementation of the Better Regulation agreement, specifically §10 (monitoring Commission follow-up to non-legislative EP resolutions) and §50 (institutional implementation monitoring with annual reporting and high-level meetings); prepare and monitor Joint Declarations on annual and multi-annual legislative programming; provide negotiation support for interinstitutional agreements arising from Better Lawmaking agreement provisions.

• **Plenary Session Coordination & Institutional Liaison** (10%): Prepare comprehensive briefings for the President during plenary sessions; coordinate plenary agenda planning and institutional participation logistics; maintain systematic dialogue with other institutional representatives regarding their support and participation; ensure continuous monitoring of institutional presence and engagement during parliamentary proceedings.

01D40.name: 01D40
original_activities: Receive and process requests for public access to European Parliament documents within regulatory deadlines; help requesters clarify the content of their request and negotiate with them when requests relate to very long and complex documents; coordinate responses with the responsible services of the European Parliament and consult third parties when requests concern third party documents.

Continue the implementation in the EP of the IIA between the EP, the Council and the Commission on the Transparency Register (TR) and apply the decisions taken by the competent authority (Bureau, SG) in this area including preparing the annual report on compliance with the rules concerning events with the participation of interest representatives; ensure internal communication on these decisions, and training on the TR and promote its use among Members and staff.

Deal with Interest Representatives (IRs), carry out eligibility and quality checks on data provided by IRs wishing to register with the RT, handle complaints submitted to the Secretariat and assist in the preparation of the annual report on the operation of the Register; organize training and communication activities to raise public and IR awareness of the Registry.

Represent the European Parliament within the joint secretariat of the RT, ensuring the implementation within the framework of the interinstitutional agreement between the European Parliament, the Council and the Commission on the Transparency Register. Prepare the annual meeting of the RT Management Board and ensure the implementation of the annual priorities of the Register.
Coordinate with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity the development of the Public Register IT tool.
Prepare the European Parliament's annual report on access to documents.
01D40.refined_description: Unit 01D40 functions as the European Parliament's specialized operational hub for transparency governance and public access to institutional information, operating as a subordinate component within Direction 01D (Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination). The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the administration of public document access procedures, the implementation and oversight of the Transparency Register framework across the Parliament, the management of interest representative registrations and compliance, and the coordination of interinstitutional transparency initiatives within the tripartite agreement framework shared with the Council and Commission. The unit serves as the Parliament's representative within the joint secretariat of the Transparency Register, ensuring faithful implementation of interinstitutional agreements and annual strategic priorities while preparing Management Board meetings and coordinating compliance activities. Through its integrated operations, Unit 01D40 directly supports the Parliament's commitment to institutional transparency, democratic accountability, and stakeholder engagement with interest representatives, while simultaneously strengthening operational effectiveness through coordination with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity on Public Register IT infrastructure development and through the preparation of comprehensive annual accountability reporting on document access and Register performance.
01D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Public Access to Documents Administration** (40%): Receipt and processing of public access requests for European Parliament documents within statutory regulatory deadlines; provision of clarification assistance to requesters regarding complex, voluminous, or lengthy documentation; negotiation with requesters concerning the scope of submissions; inter-service coordination with responsible Parliament units to facilitate responses; consultation with third parties concerning documents involving external stakeholders; coordination of responses within institutional transparency frameworks.

• **Transparency Register Governance, Compliance, and Implementation** (20%): Implementation of the Interinstitutional Agreement on the Transparency Register within the European Parliament; execution of decisions and directives issued by the Bureau and Secretary-General; internal communication and dissemination of Register-related policies and procedures; provision of training and capacity-building activities for Members and institutional staff; promotion of Register utilization and adoption across the Parliament; preparation of annual compliance reports documenting interest representative participation in Parliament events and institutional engagement.

• **Interest Representative Management and Registry Operations** (20%): Conduct of eligibility assessments and data quality verification for Interest Representatives seeking registration with the Transparency Register; receipt and processing of formal complaints; investigation and resolution of compliance issues; contribution to annual operational reporting on Register performance and effectiveness; organization of public awareness campaigns and training initiatives targeting both interest representatives and general audiences; assistance in preparing annual operational reports demonstrating Registry effectiveness.

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Strategic Register Management** (10%): Representation of the European Parliament within the joint secretariat of the Transparency Register; oversight of implementation activities within the tripartite interinstitutional framework (Parliament, Council, Commission); implementation of interinstitutional agreements and annual strategic priorities; preparation and coordination of annual Transparency Register Management Board meetings; coordination of Register priorities execution across the institutional framework.

• **Information Technology Infrastructure Development and Institutional Reporting** (10%): Coordination with the General Directorate of Information Technologies and Cybersecurity on development and enhancement of the Public Register IT platform and infrastructure (5%); preparation and publication of the European Parliament's annual report on document access, including comprehensive metrics, compliance analysis, and transparency performance indicators (5%).

01D50.name: 01D50
original_activities: Support the role and tasks of the Deputy Secretary-General in advancing strategic monitoring, analysis and reflection on future interinstitutional developments and reform, including in relation to the enlargement of the Union.
Follow and report on the activities of the other institutions in the context of future reform and enlargement of the Union. Liaise and coordinate with the responsible services.

Monitor implementation of the outcome of the Conference on the future of Europe and its follow-up and contribute to communication and outreach activities, including in relation to any related enlargement and reform processes.
Support preparatory work on a possible mechanism and/or elements of participatory democracy process in the European Union, and related structures and events.
Liaise and build close cooperative relationships with relevant services, e.g. horizontal committee coordination and parliamentary committees' secretariats.
Maintain the dedicated Conference follow-up website of the European Parliament.
Continue to provide information on the Conference experience, as well as on lessons learnt.
01D50.refined_description: Unit 01D50 operates as a specialized strategic advisory and coordination hub within the Direction of Interinstitutional Affairs and Legislative Coordination (01D), functioning as a critical operational and analytical arm for advancing the Deputy Secretary-General's mandate in institutional reform and Union enlargement processes. The unit provides comprehensive support for the Deputy Secretary-General's strategic monitoring, detailed analysis, and forward-looking reflection on evolving interinstitutional developments and reform trajectories, with particular emphasis on enlargement-related considerations (30% allocation). In parallel, Unit 01D50 systematically monitors and reports on peer European institution activities within the context of future Union reform and enlargement frameworks, while actively establishing and maintaining liaison and coordination mechanisms across responsible parliamentary services to ensure coherent institutional positioning (30% allocation). The unit maintains substantive engagement with Conference on the Future of Europe implementation and continuity, including oversight of outcome monitoring, follow-up mechanisms, and integrated communication and outreach activities that intersect with enlargement and reform processes (10% allocation). Additionally, the unit supports foundational preparatory work on potential participatory democracy mechanisms and associated institutional structures and events within the European Union framework (10% allocation), while dedicating resources to establishing and maintaining cooperative relationships with horizontal coordination committees and parliamentary committee secretariats to ensure integrated service delivery across institutional boundaries (10% allocation). Technical and informational functions comprise the remaining allocation: maintaining the dedicated Conference follow-up website as an institutional knowledge repository (5%) and continuing to disseminate Conference experience insights and documented lessons learned across the institution (5%). As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 01D50 operates without subordinate units while maintaining coordinated relationships with sibling units 01D10, 01D30, and 01D40 within the Direction.
01D50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Advisory and Institutional Analysis** (30%): Provide comprehensive support to the Deputy Secretary-General's strategic monitoring, detailed analysis, and forward-looking reflection on evolving interinstitutional developments, reform initiatives, and Union enlargement trajectories.

• **Interinstitutional Monitoring and Coordination** (30%): Systematically monitor and report on peer European institution activities within reform and enlargement contexts; establish and maintain liaison and coordination mechanisms with responsible parliamentary services to ensure coherent institutional positioning.

• **Conference on the Future of Europe: Implementation and Continuity** (10%): Monitor implementation of Conference outcomes and follow-up mechanisms; contribute to integrated communication and outreach activities related to enlargement and reform processes.

• **Participatory Democracy and Democratic Innovation** (10%): Support preparatory work on potential participatory democracy mechanisms, related institutional structures, and associated events within the European Union framework.

• **Cross-Institutional Relationships and Knowledge Management** (10%): Establish and maintain cooperative relationships with horizontal coordination committees and parliamentary committee secretariats; maintain the dedicated Conference follow-up website as an institutional knowledge repository (5%); provide ongoing dissemination of Conference experience insights and documented lessons learned (5%).

01E.name: 01E
original_activities: Direct and coordinate the activities of the units of the Resources Directorate ('Human Resources' and 'Finance and IT'), supervise internal communication and awareness activities, new projects as well as the participation of the General Directorate of the Presidency in certain events.

Provide the Deputy Secretary General (SGA) and Director General of the General Directorate of the Presidency, the Directors, Heads of Unit as well as the Cabinet of the SGA, with advice, support and assistance in the areas of human resources, finance, IT, logistics, missions, professional training and internal communication.
Exercise the function of subdelegated authorizing officer for the budget lines allocated to the General Directorate of the Presidency.

Coordinate and guide, on behalf of the general management, the preparation of files concerning the rating procedure, mobility, logistics and missions as well as the preparation, where applicable, of notes for the reorganization of services and other files of a horizontal nature.

Represent general management in various inter-DG forums (resource director meetings, etc.) and other activities, inter-service meetings or with specific general directorates (Personnel, Finance, Infrastructure and logistics, etc.).
Contribute to the annual work program and activity reports of the general management. Participate in strategic thinking on the evolution and transformation of administration and resource management.
Contribute to specific projects (Skills, Analysis of job descriptions, Training courses, etc.) of the General Directorate of Personnel or other entities.
Chair the Advisory Committee for Prevention and Protection at Work (CPPT). Plan monthly meetings (agenda, selection of guests) and write the activity program and the annual report.
01E.refined_description: Unit 01E operates as the strategic Direction-level command and coordination hub of the General Directorate of the Presidency, functioning as the primary interface between senior institutional leadership and operational resources while reporting directly to Unit 01 (the apex institutional authority). As a first-level directorate within the Resources Directorate, 01E exercises comprehensive executive, advisory, and supervisory authority over subordinate units (Human Resources and Finance and IT), directing their integrated activities while cascading institutional priorities across the Presidency's operational infrastructure. The unit delivers multifaceted services encompassing strategic project management, internal communication and awareness initiatives, institutional event coordination, specialized advisory and technical support to senior leadership across seven interconnected functional domains (human resources management, financial administration, IT infrastructure, logistics operations, official missions, professional development and training, and internal organizational communication), and fiduciary stewardship through subdelegated authorizing officer authority over all budget lines allocated to the General Directorate of the Presidency. Additionally, 01E coordinates critical personnel and administrative processes on behalf of general management—including rating procedures, staff mobility arrangements, logistics and mission documentation, service reorganization protocols, and horizontal cross-functional files—while representing institutional leadership across inter-Directorate-General forums, resource director assemblies, inter-service coordination meetings, and specialized collaborative structures with Personnel, Finance, Infrastructure, and Logistics directorates. The unit contributes substantively to institutional strategic planning through annual work program development, activity reporting, and forward-thinking analysis regarding administrative evolution and resource management transformation, while simultaneously maintaining active engagement with cross-institutional initiatives focused on skills development, job description analysis, and training program design. Through its governance of occupational health and safety matters, 01E chairs the Advisory Committee for Prevention and Protection at Work (CPPT), organizing monthly meetings, managing agendas and participant selection, and producing comprehensive annual activity programming and reporting documentation.
01E.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction, Supervisory Governance, and Operational Coordination** (25%): Direct and coordinate the integrated activities of Human Resources and Finance and IT units within the Resources Directorate; supervise internal communication and awareness initiatives; manage new strategic projects and institutional event participation; cascade institutional priorities across the Presidency's operational infrastructure.

• **Executive Advisory and Technical Support Services** (18%): Provide comprehensive advisory, support, and technical assistance to the Deputy Secretary General, Director General, all Directors, Heads of Unit, and the SGA Cabinet across human resources management, financial administration, IT infrastructure, logistics operations, official missions, professional development and training, and internal organizational communication.

• **Financial Stewardship and Budgetary Authority** (18%): Exercise subdelegated authorizing officer authority over all budget lines allocated to the General Directorate of the Presidency; maintain fiscal oversight, compliance, and resource stewardship.

• **Personnel Administration and Horizontal Administrative Coordination** (15%): Coordinate and provide strategic guidance on personnel rating procedures, staff mobility frameworks, logistics and mission documentation, service reorganization protocols, and cross-cutting horizontal administrative files on behalf of general management.

• **Inter-Institutional Representation and Strategic Planning Engagement** (7%): Represent general management interests in inter-Directorate-General forums, resource director assemblies, inter-service coordination meetings, and specialized collaborative sessions with Personnel, Finance, Infrastructure, and Logistics directorates.

• **Institutional Strategic Development and Analytical Contribution** (7%): Contribute substantively to annual work program development, activity reporting, and strategic institutional thinking regarding administrative evolution and resource management transformation.

• **Cross-Institutional Projects and Occupational Health Governance** (10%): Engage in specialized cross-institutional initiatives managed by the General Directorate of Personnel focused on skills development, job description analysis, and training program design (5%); chair the Advisory Committee for Prevention and Protection at Work (CPPT), organizing monthly meetings, managing agendas and participant selection, and producing annual activity programming and reporting documentation (5%).

01E10.name: 01E10
original_activities: Strategically and operationally ensure HR for the SGA/DG PRES Cabinet, including: managing positions, publications and recruitment, coordination and monitoring of temporary and contractual agents (AC-People), the mobility exercise; Inter-DG Steering Group HR and other horizontal working groups, such as inter-DG Action Teams for administrative simplification. Manage the position organization chart (fillings, upgrades, reinstatements and departures). Monitoring of budget envelopes.

Ensure the planning and management of professional training (monitoring the envelope). Participate in the Training Officers network. Monitor the reception of new colleagues and provide career advice (SCOP-RELOP network). Ensure the preparation and contribution of general management to 'Career Days'. Manage requests for study visits, away days, external training. Ensure the coordination and management of interns (T-People): selection, reception and monitoring.

Ensure the management of property and logistics for general management (removals and furniture requests). Ensure the management of the general management offices in the three locations, including with the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics in view of major moves. Coordinate requests for office supplies. Participate in the Inter-DG Steering Group Facility Management group and develop CO-MOVE statistics. GBI network.

Manage the missions of general management, including monitoring the budget envelope. Participate in the Inter DG Missions group. Develop mission statistics. Contribute to EMAS awareness-raising actions.

Participate in the rating exercise (eRapnot supervision), update the rated/rater and corresponding listings. Provide support to raters. Participate in the merit points exercise, perform merit point and promotion history updates. Providing responses to section 90 complaints.
Ensure the management of rights (illnesses, absences, leave, CCP, retirement, overtime); manage and monitor the budget envelope, internship reports, confirmation as CdU, working time and teleworking.
Manage documents - setting up document management rules and developing guidelines. Establish, organize and update the management table. Assist and supervise speakers.
Ensure responsibility for 'Gender and Equality Coordinators', the organization of other events; participate in horizontal projects of the General Directorate of Personnel.
01E10.refined_description: Unit 01E10 operates as the integrated administrative and human resources management office within Direction 01E of the General Directorate of the Presidency, providing comprehensive operational and strategic support to the Cabinet of the Secretary-General/Director-General and the Presidency. Functioning as a critical interface between senior leadership and the Parliament's administrative infrastructure, the unit exercises direct accountability for budget envelope monitoring across all functional domains and maintains essential coordination relationships with the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics, the Training Officers network, inter-Directorate General steering groups, and multiple horizontal working groups within the General Directorate of Personnel. Unit 01E10 operates alongside sibling unit 01E20 within the 01E Direction hierarchy to deliver the comprehensive administrative support essential to the Presidency's operational effectiveness. The unit's mission encompasses four primary integrated domains: strategic and operational human resources administration including personnel planning, recruitment coordination, and employment lifecycle management of temporary and contractual agents; professional development and career progression support; facilities, logistics, and property management across the Parliament's three institutional locations; and mission management with associated budget oversight. Beyond these core functions, the unit maintains substantive responsibility for specialized administrative governance including performance rating systems, leave and rights administration, document management, equality initiatives, and participation in institutional compliance programs including environmental awareness-raising (EMAS) activities, thereby supporting the overarching governance objectives of the General Directorate of Personnel and the broader institutional framework.
01E10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Personnel and Human Resources Administration** (40%): Strategic and operational HR management for the SGA/DG PRES Cabinet encompassing position management, recruitment coordination, temporary and contractual agent oversight (AC-People), mobility exercises, organizational chart maintenance with position fillings/upgrades/reinstatements/departures tracking, participation in inter-DG HR steering groups and administrative simplification initiatives, and budget envelope monitoring across personnel functions.

• **Professional Development and Career Management** (20%): Planning and management of professional training programs with envelope monitoring; participation in Training Officers networks and SCOP-RELOP career advisory services; new colleague reception and Career Days preparation; study visit and external training request management; coordination of intern selection, reception, and monitoring (T-People); and general management contribution to career pathway support initiatives.

• **Facilities, Logistics, and Infrastructure Coordination** (15%): Property and logistics management for general management operations across three institutional locations including removal and furniture request processing, office supply coordination, and multi-location office administration; coordination with the General Directorate of Infrastructure and Logistics for major relocation projects; participation in inter-DG Facility Management steering group; CO-MOVE statistics development; and GBI network engagement.

• **Mission Management and Budget Oversight** (15%): Comprehensive management of general management missions with budget envelope monitoring and reporting; participation in inter-DG Missions coordination group; mission statistics development; and contribution to EMAS environmental awareness-raising initiatives.

• **Performance Management, Leave Administration, and Administrative Governance** (25%): Performance rating exercise management including eRapnot supervision and rater support; merit points exercise administration with history updates; Section 90 complaint response handling; rights management encompassing illness, absence, leave, CCP, retirement, and overtime administration with budget monitoring; working time and teleworking oversight; document management system establishment with rule development and guideline creation; management table establishment, organization, and maintenance; speaker assistance and supervision; Gender and Equality Coordinators responsibility; event organization; and participation in horizontal projects of the General Directorate of Personnel.

01E20.name: 01E20
original_activities: Ensure the planning, preparation and execution of the annual budget of the General Directorate of the Presidency, including regular monitoring of the situation of credits allocated to the General Directorate and the management of the subdelegated authorizing officer delegation system.
Advise initiators upstream of procurement and budgetary procedures, including ex-ante verification of any budgetary transaction.

Ensure IT management and governance of the General Directorate of the Presidency, including the implementation of the Agile IT methodology and the establishment and monitoring of IT programs and plans.
Identify the IT needs of the Directorate-General of the Presidency and ensure the implementation of the European Parliament's IT strategy within the Directorate-General.
Manage decentralized DG PRES IT projects: plan, execute and supervise IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality.
Develop responses and write periodic reports and other reports at the request of political authorities, the General Directorate of Finance, the Internal Auditor or the Court of Auditors.
Other missions.
01E20.refined_description:
**

Unit 01E20 functions as an integrated administrative and strategic support unit within Direction 01E of the General Directorate of the Presidency, operating at the second hierarchical level of the European Parliament's institutional structure. The unit bears primary responsibility for comprehensive financial stewardship, budgetary governance, and information technology management across the General Directorate, thereby enabling effective institutional operations and strategic alignment with Parliament-wide priorities. As a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy, Unit 01E20 operates without subordinate units while maintaining coordination with sibling unit 01E10 under the strategic direction of parent unit 01E. The unit's operational mandate encompasses four integrated domains: financial planning and budgetary oversight (25% of workload), upstream advisory services and ex-ante transaction verification (20% of workload), comprehensive IT governance and strategic implementation aligned with institutional digital transformation objectives (40% of workload), and external reporting and compliance functions (10% of workload), with an additional 5% allocated to miscellaneous operational missions as assigned by institutional authorities. Through these functions, Unit 01E20 ensures fiscal accountability, procedural compliance, technological infrastructure optimization, and institutional transparency while maintaining coordination across the General Directorate's administrative and strategic operations.

**
01E20.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Financial Planning, Budgetary Management & Oversight** (25%): Ensure comprehensive planning, preparation, and execution of the annual General Directorate of the Presidency budget; maintain continuous monitoring of allocated credits and credit utilization; manage subdelegated authorizing officer delegation systems to ensure fiscal accountability, resource optimization, and compliance with budgetary regulations.

• **Budgetary Advisory Services & Transactional Verification** (20%): Provide upstream advisory support to budget initiators and procurement officers; conduct ex-ante verification of all budgetary transactions to ensure regulatory compliance, procedural correctness, financial integrity, and prevention of irregularities prior to commitment.

• **IT Governance, Strategic Implementation & Methodology** (40%): Manage IT governance and strategic implementation across the General Directorate through Agile IT methodology implementation, establishment and monitoring of IT programs and plans, and optimization of technological infrastructure (20%); identify and prioritize IT requirements for the General Directorate while ensuring implementation of the European Parliament's overarching IT strategy to align departmental capabilities with institution-wide digital transformation objectives (20%).

• **IT Project Management** (5%): Plan, execute, and supervise decentralized DG PRES IT projects with comprehensive management of scope, time, budget, and quality constraints to achieve defined organizational objectives.

• **Reporting, Compliance & External Accountability** (5%): Develop comprehensive responses and prepare periodic and ad-hoc reports for political authorities, the General Directorate of Finance, the Internal Auditor, and the Court of Auditors to ensure institutional transparency and accountability.

• **Miscellaneous Operational Missions** (5%): Execute additional assignments and other missions as directed by institutional authorities to support broader General Directorate operational requirements.

01F.name: 01F
original_activities: Monitor management actions, in particular, the development of a new electronic document management tool in the European Parliament, the establishment of structures for the processing of classified information, the development and application of artificial intelligence tools, and the digitalization of workflows.

Provide direction, coordination, animation and supervise the activities of the management in order to contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the general management and the proper functioning of the Institution.
Supervise activities in the areas of classified information, archives, official mail, and document management within the European Parliament.
Ensure the administrative management of the department as well as the monitoring of the Office's working groups and administrative groups falling within its areas of competence.
Perform the functions of Safety Certification Authority.
01F.refined_description: Unit 01F (Direction) operates as a strategic Direction-level entity within the European Parliament's apex administrative structure (Directorate General 01), functioning as the central command and oversight mechanism for institutional management and operational governance. Reporting directly to the Secretary General through the DG 01 hierarchy, this unit exercises comprehensive supervisory authority across five principal activity domains that collectively constitute 100% of its workload. Unit 01F operationalizes its strategic mandate through four subordinate operational units (01F10: Mail Services and Institutional Communications; 01F20: specialized operational support; 01F30: Archives Management and Digital Preservation; and 01F40: Document Management Center), while coordinating laterally with multiple sibling units within the broader DG 01 structure to ensure coherent institutional management across the Parliament's administrative apparatus. The unit's primary mission encompasses monitoring and advancing institutional modernization through strategic oversight of management initiatives, provision of directional guidance and organizational coordination, maintenance of specialized supervisory authority over critical administrative functions, comprehensive departmental administrative management, and execution of technical security certification responsibilities. These interconnected activities collectively enable Unit 01F to fulfill its role as the Parliament's central governance mechanism for information management, digital transformation, organizational coordination, and institutional modernization while maintaining the institutional frameworks and operational standards necessary for the Parliament's administrative effectiveness.
01F.refined_activity_summary:
• **Institutional Modernization and Digital Transformation Oversight** (30%): Monitoring and directing management actions concerning the development and implementation of new electronic document management tools, establishment of secure institutional frameworks for classified information processing, development and deployment of artificial intelligence tools, and orchestration of institutional workflow digitalization initiatives

• **Strategic Direction and Organizational Governance** (25%): Provision of strategic direction, coordination, animation, and supervisory oversight of management activities to achieve general management objectives and ensure optimal institutional proper functioning and alignment with Parliament's strategic priorities

• **Information and Document Administration Supervision** (25%): Comprehensive supervisory authority over classified information management, archives administration, official mail services, and document management operations, exercising direct oversight of these critical administrative functions throughout the Parliament

• **Departmental and Administrative Management** (18%): Administrative management of the direction's departmental operations, monitoring of Office working groups and administrative committees, and oversight of administrative governance structures falling within jurisdictional competence

• **Technical Security Certification Functions** (2%): Execution of Safety Certification Authority responsibilities and related technical security certification duties

01F10.name: 01F10
original_activities: Receive and forward mail arriving at the Institution (checking, sorting and dispatching inter-building, inter-site and inter-Institution mail, recording packages and registered mail, customer service).

Process the official mail of the European Parliament and the President's Letter (registration, summary and attribution, support for GEDA users regarding the registration of mail, active contribution to tests relating to the future BRICKS application).
Process outgoing mail (registration, postage, express mail) and manage the return/destruction of mail refused by the recipient.
Ensure coordination between sectors and the 3 sites.
Ensure budgetary management relating to the reception and dispatch of the institution's mail on the 3 sites (Strasbourg, Brussels and Luxembourg).
Contribute to the development of essential IT tools (Track & Trace, Xwiki, Various Migrations, etc.).
Update the information available to customers on various media (PRESnet, INTRANET, Counters, Wiki, etc.).
01F10.refined_description: Unit 01F10 functions as the European Parliament's central Mail Services and Institutional Correspondence Management hub, operating as a specialized operational unit within Direction 01F (itself positioned within DG 01, the institution's apex administrative structure). The unit bears primary responsibility for comprehensive lifecycle management of institutional mail flows across all three official parliamentary sites—Strasbourg, Brussels, and Luxembourg—encompassing the complete spectrum of postal and document management operations from initial reception through final dispatch. This multifaceted operational mandate requires continuous coordination across organizational sectors and institutional locations to ensure seamless mail services integration while maintaining critical support functions for official correspondence processing and emerging digital infrastructure systems. Unit 01F10 operates as a leaf-node unit within the Direction 01F hierarchy, functioning alongside sibling units 01F20, 01F30, and 01F40 to deliver integrated administrative services to the European Parliament's institutional framework. The unit's resource allocation reflects its complex operational environment, with substantial capacity dedicated to mail logistics operations (42%), official correspondence and document management (20%), institutional coordination and financial oversight (25%), and information systems development coupled with customer communication maintenance (13%).
01F10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Mail Handling and Logistics Operations** (42%): Reception, checking, sorting, and dispatching of all arriving institutional correspondence across inter-building, inter-site, and inter-institutional channels; management of package receipt and registered mail recording; provision of customer service; processing of outgoing mail including registration, postage management, and express mail handling; administration of refused and destroyed mail returns and destruction procedures

• **Official Correspondence and Document Management** (20%): Registration, summarization, and attribution of European Parliament official correspondence and Presidential letters; provision of technical support to GEDA system users; active participation in testing and development protocols for the BRICKS application infrastructure; comprehensive official mail lifecycle processing

• **Institutional Coordination and Financial Stewardship** (25%): Cross-sector and multi-site operational coordination ensuring seamless integration across Strasbourg, Brussels, and Luxembourg locations; budgetary management and financial oversight of mail reception and dispatch operations across all three institutional sites; coordination of operational synchronization across organizational sectors

• **Information Systems Development and Customer Communication** (13%): Contribution to development and enhancement of critical institutional IT infrastructure tools including Track & Trace systems, Xwiki platforms, and application system migrations; maintenance and updating of customer-facing information across multiple communication channels including PRESnet, institutional intranet systems, physical service counters, and wiki-based knowledge repositories

01F20.name: 01F20
