original_activities: Manage all aspects related to the recording, storage, access management, indexing and archiving of classified information with a view to its consultation and creation in accordance with the regulations applicable to the treatment of confidential information by the European Parliament and interinstitutional agreements on the transmission of classified information.
Manage secure facilities, including drafting necessary adaptations to operational security procedures and reviewing procedures for handling confidential information.

Present the mandatory security measures regarding classified information in collaboration with the General Directorate of Security and Protection, contribute to possible internal communication campaigns on the services available.

Assist the Secretary General in the implementation of the rules for processing classified information in the European Parliament, collect data with a view to producing statistics and draft the annual report of the Secretary General of the European Parliament to the Bureau on their application.
Provide the support requested during the negotiations of interinstitutional agreements and regulations regarding the transmission of classified information.
01F20.refined_description: Unit 01F20 operates as a specialized administrative and operational unit within Direction 01F of the European Parliament's central governance structure, serving as the institutional custodian for classified information management and security compliance throughout the complete lifecycle of confidential materials. The unit's mission encompasses comprehensive management of classified information in accordance with European Parliament regulations and interinstitutional agreements governing confidential information transmission, while maintaining operational alignment with sibling units 01F10, 01F30, and 01F40 under Direction 01F's strategic oversight. Unit 01F20 allocates substantial resources across five integrated functional domains: recording, storing, and managing access controls for classified materials; maintaining secure facilities infrastructure; developing and continuously refining adaptive operational security procedures; ensuring compliance with institutional security governance frameworks; and supporting interinstitutional negotiations and regulatory development processes. The unit collaborates actively with the General Directorate of Security and Protection to present mandatory security measures, contribute to internal awareness campaigns regarding available security services, and support the Secretary General's implementation of classified information processing rules through data collection, statistical analysis, and annual compliance reporting to the Bureau. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 01F20 functions as an essential compliance and operational security entity that ensures institutional accountability, regulatory adherence, and the protection of confidential materials across the European Parliament's administrative apparatus.
01F20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Classified Information Lifecycle Management** (40%): Recording, storage, access management, indexing, and archiving of classified information with consultation and creation capabilities in full compliance with European Parliament regulations and interinstitutional agreements on confidential information transmission

• **Secure Facilities and Operational Security Procedures** (30%): Management of secure facilities infrastructure, drafting and continuous adaptation of operational security procedures, and comprehensive review of protocols for handling confidential information to maintain infrastructure integrity

• **Security Governance, Compliance Monitoring, and Institutional Communication** (20%): Presentation of mandatory security measures in active collaboration with the General Directorate of Security and Protection; contribution to internal awareness campaigns regarding available security services; administrative support to the Secretary General in implementing classified information processing rules; data collection for statistical analysis; and preparation of annual compliance reports to the Bureau

• **Interinstitutional Coordination and Regulatory Support** (10%): Provision of specialized support during interinstitutional negotiations and regulatory development processes concerning classified information transmission protocols between EU institutions

01F30.name: 01F30
original_activities: Processing of archives to guarantee their integrity and readability over time: identify and codify each document, manage files and procedures, define classification frameworks, collect funds, ensure administrative and financial management of contracts and external service providers.

Acquisition and conservation: acquire and receive documents transferred to the Archives, organize and manage storage spaces for conservation purposes, prepare transfers to the Historical Archives of the European Union, manage consultation equipment, archive incoming/outgoing mail from the European Parliament.

Accessibility and promotion: ensuring documentary research and making documents available, facilitating research in the archives thanks to a chatbox equipped with artificial intelligence, promoting the archives and promoting their dissemination, welcoming researchers and supporting research.

Digitization: carry out digital archiving in order to guarantee the integrity and readability of digital archives over time; manage the archives information system (CLAVIS) and its integration into the European Parliament information system, ensure the online availability of communicable archives, manage digital storage spaces, manage conservation formats and development of artificial intelligence tools (Archbot).
01F30.refined_description:
Unit 01F30 functions as the European Parliament's Archives and Document Management Centre, operating as a specialized operational unit within Direction 01F (itself situated within the apex Directorate General 01). The unit serves as the institutional custodian of parliamentary documentary heritage and institutional memory, ensuring systematic preservation, accessibility, and long-term integrity of the European Parliament's comprehensive documentary archives. Operating as a leaf-node unit without subordinate divisions, 01F30 executes four interconnected operational domains that collectively enable institutional transparency, historical documentation, and knowledge preservation across the Parliament's administrative framework. The unit coordinates functionally with sibling units 01F10, 01F20, and 01F40 under the strategic direction of parent unit 01F to deliver cohesive institutional support services. Through systematic archival processing, acquisition and conservation management, research accessibility facilitation, and digital transformation initiatives—supported by advanced technological infrastructure including the CLAVIS information system and artificial intelligence tools—Unit 01F30 positions itself as a critical enabler of the European Parliament's documentary governance, ensuring that parliamentary institutional memory remains secure, accessible, and preserved for current and future scholarly and administrative purposes.

---

##
01F30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Archives Processing, Integrity Management, and Administrative-Financial Oversight** (34% of workload): Comprehensive document identification, codification, and classification framework development; archival file and procedure management; fund collection administration; contracting and financial management of external service providers and consultants; administrative oversight ensuring long-term document integrity, readability, and institutional reliability across all archival holdings.

• **Acquisition, Conservation, and Physical Asset Management** (25% of workload): Document acquisition and receipt from parliamentary sources; organization and management of physical storage spaces for conservation purposes; preparation and coordination of transfers to the Historical Archives of the European Union; consultation equipment administration and maintenance; systematic archiving and management of institutional incoming and outgoing parliamentary correspondence to ensure comprehensive institutional mail preservation.

• **Research Accessibility, Promotion, and User Services** (21% of workload): Documentary research facilitation and document availability assurance through multiple channels; deployment and administration of advanced AI-equipped chatbot research support infrastructure (Archbot); archives promotion and dissemination initiatives; researcher reception and direct research support services; enhancement of scholarly and institutional access to parliamentary documentation to advance institutional transparency.

• **Digital Transformation, Systems Administration, and Technological Innovation** (20% of workload): Digital archiving and digital archive integrity assurance to guarantee long-term digital readability; CLAVIS archival information system management and integration within broader European Parliament IT infrastructure; online availability management of communicable archived materials; digital storage space administration and optimization; conservation format standards development and implementation; development, deployment, and management of artificial intelligence tools and applications (Archbot) to enhance preservation, accessibility, and research capabilities.

01F40.name: 01F40
original_activities: Define the requirements for the future electronic document management system for Parliament - in collaboration with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity. Ensure the link with users as close as possible to their activities, including the organization of tests and analysis of results. Prepare the deployment and use of the system. Organize training - in collaboration with Learning & Development.

Provide all of Parliament's general directorates with the expertise necessary for the evaluation and simplification of their procedures from a document management point of view, including the organization of training sessions in the unit's area of ​​expertise (document management principles, e-signatures), to enable their digitization in compliance with the main regulatory frameworks established for information management.
Organize meetings of the Inter-DG Steering group on Document Management (GIDOC). Monitor it and support the activity of its members.
01F40.refined_description: Unit 01F40 operates as a specialized center of excellence within Direction 01F of the European Parliament's administrative hierarchy, serving as the institutional focal point for document management modernization and organizational digital transformation. The unit functions as a leaf-node technical authority within the Direction 01F structure, maintaining direct accountability while operating in functional coordination with sibling units (01F10, 01F20, 01F30) to advance Parliament's institutional modernization objectives. Unit 01F40's integrated mission encompasses three interconnected operational domains that collectively represent the full scope of its institutional mandate: strategic leadership of the Parliament's future electronic document management system development and deployment, delivery of specialized document management expertise across all Parliament general directorates to support procedural modernization and digitization initiatives, and facilitation of inter-directorate governance and coordination mechanisms. The unit's activities are characterized by systematic engagement with end-users throughout implementation lifecycles, direct collaboration with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity on technical and functional requirement definition, coordinated partnership with Learning & Development on training delivery, and compliance with established regulatory frameworks governing information management across the institution. Through these integrated capabilities, Unit 01F40 serves as both a strategic authority on document management policy and a practical enabler of organizational procedure simplification and digital transformation across Parliament's administrative structure.
01F40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Electronic Document Management System Development, Testing, and Deployment** (50%): Define comprehensive technical and functional requirements for Parliament's future electronic document management platform through direct collaboration with the General Directorate of Information Technology and Cybersecurity; establish direct end-user engagement mechanisms aligned with operational requirements throughout the development lifecycle; organize and execute comprehensive testing protocols with systematic results analysis; prepare and execute system deployment infrastructure and activities; coordinate training delivery in partnership with Learning & Development to ensure institutional readiness.

• **Specialized Document Management Expertise and Organizational Capability Building** (45%): Provide targeted advisory services to all Parliament general directorates for procedure evaluation and simplification from document management perspectives; deliver formalized training sessions on foundational document management principles and electronic signature technologies; enable organizational digitization initiatives across administrative units in compliance with established regulatory frameworks for information management; build institutional capacity for procedure modernization and systematic document management reform.

• **Inter-Directorate Governance, Coordination, and Steering Group Support** (5%): Organize and facilitate meetings of the Inter-DG Steering Group on Document Management (GIDOC); monitor steering group activities and progress on cross-directorate document management coordination matters; provide operational support to member organizations and facilitate cross-directorate coordination on document management policy and implementation matters.

original_activities: Ensure the security of the European Parliament, people and property at the three workplaces in compliance with regulations concerning security and protection. Provide professional general security, fire and first aid services, as well as close protection of the President and quality regulatory armed protection, meeting the highest professional standards, based on the assessment of risks tailored to parliamentary activities.

Ensure a specific personnel management policy adapted to the needs of DG SAFE by guaranteeing the recruitment, training of agents and the operational organization of their surveillance and prevention missions on an annual basis in order to guarantee the highest level of professionalization, competence and motivation. Support the operations of the DG through budgetary and financial management of contracts, budget execution and operation of IT systems.

Deploy, at the three workplaces, appropriate security measures for users and buildings. Guarantee the management of access requests and the allocation of access titles based on the regulations in force. Offer a high-level service and ad-hoc risk analyzes for the management and reception of guests and visitors to Parliament, particularly during major events taking place within the institution.

Ensure the protection of information, prevention and fight against hybrid threats and espionage. Contribute to ensuring continuity of services, risk and crisis management inside and outside Parliament premises, including business continuity planning and testing within DG SAFE. Coordinate the crisis unit and manage the European Parliament's electronic countermeasures program (TSCM).

Ensure the installation, operation and maintenance of the Institution's physical security systems on the three sites and equip the Institution with new cutting-edge technologies in security, access control and accreditation capable of assimilating future technological developments (iPACS).

Liaise with other directorates-general, European Union institutions and national security authorities, by launching both information and awareness campaigns on a participatory culture of security and safety, and by strengthening interinstitutional cooperation in this area.
12.refined_description:
Unit 12, designated as the Directorate-General for Security and Safety (DG SAFE), operates as the European Parliament's primary security authority, reporting directly to the Secretary General with comprehensive responsibility for institutional protection across all three parliamentary workplaces. The unit's mandate encompasses a multifaceted security framework that integrates professional security operations, specialized personnel management, physical access control systems, information security capabilities, technological infrastructure modernization, and interinstitutional cooperation mechanisms—all calibrated to comply with applicable security regulations and aligned with risk assessments tailored to parliamentary activities and operational continuity requirements. DG SAFE exercises governance over five subordinate organizational units (12-10, 12A, 12B, 12C, and 12D) that collectively operationalize 29 distinct activities within the comprehensive security and safety mandate. The directorate fulfills its protective mission by implementing risk-based security measures that meet the highest professional standards while simultaneously supporting the Parliament's legislative and administrative functions, ensuring safeguarding of the institution's personnel, assets, and operational infrastructure. Through its integrated organizational structure and subordinate units' specialized functions, Unit 12 maintains institutional security while preserving the Parliament's capacity for effective governance and democratic operations.

---

##
12.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Security Operations and Close Protection Services** (35%): Provision of professional general security services, fire protection, and first aid services across all three parliamentary workplaces; delivery of specialized close protection services for the President; provision of armed protection services meeting quality and regulatory standards; implementation of risk-based security assessments tailored to specific parliamentary activities; full compliance with all applicable security and protection regulations and regulatory frameworks.

• **Personnel Management, Training, and Operational Administration** (20%): Development and implementation of specialized personnel management policies adapted to DG SAFE operational requirements; recruitment and comprehensive training of security agents to maintain highest standards of professionalization and competence; annual operational organization of surveillance and prevention missions; budgetary and financial contract management; budget execution oversight and financial administration; IT systems operation and maintenance to support organizational functions and infrastructure.

• **Physical Access Control, Guest Management, and Visitor Services** (15%): Deployment of appropriate protective security measures for users and buildings across all three parliamentary workplaces; management of access requests and allocation of access credentials in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements; provision of high-level guest and visitor reception services; conduct of ad-hoc risk analyses for major institutional events; specialized management protocols for visiting dignitaries and high-risk reception scenarios; facilitation of institutional functions through professional visitor management.

• **Information Security, Threat Prevention, and Crisis Management** (15%): Protection of institutional information systems and data; prevention and active countermeasures against hybrid threats and espionage; service continuity assurance and business continuity planning with testing protocols; risk assessment and crisis management coordination for both on-premises and external scenarios; coordination of institutional crisis response unit; management of the European Parliament's electronic countermeasures program (TSCM); maintenance of organizational resilience and operational continuity across all security domains.

• **Physical Security Infrastructure and Technological Modernization** (10%): Installation, operation, and maintenance of physical security systems across all three parliamentary sites; procurement and deployment of cutting-edge security technologies; implementation of advanced access control and accreditation systems including next-generation iPACS (integrated Physical Access Control System) platform; integration of modern technological capabilities designed for technological scalability and assimilation of future security advancements; modernization of institutional protective infrastructure.

• **Interinstitutional Liaison and Security Culture Development** (5%): Establishment and maintenance of liaison relationships with other European Parliament directorates-general, European Union institutions, and national security authorities; launch and coordination of information and awareness campaigns promoting participatory security culture; strengthening of interinstitutional cooperation mechanisms in security and safety domains; development of collaborative frameworks supporting institutional and intergovernmental security objectives.

original_activities: Develop security awareness activities in the European Parliament.
Improve internal communications at the General Directorate of Security and Protection and in Parliament, including audiovisual and graphic projects.
Develop inter-institutional cooperation in security awareness.
Support the Director General in activities related to the strategic execution framework and annual reporting cycle.
Monitor legal and regulatory compliance in the areas of risks, crises and business continuity.
Provide strategic and coordination support for General Management in the area of ​​data protection.
Support the Director General's office in horizontal tasks.
12-10.refined_description: Unit 12-10 operates as a specialized strategic support and coordination directorate within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, functioning as the institutional nexus for security awareness, governance, compliance frameworks, and organizational coordination. Positioned at the first hierarchical level beneath Unit 12 (which reports directly to the Secretary General), Unit 12-10 serves as the European Parliament's primary vehicle for advancing integrated security governance while coordinating operationally with sibling units 12A, 12B, 12C, and 12D to constitute the Parliament's comprehensive security and safety apparatus. The unit's portfolio encompasses seven distinct operational domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, spanning security awareness development, internal communications infrastructure, inter-institutional cooperation frameworks, strategic executive support, compliance and risk management oversight, data protection governance, and horizontal administrative functions. Through this integrated operational structure, Unit 12-10 bridges strategic direction-setting with operational implementation, establishing foundational institutional knowledge and behavioral standards for security protocols while simultaneously amplifying the impact of security frameworks through external stakeholder collaboration and transparent information dissemination. The unit's dual mandate—combining internal institutional protection with external cooperative engagement—positions it as a critical institutional nexus that ensures alignment between operational activities, strategic objectives, and regulatory compliance requirements across the Parliament's security and governance architecture.
12-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Security Awareness and Institutional Protection** (25%): Development of comprehensive security awareness activities across the European Parliament, establishing foundational institutional knowledge and behavioral standards for security protocols while building institutional security culture.

• **Communications and Information Dissemination** (20%): Enhancement of internal communications infrastructure throughout the General Directorate of Security and Protection and the broader Parliamentary institution, encompassing audiovisual production and graphic design initiatives that facilitate transparent information dissemination and support security messaging.

• **Inter-Institutional Cooperation and Strategic Coordination** (30%): Cultivation of inter-institutional cooperation frameworks in security awareness (15%), establishing collaborative relationships with external stakeholders and partner organizations to amplify security protocol impact beyond Parliament's internal structure, combined with strategic support to the Director General regarding strategic execution frameworks and annual reporting cycles (15%), ensuring organizational alignment and institutional accountability mechanisms.

• **Compliance, Risk Management, and Data Protection Governance** (20%): Monitoring of legal and regulatory adherence within risk assessment, crisis management, and business continuity planning domains (10%), combined with strategic guidance and coordination support to General Management in implementing comprehensive data protection frameworks (10%), addressing institutional governance and protective regulatory requirements.

• **Horizontal Administrative Functions** (5%): Support services provided to the Director General's office for cross-cutting organizational tasks that facilitate institutional operations.

12A.name: 12A
original_activities: Ensure, in the three workplaces of the European Parliament, the safety of people and property in compliance with the rules established by the political authorities and their implementing measures. Guarantee a quality welcome, local security and permanent assistance to users of the Institution. Ensure the close protection of the President and the security of strategic points of the Parliament premises by armed agents.

Coordinate, at management level, the implementation of the overall security concept: define the objectives and coordinate the units of Directorate A, guarantee the harmonization of the 3 sites at the operational, administrative and logistical level. Coordinate tasks and ensure the exchange of information between the different management units and the directorates of the General Directorate of Security and Protection.

Provide support and expertise to the competent services responsible for the different phases of renovation of the SPAAK building in Brussels, as well as for other new real estate projects on the three sites.

Represent the European Parliament externally: liaise and organize the necessary collaboration with the competent authorities of the host countries and the security services of the other Institutions in its area of ​​responsibility.

Other missions: contribute to the reassessment of the technical measures necessary to strengthen security on the three sites, contribute to the projects of other departments which could have an impact on operational management.
12A.refined_description: Unit 12A functions as the directional management and operational coordination hub within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), serving as the primary interface between senior institutional leadership and the multi-site security apparatus of the European Parliament across its three geographically dispersed workplaces in Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg. The unit operates as both a strategic direction-level authority and primary operational security coordinator, maintaining dual responsibility for establishing overarching security objectives while simultaneously ensuring comprehensive personnel and asset protection in full compliance with political authority directives and implementing regulations. Unit 12A supervises multiple specialized operational units (12A20, 12A30, and associated child units) while coordinating horizontally with sibling units (12-10, 12B, 12C, and 12D) within the broader DG 12 security governance structure, thereby operationalizing strategic directives across the Parliament's distributed infrastructure. The unit's integrated functional portfolio encompasses five complementary activity streams that collectively represent 100% of documented workload allocation, spanning physical security operations, strategic coordination and harmonization, technical support for real estate projects, external institutional liaison, and strategic assessment initiatives. Through these coordinated activities executed at operational, administrative, and logistical levels across all three sites, Unit 12A ensures institutional continuity, personnel safety, operational integrity, and seamless information exchange between subordinate operational units, peer directorates, host country competent authorities, and security services of other EU Institutions within its area of responsibility.
12A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Operational Security and Physical Protection** (55%): Ensure comprehensive safety of personnel and assets across three parliamentary workplaces (Brussels, Strasbourg, Luxembourg) in full compliance with political authority directives and implementing regulations; deliver quality welcome and visitor reception services; maintain permanent on-site user assistance and local security infrastructure; execute close protection operations for the President and deploy armed security personnel for strategic Parliament premises

• **Strategic Coordination and Harmonization** (25%): Define overarching security objectives and coordinate all subordinate units within Directorate A; harmonize operational, administrative, and logistical functions across three geographically dispersed sites; facilitate inter-unit task coordination and information exchange between operational units, peer directorates within DG 12, and other General Directorate management entities

• **Technical Support and Real Estate Integration** (10%): Provide specialized technical security expertise to competent services overseeing SPAAK building renovation phases in Brussels and other new real estate development projects across all three parliamentary sites

• **External Relations and Institutional Liaison** (5%): Represent the European Parliament's security interests externally; establish and maintain collaborative coordination with host country competent authorities and security services of other EU Institutions within operational responsibility areas

• **Strategic Assessment and Cross-Departmental Initiatives** (5%): Contribute to reassessment of technical security measures necessary for site strengthening and facility protection; participate in cross-departmental projects with potential operational management implications

12A20.name: 12A20
original_activities: Ensure all operational functions relating to the security and protection of people and property in Brussels: ensure control of access to the buildings and installations of the European Parliament (people, vehicles, supplies and mail); ensure the smooth running of official visits, meetings and events of all kinds; ensure the application and compliance with national and European safety legislation.

Implement the decisions taken by the authorities of the European Parliament in matters of security: formulate opinions on the organization of events and ensure the application of instructions by staff, actively participate in the design of construction and development projects for buildings and participate in monitoring the work.

Contribute to the implementation of the overall security concept in its areas of expertise (participate in selection procedures, in the design of the organization, rules and human deployment, in the implementation of the annual operational organization and in the supervision and evaluation of staff).
Ensure liaison with national authorities and those of other institutions in matters of security (securing the European district, crimes, maintaining order, best practices, etc.).

Ensure effective coordination of internal activities between general directorates, ensuring the implementation of all operational security measures necessary for the smooth running of events, in particular those linked to the renovation of the Spaak building.
12A20.refined_description: Unit 12A20 functions as the operational security and protection unit within Direction 12A of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), serving as the primary executor of comprehensive security protocols and protective measures across the European Parliament's Brussels facilities. Operating as a critical leaf node within the institutional security hierarchy, the unit bears primary responsibility for the assurance of all operational functions relating to security and protection of people, assets, and infrastructure, encompassing the comprehensive management of physical security, access control protocols for buildings, installations, personnel, vehicles, supplies, and mail, and the facilitation of seamless operational execution for official visits, meetings, and events of all categories. Unit 12A20 simultaneously implements decisions issued by European Parliament authorities on security matters, formulates expert opinions on event organization, enforces institutional security instructions, actively participates in construction and development project design, and provides ongoing monitoring and oversight of implementation activities. The unit contributes substantively to the institution-wide security framework through participation in staff selection procedures, organizational design and rule development, human resource deployment planning, annual operational organization implementation, and continuous staff supervision and evaluation mechanisms. Unit 12A20 maintains critical liaison relationships with national authorities and counterpart EU institutions to coordinate security operations across the European district, address crime prevention and public order maintenance, and facilitate the exchange of security best practices and intelligence. The unit ensures effective horizontal coordination mechanisms across general directorates to guarantee implementation of operational security measures for institutional events, with particular emphasis on specialized security oversight for major infrastructure projects including the Spaak building renovation program. Throughout all activities, Unit 12A20 ensures continuous application and compliance monitoring of national and European safety legislation while operating under the direct supervision of Direction 12A and collaborating with sibling units (12A2001, 12A2002, 12A2003, 12A2004, 12A30, and others) to deliver integrated security outcomes.
12A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Operational Security and Access Control Management** (55%): Comprehensive assurance of all operational security and protection functions for people, property, and infrastructure within Brussels facilities; maintenance of strict access control protocols for buildings, installations, personnel, vehicles, supplies, and mail; facilitation of seamless execution of official visits, meetings, and events of all categories; continuous application and compliance monitoring of national and European safety legislation.

• **Security Decision Implementation and Infrastructure Integration** (20%): Implementation of decisions issued by European Parliament authorities on security matters; formulation of expert opinions on event organization protocols; enforcement of institutional security instructions across personnel; active participation in construction and development project design; provision of ongoing monitoring and oversight of implementation activities.

• **Security Concept Development and Staff Management** (10%): Contribution to operationalization of the Parliament's comprehensive security concept; participation in staff selection procedures; participation in organizational design and rule development; management of human resource deployment planning; implementation of annual operational security organization; conduct of staff supervision and evaluation activities.

• **Inter-Institutional Security Coordination and Liaison** (10%): Establishment and maintenance of cooperative relationships with national authorities and other EU institutions regarding security matters; coordination of European district security operations; address of crime prevention and public order maintenance; facilitation of exchange of security best practices and intelligence.

• **Cross-Directorate Coordination for Institutional Events and Major Projects** (5%): Assurance of effective horizontal coordination mechanisms across general directorates; implementation of operational security measures for institutional events; provision of specialized security oversight for major infrastructure projects, particularly the Spaak building renovation program; maintenance of operational security continuity during institutional events and infrastructure initiatives.

12A2001.name: 12A2001
original_activities: Ensure all operational functions relating to the security and protection of people and property in Brussels: ensure control of access to the buildings and installations of the European Parliament (people, vehicles, supplies and mail); ensure the smooth running of official visits, meetings and events of all kinds.

Ensure and manage the presence of security agents at posts and team rotation. Formulate opinions on the organization of events; optimize the efficiency and responsiveness of operational staff. Guarantee compliance with instructions, operational orders and planning requests. Participate in job audits (regular job inspections).

Liaise with the various services of the General Directorate of Security and Protection as well as with other DGs (INLO, COMM, PRES, etc.) to ensure the smooth running of security services and good cooperation on the ground.

Participate effectively in internal activities between General Directorates, by implementing all operational security measures necessary for the reallocation of spaces, in anticipation of the renovation of the Spaak building, in particular: Reorganization of the SPAAK building and the security system; Implementation of the WIM nursery project for access control.

Liaise with national authorities, in particular with the Belgian police (DAB and PolBru), as well as with those of other institutions in matters of security and safety, when organizing special events having an impact on the European Parliament and/or during visits by high-ranking personalities.
12A2001.refined_description: Unit 12A2001 functions as the operational security and protection command nucleus within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), serving as a critical operational node subordinate to Direction 12A with primary responsibility for comprehensive safeguarding of personnel and physical assets across all European Parliament facilities in Brussels. The unit executes its core operational security mandate through rigorous access control protocols for buildings, installations, personnel, vehicles, supplies, and mail; facilitation and coordination of official visits, meetings, and events of all categories; and maintenance of physical integrity across Parliament installations. Complementing this foundational mission, the unit manages the deployment, positioning, and rotational assignment of security personnel across designated posts while formulating advisory assessments on event organization frameworks and optimizing operational efficiency through compliance verification with institutional directives, operational orders, and scheduling requirements, including participation in regular occupational audits and performance inspections. The unit maintains continuous inter-directorate liaison and coordination with specialized services within the General Directorate of Security and Protection and external Directorates-General (notably INLO, COMM, PRES, and others) to ensure seamless security service delivery and ground-level operational cooperation. Additionally, the unit contributes substantially to cross-directorate strategic infrastructure initiatives, implementing comprehensive operational security measures in support of the Spaak building renovation program—including reorganization of security infrastructure and implementation of the WIM nursery advanced access control project. Finally, the unit sustains external liaison functions with Belgian national authorities (specifically the Directorate for Administrative and Logistics Assistance [DAB] and Brussels Police [PolBru]) and peer institutions to coordinate security protocols during high-impact special events and visits by senior dignitaries affecting Parliament's operational environment.
12A2001.refined_activity_summary:
• **Primary Operational Security & Access Control** (55%): Comprehensive execution of security and protective services across all Brussels-based Parliament facilities; rigorous access control management for personnel, vehicles, supplies, and mail; coordination and facilitation of official visits, meetings, and events of all categories; maintenance of physical integrity of Parliament buildings and installations

• **Personnel Management & Operational Optimization** (20%): Deployment, positioning, and rotational management of security agents across operational posts; formulation of advisory opinions and operational assessments regarding event organization frameworks; optimization of field staff efficiency and responsiveness; verification of compliance with institutional directives, operational orders, and planning requirements; execution of regular occupational audits and job inspections

• **Inter-Directorate Coordination & Strategic Infrastructure Integration** (15%): Active liaison with General Directorate of Security and Protection specialized services and external Directorates-General (INLO, COMM, PRES, and others); implementation of comprehensive operational security measures supporting the Spaak building renovation program, including security infrastructure reorganization; execution of the WIM nursery advanced access control project implementation; coordination of ground-level security service delivery across institutional boundaries

• **External Authority Coordination & High-Level Events Management** (10%): Liaison with Belgian national authorities (DAB and PolBru) and peer institution security services; coordination of security protocols for high-impact special events with Parliament-wide operational impact; management of security arrangements for visits by senior dignitaries and high-ranking personalities; coordination of security protocols during events affecting Parliament's operational environment

12A2002.name: 12A2002
original_activities: Ensure all operational functions relating to the security and protection of people and property in relevant buildings: ensure control of access to buildings and their installations (people, vehicles, and deliveries); ensure the smooth running of official visits, meetings and events of all kinds; ensure the application and compliance with national and European safety legislation.

Implement decisions taken by the European Parliament authorities in matters of security: formulate opinions on the organization of events; initiate and/or actively participate, in accordance with their area of ​​expertise, in the design and implementation of building construction and development projects and participate in monitoring the work.

Contribute to the implementation of the concept of global security in its areas of expertise, in particular through: participation in the design of rules and human deployment, for the implementation of the annual operational organization; supervision and continuous evaluation of personnel assigned to the service; ensure the application of instructions by personnel assigned to security posts in buildings under the jurisdiction of the service.

Ensure liaison with the various services of the General Directorate of Security and Protection, other DGs (INLO, COMM, PRES, etc.), as well as with the national authorities, to guarantee the smooth running of security services and good cooperation on the ground.

Participate effectively in internal activities between general directorates, implementing all operational security measures necessary for the reallocation of spaces, in anticipation of the renovation of the Spaak building, in particular: Establishment of the TREVE2 building and complete reorganization of the security system; Implementation of the new system in the future MONTERRA building, which will ensure continuity of operations.
12A2002.refined_description:
Unit 12A2002 functions as the operational security and building protection service within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), operating under the directional coordination of parent Unit 12A and serving as a critical component in the European Parliament's institutional security infrastructure. The unit bears primary responsibility for ensuring comprehensive security and protection of personnel and property across all European Parliament facilities through multi-faceted operational activities, translating institutional security mandates into ground-level implementation while simultaneously contributing to long-term security framework development. The unit's workload is systematically distributed across five interconnected operational domains that collectively address immediate operational security needs, institutional governance compliance, integrated security system design, inter-institutional coordination, and strategic infrastructure modernization initiatives. Its dominant responsibility encompasses the execution of all operational security functions including rigorous access control protocols for buildings, installations, personnel, vehicles, and deliveries; coordination and facilitation of official visits, meetings, and events of all categories; and implementation and enforcement of national and European safety legislation standards. The unit actively implements decisions and policies established by European Parliament authorities regarding security matters by formulating expert opinions on event organization and participating in the design, implementation, and monitoring of building construction and development projects aligned with security requirements. The unit substantively contributes to the institution's integrated security framework through participation in comprehensive security concept development, including the design of security protocols and human resource deployment strategies, continuous supervision and evaluation of assigned security personnel, and enforcement of instruction compliance at designated security posts. The unit maintains essential horizontal coordination responsibilities with the General Directorate of Security and Protection's constituent services, other relevant Directorates-General (INLO, COMM, PRES, and others), and national authorities to ensure seamless security service operations and effective ground-level cooperation. Additionally, the unit engages in strategic infrastructure transition activities, implementing critical operational security measures for space reallocation initiatives in anticipation of the Spaak building renovation, including the establishment and security system implementation of the TREVE2 building and the deployment of new security systems in the forthcoming MONTERRA building to ensure uninterrupted operational continuity.

12A2004.refined_description:

12A30.refined_description: Unit 12A30 functions as the operational security and site management unit within Direction 12A of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), bearing primary responsibility for comprehensive security infrastructure and day-to-day protective operations across the European Parliament's Strasbourg site. 12A40.refined_description: Unit 12A40 functions as the operational security and protection management unit within Direction 12A of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, bearing primary responsibility for safeguarding personnel, assets, and institutional operations across the European Parliament's Luxembourg installations. 12A50.name: 12A50
original_activities: Ensure close protection of the President of the European Parliament on the three work sites and in any other country during missions. Establish the documents, procedures and processes necessary for the operation of this service.
Organize the armed personnel service in Brussels and Strasbourg. Establish the documents, procedures and processes necessary for the operation of this service.

Contribute to the planning and coordination of close protection systems for the President and armed agents. Organize the training necessary for the exercise of the functions of the members of the two teams. Manage renewals of individual firearm permits.

Maintain collaboration with internal and external partners for the preparation of activities and the execution of missions, in particular with the Protocol department, the Office of the President, operational security units and the national police services concerned.
Maintain a high level of collaboration with the Belgian and French Federal Police to ensure continuing professional training for close protection agents and armed agents.

Other missions: ensure the application of current regulations in the countries of destination of the missions; organize the basic training necessary to obtain individual weapon carrying upon entry into service; prepare procurement procedures and manage contracts; manage equipment inventory.
12A50.refined_description: Unit 12A50, a specialized operational unit within Direction 12A of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG 12), bears primary responsibility for the comprehensive management and execution of presidential protection services across all European Parliament institutional work sites (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg) and during international missions to any country. The unit operates two equally weighted core operational pillars, each representing 35% of total workload: ensuring close protection of the President of the European Parliament across all designated locations and mission contexts, and organizing and managing the armed personnel service infrastructure in Brussels and Strasbourg. Both pillars require the establishment, maintenance, and continuous refinement of all operational documents, procedures, and institutional processes essential to service delivery. Unit 12A50 allocates 10% of its workload to strategic planning and coordination of close protection systems, organization of specialized training programs for both close protection and armed personnel teams, and management of individual firearm permit renewals and compliance activities. The unit dedicates 15% of combined resources to maintaining essential collaborative relationships with internal stakeholders—including the Protocol Department, the Office of the President, and operational security units—as well as external coordination with Belgian and French Federal Police services, encompassing both mission preparation and execution support (10%) and professional training coordination for continuing professional development standards (5%). An additional 5% encompasses regulatory compliance activities across mission destination countries, organization of mandatory foundational weapons training for newly appointed personnel, procurement procedure management and contract administration, and systematic equipment inventory management. Positioned as a peer operational entity within the broader security and protection framework, Unit 12A50 operates under directional oversight of Unit 12A while functioning alongside complementary security units, collectively positioning the unit as the operational foundation for presidential security within the European Parliament's institutional framework.
12A50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Presidential Close Protection Operations** (35%): Ensure comprehensive close protection of the President of the European Parliament across all three institutional work sites (Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg) and during international missions to any country; establish, maintain, and continuously refine all operational documents, procedures, and processes required for service delivery

• **Armed Personnel Service Management** (35%): Organize and manage armed personnel service infrastructure and operations in Brussels and Strasbourg; develop, implement, and maintain all necessary operational documentation, procedures, and institutional processes essential to service operations

• **Training, Planning & Permit Administration** (10%): Contribute to strategic planning and coordination of close protection systems; organize specialized training programs for close protection and armed personnel teams; manage individual firearm permit renewals and compliance activities

• **Internal & External Partnership Coordination** (15%): Maintain operational collaboration with internal stakeholders (Protocol Department, Office of the President, operational security units) and external partners (Belgian and French Federal Police services); coordinate mission preparation and execution activities; ensure professional training coordination and continuing professional development standards for close protection and armed personnel agents

• **Regulatory Compliance & Operational Support** (5%): Ensure application of destination-country regulations for mission sites; organize mandatory foundational weapons training for newly appointed personnel; manage procurement procedures, contract administration, and comprehensive equipment inventory systems

12B.name: 12B
original_activities: Ensure the direction, coordination, exchange of information and animation of the management units as well as guarantee the coherence of the internal processes of the management.

Contribute to the implementation of the concept of global security in its areas of expertise as well as to the definition of objectives and strategies in the field of security and their implementation by the services concerned.
Permanently provide appropriate solutions to security requirements through prevention services and remote monitoring of buildings and technical installations.

Guarantee the management of access requests and the allocation of access permits on the basis of the regulations in force and offer a high-level service for the management and reception of guests and visitors to the European Parliament, particularly during major events taking place within the institution.

Ensure at the three workplaces all functions relating to fire prevention, security and first aid to users of the institution as well as the standardization of Parliament's operational security procedures in the area of ​​competence. Conduct risk analyses, investigations and fire safety audits as a preventative measure or following safety incidents.

Formulate safety advice in matters of fire, prevention and first aid, ensuring compliance with applicable national and European legislation in the field of fire safety and ensuring liaison and necessary collaboration with the competent authorities of host countries and other institutions.
Contribute to good cooperation with the bodies of the European Parliament as well as with its Secretariat in its area of ​​competence and guarantee the implementation of their decisions.
12B.refined_description:
Unit 12B operates as a critical Directorate-level operational and strategic management component within the European Parliament's integrated security and safety framework, functioning directly under parent Directorate-General Unit 12 and exercising directional authority over specialized child units (12B10, 12B40, 12B50, and their respective sub-units 12B5010, 12B5020, 12B5030). The unit serves as the strategic direction and coordination nexus for comprehensive security management across all three parliamentary workplaces, orchestrating security operations, access management, fire safety protocols, and emergency preparedness while ensuring coherence with institutional decisions and host country regulatory requirements. Unit 12B executes its mandate through seven integrated functional domains: (1) strategic direction and management coordination ensuring directional oversight and administrative animation of subordinate management units; (2) security strategy development and policy implementation contributing to global security concept formulation, objective definition, and institutional security policy coherence; (3) operational security monitoring and prevention delivering permanent technical solutions and remote building/technical installation surveillance; (4) access management and visitor services encompassing rigorous permit allocation, high-level guest reception, and major institutional event coordination; (5) fire safety, prevention, and emergency preparedness executing fire prevention functions, conducting risk analyses and audits, standardizing operational security procedures, and maintaining emergency response capability across all institutional locations; (6) fire safety compliance and institutional liaison formulating specialized safety advice and maintaining coordination with host country competent authorities and other EU institutions; and (7) stakeholder cooperation and decision implementation fostering effective engagement with European Parliament bodies, the Secretariat, and sibling units (12-10, 12A, 12C, 12D) to guarantee implementation of institutional decisions within its sphere of competence. This comprehensive organizational structure ensures that Unit 12B functions simultaneously as a governance hub for administrative coherence, a security architecture authority for strategic and operational implementation, and an institutional liaison mechanism for regulatory compliance and cross-organizational alignment.

---

##
12B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Direction, Coordination, and Administrative Animation** (35%): Ensures directional oversight, comprehensive coordination, and information exchange among management units; guarantees internal process coherence and administrative animation of the management structure; implements institutional decisions within unit competence; aligns management operations with organizational objectives

• **Security Strategy Development and Policy Implementation** (15%): Contributes to global security concept development and formulation; defines security objectives and strategies; oversees implementation by relevant services; ensures institutional security policy coherence and objective achievement; establishes security framework for all parliamentary workplaces

• **Operational Security Monitoring and Prevention Services** (15%): Provides permanent and appropriate technical solutions to security requirements; operates prevention services across all institutional locations; conducts continuous remote monitoring of buildings and technical installations; maintains proactive security posture; implements preventive security measures

• **Access Management and Visitor Services** (15%): Manages access requests and rigorous allocation of access permits in accordance with applicable regulations; delivers high-level guest and visitor reception services; coordinates access control protocols during major institutional events; ensures secure and efficient access management across all three workplaces

• **Fire Safety, Prevention, and Emergency Preparedness** (10%): Executes fire prevention, security, and first aid functions across all three parliamentary workplaces; conducts comprehensive risk analyses, investigations, and fire safety audits; standardizes Parliament's operational security procedures; maintains emergency response capability and first aid readiness; investigates safety incidents

• **Fire Safety Compliance and External Institutional Liaison** (5%): Formulates specialized expert safety advice on fire prevention and first aid matters; ensures full compliance with applicable national and European fire safety legislation; maintains essential liaison with competent host country authorities; coordinates with other European institutions on safety matters

• **Stakeholder Cooperation and Institutional Alignment** (5%): Fosters effective cooperation with European Parliament bodies, the Secretariat, and sibling units (12-10, 12A, 12C, 12D); guarantees implementation of institutional decisions and directives; ensures organizational alignment and cross-unit accountability; facilitates information exchange and coordinated decision-making

12B10.name: 12B10
original_activities: Develop and implement measures against the risks of fire and panic in application of national and European regulations as well as first aid measures.
Establish and formulate fire safety opinions on the organization of events and/or technical installations as well as on construction and development projects for buildings.
Provide training for the various actors of the Institution in first aid, fire and evacuation.
Ensure first aid, fire and panic interventions and incidents resulting from the operation of buildings.
Liaise with national authorities and other institutions on fire safety matters.
12B10.refined_description: Unit 12B10 functions as the European Parliament's specialized operational unit responsible for comprehensive fire safety, emergency preparedness, and occupational health protection across all institutional facilities, events, and operations. Operating as a subordinate component within the parent Unit 12B's broader security and safety management structure, and in coordination with sibling units 12B40, 12B50, 12B5010, 12B5020, and 12B5030, Unit 12B10 executes its integrated mandate through five complementary operational activity streams. The unit develops and implements preventive measures against fire and panic risks in accordance with national and European regulatory frameworks, simultaneously establishing authoritative fire safety opinions on event organization, technical installations, construction projects, and building development initiatives. Unit 12B10 delivers comprehensive institutional capacity-building through specialized training programs in first aid, fire safety procedures, and evacuation protocols for all organizational actors within the European Parliament. The unit maintains direct emergency response and incident management capabilities, executing immediate first aid, fire suppression, and panic intervention services during operational emergencies and building-related incidents. Unit 12B10 maintains strategic liaison and coordination functions with national fire authorities and peer institutions on fire safety matters, ensuring regulatory compliance, knowledge exchange, and inter-institutional alignment. These five integrated activity streams collectively ensure the European Parliament maintains institutional resilience, regulatory compliance, personnel safety, and operational security across all institutional domains.
12B10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Fire Risk Management and Regulatory Compliance** (30%): Development and implementation of comprehensive preventive measures against fire and panic risks in application of national and European regulatory frameworks, including first aid protocols and safety frameworks

• **Safety Assessment and Technical Advisory Services** (25%): Formulation and delivery of specialized fire safety opinions regarding event organization, technical installations, construction projects, and building development initiatives to ensure institutional compliance

• **Personnel Training and Institutional Capacity Development** (20%): Provision of comprehensive training programs for all organizational actors covering first aid procedures, fire safety protocols, and evacuation procedures to enhance institutional readiness

• **Emergency Response and Incident Management** (15%): Direct execution of immediate first aid, fire suppression, and panic intervention services during operational emergencies and building-related incidents

• **Institutional Coordination and External Relations** (10%): Liaison and coordination activities with national fire authorities and peer institutions on fire safety compliance, regulatory alignment, and inter-institutional knowledge exchange

12B40.name: 12B40
original_activities: Issue appropriate access permits to all users of the Parliament buildings on the three sites. Produce and distribute access permits to the European Parliament buildings on the three sites in accordance with the regulations in force and the principles defined in the overall security concept, the mandate of DG SAFE and the new security culture of the European Parliament.

Ensure the good quality of the identification data collected for the preparation and production of access tickets in compliance with the regulations in force on the protection of personal data (implementation of a quality control policy). Prepare statistical reports for the different services of the European Parliament in order to quantify accreditation services by category and date.

Manage access rights (of users/populations) to the premises of the European Parliament, for all categories of users. Collaborate closely with the relevant services in the selection, acquisition and deployment of electronic systems in order to realize the process of digitalization of accreditation services. Contribute to the revision of all the rules in force on access to the European Parliament, in particular the technical provisions of DG SAFE.
Continue to establish a new policy relating to special authorizations for restricted access areas and implement an access management tool to the security areas of the European Parliament.

Improve event management and visitor reception. Modernize, strengthen and simplify the accreditation procedure for visitors to the European Parliament. Accredit all visitors to the three sites for the full implementation of the 'digital entry log' requested by the Secretary General. Promote the use of interactive terminals for the collection of access badges for each category of visitors and participants in visits, events and conferences.
12B40.refined_description: Unit 12B40, designated as the Access Control and Accreditation Management Unit, operates as a specialized operational component within Directorate Unit 12B of the European Parliament's security infrastructure, serving as the critical interface between security policy implementation and daily operational access control across all three Parliament sites. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the management of physical access permissions, visitor accreditation, and credential distribution in strict accordance with DG SAFE regulations, the Parliament's overarching security concept, and the institution's contemporary security culture framework. Operating as a leaf node with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 12B40 functions as a peer component alongside sibling units 12B10, 12B50, 12B5010, 12B5020, and 12B5030 within the broader Direction 12B framework, requiring close coordination to ensure comprehensive security operations across the institution. The unit's operational portfolio encompasses five integrated functional domains: the issuance and distribution of access permits to all user categories; the assurance of identification data integrity and compliance with personal data protection regulations; the active management of access rights and advancement of electronic accreditation system digitalization; the establishment of policy frameworks for restricted area authorizations and security zone access management; and the modernization of visitor reception and event management processes through implementation of digital entry logs and interactive terminal technologies. This multifaceted operational structure ensures that Unit 12B40 maintains comprehensive control over both routine credential management and emerging technological modernization initiatives while maintaining rigorous compliance with institutional security mandates and data protection requirements.
12B40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Access Permit Issuance and Distribution** (40%): Issue and produce access permits for all Parliament building users across three sites in accordance with DG SAFE regulations, security concept mandates, and institutional security culture requirements; distribute credentials to all user categories in compliance with established protocols

• **Data Quality Assurance, Integrity Management, and Statistical Reporting** (20%): Implement rigorous quality control policies for identification data collected during access ticket preparation and production; ensure full compliance with personal data protection regulations; generate comprehensive statistical accreditation reports disaggregated by user category and temporal parameters for distribution to Parliament services

• **Access Rights Management and Electronic System Digitalization** (20%): Actively manage access rights across all user populations and categories; collaborate with relevant institutional services in the selection, acquisition, and deployment of electronic accreditation systems to advance digitalization objectives; contribute to comprehensive revision of access regulations and technical provisions established by DG SAFE

• **Restricted Area Authorization and Security Zone Access Management** (10%): Establish and implement new policy frameworks governing special authorizations for restricted security areas; deploy dedicated and integrated access management tools for Parliament security-designated zones

• **Visitor Accreditation Modernization and Event Management Enhancement** (10%): Modernize and streamline visitor reception and accreditation procedures across all three sites; implement Secretary General's mandated 'digital entry log' system; deploy and promote interactive terminal technologies for efficient access badge collection and visitor categorization across all visit, event, and conference activities

12B50.name: 12B50
original_activities: Assume 24/7 permanence, monitoring and feedback concerning all aspects relating to security, prevention and threat analysis with the same consistency of action across the three places of employment of the European Parliament. Assume responsibility for security and safety operations outside working hours in the three European Parliament sites, ensuring the same continuity of action.

Continue the renovation and phased commissioning of the new control rooms (and fallback rooms) in Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg, as well as the management of related projects, including PSIM, VMS and REC4TOIP - in partnership with the relevant units of the Directorate General for Security and Protection as well as those of DG INLO / DG ITEC / DG LINC.

Guarantee optimal communication and proactive interaction with Directorates A, C & D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection as well as with all services of the European Parliament in general. Ensure real-time security responsiveness to crises, effective liaison between the services concerned as well as continuous communication with national emergency and security services.
Analyze and review existing processes / procedures while respecting the standards in force in the control rooms in Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg with the aim of achieving full interoperability.

Assist MPs, staff, delegations and visitors, in collaboration with security, prevention and threat analysis units 24/7. Find appropriate solutions, coordinate actions and ensure their follow-up. Provide feedback to the departments concerned.

Contribute, in cooperation with other general directorates and with the relevant units of the General Directorate of Security and Protection, to the implementation of the SEF and the strategic projects of the general directorate.
12B50.refined_description: Unit 12B50 operates as the integrated operational command and control center for the European Parliament's security and safety operations, functioning as a critical leaf-node component within the broader security framework under parent unit 12B and alongside sibling units 12B10, 12B40, 12B5010, 12B5020, and 12B5030. The unit maintains 24/7 permanence across all three parliamentary employment sites (Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg) with comprehensive monitoring, threat analysis, prevention coordination, and out-of-hours operational responsibility, ensuring consistent protective action and uninterrupted continuity of security operations (40% of workload). Unit 12B50 directs the strategic modernization of security infrastructure through phased renovation and commissioning of new control rooms and fallback facilities across all three sites while managing associated technological implementations including PSIM (Physical Security Information Management), VMS (Video Management Systems), and REC4TOIP systems in close partnership with relevant units within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, DG INLO, DG ITEC, and DG LINC (35% of workload). The unit ensures optimal inter-institutional communication and proactive coordination with Directorates A, C, and D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, maintains real-time crisis responsiveness capabilities, and facilitates effective liaison between all European Parliament services and national emergency and security services (10% of workload). Additionally, Unit 12B50 conducts systematic analysis and review of existing security control room processes and procedures across all three sites to achieve full interoperability while maintaining compliance with established standards (5% of workload), provides 24/7 integrated assistance to Members of Parliament, staff, delegations, and visitors through coordinated security and threat analysis operations with appropriate solution development, responsive action coordination, and departmental feedback mechanisms (5% of workload), and contributes to the implementation of the Strategic Excellence Framework (SEF) and general directorate strategic projects through cooperative engagement with other general directorates and relevant DG Security and Protection units (5% of workload).
12B50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Continuous Security Operations and Monitoring** (40%): Maintain 24/7 permanence with comprehensive monitoring and feedback concerning all aspects of security, prevention, and threat analysis across Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg sites; assume full responsibility for security and safety operations during non-working hours, ensuring consistent operational continuity and uniform protective action protocols across all three parliamentary employment locations.

• **Infrastructure Modernization and Integrated Systems Management** (35%): Direct the phased renovation and commissioning of new control rooms and fallback facilities across all three sites; manage PSIM (Physical Security Information Management), VMS (Video Management Systems), and REC4TOIP technology projects in strategic partnership with the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, DG INLO, DG ITEC, and DG LINC units.

• **Cross-Institutional Communication and Crisis Response Coordination** (10%): Ensure optimal proactive communication and interaction with Directorates A, C, and D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection and all European Parliament services; maintain real-time security responsiveness to crisis situations, facilitate effective liaison between concerned services, and sustain continuous coordination with national emergency and security services.

• **Operational Standardization and Process Optimization** (5%): Conduct systematic analysis and review of existing security control room processes and procedures across all three sites while maintaining compliance with established standards to achieve full interoperability and operational consistency.

• **Direct Support Services and Strategic Implementation** (10% combined): Provide 24/7 integrated assistance to Members of Parliament, staff, delegations, and visitors in collaboration with security and threat analysis units, identifying appropriate solutions, coordinating responsive actions, and ensuring follow-up mechanisms (5%); contribute to the implementation of the Strategic Excellence Framework (SEF) and general directorate strategic projects in cooperation with other general directorates and relevant DG Security and Protection units (5%).

12B5010.name: 12B5010
original_activities: Assume operational permanence on the Brussels site and 24/7, monitoring and feedback concerning all aspects relating to security, prevention and threat analysis, ensuring consistency of action on the three sites.

Guarantee optimal communication and proactive interaction with Directorates A, C & D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection as well as with all services of the European Parliament in general. Ensure real-time security responsiveness to crises, effective liaison between the services concerned as well as continuous communication with national emergency and security services.

Assist MPs, staff, delegations and visitors, in collaboration with security, prevention and threat analysis units 24/7. Find appropriate solutions, coordinate actions and ensure their follow-up. Provide feedback to the departments concerned.
Ensure the distribution of keys on the Brussels site.
12B5010.refined_description: Unit 12B5010 functions as the European Parliament's primary operational security command center, maintaining continuous 24/7 monitoring, coordination, and real-time threat response capabilities across all three parliamentary sites while serving as a critical second-level operational unit within the broader Unit 12B framework. Operating as the operational nerve center within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection's hierarchical structure, the unit assumes comprehensive responsibility for security monitoring, prevention, and threat analysis feedback across all parliamentary locations, ensuring consistency and coherence of coordinated security action across geographically dispersed sites. The unit establishes and maintains optimal communication channels and proactive interaction with Directorates A, C, and D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, as well as all European Parliament services, while ensuring real-time security responsiveness to crises and sustaining continuous liaison with national emergency and security services. Unit 12B5010 provides direct 24/7 assistance to Members of Parliament, staff, delegations, and visitors in collaboration with security, prevention, and threat analysis units, identifying appropriate solutions, coordinating actions, ensuring follow-up implementation, and providing feedback to relevant departments. The unit additionally manages the administrative distribution of access keys on the Brussels site and collaborates with sibling units 12B10, 12B40, 12B50, 12B5020, and 12B5030 to ensure comprehensive institutional security coverage across the organization's security architecture.
12B5010.refined_activity_summary:
