• **24/7 Operational Monitoring and Threat Analysis** (55%): Maintain permanent operational presence on the Brussels site with continuous 24/7 monitoring and feedback concerning all aspects of security, prevention, and threat analysis across all three parliamentary sites, ensuring consistency and coherence of coordinated security action across geographically dispersed locations.

• **Inter-Directorate Communication and Crisis Liaison** (20%): Establish and maintain optimal communication channels and proactive interaction with Directorates A, C, and D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection and all European Parliament services; ensure real-time security responsiveness to crises; maintain effective liaison between concerned services; and sustain continuous communication with national emergency and security services.

• **Direct User Support and Coordination Services** (20%): Provide 24/7 comprehensive assistance to Members of Parliament, staff, delegations, and visitors in collaboration with security, prevention, and threat analysis units; identify and implement appropriate solutions; coordinate actions and ensure follow-up implementation; and provide feedback to relevant departments.

• **Physical Access Management** (5%): Manage the distribution and administration of access keys on the Brussels site.

12B5020.name: 12B5020
original_activities: Ensure operational permanence on the Strasbourg and Luxembourg sites and 24/7, monitoring and feedback concerning all aspects relating to security, prevention and threat analysis, ensuring consistency of action on the three sites.

Guarantee optimal communication and proactive interaction with Directorates A, C & D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection as well as with all services of the European Parliament in general. Ensure real-time security responsiveness to crises, effective liaison between the services concerned as well as continuous communication with national emergency and security services.

Assist MPs, staff, delegations and visitors, in collaboration with security, prevention and threat analysis units 24/7. Find appropriate solutions, coordinate actions and ensure their follow-up. Provide feedback to the departments concerned.
12B5020.refined_description:
Unit 12B5020 functions as the operational security command and coordination center for the European Parliament, serving as a critical second-level operational unit within the parent Unit 12B's security and safety management framework. The unit's integrated mission encompasses three core operational responsibilities that collectively position it as the operational nerve center and nexus for real-time security management, inter-institutional coordination, and stakeholder support within the Parliament's broader security and protection infrastructure. Operating with continuous 24/7 operational permanence across all three parliamentary locations (Strasbourg, Luxembourg, and a third coordinated site), Unit 12B5020 maintains comprehensive monitoring and real-time feedback mechanisms addressing all dimensions of security, threat prevention, threat analysis, and incident response while ensuring consistency of protective actions and coordinated security protocols across all operational venues. The unit establishes and maintains optimal communication channels and proactive strategic engagement with Directorates A, C, and D within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection, simultaneously coordinating with all European Parliament services, national emergency authorities, and national security services to ensure rapid security crisis responsiveness, effective inter-service liaison, and continuous inter-agency coordination. Through collaborative coordination with specialized security, prevention, and threat analysis units, Unit 12B5020 provides comprehensive 24/7 assistance and support to Members of Parliament, parliamentary staff, official delegations, and visitors by identifying and implementing appropriate solutions, coordinating responsive follow-up actions, ensuring continuity of procedures, and delivering systematic feedback to relevant departments. These integrated activities collectively enable the unit to function as the operational command center for real-time security incident response, inter-institutional coordination mechanisms, and comprehensive stakeholder support services within the European Parliament's institutional security architecture.

##
12B5020.refined_activity_summary:
• **Continuous Security Operations, Monitoring, and Threat Analysis** (60%): Ensure 24/7 operational permanence across Strasbourg and Luxembourg parliamentary sites with comprehensive monitoring, security assessment, threat analysis, incident feedback mechanisms, and real-time feedback on all security and prevention aspects; maintain consistency of protective actions and coordinated security protocols across all three parliamentary locations

• **Inter-Departmental and Inter-Institutional Coordination with Crisis Response Capabilities** (20%): Establish optimal communication and proactive strategic interaction with Directorates A, C, and D of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection; coordinate with all European Parliament services; ensure real-time security responsiveness during crisis situations; maintain effective liaison between internal services and concerned departments; establish continuous engagement and coordination channels with national emergency and security authorities

• **Comprehensive Stakeholder Assistance and Support Services** (20%): Provide 24/7 assistance and support to Members of Parliament, parliamentary staff, official delegations, and visitors in collaborative coordination with security, prevention, and threat analysis units; identify and implement appropriate solutions; coordinate responsive actions and follow-up procedures; ensure systematic feedback delivery to relevant departments

12B5030.name: 12B5030
original_activities: Continue the configuration and deployment of PSIM (Physical Security Information Management) and VMS (Video Management System) for video surveillance and access control on the 3 sites of the European Parliament. Furthermore, work in close collaboration with DG ITEC and internal services of DG SAFE to ensure integration with existing networks. Provide the training necessary to achieve full technical interoperability.

Continue the renovation and gradual installation of new control rooms (and fallback rooms) in Brussels and Strasbourg (Luxembourg has been completed since the end of 2025) in partnership with the relevant units of the Directorate General for Security and Protection as well as those of DG INLO, DG ITEC and DG LINC.

Analyze and revise existing processes and procedures, respecting the standards in force in the control rooms in Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg, in order to achieve full procedural interoperability.
12B5030.refined_description: Unit 12B5030 operates as a specialized technical operations and infrastructure modernization component within the European Parliament's security and protection framework, functioning as a second-level unit subordinate to Unit 12B while serving as a critical institutional enabler of the Parliament's comprehensive security modernization strategy. The unit's primary mission encompasses three strategically interconnected activities: the continued configuration and deployment of advanced Physical Security Information Management (PSIM) and Video Management System (VMS) technologies to enhance integrated video surveillance and access control capabilities across all three parliamentary sites (Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg), executed through collaborative partnerships with DG ITEC and internal DG SAFE services to ensure seamless network integration and full technical interoperability via comprehensive staff training; the systematic renovation and phased installation of new primary and fallback control room facilities in Brussels and Strasbourg, building upon the Luxembourg facility completed by end of 2025, conducted in coordinated partnership with relevant units across the Directorate General for Security and Protection, DG INLO, DG ITEC, and DG LINC; and the analytical review and systematic revision of existing operational procedures and processes to establish full procedural interoperability and compliance with established control room standards across all three parliamentary locations. These interconnected activities collectively advance the European Parliament's institutional objectives of enhanced security infrastructure, technological modernization, and operational consistency across its geographically dispersed facilities through essential cross-directorate coordination relationships.
12B5030.refined_activity_summary:
• **Physical Security Systems Configuration and Deployment** (45%): Continued configuration and deployment of PSIM and VMS infrastructure for integrated video surveillance and access control systems across Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg; collaborative integration with DG ITEC and DG SAFE internal services; provision of comprehensive technical training to ensure full network interoperability and system integration.

• **Control Room Infrastructure Modernization** (40%): Systematic renovation and phased installation of new primary and fallback control room facilities in Brussels and Strasbourg, building upon completed Luxembourg infrastructure (finalized end of 2025); coordinated partnerships with Directorate General for Security and Protection, DG INLO, DG ITEC, and DG LINC to ensure seamless facility integration.

• **Procedural Standardization and Operational Interoperability** (15%): Analysis and systematic revision of existing operational procedures and processes across Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg control room facilities; alignment with established control room standards to achieve full procedural interoperability and institutional compliance across all three parliamentary locations.

12C.name: 12C
original_activities: Ensure and implement a strategic and innovative human resources management policy adapted to the needs of DG SAFE. Guarantee optimization of human resources by sector of activity and on the three sites, while contributing to the objectives of increasing career prospects and guaranteeing appropriate working conditions. Ensure annual planning of security and prevention agents as well as logistical management of resources.

Ensure and implement targeted safety and security training strategies for all categories of DG SAFE staff. Establish the training needs of the DG through the adoption of an annual training plan to guarantee safety, security and an inclusive work environment. Liaise with partners, including external training providers, and support the DG's capabilities in its security awareness initiatives.

Ensure the budgetary and financial management of contracts, as well as the programming of DG SAFE contracts, including the effective and efficient execution of annual tender planning. Advise and assist all services of DG SAFE in order to ensure the preparation and execution of the budget as well as the programming and monitoring of the general directorate's AOs in line with the strategic projects and its objectives.

Ensure the operation and maintenance of IT systems. Support users involved in security operations within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection through the development of up-to-date IT systems meeting the specific needs of the DG and ensuring proactive forecasting and monitoring of the submission and execution of DG SAFE IT projects under the European Parliament's IT program.

Coordinate and guarantee the smooth running of operations within the Resources Directorate and horizontal projects, while ensuring optimal management of resources consistent with the needs of the European Parliament and the Directorate General for Security and Protection.
12C.refined_description:
Unit 12C functions as the Resources and Operations Directorate within the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG SAFE), operating as a Direction-level organizational unit reporting through parent Unit 12 and serving as the strategic administrative and operational backbone supporting DG SAFE's comprehensive security mission across the European Parliament's three institutional sites. The unit exercises integrated responsibility across five interconnected functional domains representing 100% of documented workload allocation, delivering strategic human resources management, financial stewardship, technological infrastructure support, training and organizational development, and horizontal operational coordination services that collectively enable DG SAFE's security and protective mission. Unit 12C cascades its strategic directives through four specialized subordinate operational units—12C10 (Training and Professional Development), 12C20 (Human Resources and Operational Planning), 12C30 (Budget and Procurement Operations Centre), and 12C40 (Information Technology Operations and Security)—while maintaining integrated coordination with sibling units (12-10, 12A, 12B, 12D) across the broader institutional security framework under the overarching governance of Unit 12. The unit's comprehensive mandate encompasses formulation and implementation of innovative human resources policies calibrated to DG SAFE's operational requirements, optimization of personnel distribution across multiple activity sectors and three physical sites, advancement of career development and workplace conditions standards, annual planning cycles for security and prevention personnel, and logistical resource management. Simultaneously, Unit 12C designs and executes targeted safety and security training curricula applicable to all staff categories through annual training plans, needs assessment methodologies, coordination with external training providers, and fostering of inclusive work environments. The unit maintains direct responsibility for budgetary and financial stewardship of all DG SAFE contracts, including contract programming, annual tender planning execution, advisory support to all directorate services regarding budget preparation and execution, and monitoring of Annual Operating Plans and Administrative Arrangements in alignment with strategic institutional objectives. Additionally, Unit 12C ensures continuous operation and maintenance of critical IT infrastructure, provides systems development addressing DG-specific operational requirements, delivers user support to security operations personnel, and proactively manages IT project submission and execution within the European Parliament's institutional IT governance framework. Finally, the unit coordinates Resources Directorate operations and horizontal cross-functional projects, optimizing resource allocation consistent with European Parliament and DG SAFE institutional priorities.

---
12C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Strategy, Workforce Management, and Personnel Planning** (30%): Formulation and implementation of strategic and innovative human resources management policies tailored to DG SAFE's operational requirements; optimization of human resources allocation across three institutional sites and multiple activity sectors; advancement of career development prospects and workplace conditions standards; annual planning and logistical coordination of security and prevention personnel; resource distribution management across the organizational structure; operational delivery through child unit 12C20 (Human Resources and Operational Planning).

• **Training, Safety, Security Awareness, and Organizational Development** (20%): Design and execution of targeted safety and security training strategies applicable to all staff categories; establishment of annual training needs assessments and comprehensive curriculum planning; coordination with external training providers; support for organizational security awareness initiatives and inclusive work environment development; creation of structured professional development pathways; operational delivery through child unit 12C10 (Training and Professional Development).

• **Financial Management, Budgetary Stewardship, Contract Administration, and Procurement Operations** (20%): Budgetary and financial management of all DG SAFE contractual obligations; contract programming and annual tender planning execution; advisory and implementation support to all directorate services regarding budget preparation, execution, and monitoring; programming and oversight of Annual Operating Plans and Administrative Arrangements; alignment of financial operations with strategic institutional objectives and directorate priorities; specialized operations managed through child unit 12C30 (Budget and Procurement Operations Centre).

• **Information Technology Operations, Infrastructure Management, and Systems Support** (20%): Operation and maintenance of critical IT infrastructure systems supporting security operations; user support for security operations personnel; development of IT systems addressing DG-specific operational requirements; proactive forecasting and monitoring of IT project submission and execution; integration with European Parliament's institutional IT governance program framework; technical delivery coordinated through child unit 12C40 (Information Technology Operations and Security Unit).

• **Directorate Operations Coordination and Horizontal Cross-Functional Project Management** (10%): Coordination of Resources Directorate operational functions and smooth organizational functioning; management of horizontal cross-functional institutional projects; optimization of resource allocation and utilization consistent with European Parliament and DG SAFE strategic requirements; liaison and integration across organizational units and sibling directorate services.

12C10.name: 12C10
original_activities: Organize specific training in safety and prevention.
Establish the training plan for the General Directorate of Security and Protection and ensure its proper execution in order to achieve training objectives.
Develop a training program for all general management staff in order to maintain their professionalism and efficiency.
Define the training needs of the general management in order to ensure the acquisition and maintenance of security skills and techniques to meet the requirements of securing the European Parliament.
Participate in communication, training and awareness initiatives for occupants of the institution's buildings in terms of safety and security culture.
12C10.refined_description:
**

Unit 12C10, the Training and Professional Development Unit, operates as a specialized capability development hub within the Resources and Operations Directorate (12C) of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG SAFE), positioned at the second hierarchical level of the European Parliament's security infrastructure. As a leaf node unit, 12C10 functions under the strategic direction of parent Directorate 12C while coordinating with sibling units (12C20, 12C30, 12C40) to deliver integrated security and protection services across the institution. The unit's core institutional mission encompasses the comprehensive design, delivery, oversight, and strategic planning of specialized training and professional development initiatives that strengthen operational capacity, professional standards, and security culture among security personnel, management staff, and all building occupants. Unit 12C10 serves as the institutional hub for professional competency advancement, with direct accountability for establishing training frameworks, identifying capability gaps, and executing targeted interventions that ensure continuous acquisition and maintenance of security-specific skills and techniques aligned with the European Parliament's comprehensive physical infrastructure protection requirements. The unit's integrated approach combines formal training delivery with strategic needs assessment, management development, and institutional awareness-raising to embed robust safety and security consciousness throughout the Parliament's facilities and operational ecosystem. Through its five complementary functional areas, Unit 12C10 directly supports DG SAFE's mandate to maintain the highest standards of institutional security and protection while fostering a comprehensive security culture among all stakeholders.

**
12C10.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Primary Training Delivery and Safety Program Management** (40%): Organization and implementation of specialized, targeted training programs focused on safety and prevention protocols, representing the unit's principal operational focus area with direct responsibility for program design and delivery execution

• **Institutional Training Strategy Development and Execution** (30%): Establishment, coordination, and oversight of the comprehensive formal training plan for the General Directorate of Security and Protection, with direct accountability for ensuring proper execution and achievement of defined institutional training objectives

• **Professional Development for Management Personnel** (20%): Design and implementation of structured professional development programs tailored for all general management staff to maintain operational proficiency, professional competency, and institutional effectiveness within the security framework

• **Training Needs Assessment and Skills Assurance** (5%): Systematic identification, definition, and analysis of training requirements among general management personnel to ensure continuous acquisition and maintenance of security-specific skills and techniques aligned with European Parliament protection requirements

• **Institutional Security Culture and Awareness Building** (5%): Active participation in cross-institutional communication, training, and awareness-raising initiatives designed to embed safety and security consciousness among all building occupants and institutional stakeholders, fostering a robust protective culture throughout Parliament's facilities

12C20.name: 12C20
original_activities: Ensure the effective and strategic management of the general management's human resources: the careers of civil servants and other agents (competitions, recruitment, ratings, mobility, external activities, resignations, compensation, etc.), working conditions, positions on the organization chart (upgrading of positions, Ringbook movements, etc.) and the budgetary envelopes of the general management (missions, contractual agents, temporary workers).

Consolidate the annual operational organization of around 800 colleagues from DG SAFE in Brussels, Strasbourg and Luxembourg in synergy with operational services in order to cover the security needs of the institution according to parliamentary activity and to guarantee the 'private/professional life' balance of agents. Identify possible needs for revision of IT tools and planning methodology to meet the requirements of the institution.

Ensure the management of inventoried assets (GBI), equipment and annual delivery of service and protective clothing for approximately 800 agents and coordinators. Provide general management staff, and in particular agents, with the best possible working conditions in compliance with the rules in force.
Contribute to cross-functional reporting exercises such as AWP, RAA and RPA, risk management, BCP, equality and diversity issues, etc.
12C20.refined_description: Unit 12C20 functions as the Human Resources and Operational Planning division within the Resources and Operations Directorate (12C) of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG SAFE), serving as a critical administrative and operational backbone for the institution's security apparatus across multiple locations. The unit exercises dual strategic responsibilities: comprehensive human resources management for general management personnel encompassing the complete lifecycle of civil servant and agent careers—including competitive recruitment processes, performance ratings, internal mobility and position reassignment (Ringbook movements and organizational chart upgrades), external activity authorizations, resignation processing and separation procedures, compensation and benefits administration, and budgetary envelope oversight for general management operations including missions, contractual agent engagement, and temporary worker deployment—and consolidated annual operational organization and scheduling of approximately 800 security personnel deployed across Brussels, Strasbourg, and Luxembourg locations, executed in direct synergy with operational service units to ensure continuous institutional security coverage aligned with parliamentary activity calendars while maintaining optimal work-life balance for all agents. Concurrently, the unit manages inventoried general assets (GBI) and equipment, administers annual distribution of service and protective clothing to approximately 800 agents and coordinators, and ensures optimal working conditions in compliance with applicable institutional regulations and health and safety standards. The unit identifies and assesses IT tool requirements and planning methodology improvements to enhance scheduling efficiency and institutional responsiveness. Unit 12C20 contributes substantively to cross-functional institutional reporting and governance exercises including Annual Work Programmes (AWP), Results and Achievement Analysis (RAA), Risk Prevention Analysis (RPA), enterprise risk management frameworks, business continuity planning (BCP), and equality and diversity compliance obligations. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational structure, Unit 12C20 coordinates with sibling units 12C10 and 12C30 to deliver integrated resources and operations management across DG SAFE, thereby directly enabling the directorate-general's operational effectiveness and institutional compliance objectives.
12C20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources Management and Career Administration (40%)**: Strategic oversight of civil servant and agent career trajectories encompassing competitive recruitment processes, performance evaluation and rating systems, internal mobility and position reassignment (Ringbook movements and organizational chart upgrades), external activity authorization and management, resignation processing and separation procedures, compensation and benefits administration, and budgetary envelope allocation for general management operations including missions, contractual agent engagement, and temporary worker deployment.

• **Operational Planning and Workforce Scheduling (40%)**: Consolidated annual operational organization and deployment scheduling of approximately 800 security personnel across three institutional locations (Brussels, Strasbourg, Luxembourg) executed in direct synergy with operational service units to achieve continuous security coverage responsive to parliamentary activity requirements; identification and assessment of IT tool requirements and planning methodology improvements to enhance scheduling efficiency and institutional responsiveness; work-life balance optimization for all agents.

• **Asset Management and Agent Welfare (15%)**: Comprehensive management of inventoried general assets (GBI) and equipment provisioning; annual delivery and distribution of service and protective clothing to approximately 800 agents and coordinators; provision of optimal working conditions and occupational welfare support in full compliance with applicable institutional regulations and health and safety standards.

• **Cross-Functional Institutional Reporting and Governance (5%)**: Contribution to Annual Work Programmes (AWP), Results and Achievement Analysis (RAA), Risk Prevention Analysis (RPA), enterprise risk management initiatives, business continuity planning (BCP) frameworks, and equality and diversity compliance obligations across DG SAFE.

12C30.name: 12C30
original_activities: Coordinate, provide support to FMS and Webcontract users, advise and assist operational services in the preparation and execution of the budget. Ensure budgetary monitoring, monitoring of contract execution, initiation of budgetary (commitment, payment, recovery and reimbursement orders) and contractual acts, preparation of BDCs and CS, and management of assigned revenue.

Establish annual market programming and proactively monitor implementation. Assist and advise operational units from the drafting of procurement documents to the launch of procedures, evaluation and award, until the signing of contracts. Advise and support all operational services in monitoring legal issues related to the execution of contracts (amendments, dispute resolution, penalties, etc.).

Ensure ex-ante control of procurement procedures and all budgetary execution acts (financial and legal commitments), the proper maintenance of the financial inventory and the conservation of all budgetary acts and supporting documents as well as documents relating to procurement procedures.

Write periodic reports and contributions to annual activity reports as well as the work program, contribute to responses to budgetary resolutions, to questions from European Parliament services (e.g. BUDG and CONT) and members as well as to control bodies (Internal Auditor, Court of Auditors).
Monitor minimum internal control standards and assess the level of its implementation through one-off actions (e.g. periodic surveys to be launched among staff).

Represent the General Directorate of Security and Protection in different entities and its subgroups, e.g. Inter DG Steering Group Finances, Forum Marchés Publics, Building Policy Group, Working group on internal control and FMS Key users meeting.
12C30.refined_description:
Unit 12C30 functions as the integrated Budget, Finance, and Procurement Operations Centre within the Resources and Operations Directorate (12C) of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG SAFE), serving as the critical administrative and operational backbone for the organization's comprehensive financial stewardship and procurement governance. Operating as a peer unit alongside 12C10, 12C20, and 12C40 within the directorate structure with no subordinate organizational units, Unit 12C30 delivers integrated service provision directly supporting the DG's operational, financial, and compliance objectives through five interconnected functional pillars. The unit's financial operations and budget execution function (40% of workload) encompasses coordination of Financial Management System (FMS) and Webcontract user support, comprehensive advisory assistance to operational services on budget preparation and execution, continuous budgetary monitoring and contract execution oversight, initiation of all budgetary acts (commitments, payments, recoveries, and reimbursements), preparation of Budget Distribution Commitments (BDCs) and Commitment Schedules (CS), and management of assigned revenue streams. The unit's procurement strategy and contract lifecycle management function (35% of workload) includes establishment and proactive monitoring of annual market programming frameworks, provision of full-cycle procurement guidance to operational units from documentation drafting through procedure launch, evaluation, award determination, and contract signature, and continuous legal advisory support addressing contract amendments, dispute resolution mechanisms, and penalty administration. Complementing these primary functions, Unit 12C30 executes ex-ante financial and legal control mechanisms (10% of workload) encompassing rigorous oversight of all procurement procedures and budgetary execution acts, maintenance of financial inventory with proper documentation conservation, and preservation of comprehensive archival systems for all budgetary acts, supporting materials, and procurement procedure documentation. The unit's governance and institutional representation functions (15% combined allocation) include preparation of periodic reports and annual activity report contributions, development of work program inputs, coordination of responses to budgetary resolutions and European Parliament service inquiries (BUDG and CONT committees), liaison with control bodies including the Internal Auditor and Court of Auditors, monitoring and assessment of minimum internal control standards implementation through periodic surveys and targeted actions, and strategic inter-institutional representation at the Inter-DG Steering Group on Finances, Forum Marchés Publics (Public Procurement Forum), Building Policy Group, Internal Control Working Group, and FMS Key Users meetings.

---

##
12C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Budget Execution and Financial Operations Management** (40%): Coordinate FMS and Webcontract user support services; provide comprehensive advisory assistance to operational services on budget preparation, execution, and continuous monitoring; perform contract execution oversight; initiate and process all budgetary acts (commitments, payments, recoveries, reimbursements); prepare Budget Distribution Commitments and Commitment Schedules; manage assigned revenue streams

• **Procurement Strategy and Contract Lifecycle Management** (35%): Establish and monitor annual market programming implementation; provide end-to-end procurement guidance from documentation drafting through procedure launch, evaluation, award determination, and contract signature; deliver continuous legal advisory support on contract execution matters including amendments, dispute resolution, and penalty administration

• **Financial and Legal Control Oversight** (10%): Execute ex-ante control of all procurement procedures and budgetary execution acts; maintain financial inventory with rigorous accuracy standards and proper documentation conservation; preserve comprehensive archival systems for budgetary acts, supporting documentation, and procurement procedure records

• **Institutional Reporting, Parliamentary Liaison, and Audit Coordination** (5%): Prepare periodic reports and annual activity report contributions; develop work program inputs; coordinate responses to budgetary resolutions and European Parliament service inquiries (BUDG and CONT committees); liaise with control bodies (Internal Auditor, Court of Auditors)

• **Internal Control Standards Monitoring and Inter-Institutional Representation** (10%): Monitor and assess implementation of minimum internal control standards through periodic surveys and targeted assessment mechanisms (5%); represent DG SAFE at strategic inter-institutional forums including Inter-DG Steering Group on Finances, Forum Marchés Publics, Building Policy Group, Internal Control Working Group, and FMS Key Users meetings (5%)

12C40.name: 12C40
original_activities: Manage infrastructure, IT assets and access: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management, ensuring the availability, reliability and security of IT resources.
Provide end-user support: provide assistance, resolve technical problems encountered by users of computer systems, troubleshoot hardware and software problems, offer advice.
Manage applications in use: lifecycle management of custom or specialized software applications in use, rolling maintenance, training and support, testing to ensure functionality and security.

Manage computer and information security: approaches and measures to protect the organization's IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities; designing and implementing secure IT architectures, identifying vulnerabilities, performing security testing, raising awareness of cybersecurity best practices, and implementing threat protection measures.

Manage projects: planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget and quality; project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management and stakeholder communication.

Supervise and coordinate business analysis: understand business needs and objectives, translate them into IT solutions; collection and analysis of needs, facilitation of communication between stakeholders and recommendation of effective technological solutions.
Other missions.
12C40.refined_description: Unit 12C40, the Information Technology Operations and Security Unit within the Resources and Operations Directorate (12C) of the Directorate-General for Security and Protection (DG SAFE), functions as a critical operational backbone for the European Parliament's digital infrastructure, information systems governance, and cybersecurity posture. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses IT infrastructure management and asset provisioning, end-user technical support services, full-lifecycle software application management, and information security protection—collectively representing 85% of its operational capacity. Operating as a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizations, Unit 12C40 coordinates with sibling units 12C10, 12C20, and 12C30 to deliver integrated resources and operational services supporting DG SAFE's institutional mandate for security and protection. The unit's remaining 15% of workload comprises strategic and administrative functions including IT project management, business analysis supervision and coordination, and other assigned missions as directed by the parent Directorate. Through this integrated operational structure, Unit 12C40 ensures continuous availability, reliability, and security of all IT resources while maintaining organizational productivity and protecting institutional data and information assets against identified threats and vulnerabilities.
12C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **IT Infrastructure and Asset Management** (25%): Planning, provisioning, maintenance, and monitoring of comprehensive IT infrastructure including hardware, software, network components, and user access management systems; ensuring continuous availability, reliability, and security of all IT resources across the organization.

• **End-User Support Services** (25%): Provision of direct technical assistance and support to Parliament users, encompassing problem resolution, hardware and software troubleshooting, and professional advisory services to maintain operational continuity and user productivity.

• **Application Lifecycle and Maintenance Management** (20%): Full lifecycle management of custom and specialized software applications including rolling maintenance activities, end-user training, technical support delivery, and comprehensive testing protocols to ensure both functionality and security standards are maintained.

• **Cybersecurity and Information Protection** (15%): Design and implementation of secure IT architectures, vulnerability identification and assessment, security testing execution, cybersecurity awareness-raising initiatives, and implementation of comprehensive threat protection measures to safeguard organizational IT systems, data, and information assets against identified threats and vulnerabilities.

• **IT Project Management** (10%): Planning, execution, supervision, resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication for IT initiatives designed to achieve defined objectives within established constraints of scope, time, budget, and quality parameters.

• **Business Analysis Supervision and Coordination** (3%): Translation of business needs and organizational objectives into effective IT solutions through stakeholder communication, needs analysis facilitation, and technological recommendation development.

• **Other Assigned Missions** (2%): Additional tasks and responsibilities as directed by the parent Directorate 12C.

12D.name: 12D
original_activities: Provide, on an ongoing basis, specialized services in risk analysis and investigation.
Ensure the protection of information, prevention and fight against hybrid threats and espionage.
Ensure the installation, operation and maintenance of physical security systems in all Parliament workplaces.
Facilitate the strengthening of cooperation with the services of the European Parliament, the competent authorities of host countries and other Member States, as well as with the other institutions.
Coordinate the crisis unit for the security and protection of the official missions of deputies and staff outside the three workplaces.
12D.refined_description: Unit 12D functions as a specialized Directorate within the European Parliament's integrated security and safety infrastructure, operating at the first hierarchical level beneath the Directorate-General and reporting through its parent unit (Unit 12) directly to the Secretary General. The Directorate exercises direct supervisory responsibility over five subordinate operational units (12D10, 12D1010, 12D20, 12D30, and 12D3010) that collectively execute 27 discrete activities, while collaborating with four sibling directional units (12-10, 12A, 12B, and 12C) within the unified security apparatus. Unit 12D's core institutional mandate encompasses the comprehensive management of security through five equally weighted operational pillars, each representing 20% of total organizational workload allocation, that collectively constitute an integrated security framework combining intelligence-led risk management, information protection, physical security infrastructure, institutional cooperation, and crisis response capabilities. These five operational pillars encompass: (1) the provision of continuous, specialized services in risk analysis and investigation methodologies, ensuring systematic identification and assessment of security vulnerabilities and threats across parliamentary operations; (2) the protection of institutional information assets and the implementation of prevention and active countermeasures against hybrid threats and espionage activities targeting the European Parliament; (3) the installation, configuration, operation, and ongoing maintenance of comprehensive physical security infrastructure systems deployed across all three parliamentary workplaces, ensuring continuous protective coverage; (4) the facilitation and strengthening of multilateral security cooperation mechanisms involving European Parliament internal services, competent authorities of host countries, Member State security services, and peer EU institutions, thereby enhancing collective institutional resilience; and (5) the coordination of a dedicated crisis management unit responsible for security operations and protective services supporting official missions undertaken by deputies and staff at locations outside the three designated parliamentary workplaces. This balanced resource allocation reflects the Parliament's integrated approach to institutional security, operationalized through specialized investigative, operational, and coordination capabilities delivered by subordinate units.
12D.refined_activity_summary:
• **Risk Analysis and Investigation Services** (20%): Provision of continuous, specialized services in risk analysis and investigation methodologies, supporting systematic identification, assessment, and documentation of security vulnerabilities and threats across parliamentary operations to enable proactive security decision-making

• **Information Protection and Hybrid Threat Countermeasures** (20%): Protection of institutional information assets and implementation of prevention and active countermeasures against hybrid threats, espionage activities, and related security vulnerabilities targeting the European Parliament

• **Physical Security Infrastructure Management** (20%): Installation, configuration, operational management, and ongoing maintenance of comprehensive physical security systems deployed throughout all three parliamentary workplaces, ensuring continuous and integrated protective coverage across all institutional facilities

• **Multilateral Security Cooperation and External Relations** (20%): Facilitation and strengthening of multilateral security cooperation mechanisms involving European Parliament internal services, competent authorities of host countries, Member State security services, and peer EU institutions, enhancing collective institutional resilience and coordinated security posture

• **Crisis Management and External Protection Operations** (20%): Coordination of specialized crisis management units providing security and protective services for official missions undertaken by deputies and staff at locations outside the three designated parliamentary workplaces, ensuring continuity of protective coverage during field operations

12D10.name: 12D10
original_activities: Providing a specialist investigations service to carry out high-level investigations and audits in the areas of security and safety. Align this service with the requirements of the mandate of the Directorate General of Security and Protection.
Contribute to the consolidation of risk-based security management and develop security risk analyzes in the area of ​​buildings, procedures, protection of people and events.

Analyze, evaluate and consolidate the risks associated with the organization of European Parliament events and activities. Ensure the approval circuit for events with more than 100 external participants with the Office of the President.
Maintain a strategic monitoring capacity on organizations and/or people likely to represent a risk for the events and activities of the European Parliament.
Contribute to the consolidation of the professionalization of the Directorate General for Security and Protection and the strengthening of a real security culture in the European Parliament.
Assist the hierarchy during crises and/or incidents and support crisis management structures.
12D10.refined_description: Unit 12D10 is a specialized investigations and security risk management unit positioned at the secondary operational level within the European Parliament's Directorate General for Security and Protection (parent unit 12D), functioning as a critical operational pillar in the institution's integrated security ecosystem. The unit delivers comprehensive security assurance through five primary functional domains: high-level specialist investigations and audits in security and safety compliance (50% of workload), maintaining strict alignment with the Directorate General's operational mandate and institutional requirements; development and consolidation of risk-based security management frameworks encompassing detailed analyses of building infrastructure, operational procedures, personnel protection protocols, and event security frameworks (30% of workload); specialized event security governance including systematic risk analysis, evaluation, and consolidation for European Parliament events and activities, coupled with administration of mandatory approval mechanisms for external events exceeding 100 participants in coordination with the Office of the President (10% of workload); strategic threat monitoring and identification of organizations and individuals presenting potential risks to Parliament's events and operational activities (5% of workload); and institutional capacity-building through advancement of professional standards within the Directorate General for Security and Protection and reinforcement of security culture across the European Parliament (3% of workload). The unit additionally provides critical support infrastructure for crisis management operations, assisting senior hierarchy during security incidents and supporting activation of institutional emergency response structures (2% of workload). Operating synergistically with sibling units 12D1010, 12D20, 12D30, and 12D3010 under unified parent unit direction, Unit 12D10 integrates investigative, analytical, and institutional development capabilities to strengthen Parliament's security posture across all operational domains.
12D10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Investigations & Audit Services** (50%): Provision of high-level specialist investigations and comprehensive audits in security and safety domains, maintaining full alignment with Directorate General of Security and Protection mandates and operational requirements

• **Risk-Based Security Management & Analysis** (30%): Development and consolidation of sophisticated risk-based security management frameworks with comprehensive security risk analyses encompassing building infrastructure, operational procedures, personnel protection protocols, and event security frameworks

• **Event Security Governance & Approval Administration** (10%): Systematic analysis, evaluation, and consolidation of risks associated with European Parliament events and activities; administration of mandatory approval circuits for external events exceeding 100 participants in coordination with the Office of the President

• **Strategic Threat Monitoring & Intelligence** (5%): Maintenance of strategic monitoring capacity targeting organizations and individuals presenting potential security risks to Parliament's events and operational activities

• **Institutional Development, Professionalization & Crisis Support** (5%): Advancement of professional standards and security culture strengthening across the Directorate General for Security and Protection; provision of critical support infrastructure for crisis management operations, incident response assistance, and emergency structure activation to organizational hierarchy

12D1010.name: 12D1010
original_activities: Take all necessary measures to verify the circumstances of security incidents and retain evidence thereof. Propose risk prevention and mitigation measures to avoid their recurrence. (Art. 18 and 19, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

Report on investigative actions, propose measures aimed at the security of the European Parliament, inform the Office of the Secretary General of ongoing investigations, populate and maintain the security investigation archive database.

Conduct security investigations as part of security incident investigations, in order to prevent similar incidents in the future after notification to the Secretary General and prior approval of the Presidency when a Member of Parliament is affected. (Art. 28, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)
Assist the RISK Unit of the General Directorate of Security and Protection in risk and threat analysis tasks. (Art. 7-1°, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)
Liaise with national police and security services within the mandate received. (Art. 30, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

Take investigative measures as part of additional investigations at the request of competent external or internal bodies and within the limits of the mandate received. (Art. 29, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)
12D1010.refined_description: Unit 12D1010 is a specialized security investigation and incident response unit operating as a second-level organizational component within the European Parliament's security infrastructure, directly subordinate to Directorate 12D, with no subordinate organizational structures (leaf-node status). The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive security incident investigation, evidence preservation, risk mitigation, and institutional liaison functions to safeguard the Parliament's security posture and operational integrity. Operating in coordination with sibling units (12D10, 12D20, 12D30, 12D3010), Unit 12D1010 executes its mandate through five integrated functional domains: (1) verification and incident response activities wherein it verifies security incident circumstances, retains and preserves evidentiary materials, and proposes evidence-based risk prevention and mitigation measures to preclude recurrence; (2) investigative reporting and records management encompassing preparation of comprehensive investigative action reports, proposal of institutional security enhancement measures, formal notification protocols to the Office of the Secretary General regarding ongoing investigations, and systematic population and maintenance of the security investigation archive database; (3) formal security investigations conducted as integral components of security incident investigations with mandatory Secretary General notification and prior Presidency approval requirements when Members of Parliament are implicated; (4) specialized analytical support to the RISK Unit of the General Directorate of Security and Protection through collaborative risk and threat analysis activities, coupled with liaison and coordination functions with national police and security services within mandated operational parameters; and (5) execution of investigative measures commissioned by competent external or internal bodies within defined operational scope. All activities are conducted in accordance with Articles 7-1°, 18, 19, 28, 29, and 30 of Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04, establishing the unit's legal and operational framework for security incident management and investigative operations.
12D1010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Security Incident Verification and Risk Mitigation** (39%): Verification of security incident circumstances, retention and preservation of evidentiary materials, proposition and implementation of evidence-based risk prevention and mitigation measures to preclude incident recurrence (Articles 18-19, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

• **Investigative Reporting, Documentation, and Records Management** (24%): Preparation of comprehensive investigative action reports, proposal of institutional security enhancement measures, formal notification to the Office of the Secretary General regarding ongoing investigations, and systematic population and maintenance of the security investigation archive database

• **Formal Security Investigations** (18%): Execution of comprehensive security investigations as integral components of security incident investigations, with integrated prevention objectives and dual mandate of incident clarification and future occurrence prevention, subject to mandatory Secretary General notification and prior Presidency approval requirements when Members of Parliament are implicated (Article 28, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

• **Risk Analysis Support and External Coordination** (15%): Provision of specialized analytical support to the RISK Unit of the General Directorate of Security and Protection for collaborative risk and threat analysis activities (10%) (Article 7-1°, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04); liaison and coordination operations with national police and security services within mandated scope (5%) (Article 30, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

• **Commissioned Investigative Measures** (4%): Execution of investigative measures as directed by competent external or internal bodies within the limits of established operational mandate (Article 29, Bureau Decision 2018/C 79/04)

12D20.name: 12D20
original_activities: Provide advice, training, and security awareness activities to Members of Parliament and staff of the General Secretariat to improve the protection of information inside and outside the Parliament premises, as well as a specialized electronic countermeasures service.
Ensure the development, management, and monitoring of the implementation of the information security policy and its application rules.
Provide risk assessment for events in European Parliament premises through TALOS (Threat Assessment Level Overview System).
Ensure the collection, consolidation, analysis, and dissemination of information in the field of security, including hybrid, threats, and foreign interference.
Promote the application for security clearance by European Parliament staff in order to increase trust and access to information.
Guarantee the implementation of internal procedures related to clearances and ex ante security checks in close collaboration with the competent services and national authorities.

Maintain a fruitful cooperation with the security services of other institutions and the national authorities of host countries through active and regular participation in coordination meetings and a fluid exchange of information.
12D20.refined_description: Unit 12D20 operates as a specialized security operations unit within the European Parliament's Directorate 12D, functioning as a critical nexus for integrated information security and threat assessment governance across the institution's comprehensive security framework. As a leaf-node operational unit without subordinate organizational structures, Unit 12D20 coordinates with sibling units 12D10, 12D1010, 12D30, and 12D3010 under the direction of parent unit 12D to address Parliament's multi-layered security requirements through seven interconnected activity streams. The unit's primary mission encompasses the development, implementation, and continuous monitoring of integrated information security and threat assessment protocols, information security policy frameworks, and their corresponding application rules across Parliament premises and operations. Unit 12D20 delivers targeted advice, training, and security awareness programming to Members of Parliament and General Secretariat staff regarding information protection in all operational environments, while simultaneously providing specialized electronic countermeasures services to enhance information security both within and outside Parliament facilities. The unit conducts systematic risk assessments for parliamentary events through the TALOS (Threat Assessment Level Overview System) early-warning mechanism, collects and analyzes security intelligence encompassing hybrid threats and foreign interference activities, administers security clearance procedures and ex ante security checks in collaboration with competent services and national authorities, and maintains strategic cooperative relationships with security services of peer institutions and national host country authorities through active participation in coordination mechanisms and systematic information exchange.
12D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Information Security Governance, Policy Implementation & Protective Measures** (45%): Development, management, and continuous monitoring of information security policy frameworks and application rules across the institution; delivery of comprehensive advice, training, and security awareness activities to Members of Parliament and General Secretariat staff regarding information protection across all operational environments; provision of specialized electronic countermeasures services to enhance information security within and outside Parliament premises.

• **Threat Assessment & Security Intelligence Operations** (33%): Systematic risk assessment for events occurring within European Parliament premises through the TALOS (Threat Assessment Level Overview System) platform (18%); collection, consolidation, analysis, and dissemination of security-related information encompassing hybrid threats, security incidents, and foreign interference activities (15%).

• **Personnel Security, Clearance Administration & Access Control** (17%): Promotion and facilitation of security clearance applications among European Parliament staff to enhance organizational trust and information access authorization (12%); implementation of internal clearance procedures and ex ante security checks in coordinated collaboration with competent services and national authorities (5%).

• **Institutional Cooperation & Strategic External Liaison** (5%): Maintenance of strategic cooperative relationships with security services of peer institutions and national authorities of host countries through active participation in coordination mechanisms and systematic information exchange.

12D30.name: 12D30
original_activities: Ensure the installation, operation and maintenance of physical security systems at the three sites and in the Member States.
Determine the most appropriate physical security options to protect European Parliament staff and premises in all workplaces.
Design, deploy and manage innovative and effective access control and physical security systems.

Ensure the coordination and consolidation of contributions provided by the different units of the General Directorate of Security and Protection as part of activities related to securing EPLOs and Europa Experience centers.
Ensure contract execution and planning and budget implementation for technology and physical security systems.
12D30.refined_description: Unit 12D30 operates as a specialized operational unit within Directorate 12D of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Security and Protection, bearing primary responsibility for comprehensive physical security infrastructure management across all institutional sites, the three primary Parliament locations, and all Member State locations. The unit's mandate encompasses five interconnected core activities that collectively represent 100% of documented workload, positioning it as the central operational hub for physical security infrastructure governance. These activities are executed in direct coordination with sibling units 12D10, 12D1010, 12D20, and 12D3010 under the parent Directorate 12D to deliver integrated institutional security outcomes across the European Parliament's distributed operational footprint. Unit 12D30's mission advances the Parliament's institutional security objectives by establishing integrated, technologically current, and operationally sustainable physical security frameworks that protect personnel, premises, and critical institutional assets. The unit's operational scope extends from tactical implementation of security systems to strategic planning and inter-directorate coordination, ensuring that all physical security investments align with institutional priorities while maintaining operational effectiveness across diverse geographic and functional contexts.
12D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Physical Security Systems Operations & Maintenance** (25%): Installation, operation, and comprehensive ongoing maintenance of physical security infrastructure systems deployed across the three primary institutional Parliament sites and throughout all Member State locations

• **Security Assessment & Strategic Planning** (20%): Determination, evaluation, and recommendation of optimal physical security protective measures and solutions tailored to safeguard European Parliament staff and premises across all workplace environments and operational contexts

• **Access Control & Security Technology Innovation** (20%): Design, deployment, and active management of innovative and operationally effective access control systems and advanced physical security technology infrastructure

• **Inter-Directorate Coordination & Liaison Office Security** (20%): Coordination and consolidation of integrated security contributions from multiple units within the General Directorate of Security and Protection specifically for European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) and Europa Experience centers

• **Contract Execution, Operational Planning & Budget Administration** (15%): Contract execution management, operational planning, and comprehensive budget implementation oversight for all technology-based systems and physical security infrastructure investments and procurement

12D3010.name: 12D3010
original_activities: Ensure effective and qualitative participation of the Directorate General for Security and Protection in the construction projects of the European Parliament, in order to guarantee the protection of people and property within our buildings.

Ensure overall DG SAFE support for European Parliament construction and facilities projects located outside the three main sites (such as local European Parliament branches, Europa Experience Centres), while ensuring coordination of contributions from all Directorate-General units involved in security operations (engineering, risk management, budget, operational security, safety, etc.).

Coordinate the development of technical guidelines, recommendations and specifications regarding security, in order to implement the policies of the General Directorate of Building Safety and Protection.
12D3010.refined_description: Unit 12D3010 operates as a specialized security coordination hub within Directorate 12D of the European Parliament, functioning at the secondary hierarchical level while serving as a critical integration node for the institution's built environment and infrastructure protection mandate. The unit bridges strategic security policy formulation with practical operational implementation across the Parliament's distributed physical infrastructure portfolio, maintaining essential coordination mechanisms with sibling units (12D10, 12D1010, 12D20, 12D30) within the parent Directorate 12D structure. Its mission encompasses three interconnected functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload: ensuring effective and qualitative participation of the Directorate General for Security and Protection in construction projects across all European Parliament facilities to guarantee comprehensive protection of personnel and property within institutional buildings (40% allocation); providing overarching DG SAFE support for construction and facilities initiatives located beyond the three primary operational sites—including decentralized locations such as local European Parliament branches and Europa Experience Centres—while orchestrating coordinated multidisciplinary contributions from all relevant Directorate-General units encompassing engineering, risk management, budgetary oversight, operational security protocols, and safety operations (40% allocation); and coordinating the development and implementation of technical guidelines, recommendations, and detailed specifications regarding security infrastructure and protocols to operationalize the strategic policies established by the General Directorate of Building Safety and Protection (20% allocation). Through these complementary activities, Unit 12D3010 positions itself as an indispensable cross-functional security coordination mechanism that integrates strategic policy oversight with distributed facility management across the Parliament's complete institutional footprint.
12D3010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Construction Project Security Integration and Oversight** (40%): Ensures effective and qualitative participation of the Directorate General for Security and Protection in European Parliament construction projects across all facilities, with comprehensive responsibility for guaranteeing protection of personnel and institutional property within all Parliament buildings

• **Decentralized Facilities Coordination and Multi-Unit Integration** (40%): Provides integrated DG SAFE support for construction and facilities initiatives at non-primary operational locations (European Parliament branches, Europa Experience Centres) while orchestrating coordinated multidisciplinary contributions from all relevant Directorate-General units including engineering, risk management, budget administration, operational security, and safety divisions

• **Security Policy Implementation and Technical Standards Development** (20%): Coordinates development and implementation of technical guidelines, recommendations, and detailed specifications addressing security infrastructure and protocols, ensuring operationalization of General Directorate Building Safety and Protection strategic policies

SG-00.name: SG-00
original_activities: Assist the Secretary General in the exercise of his functions.
SG-00.refined_description: SG-00, the Office of the Secretary General, occupies the apex of the European Parliament's administrative hierarchy as a Directorate General-level unit with direct reporting authority to the Secretary General. Operating as a leaf node organizational unit with no subordinate divisions, SG-00 functions as the direct executive support apparatus and principal administrative mechanism enabling the Secretary General's effective exercise of all constitutional, delegated, and operational responsibilities. The unit's singular, comprehensive mission—consuming 100% of allocated organizational workload—encompasses the provision of executive and administrative assistance across all dimensions of the Secretary General's authority, including strategic decision-making, institutional representation, administrative oversight, coordination of parliamentary operations, facilitation of institutional coordination, and management of administrative priorities. This unified focus reflects the critical nature of the office, wherein all personnel and resources are dedicated to enhancing the Secretary General's operational capacity and institutional effectiveness in discharging governance and management responsibilities. SG-00's specialized positioning outside the standard Directorate General hierarchy underscores its exceptional status as a direct extension of the Secretary General's executive authority, serving as the coordination nexus between the Secretary General and all subordinate organizational units and external stakeholders.
SG-00.refined_activity_summary: • Executive and Administrative Support to the Secretary General (100%): Comprehensive provision of executive and administrative assistance enabling the Secretary General's effective exercise of all constitutional, delegated, and operational functions, encompassing strategic decision-making, institutional representation, administrative oversight, coordination of parliamentary operations, facilitation of institutional coordination with subordinate units and external stakeholders, and management of administrative priorities across all areas of organizational responsibility.

SG-10.name: SG-10
original_activities: Provide the institution with independent and objective assurance and advisory services intended to add value to and improve the operations of the institution. Evaluate governance, risk management and control processes using a systematic and methodical approach, and suggest actions aimed at improving them. Carry out the work (planning, implementation and reporting) based on a process of continuous consultation with the audited services.

Examine the follow-up given by the audited services to all actions approved during each audit, upon their expiry, as part of the biannual transversal monitoring process, and draw up a report each semester for the institution, after bilateral consultation of the services concerned.
Prepare and adopt a based annual work program whose priorities have been set based on an overall risk analysis, including direct consultation with each of the delegated authorizing officers.

Meet regularly with the bodies concerned within the framework of the governance of the institution, in particular with the Audit Panel (audit monitoring committee) and the Secretary General; prepare an annual report to the institution indicating the number and type of internal audits carried out, the main recommendations made and the follow-up given to them; present the annual report to the Bureau and the Budgetary Control Committee.
Adapt and/or develop the required methodological tools, in order to optimize the audit process and constantly improve the quality of the Internal Audit Service's services.
Provide the European Ombudsman with assurance and advisory services (as described in point 1).
SG-10.refined_description: SG-10, the Internal Audit Service of the European Parliament, operates as a Directorate-General-level independent unit reporting directly to the Secretary General, functioning as a critical institutional safeguard and catalyst for organizational improvement. The unit's foundational mandate is to deliver independent and objective assurance and advisory services that systematically evaluate governance frameworks, risk management processes, and internal control mechanisms across the institution, formulating evidence-based recommendations for continuous operational enhancement through sustained collaborative consultation with audited services (74% of workload). Complementing this core audit function, SG-10 conducts biannual transversal monitoring of audit recommendation implementation across all audited services through bilateral consultation, producing comprehensive semester reports for institutional distribution, while simultaneously maintaining strategic governance engagement with the Audit Panel and Secretary General through regular consultation meetings (8% of workload). The unit establishes its operational direction through annual risk-based work program development informed by comprehensive risk analysis and direct consultation with delegated authorizing officers, and fulfills institutional accountability obligations through annual reporting to the Bureau and Budgetary Control Committee detailing audit scope, key recommendations, and implementation progress (5% of workload). To sustain continuous service excellence, SG-10 dedicates resources to developing and adapting methodological audit tools and frameworks that optimize audit processes and enhance service quality standards (5% of workload), while extending its independent assurance and advisory capabilities to the European Ombudsman utilizing consistent institutional audit methodology and standards (3% of workload). This comprehensive mandate ensures SG-10 functions as both a guardian of institutional integrity and a strategic partner in organizational effectiveness across the Parliament's institutional ecosystem.
SG-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Audit and Assurance Services (74%)**: Provision of independent and objective assurance and advisory services; systematic evaluation of governance, risk management, and internal control frameworks; formulation of evidence-based improvement recommendations; collaborative audit planning, implementation, and reporting with audited services; application of methodical audit approaches designed to enhance institutional operations and add measurable value

• **Compliance Monitoring, Governance Engagement, and Institutional Reporting (13%)**: Biannual transversal monitoring of audit recommendation implementation with bilateral service consultation and semester reporting (8%); regular governance engagement with Audit Panel and Secretary General through consultation meetings; annual institutional reporting to Bureau and Budgetary Control Committee detailing audit scope, recommendations, and implementation status (5%)

• **Strategic Planning and Risk Management (5%)**: Development and adoption of annual risk-based work programs informed by comprehensive risk analysis and direct consultation with delegated authorizing officers; alignment of audit priorities with institutional risk landscape

• **Methodological Development and Quality Enhancement (5%)**: Continuous adaptation and development of audit methodologies, tools, and frameworks to optimize audit processes and improve service quality standards across the Internal Audit Service

• **Extended Assurance Services (3%)**: Provision of specialized assurance and advisory services to the European Ombudsman utilizing independent and objective methodology aligned with core institutional audit frameworks and standards

SG-20.name: SG-20
original_activities: Inform and advise those responsible for processing as well as employees who carry out processing on regulatory obligations in the field.
Inform those responsible for processing personal data and the persons concerned of their rights and obligations, ensure awareness-raising and training of staff.

Independently ensure the internal application of Regulation (EU) 2018/1725; monitor compliance with the Regulation, other applicable Union legislation containing data protection provisions and the controller's internal rules on the protection of personal data.
Keep a record of the various treatments carried out.
Carry out design, analysis, control and information work in the field of personal data protection, in liaison with the relevant services of the General Secretariat.
Ensure administrative coordination, prepare legal acts and monitor case law, the CPD-DPD network, interns.
Collaborate with the European Data Protection Supervisor as well as with counterpart services from other institutions.
Report processing operations likely to present particular risks to the European Data Protection Supervisor.
SG-20.refined_description: SG-20 is a specialized data protection unit operating at the Directorate-General level within the European Parliament's General Secretariat, reporting directly to the Secretary General and functioning as an independent organizational entity with no subordinate units. The unit serves as the institutional custodian of personal data protection compliance and regulatory governance, executing a comprehensive mandate that encompasses regulatory advisory services, compliance monitoring, strategic coordination, and risk management across the Parliament's data processing operations. Operating as the primary institutional resource for data protection governance, SG-20 ensures the Parliament maintains robust compliance with Regulation (EU) 2018/1725 and applicable Union data protection legislation through a multifaceted approach that integrates authoritative regulatory guidance, stakeholder education, independent monitoring, technical analysis, and formal liaison relationships with the European Data Protection Supervisor and counterpart institutional services. The unit's activities are characterized by sustained engagement with data controllers, processing personnel, and data subjects to establish a comprehensive data protection culture while maintaining formal risk-reporting obligations and administrative coordination responsibilities across the institution's data governance framework.
SG-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Regulatory Guidance and Compliance Assurance** (40%): Provision of authoritative regulatory advisory services to data controllers and processing personnel regarding their obligations under applicable data protection legislation; serving as the primary institutional resource for understanding and implementing compliance requirements in the data protection field.

• **Rights Awareness, Education, and Training** (20%): Execution of comprehensive awareness-raising and training programs targeting both institutional staff and data subjects; communication of respective rights and obligations to ensure informed participation in institutional data protection governance and promotion of data protection culture across the Parliament.

• **Independent Compliance Monitoring and Internal Governance** (20%): Independent monitoring and enforcement of Regulation (EU) 2018/1725 application across the institution; maintenance of comprehensive records of processing activities; oversight of institutional adherence to applicable Union data protection legislation and internal data protection rules.

• **Technical Analysis, Design, and Strategic Coordination** (10%): Conduct of specialized design, analysis, control, and information work on data protection matters; coordination of technical initiatives with relevant General Secretariat services; preparation and monitoring of legal instruments; tracking of case law developments relevant to institutional operations.
