• **External Liaison, Institutional Coordination, and Risk Reporting** (5%): Formal liaison and collaboration with the European Data Protection Supervisor and counterpart data protection services across other EU institutions; reporting of processing operations presenting particular risks to the EDPS; coordination of the CPD-DPD network; administrative coordination functions and intern management.

SG-30.name: SG-30
original_activities: Assist anyone in a working relationship with the European Parliament who is involved in a conflict or a difficult situation concerning a work relationship to communicate openly, explore solutions and reach an agreement through mediation with the other person(s) concerned.
Provide support to individuals involved in a conflict or a difficult situation concerning a work relationship, through conflict coaching, information provision and referrals to competent services.
Support and facilitate the prevention and resolution of workplace conflicts through awareness raising and tailored workshops addressing the unique needs, challenges and dynamics of a team or group.
SG-30.refined_description: SG-30 is a specialized organizational unit operating at the Directorate General level under direct reporting to the Secretary General of the European Parliament, functioning as the institution's primary institutional mechanism for workplace conflict resolution, relationship management, and organizational well-being. The unit's integrated mission encompasses three complementary operational pillars that collectively address the full spectrum of workplace conflict intervention across all hierarchical levels and professional categories within the European Parliament. Operating with a comprehensive, multi-layered approach, SG-30 allocates 40% of its operational capacity to direct mediation services, providing structured facilitation for employees and other individuals maintaining working relationships with the European Parliament who are experiencing conflicts or difficult interpersonal situations, with the explicit objective of enabling open communication, systematic exploration of mutually acceptable solutions, and achievement of formal agreements between all concerned parties. Concurrently, 30% of the unit's resources are dedicated to providing comprehensive individual support services, including targeted conflict coaching, informational guidance, and strategic referrals to appropriate specialized competent services for those navigating workplace conflicts or difficult professional relationships. The remaining 30% of capacity focuses on proactive conflict prevention and systemic resolution capabilities through awareness-raising initiatives and the design and delivery of customized workshops that address the specific operational challenges, relational dynamics, and particular needs of designated teams or organizational groups. Collectively, these three activity streams create a comprehensive institutional resource for fostering constructive workplace relationships, preventing escalation of interpersonal conflicts, and building organizational capacity for conflict resolution across the European Parliament.
SG-30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Direct Mediation and Facilitated Conflict Resolution Services** (40%): Structured mediation assistance for employees and working community members experiencing conflicts or difficult interpersonal situations, facilitating open communication, systematic exploration of mutually acceptable solutions, and achievement of formal agreements with all concerned parties

• **Individual Support, Coaching, and Referral Services** (30%): Personalized conflict coaching, comprehensive informational guidance, and strategic referrals to appropriate specialized competent services for individuals navigating workplace conflicts or difficult professional relationships

• **Organizational Prevention and Systemic Capacity-Building** (30%): Proactive conflict prevention and resolution support through awareness-raising initiatives and tailored workshop programming designed to address specific team operational challenges, relational dynamics, and organizational group needs

SG-40.name: SG-40
original_activities: Ensure the organisation and follow-up (draft agendas, Chair notes, Minutes, follow-up letters, Notices to Members) of the meetings of the Bureau and the Quaestors as well as of the Bureau Working Groups ('Communication, Outreach and Research', 'Sustainable Infrastructure and Services', 'High-Level Group on Gender Equality and Diversity', 'Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity and Information Security', 'Citizens' language and linguistic services', 'Audit Panel', 'Artistic Committee').

Ensure a coordinated planning of files to be referred to the Bureau and the Quaestors, in close cooperation with the CSG; Ensure the organisation and follow-up (draft agenda, Chair notes, Minutes, draft reports, letters) of the meetings of the 'Advisory Committee dealing with Harassment complaints concerning Members of the EP' via its dedicated Secretariat (Harassment Service) directly attached to the Secretariat of the Bureau and the Quaestors.

Maintain and update Parliament's Compendium of Rules in relation to Bureau and Quaestors' decisions and provide for their publication in the Official Journal (OJ) of the European Union, as appropriate; Ensure documents management and workflows via GEDA, including translation of meeting documents; Ensure that deadlines and referrals to the Bureau and the Quaestors are met; Monitor follow-up of Bureau and Quaestors' decisions.

Prepare draft replies for requests and other correspondence addressed to the President, the Secretary-General and the Quaestors in relation to the competences and activities of the Bureau and the Quaestors; Reply to queries submitted by Members via the functional email boxes of the Bureau and the Quaestors; Ensure administrative support of the assistants to the Vice-Presidents and the Quaestors, directly attached to the Secretariat, including their missions.

Provide support and act as Secretariat for activities of Vice-Presidents in relation to certain specific duties assigned to them by the Bureau (Dialogue under Article 17 TFUE; Relations with European civil society organisations); Act as Parliament's Secretariat for the dialogue with churches, religious associations or communities, philosophical and non-confessional organisations as foreseen under Article 17 of the TFEU.

Support the Quaestors in the performance of their individual responsibilities, including managing the functional mailboxes of the Quaestors' and the Bureau Secretariat; Coordinate and prepare draft replies from the President concerning requests from Members to be excused from Plenary absence/s in accordance with Article 45 of the IMMS; Act as Secretariat for the meetings of conciliation in case of dismissal of an APA, as foreseen by Article 23 of the Implementing Measures of the APA Statute.
Ensure a coordinated approach to requests for public access to documents under Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 concerning documents held by the Secretariat of the Bureau and the Quaestors.
SG-40.refined_description:
SG-40, operating at the Directorate General level and reporting directly to the Secretary-General, functions as the central Secretariat of the Bureau and the Quaestors of the European Parliament, serving as the primary administrative, organizational, and secretarial backbone for the institution's highest governing bodies and their decision-making structures. The unit's core mission encompasses the comprehensive organization, documentation, and follow-up of all Bureau and Quaestors meetings and their seven specialized working groups ('Communication, Outreach and Research', 'Sustainable Infrastructure and Services', 'High-Level Group on Gender Equality and Diversity', 'Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity and Information Security', 'Citizens' language and linguistic services', 'Audit Panel', and 'Artistic Committee'), which constitutes the predominant focus (65%) of its workload through the preparation and maintenance of draft agendas, Chair notes, Minutes, follow-up correspondence, and Notices to Members. Beyond core meeting administration, SG-40 coordinates strategic file planning with the CSG (10%), operates the dedicated Harassment Service Secretariat managing the Advisory Committee addressing harassment complaints concerning Members of the EP (10%), maintains Parliament's regulatory Compendium of Rules with publication in the Official Journal, manages document workflows and translations via GEDA, and monitors deadline compliance and decision follow-up (5%). The unit additionally prepares draft replies to executive correspondence addressed to the President, Secretary-General, and Quaestors, responds to Member queries through functional email boxes, and provides administrative support to Vice-Presidential and Quaestoral assistants including mission coordination (5%). SG-40 provides specialized secretariat services for Vice-Presidential activities including dialogue under Article 17 TFEU and relations with European civil society, churches, religious associations, philosophical and non-confessional organizations (5%), supports individual Quaestoral responsibilities through functional mailbox management, coordinates Article 45 IMMS absence excusal requests, serves as conciliation secretariat for APA dismissal proceedings, and ensures coordinated institutional compliance with public document access requests under Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 (5%). Through these integrated functions, SG-40 directly enables institutional governance, supports strategic dialogue initiatives, and maintains the administrative infrastructure necessary for Parliament's leadership bodies to execute their constitutional, operational, and fiduciary responsibilities.

##
SG-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Meeting Organization, Governance Administration, and Documentation** (65%): Organize and conduct comprehensive follow-up for all Bureau and Quaestors meetings and seven specialized working groups, including preparation of draft agendas, Chair notes, Minutes, follow-up correspondence, and Notices to Members; coordinate referral planning and strategic file preparation with CSG; maintain Parliament's Compendium of Rules relative to Bureau and Quaestors decisions with publication in the Official Journal; manage document workflows and translations via GEDA; monitor adherence to deadlines and track implementation of decisions

• **Harassment Service Administration and Specialized Committee Management** (10%): Operate and manage the dedicated Harassment Service Secretariat directly attached to the Bureau and Quaestors office; organize and administer the Advisory Committee dealing with harassment complaints concerning Members of the European Parliament; coordinate all procedural and administrative requirements for harassment complaint processing

• **Executive Correspondence and Administrative Support Services** (10%): Prepare draft replies to requests and correspondence addressed to the President, Secretary-General, and Quaestors regarding their competences and activities; respond to Member queries submitted through Bureau and Quaestors functional email boxes; provide comprehensive administrative support to Vice-Presidential assistants and Quaestoral assistants, including mission coordination and secretariat functions

• **Vice-Presidential Activities and Article 17 TFEU Dialogue Secretariat** (5%): Serve as designated Secretariat for Vice-Presidential activities related to specific Bureau-assigned duties, including dialogue under Article 17 TFEU and relations with European civil society organizations; act as Parliament's official Secretariat for dialogue with churches, religious associations or communities, and philosophical and non-confessional organizations

• **Quaestorial Operations, Member Services, and Conciliation Administration** (5%): Support individual Quaestoral responsibilities through functional mailbox management; coordinate and prepare draft Presidential replies concerning Member requests for Plenary absence excusals in accordance with Article 45 of the IMMS; serve as Secretariat for conciliation meetings regarding APA dismissals under Article 23 of the APA Statute Implementing Measures

• **Transparency Compliance and Public Access to Documents Administration** (5%): Ensure coordinated institutional approach to public access requests for documents held by the Secretariat of the Bureau and the Quaestors under Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001; maintain compliance with EU transparency requirements and monitor deadline compliance for document access requests

SG-4010.name: SG-4010
original_activities: Ensure the functioning of the Secretariat of the Advisory Committee responsible for examining complaints of harassment concerning Members of the European Parliament; organize and prepare meetings of this body, including hearings of those concerned, training seminars and conferences; prepare agendas, chair sheets, minutes and meeting follow-up letters and any other relevant documents.
Prepare files and documentation regarding cases submitted to the Committee and reports on harassment complaints.

Prepare documents/provide information relating to measures taken by Parliament to deal with conflicts and harassment between MEPs, MEPs' assistants, interns or other staff; coordinate external representation of the Advisory Committee (e.g., before political groups, parliamentary committees and public hearings).

Ensure liaison with the offices of the President and the Secretary General, and with the relevant services, in particular the General Directorate of Personnel, the Legal Service, the General Directorate of Translation, the General Directorate of Communication (for press questions concerning cases of harassment).
Manage the confidential internal archives of the Secretariat; ensure the updating of the Advisory Committee intranet site.
SG-4010.refined_description:
Unit SG-4010 functions as the dedicated operational secretariat supporting the Advisory Committee on Member Harassment and Complaints, reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament as a specialized oversight body responsible for examining harassment allegations involving Members of the European Parliament, their accredited assistants, interns, and Parliament staff. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses full administrative, operational, logistical, and coordination functions to ensure the effective institutional functioning of this independent committee and to support Parliament's commitment to maintaining a safe and respectful working environment across all organizational levels. SG-4010 maintains critical liaison relationships with the offices of the President and Secretary General while coordinating systematically with specialized Parliament services including the General Directorate of Personnel, the Legal Service, the General Directorate of Translation, and the General Directorate of Communication, thereby serving as the central institutional interface for all Advisory Committee operations and external communications. The unit's activities encompass the complete lifecycle of harassment complaint management—from initial case documentation through formal reporting—alongside the preparation of institutional documentation regarding Parliament's anti-harassment and conflict-resolution measures, ensuring comprehensive institutional accountability mechanisms are maintained. Through its integrated approach to secretariat administration, case management, institutional representation, inter-service coordination, and secure information management, SG-4010 provides essential operational infrastructure that enables the Advisory Committee to fulfill its independent mandate while maintaining confidentiality standards and ensuring accessibility of current information through dedicated digital platforms.

##
SG-4010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Committee Administration and Meeting Management** (45%): Ensure the operational continuity and functioning of the Advisory Committee's secretariat; organize and facilitate all committee meetings, structured hearings of complainants and respondents, professional development seminars, and institutional conferences; prepare comprehensive meeting documentation including agendas, chair briefing sheets, official minutes, follow-up correspondence, and supporting materials.

• **Case File and Documentation Management** (25%): Prepare and maintain detailed case files and comprehensive supporting documentation for all harassment complaints submitted to the Committee; produce formal reports analyzing harassment allegations, institutional responses, and case materials.

• **Institutional Documentation and External Representation** (15%): Prepare institutional documentation regarding Parliament's anti-harassment and conflict-resolution measures applicable to Members, their accredited assistants, interns, and Parliament staff; coordinate external representation and communications of the Advisory Committee before political groups, parliamentary committees, and public forums; serve as primary institutional interface for Committee communications.

• **Inter-Service Liaison and Coordination** (10%): Maintain operational liaison with the offices of the President and Secretary General; coordinate with the General Directorate of Personnel, Legal Service, General Directorate of Translation, and General Directorate of Communication; manage external communications and media inquiries concerning harassment cases.

• **Confidential Records and Digital Infrastructure Management** (5%): Manage confidential internal archives and secure storage of sensitive Committee materials; maintain and update the Advisory Committee's dedicated intranet platform to ensure current information accessibility and ongoing administration of digital systems.

SG-50.name: SG-50
original_activities: Support and coordinate the implementation across the Institution of the European Parliament's Risk Management policy, Business Continuity policy and Internal Control framework. Maintain corporate policy and guidance up-to-date.

Ensure, guide and coordinate the continuous process carried out by the Directorates-General consisting of the identification, analysis and treatment of their risks. In cooperation with the Directorates-General, establish action plans for cross-cutting risks. Issue at least once per year to the attention of the Secretary-General an overview of the main risks at corporate level and their related controls.

Maintain the European Parliament's Business Continuity Plan up-to-date at the corporate level to ensure continuity of Parliament's core activities in case of crisis or severe disruption. Guide and support Directorates-General in the elaboration, implementation, exercising and testing of their respective Business Continuity Plans at the local level. Prepare training and awareness raising activities in the field of Crisis and Business Continuity Management.

Develop guidelines and protocols for timely, efficient and coordinated corporate-level Crisis Management by the Secretary-General. In particular, facilitate decision-making within the Crisis and Business Continuity Management Team. Ensure the readiness of Parliament's Crisis and Business Continuity Management System to respond to a crisis or disruptive event including the SMS Crisis Communication system.

Support the implementation and assessment by the Directorates-General of the Minimum Internal Control Standards via appropriate guidance and knowledge sharing. Provide expertise and guidance to Internal Control Coordinators. Design and help deliver training and awareness raising activities in the field of Internal Control.
Coordinate the activities of the Working Groups on Risk Management, Internal Control and Business Continuity Management and collaborate with other Inter-DG Steering Groups in Parliament.
Ensure the implementation of audit recommendations in the areas under the responsibility of the Unit.
SG-50.refined_description:
SG-50 is a Directorate-General level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary-General of the European Parliament, serving as the institution's central governance and resilience authority responsible for institutional risk management, business continuity, and internal control frameworks. The unit's primary mission encompasses the strategic oversight and operational coordination of three interconnected institutional pillars—Risk Management, Business Continuity, and Internal Control frameworks—which are institutionalized and coordinated comprehensively across the entire Parliament. SG-50 maintains and coordinates the implementation of the Parliament's Risk Management policy, Business Continuity policy, and Internal Control framework across all institutional structures while ensuring corporate policy documentation and guidance materials remain current, actionable, and aligned with institutional needs. The unit orchestrates a continuous, institution-wide risk identification, analysis, and treatment process conducted by all Directorates-General, establishes coordinated action plans for cross-cutting organizational risks, and delivers annual comprehensive risk assessments to the Secretary-General that synthesize main corporate-level risks and their associated control mechanisms. SG-50 maintains the Parliament's Business Continuity Plan at the strategic corporate level to ensure organizational resilience and continuity of core parliamentary activities during crises or severe operational disruptions, while simultaneously guiding and supporting individual Directorates-General in developing, implementing, exercising, and testing their own localized Business Continuity Plans, supplemented by targeted training and awareness initiatives in Crisis and Business Continuity Management. The unit develops comprehensive guidelines and protocols for coordinated corporate-level Crisis Management decision-making by the Secretary-General, facilitates the Crisis and Business Continuity Management Team's operational effectiveness, and maintains the operational readiness of Parliament's integrated Crisis and Business Continuity Management System, including the SMS Crisis Communication infrastructure. Additionally, SG-50 provides expert guidance on Minimum Internal Control Standards implementation and assessment across Directorates-General, supports Internal Control Coordinators through knowledge-sharing mechanisms and technical expertise, coordinates all Working Groups focused on Risk Management, Internal Control, and Business Continuity Management while collaborating with other Inter-Directorate-General Steering Groups, and ensures systematic implementation of audit recommendations within its areas of responsibility.

---
SG-50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Corporate Policy Framework Development, Maintenance & Governance** (30%): Support and coordinate institution-wide implementation of Risk Management policy, Business Continuity policy, and Internal Control framework across all Directorates-General; maintain corporate policy documentation and guidance materials in current, effective, and actionable operational status; ensure alignment with institutional needs and requirements

• **Risk Identification, Analysis, Governance & Strategic Coordination** (20%): Ensure and guide continuous risk identification, analysis, and treatment processes conducted by all Directorates-General; establish coordinated action plans for cross-cutting organizational risks; deliver annual comprehensive corporate-level risk assessments and control evaluations to the Secretary-General that synthesize main institutional risks and associated control mechanisms

• **Business Continuity Planning, Crisis Management & Organizational Resilience** (20%): Maintain Parliament's corporate-level Business Continuity Plan to ensure organizational resilience and continuity of core parliamentary activities during crises or severe operational disruptions; guide and support individual Directorates-General in developing, implementing, exercising, and testing their localized Business Continuity Plans; develop comprehensive guidelines and protocols for coordinated corporate-level Crisis Management decision-making; design and deliver Crisis and Business Continuity Management training and awareness initiatives

• **Crisis Management System Readiness & Coordination** (10%): Develop comprehensive guidelines and protocols for corporate-level Crisis Management under Secretary-General authority; facilitate the Crisis and Business Continuity Management Team's operational effectiveness and decision-making processes; maintain operational readiness of Parliament's integrated Crisis and Business Continuity Management System, including SMS Crisis Communication infrastructure

• **Internal Control Standards Implementation & Support** (10%): Support Directorates-General in implementing and assessing compliance with Minimum Internal Control Standards; provide expert guidance and knowledge-sharing mechanisms to Internal Control Coordinators; design and deliver specialized Internal Control training and awareness programs

• **Inter-Directorate Coordination & Audit Compliance** (5%): Coordinate all Working Groups focused on Risk Management, Internal Control, and Business Continuity Management; collaborate with other Inter-Directorate-General Steering Groups on governance matters

• **Audit Recommendation Implementation** (5%): Ensure systematic implementation of audit recommendations within the unit's areas of responsibility across Risk Management, Business Continuity, and Internal Control domains

SG-6010.name: SG-6010
original_activities: Contribute to the simplification and rationalization of the administrative planning, monitoring and reporting cycle.
Support the implementation of the administration's priorities, including the simplification actions of the Action Plan as well as the eLegislate project and related applications.
Coordinate and support the process of developing work programs and annual activity reports within the European Parliament administration.
Develop, maintain and manage a central platform of administration planning tools (integrated methodologies, tools and digital models as well as their database).
Simplify and harmonize the indicators with regard to the objectives set by the general management.

Support process improvement within and across branches. Focus on improving efficiency and effectiveness through simplification in order to optimize resources and create synergies within the administration.
SG-6010.refined_description: SG-6010 is a Directorate-General level organizational unit reporting directly to the Secretary General of the European Parliament, functioning as the central administrative modernization, planning, and institutional optimization hub for the Parliament. Operating as a specialized staff function with no subordinate units, SG-6010 serves as the primary driver of comprehensive administrative transformation across the institution through six strategically interconnected activities that collectively address planning architecture, digital modernization, institutional coordination, technical infrastructure, performance management, and cross-organizational efficiency optimization. The unit exercises coordinating authority over the Parliament's administrative planning, monitoring, and reporting cycles while simultaneously supporting the implementation of institution-wide strategic priorities including the Action Plan simplification initiatives and the eLegislate project ecosystem. SG-6010 maintains direct operational responsibility for developing, maintaining, and managing the centralized integrated administration planning platform—encompassing methodologies, digital models, and associated databases—that provides the essential technical and procedural infrastructure enabling institutional planning across all administrative branches. The unit's mission encompasses the systematic simplification and rationalization of administrative processes, standardization of planning methodologies, harmonization of performance measurement frameworks aligned with general management objectives, and facilitation of cross-branch process improvement initiatives designed to maximize resource utilization and generate organizational synergies. As a direct accountability extension of the Secretary General's administrative governance agenda, SG-6010 functions as an integrated service provider responsible for institutional optimization through administrative modernization.
SG-6010.refined_activity_summary:
• **Administrative Cycle Rationalization & Planning Architecture** (25%): Contribute to the simplification and rationalization of the administrative planning, monitoring, and reporting cycle, establishing the foundational governance framework and procedural architecture for institutional operations and efficiency.

• **Strategic Implementation & Digital Transformation** (20%): Support implementation of administration-wide priorities including the Action Plan simplification initiatives, eLegislate project deployment, and associated digital application ecosystems to ensure strategic alignment across technological and operational domains.

• **Work Program Development & Institutional Coordination** (20%): Coordinate and support the development of work programs and annual activity reports throughout the European Parliament administration, standardizing planning methodologies and reporting processes across all organizational branches.

• **Planning Tools Infrastructure & Platform Management** (15%): Develop, maintain, and manage the centralized integrated administration planning platform comprising methodologies, digital models, and comprehensive databases, providing the technical infrastructure enabling institutional planning requirements.

• **Performance Harmonization & Cross-Branch Process Improvement** (20%): Simplify and harmonize performance indicators in alignment with general management objectives (10%), while supporting cross-branch process improvement initiatives focused on efficiency and effectiveness optimization through systematic simplification to maximize resource allocation and create administrative synergies (10%).

SG-70.name: SG-70
original_activities: Coordinate and implement the European Parliament's EMAS Action Plans in line with the environmental policy and targets set by the Bureau to ensure that the institution remains registered at its three workplaces in accordance with the high EU EMAS standard for environmental management, so that Members and citizens can rely on the European Parliament openly respecting all existing environmental legislation.

Carry out data collection, calculation and reporting to the Bureau and the public on the environmental performance of the European Parliament, including the institution's carbon footprint, the annual environmental management review and the Parliament's environmental statement, providing expert advice on environmental and sustainability issues and preparing policy and targets for the 2024-2029 legislature.

Organize communication and training initiatives to raise awareness among MPs, hierarchy and staff with the aim of encouraging their contribution to reducing Parliament's impact on the environment. Follow up on questions and suggestions aimed at improving the environmental management of the institution.
Extend the European Parliament's Environmental Management System and EMAS registration to other buildings in the three workplaces and to Parliament's liaison offices in seven Member States.

Organize internal, legal and external EMAS audits of Parliament's activities and buildings in Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg and, in cooperation with the European Commission, liaison offices in seven Member States, including legal audits and audits relating to the institution's carbon footprint, and ensure follow-up, to verify compliance and improvements.

Organize the governance structure for environmental management, including meetings of the inter-DG environmental management steering group, the environmental management steering committee, working groups on sustainable public procurement and waste, inter-institutional meetings, networking with national parliaments, participation in relevant administrative working groups, contribution to meetings of the Bureau and its working groups.

Provide technical advice to departments on the integration of environmental management practices, including advice in areas such as resource efficiency, environmental building retrofit, waste management, storage of hazardous chemicals and overall sustainability.

Launch public procurement procedures, particularly on carbon offsetting, deal with financial and budgetary issues (e.g. activity reports), as well as administrative issues such as management of the SPP Helpdesk.
SG-70.refined_description: SG-70 is a specialized organizational unit operating at the Directorate General level and reporting directly to the Secretary General, serving as the European Parliament's central environmental management and sustainability authority responsible for advancing institutional environmental governance across all three primary workplaces (Brussels, Luxembourg, and Strasbourg) and liaison offices in seven Member States. The unit's core mission encompasses the comprehensive coordination and implementation of the European Parliament's EMAS (Eco-Management and Audit Scheme) Action Plans and certification framework, ensuring continuous compliance with EU environmental management standards while maintaining active EMAS registration across existing facilities and systematically extending this certification to additional buildings and Member State liaison offices. SG-70 executes systematic environmental performance monitoring and data collection, producing carbon footprint assessments, annual environmental management reviews, and official environmental statements for Bureau reporting, while simultaneously providing expert policy guidance and establishing environmental targets and strategic frameworks for the 2024-2029 legislative term. The unit implements a comprehensive governance and compliance infrastructure encompassing internal, legal, and external EMAS audits across all Parliament facilities and liaison offices, inter-Directorate General steering committees, specialized working groups on sustainable public procurement and waste management, inter-institutional coordination mechanisms, and systematic follow-up procedures to verify compliance and document improvements. SG-70 drives institutional cultural transformation through structured communication and training programs targeting Members, management, and staff, manages environmental improvement suggestions and compliance inquiries, and provides technical advisory services to Parliament departments on resource efficiency optimization, environmental building retrofits, waste management protocols, hazardous chemical storage compliance, and integrated sustainability practices. The unit administers public procurement procedures with emphasis on carbon offsetting initiatives, manages financial and budgetary documentation, operates the Sustainable Public Procurement Helpdesk, and maintains the institutional governance infrastructure through coordination of inter-Directorate General steering groups and participation in Bureau meetings and relevant administrative forums.
SG-70.refined_activity_summary:
• **EMAS Certification, Compliance, and Registration Management** (30%): Coordinate implementation and maintenance of EMAS registration and active certification across Parliament's three principal workplaces (Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg) in accordance with EU environmental management standards and regulatory requirements (20%); systematically extend Environmental Management System certification to additional buildings within the three primary workplaces and to liaison offices across seven Member States, supported by comprehensive expansion planning and implementation frameworks (10%).

• **Environmental Performance Monitoring, Data Management, and Strategic Planning** (20%): Execute systematic data collection, calculation, and analysis of environmental performance metrics; prepare carbon footprint assessments and annual environmental management reviews; publish Parliament's official environmental statement for Bureau reporting and public accountability; provide expert policy guidance on environmental and sustainability matters; establish environmental targets and develop strategic frameworks for the 2024-2029 legislative term.

• **Governance Infrastructure, Auditing Framework, and Technical Advisory Services** (30%): Organize and oversee internal, legal, and external EMAS audits across Brussels, Luxembourg, Strasbourg facilities and Member State liaison offices, with implementation of systematic follow-up mechanisms to verify compliance and measure improvements (10%); establish and coordinate inter-Directorate General environmental management steering groups, committees, specialized working groups on sustainable public procurement and waste management, inter-institutional collaboration mechanisms, and active participation in Bureau meetings and administrative forums (10%); provide technical advisory services to Parliament departments on integrating environmental management practices, including resource efficiency optimization, environmental building retrofits, waste management protocols, hazardous chemical storage compliance, and broader sustainability integration (10%).

• **Stakeholder Engagement, Awareness-Raising, and Institutional Culture Change** (15%): Design and organize communication and training initiatives targeting Members, management hierarchy, and staff to promote environmental responsibility and behavioral change; process and respond to environmental improvement suggestions and compliance inquiries; foster institutional commitment to environmental stewardship and sustainability contributions.

• **Administrative Operations and Procurement Management** (5%): Manage public procurement procedures with emphasis on carbon offsetting initiatives; handle financial and budgetary documentation and activity reporting; operate the Sustainable Public Procurement Helpdesk to support institutional stakeholders in integrating environmental considerations into procurement decisions.

SG-75.name: SG-75
original_activities: Create innovative and creative multi- and cross-media products in support of a limited selection of complex, high-impact communications campaigns aimed at a broad audience within the European Parliament and beyond. This category includes: video tutorials for MEPs; short films and documentaries on figures from the European Parliament ('Oral History' series).

Create innovative multi- and cross-media products for a limited selection of complex, high-impact communication campaigns, for a wide internal audience (at inter-DG level) and beyond the EP. This includes creative staging videos, both for General Secretariat events (e.g. EP CuP) and for all its staff (e.g. well-being); promotional videos for all staff that can also use advanced animation techniques.

Take the key role in staging important internal communication events and campaigns, such as the 'EP Management Dialogues', by creating visual identities and tailor-made multi- and cross-media products and narrative videos, with the aim of promoting the strategic execution framework, inter-DG or even inter-institutional projects (ESPAS) or other similar types of projects. Produce promotional films.

Produce and deliver: sound design (with the possibility of composing/producing music in-house and recording voice-overs) as part of a large campaign; photography services, in studio or not, as part of a large campaign or on an ad hoc basis; objects made at the 'Fab-Lab' to support the visual identity of a campaign (for example, for signage, etc.).
Contribute to strengthening strategic communication within the European Parliament administration by participating in strategic working groups (e.g. Internal Communication).
SG-75.refined_description:
SG-75 operates as a specialized creative and multimedia production unit positioned at the Directorate General level within the European Parliament's General Secretariat, reporting directly to the Secretary General. The unit functions as the institution's primary strategic creative hub, responsible for conceptualizing, producing, and delivering innovative multi- and cross-media communications products that advance the European Parliament's institutional messaging across both internal and external stakeholder audiences. SG-75's operational mandate encompasses the creation of sophisticated multimedia assets supporting complex, high-impact communications campaigns—including specialized educational content such as MEP video tutorials and documentary-format institutional narratives through the 'Oral History' series—alongside equally weighted creative production for inter-Directorate General coordination and internal stakeholder engagement, encompassing staff wellness initiatives, institutional event documentation, and animation-supported promotional content for General Secretariat events such as EP CuP. The unit strategically orchestrates major internal communication events and campaigns—including the 'EP Management Dialogues'—through the development of distinctive visual identities, custom narrative videography, and promotional materials that reinforce the Parliament's strategic execution frameworks and inter-institutional collaborative projects including ESPAS. Complementing these primary creative functions, SG-75 delivers specialized technical production services encompassing comprehensive sound design with in-house music composition and voice-over recording capabilities, professional photography services (both studio and on-location), and custom fabrication through Fab-Lab facilities to create campaign-specific visual identity materials and promotional objects such as signage. Finally, the unit contributes to strengthening the Parliament's strategic communication infrastructure by participating in cross-organizational working groups focused on internal communications strategy and inter-institutional coordination mechanisms.

##
SG-75.refined_activity_summary:
• **External and Member-Facing Multimedia Production** (35%): Creation of innovative, high-impact multi- and cross-media communications products for external audiences and Members of the European Parliament, including specialized educational video tutorials for MEPs and documentary-format institutional narratives featuring parliamentary figures through the 'Oral History' series

• **Internal Institutional Communications and Staff Engagement** (35%): Development of sophisticated creative staging videos and promotional multimedia content for inter-Directorate General audiences and internal stakeholder engagement, including General Secretariat institutional events (EP CuP), staff wellness initiatives, and advanced animation-supported promotional materials designed to maximize engagement across diverse internal and external audiences

• **Strategic Event Staging and Campaign Visual Identity Leadership** (20%): Primary responsibility for conceptualizing and executing distinctive visual identities, custom narrative videography, and promotional films for critical internal communication events and campaigns (including 'EP Management Dialogues'); creation of materials supporting strategic execution frameworks, inter-DG projects, and inter-institutional initiatives such as ESPAS

• **Technical Production and Specialized Services** (9%): Delivery of comprehensive technical production capabilities including sound design with in-house music composition and voice-over recording; professional photography services (studio and on-location); and Fab-Lab custom fabrication of campaign-specific visual identity objects, signage, and branded promotional materials

• **Strategic Communications Governance and Institutional Coordination** (1%): Participation in cross-organizational working groups and strategic initiatives focused on strengthening the Parliament's internal communications strategy, inter-institutional coordination mechanisms, and overall strategic communication framework

SH.name: SH
original_activities: Ensure liaison between the General Secretariat and the political groups.

Assist the Secretary General in the allocation to political groups of certain technical facilities such as offices and other work spaces, meeting rooms, etc., as well as providing support in administrative liaison with MPs and groups.
Ensure liaison between the administration and political groups with regard to the establishment and constitution of official intergroups in accordance with the regulations in force.
Assist the Secretary General in organizing a welcome service for new Members.

Ensure liaison between the administration and political groups in the organization and monitoring of internal 'bridge' competitions provided for in art. 29(3) of the Staff Regulations of Officials of the European Union.

Ensure liaison between the administration and political groups on important action plans in several areas, for example gender parity, diversity and the fight against discrimination, EMAS objectives, etc.
SH.refined_description: The Secretariat of the Groups (SH) is a strategic Directorate-General-level organizational unit operating under the direct authority of the Secretary General of the European Parliament, serving as the primary institutional liaison mechanism between the General Secretariat's administrative apparatus and the Parliament's recognized political groups. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structure, SH fulfills a critical coordinating function that ensures seamless operational integration across the institution's political structures while advancing multiple institutional priorities. The unit's core mission encompasses five major interconnected operational domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation: establishing and maintaining comprehensive liaison between the General Secretariat and political groups (35%); managing the allocation and administration of technical facilities including offices, workspaces, and meeting rooms while providing administrative support to Members and groups, assisting the Secretary General in this allocation function (30%); facilitating the establishment and constitutional recognition of official intergroups in accordance with applicable regulatory frameworks through administrative-political group coordination (10%); organizing and coordinating welcome services and integration support for newly elected Members while facilitating liaison in the organization and monitoring of internal "bridge" competitions outlined in Article 29(3) of the Staff Regulations of Officials of the European Union in coordination with political groups (10%); and serving as the institutional liaison point for cross-cutting strategic initiatives including gender parity objectives, diversity and anti-discrimination action plans, and environmental management objectives such as EMAS compliance (5%). Collectively, these activities ensure that political groups operate within a well-coordinated administrative environment while enabling effective interaction between the Parliament's administrative infrastructure and its political constituency across multiple policy dimensions.
SH.refined_activity_summary:
• **Political Group Liaison & Coordination** (35%): Establish and maintain comprehensive liaison between the General Secretariat and political groups, serving as the primary institutional interface for administrative and organizational coordination between Parliament's administrative infrastructure and political constituency.

• **Facilities & Administrative Support** (30%): Manage allocation of technical facilities (offices, workspaces, meeting rooms) to political groups and provide direct administrative liaison support to Members and parliamentary groups, assisting the Secretary General in this allocation function.

• **Intergroup Affairs & Member Integration Services** (20%): Facilitate establishment and constitutional recognition of official intergroups in accordance with regulatory requirements through administrative-political group coordination (10%); organize and coordinate comprehensive welcome services and integration support for newly elected Members (10%).

• **Staff Career Administration & Procedures** (10%): Facilitate liaison in the organization and monitoring of internal "bridge" competitions as provided in Article 29(3) of the Staff Regulations of Officials of the European Union in coordination with political groups.

• **Strategic Institutional Initiatives** (5%): Provide liaison support for cross-cutting action plans including gender parity, diversity, anti-discrimination objectives, EMAS environmental management compliance, and related institutional policies.

original_activities: Organize the activities of the General Directorate and ensure the achievement of its strategic objectives to support the legislative, budgetary and control functions of Parliament, while ensuring quality multilingual communication with citizens through high-end translation services.

Ensure the implementation of the strategic projects of the General Directorate of Translation. This portfolio of projects supports Parliament's core activities and strategic priorities through initiatives focused on translation, plain language, accessibility and technology, including artificial intelligence.
Ensure communication with staff, managers, the delegation of translators and the delegation of assistants.
Manage and monitor specific files: annual and intermediate activity reports, discharge, audit, etc.
Perform the functions of delegated authorizing officer.
Represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament, in internal meetings and in inter-general management relations.
Represent and/or have the general management represented in joint committees, interinstitutional forums and promote relations with citizens: cooperation with universities and other events.
Ensure the management of the personnel policy of the general management, as well as the rating procedure and other missions.
07.refined_description: Unit 07, the strategic apex of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Translation, operates as the executive governance hub reporting directly to the Secretary General. This organizational unit serves as the primary institutional mechanism for orchestrating all General Directorate activities and ensuring achievement of strategic objectives that underpin Parliament's legislative, budgetary, and control functions through excellence in multilingual translation services and citizen communication. Unit 07 directs the implementation of the General Directorate's transformative strategic project portfolio—encompassing translation services, plain language accessibility initiatives, technological innovation, and artificial intelligence applications—that directly anchor Parliament's core legislative activities and organizational priorities. The unit maintains comprehensive internal and external stakeholder communication infrastructure with personnel, management structures, translator delegations, and assistant delegations, while simultaneously fulfilling critical administrative stewardship responsibilities including annual and intermediate activity reporting, discharge procedures, audit compliance, and specialized file management. Unit 07 exercises delegated authorizing officer authority for budgetary and financial governance, represents general management across Parliament's internal governance bodies, inter-directorate forums, and interinstitutional relationships, and functions as the primary interface for external institutional engagement through joint committees, interinstitutional forums, university partnerships, and citizen engagement initiatives. The unit further ensures comprehensive personnel policy management, performance rating procedures, and human resources functions for the directorate. Through its eight subordinate specialized units (07-10: Strategic Direction and Innovation Office; 07-20: Strategic Language Services and Enlargement Planning; 07-30: Strategic Management and Communications; 07-40: Quality Assurance and Standards; 07A, 07B, and two additional specialized governance offices), Unit 07 coordinates integrated institutional operations that sustain Parliament's translation capacity and institutional effectiveness.
07.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Leadership & Governance** (25%): Orchestration of General Directorate activities and achievement of strategic objectives supporting Parliament's legislative, budgetary, and control functions through quality multilingual translation services and high-end citizen communication

• **Strategic Project Implementation & Innovation** (15%): Implementation and direction of the General Directorate's strategic project portfolio encompassing translation services, plain language accessibility, technological innovation, and artificial intelligence initiatives aligned with Parliament's core activities and institutional priorities

• **Stakeholder Communication & Coordination** (13%): Comprehensive communication management and continuous coordination infrastructure with internal staff, management structures, translator delegations, and assistant delegations across organizational hierarchy to ensure alignment and operational coherence

• **Administrative & Financial Stewardship** (20%): Management of institutional documentation including annual and intermediate activity reports, discharge procedures, audit compliance, and specialized file management; exercise of delegated authorizing officer authority for budgetary and financial governance

• **Institutional Representation & External Relations** (20%): Representation of general management within Parliament's governance bodies, inter-directorate forums, internal coordination meetings, joint committees, and interinstitutional frameworks; coordination of external stakeholder relationships including university partnerships and citizen engagement initiatives

• **Personnel Management & Human Resources** (7%): Comprehensive personnel policy administration for the directorate, including performance rating procedures and supplementary human resources functions

original_activities: Provide strategic and innovative input to the work of the Directorate-General, including to the Annual Work Programme, the Action Plan on Administrative Simplification and the Annual Activity Report.
Conceive, implement, monitor, review and evaluate the Directorate-General's strategic projects included in the Annual Work Programme and the Action Plan on Administrative Simplification.
Identify innovative working methods, and streamline and coordinate effective innovation in the Directorate-General for Translation.

Work on strengthening cooperation on strategic issues with external partners, including coordinating the work on a new Code of Conduct on Multilingualism and a renewed agreement between DG TRAD and DG COMM.

Organise and follow interinstitutional issues and relations with international organisations, including co-organisation of the 2026 Annual Meeting of AMLADP, an international forum and network of managers of international organizations employing conference and language service providers.
Manage the risk register and monitor business continuity.
07-10.refined_description:
Unit 07-10 operates as the Strategic Governance and Innovation Directorate within the General Directorate of Translation (DG TRAD), functioning as a first-level direction-level entity reporting to Unit 07, which maintains direct accountability to the Secretary General. As a leaf-node unit with no subordinate organizational structures, Unit 07-10 serves as the strategic nerve center of DG TRAD, providing executive-level strategic guidance and operational coordination across the organization while maintaining lateral coordination with sibling units (07-20, 07-30, 07-40, 07A, 07B, and additional peer units) to ensure organizational coherence and strategic alignment. The unit's primary mission encompasses formulating and executing strategic organizational initiatives, managing the complete lifecycle of strategic projects, advancing organizational innovation, managing external institutional partnerships, and ensuring organizational resilience through risk management and business continuity monitoring. Unit 07-10's workload allocation reflects its multifaceted strategic responsibilities: 25% is dedicated to providing strategic and innovative input to the Directorate-General's foundational planning instruments (Annual Work Programme, Action Plan on Administrative Simplification, and Annual Activity Report), thereby establishing the intellectual and policy framework for organizational operations; 20% is allocated to conceiving, implementing, monitoring, reviewing, and evaluating strategic projects embedded within these planning frameworks; 20% focuses on identifying innovative working methodologies and coordinating the streamlining and advancement of innovation initiatives across DG TRAD's operations; 15% addresses strengthening strategic cooperation with external partners through coordination of the new Code of Conduct on Multilingualism and negotiation of renewed inter-directorate agreements between DG TRAD and DG COMM; 10% encompasses organizing interinstitutional relations and international organization engagement, including co-organization of the 2026 Annual Meeting of AMLADP—a prominent international forum and network of senior managers from international organizations employing conference and language service providers; and the remaining 10% is allocated to risk register administration and business continuity monitoring to ensure organizational resilience and operational sustainability.

---

##
07-10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Planning, Guidance, and Programme Development** (25%): Provision of strategic and innovative input to the Directorate-General's foundational governing instruments—the Annual Work Programme, Action Plan on Administrative Simplification, and Annual Activity Report—establishing organizational direction and policy priorities; conception, implementation, monitoring, review, and evaluation of all strategic projects and administrative simplification initiatives embedded within these planning frameworks.

• **Innovation and Operational Modernization** (20%): Identification and operationalization of innovative working methodologies; streamlining and coordination of innovation initiatives across DG TRAD to enhance organizational effectiveness and operational efficiency.

• **External Strategic Partnership Development and Institutional Cooperation** (25%): Strengthening strategic cooperation with external partners through coordination of the Code of Conduct on Multilingualism development and negotiation of renewed interinstitutional agreements between DG TRAD and DG COMM (15%); organization and management of interinstitutional relations and international organization engagement, including co-organization of the 2026 Annual Meeting of AMLADP, an international forum and network of senior managers from international organizations employing conference and language service providers (10%).

• **Organizational Resilience, Risk Management, and Business Continuity** (10%): Administration of the organizational risk register; monitoring and maintenance of business continuity protocols and crisis management frameworks to ensure operational sustainability and organizational resilience.

original_activities: Ensure high-quality language services into enlargement and third-country languages according to Parliament's needs and taking into account available resources, with a particular attention to enlargement languages and non-EU languages of the United Nations.
Build on internal capacities for language services and quality control in the strategic enlargement and third-country languages, in particular for Arabic, Chinese, Russian and Ukrainian.
Monitor the most strategically needed languages in order to prepare the Directorate-General for Translation for future enlargements, in particular for the languages of the Western Balkans countries.

Develop contacts with international partners, such as the United Nations, and universities in enlargement and key third countries with the aim of training language professionals who meet Parliament's standards.
Look for innovative solutions for work organisation of language services into non-EU languages.
Contribute to Parliament's outreach activities and organise workshops, targeting audiences both within and outside the European Union in languages other than official European Union languages.
07-20.refined_description: Unit 07-20 operates as the Strategic Language Services and Enlargement Planning directorate within the Directorate General for Translation, functioning as a specialized first-level directional unit reporting to parent Unit 07 and coordinating alongside sibling units 07-10, 07-30, 07-40, 07A, and 07B. The unit bears primary institutional responsibility for ensuring the European Parliament maintains high-quality language service delivery into enlargement and third-country languages, with particular emphasis on non-EU languages recognized by the United Nations, while simultaneously building robust internal organizational capacities and quality control mechanisms in strategically critical languages including Arabic, Chinese, Russian, and Ukrainian. Unit 07-20 proactively monitors linguistically strategic languages necessary for preparing the Directorate-General for Translation to meet future institutional enlargements, with focused attention on Western Balkans country languages and geopolitical developments affecting language service requirements. The unit cultivates and develops strategic international partnerships with organizations such as the United Nations and academic institutions across enlargement and key third countries to identify, recruit, develop, and train language professionals meeting Parliament's rigorous professional standards. Additionally, Unit 07-20 identifies and implements innovative organizational solutions for structuring and delivering language services into non-EU languages while contributing substantively to Parliament's external engagement and outreach mandate by organizing and delivering educational workshops and cultural activities in non-official EU languages for audiences both within and outside the European Union. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 07-20 ensures the Parliament's linguistic capabilities remain responsive to institutional expansion scenarios, international engagement objectives, and evolving institutional language needs.
07-20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Quality Assurance and Service Delivery** (25%): Ensure high-quality language services into enlargement and third-country languages according to Parliament's needs and available resources, with particular emphasis on enlargement languages and non-UN official languages.

• **Internal Capacity Building and Quality Control Development** (20%): Build internal organizational capacities and quality control mechanisms in strategically critical languages, specifically Arabic, Chinese, Russian, and Ukrainian.

• **Strategic Language Monitoring and Institutional Preparedness** (20%): Monitor strategically essential languages to prepare the Directorate-General for Translation for future enlargements, particularly languages of Western Balkans countries and languages required for geopolitical responsiveness.

• **External Partnerships and Professional Training Networks** (15%): Develop and maintain strategic contacts with international partners including the United Nations and universities in enlargement and key third countries to identify, recruit, and train language professionals meeting Parliament's professional standards.

• **Innovation, Outreach, and External Engagement** (20%): Identify and implement innovative organizational solutions for structuring language services into non-EU languages (10%) and contribute to Parliament's external engagement mandate by organizing and delivering educational workshops and cultural activities targeting audiences within and outside the European Union in non-official EU languages (10%).

original_activities: Manage performance indicators (qualitative and quantitative indicators) of general management, develop statistical reports, manage general management metrics.
Coordinate the internal and external communications of the general management, including the general management's websites and social media, and provide strategic advice to the Director General.
Organize, coordinate and lead visits and events to promote multilingualism and support various awareness-raising activities.
Collaborate with universities and colleges offering linguistic and translation studies.
Other missions.
07-30.refined_description:
**

Unit 07-30 operates as the strategic performance and communications directorate within the General Directorate of Translation, functioning as a Direction-level unit under parent unit 07, which reports directly to the Secretary General. The unit's primary mission encompasses four interconnected operational domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload allocation, functioning as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while maintaining coordination with sibling units (07-10, 07-20, 07-40, 07A, 07B, and additional units) to ensure coherent execution of the General Directorate's comprehensive mandate. Unit 07-30 translates organizational strategy into measurable outcomes by developing and managing comprehensive performance indicators—both qualitative and quantitative metrics—pertaining to general management operations, with particular emphasis on statistical reporting and metric management systems that inform evidence-based decision-making and organizational accountability. The unit coordinates all integrated internal and external communications infrastructure for general management, including digital presence management across institutional websites and social media platforms, while simultaneously providing strategic advisory services directly to the Director General. Additionally, Unit 07-30 organizes, coordinates, and leads institutional visits and high-impact events specifically designed to promote multilingualism and support awareness-raising initiatives across the organization's stakeholder community, thereby advancing institutional visibility and community engagement. The unit maintains collaborative partnerships with universities and colleges offering linguistic and translation studies programs, fostering institutional knowledge networks, academic engagement, and talent pipeline development essential to the sector. These activities collectively support the overarching institutional objectives of the General Directorate of Translation while maintaining organizational flexibility through discretionary capacity allocation.

**
07-30.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Performance Management & Institutional Analytics (30%):** Development and management of qualitative and quantitative performance indicators for general management operations; production of statistical reports; administration and monitoring of general management metrics systems to support evidence-based decision-making and organizational accountability

• **Strategic Communications & Executive Advisory (25%):** Coordination of integrated internal and external communications strategies for general management; stewardship of digital platforms including institutional websites and social media channels; provision of strategic counsel and advisory services to the Director General

• **Multilingualism Promotion & Events Leadership (25%):** Organization, coordination, and leadership of institutional visits and high-impact promotional events; support and implementation of multilingualism awareness-raising activities; facilitation of stakeholder engagement and community engagement initiatives across European institutional contexts

• **Academic & Institutional Partnership Development (15%):** Collaboration with universities and colleges offering specialized linguistic and translation studies programs; cultivation and maintenance of institutional relationships within the higher education sector; support for talent development and academic networking initiatives essential to the sector

• **Discretionary Organizational Missions (5%):** Execution of additional tasks, ad-hoc support functions, and missions as assigned by organizational leadership to support broader organizational objectives

original_activities: Handle and monitor questions and requests related to the quality of originals and translations by ensuring the link between the general translation department and the requesting services.

Develop, implement and coordinate the quality policy of the general management (among other things, prepare guidelines and reports, ensure project management in the field of quality, organize meetings of quality coordinators, provide advice on quality-related topics).
Monitor developments and participate in discussions relating to quality at interinstitutional (interinstitutional quality network) and international (relevant ISO committee) levels.
Establish and prepare statistics in the field of quality (based in particular on regularly carried out quality controls and customer observations).
Process correction requests relating to translations already carried out.
Other missions.
07-40.refined_description: Unit 07-40, the Quality Assurance and Standards Directorate, operates as a first-level directorate within the General Directorate of Translation (Unit 07), reporting through the institutional hierarchy to the Secretary General. Functioning as the central institutional guardian of translation quality standards, Unit 07-40 serves as the critical operational and strategic nexus bridging general translation services with requesting institutional departments while advancing comprehensive quality governance frameworks across all European Parliament translation operations. The unit's integrated mission encompasses five interconnected operational domains: establishing and maintaining the institutional quality liaison interface; developing and implementing normative quality policy frameworks aligned with internal standards and international requirements; monitoring developments and actively participating in both interinstitutional quality networks and international ISO standardization initiatives; generating evidence-based quality metrics through systematic statistical analysis of quality control assessments and customer observations; and processing post-delivery quality remediation requests for previously completed translations. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 07-40 ensures systematic quality governance, institutional standardization compliance, continuous improvement mechanisms, and strategic operational alignment with sibling units (07-10, 07-20, 07-30, 07A, 07B, and others) within the General Directorate of Translation, thereby safeguarding translation excellence across the Parliament's multilingual operations while maintaining compliance with both internal protocols and international quality frameworks.
07-40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Quality Assurance and Client Interface Management** (40%): Handle and monitor inquiries regarding the quality of original documents and translations; establish and maintain the essential operational link between the general translation department and requesting institutional services; serve as the critical liaison mechanism ensuring bidirectional communication on quality-related matters.

• **Quality Policy Development and Institutional Governance** (35%): Develop, implement, and coordinate comprehensive quality policy frameworks for general management; prepare normative guidelines and analytical reports; execute project management responsibilities in quality-related initiatives; organize and facilitate quality coordinator networks and meetings; provide specialized advisory services on quality-related topics and institutional standards.

• **External Standards and Interinstitutional Engagement** (10%): Monitor developments and actively participate in quality-related discussions at interinstitutional levels through engagement with the interinstitutional quality network; participate in international quality standardization initiatives through relevant ISO committee membership and collaborative engagement.

• **Quality Measurement and Performance Analytics** (7%): Establish and prepare comprehensive statistical analyses in the quality domain; analyze data derived from regularly conducted quality control assessments and documented customer observations to support evidence-based decision-making and continuous improvement.

• **Post-Delivery Quality Remediation and Miscellaneous Operations** (8%): Process and manage correction requests relating to translations already carried out; execute other assigned institutional missions and ad hoc responsibilities as required by organizational priorities and strategic directives.

07A.name: 07A
original_activities: Establish, jointly with the units, the development strategy for each of the Department's sectors, following the strategic priorities of General Management; monitor the implementation of these strategies; hold regular meetings with each unit, as well as weekly management meetings.

Establish the IT plan for the general translation department; pursue an effective method for the identification and application of new technologies, including in the field of artificial intelligence, and provide the departments responsible for purchasing with the necessary support to acquire them.

Ensure business analysis activities within the general translation department by bringing together operational needs and strategic priorities, on the one hand, and technological solutions, on the other.
Monitor contracts for external translation services obtained through tender procedures.
Manage the COCOM (IT Coordination Committee DG TRAD - DG ITEC).
Perform the functions of subdelegated authorizing officer.
Participate and, where appropriate, represent the general management in the bodies of the European Parliament, etc.
Other missions.
07A.refined_description: Unit 07A functions as the Strategic Direction and Governance Office of the General Directorate of Translation, serving as the primary strategic planning, operational coordination, and innovation management hub positioned directly beneath parent Unit 07 within the organizational hierarchy. The unit exercises executive oversight across the translation department's operational portfolio by establishing and jointly developing comprehensive, sector-specific development strategies in alignment with General Management's strategic priorities, while simultaneously monitoring implementation fidelity through structured bilateral consultation meetings with its four subordinate units (07A10, 07A20, 07A30, and 07A40) and weekly management assemblies. Unit 07A maintains primary responsibility for formulating and executing the IT strategic plan for the General Translation Department, identifying and integrating emerging technological solutions—particularly artificial intelligence applications—and providing critical procurement support to acquisition-responsible departments, thereby positioning the unit as the strategic orchestrator for the department's technological advancement. The unit performs essential business analysis functions that bridge operational requirements with strategic objectives and technological capabilities, ensuring coherent organizational alignment between institutional needs and digital solutions. Additionally, Unit 07A oversees contract management for externally-procured translation services obtained through formal tender procedures, manages the IT Coordination Committee (COCOM) governing inter-directorate collaboration between DG TRAD and DG ITEC, exercises subdelegated authorizing officer functions within financial management protocols, represents General Management within European Parliament institutional governance bodies as required, and executes other assigned institutional missions. Through these integrated activities, Unit 07A serves as the strategic nerve center coordinating departmental resource optimization, technological advancement, and institutional integration across the translation function.
07A.refined_activity_summary:
• **Strategic Planning and Departmental Governance** (40%): Establish and jointly develop sector-specific development strategies with subordinate units (07A10, 07A20, 07A30, 07A40) aligned with General Management strategic priorities; monitor implementation fidelity through regular bilateral unit consultations and weekly management coordination assemblies; ensure organizational coherence across the translation department

• **Information Technology Strategy, Planning, and Innovation Management** (22%): Formulate and execute comprehensive IT strategic plans for the General Translation Department; identify, evaluate, and integrate emerging technological solutions including artificial intelligence applications; provide critical procurement support to acquisition-responsible departments for technology acquisition and implementation

• **Business Analysis and Technological Integration** (20%): Conduct comprehensive business analysis activities synthesizing operational requirements with strategic objectives and technological capabilities; ensure coherent alignment between departmental needs and system implementations; bridge institutional requirements with digital solutions

• **External Services and Contract Management** (10%): Monitor and oversee contracts for external translation services procured through formal tender procedures; ensure compliance and service quality standards

• **Governance, Coordination, and Administrative Functions** (8%): Manage IT Coordination Committee (COCOM) liaison between DG TRAD and DG ITEC, governing inter-directorate collaboration (2%); exercise subdelegated authorizing officer responsibilities within financial management protocols (2%); represent General Management within European Parliament institutional governance bodies as appropriate (2%); execute other assigned institutional missions (2%)

07A10.name: 07A10
original_activities: Manage applications in use - lifecycle management of custom or specialized software applications in use: evolutive maintenance, training & support, testing to ensure functionality and security.

Manage projects - planning, executing, and overseeing IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints, such as scope, time, budget, and quality: project planning, implementation, resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication.

Assist the Directorate-General and DG ITEC in analysing and designing the business processes in the Directorate-General for Translation from a functional and technical point of view and in developing the strategy for the future IT landscape, including the establishment and implementation of the IT plan and the implementation of the cloud first policy.
Represent the Directorate-General for Translation in internal or interinstitutional bodies related to its mandate.
