Follow the general translation technology market and participate in the interinstitutional technical cooperation fora in order to ensure that the Directorate-General for Translation has the best possible tools available.
Other missions.
07A10.refined_description: Unit 07A10, the Information Technology and Applications Management division within Directorate 07A (Strategic and Operational Management) of the Directorate-General for Translation, serves as the institution's primary custodian of information technology infrastructure and the custom and specialized software application portfolio. Operating as a leaf node with no subordinate units while coordinating strategically with sibling units 07A20, 07A30, and 07A40, Unit 07A10 fulfills a comprehensive mandate spanning operational technology stewardship, enterprise IT governance, and organizational digital transformation. The unit's workload allocation reflects its multifaceted responsibilities: maintaining and enhancing the operational application ecosystem through lifecycle management activities; directing the planning, execution, and governance of IT projects across the organization; collaborating with DG ITEC on business process analysis and IT strategy development to establish the future IT landscape and operationalize cloud-first policy directives; representing the Directorate-General's IT interests within internal and interinstitutional governance structures; and maintaining systematic market intelligence on translation technology solutions through active participation in interinstitutional technical cooperation forums. This integrated operational and strategic positioning enables Unit 07A10 to ensure continuous system functionality and security compliance while simultaneously advancing the organization's digital transformation objectives and institutional IT planning initiatives.
07A10.refined_activity_summary:
• **Application Lifecycle and Technical Operations Management** (60%): Comprehensive management of custom and specialized software applications in operational use throughout the organization, encompassing evolutive maintenance activities, end-user training and technical support service delivery, and rigorous functional and security-focused testing protocols to maintain system integrity, continuous functionality, and organizational productivity

• **IT Project and Program Governance Management** (25%): Strategic planning, implementation execution oversight, and full-spectrum governance of IT projects including resource allocation optimization, enterprise risk management, multi-stakeholder communication frameworks, and management within defined parameters of scope, timeline, budget, and quality standards

• **Strategic IT Planning and Digital Transformation Collaboration** (8%): Collaborative analysis and design of business processes from both functional and technical perspectives in partnership with the Directorate-General and DG ITEC; development of future IT landscape strategy; establishment and implementation of institutional IT plans; and operationalization of cloud-first policy initiatives and directives

• **Institutional Representation and Translation Technology Market Intelligence** (5%): Formal representation of the Directorate-General for Translation within internal and interinstitutional governance bodies aligned with organizational mandate (3%); systematic monitoring of translation technology market developments and active participation in interinstitutional technical cooperation forums to ensure the organization maintains access to optimal tools and solutions (2%)

• **Miscellaneous Organizational Missions** (2%): Execution of additional institutional duties and assignments as directed by parent directorate and organizational priorities

07A20.name: 07A20
original_activities: Manage translation requests entrusted to external contractors using the Iris application.
Perform the functions of authorizing officer by subdelegation for line 1420 - 'External translation services'.
Manage external translation contracts.
Participate in procurement procedures in the field of external translation.
Participate in carrying out studies regarding IT developments related to unit-specific applications.
07A20.refined_description: Unit 07A20 functions as the External Translation Services Management and Administration unit within Direction 07A of the General Directorate of Translation, serving as a critical operational hub that bridges the European Parliament's translation requirements with external contractor networks. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while coordinating with sibling units (07A10, 07A30, 07A40) under the strategic direction of parent Unit 07A, the unit executes a dual-focus operational model centered on request management and financial stewardship. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management of external translation service procurement, contract administration, budgetary oversight, and financial authorization, with integrated responsibilities spanning the complete lifecycle from request processing through vendor relationship management and compliance monitoring. Through its coordinated activities, Unit 07A20 ensures effective external translation service delivery to Parliament, maintains rigorous financial controls and institutional accountability, supports competitive procurement processes aligned with institutional regulations, and contributes to organizational modernization through participation in information technology development initiatives. The unit's operational structure and resource allocation reflect its critical role as an administrative and financial control mechanism within the broader institutional translation service delivery framework of the European Parliament.
07A20.refined_activity_summary:
• **External Translation Request and Service Administration** (30%): Management of translation requests assigned to external contractors through the Iris application platform, ensuring efficient request processing, contractor coordination, and timely service delivery

• **Financial Authorization and Budgetary Control** (30%): Exercise of authorizing officer functions by subdelegation for budget line 1420 ('External translation services'), encompassing financial authorization, commitment management, expenditure control, and budgetary accountability for all external translation service expenditures

• **Contract and Vendor Management** (20%): Comprehensive administration of the complete external translation contract lifecycle, including contract negotiation, performance monitoring, vendor relationship management, and contractual compliance oversight

• **Procurement and Competitive Procedures** (15%): Active participation in procurement procedures for external translation services, supporting competitive tendering processes, vendor evaluation, supplier selection activities, and adherence to institutional procurement regulations

• **Information Technology Systems Development** (5%): Participation in feasibility studies and initiatives concerning information technology developments, enhancements, and modernization of unit-specific operational applications and service delivery systems

07A30.name: 07A30
original_activities: Ensure monitoring of the automatic pre-processing of translation requests and specific pre-processing (session, references, special pre-processing, including consolidated texts, information sheets, budget, ITER titles); ensure the supply and maintenance of common translation memories in Euramis.

Maintain, develop and test SPA+, in collaboration with the Applications and IT Systems Development Unit (DAS); establish common workflows for the use of computer-assisted translation tools (CAT tools); write and update instructions and user guides for computer-assisted translation tools and computer applications.
Ensure technical verification of originals, including corrections, advice and tests.
Assist users by maintaining a Help Desk, including internal IT support.
Organize and participate in internal and external training, meetings and working groups, including inter-departmental and inter-institutional cooperation.

Participate in the development, testing and maintenance of IT applications of the General Directorate of Translation, other than SPA+ (CAT tools, Sirius, Fullcat+ etc.), as well as in the integration tests of inter-General Directorate projects/products (eLegislate etc.), in collaboration with the DAS Unit and users; participate in the development and testing of interinstitutional applications (Euramis).
Ensure the execution of tasks related to management and administration (human resources management, assistance to the head of unit, statistics, website maintenance, unit meetings).
Other missions.
07A30.refined_description: Unit 07A30, the Technical Translation Support and IT Systems Unit, functions as a critical operational support directorate within the General Directorate of Translation (parent unit 07A), positioned alongside sibling units 07A10, 07A20, and 07A40. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses the management, development, and maintenance of translation infrastructure systems and computer-assisted translation environments that enable the Parliament's multilingual translation operations. Unit 07A30 operates as the central hub for technical translation request processing, CAT tool development and support, quality assurance verification, user assistance, training coordination, and IT applications integration across the General Directorate and inter-institutional systems. The unit's work is characterized by sustained collaboration with the Applications and IT Systems Development Unit (DAS) on application development initiatives, direct engagement with end-user departments, and participation in both internal organizational governance and external inter-institutional working groups. Through its integrated technical, support, and administrative functions, Unit 07A30 ensures the operational continuity and technological advancement of the Parliament's translation capacity while maintaining rigorous quality standards and user capability development across all organizational levels.
07A30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Translation Request Processing and Infrastructure Management** (39%): Comprehensive monitoring of automatic and specialized pre-processing of translation requests including session management, reference handling, special pre-processing protocols, consolidated text processing, information sheet generation, budget documentation, and ITER title management; supply and maintenance of common translation memories within the Euramis platform

• **Computer-Assisted Translation Systems Development and Support** (25%): Development, testing, and maintenance of SPA+ application in direct collaboration with DAS Unit; establishment of standardized CAT tool workflows; creation and updating of comprehensive instructions and user guides for CAT tools and computational applications; participation in development, testing, and maintenance of specialized General Directorate IT applications (Sirius, Fullcat+, CAT tools); integration testing of inter-directorate projects (eLegislate) and inter-institutional applications (Euramis); Help Desk operations providing internal IT support to all organizational users

• **Technical Quality Assurance and Verification** (12%): Rigorous technical verification of original documents; provision of corrections and advisory support; execution of systematic testing protocols to ensure translation system and material integrity

• **Training, Coordination, and Organizational Development** (11%): Organization and active participation in internal and external training initiatives; facilitation of inter-departmental and inter-institutional working groups, meetings, and cooperation initiatives; enhancement of organizational capability through knowledge transfer and coordination activities

• **Administrative and Governance Functions** (6%): Human resources coordination; executive support to unit head; statistical analysis and reporting; website maintenance; unit meeting coordination; miscellaneous operational missions as required by organizational priorities

07A40.name: 07A40
original_activities: Create and manage terminology projects (term bases, projects for interns, terminology projects with universities, etc.) and coordinate work in IATE for all procedural units, as well as non-procedural units (user management, helpdesk, updates, cooperation with translation unit terminologists, Terminology Network).

Create and manage termbases as well as coordinate activities related to the use and development of terminology functionalities in translation tools (integration of termbases, user assistance, training, liaison with developers, participation in user groups, etc.).

Prepare and coordinate all work related to terminology tools and materials (TermSafe, Multiterm, Glossary Converter, Glossary Search Tool, Documentary Search Tool, Term Extraction, EP Establishment Plan, Codict, guides on IATE).

Cooperate with other institutions (IATE Management Group, IATE Handbook Task Force, Data Clean-up Task Force, IATE 2 Task Force, IATE-Studio Integration Working Group, Terminology Coordination Services).

Ensure the management of the “TermCoord” section of the internet domain europarl.europa.eu, including the regular updating of terminology resources in this same area intended for language professionals at the General Directorate for Translation and external contractors.
Organize seminars and workshops, prepare content and provide training related to IATE and other terminology topics.
Cooperate with services from other directorates-general upon request, prepare requested terminology material and provide training for MPs and drafters of legislative texts.
Develop and maintain relationships with universities and other stakeholders for IATE terminology projects and other missions.
07A40.refined_description: Unit 07A40, the Terminology Coordination and Management Centre within Direction 07A of the General Directorate for Translation, serves as the European Parliament's central institutional hub for comprehensive terminology governance and linguistic standardization across all parliamentary translation operations. Positioned as a specialized strategic operational unit, Unit 07A40 executes an integrated multifaceted mandate encompassing the creation, management, and coordination of terminology projects and resources; the development and maintenance of terminology functionalities within translation tools; the administration of specialized terminology tools and materials; inter-institutional cooperation through participation in multiple IATE-related task forces and working groups; the management of web-accessible terminology resources; and the delivery of training and cross-directorate support services. The unit's operational framework allocates resources across five primary functional domains: terminology project creation and IATE coordination (40%), which includes term bases, intern initiatives, university partnerships, user management, helpdesk operations, system updates, and Terminology Network liaison; terminology tools and technical infrastructure development (25%), encompassing termbase integration, translation tool functionality enhancement, user assistance, developer liaison, and administration of TermSafe, Multiterm, Glossary Converter, Glossary Search Tool, Documentary Search Tool, Term Extraction, EP Establishment Plan, Codict, and comprehensive IATE guides; inter-institutional cooperation and strategic partnerships (10%), including active participation in the IATE Management Group, IATE Handbook Task Force, Data Clean-up Task Force, IATE 2 Task Force, IATE-Studio Integration Working Group, and Terminology Coordination Services, alongside relationship development with universities and external stakeholders; web resource management (5%), encompassing regular updates to the TermCoord section of europarl.europa.eu for language professionals and external contractors; and training, capacity building, and cross-institutional support (10%), including seminar and workshop organization on IATE and terminology topics, terminology material preparation, and training delivery to other directorates-general, Members of Parliament, and legislative text drafters. Through these integrated activities, Unit 07A40 functions as the critical institutional infrastructure for terminology management, directly supporting the translation quality, linguistic consistency, and institutional communication objectives of Direction 07A and the broader General Directorate for Translation.
07A40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Terminology Project Creation and IATE Coordination** (40%): Creates and manages comprehensive terminology projects including term bases, intern initiatives, university-based terminology partnerships, and coordinates all IATE activities across procedural and non-procedural units, encompassing user management, helpdesk support, system updates, collaboration with translation unit terminologists, and Terminology Network liaison.

• **Terminology Tools and Technical Infrastructure** (25%): Develops and maintains terminology functionalities within translation tools (15%)—including termbase integration, functionality enhancement, user assistance, training delivery, developer liaison, and specialized user group participation—while administering specialized terminology tools and materials (10%) including TermSafe, Multiterm, Glossary Converter, Glossary Search Tool, Documentary Search Tool, Term Extraction utilities, EP Establishment Plan resources, Codict, and authoritative IATE guides.

• **Inter-Institutional Cooperation and Strategic Partnerships** (10%): Manages relationships with external institutions and internal stakeholders through active participation in the IATE Management Group, IATE Handbook Task Force, Data Clean-up Task Force, IATE 2 Task Force, IATE-Studio Integration Working Group, and Terminology Coordination Services, while developing and maintaining strategic relationships with universities and external stakeholders to advance IATE terminology projects and institutional missions.

• **Web Resource Management and Content Provision** (5%): Manages and regularly updates the TermCoord section of europarl.europa.eu, ensuring that terminology resources remain current and accessible to language professionals within the General Directorate for Translation and external contractors.

• **Training, Capacity Building, and Cross-Institutional Support** (10%): Organizes and delivers seminars, workshops, and specialized training on IATE and terminology topics for internal staff (5%), while providing terminology material preparation and training support to other directorates-general, Members of Parliament, and legislative text drafters upon request (5%).

07B.name: 07B
original_activities: Identify management's human, financial and IT resource needs and ensure their optimal use and development; coordinate staff career evaluation and monitoring procedures.

Define the objectives of Directorate B, based on the projects of the general management and jointly with the units, and implement them. To do this, ensure planning of the management's activities and its new missions, manage change in management, monitor the harmonization of working methods within the units, optimize the efficiency of the internal and external translation process, and actively contribute to the development of IT tools.

Ensure the direction and coordination of the units of Directorate B. Ensure that the missions of the 24 linguistic units, as well as those of Planning, are fulfilled. Ensure the high quality translation of documents, within the required deadlines and in compliance with the rules in force. Integrate new job profiles within management and promote clear language in language services.
Participate in committees or intra- and inter-general directorate working groups, in interinstitutional forums, etc.

Ensure coordination with the three other directorates regarding matters falling within their respective competences and having implications in Directorate B and guarantee smooth and efficient sharing of internal linguistic resources with Directorate D.

Ensure cooperation and communication within management and with requesting services and end users; provide support and advice in the area of ​​competence to the Director General and his hierarchy, as well as to the Members.
07B.refined_description: Unit 07B operates as the strategic Direction within the General Directorate of Translation, functioning as the primary governance, managerial, and coordinative nexus responsible for overseeing the comprehensive linguistic and translation services infrastructure of the European Parliament. The unit serves as the essential operational hub coordinating 24 linguistic units and the Planning function of Directorate B, while maintaining coordinated relationships with sibling units 07A, 07-10, 07-20, 07-30, and 07-40 within the parent Unit 07 structure. Unit 07B's core mission encompasses six integrated functional domains that collectively constitute 100% of documented workload, positioning it as the critical interface between institutional strategic priorities and subordinate unit implementation. The unit's activities span human resource optimization and organizational development, strategic planning and change management aligned with general management directives, directional leadership and quality assurance across all Directorate B operations, inter-institutional engagement and external representation, cross-directorate coordination and resource-sharing arrangements, and internal/external communication and advisory support. Through these integrated activities, Unit 07B ensures mission fulfillment across its 25 child units, guarantees high-quality document translation delivery within prescribed deadlines and regulatory compliance, harmonizes working methodologies and translation processes across the directorate, and actively contributes to IT tool development and institutional capacity-building initiatives while providing specialized advisory support to the Director General hierarchy and parliamentary Members.
07B.refined_activity_summary:
• **Human Resources, Financial Management & Organizational Development** (30%): Identification and optimization of management's human, financial, and IT resource requirements; coordination of comprehensive staff career evaluation and monitoring procedures; support for institutional capacity-building and personnel development initiatives.

• **Strategic Planning, Change Management & Process Optimization** (28%): Definition and implementation of Directorate B's strategic objectives aligned with general management priorities and subordinate unit input; comprehensive activity and mission planning; coordination of organizational change initiatives; harmonization of working methodologies across all 24 linguistic units and Planning function; optimization of internal and external translation processes; and active contribution to IT tool development.

• **Directional Leadership, Quality Assurance & Service Delivery** (20%): Executive direction and coordination of 24 linguistic units and Planning function; oversight of mission fulfillment and high-quality document translation delivery within required deadlines and regulatory compliance; integration of emerging professional profiles within management structures; and promotion of clear language standards throughout language services.

• **Inter-institutional Engagement & Cross-Directorate Coordination** (16%): Participation in departmental committees, intra- and inter-directorate working groups, and interinstitutional forums (10%); seamless alignment and liaison with three sibling directorates regarding matters with Directorate B implications; and management of efficient linguistic resource-sharing arrangements with Directorate D (6%).

• **Internal and External Communication & Advisory Support** (6%): Facilitation of cooperation and communication within management structures and with requesting services and end-users; provision of specialized advisory support to the Director General hierarchy and parliamentary Members within areas of competence.

07B11.refined_description:
**

Unit 07B11 functions as a specialized translation execution and quality assurance unit operating at the second hierarchical level within Direction 07B of the General Directorate of Translation, serving as one of 24 language-specific operational units coordinating multilingual institutional translation services across the European Parliament. As a leaf-node operational execution center without subordinate units, 07B11 maintains primary responsibility for comprehensive translation workflows encompassing the full cycle from initial translation through quality assurance and finalization, leveraging the specialized linguistic and technical competencies of its multilingual professional staff. The unit's workload distribution reflects a strategic balance between core translation production (61%), complementary translation finalization and quality assurance functions (22%), and specialized institutional services including real-time translation of Official Journal (OJ) and Plenary Verbatim (PV) documents alongside transcription of Committee Room Edition (CRE) records during parliamentary sessions in both Strasbourg and Brussels venues (6%). Beyond core translation activities, Unit 07B11 contributes to the European Parliament's institutional terminology infrastructure through systematic population and maintenance of the IATE (Inter-Active Terminology for Europe) terminology database (3%), while coordinating translation circuits and managing workload distribution oversight (4%), participating in thematic group activities and correspondent network functions (2%), and executing supplementary linguistic tasks including audio capacity work and administrative responsibilities such as competition jury participation and working group engagement (2%). Positioned within the broader Direction 07B coordination framework encompassing 24 sibling units (07B12 through 07B35), Unit 07B11 collaborates across the directorate's integrated structure to ensure comprehensive multilingual accessibility of parliamentary documents and proceedings while maintaining standardized institutional translation quality and terminology standards.

---

**
07B11.refined_activity_summary:

• **Translation Finalization and Quality Assurance** (22%): Preparation and finalization of translations by translation assistants (11%) combined with systematic revision, proofreading, and quality control evaluation of translation outputs (11%), ensuring institutional standards compliance and translation circuit management with workload distribution oversight (4%)

• **Specialized Institutional Documentation Services** (6%): Real-time translation and transcription of Official Journal (OJ), Plenary Verbatim records (PV), and Committee Room Edition (CRE) during parliamentary sessions conducted in Strasbourg and Brussels locations

• **Terminology Management and Database Development** (3%): Execution of terminological tasks with particular emphasis on populating and maintaining the IATE (Inter-Active Terminology for Europe) terminology database to support standardized institutional language usage across parliamentary operations

• **Collaborative, Coordination, and Supplementary Linguistic Functions** (8%): Participation in thematic group activities and correspondent network coordination (2%), execution of supplementary linguistic services including audio capacity work (2%), and administrative responsibilities including competition jury participation and institutional working group engagement (2%)

The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive management of translation workflows through the allocation, coordination, and linguistic processing of texts by professional translators and translation assistants (70% of workload), supported by the preparation and finalization of translated materials by translation assistants (12%) and the critical quality assurance function of revising, proofreading, and evaluating translation outputs to maintain institutional standards (9%). The unit further fulfills institutional coordination functions through the execution of thematic group responsibilities and maintenance of correspondent networks to support coordinated translation policy and knowledge-sharing across the directorate (2% of workload), complemented by ancillary administrative and linguistic support tasks including competition jury participation, recruitment coordination, internal training delivery, intern selection and monitoring, and external representation functions (2% of workload). Additionally, the unit commits 1% of its workload to terminological development and knowledge management, specifically the systematic population and maintenance of the IATE (Inter-Active Terminology for Europe) terminology database. Collectively, these interconnected functions position Unit 07B31 as a critical operational component contributing to the Parliament's multilingual institutional capacity while maintaining operational independence and professional standards.

07B39.refined_description: Unit 07B39 functions as the central coordination and oversight hub within Direction 07B of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Translation, operating at the second hierarchical level beneath the Directorate-General and serving as the essential operational and strategic interface between the Parliament's diverse translation demands and institutional capacity. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive coordination of translation needs across all legislative and administrative documents of the European Parliament, requiring continuous systematic monitoring of the entire translation workflow to assess current resource utilization and strategically plan for future capacity demands across all subordinate translation units (80% of workload). In its strategic advisory capacity, Unit 07B39 identifies and analyzes the specific characteristics and complexities of incoming translation requests, escalating these findings to both the Directorate for Translation and the broader Directorate-General level to ensure that emerging translation challenges receive appropriate strategic attention and institutional response (7% of workload). The unit maintains operational continuity through the administrative coordination of its three constituent services and the execution of comprehensive unit-level management functions and administrative governance (5% of workload), while simultaneously providing specialized IT coordination for the GEPRO+ translation request management tool and actively contributing to the development and enhancement of information technology solutions that strengthen translation service delivery (5% of workload). Additionally, Unit 07B39 maintains institutional cooperation and dialogue with the translation services of other European Institutions to ensure consistency and efficiency across the European institutional framework (2% of workload), with minor residual functions comprising the remaining 1% of documented activities.
07B39.refined_activity_summary:
• **Translation Process Coordination & Workload Management** (80%): Coordinate translation needs for all European Parliament legislative and administrative documents; systematically monitor the entire translation process; assess current resource utilization and translation unit capacity; strategically plan future workload distribution and resource allocation across all subordinate translation units

• **Strategic Escalation & Institutional Liaison** (7%): Identify and analyze specificities and distinctive characteristics of incoming translation requests; escalate findings to the Directorate for Translation and Directorate-General for strategic-level decision-making and institutional follow-up

• **Operational Management & Administrative Governance** (5%): Coordinate the work of the unit's three constituent services; provide comprehensive administrative management and operational oversight of Unit 07B39

• **Information Technology Coordination & Systems Development** (5%): Provide IT coordination for the GEPRO+ translation request management tool; suggest and actively contribute to IT developments and system enhancements in translation-related information technology infrastructure

• **Inter-Institutional Cooperation & Miscellaneous Activities** (3%): Cooperate with and maintain institutional dialogue with translation services of other European Institutions; execute miscellaneous other functions

07C.refined_description: Unit 07C, designated as the Directorate for Resources within the Directorate-General for Translation, functions as the principal administrative and operational backbone providing integrated support infrastructure across the entire organization's human capital, financial operations, information technology systems, and strategic resource management. The unit exercises comprehensive responsibility across six interconnected functional domains, operationalized through four specialized child units (07C10 for Human Resources and Administrative Management, 07C20 for Financial Management and Budget Execution, 07C30 for Operational Support Services, and 07C40 for IT Support and Infrastructure Management) that collectively execute 27 distinct operational activities while maintaining lateral coordination with five sibling directorate units within the parent Unit 07 structure. Unit 07C delivers integrated support services to the Director-General, all Directorates, and the broader staff complement while simultaneously managing the strategic development of the Directorate for Resources' service portfolio in alignment with both the Directorate-General's institutional priorities and the European Parliament's overarching strategic directives. The unit maintains responsibility for comprehensive project management and horizontal activity coordination across the Directorate, ensuring integrated execution of cross-functional initiatives that span multiple operational areas. Unit 07C exercises delegated financial authority through authorizing officer responsibilities and maintains capacity for emerging short-term projects and ad hoc initiatives, while ensuring effective internal and external communication to keep Directorate-General leadership informed of developments within its competence areas and representing the unit's interests and expertise in inter-Directorate-General coordination and external institutional relationships.
07C.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Support Services and Administrative Assistance** (45%): Comprehensive provision of human resources management, financial administration, information technology support, professional development and training programs, traineeship coordination, and logistical operations to the Director-General, all Directorates, and staff members across the Directorate-General for Translation

• **Strategic Management and Directorate Governance** (25%): Management, coordination, and continuous development of the Directorate for Resources' service portfolio; establishment of strategic frameworks, organizational objectives, and comprehensive planning mechanisms; systematic monitoring of execution in alignment with Directorate-General and European Parliament strategic priorities

• **Project and Horizontal Activity Management** (20%): Comprehensive management of the Directorate's portfolio of projects and active coordination of horizontal cross-functional activities spanning multiple operational areas

• **Institutional Communication and External Representation** (5%): Internal communication to Directorate-General leadership regarding significant developments within the Directorate's competence areas; external representation of the Directorate's positions and interests to peer Directorates-General and external institutional stakeholders

• **Financial Authorization and Ad Hoc Operations** (5%): Execution of delegated authorizing officer responsibilities through sub-delegation arrangements (3%); coordination and execution of various short-term projects and emerging initiatives (2%)

Provide advice and assistance on all questions related to human resources and the application of the status of civil servants (establishment courses, updating job descriptions, photography of services, rating, missions, complaints, professional inadequacy, medical checks) and drafting of the necessary notes.

Other missions.
07C10.refined_description:
Unit 07C10 functions as the Human Resources and Administrative Management Unit within the Directorate for Resources (07C) of the Directorate-General for Translation, serving as a critical operational support function for institutional personnel and asset management across the entire General Directorate. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy and functioning alongside sibling units 07C20, 07C30, and 07C40 under parent Directorate 07C, the unit delivers integrated resource management services essential to the translation directorate's operational effectiveness. The unit's comprehensive mission encompasses strategic human resources administration, specialized advisory services on civil service statutory compliance, asset and data management operations, career development and staff mobility facilitation, institutional representation in cross-organizational forums, and miscellaneous support functions as assigned. Through its integrated service delivery model, Unit 07C10 directly supports the Directorate for Resources' mandate to provide comprehensive administrative and personnel management services while maintaining coordination with general management and senior leadership across internal and interinstitutional levels.

• **Human Resources Advisory Services and Civil Service Statutory Compliance** (25%): Specialized advisory and assistance services on all human resources matters and civil servant status application, including establishment course administration and coordination, job description development and updates, service photography documentation and records maintenance, performance rating procedures, official mission authorization, formal complaints processing, professional inadequacy determinations and assessments, medical examination coordination, and preparation of all supporting administrative notes and documentation.

• **Miscellaneous Institutional Support Functions** (1%): Execution of additional ad hoc assignments and institutional support functions as required by general management.

07C20.refined_description: Unit 07C20 functions as the Financial Management and Budget Execution Unit within the Directorate for Resources (07C) of the Directorate-General for Translation, serving as a critical operational and administrative center for institutional financial stewardship and budgetary oversight. The unit's integrated mission encompasses four complementary operational domains that collectively ensure comprehensive financial governance across the DG Translation organizational structure. As a leaf-node operational unit, Unit 07C20 operates collaboratively with sibling units (07C10, 07C30, and 07C40) under parent Directorate 07C to deliver integrated resource management services. The unit's primary responsibility—representing 75% of total workload—involves the execution of the institutional budget through financial initiation procedures and ex ante verification mechanisms, ensuring compliance with European Parliament financial regulations and expenditure controls while maintaining vendor accountability and procurement compliance. Supporting these core financial operations, the unit allocates 10% of capacity to the preparation, launch, monitoring, and execution of market procedures alongside comprehensive contract management activities that directly support institutional acquisition operations. Additionally, the unit dedicates 8% of its workload to the preparation and maintenance of comprehensive documentation and analytical files concerning financial and contractual management matters, including the production of periodic reports, annual reports, and specialized reporting submissions to external oversight bodies including DG TRAD, DG FINS, the Internal Auditor, the Court of Auditors, and the Central Financial Unit. The remaining 7% of resources are devoted to budgetary management operations, encompassing the preparation and continuous monitoring of annual budget allocations, the compilation of budgetary and accounting statistics for institutional planning and accountability, and the provision of analytical support for institutional forecasting purposes.
07C20.refined_activity_summary:
• Financial Execution and Verification (75%): Executes institutional budget through financial initiation procedures and ex ante verification mechanisms, ensuring compliance with European Parliament financial regulations, expenditure controls, and vendor accountability—representing the unit's primary operational focus and core contribution to institutional financial governance

• Procurement and Contract Administration (10%): Prepares, launches, monitors, and executes market procedures with comprehensive contract management oversight, ensuring procurement compliance and operational continuity across institutional acquisition activities

• Financial and Contractual Reporting and Documentation (8%): Prepares and maintains comprehensive documentation and analytical files for financial and contractual management, producing periodic reports, annual reports, and specialized submissions to DG TRAD, DG FINS, Internal Auditor, Court of Auditors, and Central Financial Unit for external oversight and accountability purposes

• Budgetary Planning, Monitoring, and Analysis (7%): Manages budgetary operations including annual budget preparation, continuous monitoring, compilation of budgetary and accounting statistics, and analytical support for institutional forecasting and accountability reporting
