Coordinate internships for the General Directorate of Translation.
Ensure the financial management of budget line 1612-05 for external training.
Liaise with the intern recruitment unit of the General Personnel Directorate.
Liaise with training managers from other directorates-general and the learning and development unit of the Directorate-General for Personnel.
07C30.refined_description: Unit 07C30, positioned as a Level 2 operational unit within Directorate 07C (Resources) of the Directorate-General for Translation, functions as the primary institutional hub for human capital development, professional competency enhancement, and internship coordination within the General Directorate. The unit executes an integrated portfolio of human resource development activities encompassing the systematic identification and monitoring of general management training requirements with follow-up to completion and evaluation (20% of workload), the design, organization, and delivery of internal training initiatives including seminars and presentations tailored to the needs of organizational units and services across the Directorate-General (20% of workload), and the procurement, administration, and coordination of external professional development opportunities (20% of workload). Unit 07C30 bears responsibility for managing comprehensive internship programs specifically for the General Directorate of Translation, including coordinated placement, program oversight, and liaison activities with the intern recruitment unit within the General Personnel Directorate (20% of workload). The unit maintains rigorous financial stewardship of budget line 1612-05 allocated for external training expenditures (10% of workload) and sustains critical inter-institutional liaison relationships with training managers across other directorates-general and the learning and development unit of the Directorate-General for Personnel (5% of workload). Through these integrated functions, Unit 07C30 contributes directly to organizational effectiveness by ensuring that personnel possess requisite competencies, that management operates at optimal capacity, that the institution maintains access to both internal expertise and external specialized knowledge, and that the European Parliament's translation infrastructure benefits from systematic workforce development and rigorous administrative and financial controls.
07C30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Training Needs Assessment & Management** (20%): Systematic identification of general management training requirements with comprehensive follow-up of identified needs through to completion and evaluation, ensuring organizational learning objectives are achieved.

• **Internal Training Development & Delivery** (20%): Design, organization, and facilitation of internal training actions, seminars, and presentations tailored to build competencies across multiple units and services within the Directorate-General for Translation.

• **External Training Administration & Procurement** (20%): Management, procurement, coordination, and vendor selection for external professional development programs, including enrollment administration and participant support.

• **Internship Program Coordination** (20%): Comprehensive oversight of internship placements and program administration for the General Directorate of Translation, including coordinated placement activities and liaison with the General Personnel Directorate's intern recruitment unit.

• **Financial Management & Inter-Institutional Coordination** (15%): Stewardship of budget line 1612-05 dedicated to external training expenditures (10%) and coordination with training managers across other directorates-general and the Directorate-General for Personnel's learning and development unit to ensure organizational coherence in human resource development across the European Parliament structure (5%).

07C40.name: 07C40
original_activities: Provide support to end users of the Translation Branch: Provide assistance, resolve technical issues encountered by users of IT systems (hybrid, portable, VDI platforms, etc.), communicate proactively about issues and solutions, troubleshoot hardware and software issues.

Manage the infrastructure, IT assets and access necessary for the IT activities of the Translation Directorate General: Plan, provision, maintain and monitor the IT infrastructure, including hardware, software, network components and user access management.

Provide level 1, and sometimes level 2, support for applications specific to the general translation department: Monitoring of evolutionary maintenance and changes made by the development units concerned, training and documentation, user support, tests to guarantee functionality and security.

Represent the Directorate-General for Translation and coordinate the IT activities of the Directorate-General's units within the EP and at interinstitutional level, for all matters related to IT services.

Managing IT and information security at DG TRAD systems level: Approach and measures to protect IT systems, data and information assets against threats and vulnerabilities - implementation of secure IT architectures, identification and correction of vulnerabilities.
Manage internal IT projects at the General Directorate of Translation: Planning, execution and supervision of IT projects to achieve specific objectives within defined constraints.

Provide technical assistance for business analysis: Help translate business needs and objectives into IT solutions - facilitation of communication between stakeholders and DG ITEC, recommendation of effective technological solutions.
Carry out various other small missions related to the IT activities of the General Directorate of Translation in the broad sense.
07C40.refined_description: Unit 07C40, the Information Technology Service Unit within the Directorate for Resources (07C) of the Directorate-General for Translation, functions as the critical technical backbone enabling operational continuity and digital resilience across DG TRAD's translation, administrative, and institutional functions. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy while collaborating with sibling units 07C10, 07C20, and 07C30, the unit delivers comprehensive IT service delivery, infrastructure stewardship, information security governance, and strategic coordination across all DG TRAD operations at both institutional and interinstitutional levels within the European Parliament. The unit's mission encompasses direct end-user technical support, complete infrastructure asset management and access governance, application-specific support and maintenance, institutional IT representation and cross-directorate coordination, information security and vulnerability management, internal IT project execution, and technical business analysis facilitation. Through these coordinated activities, Unit 07C40 ensures that DG TRAD maintains operational resilience, security compliance, technological alignment with European Parliament standards, and effective integration of business requirements into IT solutions while supporting the broader resource management objectives of the Directorate for Resources.
07C40.refined_activity_summary:
• **End-User Support and Technical Assistance** (25%): Comprehensive technical support delivery to Translation Branch personnel across hybrid, portable, and VDI platforms, including hardware and software issue resolution, troubleshooting, and proactive communication regarding system issues and available solutions.

• **Infrastructure and Asset Management** (24%): Planning, provisioning, maintenance, and continuous monitoring of complete IT infrastructure encompassing hardware, software, network components, and user access management systems; strategic stewardship of all technical assets necessary for DG TRAD operational continuity.

• **Application Support and Maintenance** (23%): Delivery of Level 1 and selective Level 2 support for translation department-specific systems, including monitoring of evolutionary maintenance and changes, comprehensive user training and documentation development, functionality and security testing, and coordination with development units regarding change management.

• **Strategic Coordination and Governance** (9%): Institutional representation of the Directorate-General for Translation at European Parliament and interinstitutional levels; coordination of IT activities and IT service-related matters across all DG TRAD units within broader EP IT governance structures.

• **Information Security and Vulnerability Management** (8%): Implementation of IT and information security measures at systems level, including threat and vulnerability assessment, identification and remediation of security vulnerabilities, and secure IT architecture implementation to protect IT systems and information assets against identified threats.

• **IT Project Management** (5%): Planning, execution, and supervision of internal IT projects designed to achieve specific DG TRAD objectives within defined resource and temporal constraints.

• **Technical Business Analysis and Advisory Support** (5%): Facilitation of business requirement translation into IT solutions through stakeholder communication, technology recommendations, and coordination with DG ITEC to enable effective organizational technology alignment.

• **Miscellaneous IT Operations** (1%): Execution of small-scale missions and emerging operational needs directly related to DG TRAD's broader IT activities and technology requirements.

07D.name: 07D
original_activities: Provide leadership and coordination of Directorate units to ensure the efficient and agile delivery of plain language services to support the EP administration and its MEPs in their multilingual communication with citizens, ensuring that content is clear, high quality, relevant, available in a variety of formats and media and in all official and working languages ​​of the European Union.

Set the management's objectives, coordinate the implementation of new work flows and methods, ensure planning of the management's activities and its new missions, and manage organizational changes resulting from the management's matrix structure. Ensure that the missions of the five units are completed.
Ensure the harmonization and optimization of working methods within the matrix team of language and intercultural professionals, and proofreaders/linguistic checkers.

Ensure cooperation and communication within the directorate, with the other directorates of the General Directorate of Translation and with the requesting services in a spirit of partnership. Provide support and advice, in the area of ​​competence, to the Director General and his hierarchy, as well as to deputies.
Participate in intra- and inter-general directorate committees and working groups, in interinstitutional forums, etc. and promote the language of citizens within the institution and outside it.
07D.refined_description:
**

Unit 07D functions as a Hierarchy Level 1 Direction within the General Directorate of Translation, serving as the strategic executive and coordinative hub responsible for institutional plain language services across the European Parliament. The unit exercises comprehensive leadership and coordination of five specialized subordinate units (07D10, 07D20, 07D30, 07D40, and 07D50) to ensure cohesive, efficient, and agile delivery of multilingual plain language communication services that support both EP administration and individual MEPs in their citizen-facing communications across all official and working languages of the European Union, maintaining consistently high quality standards, relevance, and accessibility across multiple formats and media channels. Unit 07D establishes directorate-level management objectives, coordinates the implementation of new workflows and methodologies, ensures systematic planning of directorate activities and emerging missions, actively manages organizational changes arising from the directorate's matrix structure composition, and guarantees successful completion of all five subordinate units' assigned missions. The unit harmonizes and optimizes working methodologies within its matrix team composition of language and intercultural professionals alongside proofreaders and linguistic checkers, fostering integrated operational consistency and professional excellence across specialized roles. Additionally, Unit 07D ensures continuous cooperation and strategic communication within the directorate, across peer directorates within the General Directorate of Translation, and with requesting services throughout the institution in a partnership-oriented approach, while providing specialized support and advisory services to the Director General and executive hierarchy. The unit actively participates in intra- and inter-general directorate committees, working groups, interinstitutional forums, and external venues, championing citizen-centered language advocacy and principles both within the institution and in external stakeholder environments.

**
07D.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Strategic Leadership and Service Delivery** (40%): Provides directorate-wide leadership and coordination of five subordinate units to deliver efficient, agile, and high-quality plain language services across multiple formats and all EU official and working languages, ensuring content clarity, relevance, and accessibility for EP administration and MEP citizen communications

• **Executive Management and Organizational Governance** (20%): Establishes management objectives, coordinates implementation of new workflows and methodologies, ensures systematic planning of directorate activities and emerging missions, manages matrix structure organizational transitions, and oversees completion of all five subordinate units' assigned missions

• **Professional Harmonization and Methodology Optimization** (20%): Harmonizes and optimizes working methods and practices within the matrix team of language and intercultural professionals, proofreaders, and linguistic checkers to promote operational consistency, coordination, and professional excellence across the directorate

• **Institutional Cooperation and Executive Support** (10%): Ensures intra-directorate and inter-directorate cooperation and strategic communication within the General Directorate of Translation, coordinates with requesting services across the institution in partnership-based models, and provides specialized support and advisory services to the Director General, executive hierarchy, and deputies

• **Interinstitutional Advocacy and Institutional Representation** (10%): Participates actively in intra- and inter-general directorate committees, working groups, interinstitutional forums, and external venues while promoting citizen-centered language principles and advocacy within the European Parliament and external institutional stakeholder contexts

07D10.name: 07D10
original_activities: Provide linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation such as translation, localization, adaptation and transcreation of different EP contents according to plain language principles in text, audio and video formats (subtitling and voice-over).

Ensure the coordination of the work of the members of the team of language and intercultural professionals and linguistic correctors/verifiers (within the Citizens' Language Directorate) as well as control of the workload - Coordination Department.

Ensure the verification (= linguistic and technical revision) of INI texts (PR, RR, PA, AD, compromise amendments), parliamentary questions (QE, QO) and session resolutions and amendments (RE, RC, AM) written in English and French - Editorial verification service.
Ensure the verification (editing) of non-legislative texts on request - Editorial verification service.
Provide writing workshops as well as workshops dedicated to plain language, in English and other languages ​​if necessary; carry out awareness-raising actions on the benefits of clear language.
Provide linguistic support to the authors of the texts to be verified and ensure contact with customers of the Telephone Helpline.
07D10.refined_description:
**

Unit 07D10 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 07D of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Translation, functioning as the institutional hub for plain language services and linguistic quality assurance across parliamentary content. As a leaf node reporting directly to Direction 07D and coordinating with sibling units 07D20, 07D30, 07D40, and 07D50, the unit delivers integrated linguistic services across five complementary operational domains. The unit's primary mission encompasses comprehensive provision of multilingual linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation (40% of workload), including translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of diverse European Parliament content according to plain language principles across text, audio, and video formats—specifically including subtitling and voice-over production. Concurrently, Unit 07D10 maintains critical supervision and coordination responsibilities (23% of workload) over language and intercultural professionals and linguistic correctors/verifiers within the Citizens' Language Directorate, managing team workload allocation, performance standards, and quality control oversight across the directorate. The unit simultaneously fulfills essential editorial verification functions (30% combined workload): mandatory linguistic and technical revision of all INI texts and legislative instruments—including parliamentary reports (PR), resolutions (RR), amendments (PA, compromise amendments), administrative documents (AD), parliamentary questions (QE, QO), and session resolutions and amendments (RE, RC, AM)—in English and French (20%), complemented by discretionary editorial verification services for non-legislative texts on request (10%). Additionally, Unit 07D10 strengthens institutional capacity through educational and awareness-raising initiatives (4% of workload), delivering writing workshops and plain language training programs in English and other languages as required, while providing direct linguistic support to text authors undergoing verification processes and managing customer relations through the Telephone Helpline service (3% of workload). These integrated activities collectively support the European Parliament's institutional commitment to accessible, clear communication while ensuring consistent linguistic quality across all parliamentary outputs.

---

**
07D10.refined_activity_summary:
**

• **Core Linguistic Service Delivery and Content Mediation** (40%): Provision of comprehensive multilingual linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation, encompassing translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of diverse European Parliament content in strict compliance with plain language principles across multiple formats including text, audio, video, subtitling, and voice-over production.

• **Coordination, Supervision, and Quality Management** (23%): Oversight and supervision of language and intercultural professionals and linguistic correctors/verifiers within the Citizens' Language Directorate; management of team workload allocation, performance standards, and quality control across the directorate.

• **Editorial Verification, Legislative Compliance, and Quality Assurance** (30%): Mandatory linguistic and technical revision of all legislative instruments and INI texts—including parliamentary reports (PR), resolutions (RR), amendments (PA, compromise amendments), administrative documents (AD), parliamentary questions (QE, QO), and session resolutions and amendments (RE, RC, AM)—in English and French (20%); discretionary editorial verification services for non-legislative texts on request (10%).

• **Capacity Building, Professional Development, and Institutional Awareness** (4%): Delivery of writing workshops and plain language training sessions in English and additional languages as required; execution of awareness-raising initiatives promoting the benefits of clear communication and institutional linguistic standards.

• **Direct Author Support and Customer Relations Management** (3%): Provision of personalized linguistic support to document authors undergoing verification processes; management of customer inquiries and assistance through the Telephone Helpline service.

07D20.name: 07D20
original_activities: Ensure the linguistic services of intercultural and linguistic mediation, such as translation, localisation, adaptation and transcreation of different content of European Parliament according to the principles of clear language in text, audio and video format (subtitling and voice-over).

Ensure the daily programming of the European Parliament audio web-platform Europarl Audio Capacity with an offer of daily News in Brief in 24 languages, plus different podcasts in six languages related to the activities of the EU and Parliament, and with European music. Ensure the distribution of content on the Citizens' App. Ensure distribution to local and regional radio stations.
Ensure the production of audio versions of stories for My House of European History and support the collection of stories and the production of other formats.
Ensure the production of programmes for other European Parliament services, for example DG COMM and the Office of the Secretary-General.
Train the Directorate-General for Translation staff in how to use their voices for recording purposes and in technical aspects of recording.
07D20.refined_description: Unit 07D20 operates as the specialized Linguistic Services and Multimedia Content Production unit within Direction 07D of the General Directorate for Translation, functioning as the institutional hub for multilingual audio and audiovisual content production across the European Parliament. The unit's integrated mission encompasses the delivery of comprehensive intercultural and linguistic mediation services—including translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of institutional content across text, audio, and video formats—executed in accordance with clear language principles and encompassing professional subtitling and voice-over production (40% of workload). Concurrently, the unit manages daily operational programming and content distribution for the Europarl Audio Capacity web-platform (40% of workload), delivering multilingual News in Brief in 24 languages, producing thematic podcasts in six languages covering EU and Parliamentary activities, curating supplementary European music selections, and ensuring strategic content distribution through the Citizens' App and partnerships with local and regional radio stations. The unit additionally contributes to institutional cultural initiatives through audio production and narrative adaptation for My House of European History (10% of workload), provides specialized audio and multimedia content production services to other European Parliament directorates including DG COMM and the Office of the Secretary-General (5% of workload), and delivers comprehensive technical training to General Directorate for Translation staff in voice recording methodology, vocal technique, and professional recording protocols (5% of workload). Through these integrated activities, Unit 07D20 ensures that the European Parliament's institutional messaging reaches diverse audiences across multiple platforms and linguistic communities while maintaining accessibility and quality standards consistent with institutional communication and accessibility objectives.
07D20.refined_activity_summary:
• **Linguistic Mediation and Audiovisual Content Adaptation** (40%): Provision of comprehensive intercultural and linguistic mediation services encompassing translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of institutional content across text, audio, and video formats, with emphasis on clear language principles, professional subtitling, and voice-over production

• **Multilingual Digital Audio Platform Operations and Content Distribution** (40%): Daily programming and operational management of the Europarl Audio Capacity web-platform, delivery of News in Brief in 24 languages, production and distribution of thematic podcasts in six languages covering EU and Parliamentary activities, curation of European music programming, and strategic content distribution through the Citizens' App and partnerships with local and regional radio stations

• **Institutional Cultural and Historical Content Production** (10%): Production of audio versions and narrative adaptation for My House of European History initiatives, including story collection support and multi-format content development

• **Cross-Directorate Multimedia Service Provision** (5%): Production of specialized audio and multimedia programming content for other European Parliament institutional services, including DG COMM and the Office of the Secretary-General

• **Staff Development and Technical Capacity Building** (5%): Delivery of specialized technical training to General Directorate for Translation personnel addressing voice recording methodology, vocal technique for recording applications, and professional audio production protocols

07D30.name: 07D30
original_activities: Contribute to the implementation within the General Directorate and the EP of the targeted IT solution developed as part of an innovation partnership with suppliers in the voice recognition and machine translation market in order to increase the accessibility of parliamentary debates in real time. Continue to explore the market for artificial intelligence (AI)/natural language processing (NLP) related products.

Establish and strengthen cooperation and good relations with counterparts and users, including the procedural units of the General Directorate of Translation. Promote the new high-quality multilingual services of the Directorate-General for Translation.

Cooperate with other services of the European Parliament and the institutions of the European Union in the field of voice recognition, machine translation and natural language processing (NLP) in general.
Other missions.
07D30.refined_description: Unit 07D30 operates as a specialized technological innovation and stakeholder engagement unit within Direction 07D of the General Directorate for Translation, functioning at the second hierarchical level of the European Parliament's translation infrastructure with explicit responsibility for advancing digital transformation and accessibility across the institution's multilingual communication services. The unit's primary mission (60% of workload) encompasses leading the implementation of targeted information technology solutions developed through strategic innovation partnerships with external suppliers specializing in voice recognition and machine translation technologies, while simultaneously conducting continuous market exploration and analysis to identify and evaluate emerging artificial intelligence and natural language processing products that could enhance real-time accessibility of parliamentary debates across all official languages and strengthen institutional service delivery capabilities. Concurrently, Unit 07D30 dedicates 25% of its operational capacity to establishing, cultivating, and maintaining cooperative relationships with internal stakeholders—particularly procedural units within the General Directorate of Translation—and external end-users, functioning as a strategic liaison that actively promotes and advocates for the Directorate-General's high-quality multilingual service offerings. The unit allocates 10% of its workload to inter-institutional collaboration, specifically coordinating with other European Parliament services and European Union institutions regarding voice recognition, machine translation, and natural language processing technologies, initiatives, and applications. The remaining 5% of capacity is reserved for miscellaneous institutional missions, emerging priorities, and administrative requirements as directed by the parent Direction 07D. Through these integrated and coordinated activities, Unit 07D30 directly supports Direction 07D's strategic mandate for institutional plain language service delivery while maintaining functional alignment with sibling units (07D10, 07D20, 07D40, 07D50) within the broader translation and linguistic services framework.
07D30.refined_activity_summary:
• **Digital Transformation and Technology Implementation** (60%): Lead implementation of innovation partnership-developed IT solutions for voice recognition and machine translation technologies; conduct continuous market exploration and analysis to identify and evaluate emerging artificial intelligence and natural language processing products that enhance real-time accessibility of parliamentary debates across all official languages

• **Stakeholder Relationship Management and Service Promotion** (25%): Establish, cultivate, and strengthen cooperative relationships with procedural units within the General Directorate of Translation and external end-users; function as strategic liaison actively promoting and advocating for the Directorate-General's high-quality multilingual service offerings

• **Inter-Institutional Collaboration in Language Technologies** (10%): Coordinate with other European Parliament services and European Union institutions regarding voice recognition, machine translation, and natural language processing technologies, initiatives, and applications

• **Miscellaneous Institutional Functions and Emerging Priorities** (5%): Execute additional assigned missions, emerging institutional priorities, and administrative requirements as directed by parent Direction 07D

07D40.name: 07D40
original_activities: Provide linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation such as translation, localization, adaptation and transcreation of different EP contents according to plain language principles in text, audio and video formats (subtitling and voice-over).

Provide and manage subtitling and voice-over linguistic services in a cooperative working environment within a cross-functional unit. Organize the work of the unit and establish internal working procedures, with particular emphasis on cooperation with other non-procedural units and procedural units of the Translation Directorate.

Contribute to improving the quality of subtitling and voice-over services through specific training, skills sharing and quality assurance and control measures. Develop technical expertise in audiovisual translation and innovative IT solutions.
Contribute to the use of clear language in subtitling and voice-over products in 24 languages.
07D40.refined_description: Unit 07D40 operates as a specialized audiovisual translation and linguistic mediation unit within Direction 07D of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Translation, functioning as a critical service provider for the institution's plain language communication infrastructure. The unit's primary mission encompasses the delivery of comprehensive linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation—including translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of diverse European Parliament contents—across multiple delivery formats (text, audio, and video) in accordance with established plain language principles. Operating as a leaf-node organizational entity without subordinate structures, Unit 07D40 maintains collaborative relationships with sibling units (07D10, 07D20, 07D30, 07D50) under Direction 07D's strategic oversight while functioning within a cooperative, cross-functional working environment that necessitates both internal operational leadership and inter-unit collaboration with procedural and non-procedural units throughout the Translation Directorate. The unit simultaneously develops advanced technical expertise in audiovisual translation methodologies and pioneering information technology solutions while contributing substantially to institutional quality assurance through dedicated quality improvement initiatives, specialized training programs, inter-unit skills-sharing mechanisms, and rigorous quality control measures. Unit 07D40 maintains specialized focus on promoting and implementing clear language principles across all subtitling and voice-over products delivered in the European Parliament's complete portfolio of 24 official languages, ensuring accessibility and comprehension across the institution's multilingual audience.
07D40.refined_activity_summary:
• **Core Audiovisual Translation & Linguistic Mediation Services** (40%): Provision of comprehensive linguistic services encompassing translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of European Parliament contents in accordance with plain language principles, delivered across text, audio, and video formats with particular specialization in subtitling and voice-over production.

• **Service Management, Operational Coordination & Inter-Unit Collaboration** (40%): Management and delivery of subtitling and voice-over linguistic services within cooperative, cross-functional environments; organizational leadership of internal unit operations; establishment of standardized internal working procedures and procedural frameworks; and facilitation of strategic collaboration with both procedural and non-procedural units throughout the Translation Directorate.

• **Quality Assurance, Professional Development & Technical Innovation** (15%): Implementation of quality improvement initiatives through targeted training programs, inter-unit skills-sharing mechanisms, and rigorous quality assurance/control measures; development of advanced technical expertise in audiovisual translation methodologies; and research, development, and implementation of innovative information technology solutions to enhance operational efficiency.

• **Multilingual Plain Language Standardization** (5%): Promotion and application of clear language principles specifically within subtitling and voice-over products distributed across the European Parliament's 24 official languages to ensure institutional communication accessibility and audience comprehension.

07D50.name: 07D50
original_activities: Provide linguistic services for intercultural and linguistic mediation such as translation, localization, adaptation and transcreation of different EP contents according to plain language principles in text, audio and video formats (subtitling and voice-over).

Facilitate and promote the sharing of testimonies between European citizens via the MyHEH platform; conceptualize and create audiovisual content in 24 languages ​​based on these testimonies and publish them on the platform, on the Citizens' App and through other channels.
Increase the visibility and accessibility of MyHEH through awareness campaigns among universities, museums and archives, media and other stakeholders and explore possibilities for collaboration.
Other missions.
07D50.refined_description: Unit 07D50 operates as a specialized operational unit within Direction 07D of the European Parliament's General Directorate of Translation, functioning as a dual-mandate center for linguistic services and citizen engagement that directly supports the institution's multilingual accessibility and democratic participation objectives. The unit delivers comprehensive linguistic mediation and content adaptation services encompassing translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation of diverse European Parliament content in accordance with plain language principles, with delivery capabilities spanning multiple formats including text, audio, and video (including specialized subtitling and voice-over production). Concurrently, Unit 07D50 manages and promotes the MyHEH (My Europe, Her History) platform as a critical bridge between European citizens and institutional content, facilitating the conceptualization, creation, and publication of multilingual audiovisual content in 24 languages derived from citizen testimonies, with subsequent distribution across the MyHEH platform, the Citizens' App, and complementary dissemination channels. The unit strategically enhances MyHEH's visibility and accessibility through targeted awareness campaigns and outreach initiatives directed at universities, museums, archives, media organizations, and other key institutional stakeholders, while actively exploring and developing collaborative partnership opportunities to expand platform reach and impact. Operating as a leaf node within the organizational hierarchy without subordinate units, Unit 07D50 functions in coordination with its sibling units (07D10, 07D20, 07D30, 07D40) under the strategic direction of parent unit 07D, with capacity reserved for additional institutional missions as assigned.
07D50.refined_activity_summary:
• **Linguistic Mediation and Multilingual Content Adaptation Services** (40%): Comprehensive provision of translation, localization, adaptation, and transcreation services for European Parliament content across text, audio, and video formats (including subtitling and voice-over production), guided by plain language principles to ensure accessibility and clarity for diverse audiences and intercultural communication objectives

• **MyHEH Platform Management and Multilingual Audiovisual Content Production** (40%): Facilitation and promotion of citizen testimony sharing through the MyHEH platform, including conceptualization, creation, and publication of audiovisual content in 24 languages derived from European citizen testimonies, with distribution across the MyHEH platform, Citizens' App, and complementary communication channels

• **Stakeholder Engagement and Platform Visibility Enhancement** (15%): Implementation of strategic awareness campaigns and visibility promotion initiatives targeting universities, museums, archives, media organizations, and other institutional stakeholders, combined with identification and active development of collaborative partnership opportunities to expand MyHEH's reach, impact, and accessibility

• **Ancillary Institutional Missions** (5%): Execution of supplementary organizational assignments and responsibilities as directed by parent unit 07D and institutional priorities
